entries: A knuckle duster is a simple melee weapon, usually made of metal or another hard material, worn around the knuckles of one hand. They deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage on a hit and have the Attached, Light, and Pugilist properties.

baseitem.name: Nunchaku
entries: Nunchaku are martial melee weapons composed of two hard batons connected to one another by a short chain or tether.

baseitem.name: Slingshots
entries: Slingshots are simple ranged weapons comprising an elasticated material strung between a Y-frame brace.

baseitem.name: Spiked Cesti
entries: A spiked cestus is a simple melee weapon worn around the knuckles of one hand that deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage on a hit and has the Adaptable (piercing), Attached, and Pugilist properties.

baseitem.name: Starknives
entries: Starknives are martial melee weapons; three to five double-edged blades set on a central handle that are most effective as thrown weapons but can also be used for close combat. They deal 1d4 piercing damage on a hit, can count as a monk weapon, and have the Adaptable (slashing), Finesse, Light, Thrown (range 20/60), and Thrown Versatility (1d6) properties.

baseitem.name: Tetherhooks
entries: A tetherhook is a martial melee weapon. It is comprised of a hook attached to a chain or strap

baseitem.name: Twinblades
entries: Twinblades are martial melee weapons; double-bladed polearms that rely on the user's dexterity to keep them in constant motion.

baseitem.name: War Crescents
entries: War crescents are simple melee weapons consisting of large, convex curved blades, meant to be wielded with one or two hands. They deal 1d6 slashing damage on a hit, and have the Versatile (1d8) property.

itemType.name: Magitech Firearm
entries: Magitech firearms (martial ranged weapons) use a small arcanomagnetic engine to propel their ferrous projectiles at incredible velocities. Though their means of propulsion is magical, the damage they deal remains nonmagical. Such firearms are always loud and tend to have poor accuracy over long ranges, although the recently-introduced rifling found in revolvers and rifles increases their effective range.

entries.name: Extra Reloads
entries: More expensive models of magitech firearms have a self-reloading mechanism that harnesses the arcanomagnetic engine to re-cock the weapon. If you wish, you can increase the magazine capacity of a magitech firearm up to a maximum of 6, ensuring that the price increases proportionally. For example, a pistol typically costs 200 gp and has reload (2). If you increased this to reload (6), you would multiply the price by 3 for a new cost of 600 gp.

itemTypeAdditionalEntries.name: Magitech Firearm
entries: Magitech firearms (martial ranged weapons) use a small arcanomagnetic engine to propel their ferrous projectiles at incredible velocities. Though their means of propulsion is magical, the damage they deal remains nonmagical. Such firearms are always loud and tend to have poor accuracy over long ranges, although the recently-introduced rifling found in revolvers and rifles increases their effective range.

entries.name: Extra Reloads
entries: More expensive models of magitech firearms have a self-reloading mechanism that harnesses the arcanomagnetic engine to re-cock the weapon. If you wish, you can increase the magazine capacity of a magitech firearm up to a maximum of 6, ensuring that the price increases proportionally. For example, a pistol typically costs 200 gp and has reload (2). If you increased this to reload (6), you would multiply the price by 3 for a new cost of 600 gp.

entries.name: Ichorous Blight
entries: While wielding this dagger, you can use a bonus action to imbue its next strike with an inky-black ichor that gets injected into its victim. The next time you hit a creature with the dagger before the end of your next turn, it must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes an additional {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage and is {@condition Blinded} for {@b 1 minute}. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition Blinded}. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Eyes of the Forgotten
entries: This ring has {@b 1 charge}, represented by a single open eye, and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Evil Eye
entries: As an action, you can expend {@b 1 charge}, thrusting the ring towards a creature you can see within {@b 60 feet} of you while speaking the command word. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be affected by a randomly determined effect from the {@spell Eyebite} spell (asleep, panicked, or sickened) for {@b 1 minute}.

A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. While a creature is under this effect, the eye on the ring becomes an inky void, imbued with dread power. When the effect ends, the eye closes.

itemGroup.name: Lance of the Crab Knight

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Being underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

entries.name: Crab Knight
entries: While holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell Phantom Steed} spell. The steed always takes the form of a giant centenary crab, which has a swimming speed equal to its walking speed. Once this property of the lance has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Clawful
entries: Once on each of your turns, you can replace one of the attacks you make with this lance as part of the Attack action with an attempt to capture a creature with it, causing the missing half of the crab's claw to appear as a spectral, shimmering force and close itself around the target. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be {@condition Grappled} by the lance for {@b 1 minute} (escape {@dc 15}), until you release the target, or until you are no longer holding the lance (no action required).

While you're grappling a creature with the lance, you can only use the lance to attack that creature, crushing it with the claw. These attacks deal Bludgeoning damage instead of Piercing damage, deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage on a hit, and don't suffer from {@b disadvantage} as a result of you being within 5 feet of the target.

items: Lance of the Crab Knight (Very Rare)|LALoTT

itemGroup.name: Rapier of the Yellow King
entries: You can speak and understand Deep Speech.

entries.name: Imposing Presence
entries: While holding this rapier, you can cast the {@spell Dominate Person} spell (save {@dc 17}). Once the spell has been cast in this way, it can't be cast again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Shackles of Pain
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose one creature you can see within {@b 60 feet}. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 17} Charisma saving throw or become cursed and bound to you for {@b 1 minute}. So long as it is on the same plane of existence as you, whenever you take damage from any source, the cursed creature takes half as much damage of the same type. This effect can be removed by any spell or effect that removes curses. Once this property of the rapier has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: Rapier of the Yellow King (Very Rare)|LALoTT

entries.name: Tentacle Lash
entries: As an action, you can speak the ring's command word to cause your arm to elongate into a long, wet, suckered tentacle for {@b 1 minute}. As part of that action, and as an action on each of your turns, you can perform a melee spell attack ({@hit +5} to hit) against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d8} Bludgeoning damage and, if it is a Huge or smaller creature, it must make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw. On a failure, you can choose one of the following effects:

items: You disarm the target; you take one item or weapon the target is carrying or wielding (and that isn't fastened, e.g. armour) into your tentacled hand. If your tentacled hand isn't free, the item falls at your feet instead.

entries: Once this property has been used, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, it can't be used again until the ring spends 1 continuous hour submerged in water.

itemGroup.name: Runestone of Ocean Currents
entries: This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Saltbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the stone, you can speak its command word, expending 1 charge and causing a frothy, turquoise bolt of force to strike a target within {@b 120 feet} of you. The target takes cold damage and force damage. A creature under the influence of the {@spell shield} spell or similar magic takes no damage from this effect.

itemGroup.name: Sceptre of the Sunken King
entries: You can use the sceptre as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Power of the Depths
entries: While holding this rod, you gain benefits relating to the ocean.

items: You have resistance to cold damage.
You have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

While at least waist-deep in water, you have {@b advantage} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, and can cast any of the following spells at any level between its normal level and 4th-level (save {@dc 16}): {@spell control water}, {@spell create or destroy water}, {@spell riptide|LALoTT}, {@spell water breathing}. After you do so, roll a {@dice d6}. If the result is equal to or less than the level the spell is cast at, the sceptre's eye goes dim and you can't use the rod to cast spells in this way until the next dawn.
Sceptre of the Sunken King (Very Rare)|LALoTT

itemGroup.name: Breastplate of the Sunken King
entries: This armour has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lurker in the Deep
entries: While wearing this armour, you have the following benefits:

items: You can't float in water and instead sink at a rate of {@b 60 feet} per round. Walking on the bed of a water body doesn't cost you extra movement.

entries.name: Sunken King's Favour
entries: As an action while wearing the armour, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell bigby's hand||arcane hand} spell. When cast in this way, the spell is modified as follows:

items: You create two hands instead of one. They take the form of Medium spectral tentacles that sprout from the shoulders of the Breastplate. They occupy your space, move with you, and can't be moved away from you. You can only command one of the tentacles with your bonus action each turn.
The reach of 'Clenched Fist', 'Forceful Hand', and 'Grasping Hand' is increased to {@b 15 feet}.
Breastplate of the Sunken King (Very Rare)|LALoTT

itemGroup.name: Fishfolk Sword
entries: This weapon has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Blessing of the Deep
entries: As a bonus action, you can coat this blade with water from a water source within your reach and make a quick prayer to the Deep One, expending 1 charge and turning the coating to flawless ice for {@b 1 minute}. While coated in ice, the blade deals an extra {@damage 1d6} cold damage to any target it hits.

itemGroup.name: Sea Serpent's Tooth
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Sea Serpent's Might
entries: As an action, you can slam the tooth into the ground or thrust it into a body of water, creating a shockwave in a {@b 30-foot cone}. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 5d10} thunder damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't knocked prone. Once this property of the club has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Blood Trail
entries: As an action, you can add one drop of a creature's blood to the candle, causing it to imprint on that creature. Once a bloodwax candle has imprinted on a creature, it can't imprint on another. You can then light the candle as an action, taking {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage as your blood is drawn from your body to form a ghastly red trail in the air, leading directly to the creature the candle has imprinted on. For every 10 minutes that the candle remains lit, it deals another {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage to the creature who lit it. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.
Once the candle has burnt for 1 hour in total, its wax is depleted and can't be used again. Each time the candle is lit and extinguished, a minimum of 10 minutes' worth of wax is consumed.

The trail is undisturbed by wind of any strength and can't pass through solid objects, but will navigate through holes as small as 1 inch in diameter. If all paths to the creature are blocked by solid objects, the trail ends as close to the creature as possible. The candle can't distinguish between identical twins or clones, and will instead create a trail to each. If the creature is on another plane, the pall of red smoke listlessly hangs in the air.

entries.name: Beastblood Serum
entries: The dial around the plunger can be rotated to select one of the following symbols, changing the hue of the blood within: bear (iridescent green); bull (sanguine red); cat (silky purple); eagle (piercing yellow); fox (opulent orange); and owl (midnight blue). As an action, you can select a symbol and inject the blood in the syringe into a willing creature within your reach. The creature gains the benefits of the {@spell enhance ability} spell that correspond to the chosen animal for 10 minutes. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dark Empowerment
entries: While wearing this mask, you have two additional 1st-level spell slots, which you regain daily at dawn. In addition, you can cast the {@spell augury} and {@spell commune} spells from the mask. Once the mask has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn. When you cast {@spell commune} using the mask, your questions are answered by the deity or power associated with this item: an entity of death and decay.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This mask is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. When you first don this item after attuning to it, the silver tentacles reshape and tighten around your neck. While cursed, you can't remove the mask, and each time you cast a spell, you take {@damage 1d4} psychic damage.

entries.name: Ethereal Haunt
entries: As an action, choose a creature within {@b 60 feet} of you that can hear you. It must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw as occult whispers wrack its psyche. The DC for this saving throw increases by 1 for each creature friendly to you also wearing cultist robes within 15 feet of you (maximum 5). On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 3d6} psychic damage, becomes {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute, and must immediately use its reaction to move a distance equal to its speed away from you, avoiding obviously dangerous ground. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage, isn't {@condition frightened}, and doesn't move away.

A creature {@condition frightened} of you in this way can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dark Devotion
entries: If you spend 1 hour in prayer to the being in whose name these robes were made (which can be done as part of a short or long rest), you gain {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Eldritch Light
entries: When found, this candle has enough wax to burn for 1 hour. As an action, you can light this candle, causing it to emit dim light in a 15-foot radius. Whenever a creature other than you moves within 15 feet of the candle for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, the creature must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw as shadowy tentacles lash out at it. The creature takes {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more has its speed reduced to 0 feet by the tentacles until the start of its next turn.

No amount of wind can extinguish the candle. The candle can be extinguished by you as an action, by a creature within 5 feet of you that uses its action to make a successful {@dc 15} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, or by the use of a {@spell dispel magic} spell or similar magic. Each time the candle is lit and extinguished, a minimum of 10 minutes' worth of wax is consumed.

entries.name: Lifelink
entries: When a creature willingly drinks from the chalice, its soul becomes linked to the item, causing one of the eyes to burst aflame. Once a second creature willingly drinks from the chalice, both eyes light up and the tentacles writhe and stretch. The two creatures' souls are linked together for the next 7 days. During this time, if either creature dies, the other one does too, and both eyes return to a dark, waiting state.
While two souls are linked, drinking from the chalice has no effect. Unlinking the souls early can't be achieved by any means short of a {@spell wish} spell.

Once a creature drinks from the chalice, there is no limit to the interval before another creature must drink from it. Indeed, finding a dead man's chalice with one eye aflame is an intriguingly mysterious dilemma.

entries.name: Ritual Branding
entries: As an action, you can place a brand on yourself, choosing from the list below and taking {@damage 1d8} fire damage. The brand lasts until the next dawn, or until removed by a {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic. Once you brand yourself, you can't do so again until the next dawn.

items.name: Brand of the Devout
entries: As a bonus action while you hold this weapon, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus (minimum 2). You regain one expended spell slot of a level up to half the number of Hit Dice expended.

items.name: Brand of the Flagellated
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack with this weapon, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus, dealing an additional {@damage 2d6} damage of the attack's type per Hit Die expended.

items.name: Brand of the Zealot
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus. You have {@b advantage} on the next number of attack rolls equal to the number of Hit Dice expended that you make before the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Forbidden Flower
entries: The skull contains 7 petals. While holding it, you can use an action to pluck a petal to cast the {@spell guiding bolt} spell ({@hit 5} to hit), dealing necrotic damage instead of radiant damage. The skull regrows {@dice 1d3} plucked petals daily at dawn. If you pluck the last petal, the skull crumbles to dust and is destroyed.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This skull is cursed, and plucking three or more petals in a day extends this curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you are unwilling to part with the skull, keeping it within reach at all times, and whenever you finish a long rest, a black petal grows on your head. These black petals can't be removed physically, and any attempt to do so causes you to take {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. If you travel to a different plane while cursed, you grow an additional {@dice 1d4} black petals. When the seventh petal has grown on your head, you die and your body turns to dust, except for your skull, which becomes a new infested cultist's skull. If the curse is removed, the petals on your head and the infested cultist's skull crumble to dust and are destroyed.

itemGroup.name: Juvenile Spawn Elixir
entries: When you drink this potion, you become the host of a parasitic scleraworm. The parasite burrows through your flesh, dealing {@damage 3d8} necrotic damage to you. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way. Upon reaching your brain, it erodes its way through your skull, appearing in the centre of your forehead.

entries.name: Mutually Beneficial?
entries: For as long as you host the parasite, you have resistance to psychic damage and can use a bonus action to speak telepathically to creatures you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. You can cast the {@spell detect thoughts} spell at will (save {@dc 15}), taking {@damage 4d8} psychic damage when you do so. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

entries.name: Proliferation
entries: Whenever you finish a long rest, you must succeed on a {@dc 10} Intelligence saving throw or gain an additional eye on your head. You automatically fail if you roll a 1 on the d20. Once you have ten additional eyes, you become completely overwhelmed by the parasite and are no longer in control of your actions.

entries.name: Removal
entries: Over the course of an hour, a creature can perform an operation to remove the parasite from a willing creature. The operating creature must make two {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine} or surgeon's tools) checks. If the creature succeeds on both checks, the scleraworm is removed and can be bottled. If only one is successful, there is no change. If the creature fails both checks, both patient and the scleraworm are killed.

itemGroup.name: Runewarding Ring
entries: This item can hold a maximum of 3 charges and loses all charges daily at dawn. As an action while holding it, you can slice open your hand, taking {@damage 1d4} slashing damage and expending a Hit Die, and smear the fresh blood across the symbol. If you do, the glowing veins flare a bright, molten red, and the ring gains {@dice 1d3} charges. This slashing damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

entries.name: Blood Ward
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and press the ring against a nonmagical surface or object, marking it with a glowing red symbol visible only to you. The next creature other than you that comes within 5 feet of the symbol must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 5d10} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A mark fades when it deals damage, after 8 hours, or if you use the ring to create a second mark.

itemGroup.name: Thurible of the Elder Deacon
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The flail has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Gaseous Spells
entries: While holding it, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it (save {@dc 13}): {@spell fog cloud} (1 charge), {@spell misty step} (2 charges), and {@spell stinking cloud} (3 charges). You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend.

itemGroup.name: Twinspawn Maulhammer
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can separate this maul into two warhammers, or convert them back to a maul. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with either form of this magic weapon.

entries.name: Duplicate
entries: When you separate the warhammers, or as a bonus action when they're already separated, you can cause one of the warhammers to float in the grasp of an illusory double of yourself for 1 minute. When you create the illusion, and as bonus action on each of your turns, you can move the duplicate and hammer up to 20 feet to a point you can see and make a melee weapon attack against a target within 5 feet of the hammer. Use your Charisma modifier for attack and damage rolls made in this way. The duplicate can't be harmed, though it can be dispelled by a {@spell dispel magic} spell or similar magic. A creature that succeeds on a {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check or that physically interacts with the duplicate perceives that it is illusory and that the weapon is floating.
Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Andvaranaut
entries: Glowing with tiny motes of pulsing light, this brilliant golden ring is inscribed with runes of wealth and prosperity.

entries.name: Andvari's Gift
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the ring's command word—"plunder"—to form a trail of softly glowing golden dust that leads to the nearest source of gold worth at least 5 gp that isn't currently carried or worn by a creature. The trail can't pass through solid objects but will navigate through holes as small as 1 inch in diameter. If all paths to the gold source are blocked by solid objects, the trail ends as close to the gold as possible. The trail disappears immediately if the gold source is destroyed, transported to another plane, or picked up by a creature.

itemGroup.name: Allfather's Eye
entries: You gain a bonus to your passive Perception score while you wear this magic ring.

entries.name: Wise Beyond Years
entries: Immediately after you make a Wisdom check while wearing this ring, you can expend 1 charge to roll an additional d20, using the higher of the two results.

entries.name: Selective Attunement
entries: If this item was crafted using your own eye, it doesn't require attunement. However, if you ever regain your eye (such as by the {@spell Regenerate} spell), the allfather's eye requires attunement.

itemGroup.name: Branch of the Worldtree
entries: This wand can be used as a spellcasting focus for your druid or ranger spells and has charges, represented by its green-gold leaves. When you expend a charge, a leaf withers and drifts to the ground. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the wand withers and petrifies, losing all magical properties.

entries.name: Regrowth
entries: The wand can enhance your healing spells. When you cast a spell that restores hit points and deals no damage while holding the wand, you can expend a number of charges equal to the spell's level to increase that level by 1. You can use this property only once per casting.

entries.name: King of Plants
entries: While holding the wand, you can cast the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip at will. In addition, you can expend charges to cast certain plant-based spells.

itemGroup.name: Drakenhorn
entries: This horn has 5 charges, symbolised by the waxed cloth wrapped five times around the horn. For each charge expended, one loop of the cloth magically burns away. Over the course of 10 minutes, you can wrap a new cloth around the horn while singing a ballad of a legendary dragon. As long as no properties of the horn are used, the horn regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges 8 hours later. Whenever a property calls for a cone, the size of the cone equals 10 times the number of charges expended in feet.

entries.name: Embolden
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges as you blow into the horn, creating a loud noise audible from as far away as 300 feet. All creatures in a cone originating from you become immune to the {@condition frightened} condition for the next minute.

entries.name: Charm Drake
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges and blow into the horn, creating a sound that is beguiling to dragons and audible to all creatures within 300 feet. Each dragon in a cone originating from you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you for 1 hour. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw is immune to the horn's Charm Drake property for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Roar
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges as you blow into the horn, creating a loud noise audible from as far away as 300 feet. Each creature in a cone originating from you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effects on itself on a success. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw is immune to the horn's Roar property for the next 24 hours.

itemGroup.name: Gullinbursti Tusks
entries: You can attune to two of these daggers and they count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. While wielding two of these daggers, you gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with them, and you can use the Boar's Charge and Spirit of the Golden Boar properties.

entries.name: Boar's Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet straight towards a creature and then immediately hit it with an attack using this dagger, the target takes additional piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Spirit of the Golden Boar
entries: You call forth Gullinbursti's residual power. As an action, you can speak the command word, aim your daggers, and a golden boar charges in a 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line from you. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Isfjell
entries: As a reaction to taking damage, or as a bonus action, you can spin the ring's jewel, causing it to expand into a hollow block of clear ice that completely surrounds you. This breaks any grapples in which you are involved and pushes any other creatures in your space out of it. The iceberg occupies your space and creatures can't pass through it, regardless of size. There is enough space within the iceberg to turn around, but you can't move more than 6 inches in any direction. Spells and other magical effects can't extend through the iceberg or be cast through it. While encased in ice, you can't take any reactions, and the only action you can take is a bonus action to cease the jewel's spinning, causing the effect to end.

The iceberg takes as much of any triggering damage as possible. The iceberg can't regain hit points, has immunity to cold, poison, and psychic damage, and has vulnerability to fire damage. Creatures that hit the iceberg with a melee attack take {@damage 2d8} cold damage. The iceberg lasts up to 1 minute, until it is reduced to 0 hit points, or until you use a bonus action to cease the jewel's spinning. When it ends, you can choose to have it explode, launching sharp shards of ice at all creatures within 15 feet of you. Each creature must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking cold damage and piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the ring spends 8 continuous hours in snow, ice, or cold water.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to the ring, you gain resistance to cold damage while you wear it.

itemGroup.name: Skinwalker Staff
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a bard, druid, or ranger

entries.name: Spells
entries: This staff can be used as a spellcasting focus for your bard, druid, or ranger spells. While wielding the staff, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast various spells, using your spell save DC.

If you expend the last charge, the skull on the staff withers and cracks, losing its magic. As part of an 8-hour ritual, the skull can be replaced by that of a beast slain within the last 24 hours. At the next dawn, the staff regains charges as normal.

entries.name: Wolf Form
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell polymorph} spell on yourself. When cast in this way, the spell doesn't require concentration, and you can only transform into a specific wolf form, ignoring the spell's normal restrictions on CR and creature type.
You can end this spell early at any time (no action required). Once this property of the cowl has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Spear of the Stormsinger
entries: When inactive, this weapon appears and behaves as an elegantly adorned metal quarterstaff.

entries.name: Crackling Tip
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak this magic weapon's command word, causing crackling lightning to appear at its tip. The weapon becomes a spear, sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet, and deals lightning damage instead of piercing damage. While activated, whenever you make a ranged attack with this spear, a small lightning bolt shoots forth; you need not throw the weapon itself. You can use a bonus action to cause the lightning to disappear and the weapon once again becomes a quarterstaff.

entries.name: Allfather's Wrath
entries: As an action, you can cast certain spells. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you spend 1 hour outside singing a storm song in praise of the Allfather (which can be done as part of a short or long rest). Upon completion, a lightning bolt strikes the weapon and Allfather's Wrath can be used again.

itemGroup.name: Worldshaper
entries: While this weapon is on your person, you can speak, read, and write Dwarvish. In addition, you have {@sense {@sense darkvision}} out to a range of 60 feet. As an action, you can feel the ground, gaining tremorsense out to a range of 15 feet until the end of your next turn. If you are underground, this distance is doubled to 30 feet.

entries.name: Stone Shaper
entries: While holding the axe, you can cast various earth-related spells. After you cast one of these spells in this way, roll a {@dice d8}. If the result is less than or equal to the spell's level, this property of the axe can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This weapon is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the weapon, keeping it within reach at all times. While cursed, each time you finish a long rest, you must make a Constitution saving throw. A dwarf has disadvantage on this saving throw. On a failure, you slowly begin to petrify and your speed is reduced by 5 feet. When your speed becomes 0 feet due to this curse, you become fully petrified, even if your attunement to this weapon ends. Each time {@spell lesser restoration} is cast on you, your speed increases by 5 feet, up to your normal maximum.

itemGroup.name: Aldrnari Blade
entries: This magic weapon has charges and regains expended charges after it spends 8 continuous hours in fiery embers. As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to ignite the blade for {@b 10 minutes}, or until you sheathe it. Flames erupt along the sword's length; it sheds bright light in a {@b 40-foot radius} and dim light for an additional 40 feet, and it deals extra fire damage on a hit.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: If you undergo a branding ritual to become bonded to the blade, it gains the Brand property. In addition, when the weapon regains charges, it regains all expended charges. Once you have undergone this branding ritual, you can't willingly end your attunement to the blade until another creature willingly undertakes the branding ritual, at which point it becomes attuned to the sword instead.

entries.name: EA: Brand
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell heat metal} spell.

itemGroup.name: Frostblessed
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Hypothermia
entries: When you hit with an attack using this weapon, you can attempt to absorb the target's heat. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take cold damage, have its speed halved, and be unable to take reactions until the end of your next turn. After you use this property, roll a d6. On a 1-2, you can't use this property again until the next dawn. This property has no effect on creatures with immunity to cold damage.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: When you slay a giant while wielding this blade, you can choose to accept the Allfather's blessing, enhancing your attunement to it. If you accept the blessing, you gain access to the Fallen Brothers property. Once you accept the blessing, you can't willingly end your attunement to the blade until another creature kills a giant while wielding the weapon and chooses to accept the Allfather's blessing, at which point it becomes attuned to the sword instead.

entries.name: EA: Fallen Brothers
entries: As an action, you can kneel and utter a prayer. In response, the gates of Valhalla briefly open and a wave of spectral warriors attack all creatures in a cone originating from you. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking cold damage and radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until you spend 1 hour recounting the tales of past heroes, an act of prayer to the Allfather (which can be done as part of a short or long rest).

itemGroup.name: Frostrune Axe
entries: This axe has charges and regains expended charges after it spends 8 continuous hours in snow, ice, or cold water. As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to cause cold mist to emanate from the axe for {@b 10 minutes}. It glows a soft blue, shedding bright light in a {@b 10-foot radius} and dim light for an additional 10 feet, and deals extra cold damage on a hit.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement: Brand
entries: Over the course of an hour, you can undergo a branding ritual to become bonded to the axe, gaining access to the Razorice property. In addition, when the weapon regains charges, it regains all expended charges. Once you have undergone this branding ritual, you can't willingly end your attunement to the axe until another creature willingly undertakes the branding ritual, at which point it becomes attuned to the axe instead.

entries.name: EA: Razorice
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell spike growth} spell, creating spikes of razor-sharp icicles.

itemGroup.name: Suneater Axe
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The axe has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Suneater
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell darkness} spell. When you cast the spell in this way, you can choose to expend 1 additional charge. If you do, you can see through the magical darkness of the spell while holding the suneater axe.

entries.name: Gleipnir
entries: When you make an attack with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to launch the axe's blade from the wolf's mouth, attached to the weapon by a long thread. Instead of a melee weapon attack, make a ranged weapon attack against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you, using your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or become {@condition grappled} as the thread wraps itself around it. A creature that fails this saving throw by 5 or more is {@condition restrained} while it is grappled in this way. Huge or larger creatures have disadvantage on the saving throw. The grappled creature or a creature within reach of it can attempt a Strength check, ending the grapple on a success. The grapple also ends if you pull the axe's blade back towards the weapon (no action required) or if you let go of the axe.

While you have a creature grappled in this way, you can't attack with the suneater axe. As a bonus action, you can try to reel the creature in, making a Strength (Athletics) check, contested by one from the grappled creature. On a success, you pull the target up to 15 feet towards you.

itemGroup.name: Winter's End
entries: While holding this bow, you can cast the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip. In addition, when an arrow is nocked on this bow, the arrow immediately lights up, emanating blue mist and shedding bright light in a {@b 15-foot radius} and dim light for an additional 15 feet. This light lasts for {@b 1 minute} after the arrow is shot from the bow.

entries.name: Winter Hunt
entries: This weapon deals extra cold damage to each creature it hits that is in freezing temperatures or is standing on snow or ice.

entries.name: Ice Shower
entries: As an action, you can choose a point you can see within the normal range of this weapon and fire a volley of arrows. These arrows levitate in the air when they enter a radius centred on that point. For {@b 1 minute}, or until all the arrows are used, whenever a creature other than you enters the area for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, one arrow shoots at it. The creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take piercing damage and cold damage. Once this property of the bow has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Blast & Slash
entries: A rudimentary firearm, this item has been modified and mounted with an axe head fashioned from scrap metal. Remnants of oak wood on the handle and the firing mechanism are all that is left from the original firearm. The result is a magical axe-blunderbuss hybrid that can only be used at point blank range due to its poor precision and limited blast power.

entries.name: Point Blank Blast
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can activate its firing mechanism (no action required). When you do so, the creature takes extra piercing damage from the attack and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or suffer severe burns for 1 minute. A burned creature must repeat the saving throw at the start of each of its turns, taking fire damage on a failed save, or ending the effect on a successful one. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you spend 1 hour rearranging the mechanisms, which can be done as part of a short or long rest.

itemGroup.name: Heavy Mobile Drill
entries: A wonder of mining technology, this drill can turn even the most meagre gnome into a walking excavator.
While wearing this item, you can't jump. However, you can use the drill arm to raise you up to 10 feet off of the ground (no action required). You can't use the drill's properties while raised.

entries.name: Weapon
entries: While wearing this item, you can use it as a simple melee weapon that doesn't require the use of your hands. You are proficient with this weapon, which deals {@damage 1d10} piercing damage on a hit. You have {@b advantage} on melee attack rolls made with the drill against creatures made of inorganic material such as stone, crystal, or metal, and it deals double damage to structures.

entries.name: Excavate
entries: As an action, you can choose a portion of nonmagical soil, sand, or rock within 5 feet of you that fits within a 10-foot cube and excavate it, depositing it up to 10 feet away.

entries.name: Owlsight
entries: While wearing these goggles, you have {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet (if you already have {@sense darkvision}, wearing the goggles increases its range by 60 feet) and attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged weapon attack rolls. In addition, you have {@b advantage} on Perception checks made to spot Small or smaller creatures.

itemGroup.name: Privateer Coat
entries: Worn to shield airship crewmates against cold and violent winds, this jacket is equipped with pockets and pouches fit for repair tools and weapons. Some coats are adorned with decorative shoulder pads and pins to distinguish rank and aerial prowess.

entries.name: Built for the Skies
entries: While wearing this coat, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to overcome the effects of cold weather or violent winds. You also have {@b advantage} on saving throws against effects that deal cold damage. Finally, you have {@b advantage} on Charisma checks against any creature that perceives itself to be below the rank indicated by the coat. When you find this coat, roll a {@dice d4} to determine its rank:

itemGroup.name: Scorching Fists
entries: While wearing these gloves, you can handle white-hot metal without fear of burning yourself. This item has 10 charges. Over the course of 1 minute, you can fill the gloves' fuel canisters with 10 gp of high quality oil, causing it to regain {@dice 1d8 + 2} expended charges. Once this item regains charges, it can't regain charges again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Scorching Fist
entries: While wearing this item, you can expend 1 charge (no action required) to deal additional fire damage whenever you hit with an unarmed strike with your fist on your turn.

entries.name: Overheat
entries: As an action, you can expend 3 charges to remove the gloves' limiter, causing a flaming blade to blossom from each one. The gloves shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. These blades last for 1 minute or until you extinguish them (no action required). They are simple melee weapons with the Finesse and Light properties, with which you are proficient. Each deals fire damage on a hit.

itemGroup.name: Sir Wrenchinald the Third
entries: Sir Wrenchinald the Third is an eccentric wrench, given sentience after years of being used to create items imbued with magic.

The wrench is contained in a scabbard; unsheathing it reveals a blade and allows it to be used as a dagger instead of a mace. If you aren't attuned to the wrench when you unsheathe the dagger, you take {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage as the wrench closes around your hand.

entries.name: Foul Mouth
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to cast {@spell vicious mockery}. When the spell is cast in this way, Sir Wrenchinald hurls insults at the target.

entries.name: Pun Master
entries: As an action, you can hold out Sir Wrenchinald toward a creature, causing it to spurt out a series of puns as you cast {@spell Tasha's Hideous Laughter|hideous laughter} on the creature. This property can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: The wrench is a sentient lawful neutral mace with an Intelligence of 18, a Wisdom of 10, and a Charisma of 14. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 120 feet. The wrench communicates by speaking out loud and can speak, read, and understand Binary (a language spoken in the Clockwork Arcadia), Common, and Gnomish.

entries.name: Personality
entries: While Sir Wrenchinald is being wielded, it emits a constant stream of peculiar observations and insults, directed at anyone who would listen. Sir Wrenchinald values intelligence and mechanical skill above all, and may occasionally challenge its wielder with contests of wit in order to test them. Conflict arises if it feels that its wielder doesn't match its intelligence or makes decisions it views as foolish. Although Sir Wrenchinald acts as if all challenges are beneath it, it is easily intrigued by a puzzle or strange problem.

itemGroup.name: Technomancer Staff
entries: A focus for pliers of electrical currents and lightning-based magic, this staff represents the evolution of wizardcraft and artifice. While old-fashioned mages potter about with candles and arcane runes, the new blood embraces technology. This staff contains the latest in magic energy catalysts, allowing it to recycle and redirect residual magical energy.
Whenever you cast a spell that deals lightning damage while holding this quarterstaff, roll a die and add the result to one lightning damage roll of that spell against one of its targets.

entries.name: Lightning Rod
entries: When you or a creature within 20 feet of you takes lightning damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage as the staff absorbs the energy.

entries.name: Residual Charge
entries: After you cast a spell that deals lightning damage or use the Lightning Rod property, purple crackling energy gathers at the tip of this quarterstaff. The next time you hit with a melee attack using this weapon within the next minute, you deal extra lightning damage to the target.

itemGroup.name: B.O.N.K.
entries: When you hit a Small or larger creature with an attack using this weapon, it gains {@b 1 charge} as the pressure within builds, up to a maximum number of charges. If no charges are expended for {@b 1 minute}, the weapon loses all charges as it depressurises.

entries.name: B.O.N.K
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to activate its built in engine and boost the next attack you make with it before the start of your next turn, which deals an extra {@damage 1d8} bludgeoning damage on a hit.

entries.name: B.O.O.O.N.K
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can expend 3 charges to fire a bolt of lightning in a {@b 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line}. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Shadowglide
entries: Whenever you fall from a height of 10 feet or more while wearing this armour, you can use a reaction to glide. While gliding, you move 1 foot horizontally for each 1 foot you fall, take no falling damage when you land, and have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks to move silently.

entries.name: Take-off
entries: As a bonus action, you can activate your wings and gain a flying speed of {@b 30 feet} for {@b 10 minutes}. Once this property of the armour has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Death From Above
entries: While wearing this armour, you can use a bonus action while in the air to fold yourself into a diving position and move straight down. If you make a melee weapon attack against a creature immediately after moving in this way and hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage for every 10 feet you moved, and you can immediately move another 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If the attack misses, you crash, taking {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet you moved and landing {@condition prone}.

itemGroup.name: Sparkblade
entries: This item has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Arcanodynamo
entries: While holding this weapon, you can cause it to regain expended charges by swinging it vigorously for {@b 10 minutes} without interruption. At the end of this time, make a {@dc 10} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check. The item regains expended charges on a successful check, or half as many charges on a failed one. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Lightning Blade
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can pull the trigger 1, 2, or 3 clicks, energising the blade for {@b 1 minute} as described below:

items.name: 1 Click
entries: You expend 1 charge. For the duration, each time you hit with an attack using this weapon, you deal extra lightning damage.

items.name: 2 Clicks
entries: You expend 3 charges. For the duration, each time you hit with an attack using this weapon, you deal extra lightning damage. The first time you hit a creature with this weapon each turn, the creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be unable to take reactions until the start of its next turn.

items.name: 3 Clicks
entries: You expend 6 charges. For the duration, each time you hit with an attack using this weapon, you deal extra lightning damage. The first time you hit a creature with this weapon each turn, the creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be unable to take reactions until the start of its next turn. If it fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it is {@condition stunned} until the start of its next turn.

itemGroup.name: Bracers of the Jaguar Warrior

entries.name: Jaguar Spirit Claw
entries: While wearing these bracers, you can use a bonus action to speak their first command word, causing three radiant claws to appear from the gems. For the next minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your unarmed strikes deal radiant damage instead of bludgeoning damage.
You can add your Wisdom modifier to the damage rolls of your unarmed strikes.

entries.name: Jaguardian
entries: While you wear these bracers, you can speak their second command word to cast the {@spell spirit guardians} spell, calling forth jaguar spirits to protect you.

items: Bracers of the Jaguar Warrior (Very Rare)|LALoTT

itemGroup.name: Helmet of the Dino Slayer
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Intimidation} checks.

entries.name: Saurian Resilience
entries: When a creature whose size is larger than yours reduces you to 0 hit points with a melee attack while you're wearing this helm, make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 10 or half the damage taken (whichever is higher). On a successful save, you instead drop to 1 hit point. Success or failure, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: One with Nature
entries: You have mastered survival in hostile jungles. While wearing this helm, you can cast the {@spell animal friendship}, {@spell speak with animals}, and {@spell locate animals or plants} spells from it. Once the helmet has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

items: Helmet of the Dino Slayer (Very Rare)|LALoTT

entries.name: Jungle Dweller
entries: You ignore difficult terrain caused by thick vegetation.

entries.name: Landscaper
entries: You have perfected the art of navigating through dense foliage and vines, and know exactly where to strike with your machete to make quick work of any plant. You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with this weapon against plants and on Tracking checks made to navigate in jungle terrain.

entries.name: Leech
entries: As a bonus action, you can attach the slug to a piece of ammunition or load it as ammunition in a blowgun or sling. The next time you hit with a weapon using the ammunition, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become numbed as the slug sinks into the target's skin. For each slug attached to a numbed creature, its speed is reduced and it takes necrotic damage at the end of each of its turns. A creature can use its action to make a Strength check, dislodging all leeches currently attached to it or a creature within its reach on a success. The slugs have no effect on incorporeal creatures, constructs, elementals, oozes, or undead. The slugs die after being dislodged from a creature and can't be reused.

itemGroup.name: Mace of the Torchbearer
entries: As an action while holding it, you can cast the {@spell sacred flame} cantrip.

entries.name: Spread the Light
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the weapon's command word, igniting a magical flame surrounded by small floating debris. It sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. While ablaze, the mace deals an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage to any target it hits. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you are no longer holding the mace.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you. While cursed, you are unwilling to part with the mace, keeping it within reach at all times, and you can hear the Sun God speak to you through the flames. When you move within 30 feet of a source of fire at least 1 foot in diameter for the first time on your turn or start your turn there, you must make a {@dc 10} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you must spend the rest of the turn moving towards the fire by the shortest safe route. You touch the fire when it's within reach, taking {@damage 1d8} fire damage. On a successful saving throw, or after touching the fire, you come to your senses and are immune to the call of this particular fire for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Bark Armour
entries: As an action, you can place the shield on your torso and speak its command word, causing the wood and vines to wrap around your figure. For 1 hour, or until you use an action to end this effect, you are unable to hold a shield, and you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain the benefits of the {@spell barkskin} spell (no concentration required).
You have {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}.

entries: When this effect ends, the overgrown barkshield falls from its place on your torso, fading to a lifeless shade of grey. This property can't be used again until the next dawn, when the shield blooms with life once more.

entries.name: Vines and Thorns
entries: While touching this shield, you can use an action to animate one of the shield's thorny vines and make a melee weapon attack against one target within 30 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes piercing damage, and, if the target is a Large or smaller creature or an object that weighs less than 500 pounds, you can pull it up to 10 feet closer to you.

itemGroup.name: Scorching Wrath
entries: This dagger has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Eruption
entries: As an action, you can thrust the dagger into the ground and expend 1 charge. A 5-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder of lava erupts, centred on a point you can see on the ground within 30 feet of you. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Lunch Break
entries: As an action, you can put the cloak on a flat, nonmagical surface up to 5 feet wide and 10 feet long. It adapts to the surface and matches its appearance. If a creature uses its action to examine the area the cloak is in, the creature can spot the hidden carnivorous item with a successful Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check. If a creature enters the space occupied by the cloak, it must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Afterwards, or if a creature that has spotted the item uses its action to make a Dexterity check, the cloak shifts and detaches from the surface. You automatically succeed on this check.

entries.name: Take-Out
entries: When a creature enters a space within 10 feet of you, you can use your reaction to have the cloak pounce and wrap around the creature if the cloak is within 10 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check or become {@condition grappled} by the cloak. A Gargantuan creature automatically succeeds on this check. While grappled in this way, the creature is {@condition restrained} and takes acid damage at the end of each of its turns. When a creature frees itself, the cloak falls to the ground and moves 10 feet closer to you at the start of each of your turns, until it reaches you. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: The cloak must feast on the corpse of at least one Small or larger creature every 24 hours. Failing to do so causes it to slowly digest you during a long rest. When you finish a long rest, you recover one less Hit Die and take {@damage 2d6} acid damage upon waking up. Removing the cloak fails to end the curse and it always seems to find its way back to you by the end of a long rest.

itemGroup.name: Ceremonial Macuahuitl
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a creature with a Strength of 16 or higher
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The sword has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Eclipse!
entries: While holding this sword in sunlight, you can expend 1 charge to ask the Sun God to hide its presence and cast the {@spell darkness} spell.

entries.name: Might of the Sun God
entries: While holding this sword under the night sky or in darkness, you can expend 1 charge to call upon the help of the Sun God and cast the {@spell daylight} spell. You may also be able to expend charges to cast the {@spell sunbeam} spell.

itemGroup.name: Arm'onica
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a creature missing an arm
entries: You must have a missing arm to attune to the arm'onica. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing arm and can't be removed against your will. In addition, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells, and if you are a bard, you gain a bonus to your Bardic Inspiration rolls.

entries.name: Dissonant Harmonies
entries: As an action, you can blow into the arm'onica, choosing to cast certain spells.

Once this item has been used to cast a spell, roll a die. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, the arm'onica becomes clogged and this property can't be used again until the next dawn, when it magically cleans itself.

itemGroup.name: Eye of the Planesrunner
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a creature missing an eye
entries: You must have a missing eye to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing eye, you can see through it as if it were a normal eye, and it can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Planar Magic
entries: As an action, you can channel the eye's planar energy to cast certain spells.

Once this item has been used to cast a spell, roll a die. If the result is equal to or lower than the level of the spell, the eye's energy floods into your brain; you take psychic damage equal to twice the number rolled and this property of the eye can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Flesh Symbiont, Hand
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a spellcaster missing a hand
entries: You must have a missing hand to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing hand and functions as a normal hand. The results aren't always perfect; roll {@dice 1d6 + 1} to determine the number of digits your new hand has. The first digit is always a thumb, and a mouth forms in the hand's palm.

entries.name: Cost
entries: The symbiont feeds off of you, syphoning your magical energy. Each time you finish a long rest, you must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you lose one spell slot of the lowest level you can cast.

entries.name: Mockery
entries: You can use the hand to cast the {@spell vicious mockery} and {@spell words of discouragement|LALoTT} spells at will, using your spell save DC. If a creature succeeds on its saving throw against a {@spell words of discouragement|LALoTT} spell cast in this way, the mouth blurts out insults and mockeries at you, causing you to become timorous for 1 minute, as per the spell. As a bonus action while timorous, you can punish your insolent hand, taking {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage, restoring your sense of self-worth, and ending this effect.

entries.name: Symbiotic Nature
entries: The symbiont can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the symbiont ends and it falls off, reverting to a dormant lump of flesh.

entries.name: Revive
entries: As an action, you can press this heart to the chest of a humanoid corpse within reach. The heart burrows into the corpse's chest, consuming any existing heart. Over the course of 1 hour, it grows to resemble the original heart, returning the creature to life.

entries.name: Curse
entries: Pressing the heart into a creature forcibly attunes the creature to the heart and prevents it from being removed by any means. If the creature is already attuned to the maximum number of items, it loses its attunement to one at random. While attuned to the heart, the creature is cursed and has disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks when it, the target of its attack, or whatever it's trying to perceive is in direct sunlight. In addition, it gains resistance to necrotic damage and can use the Symbiotic Vigor property. If a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic is cast on the heart, or a creature ends its attunement to it, it rots and is destroyed, killing its host.

entries.name: Symbiotic Vigor (Requires Attunement)
entries: The heart has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} charges daily at dawn. These charges manifest as lidless eyes appearing on random locations across your body. While the heart is inside you, you can use your reaction when you take damage to expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell false life} spell, gaining the temporary hit points immediately before you take the triggering damage. You can't use this property of the heart while in direct sunlight. Whenever you expend a charge, one of the eyes closes.

itemGroup.name: Mechanical Tail
entries: When you attune to the mechanical tail, it attaches to your lower back and can't be removed against your will. You can use the tail to make grapples and have advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to grapple certain creatures with it.

entries.name: Steam-Powered Strike
entries: As an action, you can sweep your tail with wicked, steam-powered ferocity. Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes slashing damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful one, it takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the tail has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, the tail runs out of steam and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Ornate Ceramic Prosthesis
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a creature missing a limb
entries: You must have a missing limb to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it reshapes itself to take the place of your missing limb, functions as a normal limb, and can't be removed against your will.

When you make an unarmed strike using this prosthesis, its plating morphs into a sharp, ceramic blade. Unarmed strikes made with the prosthesis count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage and deal slashing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Full Plating
entries: As an action, you can speak the prosthesis' command word to activate the ceramic plating. It grows to cover your entire body, giving you the appearance of an articulated porcelain doll for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain a +2 bonus to AC. Additionally, you have vulnerability to bludgeoning damage and resistance to certain damage.

itemGroup.name: Phantom Limb
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a creature missing a limb
entries: You must have a missing limb to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing limb, functions as a normal limb, and can't be removed against your will. As a bonus action, you can cause the phantom limb to disappear, or manifest again. If you have multiple phantom limbs, they count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items to which you can attune.

entries.name: Mage Limb
entries: As an action, you can cause the limb to detach from your body and manipulate it like the hand produced by the {@spell mage hand} spell. The limb vanishes when you dismiss it (no action required) and reappears attached to you once more.

entries.name: Limber
entries: The reach of unarmed strikes made with the phantom limb and the reach of attacks made with weapons that have the Light property held by the phantom limb increases by 5 feet.

itemGroup.name: Spiritual Armblade
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a cleric, druid, monk, paladin, ranger, or warlock missing a hand
entries: You must have a missing hand to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it wraps itself around the site of your lost hand and can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Summon Armblade
entries: As a bonus action, you can summon a simple melee weapon made of spiritual energy from the prosthesis, or cause it to disappear. You are proficient with this weapon and use your spellcasting ability for the attack rolls made with this weapon (or your Wisdom modifier if you are a monk). The weapon deals damage on a hit. Its appearance and damage type are linked to your alignment, as detailed below:

items: {@b Good Alignment:} A golden blade that deals radiant damage.
{@b Neutral Alignment:} A translucent-white weapon that deals force damage.
{@b Evil Alignment:} A shaft of bruised mauve that deals necrotic damage.

entries.name: Shield
entries: When a creature you can see attacks you while the weapon is summoned, you can use your reaction to shift it into a shield, gaining a bonus to AC against the triggering attack. The shield reverts to a weapon immediately after.

itemGroup.name: Tail of the Mermalia
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a creature missing its legs
entries: You must be missing your legs to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, the tail grafts into place, the scales slowly petering out into your normal skin, and can't be removed against your will. You gain the shapechanger type and can breathe bGV|DMG air and water.

entries.name: Morph
entries: As an action, you can morph your tail into legs or vice-versa, gaining the benefits indicated below.

items.name: Legs
entries: You have a walking speed of 20 feet. You can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.

items.name: Tail
entries: You have a swimming speed of 60 feet and advantage on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid being grappled or restrained while underwater. On land, you are always prone, can't stand up, and have a walking speed of 5 feet.

itemGroup.name: Wings of Prey
entries: When you attune to the wings, they attach to your back and can't be removed against your will. As a bonus action, you can speak the wings' command word to cause them to disappear or reappear. Additionally, the wings have charges and regain expended charges daily at dawn. While the wings are present, you have a flying speed equal to 5 times the number of remaining charges in feet.

entries.name: Razor Sharp
entries: As an action while the wings are present, you can expend charges to hurl a flurry of razor-sharp feathers in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Assemble the Mob!
entries: When you open this satchel as an action, you can designate up to four willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of you to be 'multiplied' for 1 hour. Each affected creature sprouts nine illusory duplicates that all have minor alterations: different clothes, hair, and heights. The duplicates surround and follow the original and seem completely real, including sounds, smells, and temperature appropriate to the creature depicted. So long as you and any multiplied creatures remain within 100 feet of the satchel, these duplicates muddle about, shouting vaguely angry phrases such as "Get them!", "Begone!", and "Why is existence vacuous and happiness illusory!?" If a duplicated creature is ever more than 100 feet away from a satchel, its duplicates vanish.

A creature that uses its action to examine the mob can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check, ascertaining which members of the mob are real creatures.

If a creature targets a member of the mob with an attack, roll a {@dice d10}. On a 10, it hits a real creature within the mob (GM's choice). On a 1-9, it hits one of the duplicates, who doesn't react, causing the creature to gain {@b advantage} on all Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks to see through the illusion, and it can immediately make one as part of its attack. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Inner Monster
entries: Tied up in a protective claw is a philter of bubbling red liquid. You can use an action to drink this potion, filling you with a sense of rage and selfishness as your muscles inflate and your veins bulge. You turn into a brute, the physical and mental manifestation of your most evil and selfish thoughts, for 10 minutes, and undergo certain changes.
When the effect ends, you return to normal and make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of {@condition exhaustion}.

itemGroup.name: Gentleman's Umbershield
entries: This wand regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Reflect
entries: When you are hit by an attack that you can see while holding the wand, you can use your reaction to open the umbrella and roll a die. You expend charges equal to the result of the roll, or however many remain in the wand (whichever is lower), and gain a bonus to AC equal to the number of charges expended until the start of your next turn. If this results in the triggering attack missing you, the attack bounces back at the creature that made it using the original attack roll, potentially causing the attacker to hit itself.

itemGroup.name: Graveshredder
entries: This magic weapon can also act as a lute. If you are proficient with a stringed instrument, you are proficient with this battleaxe, can use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells, and can use the Shredder property. The battleaxe has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shredder
entries: While holding this axe, you can expend charges in the following ways:

items: As an action, you can sweep-pick a sick lick, expending 2 charges to cast the {@spell speak with dead} spell. Whenever you ask questions, the 'question and answer' takes the form of a 'call and response' duet between you the guitarist, and the corpse as a singer.

Over the course of 1 minute, you can rip a face shredding solo, expending 2 charges to cast the {@spell animate dead} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, you command creatures created by it by playing a killer riff.
As an action, you can fingerpick a mournful instrumental ballad, expending 4 charges to cast the {@spell revivify} spell. Casting the spell in this way requires its material components.

entries: When you expend 1 or more charges, you must make a Dexterity or Charisma (stringed instrument) check (your choice). The DC for this check equals 10 + 1 for each charge expended. On a success, the spell is cast as normal. On a failure, the spellcasting action, any material components, and the charges are wasted.

entries.name: Grave's Embrace
entries: Trailing from your back, ragged scraps of cloth and bandage float in an ethereal wind, even on the calmest of days. As an action, you can speak the cloak's first command word, causing shreds of it to pull away and begin entangling a Large or smaller creature you can see within 10 feet of you. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained} for 10 minutes. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way takes necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns as it desiccates. A creature can use its action to make a Strength check, breaking the restraints and freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success.
As a bonus action, you can speak a second command word, releasing the target and ending the effects as the scraps of cloth fall lifelessly to the ground.

itemGroup.name: Glimmerbolt
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Glimmerbolt
entries: When you take the Attack action on your turn while holding this crossbow, you can speak its first command word, expending 1 charge and causing a piece of radiant ammunition to load into the crossbow. The next attack you make with the crossbow before the end of your turn deals radiant damage instead of piercing damage, and deals an extra radiant damage on a hit.

entries.name: Lumoflare
entries: As an action while holding this crossbow, you can speak its second command word and expend 3 charges to fire an especially bright bolt at a point within {@b 60 feet} of you, where it detonates. Each creature in a {@b 20-foot radius} must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn. After this flash, the light, now dimmer, hangs in the air for {@b 1 minute}, shedding bright light for 20 feet, and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

itemGroup.name: Palisade
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical weapon.

entries.name: Fencing
entries: As an action, you can stab palisade into the ground, causing a 1-inch-thick wrought iron fence to burst from the earth at a point you can see within {@b 60 feet} of you, twisting into a latticework of thorny vines. It can be either 30 feet long and 10 feet high or form a ringed wall up to 20 feet in diameter and 20 feet high. Each 5-foot-square section has an AC of 10, 15 hit points, and immunity to psychic and poison damage. The wall doesn't block line of sight, but Small or larger creatures can't move through it.

When the fence appears, each creature within its area is pushed to one side of the fence (your choice) and must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} piercing damage. A creature attempting to climb the fence must make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, with {@b advantage} if it has a climbing speed. On a failure, the creature slips, taking {@damage 2d8} piercing damage and falling {@condition prone} at the base of the fence.

Ranged attack rolls that pass through the fence are made with {@b disadvantage}. The fence persists for {@b 10 minutes}, until you lose concentration (as if concentrating on a spell), or until it is destroyed, whereupon it crumbles into dust. Once you have used this property, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Quicksilver Blade
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Freeflow
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to cause its form to shift to that of any melee weapon with which you are proficient that lacks the {@itemProperty 2H|Two-handed} and {@itemProperty H|Heavy} properties.

entries.name: Throw
entries: When you make an attack with this weapon, you can instead flick a shard of the quicksilver blade towards a target, making a ranged weapon attack (range 30/90) that uses your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. On a hit, it deals the weapon's normal damage plus an extra {@damage 2d6} poison damage, or {@damage 4d6} if the target is a shapechanger. Once this property is used, the quicksilver blade becomes a dagger with no other properties until the next dawn, when it regrows to its original form and regains all expended uses of this property.

itemGroup.name: Sawbones' Bonesaw
entries: You have {@b advantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) checks made to perform surgical procedures using this sword.

entries.name: Amputation
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can speak its command word, causing it to momentarily become incredibly sharp and able to cut through limbs with even a glancing blow.
