The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes extra slashing damage and loses a randomly determined limb (use the table below). On a success, it takes half as much damage and doesn't lose a limb. Creatures with legendary resistances automatically succeed on this saving throw.

How the creature is affected depends on the creatures' anatomy and is up to the GM's discretion. If the GM decides the creature can't lose a limb, this property deals extra slashing damage. Below are examples for a humanoid.

rows: The target's walking speed is halved and it has {@b disadvantage} on Dexterity saving throws.

The target drops whatever it is holding in this hand (if any) and has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls using its arms. If the target has a feature or trait that allows it to make multiple attacks using its arms, it makes one fewer.
You cut off one of the creature's heads. The creature dies if it can't survive without the lost head.

entries.name: Avoiding Amputation
entries: If you don't want to include amputation in your game, replace the slashing damage with psychic damage and have the Amputation property instead temporarily disable the target's limb until it finishes a short or long rest.

itemGroup.name: Arcanomagnetic Rings
entries: This pair of rings forms an arcanomagnet. One, a brilliant gold that reflects light in wondrous prismatic hues, is intensely magical, while the other, a dull grey that grows darker as the surrounding light brightens, is its antimagical opposite. By creating pockets of magical and antimagical energy, you can bend the Weave, causing magic to veer off its intended trajectory.
These rings can only be attuned to as a pair and count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to.

entries.name: Spellbend
entries: Whenever a creature or point you can see within a certain range of you is targeted by a ranged spell attack, an attack using a magic ranged weapon or magic ammunition, or a spell with an area of effect centred on a point, you can use your reaction to redirect it.

You cause a ranged attack to target a different target of your choice (after the attack roll is made but before the outcome is determined). Alternatively, you move the area of effect's point of origin. After you use this reaction, roll a die. On a 1-2, the rings become demagnetized and can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Leaden Manacles
entries: The stink of burnt flesh and vapours of boiling sweat waft from the wrists of the shackled spellcaster. As you watch, the creature mutters an incantation and the lead begins to glow once more. The creature lets out a pained wail, its concentration broken, its wrists seared.

entries.name: Arcane Forbiddance
entries: You can use an action to place these manacles on an {@condition incapacitated} creature. The shackles adjust to fit a creature of Small to Large size. Leaden manacles behave as normal manacles with the following addition: A creature that attempts to cast a spell while wearing the manacles must make an ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell's magic is cancelled; the action to cast the spell is wasted but the creature doesn't expend a spell slot or a use of the spell. The creature also takes fire damage plus extra fire damage per level of the spell on a failed check, or half as much damage on a success.

entries.name: Suppression Serum
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can inject some of the dagger's fluid into the creature. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be unable to concentrate on spells until the end of its next turn. In addition, if it tries to cast a spell before the end of its next turn, it must make an ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, it wastes its action but doesn't expend a spell slot or use of the spell. This property can be used multiple times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Deflect Spell
entries: When you are hit by a spell attack or subjected to a spell or magical effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to reduce or avoid the damage taken while holding this shield, you can use your reaction to deflect some of the spell's energy. When you do so, the damage you take from the attack or effect is reduced by a certain amount.

If you reduce the damage to 0, the spell has no effect on you, and you can attempt to reflect the spell's raw energy at its origin. Make a ranged attack roll against the target, dealing force damage equal to the result of your damage reduction roll on a hit. Once this property of the shield has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Applying the Gag
entries: As an action, you can attempt to gag a creature you are grappling. Make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check contested by the target's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. On a success, you get the gag in place and the leather straps magically fasten.

entries.name: The Gagged
entries: The gagged creature can't verbalise in any way, which includes speaking, casting spells that require a verbal component, or even simply humming and grunting.

entries.name: Removing the Gag
entries: You and any creature you designate when you use the gag can use an action to remove it. The gagged creature or a creature within reach of it can use its action to make a Strength or Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check, removing the gag on a success. The {@spell knock} spell can also unfasten the leather straps, causing the gag to fall off.

entries.name: Pandora's Box
entries: As an action while holding this box, you can place it down and unlock it, causing it to slowly and eerily creep open. At the end of your turn, black mist spews forth in a 20-foot-radius sphere centred on the box, soon followed by writhing, ethereal purple tentacles that flail and thrash wildly.

Each creature that starts its turn in the area or moves into it for the first time on its turn must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature is under the effect of a spell or concentrating on one, it has {@b disadvantage} on the saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes psychic damage, it loses concentration if it is concentrating on a spell, and any magical effect or spell that is affecting it is dispelled up to a certain level. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and no additional effects occur.

As an action, a creature within reach of the box can attempt a Strength check, closing it on a success. Otherwise, after 1 minute, these abyssal creatures satiate themselves, retreat back into the box, the effect ends, and the box can be closed normally. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Lichslayer
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Soultrap
entries: This weapon can store a maximum number of charges. When you use this weapon to kill a creature capable of casting spells, the creature's soul is trapped in the weapon, which gains a number of charges equal to half the creature's total number of Hit Dice. As these charges are expended, the creature's soul is shredded. A creature whose soul is trapped or shredded can't come back to life by any means short of a {@spell true resurrection} spell or similar magic.

entries.name: Empower
entries: When you hit a target with an attack using this weapon, you can expend charges, dealing extra force damage for each charge expended.

itemGroup.name: Magehunter Blade
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Dispel Magic
entries: When you hit a target that is under the effect of one or more spells with an attack using this weapon, you can choose to dispel one of the spells affecting it if the spell is of a certain level or lower. If you do, the target takes {@damage 1d6} force damage for each level of the spell (dispelled cantrips deal no extra damage). Once a spell is dispelled, roll a die. If the number is equal to or lower than the level of the dispelled spell, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Absorption
entries: While wearing this armour, when you are subjected to a magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success and only half as much damage on a failure. This property can trigger a number of times before it can't trigger again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Spellbreak
entries: While wearing this armour, acid, cold, fire, lightning, and thunder damage you take from magical sources, as well as magical healing you receive, is reduced.

entries.name: Spelldrain
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this ammunition, it projects a sphere of magic suppression, centred on the creature, for {@b 1 minute}. When a creature in the sphere tries to cast a spell, it must make an ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell fails. On a success, it casts the spell as normal. After this property of the ammunition is used, the ammunition is destroyed.

itemGroup.name: Coralborn Ring
entries: This coralborn-crafted trinket is often gifted to visiting dignitaries to protect them during journeys through the abrasive subterranean coral tunnels.

entries.name: Coralskin
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to speak its command word, casting the {@spell coralskin|LALoTT} spell on yourself. This property regains all expended uses daily at dawn.

itemGroup.name: Coral Wand
entries: Such wands often take months to fashion, requiring the formation of an entire coral ecosystem. From molluscs to cnidarians and seaweeds, the inhabitants of such an item share one common theme—they all carry a deadly toxicity.
While holding this wand, you can cast the {@spell poison spray} cantrip.

entries.name: Toxic Discharge
entries: When you cast {@spell poison spray} using the wand, you can incite one of the organisms inside it to give the spell an extra boost. Roll a die and consult the table below to determine what additional effect the target suffers on a failed save. The wand wilts and the Toxic Discharge property can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Hat of the Hermit Hag

entries.name: Hag's Magic
entries: While wearing this hat, you can use it to cast certain spells. When you use the hat to cast a spell, roll a die. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, the hat can't be used to cast any spells until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hermit
entries: As a bonus action while wearing the hat, you can hide inside of it, causing it to expand in order to accommodate you. Until you emerge, you gain three-quarters cover from attacks and effects originating from outside the hat, your speed can't exceed 5 feet, you are {@condition blinded}, you have {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks, and you automatically fail Dexterity saving throws. You can use your action to emerge from the hat. If you are knocked {@condition prone} while within the hat, your underbelly is exposed, and you no longer have three-quarters cover.

items: Hat of the Hermit Hag (Very Rare)|LALoTT

itemGroup.name: Ghost Cutlass
entries: This item appears to be a round, rusted hilt. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause an ethereal blade to spring into existence, or make the blade disappear. You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals psychic damage instead of slashing damage.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

itemGroup.name: Shrimp Pistol
entries: This pistol fires its ammunition at such tremendous velocities that cavitation bubbles form, dealing extra thunder damage to targets it hits.

entries.name: Cavitation Blast
entries: As an action while you are underwater and holding this weapon, you can release a series of pressure waves from the pistol, causing pockets of cavitation. Each creature in a cone must make a Constitution saving throw, taking thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property of the pistol has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Misfire
entries: If you roll a 1 on your attack roll with this weapon, the ammunition itself disintegrates, clogging the barrel. The attack automatically misses and you must spend an action to unclog this weapon before it can be used again.

entries.name: Anchor
entries: As an action, you can point this weapon at a point on a surface within 60 feet of you, causing an ethereal chain to tether you to that point for the next 10 minutes, or until you dismiss it as a bonus action. For the duration, you can't be willingly moved away from that point, except by a weight of 8,000 pounds or more, or if a creature trying to move you succeeds on a {@dc 25} Strength check, which causes the chain to snap and the effect to end. The effect also ends early if you ever willingly move more than 60 feet from the point or if you fall unconscious.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

itemGroup.name: Trident of the Electric Eel

entries.name: Dinglehopper
entries: As an action, you can command this weapon to magically shrink down to the size of a common oyster fork, known as a dinglehopper, or to return to its normal size.

entries.name: Discharge
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack using this weapon, you can speak its command word as a bonus action, causing it to emit a burst of lightning. On a hit, the target and each creature within 5 feet of it (or within 10 feet of it while underwater), including you, must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take lightning damage.

entries.name: Lightning
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can cast a spell. Once you do so, the Discharge and Lightning properties can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: Trident of the Electric Eel (Very Rare)|LALoTT

entries.name: Urchin Nature
entries: This magic weapon deals piercing damage instead of bludgeoning damage. You can use it to make a ranged weapon attack (range 20/60) called a spikeshot, which uses your Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls and deals {@damage 1d6} piercing damage.

entries.name: Spikey Death Balls
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can fire all the flail's spikes at a point you can see within 60 feet of you, where they blossom into a field of squirming, darting, baby urchins. When a creature enters this area for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, it must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature moving through the area at half speed makes this saving throw with {@b advantage}. On a failed save, a creature takes piercing damage and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until it takes an action to remove the urchins. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced. Once this property of the weapon has been used, the weapon loses its Urchin Nature and Spikey Death Balls properties until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

itemGroup.name: Scythe of the Headless Seahorseman

entries.name: Fearflame
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the weapon's first command word, causing an eerie green flame to shimmer along the blade's length for {@b 1 minute}. For the duration, whenever you hit with this magic weapon, the target takes extra psychic damage and must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of you until the start of your next turn. This property can be used a number of times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Find Steed
entries: As an action, you can speak the scythe's second command word and cast the {@spell find steed} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, it always summons an amphibian seamare, which has the statistics of a {@creature giant sea horse|MM} with the following changes:

items: It has a walking and swimming speed of 60 feet.
It can breathe both air and water.

It can use an action to roar, forcing each creature of its choice within 60 feet of it that can hear it to succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of the seamare for {@b 1 minute}. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to this action for 24 hours.

entries: Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

items: Scythe of the Headless Seahorseman (Very Rare)|LALoTT

itemGroup.name: Allure
entries: While wearing this circlet, you can cast the {@spell dancing lights} cantrip.

entries.name: Lure
entries: While wearing this circlet, you can use your action to briefly create an alluring pattern of glowing lights around you. Each creature within a certain distance of you who sees the pattern must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes {@condition charmed} until the start of your next turn. While {@condition charmed} in this way, the creature is {@condition incapacitated} and must use all its movement to approach you. If a creature ends its movement within 5 feet of you, it reaches towards your circlet. As a reaction, you can cause the circlet to bite down on the creature, dealing piercing damage and poison damage to it.

entries.name: Diurnal Dream
entries: As an action during the day, you can play one of the following tunes, targeting one creature that can hear you within 30 feet of you, which must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or suffer the tune's effects as described below. The effect ends at the start of your next turn, unless you immediately use your action that turn to continue playing the tune, which extends the duration until the end of your next turn. You can play a tune uninterrupted for a maximum of 1 minute.

items: {@b Charming Melody.} The creature is {@condition charmed}. The effect ends early if you or any of your allies harm the creature. When the effect ends, the creature knows it was {@condition charmed} by you.
{@b Gentle Lullaby.} The creature falls asleep and becomes {@condition unconscious}. The effect ends early if the creature takes damage or if someone uses an action to shake the creature awake.

{@b Mesmerising Canon.} The creature is {@condition incapacitated}, using all its movement to follow you. If it is unable to follow you, or if doing so would harm it, it remains motionless until the effect ends. The effect ends early if the creature takes damage or is ever more than 30 feet from you.

entries.name: Nocturnal Nightmare
entries: As an action during the night, you strike a dissonant chord that scrapes at the hearing organs and cochlear nerves of all creatures in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes psychic damage and is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition frightened}. After this property has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1-2, all the small feeding medusozoa retreat back into the coral and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Prismatic Shimmer
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause it to shimmer in a pleasing and distracting array of colours for 10 minutes. For the duration, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Deception}, {@skill Performance}, and {@skill Persuasion} checks. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Nematocyst Fibres
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause its fibres to become aggressive for 1 minute. The cloak shimmers in shades of red and mauve, giving you {@b advantage} on Intimidation checks against creatures that can see you. As part of the action to activate the fibres, and as an action on subsequent turns, you can cause the cloak's filaments to brush against a creature within 10 feet of you. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the start of your next turn. Until this poison ends, the poisoned creature suffers a condition. After a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to the effects of the cloak for the next 24 hours. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Shark Senses
entries: While wearing this helmet, your peripheral vision extends behind you and your sense of smell is enhanced, granting you {@b advantage} on initiative rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that involve smell. In addition, you can inhale deeply as an action, gaining blindsight to a range of 30 feet until the start of your next turn. This blindsight can only detect a creature that has blood and is below its hit point maximum.

entries.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: While wearing this helmet, you can cast the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell on yourself. You might not be able to use this property again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This helmet is cursed with the essence of its former occupant, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to remove the helmet. Over the course of a week, you begin to develop shark-like features.

First, your skin develops small patches of tough, iridescent scales. Next, your fingers and toes become webbed, and small fins appear on your elbow and neck. Lastly are the teeth. They become a jagged conveyor belt of razors, a natural weapon which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with it, you deal piercing damage equal to {@damage 1d6} + your Strength modifier. Once you grow these shark teeth, the curse is complete and you are constantly under the effects of the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell.

A {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic ends the curse and the effects of the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell, but doesn't undo any changes to your appearance that have already occurred, including the natural weapon.

entries.name: Hammerhead Knight's Legacy
entries: While attuned to the helmet of the hammerhead knight and {@item sharktooth|LALoTT}, you gain {@b advantage} on Dexterity saving throws against effects you can see or smell, and you can't be surprised. In addition, you can use your action to grow gills and morph your legs into a tail, or to reverse this transformation. While morphed, you gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, your walking speed can't exceed 5 feet, and you can breathe both air and water.

itemGroup.name: Flumfpot
entries: A creature can use its action to pour a potion into an empty flumfpot within its reach.

entries.name: Mastery and Movement
entries: After a creature touches the flumfpot with its lips, it becomes the flumfpot's master. The flumfpot follows its master around, hovering just over their shoulder. It has a flying speed of 30 feet and a swimming speed of 60 feet. If it is within 5 feet of its master when its master teleports, it teleports with them. Otherwise, it remains in place until touched by another creature's lips. A flumfpot that is prevented from moving will fly towards its master via the most direct route when released.

entries.name: Send Aid
entries: As a bonus action, you can command the flumfpot to move up to its speed towards a willing or {@condition unconscious} creature you can see and feed its contents to it.

itemGroup.name: Tidecutter
entries: This item appears to be a sword hilt stylized with tentacles. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of rushing water to spring forth, or make it disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the Finesse property. If you are proficient with shortswords or longswords, you are proficient with the tidecutter.

entries.name: Water Lash
entries: As an action, you can cause the sword's blade to elongate into a lash and make a melee weapon attack using it against a target within a certain distance of you. On a hit, if the target is a smaller creature or object, you can pull it closer to you. If the target is a fixed object or a larger creature, you can pull yourself towards the target.

entries.name: Waterborne
entries: When submerged in water, this longsword gains the Reach property.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

itemGroup.name: Coral Crusher
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Shred
entries: Once on each of your turns when you hit a target with a melee attack using this weapon, you can cause coral shrapnel to burst out. The target and each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take piercing damage equal to one of the weapon's damage dice. You have {@b advantage} on this saving throw.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

itemGroup.name: Crosstacean
entries: This weapon deals extra necrotic damage to any target it hits.

entries.name: The Seize
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to enhance one piece of loaded ammunition with debilitating, arthritic energy that causes the target's joints to seize solid. If the next attack you make with the weapon hits, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition restrained} until the end of your next turn. If the target fails by 5 or more, it is {@condition paralyzed} instead of restrained. After this property has been used, roll a {@dice d6}. Depending on the roll, the lobster's colour may fade to grey, and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Sharktooth
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Maim
entries: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using this weapon, you can speak its command word, causing its teeth-like protrusions to rip through the target. Roll a {@dice d4} and consult the following table to determine the effect:

rows.name: Hamstring
entries: The creature's speed is reduced to 0 feet until the end of its next turn.

rows.name: Rend
entries: The creature has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

rows.name: Sever
entries: If the creature has blood, it begins bleeding for {@b 1 minute}. Each time it takes bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage while bleeding, it takes an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage. The target or a creature within reach can use an action to make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check, stopping the bleeding on a success.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

entries.name: Hammerhead Knight's Legacy
entries: While attuned to the {@item helmet of the hammerhead knight|LALoTT} and sharktooth, you gain {@b advantage} on Dexterity saving throws against effects you can see or smell, and you can't be surprised. In addition, you can use your action to grow gills and morph your legs into a tail, or to reverse this transformation. While morphed, you gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, your walking speed can't exceed 5 feet, and you can breathe both air and water.

itemGroup.name: Seashell Weapon
entries: You may gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon while underwater.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

itemGroup.name: Flutterby Rod
entries: While holding the rod, you can cast the {@spell light} cantrip, targeting only the rod.

entries.name: Flutterbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the rod, you can expend charges to shoot shimmering green, butterfly-shaped flutterbolts at targets within 60 feet of you, creating a separate flutterbolt for each charge expended. You can direct the flutterbolts at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate ranged spell attack for each flutterbolt. On a hit, the target takes force damage and is outlined in sparkling green butterflies. The next attack roll made against this target before the end of your next turn has {@b advantage}, and the target can't benefit from being {@condition invisible} until the end of your next turn.

itemGroup.name: Lilypad Sunhat
entries: The water in the top of this hat never sloshes and can't be poured out. It can, however, be scooped out in a drinking vessel, and contains up to four pints. Once water is removed, the hat fills back up over the course of 1 minute.

While you wear the hat outdoors during the day, it can feed you through photosynthesis. The time you take to gain a day's worth of nourishment depends on the brightness: 1 hour in bright, direct sunlight or 8 hours in overcast conditions.

entries.name: Thrive in the Sun
entries: If you wear this hat in sunlight during a short rest in which you spend at least one Hit Die to regain hit points, you regain additional hit points at the end of the rest. Alternatively, you can choose to end the {@condition poisoned} condition on yourself or cure a disease that's currently affecting you.

itemGroup.name: Ring of Birdseye Maple
entries: This ring has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Watchful Woods
entries: While wearing the ring, whenever you touch a plant or wooden surface, you can expend 1 charge to cause an eye to grow anywhere on that plant or surface within 60 feet of you. The eye lasts for 1 hour, until you cease touching the plant or surface, or until you create a new eye. You can see out of the eye for as long as you remain touching the plant or surface. While you look out of the eye, you are {@condition blinded} with regards to your own senses.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, the eye doesn't disappear if you cease touching the plant or surface, and you can use the Eyesoar property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Eyesoar
entries: So long as the eye is within 300 feet of you, you can use an action to look through the eye. While looking through the eye, you can use your action to move the eye up to 30 feet along the plant or surface.

entries.name: Fungal Conjuration
entries: If you can read Druidic, Elvish, Sylvan, or Undercommon, you can use an action while holding this scroll to speak the script aloud and summon one of the following mushrooms of your choice, which appears in your hand. As part of this action, you can use the mushroom as described below. The summoned mushroom withers and dies after 1 hour. Once this property of the scroll has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: {@b Boomshroom.} As an action, a creature can throw this volatile mushroom at a point it can see within 60 feet of it, where it explodes in a 10-foot-radius sphere of flames. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.
{@b Bloomshroom.} As an action, a creature can eat this mushroom, regaining hit points and gaining {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws for 1 minute.

{@b Gloomshroom.} As an action, a creature can eat this mushroom. For 1 hour, it gains {@sense {@sense darkvision}}. If it already has {@sense {@sense darkvision}}, the range of this vision is increased.

itemGroup.name: Waspberry Jam
entries: This terracotta jar, 4 inches in diameter, contains {@dice 1d4 + 1} doses of a sweet, sticky, pink jam. The jar and its contents weigh ½ pound.

entries.name: Insect Trap
entries: As an action, you can spread a single dose of jam to cover a 5-foot-square surface. Tiny, venomous waspberries immediately teleport from the Plane of Fey, forming a swarm of insects that stays in the same spot as the jam and takes its turn on initiative count 0 of each round. The swarm attacks a creature it shares a space with, and otherwise takes the Dodge action. A creature that starts its turn in the swarm's area or enters the area for the first time on its turn must make a {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw, taking piercing damage and poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If the swarm has half its hit points or fewer, creatures take reduced or no damage.

It takes the swarm 1 hour to completely consume the jam, after which it returns to the Plane of Fey. The swarm doesn't like smoke; if the area where the jam is applied is ever heavily obscured by smoke, the swarm teleports back to the Plane of Fey. If the smoke disappears, the swarm immediately returns to continue eating the jam.

itemGroup.name: Xyxlwood Arrow
entries: The incredible durability of xyxlwood means that a single arrow can see many battlefields and be wielded by multiple generations of a sylvan family.
Xyxlwood arrows take nature-based enchantments exceedingly well and have been crafted with a plethora of effects. After an effect triggers, the arrow is destroyed.

entries.name: Reroot
entries: If you find a rootbound multiweapon, roll a {@dice d3} and consult the table below to determine which type of weapon it is. As a bonus action, you can speak the weapon's command word, causing it to shift into another weapon of the same type.

rows: Simple melee weapon without the Two-handed property
Martial melee weapon without the Two-handed property
Martial melee weapon with the Two-handed property

itemGroup.name: Thornbow
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sykl Arrow
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to cause a thorny arrow to grow from the bow, choosing whether it is a Bramble or Splinter arrow. The next attack you make with the bow has additional effects on a hit, as described below:

items.name: Bramble
entries: The arrow transforms into grasping vines. The target of the attack must succeed on a Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained} by the vines until the end of its next turn. A Large or larger creature has {@b advantage} on this saving throw.

items.name: Splinter
entries: The arrow erupts in a storm of splinters and thorns. The target of the attack and each creature within 5 feet of it must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Overgrown
entries: As an action, you can expend 3 charges to cause three thick-limbed, thorny arrows to grow from the bow and fire them at a point within {@b 120 feet} of you. Where the arrows land, a mass of syklthorn brambles grow, creating an effect identical to the {@spell wall of thorns} spell, except it deals damage.

itemGroup.name: Xyxlbark Armour
entries: The armour weighs half as much as normal and, if it normally imposes {@b disadvantage} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks or has a Strength requirement, the xyxlbark version of the armour doesn't.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to xyxlbark armour, it nourishes you, causing you to gain temporary hit points at the start of each of your turns while wearing it.

itemGroup.name: Xyxlwood Sporeshiv
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges after it spends 8 continuous hours buried in soil.

entries.name: Witherblight Spore
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to make a special attack with this weapon. On a hit, the target takes the attack's normal damage and is injected with a rapidly growing witherblight spore. As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can will the spore to grow, consuming the target from the inside out; the host takes necrotic damage and deals only half damage with weapon attacks that use Strength until the end of its next turn.

A creature that hosts a spore can make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ridding itself of the spore on a success. Creatures that don't have flesh or that are immune to necrotic damage or disease are immune to this effect. If you inject a spore into a second creature, the first spore dies and the target is no longer affected.

itemGroup.name: Sins
entries: Sins are a form of "item modification", a modular way of adding to the properties an item possesses. In return for completing a sinful task appointed by a fiend, a creature can attune to—or enhance their attunement to—a magic item, which gains a powerful new property.

entries.name: OA or EA: Envy
entries: While you hold the item, when a creature within a certain distance of you that you can see makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can use your reaction to capture the creature's reflection in the item's surface and store a copy of the result of that creature's roll before any modifiers are applied to it. You can decide to use this reaction after the die is rolled, but before the GM determines if the result succeeds or fails. Once within the next minute, after a creature you can see within a certain distance of you makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can choose to replace its roll of the d20 with this stored die roll (no action required). You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn. After you replace a die in this way, until the end of your next turn, if the creature you mirrored takes damage, you take half as much damage as psychic damage. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: OA or EA: Gluttony
entries: While the item is on your person, whenever you roll Hit Dice to recover hit points at the end of a short rest, you regain additional hit points for each die rolled. You require twice as much food to sustain yourself each day.

entries.name: OA or EA: Greed
entries: While it is on your person, you can cast the {@spell locate object} spell. If there are no such treasures within range of the spell, you become demotivated, gaining {@b disadvantage} on all Charisma and Wisdom checks for the next hour.

entries.name: OA or EA: Lust
entries: While the item is on your person, you can cast the {@spell charm person} spell at will. If your target succeeds on the saving throw, you feel the sting of rejection; you are afflicted by the {@spell bane} spell for the next hour and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: OA or EA: Pride
entries: While the item is on your person, you can use a bonus action to cast the {@spell bless} spell on yourself (no concentration required). While you are blessed in this way, you become hard-headed; you can't benefit from the Help action and you are always considered an unwilling target for other creatures' spells. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: OA or EA: Sloth
entries: While the item is on your person, you can cast the {@spell slow} spell. For the spell's duration, your speed is halved and you can't take reactions. After this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: OA or EA: Wrath
entries: While the item is on your person, you can use a bonus action to enter a fiendish rage for 1 minute. When you activate this property and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for the duration, you gain additional movement equal to half your speed until the start of your next turn. For the duration, if you move at least {@b 15 feet} straight towards a creature and then immediately hit it with a melee attack, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. While this property is active, you lose all sense of self-preservation and are filled with bloodlust; any movement you make must be towards a hostile creature, and the only actions you can take on your turn are the Attack and Dash actions, or an action to break free of restraints or a grapple. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Abyssal Bane
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls you make with this magic weapon.

The power of such a weapon is linked to the number of eyes used to craft it, with each open eye representing one unexpended charge. This weapon has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bonerip
entries: As a bonus action when you hit a target with this weapon, you can call the name of the demon to whom the eyes belonged and expend 1 charge. The weapon mobilises, boney teeth and necrotic energy ripping through the target's defences. The target gains a permanent and cumulative -1 penalty to its AC. Its AC can't be reduced to less than 10 + its Dexterity modifier. The target can repair its armour with enough rest (for natural armour) or at an appropriate armoursmith's (for worn armour) at the GM's discretion.

entries.name: Syphon
entries: If you hit a creature that has blood with an attack using this weapon on your turn and that attack leaves the creature below half of its maximum hit points, you can use a bonus action to cause the weapon to latch onto the creature and begin syphoning its blood. The creature is {@condition grappled}. While grappled, the creature takes necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns and you regain hit points. You can't use the blood drinker to attack another target, and you must hold on to the blood drinker with at least one hand to maintain the grapple; if you let go of the blood drinker with both hands, the grapple ends. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: SoarBones
entries: You can use this weapon to cast the {@spell phantom steed} spell. The weapon grows skeletal wings and feet, turning into a vicious looking crow named SoarBones. In addition to the steed's normal statistics, it has a flying speed equal to its walking speed. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Bloodshot
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Feed the Beast
entries: This weapon has {@b 10 charges}. Over the course of a short or long rest, any creature can cause bloodshot to regain expended charges by dripping their own blood down its barrel and spending Hit Dice. Roll each Hit Die spent in this way, and the weapon regains a number of charges equal to the total. After absorbing 3 Hit Dice worth of blood, the weapon can't absorb more blood in this way until the next dawn.

Alternatively, over the course of a minute, you can extract blood from the corpse of a creature that has been dead for no longer than {@b 10 minutes}. When you do so, the weapon gains a number of charges equal to the roll of one of the dead creature's Hit Dice. After absorbing the blood from a corpse in this way, the weapon can't do so again until {@b 1 hour} has passed.

entries.name: Shot Selection
entries: This weapon doesn't have the Reload or Ammunition properties. Instead, in order to make a ranged weapon attack with it, you must expend charges in one of the following ways:

items.name: Bloodshard (1 charge)
entries: On a hit, the weapon deals its normal damage.

items.name: Septic Shock (3 charges)
entries: On a hit, the weapon deals its normal damage, and the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn as its blood becomes corrupted. A creature immune to disease automatically succeeds on this saving throw.

itemGroup.name: Demonbane Armour
entries: This armour has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Consume Energy
entries: When you take cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage, you can use your reaction to speak the armour's first command word and expend 1 charge. The mouth opens, consuming some of the incoming energy. You gain resistance to the triggering damage and one of the armour's eyes lights up with the stored energy. The armour has a number of eyes, and an eye can't store more energy if it already is storing some.

entries.name: Unleash Energy
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can utter the armour's second command word, unleashing the energy stored in all of the armour's eyes. For each eye that was storing energy, the creature takes damage of the stored type, after which the stored energy disappears.

itemGroup.name: Demonic Siege Bow
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a creature with a Strength score of 13 or higher
entries: The damage die of this weapon is increased. The bow has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Siege Brace
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge and speak the bow's command word, causing a skeletal, demonic hand to burst from the ground or another surface and hold the bow steady until you use another bonus action to dismiss it. The bow can't be moved while grasped by the skeletal hand. While in this 'siege mode', each time you make a ranged weapon attack using this bow, you can make one additional attack against the same target (no action required). When you do so, both attacks are made with {@b disadvantage}.

itemGroup.name: Hellscape Chatterbane
entries: While holding this magic weapon, you can use an action to cast the {@spell produce flame} cantrip.

entries.name: Posthumous Mirth
entries: As an action, you can shake the weapon to cast the {@spell Tasha's hideous laughter|hideous laughter} spell, causing the skull's jaw to rattle up and down, and a malevolent cackling sound to fill the air. This property can be used a number of times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Blood Bond
entries: When you damage a creature that has blood with this magic weapon, or it touches blood spilled from a creature less than 24 hours ago, the light pink crystals that make up its blade turn a deep, sanguine red as the weapon becomes bonded to the creature, which becomes its quarry. For the next 24 hours you can, as an action, toss the dagger in the air and speak its command word. For 6 seconds after, it hovers in the air, pointing in the direction of its quarry. If its quarry is on a different plane or is warded against divination magic, the dagger spins wistfully in the air. Once the dagger absorbs blood, it can't absorb another creature's blood until 24 hours have passed and it returns to its normal pink colour.

entries.name: Charges & Charging
entries: An emberink stationery can hold a maximum amount of charges and regains charges when the ink pot is used to capture a fiend's blood. As fiends' bodies disappear from the Material Plane on death, fiendish blood must be extracted while the fiend is still alive. You can use an action to extract blood from a living fiend within your reach. If the fiend is unwilling, the fiend must be {@condition restrained} or {@condition incapacitated} and be below half of its maximum hit points. When blood is extracted in this way, the stationery regains a number of charges equal to a roll of one of the fiend's Hit Dice, and once it regains charges, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Contractual Obligation
entries: As an action, you can use the stationery to write on the skin of a willing humanoid. The ink burns into the flesh of your target, raking a cauterised scar, while an identical burn appears in a similar place on your own body. Both you and the creature take 1 fire damage. Choose one of the following glyphs and expend the appropriate number of charges; for 1 hour, you are both subjected to the associated magical effects.

items.name: Ward (1 charge)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, you both have a +1 bonus to AC. While you are more than 30 feet apart, you both suffer a -1 penalty to AC.

items.name: Warp (2 charges)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, each affected creature can choose whether it is enlarged or reduced, as per the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell. An affected creature can use its action to return to normal size, or to enlarge or reduce itself if it's already normal size. While you are more than 30 feet apart, both creatures return to their normal size.

items.name: Wrath (1 charge)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, you both gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. While you are more than 30 feet apart, you both suffer a -1 penalty to attack and damage rolls.
Once the stationery has been used to sign a glyph, the ink turns dark and it can't be used again until the current contract is complete (i.e., the hour has elapsed).

itemGroup.name: Heart of Stone
entries: The heart can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Soultrap
entries: When a fiend dies within 10 feet of the heart and the heart doesn't already have a soul trapped within it, the fiend's soul becomes trapped within the heart. The heart gains a number of charges equal to the maximum number of Hit Dice the fiend possessed. If all these charges are expended, the soul is shredded and destroyed.
While you hold the stone, you can communicate telepathically with the imprisoned soul, if it is willing. You can release an imprisoned soul back to the Lower Planes as an action.

entries.name: Soulshred
entries: While you hold the heart, you can expend charges from it to cast certain spells.

After you cast a spell in this way, you must make a Charisma check contested by the imprisoned fiend's. On a failure, the fiend's soul escapes and is released back to the Lower Planes, and the heart loses all its remaining charges. If a soul escapes or is released while you're on one of the Lower Planes, the fiend can immediately materialise with all of its hit points in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of the heart.

entries.name: Real Reflections
entries: As an action while holding the mirror, you can speak its command word to cause its reflective surface to disappear for 1 minute. During that time, a creature that looks through the mirror while holding it has truesight. You might not be able to use this property again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hit
entries: When you hit with this arrow, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have shattered bone fragments lodged in obtrusive places within its body. While a creature has these fragments in it, it takes piercing damage when it moves for the first time on a turn and whenever it takes the Attack action. As an action, an affected creature can make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Miss
entries: When you miss with this arrow, the arrow changes direction to hit the nearest surface to the target, where it explodes into sharp fragments. Each creature within 5 feet of the arrow must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take piercing damage.

entries.name: Peekaboo
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to telepathically link yourself to one eyeball. While linked, you see through the eyeball, which has {@sense darkvision} out to 120 feet, and you are blind with regards to your own vision. As a bonus action while linked to the eyeball, you can move the eyeball up to 30 feet in any direction. There is no limit to how far away from you the eye can move, but it can't enter another plane of existence. The eyeball has 2 hit points and an AC of 15. After 10 minutes, if the eyeball is reduced to 0 hit points, or if you sever the connection (no action required), the eyeball disappears, temporarily returning to its fiendish donor.

entries.name: Searing Vengeance
entries: As a reaction when a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, you can pull a trigger on the shield's handle, causing it to breathe a plume of flame at the creature. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking fire damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. After you use this property, roll a die. On a 1-2, the shield runs out of fuel and this property can't be used again until you feed the shield 1 gp of coal or other fuel and spend 1 hour tending to the flame (which can be done during a short or long rest), as the shield burns and chews its way through your offering.

itemGroup.name: Soulkeeper Pendant
entries: When a humanoid dies within 5 feet of the pendant and the pendant doesn't already have a soul trapped in it, its soul becomes trapped in a flame-filled, hellish demiplane, causing the pendant's eyes to glow fiery red. Casting the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic on the pendant releases a soul from its captivity. A soul can be stored indefinitely, and time spent in captivity within the pendant doesn't count against the time limit of resurrection spells such as {@spell raise dead}. A creature who is resurrected after its soul has been stored within the pendant must make a Wisdom saving throw or develop one long-term madness effect. The DC for the saving throw equals 10 + the number of days the soul was trapped in the pendant.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Sellsoul
entries: Upon attuning to this pendant, a devil, Eebezelbub, appears to you with an offer. She will trick souls you capture into following her to the Hells in exchange for granting you minor favours. If the pendant has a soul trapped in it, you can use an action to call Eebezelbub's name and permit her entry to the pendant. Roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1, the soul refuses Eeb's offer and drifts away, causing the miniature animal's eyes to slowly dim. On a 2-6, the soul accepts her offer and the skull flashes brightly before petering out. Eebezelbub reappears {@dice 1d20} minutes later, offering to perform one of the following tasks of your choice:

items: She will deliver and return one message, as per the {@spell sending} spell, and can send the message to other planes of existence without the possibility of failure, but she can't enter good-aligned planes to deliver the message.
She will answer three questions, as per the {@spell commune} spell.
She will divulge information on a topic, as per the {@spell legend lore} spell.

entries: Eebezelbub needs time to process the souls that accept her offers, and after getting souls from the pendant, she won't take any more until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Wings of the Hellspawn
entries: Until your attunement to the wings ends, you can't be parted from them, willingly or otherwise.

entries.name: Fiendish Flight
entries: When you fall and aren't {@condition incapacitated}, you can subtract up to 100 feet from the fall when calculating falling damage, and you can move up to 2 feet horizontally for every 1 foot you descend.

entries.name: Devil's Cocoon
entries: As an action, you can cause these wings to wrap around you, forming a fleshy carapace. You gain temporary hit points that last for 1 hour. If you use the Fiendish Flight property, you lose any temporary hit points you have from this property. Once this property of the wings has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Astrochronometer
entries: While wearing this watch, you don't need to sleep (although you can only engage in light activity in order to benefit from a long rest), and you have advantage on checks and saving throws made to avoid {@condition exhaustion}. It can also be used to tell the current time in whatever plane you're in.

entries.name: Float
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use a bonus action to cause the tentacles to lift you a foot off of the ground, or to cause you to land again. While floating in this way, you can traverse low-lying obstacles (such as caltrops) and difficult terrain without impediment (at the GM's discretion). In addition, while floating, always under the effects of the {@spell feather fall} spell. You can't float while the Distort property is active.

entries.name: Distort
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use an action to cause the tentacles to twist and shimmer, distorting your outline as you cast the {@spell blur} spell. If you are floating as per the Float property when you activate this property, you land back on the ground. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Evening Moon
entries: This weapon returns to your hand immediately after it is used to make a ranged attack. When you hit a creature with the Shapechanger trait with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or instantly revert to its original form and be unable to assume a different form for 1 minute.

entries.name: Dusk
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use an action to create a sphere centred on you which is filled with dim light. This sphere follows you when you move. While in this area, you can try to hide when lightly obscured, and you can take the Hide action as a bonus action. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

entries.name: Eclipse, the Moon and Stars
entries: When the magics of {@item morning star|LALoTT} and {@item evening moon|LALoTT} become intertwined through attunement to a single wielder, their innate dunamantic magics coalesce to create a miniature solar system. While you are attuned to both morning star and evening moon, you gain the following benefits:

items: The attack and damage bonus of each weapon increases by 1, to a maximum of +3.
You can use the Orbit and Eclipse properties, described below.

entries.name: Orbit
entries: While holding both evening moon and morning star, you can use a bonus action to cause evening moon to begin orbiting you. As part of this bonus action, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for as long as it remains in orbit, you can make one attack with evening moon against any creature within 15 feet of you. Evening moon orbits you at a distance of two feet, and while in orbit, you can't use it to attack except with this property. You can catch evening moon (no action required) at any point. A creature within 5 feet of you can use its action to make a {@dc 21} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, catching evening moon on a success or taking {@damage 1d6} slashing damage on a failure.

entries.name: Eclipse
entries: As a bonus action while evening moon is orbiting you, you can cause it to hover in front of you instead, casting you in a dark obscuring light and granting you the benefits of the {@spell blur} spell for 1 minute, or until you catch it or make an attack with it. Once this property of the set has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dawn
entries: As an action, you can cause morning star to shine a gloriously bright light at all creatures within a cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Ultraviolet
entries: After you use the Dawn property, but before it can be used again at the next dawn, you can use an action to cause morning star to shine a focussed beam of ultraviolet light in a line. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Eclipse, the Moon and Stars
entries: When the magics of {@item morning star|LALoTT} and {@item evening moon|LALoTT} become intertwined through attunement to a single wielder, their innate dunamantic magics coalesce to create a miniature solar system. While you are attuned to both morning star and evening moon, you gain the following benefits:

items: The attack and damage bonus of each weapon increases by 1, to a maximum of +3.
You can use the Orbit and Eclipse properties, described below.

entries.name: Orbit
entries: While holding both evening moon and morning star, you can use a bonus action to cause evening moon to begin orbiting you. As part of this bonus action, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for as long as it remains in orbit, you can make one attack with evening moon against any creature within 15 feet of you. Evening moon orbits you at a distance of two feet, and while in orbit, you can't use it to attack except with this property. You can catch evening moon (no action required) at any point. A creature within 5 feet of you can use its action to make a {@dc 21} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, catching evening moon on a success or taking {@damage 1d6} slashing damage on a failure.

entries.name: Eclipse
entries: As a bonus action while evening moon is orbiting you, you can cause it to hover in front of you instead, casting you in a dark obscuring light and granting you the benefits of the {@spell blur} spell for 1 minute, or until you catch it or make an attack with it. Once this property of the set has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Gravity Cube
entries: As an action, you can program and throw this cube at a surface you can see within 50 feet of you, where it sticks to the surface, expending charges to warp a 30-foot-radius sphere centred on that point in one of the ways described below. If an effect requires you to expend charges each turn, you expend them at the start of your turn. If you don't, the effect ends.

entries.name: Nullify (1 Charge/Turn)
entries: Gravity in the area is nullified, causing all objects and creatures to drift freely. When the cube activates, each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature that enters the area for the first time on its turn while the effect lasts must also make this saving throw. On a failure, a creature rises vertically, up to 5 feet, and remains suspended in mid-air for the duration of the effect. On a success, a creature clings to the nearest surface. Suspended creatures can only move within the area by pushing off surfaces or other creatures.

entries.name: Intensify (1 Charge/Turn)
entries: Gravity in the area is intensified. For the duration, a creature that moves in the area must spend 3 feet of movement for each foot that it moves, and each creature's jump distance is divided by 3. A flying creature that starts its turn in the area, enters the area for the first time on its turn, or tries to begin flying in the area must make a Strength check. On a failure, it is safely pulled to the ground and has a flying speed of 0 feet until the start of its next turn.

entries.name: Attract (2 Charges)
entries: Each creature and unsecured object within the area must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled 20 feet towards the cube. Creatures and objects that collide as a result of this each take {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Repel (2 Charges)
entries: Each creature and unsecured object within the area must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 20 feet directly away from the cube. Creatures and objects that collide as a result of this each take {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Planar Infusion
entries: When you imbibe this potion as an action, your mind, body, and soul become bound with that of an entity from another plane. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table to determine the entity. You gain the statistics of that creature, as per the {@spell polymorph} spell. These effects last for a duration, or until you fail a saving throw against the {@spell banishment} spell or a similar effect (which banishes the planar entity, not you), or your assumed form is reduced to 0 hit points. {@spell Dispel magic} does not end the potion's effects.

entries.name: Consequences
entries: After the duration elapses, your mind is strained and can even break. Make a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, you take {@damage 4d6} psychic damage and are insane until you finish a long rest. While insane, you can't take actions, can't understand what other creatures say, can't read, and speak only in gibberish. A {@spell greater restoration} spell cast on you ends this effect.

itemGroup.name: Quantum Earring of the Paranoid

entries.name: Hot Ear
entries: Whenever someone talks about you while you wear this earring—regardless of the plane on which you both currently reside—your ear becomes uncomfortably warm. As a bonus action, you can twist the earring, activating it in an attempt to listen in to the conversation. The creature that activated your hot ear must succeed on a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, an invisible sensor is created within 10 feet of the target that lasts for 10 minutes. For the duration, you can hear through the sensor as if you were there. The sensor moves with the creature that triggered your hot ear, remaining within 10 feet of it for the duration.

items: Quantum Earring of the Paranoid (Very Rare)|LALoTT

entries.name: Gravity Shard
entries: While holding the weapon, you can use a bonus action to produce a spearhead of force energy, which sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. While this spearhead lasts, the weapon can be used as a quarterstaff or a spear, and on a hit, it deals force damage instead of the weapons' usual bludgeoning or piercing damage. In addition, you can use either your Intelligence or Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls you make with this weapon. The energy lasts until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or sheathe the weapon.

entries.name: Gravity Shunt
entries: As an action, you can use the weapon to telekinetically move creatures or objects within range of you.

itemGroup.name: Starfallen Force Shield
entries: This shield has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Repulsion
entries: When a creature misses you with a melee attack while you hold this shield, you can use your reaction to pull a trigger, expending 1 charge and sending a pulse of energy through the attacking creature. The creature must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature is knocked {@condition prone}, and is disarmed of the weapon with which it made the attack, or of its spellcasting focus or component if it was a melee spell attack. The item flies 15 feet away in a random direction (roll a {@dice d8} to determine the direction where a 1 = North, 2 = Northeast, etc.)

entries.name: Starfield Bulwark
entries: As an action, you can twist the handle of the shield, expending 3 charges and causing it to deploy in a 5-foot-high barrier of force energy up to 25 feet long. Nothing can physically pass through the wall; a creature that attempts to run through or climb over the barrier takes {@damage 2d6} force damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. Creatures directly behind the barrier have three-quarters cover against attacks that originate from the other side of the barrier. Any attack that misses a creature directly behind the barrier automatically hits the barrier, which has hit points. The barrier remains in place until 1 minute elapses, a creature uses its bonus action to pick up the barrier on the side from which it was deployed, it is hit by the {@spell disintegrate} spell, or it loses all its hit points.

entries.name: Psionic Deflection
entries: While spinning the twinblade as part of its Special: Whirl property, whenever a creature misses you with a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to divert the projectile back to its origin. Make an attack roll against the creature using your Intelligence modifier. On a hit, the creature takes the damage from its own attack.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Psionic Shot
entries: If you attune to this item, when you make an attack with this weapon as part of the Attack action, you can mime pulling back on it as if it were a bow, causing an arrow of shimmering psionic energy to materialise, and make a ranged weapon attack, using the twinblade as a shortbow with which you are proficient. You can use either your Dexterity or Intelligence modifier for attack and damage rolls made with the twinblade in this way, and it deals psychic damage instead of piercing damage.

entries.name: Homebound
entries: As an action, you can light the candle, expending 1 charge and causing it to cast a dim, purple light in a 10-foot-radius sphere. This light slowly brightens over the course of a minute. At the end of the minute, an eruption of glowing purple sigils forms a teleportation circle in the lit area. The circle appears on initiative 20 of the 10th round after the candle was lit and lasts until initiative 20 of the following round. Each creature in the area when the circle forms, or that moves into the area while the circle is active, is teleported to the destination teleportation circle where the candle was crafted. When the teleportation circle ends, the candle also teleports to the destination circle.

A creature can use its action to snuff out a candle within its reach that isn't being held by another creature. If the candle is held by another creature, the creature trying to snuff out the candle must succeed on a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check contested by the holding creature's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}). If the candle is snuffed out before the minute elapses, the teleportation circle never forms, and any expended charges are wasted.

entries.name: Stardust Ammo
entries: Instead of normal ammunition, flarefuses consume stardust to fire. A sufficient amount of stardust for a single shot is generally worth 1 gp. You load stardust into the firearm as you would normal ammunition.

entries.name: Firing Modes
entries: Refractive prisms allow a flarefuse to fire in one of two modes; focused or flared. As a bonus action, you can switch between these firing modes. Regardless of the firing mode, each shot uses up the same amount of stardust.

items.name: Focused
entries: The weapon deals extra radiant damage on a hit.

items.name: Flared
entries: Instead of making an attack roll, you shoot a wave of radiant shrapnel in a {@b 15-foot cone}. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take radiant damage.

itemGroup.name: Psionblade
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, while wielding this weapon, you can use your mind to manipulate and empower its blade. You can use your Intelligence modifier for the attack and damage rolls of this weapon.

entries.name: Psionslice
entries: While holding the weapon, you can use a bonus action to cause it to switch from an ethereal blade of nebulous energy that deals psychic damage on a hit to a hardened, crystalline structure that deals the sword's normal damage type on a hit, or vice-versa.

entries.name: Thoughtstrike
entries: When you make an attack with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to launch it out of your hand, controlling it with your mind to make a melee weapon attack against a target up to {@b 30 feet} away. When making an attack in this way, you must use your Intelligence modifier for the attack and damage rolls. After this attack, the sword flies back to your hand.

entries.name: Dopplerstrike
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you make an attack with a starforged weapon, you can choose to simultaneously use your movement to move 5 feet directly towards or away from your target as part of the attack, generating a blue-shift strike or red-shift strike, respectively.

items.name: Blue Shift
entries: To perform a blue-shift strike, your target must be within your weapon's reach at the end of this 5-foot movement but does not need to be within your weapon's reach when you start this movement. You gain a bonus to your attack roll, which deals extra radiant damage on a hit.

items.name: Red Shift
entries: To perform a red-shift strike, your target must be within your weapon's reach at the start of this 5-foot movement but does not need to be within your weapon's reach at the movement's conclusion. On a hit, the target takes extra cold damage and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn. A creature that loses its reaction in this way does so before your movement triggers an opportunity attack.

itemGroup.name: Arcanowax
entries: When found, an arcanowax candle has charges.

entries.name: Arcanowax Circle
entries: As an action, you can light the candle, expending 1 charge and causing it to shed bright light in a 10-foot-radius, 20-foot-tall cylinder for 1 minute. The area of the light replicates the effects of the {@spell magic circle} spell, except it might only affect a specific type of creature. If the candle is moved in such a way that would result in one of those creatures entering the area of the light, the candle is snuffed out.

A creature can use its action to snuff out a candle within its reach that isn't being held by another creature. If the candle is held by another creature, the creature trying to snuff out the candle must succeed on its choice of a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check contested by the holding creature's choice of Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}).
The candle's effects end immediately when the candle is snuffed out.

entries.name: False Authority
entries: This badge is enchanted to appear as a document that gives its holder the necessary authority they require to outrank the observer. You can use a bonus action to show this badge to a creature to gain {@b advantage} on {@skill Deception} checks made to convince it that you're important. A creature that spends at least 1 minute taking a close look at the badge can see through the illusion with a successful Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check. While flashing the badge might convince indifferent creatures, creatures that are suspicious of you might demand more time to closely review your documentation for legitimacy.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to this item, you can use additional properties while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Take Cover
entries: As a bonus action, you can tap the badge and shout the command words "take cover", causing the badge to become a floating shield that follows you for 1 minute, or until you use a bonus action to return it to its normal form. If you aren't wearing a shield, you gain a +5 bonus to AC for the duration. This effect ends early if you make an attack or cast a spell that affects an enemy creature. After you use this property, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Shoot
entries: As an action, you can point the Ka-Ma-Ra in a direction of your choice and pull the trigger, flashing bright light in a 20-foot cone. At the start of your next turn, the imp hands you a small, 3" by 2" picture of whatever the Ka-Ma-Ra was pointing at. If you move or use a reaction between taking the action and the start of your next turn, the picture is blurry; you must remain perfectly still while the imp is at work. If the Ka-Ma-Ra is left unused for too long, the imp within may produce risqué images of other imps in an attempt to ward off boredom.

entries.name: Investigation
entries: While looking through this magnifying glass, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Investigation} checks made to find clues and hidden messages within writing.

entries.name: Tracking
entries: While looking through this magnifying glass, you can see the tracks of all creatures that travelled through the area in the last 24 hours as glowing imprints, unless such a creature's passage was disguised by magic, and have {@b advantage} on ability checks made to identify the creatures that left those tracks.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: Lenz is a sentient chaotic good magic item with an Intelligence of 10, Wisdom of 13, and Charisma of 17. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to 60 feet, and it can speak and understand Common.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Lenz is charismatic and slightly distracting, taking great joy in pointing out entirely obvious and irrelevant facts. While holding Lenz, you can make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check to ask it for extra help. On a success, its tips are helpful and grant you {@b advantage} on ability checks made to gather information on creatures, objects, and locations through observation for 10 minutes. On a failure, it gets distracted and starts telling a tale, such as the time it mistook a duck's footprint for that of a venomous dire platypus, giving you {@b disadvantage} on the same ability checks for 10 minutes. Once Lenz has been successfully persuaded to help, any attempts to ask for help from it again before the next dawn automatically fail.

itemGroup.name: Sleuth's Shadowcoat
entries: This item regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shadow Stealth
entries: While wearing this coat, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge and turn {@condition invisible} until the start of your next turn. If you are still {@condition invisible} at the start of your next turn, you can choose to immediately expend 1 charge (no action required) to prolong the effect for another turn.

entries.name: Hide in Plain Sight
entries: While wearing the coat, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell chameleon skin|LALoTT} spell on yourself.

entries.name: Disappearing Act
entries: While wearing the coat, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell fog cloud} spell centred on yourself. When you cast the spell in this way, it only lasts up to 1 minute, and you can immediately move up to half your speed.

entries.name: Stun Baton
entries: You can use this wand as a simple melee weapon with the {@itemProperty F|Finesse property}, dealing 1 piercing damage on a hit. Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack using the wand, you can cause it to discharge in one of the following ways:

items: {@b Sap.} The creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its speed is reduced to 0 feet and it can't take bonus actions or reactions until the end of its next turn. A creature immune to being {@condition stunned} automatically succeeds on this saving throw.
{@b Zap.} The creature takes extra lightning damage and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn unless it is immune to being {@condition stunned}.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Stunspell
