entries: If you are a spellcaster, you can attune to this item. You know certain spells, and you always have them prepared. While holding the wand, you can cast one of those spells without expending a spell slot, using your spell save DC. Once you use the Stunspell property, you may not be able to use it again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Phantom Blade
entries: This magic weapon deals extra psychic damage to any target it hits. This damage is multiplied against undead with the Incorporeal Movement trait.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: A phantom blade is a sentient, lawful neutral item with an Intelligence of 14, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 12. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of {@b 120 feet}. The weapon can speak, read, and understand Common.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Phantom blades see themselves as righteous defenders of inanimate objects and take umbrage at being used to break down doors or crack open chests. In addition, their own experience with death makes them particularly sensitive to inflicting it upon others. Whenever you use the phantom blade to kill a creature that isn't undead, the sword attempts to haunt you. You must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or develop a short-term madness effect. Once the sword has attempted to haunt a creature, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Piecemaker
entries: You may gain a bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Elemental Ammunition
entries: Instead of normal ammunition, this weapon uses special capsules of elemental energy. The weapon can hold one capsule at a time, which can be loaded into it as an action and, while using these capsules, you ignore the Loading or Reload properties of the weapon if it has them. Each capsule contains 6 charges, and new capsules can be requisitioned from the quartermaster or purchased on the grey market for 5 gp each. Capsules come in five flavours: acid, cold, fire, lightning, and thunder. Each attack with the piecemaker expends 1 charge from the loaded capsule, and deals damage of the capsule's type instead of its normal damage type.

entries.name: Conflict Avoidance
entries: If the target of your attack is an unarmed creature, the weapon doesn't fire and the attack is wasted. The piecemaker is sophisticated enough to detect whether a creature has natural weapons, such as a bear's claws and bite. If a creature's natural weapons are restricted—such as handcuffed claws, or a muzzled maw—the piecemaker deems the target to be unarmed. Unarmed strikes notably aren't classified as weapons. For this reason, the Shadow Enclave's agents often manage to escape confrontations with the FBI unscathed.

entries.name: Enhance Ammo
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can enhance the capsule currently loaded into it. Each of the attacks you make that expend charges from that capsule has an additional effect depending on its flavour. Removing an enhanced capsule from the weapon removes the enhancement as well. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Banner of Bloody Transfusion
entries: Crafted from a tough, stain-proof, white fabric, this velvet-trimmed banner measures 5 feet high and 3 feet wide. An embroidered depiction of a blood sac becomes a three-dimensional sac containing a blood-based potion while the banner is unfurled. It can be attached to a 10-foot pole to serve as a standard. Furling or unfurling the banner requires an action. While unfurled, the banner has the following properties:

entries.name: Mark of Vitality
entries: You can touch the banner as a bonus action, causing it to emanate vitality. For a duration or until the banner is furled, you and your allies have {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws while within range of the banner. Once this property of the banner has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Stabilising Standard
entries: While within range of the banner, you can use a bonus action to stabilise a creature you can see that has 0 hit points and is within range of the banner.

entries.name: Transfusion
entries: You can touch the banner and mentally command its transfusion hose to connect with one willing or {@condition unconscious} creature within range of the banner, choosing one of the following effects:

items.name: Heal
entries: As a bonus action, you cause the creature to regain hit points.

items.name: Restore
entries: As an action, you cast a spell on the creature.
After this property of the banner has been activated, roll a die. On a 3 or lower, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Bloodletter Opener
entries: This weapon has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Draw Blood
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can use it to draw blood from a willing creature within your reach. Alternatively, when you hit a creature that has blood with a weapon attack using the bloodletter opener, you can draw some of its blood into the weapon's reservoir. While the weapon contains blood, you can use a bonus action to flick a switch on its handle and expend 1 charge to either augment or corrupt the blood. Unused blood in the dagger vanishes at dawn.

items.name: Augment
entries: The blood becomes enhanced with healing properties. As an action, you can inject the blood into the creature from whom it was extracted. The creature regains hit points and gains additional benefits for a duration.

items.name: Corrupt
entries: The blood becomes corrupted into a poison bespoke to the creature from whom it was extracted. When you hit that creature with an attack using the weapon, you can inject the bloodletter opener's contents into the creature. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for a duration. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}.

entries.name: Illumination
entries: As a bonus action while you wear the cowl, you can cause it to shed bright light in a radius of up to 20 feet and dim light for an additional number of feet equal to the chosen radius, or extinguish the light.

entries.name: Psionics
entries: While you wear this cowl, you can cast certain spells. After you cast a spell in this way, roll a die. If you roll equal to or lower than the spell's level, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Deathfibrillator
entries: This item has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it in your hand, unarmed strikes you make that hit with that hand deal piercing damage instead of bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Clear!
entries: While holding a deathfibrillator, you can expend 1 or more charges in the following ways:

items.name: Zap (1 charge)
entries: You squeeze this item's handle when you hit a target with an unarmed strike using it, dealing extra lightning damage.

items.name: First Aid (3 charges)
entries: As an action, you touch a {@condition stunned} or {@condition paralysed} creature within your reach, sending a wave of electrostatic energy through it. The creature takes lightning damage and is no longer {@condition stunned} or {@condition paralysed}.

entries.name: Fiendrenaline
entries: As a bonus action, you can jab this apparatus into a willing creature within your reach, dealing {@damage 1d4} piercing damage to it. For a duration, the creature's speed is doubled, it gains a bonus to its Strength and Dexterity scores, and it ignores the effects of the {@condition exhaustion} condition. When these effects end, the creature gains 1 level of {@condition exhaustion} and must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be possessed by a fiendish spirit for 1 hour. During that time, the creature is {@condition incapacitated}, and the fiend controls its body but doesn't deprive it of awareness. The fiend can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, and it retains its alignment. It otherwise uses the creature's statistics, but doesn't gain access to its knowledge, class features, or proficiencies. After the possession ends, the fiend returns to its plane of origin. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn, when the device refills itself with refined fiend blood.

entries.name: Healping Hand
entries: As an action while holding the healper, you can speak the command phrase "Healp! Healp!" followed by one of the commands below. The bundle of alchemical paraphernalia springs into life for 1 minute. It is a Tiny construct with immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It regains expended charges daily at dawn.

The healper takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to activate it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can give it one of the following instructions to use as its action on its next turn, which expends the listed number of charges:

items.name: Stabilise (0 charges)
entries: The healper casts the {@spell spare the dying} cantrip.

items.name: Heal (1 charge)
entries: The healper pours a healing draught into the mouth of a willing creature within its reach, restoring hit points to the creature.

items.name: Restore (2 charges)
entries: The healper performs a minor surgery on a willing creature within its reach, ending certain conditions on that creature.

itemGroup.name: Heart Rob
entries: You gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to piercing damage.

entries.name: Heartbreaking
entries: When you attack a creature that has at least one heart with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you stab the creature directly in the heart. The creature immediately gains levels of {@condition exhaustion} and begins suffocating. If its heart is healed with the {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic, it stops suffocating. A creature is immune to this effect if it is immune to piercing damage, doesn't have or need a heart, has legendary actions, or the GM decides that the creature is too big for its heart to be punctured with this weapon. Such a creature instead takes extra piercing damage from the hit.

entries.name: Pathogen Proof
entries: While wearing this garb, you are immune to disease. In addition, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to resist curses. Each day at dawn, this garb is magically cleaned and pressed, ready for action on the wards.

entries.name: Useful Things
entries: The garb has pockets and pouches containing useful things. It has one antitoxin, one dose of essence of ether poison, one healer's kit (with 5 uses), and one {@item potion of healing}. Each day at dawn, the garb replenishes all removed items, and items that were removed from the garb but not used spoil and become useless.

entries.name: Knightly Medicine
entries: As an action while holding Ser Boncou Rage, you can speak the command phrase "Bon courage!" followed by one of the commands below. The armed and armoured glass bottle bounds into life for 1 minute. It is a Tiny construct with a Strength score of 5, immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It regains expended charges daily at dawn.

Ser Boncou Rage takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to activate it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can give it one of the following instructions to use as its action on its next turn, which expends the listed number of charges:

items.name: Snap Out of It! (1 charge)
entries: Ser Boncou Rage yells at a creature within range. If that creature can hear the yell and is {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, or {@condition stunned} by an effect that lets it repeat the saving throw to end the condition at the end of each of its turns, the creature can immediately repeat one such saving throw of its choice.

items.name: Once More into the Breach! (2 charges)
entries: Ser Boncou Rage lets out a gallant yell. Up to a number of creatures of its choice within 30 feet of it, other than itself, gain a die if they can hear the yell. Once within the next minute, a creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the {@dice d20} before deciding to use the die, but must decide before the GM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can't use this die in conjunction with a Bardic Inspiration die.

items.name: Freedom! (3 charges)
entries: Ser Boncou Rage pours its contents into the mouth of a willing creature whose space it shares. For a duration, the creature is immune to the {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened} conditions.

entries.name: Venomous Intentions
entries: As an action while holding the torture tincture, you can speak the command phrase "Poison please". This minuscule glass bottle creeps into life for the next minute. It is a Tiny construct with walking and climbing speeds of 30 feet, a Strength score of 1, a Dexterity score of 20, proficiency in the Stealth skill (+7 bonus), immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It contains one dose of poison and regains an expended dose daily at dawn.

The torture tincture takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to summon it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can command it to deposit a poison in a vessel, food, or drink as its action on its next turn.

itemGroup.name: Longshot
entries: When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using this ammunition, the elixir within is injected into the creature, which suffers its effects depending on the type of longshot ammunition used, as described below. A creature can choose to make itself an easy target against this ammunition, giving you {@b advantage} on the attack roll. If the attack misses, the elixir is ejected and wasted. Either way, the ammunition behaves like ordinary magical ammunition until the next dawn, when it is magically refilled.

entries.name: Red Restoration; Uncommon
entries: On a hit, the attack doesn't deal any damage. Instead, the target is cured of one disease or condition (target's choice). The condition can be {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralyzed}, or {@condition poisoned}.

entries.name: Teal Torpor; Rare
entries: On a hit, this ammunition deals the attack's normal damage. In addition, the creature must succeed a Constitution saving throw or suffer the effects of the {@spell hold person} spell until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Blue Benevolence; Rare
entries: On a hit, the attack doesn't deal any damage. Instead, the target regains hit points and gains the benefits of the {@spell bless} spell for {@b 1 minute}.

entries.name: Indigo Insanity; Very Rare
entries: On a hit, this ammunition deals the attack's normal damage. In addition, the creature must succeed a Constitution saving throw or suffer the effects of the {@spell confusion} spell for {@b 1 minute}. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

entries.name: Purple Petrification; Legendary
entries: On a hit, this ammunition deals the attack's normal damage. In addition, the creature must succeed a Constitution saving throw or suffer the effects of the {@spell flesh to stone} spell. You must concentrate on this effect as you would on a spell.

entries.name: Triage
entries: While wearing this mask, you can cast the {@spell detect poison and disease} spell at will. The spell ends early if you remove the mask.

itemGroup.name: Amethyst Cosmos
entries: Suspended in an invisible gravimantic field, the shards of gemstone that float around this item refract a kaleidoscopic display of light in shades of mauve, indigo, and lilac. The concentric disks mimic a solar system, a homage to the amethyst dragons and their adoration of celestial bodies.
This item has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Irresistible Force
entries: As a bonus action while holding it, you can expend 1 charge to target a creature you can see within 60 feet of you, forcing it to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is pulled or pushed directly toward or away from you (your choice). A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

entries.name: Planetary Prison
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can cause the stones hovering around its tip to detach from their metal disks and increase in size as they fly towards a Medium or smaller creature you can see within 30 feet of you, attempting to coalesce around it in a rocky prison. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw, with {@b disadvantage} if it's an aberration. On a failed save, the creature takes bludgeoning damage and is {@condition restrained} inside a 5-foot diameter sphere of rock, which hovers a few feet above the ground. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't trapped.

A creature trapped in this way is restrained and can't breathe. Attacks, spells, and other effects can't extend through the sphere or be cast through it. As a bonus action while a creature is trapped, you can cause the sphere to contract and crush the creature within, dealing bludgeoning damage to it. The sphere lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it (no action required), causing the stones to shrink and teleport back to this item. A trapped creature can use an action to make a Strength check, freeing itself on a success. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Copper Cuirass
entries: To complement this robust but aerodynamic suit of armour, the ingenious smith Brimbor devised a pair of intricate mechanical wings, powerful enough to raise their wearer aloft and imbue them with dizzying speed and agility. They produce a soft, soothing hum when gliding through the air.
The armour has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aerial Dash
entries: As a bonus action while wearing it, you can expend 1 charge to gain a flying speed until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Trick of the Flight
entries: When you make a Dexterity saving throw while wearing this armour, you can use your reaction to expend charges in order to fly up to 20 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If this movement takes you out of the area of the effect that triggered the saving throw, you gain {@b advantage} on the saving throw and, if you would take half damage on a successful save, you instead take no damage on a success.

itemGroup.name: Crimson Tyrant
entries: This magic axe deals extra fire damage to any target it hits. While holding the axe, you have {@b advantage} on Intimidation checks against humanoids that perceive themselves as having a lower social standing than you (GM's discretion). The axe has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Kneel Before Me
entries: As a bonus action while holding this axe, you can target a creature you can see within 30 feet of you and expend charges to channel the tyrannical might of a red dragon. When you do, your axe flares with intense flames and an illusory burning crown momentarily manifests over your head. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or fall {@condition prone}, prostrating itself before you.

entries.name: Punish the Defiant
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you succeeds on a saving throw against one of your spells or effects and you are holding this axe, you can use your reaction to expend charges to force it to make a Constitution saving throw as hot air surrounds the target. The creature takes fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Toxic Shot
entries: As an action while holding this firearm, you can release a burst of toxic spores in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't poisoned. A poisoned creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property of the firearm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Midnight Corrosion
entries: This magic sword deals extra acid damage on a hit. The sword has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Killsense
entries: While holding this sword, you always know which hostile creature within 30 feet of you that isn't behind total cover currently has the fewest hit points.

entries.name: Exploit Weakness
entries: As a bonus action while you are within 30 feet of the creature that this sword indicates has the fewest hit points, you can expend charges to gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make against that creature before the end of your current turn.

entries.name: Sweet Savagery
entries: Whenever you score a critical hit against a creature with this weapon, your soul sings with the sweet savagery of battle and you gain temporary hit points, which last for 1 minute.

itemGroup.name: Topaz Despoiler
entries: The articulated mechanical hand at the tip of this item sporadically animates with bursts of negative energy. The fingers fondly caress the flawless topaz orb fixed to the palm, which pulses hypnotically with a gentle, steady glow.

entries.name: Entrap Essence
entries: While holding this item, you can absorb the last breath of a dying creature. When you reduce a Small or larger creature to 0 hit points, you gain temporary hit points.

entries.name: Induce Decay
entries: You can use this item to feed off of the imitation of life you shield yourself with in order to harm and destroy. When you hit a creature with an attack while you are holding this item, you can expend temporary hit points to deal extra necrotic damage to the creature equal to the number of temporary hit points expended.

itemGroup.name: Viridian Deceiver
entries: This magic weapon deals extra poison damage on a hit.

entries.name: Breath of Betrayal
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can exhale a subtle, poisonous mist that permeates the area around you. Affected creatures must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the end of their next turn. A creature that starts its turn while poisoned in this way must immediately use its reaction to make a weapon attack against another creature of your choice within range (or a random one if you don't choose a creature or are incapacitated). Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Cunning Feint
entries: While holding this weapon, you gain the sly intelligence of a green dragon. As a bonus action, you can attempt to deceive a creature within 60 feet of you that can see you, tricking it into defending early against your attacks. Make a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check, contested by that creature's Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check. If you succeed, you gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make against that creature before the end of your current turn.

entries.name: Ice Glide
entries: When you step onto slippery terrain, such as ice or that created by the {@spell grease} spell, while wearing these boots, magical blades of frost instantly form under your feet, allowing you to glide smoothly and easily on the hazardous ground. While these blades are out, you ignore difficult terrain caused by slippery terrain. In addition, while on that type of terrain, you have {@b advantage} on Dexterity saving throws, you automatically succeed on saving throws made to avoid falling {@condition prone}, and your walking speed is increased by 10 feet.

entries.name: Slip and Slide
entries: As an action while wearing these boots, you can release a burst of chilling magic that freezes the ground within 15 feet of you, covering it in a thick layer of slippery ice and turning it into difficult terrain. When the ice appears, each creature standing in its area must make a Dexterity saving throw or fall {@condition prone}.

itemGroup.name: Azulash
entries: While grasping the handle of this weapon, you can use a bonus action to cause a lightning whip to form from its tip, or make it disappear. This magic whip adds 10 feet to your reach when you attack with it on your turn (instead of 5 feet), and deals lightning damage instead of slashing damage.

entries.name: Static Field
entries: While holding the whip, a 20-foot-radius sphere of static electricity centred on you causes creatures' hair to stand on end. Whenever a creature you can see moves into or within this sphere, you can use your reaction to expend a charge, forcing the creature to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes lightning damage and its speed is reduced.

entries.name: Patient Observer
entries: While holding this weapon, you gain the patient observational habits of a hunting blue dragon. As a bonus action, you can attempt to discern weaknesses and openings in a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. Make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check contested by that creature's Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check. If you succeed, you gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make against that creature before the end of your current turn.

entries.name: Protective Lining
entries: The magic of the cloak shields its wearer against extreme cold and heat. While wearing it, you and everything you wear and carry are unharmed by extreme temperatures.

entries.name: Sound Sleep
entries: This cloak can be rolled around you and used as a cosy sleeping bag. When you spend a short rest using the cloak as a sleeping bag, if you roll any Hit Dice to regain hit points, you can reroll any 1s or 2s. You must use the new rolls.

entries.name: Adaptable Conversationalist
entries: While wearing the cloak, you gain a brass dragon's appetite for conversation and can cast a spell from the cloak.

entries.name: Repelling Blast
entries: When you are hit by a melee attack while holding this shield, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and release a blast of repulsive energy, forcing the attacker to make a Strength saving throw if it is within 10 feet of you. On a failed save, the attacker is pushed up to 30 feet directly away from you and knocked {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Deflection
entries: You can also use this shield's repelling property to attempt to deflect projectiles while holding it. When you're hit by a ranged weapon attack roll, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge to force the attacker to reroll the attack roll.

entries.name: Surf's Up!
entries: The shield is also capable of producing a gentle repelling field when placed on a liquid, allowing it to hover a few inches above the surface. While hovering in this way, you can stand on the shield and speak its command word to make it glide across the liquid. You control the movement and trajectory of the shield, which has a speed of 60 feet and can carry up to 400 pounds.

entries.name: Channel the Sun
entries: While holding this spyglass during the daytime, you can expend 1 charge to channel the cleansing power of sunlight. A beam of positive energy erupts from the tip of the spyglass towards a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. If that creature is under an effect that allows it to make a saving throw at the start or end of its turn to end it, it can immediately make an additional saving throw, with {@b advantage}, and without suffering from the effects of a failed saving throw.

entries.name: Study the Stars
entries: This property can only be used at night. You can spend 10 minutes examining the constellations through the spyglass, searching for signs or omens to interpret. At the end of that time, you can expend charges to cast a divination spell.

entries.name: Tales of the Past
entries: The book holds information on unique areas. Whenever you make an Intelligence check to recall or learn information about recorded areas, you gain {@b advantage} on that check if you take 10 minutes to consult the book.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: While you are attuned to this book, you can use the Write & Record and Glimpse of the Past properties.

entries.name: OA: Write & Record
entries: While holding the book, you can take 1 hour to attune your senses to the land within 1 mile of you, concentrating on this process as if you were concentrating on a spell. At the end of the duration, a portion of the book's pages are filled with fragments of information regarding the area's flora, fauna, geography, history, customs, and folklore.

entries.name: OA: Glimpse of the Past
entries: While holding the book, you can cast the {@spell hallucinatory terrain} spell from it, but only to create an illusion of a portion of recorded areas.

entries.name: Draconic Roar
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this helmet, you can expend 1 charge to let out a fierce, earth-shaking roar that weakens your foes' bodies and resolve while emboldening your allies. Each hostile creature within 15 feet of you that can hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, whenever the creature makes an attack roll before the end of its next turn, it must roll a {@dice d4} and subtract the number rolled from the result.

entries.name: Wealthy Diet
entries: While wearing this helmet, you know the location of precious gemstones worth at least 10 gp within 30 feet of you (including through total cover). In addition, you're able to consume these gems to bolster your vitality.

entries.name: Planar Portals
entries: As an action while wearing this ring, you can open a portal to another plane of existence in an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of you. The portal is a 5-foot diameter opening, through which you can see your destination. Its entrance remains open for 1 minute.

entries.name: Sweet Dreams
entries: As an action, you can use the ring to cast the {@spell dream} spell. Once this property of the ring has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Unrelenting Fury
entries: Like a white dragon, you are able to channel a singular focus on slaughtering an enemy. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make with this weapon against the same creature before the end of the current turn. If you hit with that attack, you can reroll any 1s or 2s that you roll on the weapon's damage die or the extra cold damage it deals.

entries.name: Strategic Pinpoint
entries: The compass's divination magic helps you detect objects and creatures of your choice.

entries.name: Tinnitus
entries: This compass mimics a sapphire dragon's ability to manipulate sound. Whenever a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you makes a Dexterity, Wisdom, or Intelligence check or saving throw, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and emit a subtle pulse from the compass.

entries.name: Hydro Cannon
entries: As an action while holding this device, you can expend 1 charge to shoot a jet of highly-pressurised water at a creature you can see within 10 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take bludgeoning damage, be pushed up to 20 feet directly away from you, and fall {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Fickle Faces
entries: While wearing this mask, you have advantage on ability checks using a certain skill, depending on which face is at the front. When you first attune to the mask, and each day at dawn while you remain attuned to it, you can select the mask's expression. You can use a bonus action to shift a different expression to the front, but spiked chains drag across your skin when you do so, and you take damage.

entries.name: Arterial Slash
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to attempt to slice a crucial blood vessel, forcing the creature to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature begins to bleed profusely for 1 minute. While bleeding, the creature takes slashing damage at the end of each of its turns, and any time it regains any hit points, it regains only half as many. A creature without blood automatically succeeds on this saving throw.

itemGroup.name: Bloodhound's Widebrim
entries: This hat has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hunter's Spirit
entries: As an action while wearing this hat, you can expend 1 charge to summon a spectral red hunter to your side for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a bonus action. This hunter takes the form of either a bloodhound or a falcon (your choice), doesn't occupy its space, can't interact physically with creatures or objects, and never leaves your side. As a bonus action, you can make the hunter invisible to all creatures but you, or visible again. Depending on the chosen form, the spectral hunter grants you different benefits.

itemGroup.name: Candlebrand Ring
entries: As a bonus action while wearing the ring, you can speak the ring's command word, causing the waxen god's emblem to become searing hot until you remove it, or until you use a bonus action to repeat its command word and deactivate it.

entries.name: Brand of Protection
entries: While the ring is heated, you can use a bonus action to touch it to a willing creature within your reach and expend 1 charge. The target takes {@damage 1d4} fire damage and gains the benefits of the {@spell shield of faith} spell for 10 minutes.

entries.name: Brand of Castigation
entries: While the ring is heated, you can use an action to make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of you and expend 1 charge. On a hit, the creature takes {@damage 2d4} fire damage and is cursed for 1 minute. While cursed in this way, its AC is reduced by 2. The creature can make a Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the curse on a success.

itemGroup.name: Candlelight Mitre
entries: This hat regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Cleanse the Sins
entries: While wearing the hat, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge and light a searing flame at the top of it. For 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to put it out, this flame casts bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. At the end of each of your turns while this effect is active, each creature in the flame's bright light (including you) must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take fire damage.

entries.name: Burn the Lies
entries: While wearing the hat, you can use an action to expend charges and cause two orbs of flame to form in the mitre's eyes, taking {@damage 2d6} fire damage in the process. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to end it. While the mask's eyes are ablaze, you have {@b advantage} on Intimidation and Insight checks, and you can see {@condition invisible} creatures and objects and automatically pierce through visual illusions.

itemGroup.name: Clutching Crown
entries: The veil on the crown gives other creatures {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made against you, without hindering your vision.

entries.name: Touch of the Grave
entries: While wearing this crown, you can cast the {@spell chill touch} cantrip at will. When you cast this cantrip, you can expend charges to empower it in different ways.

entries.name: Helping Hands
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can cast the {@spell mage hand} cantrip at will, or expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell bigby's hand||arcane hand} spell. When either spell is cast in this way, the hand it produces is always somehow wrong. In addition, when you cast the {@spell bigby's hand||arcane hand} spell using the cloak, it deals necrotic damage instead of force damage, and you take necrotic damage immediately before you begin concentrating on the spell.

entries.name: Rip Cage
entries: While holding the flail, you can use an action to magically rip your own heart from your chest and transfer it to the cage in the weapon without killing yourself, taking necrotic damage in the process. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way. While your heart is in the cage, your creature type is undead, and the flail deals extra necrotic damage on a hit. Your heart remains in the cage until you use a bonus action to take it back into yourself, or until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Share the Pain
entries: When a creature hits you with a melee attack while you're holding the shield, its spikes extend to pierce that creature in return, dealing {@damage 1d8} piercing damage to it. Once this happens, it can't happen again until the end of your next turn.

itemGroup.name: Bloodhound's Immobiliser
entries: This firearm has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Dead Stop
entries: A special chamber within the firearm is loaded with sharp iron nails rather than normal ammunition. Once on each of your turns when you make an attack against a creature with this weapon, you can expend charges to fire a nail instead, which magically increases in size to become a 9-inch-long metal spike, dealing extra piercing damage on a hit.

In addition, if the target is a Large or smaller creature that isn't immune to the grappled or restrained conditions, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pinned against an adjacent surface (GM's discretion). If there's no surface to pin the creature to, it automatically succeeds on this saving throw. While pinned in this way, the creature's speed is 0 feet. A creature within 5 feet of the pinned creature (including itself) can use an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check, removing the spike and freeing the creature on a success.

itemGroup.name: Hand of the King
entries: When fresh blood is spilled on the pristine blade of this sword, its true, corroded appearance is unveiled, a reminder that a glamorous appearance doesn't guarantee noble intentions.
This item has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Decaying Smite
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this sword, you can expend charges and spell slots to deal necrotic damage to the target, in addition to the attack's damage. The extra damage depends on the level of the spell slot spent.

entries.name: Eldritch Empowerment
entries: You can hold this sword by the blade to use it as a spellcasting focus, taking {@damage 1d8} slashing damage when you do so, casting the spell out of the pointing finger on the hilt. When you do so, you can expend charges to cause the spell to be cast at one level higher.

itemGroup.name: Waxen Sceptre
entries: Sinister yet holy, the waxen god blesses those who endure their molten ministrations, restoring their fortitude, protecting their skin, and purifying their words. When lit, the candlesmoke of this sceptre carries the subtle, sweet scent of charred flesh while its tendrils form the screaming visages of those it has blessed.

entries.name: Wax On
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can drop some wax from one of its candles on a willing creature within 5 feet of you. The effects of this wax depend on the colour of the candle you choose:

items.name: White
entries: The wax of this cleansing candle seeps into the skin to purify the body. The creature gains the benefits of the {@spell lesser restoration} spell.

items.name: Orange
entries: The wax of this warding candle expands to form a protective layer over the creature. The creature gains the benefits of the {@spell barkskin} spell for 10 minutes (no concentration required).

items.name: Red
entries: The wax of this overbearing candle forms a barrier over the creature's mouth, preventing it from telling any lies but letting the truth pass. The creature is under the effects of the {@spell zone of truth} spell for 10 minutes.

entries.name: Under Wing
entries: While wielding this shield, when a creature you can see hits a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge to have the shield open its wings in front of the target, giving it a +2 bonus to AC against that attack and potentially causing it to miss.

entries.name: Overgrowth
entries: As an action while wearing these gloves, you can touch a creature, forcing it to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition restrained} for 1 minute as brambles from one of the gloves spread to it, enveloping it in a mass of vines and thorns. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way can use an action to make a Strength check, ending the effect on a success. While the creature is {@condition restrained}, you can use a bonus action to control the writhing brambles.

itemGroup.name: Cloak of the Moth Rider

entries.name: Flight of the Giant Moth
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause it to transform into a {@creature giant moth|LALoTT}, which appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of you. The moth is friendly to you and your companions, it obeys your verbal commands, and it disappears after 1 hour, if you dismiss it as an action, or when it drops to 0 hit points, at which point it transforms back into the cloak. Once this property of the cloak is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: Cloak of the Moth Rider (Very Rare)|LALoTT

entries.name: Slinging Scarab
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this lance, you can expend 1 charge to try to throw it behind you, forcing the creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is tossed onto an unoccupied space within 15 feet of you and lands {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Lock Horns
entries: Whenever a creature hits you with an attack using a melee weapon while you're wearing this helmet, you can use your reaction to expend a charge and gain a +2 bonus to AC against that attack as you attempt to catch the weapon with the horn, potentially causing the attack to miss. If the attack misses, the creature must make a Strength saving throw.

entries.name: Stalwart Scarab
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition grappled} condition, being knocked {@condition prone}, or being moved against your will.

entries.name: Unseelie Gift
entries: As an action while wearing this dress, you can expend 1 charge to pluck one of its feathers and give it to a willing creature, whereupon a bond is created between the feather and its recipient.

entries.name: Night Light
entries: The lantern exists in one of three states: unravelled, shaded, or cocooned. As a bonus action while holding this lantern, you can cause it to change states.

itemGroup.name: Black Bloom
entries: This item has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Puppet of the Rose
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can expend charges. For each charge, one thin, thorny vine shoots towards a different creature you can see within 20 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack against that creature, which takes piercing damage and must make a Constitution saving throw on a hit. On a failed save, a black rose vine grows where the creature was hit and remains for 1 minute, wrapping around the creature's limbs.

While a creature is under this effect, you can manipulate those vines to exert limited control over it. Whenever it would be able to use its reaction, you can use your own reaction to force it to do so, treating all creatures as hostile for the purposes of opportunity attacks. A creature under this effect can't take reactions other than the ones you command it to, and it repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

itemGroup.name: Faerie Moon
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Wax and Wane
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can cause the orb at its centre to shift, representing one phase of the moon. The weapon has different abilities depending on which phase is currently represented. You can activate the properties that require a bonus action as part of the bonus action taken to change the phase of the moon.

itemGroup.name: Flutterblade
entries: You may gain a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. A creature that tries to wield the sword without being attuned to it takes piercing damage at the end of each of its turns as thorny vines around the hilt dig into its skin.

entries.name: Flutterstrike
entries: This sword has the Thrown property (range 20/60 feet), and it flies back to your hand immediately after you use it to make a ranged attack.

entries.name: Radiant Lure
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can cause its hilt to cast dim light in a 10-foot radius, or to cease casting light. While the sword is casting light, you can use an action to summon a moth. As part of this action, you can cast the {@spell animal messenger} spell on it. Once this spell is cast in this manner, it can't be cast again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Verdure
entries: This axe has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Rising Leaves
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can expend charges and swing it in the air to conjure a whirlwind of leaves in the space of a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, forcing it to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is lifted 30 feet straight up into the air, after which it falls normally. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

entries.name: Falling Leaves
entries: While holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell feather fall} spell at will.

itemGroup.name: Willbow
entries: This bow has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. Attacks you make with this bow may deal extra fire damage.

entries.name: Unseelie Lights
entries: As a bonus action while carrying this bow, you can expend charges to imbue your next attack with it with the ghostly light from the bow's bulbs. If you hit a creature with it, that creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or come under the effects of the {@spell faerie fire} spell for 1 minute. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

itemGroup.name: Arcane Treaders
entries: While wearing these boots, you ignore difficult terrain caused by spells or other magical effects.

entries.name: Retrace Steps
entries: At the start of each of your turns, the boots produce an ethereal afterimage of themselves, which stays in the same spot, mimicking the motions of your feet as you move. You can expend 1 charge to teleport to the space of the afterimage, as long as it's unoccupied; you can do so as a bonus action, or as a reaction when you trigger a trap or an opportunity attack as part of your movement (potentially avoiding the trap or attack). The afterimage fades after you teleport to it or at the end of your turn.

entries.name: Cantrip Codex
entries: As a bonus action while within 30 feet of it, you can verbally command the book to spread its pages and follow you, or have it become inanimate. While following you, the book has a flying speed of 30 feet and attempts to stay within 30 feet of you at all times. You can give the book a spell scroll containing a cantrip that has a casting time of one action or bonus action and doesn't deal damage, and order it to incorporate it into its pages, destroying the scroll in the process. Once it's done so, you can use an action to command the book to cast that cantrip if it's following you, or cast it yourself while holding the inanimate book. Giving it a new cantrip erases previous ones from its pages.

entries.name: Pass Notes
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell sending} spell while holding the book. When you cast the spell in this way, one of the book's pages tears off and folds into a paper aeroplane, which then disappears and reappears next to its intended target. The target of the spell has 1 minute to reply by writing a message on the back of the page. After this limit elapses, the aeroplane disappears. The word limit for either message equals five times the result of the writer's Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check, instead of twenty-five.

entries.name: Five-Shooter
entries: You can use these gloves as a spellcasting focus for your spells. When you do so, the spells are shot from the tips of your outstretched fingers.

entries.name: Quickdraw
entries: As a reaction when you roll for initiative while wearing these gloves, you can cast a cantrip that you know with a casting time of one action or bonus action.

entries.name: Transmogrification
entries: When you drink this potion, part of your body acquires the characteristics of an animal for 24 hours.

entries.name: Retractable Tip
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can have it change into a mundane looking quill, or back into its original shape. While in the shape of a quill, it can't be used to make weapon attacks.

entries.name: Illustrated Bestiary
entries: As an action while holding this sword in either form, you can expend 1 charge to eject ink from its tip into an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. The ink then rapidly grows and coalesces into the shape of a beast, as long as there is room to accommodate it. The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and takes its turn immediately after yours. The ink creature dissolves at the end of your next turn.

itemGroup.name: Orb of the Prescient Protector
entries: You can use this item as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Future Shield
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to choose a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you and look into its future, predicting an attack and placing a magical ward over it for the next minute. When that creature is hit by an attack during this time, it gains a +5 bonus to its AC until the end of the turn, potentially causing the triggering attack to miss. This effect ends early after it causes an attack to miss or if you use this property again.

items: Orb of the Prescient Protector (Very Rare)|LALoTT

entries.name: Magical Armament
entries: This weapon has the {@itemProperty F||Finesse property} and can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells. If you know or have prepared a spell from the school of necromancy or conjuration, you are considered proficient with this sickle.

entries.name: Raise Hell
entries: As an action while holding this sickle, you can expend 1 charge to summon a {@creature swarm of skeletal imps|LALoTT} that claw their way out of the sickle's blade and congregate in a space within 5 feet of you. The swarm disappears after 1 hour, when you lose concentration on this effect (as if concentrating on a spell), or when it drops to 0 hit points.

entries.name: Liftoff!
entries: While holding this firework, you can use an action to point it at a creature within 30 feet of you and speak its command word, causing a firework to shoot at the target. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target is moved up to 30 feet by the firework. Each creature within 15 feet of the target must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking fire damage and thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

itemGroup.name: Baton of the Mindbender
entries: Illusion magic fools the senses, enchantments trick the mind, and this device uses both to warp the reality of its weak-willed targets. No two people can agree on what this item looks like: it tailors its illusions to appear in a form that each observer finds most mesmerising.
This item has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Forget
entries: While holding this item, you can manipulate a creature's memory, erasing realisations and reasserting illusions. When a creature within 60 feet of you that you can see succeeds on a saving throw against an illusion spell or an ability check to disbelieve an illusion, you can use your reaction and expend 1 charge to force that creature to reroll the {@dice d20} with {@b disadvantage}. On a failure, the creature doesn't know it initially succeeded.

entries.name: Remember
entries: As an action when you speak a lie while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge to implant a false memory in the mind of a creature within 30 feet of you that can hear you. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or believe the lie you tell. A creature you are fighting has {@b advantage} on this saving throw. The lie must be worded in such a manner as to sound reasonable. A creature reacts to the lie and acts upon its new beliefs in a way that follows its natural inclinations, alignment, or beliefs. The false memory disappears after 1 hour; during this time, the creature experiences illusions that reinforce the lie.

itemGroup.name: Sceptre of the Spellcrafter
entries: This gold and onyx length of wood is ornately carved with the likeness of black dragon, and houses four large gemstones: a ruby, emerald, sapphire, and diamond. Each gem flickers with an inner light, a core of elemental magic. Blending evocation and transmutation magic, this device allows the destructive forces of the elements to be shaped to the whim of its wielder.
This item has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spellcrafter
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge and activate one of the gems to create an elemental blast, choosing either a damage type or the type of area the effect occupies from the table below. Roll a {@dice d4} to randomly determine the parameter you didn't choose; for example, if you choose a 40-foot cone, randomly determine the damage type. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking damage of the respective type on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

rows: 10-foot-radius, 60-foot-high cylinder centred on a point of your choice within 120 feet of you
20-foot-radius sphere centred on a point of your choice within 60 feet of you

itemGroup.name: Spellsling
entries: This sling has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Omnicast
entries: You can use this weapon as a spellcasting focus for your spells. When you do so, the spell takes the appearance of a little sphere of swirling magic, and is shot from the weapon. In addition, you can use an action to insert a spell scroll into the compartment in the weapon's handle. While the weapon contains a spell scroll, you can cast the spell in it regardless of whether or not it's on your class's spell list. If you are normally unable to cast spells, you must make the ability check to determine whether you cast the spell from the scroll successfully for any level of spell, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability. The scroll is destroyed as normal once the spell is cast.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Scholastic Madness property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: Scholastic Madness
entries: Once per turn, when you make an attack with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to imbue the ammunition with unpredictable power from one of the eight schools of magic. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table below to determine the outcome.

rows: Hit or miss, the projectile returns to orbit you, granting you a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

On a hit, the target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be teleported to an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within 20 feet of it. The chosen space must be on a surface or in a liquid that can support the target without the target having to squeeze.
You gain {@b advantage} on all attacks made with this weapon against the target, including this one, until the end of your next turn.
On a hit, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you until the end of its next turn.
On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d4} acid, {@damage 1d4} cold, {@damage 1d4} fire, and {@damage 1d4} lightning damage.
On a hit, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.
On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage, and you regain an equal number of hit points.
On a hit, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be transformed into a rat, as per the {@spell polymorph} spell (no concentration required), until the end of its next turn.

itemGroup.name: Aperture Sword
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thinking with Portals
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can expend charges to create a small blue portal in an unoccupied space you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. When you make an attack roll with the sword, you can have it create a temporary orange portal and attack through it, allowing you to target creatures within your reach of the blue portal as if you were in its space. Additionally, creatures provoke opportunity attacks from you when moving away from the blue portal.

itemGroup.name: Blade of Prey
entries: This sword has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lockjaw
entries: As an action or attack replacement while holding the sword, you can expend charges to perform a special melee weapon attack with the sword, which splits down the middle and tries to close itself around the target. On a hit, the target takes piercing damage and is {@condition grappled}. While a creature is grappled by the sword, you can't use the sword to attack another creature. You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with the sword against a creature grappled by it, and those attacks deal piercing damage.

entries.name: Haemoblade
entries: When you reduce a Small or larger creature that has blood to 0 hit points with an attack using this sword, some of the target's blood temporarily coalesces around the blade, sharpening it. You gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make with this sword before the end of your next turn, and it deals extra damage if that attack hits.

itemGroup.name: Broken Promise
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Maker's Wrath
entries: This weapon deals double damage to objects, structures, and constructs.

entries.name: Blade Storm
entries: Once on each of your turns when you take the Attack action, you can expend charges to replace one of your attacks with a special strike, shooting the broken fragments of the sword.

itemGroup.name: Buzzkill
entries: This sword has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. Whenever you make an attack roll with this weapon without being attuned to it, you take {@damage 1d4} piercing damage as bees sting you.

entries.name: Honey Coating
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can expend charges to launch a spray of sticky honey in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be covered in honey until the end of its next turn, when the honey dissolves. A creature covered in honey has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws, and its speed is halved.

entries.name: Sugar Rush
entries: While holding this sword, you can expend charges to cast the {@spell sugar rush|LALoTT} spell.

itemGroup.name: Coruscant Cutlass
entries: This item has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Prismatic Ray
entries: Once on each of your turns when you take the Attack action while holding this sword, you can expend charges to replace one of your attacks with a ray of randomly coloured light that you shoot from the sword at a target you can see within 60 feet of you.

itemGroup.name: Coward's Bane
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Brutal Wounds
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this weapon, you can expend charges to inflict a grievous wound upon it. Whenever the wounded creature takes an action, bonus action, or reaction that doesn't deal damage, or the first time on its turn that it uses any movement, it takes necrotic damage. The wounded creature or another creature within reach of it can take an action to make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check, ending the effect on a success. If the wounded creature regains 5 or more hit points from a single source, the wound is also healed. A creature can suffer from only one such wound at a time.

entries.name: Scent of Pain
entries: While holding this weapon, you have an uncanny ability to detect creatures by their suffering. You have {@b advantage} on track checks against wounded creatures.

itemGroup.name: Curse of Three
entries: This weapon has charges, each represented by a red and black spectral skull that floats around the blade, and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Three Strikes
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this sword, you can expend charges to attempt to curse the target with misfortune. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have {@b disadvantage} on the next three attack rolls it makes within 1 minute, after which the curse ends.

itemGroup.name: Desire
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Deadly Sins
entries: As a bonus action when you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend charges to afflict the creature with a sinful desire.

itemGroup.name: Electro-Immolator
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Safety Violation
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can expend charges to cause the machinery mounted along the blade to activate. When you do, choose lightning or fire. Until the start of your next turn, attacks made with this sword deal extra damage of the chosen type.

itemGroup.name: Epiphany
entries: This weapon has charges, represented by the colourful gems along the blade that become dull when expended, and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Open Chakra
entries: While holding this sword, you can use an action to expend charges and unlock a portion of your inner potential by casting the {@spell guidance|} cantrip, targeting yourself.

entries.name: Enlightened State
entries: When you expend the last of the weapon's charges, you gain {@b inspiration} and can hover above the ground.

entries.name: Surrender Self
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can speak its command word to encase yourself in gold until the start of your next turn as the sword liquefies and flows over your body. While you are encased in this way, you are {@condition petrified}, you are immune to all damage except force damage, and you remain aware of your surroundings.

entries.name: Chainbreaker Strike
entries: You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with this sword against creatures {@condition grappling} an unwilling target. The first time each turn you hit such a creature, you deal extra radiant damage, and the grappled creature can react to escape.

entries.name: Limitless Shapes
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can cause the paint that makes up its blade to shift, changing its size and shape to transform the weapon into any type of sword.

entries.name: Boundless Imagination
entries: While holding this sword, you can cast the {@spell minor illusion} cantrip at will or cast illusion spells.

itemGroup.name: Nature's Vengeance
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Illusory Appearance
entries: As an action, you can disguise this weapon to look like a mundane walking stick with a magical illusion.

entries.name: Steal Life
entries: When you hit a non-construct creature with an attack roll using this weapon, you can expend charges to syphon the target's life force.

itemGroup.name: Needlemonger's Sting
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Darts and Crafts
entries: While holding this sword, you can pull pins out of the cushion at its hilt and use them as magical darts. You can expend charges to cast the {@spell pins & needles|LALoTT} cantrip.

entries.name: Shadow Stitcher
entries: When you hit a creature in dim or bright light with this sword, you can expend charges to manifest threads that stitch the creature to its own shadow.

itemGroup.name: Phantom Cutters
entries: As a bonus action, you can cause the twinblade to separate at the hilt, becoming two separate shortswords, or to rejoin into a single twinblade.

itemGroup.name: Radiant Dark
entries: This weapon may deal extra radiant or necrotic damage on a hit.

entries.name: Mystical Dichotomy
entries: While holding this sword, you can cast paired spells from the Positive and Negative Planes.

entries.name: Dramatic Score
entries: When you roll a 1 or a 20 on an attack roll made with this magic weapon against a creature of CR 1 or above, it produces a song that grants benefits to you and an ally.

itemGroup.name: Scalestriker
entries: A creature holding this blade has advantage on Carving checks on corpses that have scales. Weapon attacks against scaled creatures have an increased critical range.

entries.name: Reap What You Sow
entries: When you hit a creature of CR 1 or higher with an attack using this sword, it drains vitality to gain charges. You can expend charges to regain hit points.

itemGroup.name: Stellar Remnant
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Event Horizon
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can expend charges to exert gravitational pull on your foes.

itemGroup.name: Storm's Eye
entries: While grasping the hilt of this sword, you can form a blade of pure lightning. The sword has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Blade of Lightning
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can expend charges to increase the reach and deal extra lightning damage.

itemGroup.name: Sunken Slasher
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. This sword deals slashing damage instead of its normal damage type.

entries.name: Drop the Anchor
entries: As a reaction when you would be moved against your will, you can slam this sword into an adjacent surface to stay in place.

entries.name: Bubble Trouble
entries: While holding this sword, you can expend charges to cast the {@spell Otiluke's resilient sphere||resilient sphere} spell.

itemGroup.name: That-Which-Hungers
entries: This sword has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aberrant Infection
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this sword, you can expend charges to infect it. You can deal necrotic damage and heal yourself from infected targets.

itemGroup.name: Titan Feller
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shadow of the Colossus
entries: While holding this sword, you have advantage on checks to climb onto larger creatures and avoid grapples by them.

entries.name: The Bigger They Are...
entries: As an action, you can expend charges to attack a larger creature, dealing extra damage and knocking it prone.

itemGroup.name: Unmarked Grave
entries: This sword deals extra necrotic damage as ghostly hands angrily claw at anything within reach.

entries.name: Familiar Apparition
entries: As an action, you can target a humanoid you can see within 60 feet of you, causing a ghostly apparition to emerge from the rift in the blade into a space within 5 feet of you. The apparition resembles the target, but bears wounds and disfigurements suggesting how it might die.

When the apparition first emerges, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of it for 1 minute. A creature frightened in this way takes psychic damage at the start of each of its turns if the apparition is within 20 feet of it and it can see the apparition. You can use a bonus action on each of your turns to move the apparition up to 20 feet to a space you can see. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The apparition disappears when the target is no longer frightened of it, or if it's ever more than 60 feet away from you. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Vitalophage
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lysogenic Alchemy
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend charges to inject the creature with biological material. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be infected with a disease for 1 minute, during which you can't infect another creature. As an action, you can sink the sword into the corpse of a creature that died while infected and extract a restorative liquid into the detachable pommel or another appropriate container, producing a healing potion.

itemGroup.name: Weaveshaped Sword
entries: While holding this book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells and, if you are a wizard, you can also use this sword as a spellbook.

entries.name: Spellblade
entries: As a bonus action while holding the book, you can form a blade of magical force from its pages, or make the blade disappear. You can use your spellcasting ability in place of your Strength or Dexterity when making attack rolls with it. On a hit, the sword deals force damage instead of its normal damage type.

entries.name: Malleable Magic
entries: When you deal damage to a creature using a spell, you can change the sword's damage type to that of the spell until you cause the blade to disappear or you use a bonus action to change its damage type back to force.

entries.name: Mana Drain
entries: When you hit a spellcasting creature with this sword, you can choose to drain some of its magical power. If the creature has spell slots, it loses its highest unexpended spell slot of a specified level or lower, and you regain one expended slot of the same level or lower. If it doesn't have spell slots, it loses one use of a spell of your choice of a specified level or lower, and you regain one spell slot of the same level as that spell or lower. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Wings of Chaos
entries: This weapon has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Butterfly Effect
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this weapon, you can expend charges to cause the butterfly wings on the sword to flap. Through a convoluted series of increasingly unlikely events, this produces a random and unexpected effect.

itemGroup.name: Barrier Bracer
entries: This item regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hexpansive Defence
entries: While wearing this bracer, when you or a creature you can see within 5 feet of you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to expend up to 5 charges, causing its shield to grow progressively larger with each one. The target of the attack gains a +1 bonus to AC against the triggering attack for each charge expended.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Flashfreezer
entries: The staff regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Nitrogen Spray
entries: As a bonus action while holding this staff, you can expend 1 charge to spray a cloud of freezing mist from its tip in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes cold damage and its speed is reduced until the end of its next turn, as small patches of ice form along its body. A creature whose speed is reduced to 0 feet by this effect is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the staff, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the staff has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Frost & Flame
entries: These two daggers count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. When you're holding both of these daggers, each one deals {@damage 1d4} extra damage to targets it hits. The extra damage of frost is cold damage, and the extra damage of flame is fire damage. The daggers regain expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thermic Shock
entries: As an action while holding both of these daggers, you can expend 1 charge to supercharge them and lash out with both of them at once. Make a melee attack with the daggers against a target. On a hit, the target takes fire damage and cold damage and, if it's a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls until the start of your next turn as it reels from the abrupt difference in temperatures.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the daggers, which immediately destroy the gemstone and regain 1 charge. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Hexaminator
entries: This mask regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Detect Everything
entries: While wearing this mask, you can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells: {@spell detect evil and good}, {@spell detect magic}, {@spell detect poison and disease}, or {@spell find traps}. When you cast the {@spell find traps} spell in this way, you can see the location of traps within range, which appear surrounded by a red glow. Spells cast in this way don't require concentration and end early if you remove the mask.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 100 gp or more into the mask, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the mask has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Hexecutioner Blade
entries: This sword has 12 charges and regains {@dice 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Growing Pains
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to cause an oversized energy blade to form around the sword until the end of your turn. While this blade exists, the sword becomes a greatsword without the {@itemProperty 2H||Two-handed} and {@itemProperty H||Heavy} properties, and with the {@itemProperty L||Light} and {@itemProperty F||Finesse} properties. You are considered proficient with this greatsword.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the sword, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 2 charges. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Hex Runners
entries: This item can function as a pair of prosthetic legs or can encase already existing legs. Either way, it can't be removed against your will while you remain attuned to it. While wearing the legs, your walking speed is increased by 5 feet.

entries.name: Kick into Overdrive
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this item, you can force the arcane crystals to pump even more energy into them. You take {@damage 1d4} fire damage as they overheat, and your walking speed increases until the start of your next turn.

itemGroup.name: Hovercutter
entries: The dagger regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Flick of the Wrist
entries: This dagger is composed of a gauntlet and a blade, has no handle, and can't be wielded by holding it. Instead, the blade hovers a few inches above the gauntlet at all times, can't be removed against your will, and can't be used to make ranged attacks except with the Daggerflight property. While the blade is hovering over the gauntlet and that hand is free, you can use it to attack normally.

entries.name: Daggerflight
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to make a ranged attack with the dagger. Hit or miss, you can then expend 1 additional charge to make another ranged attack with the dagger (no action required) against a different target within 20 feet of the first one. You can make a total of up to three attacks in this way, after which the dagger returns to hover over the gauntlet. The dagger manoeuvres around obstacles mid-flight, and ranged attacks made with it ignore half and three-quarters cover.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the dagger, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the dagger has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Preying Mantis
entries: This item can function as a prosthetic forearm or can be inserted into your arm as part of the attuning process. Either way, it can't be removed against your will while you remain attuned to it. The item regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Mantis Jab
entries: While wearing the prosthesis, you can use a bonus action to extend or retract the blade within it, which functions as a shortsword while it's extended. While the blade is extended, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and make a special melee attack with it. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and must make a Constitution saving throw, taking poison damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. A spellcaster that fails this save takes extra poison damage instead and can't cast spells of 1st level or higher until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 250 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Bard's Best Friend
