entries: This item is a musical instrument that you can play by tapping and rubbing its crystals. It can mimic the sounds of three different instruments, randomly determined upon the item's creation, or produce its own limpid, melodious tone. As a bonus action, you can command the instrument to follow you or go inert. While following you, it stays within your reach at all times, hovering a few feet above the ground.

itemGroup.name: Caster Blaster
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. The firearm doesn't have the Ammunition or Reload properties. Instead, it shoots rays of magical energy, dealing force damage instead of its normal damage type, and you must expend 1 charge each time you make an attack with it.

entries.name: Hex-Rays
entries: A few of the crystals in this weapon have been infused with elemental magic. When you make an attack with this weapon, you can choose to empower it with one of the elemental options.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the firearm, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains charges. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Railbow
entries: This bow may have charges and regain expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hexxeleration
entries: Attacks made with this bow deal extra lightning damage if the target is more than 30 feet away from you. In addition, attacking at long range doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on your attack rolls made with this bow.

entries.name: Cover Blower
entries: As a bonus action while holding the bow, you can expend charges to supercharge it, piercing through magical and nonmagical cover.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the bow, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the bow has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Stungunblade
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Concussive Blast
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can expend charges to fire a blast of concussive energy, pushing creatures and knocking them prone or stunning them.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 100 gp or more into the sword, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Axe of the Cretan Bull

entries.name: Maze of the Mind
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using the axe, you can force the target to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is disoriented and forced to move in a random direction.

items: Axe of the Cretan Bull (Very Rare)|LALoTT

entries.name: Searing Winglash
entries: As a bonus action, you can cause the decorative wings on the armour's back to be enveloped in undulating flames. Whenever a hostile creature comes near, the burning wings lash out at it, dealing fire damage on a failed Dexterity saving throw.

entries.name: Rise from the Ashes
entries: When you drop to 0 hit points, you can choose to have yourself and everything you're wearing and carrying go up in flames and turn into a pile of ash. At the start of your next turn, you regain hit points and emerge from the ashes fully-formed.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This armour is cursed. While cursed, you have vulnerability to cold damage, and taking cold damage prevents you from using its properties until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Ashes to Ashes
entries: This weapon deals extra fire damage to undead creatures and bypasses Undead Fortitude, instantly killing them when reduced to 0 hit points.

itemGroup.name: Kraken-on-a-Stick
entries: This item has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Kraken's Haze
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge to produce a cloud of black ink that heavily obscures an area and can poison creatures inside it.

entries.name: Ink Step
entries: As a bonus action while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge to teleport to an unoccupied space within the ink cloud or an ink-coated surface.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed. While cursed, you are unwilling to part with the item. Whenever you become frightened or surprised, the item expels ink into your eyes, potentially blinding you.

entries.name: Golden Lining
entries: Wearing this armour during a long rest has no detrimental effects. Damage taken while wearing the armour is reduced.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This armour is cursed. While cursed, you can't remove the armour, have disadvantage on avoiding or escaping grapples, and your Constitution modifier is considered to be 0 for determining breath holding or choking time.

itemGroup.name: Stheno's Bow
entries: This bow has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Serpentine Shot
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you make an attack with the bow, you can expend a charge to transform the fired arrow into a snake that attacks the target.

entries.name: Volley of Snakes
entries: As an action while holding this bow, you can shoot an arrow that splits into multiple copies, dealing piercing damage in a cone and forming a swarm of snakes.

itemGroup.name: Aurumplate Robes
entries: These robes can hold a maximum of 5 charges and lose all charges daily at dawn. When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher while wearing the robes, they gain a number of charges equal to the level of the spell.

entries.name: Discharge
entries: As a reaction when a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can expend any number of charges. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw, taking force damage for every charge expended on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Recharge
entries: As a reaction when you fail a Constitution saving throw made to maintain concentration on a spell, you can expend a number of charges equal to the level of the spell (minimum of 1) to reroll the saving throw.

entries.name: Mesmerise
entries: While wearing this crown in bright or dim light, you can use an action and expend 3 charges to cause the crystals to shift and shimmer hypnotically, casting {@spell hypnotic pattern} with the following changes:

items: The spell affects each creature within 10 feet of you that can see you, instead of a 30-foot cube. If you move more than 10 feet away from an affected creature, the effect ends for it.

The crown continues to shift and shimmer for the duration of the spell. Whenever a creature starts its turn within 10 feet of you while this is happening, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and force it to make a saving throw against the spell.

itemGroup.name: Blessed Bastion
entries: You can use this shield as a spellcasting focus for your cleric and paladin spells.

entries.name: Symbol of Good
entries: The mere sight of this shield gives evil pause. While you're wielding the shield, hostile fiends and undead that can see it have {@b disadvantage} on initiative rolls. When you roll initiative, you gain temporary hit points for each creature affected by this property. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: If you are a cleric, you can enhance your attunement to this shield by proving that evil flees before your holy righteousness. In order to do so, you must use your Channel Divinity: Turn Undead feature to turn a total of 20 hostile undead while attuned to the shield. While enhanced, this shield gains the Vade Retro property:

entries.name: EA: Vade Retro
entries: As an action, you can speak the shield's command word to emit a pulse of divine magic from it, which affects all fiends and undead within 15 feet of you in one of the ways described below. Once this property of the shield has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: If a creature is undead, the ground surges upward to wrap around it, swallowing it into the grave it never should have left. The creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be {@condition restrained} for 1 minute. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way can use its action to make a Strength check, freeing itself on a success. Undead more than 10 feet from the ground are unaffected by this property.

If a creature is a fiend, it is partially banished back to its plane of origin. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or become translucent and wispy for 1 minute. While in this state, it can move and interact with the world as normal, but its attacks, spells, and abilities deal half damage. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

itemGroup.name: Cosmic Starknife
entries: The starknife regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Galactic Disc Throw
entries: As an action while holding the starknife, you can expend 1 charge to channel the cosmic energy in the weapon and throw it in a line. When you do, the starknife spins rapidly and takes the shape of a disc galaxy as it travels. Make a ranged weapon attack with the starknife against each creature in the line. After it reaches the end of the line, the starknife stops and spins in place until the end of the turn, at which point it returns to you in a straight line. When it does, make another ranged weapon attack with the starknife against each creature in that line. Attacks made with this property deal slashing damage instead of piercing damage. If the spinning starknife hits a Medium or larger object at any point, it stops spinning and flies back to your hand.

itemGroup.name: Magebind Shackles
entries: These shackles function similarly to a mundane pair of manacles, but have 50 hit points instead of 15 and move with the {@condition restrained} creature if it teleports.

entries.name: Cold Clasp
entries: As an action while holding these shackles, you can speak their command word to attempt to teleport them directly onto a fey or spellcaster you can see within 10 feet of you. The target creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be suddenly {@condition restrained} by the shackles. The shackles typically fasten themselves around a creature's wrists and neck, but can change their form to accommodate different sizes and anatomies.

The {@condition restrained} creature can attempt a Charisma check to escape them, teleporting them back to the original wielder on a success. Breaking out of the shackles with a successful Strength check only forces them open and doesn't destroy them. In addition, every time the {@condition restrained} creature takes an action, bonus action, or reaction, the shackles deal force damage to it. If any of those are taken to cast a spell, the shackles deal more damage.
This property of the shackles can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

itemGroup.name: Mete-ore Bracers
entries: These bracers regain expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Gravity Manipulation
entries: As an action while wearing the bracers, you can expend 1 charge and touch a creature to produce one of the following effects:

items.name: Zero Gravity
entries: You cast the {@spell levitate} spell on that creature.

items.name: Augmented Gravity
entries: The creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be overwhelmed by immense gravitational pressure, becoming {@condition restrained} for 1 minute or until you lose concentration on this effect, as if concentrating on a spell. A creature {@condition restrained} by this effect takes force damage from the pressure at the start of each of its turns.

entries.name: Power from Pain
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can sacrifice your own vitality to empower it. You take {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage, and the next time you hit with an attack using this weapon before the end of your turn, it deals extra necrotic damage. Damage you take from this property ignores temporary hit points and can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you are below half of your hit point maximum when you use this property, the extra damage increases.

itemGroup.name: Spellbreaker
entries: This weapon deals extra damage to fey and spellcasters. It regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Antimagic Strike
entries: When you hit a creature concentrating on a spell with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to give it {@b disadvantage} on its Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration.

itemGroup.name: Blood Mithral Plate
entries: You gain a bonus to AC while wearing this armour. If the armour normally imposes {@b disadvantage} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks or has a Strength requirement, the blood mithral version doesn't.

entries.name: Aegis of Agony
entries: As an action while wearing this armour, you can take necrotic damage and gain twice as many temporary hit points, which last for 1 hour. Damage you take from this property ignores temporary hit points and can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you are below half of your hit point maximum when you use this property, you can do so as a bonus action instead of an action, and you gain extra temporary hit points.

itemGroup.name: Consecrated Weapon
entries: You can use this weapon as a spellcasting focus for your cleric and paladin spells.

entries.name: Intimidating Radiance
entries: This weapon deals extra radiant damage to fiends and undead. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon and roll high on the extra damage die, the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement: Paladin
entries: If you are a paladin, you can enhance your attunement to this weapon by using it to make an attack that deals a significant amount of damage to a hostile fiend or undead in a single hit. While enhanced, this weapon gains the Ultimate Smite property.

itemGroup.name: Runegold Conduit
entries: This blade may have charges and regain expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spellblade
entries: When you cast a cantrip that requires you to make a single spell attack while holding this weapon, you can replace that spell attack with a melee weapon attack using this sword. On a hit, the target takes the weapon's damage and suffers the effects of the cantrip as normal.

entries.name: Crystal Seed
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this hammer, you can implant a small piece of crystal within the target, which slowly grows to envelop it, restricting movement and eventually encasing the creature entirely.

itemGroup.name: Catclaw & Sharkfin
entries: These two claws count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. The claws can change shape so that either one can be worn on either hand.

entries.name: Set Up
entries: Attacks made with catclaw deal force or slashing damage (your choice). When you hit a creature with this weapon, it becomes marked until the end of the current turn.

entries.name: Finishing Touch
entries: Attacks made with sharkfin deal piercing or slashing damage (your choice). If a creature is marked by catclaw, you deal extra damage on a hit.

itemGroup.name: Dingotamatone
entries: This curious item can be used as a musical instrument by squeezing its head to produce sound and placing your fingers on the stem to control the pitch. If you are proficient with a musical instrument, you are proficient with the dingotamatone, which can be used as a spellcasting focus for bard spells. This item regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Dance-Off
entries: As an action, you can choose a creature within 30 feet that can hear you and that you can see, expending 1 charge to challenge it to a dance competition. The dingotamatone begins to play a song in a random key, and you and the selected creature must make contested Charisma ({@skill Performance}) checks.

itemGroup.name: Dungeoneer's Shield
entries: This shield regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sharp Surprise
entries: As a bonus action while wielding this shield, you can extend or retract the blade within it. While the blade is extended, it can be used as a magical shortsword. If you are proficient with shortswords or shields, you are considered proficient with this shield and shortsword.

entries.name: Tier List
entries: As a bonus action while wielding the shield, you can expend 1 charge to ask a question about a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you.

entries.name: Planar Passport
entries: When this cloak visits a plane of existence, it becomes capable of acting as the material component for the {@spell plane shift} spell, attuned to that particular plane. If found, the cloak is already attuned to {@dice 1d4} different planes of existence.

entries.name: Controlled Blink
entries: While wearing the cloak, you can cast the {@spell blink} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, you roll a second {@dice d20} at the end of each of your turns and choose which result to take.

entries.name: Helpful Nudge
entries: While wearing the helm, when a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your reaction to turn the d20 to one of the faces adjacent to the original result, taking the number on it as the result of the roll. Once this property of the helm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Archmage's Hand
entries: While wearing these gloves, you can cast the {@spell mage hand} cantrip, with the following changes:

items: The spell lasts until you are {@condition unconscious} or choose to end it (no action required). If a hand is ever more than 30 feet away from you, it immediately disappears and reappears in your space.
The hands are stronger than normal, and each is capable of lifting a weight in pounds equal to 10 x your proficiency bonus.
The hands are more dexterous than normal, and you can use them to perform ability checks that you would be able to perform using your hands.

entries.name: Moonlight Map
entries: As an action while holding this item in dim or bright light, you can point it at a flat surface within 10 feet of you, causing light to shine through the magnifying glass and form the image of a map on that surface for 10 minutes or until you speak a command word to make it disappear. This map represents the area in a square centred on you, at your elevation. If a creature, object, or area is warded against divination magic, it doesn't show on the map.

itemGroup.name: Monkey's Paw
entries: The arm regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Costly Attunement
entries: When you attune to this item, its corrupted, abyssal flesh merges with your own, replacing a missing arm or consuming an existing one (your choice). An arm consumed in this way doesn't reappear when your attunement to the item ends.

entries.name: Trick Weapon
entries: As a bonus action, you can cause the arm to enlarge to absurd proportions or revert to its normal size. The arm has different properties depending on its size, as described below:

items.name: Normal
entries: You can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells from the arm: {@spell counterspell}, {@spell fear}, or {@spell hypnotic pattern}.

items.name: Enlarged
entries: Your arm becomes a maul with which you are proficient, and can't be used to wield other weapons or shields, or otherwise handle items or perform the somatic component of spells. This maul has the {@itemProperty R||Reach property} and doesn't have the {@itemProperty 2H||Two-handed property}.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This arm is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed in this way, you become tremendously unlucky. The GM decides how this lack of luck manifests itself. In addition, if you cast the {@spell wish} spell to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell, it comes into effect in a way that brings you as much misfortune as possible while still fitting your literal wording.

entries.name: Artistic Licence
entries: As an action while holding the paintbrush, you can paint one monstrous feature of your choice from the list below on a willing creature within 5 feet of you, granting it a special benefit. The feature and its benefits fade after a certain amount of time, or when a new feature is painted on the creature.

items.name: Claws, Horns, or Tail
entries: The creature gains a natural weapon that deals bludgeoning (tail), piercing (horns), or slashing (claws) damage. It can use a bonus action to make an attack with this natural weapon.

items.name: Extra Eyes
entries: The creature gains darkvision and {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight.

items.name: Fins and Gills
entries: The creature gains a swimming speed and can breathe underwater.

items.name: Wings
entries: The creature gains a flying speed.

itemGroup.name: Portable Potion Press
entries: You can use this item as a set of {@item alchemist's supplies|PHB} and as a {@item poisoner's kit|PHB}.

entries.name: Waste Not
entries: As an action, you can insert a body part from a creature of CR 1 or higher that has been dead for no more than 1 hour into the alchemical receptacle on this backpack. Over the course of 1 hour, the backpack presses and distils this body part into restorative fluid. Once this process is complete, roll Hit Dice of the creature the body part belonged to and add the result to the backpack's pool of points, which is stored in a small, detachable bottle. As an action, a creature can remove this bottle and drink its contents, gaining temporary hit points equal to the number of points in the pool, which last for 1 hour. If you are wearing the backpack, you can do this as a bonus action through the use of a drinking tube.

itemGroup.name: Felix's Hammer of Fury
entries: This weapon deals extra fire damage. It has charges and regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Suffer the Consequences
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose a creature within range that can hear you and expend charges to name an action that it's forbidden from taking. If the creature takes the forbidden action before the start of your next turn, you can immediately use your reaction to make an attack against it using the hammer, potentially manifesting a spectral gavel to attack at a distance. The attack deals extra psychic damage on a hit.

itemGroup.name: Warhammerhead
entries: This weapon has charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aquatic Predator
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon. In addition, while underwater, the weapon has the Thrown property, and returns to your hand immediately after you make a ranged attack with it.

entries.name: Hunter's Aura
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can expend charges to tap into the primal hunger of the shark, becoming more adept at sensing blood and weakness until the start of your next turn. Until then, you have {@b advantage} on Perception checks made to detect creatures within range that don't have all of their hit points, as well as {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with the hammer against them.

entries.name: Ice Shot
entries: This weapon doesn't use normal ammunition. Instead of firing bolts, when you make an attack with it, an icicle forms already loaded into the crossbow and ready to fire. At the start of your turn, you can choose to ignore the Loading property of the crossbow until your next turn, taking fire damage as the weapon vents heat into you to form more icicles.

entries.name: Deer Head Lights
entries: As an action while holding the crossbow, you can cause the deer's eyes to emit bright light in a 30-foot cone, or turn the light off. When you activate the light, you can choose to have it flair with momentary intensity, blinding creatures in the cone.

itemGroup.name: Bloom Boots
entries: These boots regain expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Plantstep
entries: While wearing these boots, grass grows and flowers bloom from the ground where you step, remaining for a few seconds before disappearing. You can move through plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard. In addition, you have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed when climbing trees and other plants, as small branches and leaves quickly grow to support you, then wither.

entries.name: Induce Growth
entries: While wearing these boots and standing on the ground, you can expend 1 or more charges to stomp one of your feet and produce effects:

items: You cast the {@spell plant growth} spell, centred on yourself and as an action only.
As a bonus action, you cause a large vine to grow beneath your feet, lifting you up to 30 feet straight up. This vine remains for 1 minute, after which it withers and disappears.

As a reaction when you're subjected to an effect that would move you against your will, you cause small vines to wrap around your feet, preventing you from being moved until the start of your next turn.

itemGroup.name: Huntsman's Goggles
entries: This item regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thermal Vision
entries: As a bonus action while wearing these goggles, you can expend 1 charge to activate them. For 1 minute, you can see creatures and objects within 60 feet of you coloured in a spectrum from red for warm creatures to blue for cold ones. This grants you {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight made to detect creatures that emit heat, and you can always see those creatures regardless of light conditions, obscurement, or invisibility, but not through total cover. Some constructs, elementals, and most undead exist at ambient temperature and can't be seen with this property (GM's discretion).

entries.name: Target Acquired
entries: Alternatively, while wearing these goggles, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell hunter's mark} spell. When you cast the spell in this way, the extra damage dealt by it is cold damage, and you can see the target with the goggles' Thermal Vision property even if you normally couldn't.

itemGroup.name: Living Flute
entries: This item regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Song of Spring
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to play a beautiful tune on the flute, which causes its flowers to bloom and emit a sweet, soothing scent. Each other creature within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by every creature it can see for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature or any of its companions are harmed or targeted by a spell or ability by one of the creatures it's {@condition charmed} by, it's no longer {@condition charmed} by that creature. Once this property of the flute has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Petal Storm
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to play a frantic tune on the flute, which causes its flowers to shed their petals. These petals fly in a flurry towards a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, forcing it to make a Strength saving throw (the target can choose to fail this save). On a failed save, the creature is lifted up by the petals and carried 30 feet in any direction of your choice.

itemGroup.name: Quick-Grow Acorns
entries: These acorns are usually found in bundles of {@dice 1d4 + 1}.

entries.name: Timber!
entries: As an action, you can plant one of these acorns in an unoccupied space on the ground within your reach, causing it to rapidly grow into a tree bearing gorgeous autumn foliage. The tree is an ally to you and your companions, and it shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). At the end of its turn, it rapidly decays and falls in a 15-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line in a direction of your choice. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

itemGroup.name: Scorch Helm
entries: This helmet regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Warm Summer Knight
entries: You can expend 1 charge to spread the flames on the helmet over your entire body, coating you in golden fire for 1 minute. During this time, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your melee weapon attacks deal extra fire damage.
When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, it takes fire damage.
When you successfully grapple a creature, it takes fire damage. A creature grappled by you or grappling you takes fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

entries.name: Swallow's Flight
entries: This weapon has the Thrown (range 20/60) property. When thrown, it functions similarly to a boomerang, spinning as it flies through the air and returning to your hand after the attack hits or misses.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Flock On property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Flock On
entries: As an action while holding the sword, you can speak its command word, causing the flocks of metallic swallows to fly towards a creature of your choice within 30 feet of you, circling and harassing it for 1 minute, or until the creature is reduced to 0 hit points, you are {@condition unconscious}, or you speak the command word again. At the start of each of its turns, the creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes slashing damage and has {@b disadvantage} on ability checks and attack rolls until the start of its next turn. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Staff of Decay
entries: If you expend the last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the staff withers away and is destroyed.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While holding this staff, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast spells from it.

entries.name: Corpse Expert
entries: While holding this staff, you can use an action to touch a corpse and immediately know how long ago it died, in hours if it's been dead for less than two days, and in days otherwise.

itemGroup.name: Sundial Ring
entries: This ring functions as a sundial even when not exposed to the sun, allowing you to tell the time in any location, regardless of light conditions, as long as you can see the ring.

entries.name: Circadian Clock
entries: While wearing this ring, you gain different benefits depending on the time of day, as described below. This property only works on planes of existence that have a day-night cycle.

items.name: Dawn
entries: As the day begins, you are inspired to make the most of it. You gain inspiration and {@dice 2d10} temporary hit points, both of which last until daytime.

items.name: Daytime
entries: As the sun shines brightly in the sky, you are at your strongest. You gain a +1 bonus to AC, and when you deal damage with a weapon attack, you can reroll the weapon's damage dice, taking the second result.

items.name: Dusk
entries: As the daylight dwindles, you become more alert and wary of your surroundings. You have {@b advantage} on initiative rolls and Perception checks.

items.name: Night
entries: As darkness covers the land, you cling to any vestiges of light and warmth that you can find. You gain darkvision out to 30 feet. If you already have darkvision from another source, its range is increased by 30 feet.

itemGroup.name: Sunsplitter
entries: This spear has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Luminous Refraction
entries: As an action while outside during the day, you can lift this spear towards the sky and expend 1 charge, causing a beam of pure radiance to descend from the sun into the spear's crystal. The crystal splits the beam into your choice of 2 to 6 smaller beams, each of which targets a different creature you can see. Each creature must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking an amount of radiant damage dependent on how many beams you produce on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success.

entries.name: Aura of Decay
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this hat, you can cause it to emit an aura of decay, or deactivate the aura. While the aura is active, whenever a creature near you takes damage, it takes extra necrotic damage, and whenever a creature near you regains hit points, it regains fewer hit points. In addition, you can use an action to cause any number of corpses within the aura to disintegrate, preventing them from being resurrected or raised as undead.

itemGroup.name: Arm of the Grey Knight
entries: {@i Component: undead arm of the grey knight}

A shining beacon of tenacity and unwavering resolve, this amputated arm fights on with the strength of many men no matter how many times it's cut down or crushed into a pulp. Stubborn to a fault, it refuses to listen to anyone but the creature attuned to it, and occasionally makes rude gestures at passersby behind its owner's back, attempting to goad them into a fight.

entries.name: Helping Hand
entries: The arm is an object with AC 14 and 20 hit points. If it's ever more than 30 feet away from you, it moves 20 feet towards you at the start of each of your turns by crawling along the ground. As a bonus action, you can command it to move up to 20 feet and perform one of the following tasks:

items: The arm manipulates an object in the same way as the {@spell mage hand} cantrip.
The arm punches a creature within 5 feet of it, making an attack roll against it (+5 to hit) and dealing 1d6 + 3 bludgeoning damage on a hit.

The arm attempts to grab a creature within 5 feet of it, which must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the arm latches on to it, reducing its speed by 10 feet. A creature can use its action to make a DC 13 Strength check, pulling the arm off of it or a creature within its reach on a success. The arm can't perform other tasks while holding onto a creature.

entries.name: Just a Flesh Wound
entries: If the arm is reduced to 0 hit points, it can't move or be commanded, but its remarkable resilience and regenerative abilities ensure that this doesn't last for long. The arm recovers all its hit points after 1d4 hours. A spellcaster can also use an action to touch the arm and spend a spell slot of any level to cause the arm to recover all of its hit points.

itemGroup.name: Osseous Spike
entries: {@i Component: astral bone (unique - bonemonger)}

The essence of the malicious bonemonger always hungers for more victims, and this craving persists even after death. Those who hold this jagged lance can feel its insatiable desire to be bathed in blood, and see shadowy claws crawl across its surface when it has gone too long without...
This lance has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Syphoning Splinters
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this lance, you can expend 1 charge to cause the tip of the lance to explode into sharp splinters; the creature must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 4d4 piercing damage and some of the splinters are embedded inside of it for 1 minute. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects. A creature with splinters inside of it takes 2d4 necrotic damage at the end of each of its turns, and you regain hit points equal to half the necrotic damage dealt this way as the splinters syphon the victim's vitality into you. A creature with splinters inside it or another creature within 5 feet of it can use an action to make a DC 15 Wisdom (Medicine) check, removing the splinters on a success.

itemGroup.name: Robes of the Enchanter
entries: {@i Component: astral heart (unique - bonemonger)}

Many scholars believe the name of this extravagant garment to be a grave misnomer, as it has no apparent relation whatsoever to enchantment magic. In truth, the robes are named after the man who devised and created the first set, a legendary sorcerer of tremendous power and curious explosive proclivities... as well as astonishing ignorance regarding the schools of magic.

entries.name: Spontaneous Detonation
entries: As an action while wearing these robes, you can wave your arms dramatically to create a small explosion at a point you can see within 30 feet of you. Each creature within 5 feet of the point must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 1d8 fire damage, get pushed back 10 feet, and be knocked prone.

itemGroup.name: The Sword in/out of the Stone
itemGroup.reqAttune: by a creature with a Strength score of 16 or higher
entries: {@i Component: astral brain (unique - bonemonger)}

The Lady of the Lake, her arm clad in the purest shimmering samite, once bestowed a blade just like this one unto the rightful ruler of the land, that he might wield it in defence of the downtrodden and oppressed. This replica is just as good as the real thing... but its craftsman seems to have forgotten a tiny little detail.
items: The Sword out of the Stone|HelianasGuidetoMonsterHunting

entries.name: False Glamour
entries: When you first don this dress after attuning to it, and each day at dawn as long as you're wearing it, it casts the {@spell sanctuary} spell on you, with the following changes:

items: It lasts for 8 hours instead of 1 minute.
You're endowed with an aura of magnetic charisma while the spell lasts, granting you {@b advantage} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks.
It doesn't end early if you make an attack or cast a spell that affects an enemy creature, but it ends if a creature hits you with an attack.

When the spell ends, your face is briefly replaced with a horrifying ophidian visage. Each creature of your choice within range that can see you must make a Wisdom saving throw. A creature that fails its saving throw takes psychic damage and is {@condition frightened}.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This dress is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you until you're targeted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you can't remove the dress, and if you look at your own reflection while not under the effect of the dress' {@spell sanctuary} spell, you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be {@condition restrained} as your body starts to turn to stone. You must repeat the saving throw at the end of your next turn, becoming {@condition petrified} on a failure or ending the effect on a success. The petrification lasts until the next dawn, when the dress casts {@spell sanctuary} on you again. Once you succeed on a saving throw against the curse, you can't be affected by it again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Inkstaintaneous Blast
entries: As an action, you can light the fuse on this bomb and toss it up to 30 feet away, causing it to detonate in an explosion. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw or be covered in ink and {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. A creature covered in ink can't benefit from being {@condition invisible}. A creature {@condition blinded} by this effect takes poison damage at the start of each of its turns, and can make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@condition blinded} condition on itself on a success.

entries.name: Delayed Inksplosion
entries: Alternatively, you can use an action to set this bomb down and specify a special trigger for its detonation. This can be a visual trigger that occurs within 15 feet of the bomb, such as a visible creature moving into that range, or a special command word spoken by a creature as a bonus action while within 30 feet of the bomb. When the conditions for the trigger are met, the bomb explodes as per the Inkstaintaneous Blast property.

itemGroup.name: Lernaean Pendant
entries: This pendant regains expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Better Than One
entries: While you are wearing this pendant and make a saving throw against an effect that would cause you to become {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, or {@condition frightened}, you can expend 1 charge as a reaction to grow a second head. Each of your heads makes a separate saving throw against the condition, after which you choose one of them to shrivel and fall off, taking the result of the saving throw for the head that remains. When you use this property, you also regain hit points.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, when you take fire damage, you can't regain hit points or use the Better Than One property until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Reactive Strikes
entries: While wielding this weapon, you gain a special reaction that you can take once on each round. This special reaction can only be used to make an opportunity attack with the flail, and you can't use it on the same turn that you take your normal reaction.

entries.name: Clamp Down
entries: When you hit a creature with an opportunity attack using this flail, you deal extra piercing damage to it and can choose to immediately grapple the target, as the hydra heads bite down on it. The grapple ends if you let go of the flail. While a creature is grappled in this manner, you can't use the flail to attack a different target.

entries.name: Labyrinth Dweller
entries: While wearing this helm, you can perfectly recall any path you've travelled and can't get lost by nonmagical means. In addition, you automatically succeed on Intelligence checks made to escape the {@spell maze} spell, and have {@b advantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks made to spot and open secret doors and passages.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Maze Architect property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Maze Architect
entries: While wearing this helm, you can cast the {@spell passwall} spell from it. When the spell is cast in this way, you can end it early as a bonus action, and when a creature first enters the passage created by the spell, you can deal {@damage 1d6} psychic damage to it. Once this property of the helm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This helm is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, whenever you start your turn and can see a creature primarily coloured or dressed in red, you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you must spend your turn moving towards that creature and, if possible, making melee attacks against it. Once you succeed on this saving throw, you can't be affected by the curse again for 24 hours.

entries.name: Offensive Defence
entries: This weapon deals extra damage to creatures it hits that have a lower AC than yours.

entries.name: Triumphant Roar
entries: When you roll a 20 on an attack roll made with this weapon, the lion's visage lets out a ferocious roar. Each creature other than you that can hear it within 20 feet of you takes thunder damage and must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.

itemGroup.name: Origami Animals
entries: Over the course of a minute, you can fold this enchanted paper into an animal, activating its magical potential

entries.name: Ichorous Blight
entries: While wielding this dagger, you can use a bonus action to imbue its next strike with an inkyblack ichor that gets injected into its victim. The next time you hit a creature with the dagger before the end of your next turn, it must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes an additional {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage and is {@condition Blinded} for {@b 1 minute}. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition Blinded}. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Dagger of the Deep (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Ichorous Blight
entries: While wielding this dagger, you can use a bonus action to imbue its next strike with an inkyblack ichor that gets injected into its victim. The next time you hit a creature with the dagger before the end of your next turn, it must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes an additional {@damage 4d6} Necrotic damage and is {@condition Blinded} for {@b 1 minute}. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition Blinded}. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Dagger of the Deep (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Ichorous Blight
entries: While wielding this dagger, you can use a bonus action to imbue its next strike with an inkyblack ichor that gets injected into its victim. The next time you hit a creature with the dagger before the end of your next turn, it must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes an additional {@damage 6d6} Necrotic damage and is {@condition Blinded} for {@b 1 minute}. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition Blinded}. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Dagger of the Deep (Legendary)
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Ichorous Blight
entries: While wielding this dagger, you can use a bonus action to imbue its next strike with an inkyblack ichor that gets injected into its victim. The next time you hit a creature with the dagger before the end of your next turn, it must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes an additional {@damage 8d6} Necrotic damage and is {@condition Blinded} for {@b 1 minute}. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition Blinded}. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Eyes of the Forgotten (Uncommon)
entries: This ring has {@b 1 charge}, represented by a single open eye, and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Evil Eye
entries: As an action, you can expend {@b 1 charge}, thrusting the ring towards a creature you can see within {@b 60 feet} of you while speaking the command word. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be affected by a randomly determined effect from the {@spell Eyebite} spell (asleep, panicked, or sickened) for {@b 1 minute}.

A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. While a creature is under this effect, the eye on the ring becomes an inky void, imbued with dread power. When the effect ends, the eye closes.

item.name: Eyes of the Forgotten (Rare)
entries: This ring has {@b 2 charges}, represented by two open eyes, and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Evil Eye
entries: As an action, you can expend {@b 1 charge}, thrusting the ring towards a creature you can see within {@b 60 feet} of you while speaking the command word. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be affected by a randomly determined effect from the {@spell Eyebite} spell (asleep, panicked, or sickened) for {@b 1 minute}.

A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. While a creature is under this effect, one of the eyes on the ring becomes an inky void, imbued with dread power. When the effect ends, the eye closes.

item.name: Eyes of the Forgotten (Very Rare)
entries: This ring has {@b 3 charges}, represented by three open eyes, and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Evil Eye
entries: As an action, you can expend {@b 1 charge}, thrusting the ring towards a creature you can see within {@b 60 feet} of you while speaking the command word. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or be affected by a randomly determined effect from the {@spell Eyebite} spell (asleep, panicked, or sickened) for {@b 1 minute}.

A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. While a creature is under this effect, one of the eyes on the ring becomes an inky void, imbued with dread power. When the effect ends, the eye closes.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Being underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

entries.name: Crab Knight
entries: While holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell Phantom Steed} spell. The steed always takes the form of a giant centenary crab, which has a swimming speed equal to its walking speed. Once this property of the lance has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Clawful
entries: Once on each of your turns, you can replace one of the attacks you make with this lance as part of the Attack action with an attempt to capture a creature with it, causing the missing half of the crab's claw to appear as a spectral, shimmering force and close itself around the target. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be {@condition Grappled} by the lance for {@b 1 minute} (escape {@dc 15}), until you release the target, or until you are no longer holding the lance (no action required).

While you're grappling a creature with the lance, you can only use the lance to attack that creature, crushing it with the claw. These attacks deal Bludgeoning damage instead of Piercing damage, deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage on a hit, and don't suffer from {@b disadvantage} as a result of you being within 5 feet of the target.

item.name: Lance of the Crab Knight (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Being underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

entries.name: Crab Knight
entries: While holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell Phantom Steed} spell. The steed always takes the form of a giant centenary crab, which has a swimming speed equal to its walking speed. Once this property of the lance has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Clawful
entries: Once on each of your turns, you can replace one of the attacks you make with this lance as part of the Attack action with an attempt to capture a creature with it, causing the missing half of the crab's claw to appear as a spectral, shimmering force and close itself around the target. The target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be {@condition Grappled} by the lance for {@b 1 minute} (escape {@dc 16}), until you release the target, or until you are no longer holding the lance (no action required).

While you're grappling a creature with the lance, you can only use the lance to attack that creature, crushing it with the claw. These attacks deal Bludgeoning damage instead of Piercing damage, deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage on a hit, and don't suffer from {@b disadvantage} as a result of you being within 5 feet of the target.

item.name: Rapier of the Yellow King (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You can speak and understand Deep Speech.

entries.name: Imposing Presence
entries: While holding this rapier, you can cast the {@spell Dominate Person} spell (save {@dc 16}). Once the spell has been cast in this way, it can't be cast again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Shackles of Pain
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose one creature you can see within {@b 60 feet}. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Charisma saving throw or become cursed and bound to you for {@b 1 minute}. So long as it is on the same plane of existence as you, whenever you take damage from any source, the cursed creature takes half as much damage of the same type. This effect can be removed by any spell or effect that removes curses. Once this property of the rapier has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Rapier of the Yellow King (Legendary)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage to any target it hits. You can speak and understand Deep Speech.

entries.name: Imposing Presence
entries: While holding this rapier, you can cast the {@spell Dominate Person} spell (save {@dc 17}). Once the spell has been cast in this way, it can't be cast again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Shackles of Pain
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose one creature you can see within {@b 60 feet}. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 17} Charisma saving throw or become cursed and bound to you for {@b 1 minute}. So long as it is on the same plane of existence as you, whenever you take damage from any source, the cursed creature takes half as much damage of the same type. This effect can be removed by any spell or effect that removes curses. Once this property of the rapier has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Tentacle Lash
entries: As an action, you can speak the ring's command word to cause your arm to elongate into a long, wet, suckered tentacle for {@b 1 minute}. As part of that action, and as an action on each of your turns, you can perform a melee spell attack ({@hit 5}) against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d8} Bludgeoning damage and, if it is a Huge or smaller creature, it must make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw. On a failure, you can choose one of the following effects:

items: You disarm the target; you take one item or weapon the target is carrying or wielding (and that isn't fastened, e.g. armour) into your tentacled hand. If your tentacled hand isn't free, the item falls at your feet instead.

entries: Once this property has been used, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, it can't be used again until the ring spends 1 continuous hour submerged in water.

entries.name: Tentacle Lash
entries: As an action, you can speak the ring's command word to cause your arm to elongate into a long, wet, suckered tentacle for {@b 1 minute}. As part of that action, and as an action on each of your turns, you can perform a melee spell attack ({@hit 5}) against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 6d8} Bludgeoning damage and, if it is a Huge or smaller creature, it must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failure, you can choose one of the following effects:

items: You disarm the target; you take one item or weapon the target is carrying or wielding (and that isn't fastened, e.g. armour) into your tentacled hand. If your tentacled hand isn't free, the item falls at your feet instead.

entries: Once this property has been used, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, it can't be used again until the ring spends 1 continuous hour submerged in water.

item.name: Runestone of Ocean Currents (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Saltbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the stone, you can speak its command word, expending 1 charge and causing a frothy, turquoise bolt of force to strike a target within {@b 120 feet} of you. The target takes {@damage 1d6} cold damage and {@damage 1d6} force damage. A creature under the influence of the {@spell shield} spell or similar magic takes no damage from this effect.

item.name: Runestone of Ocean Currents (Rare)
entries: This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Saltbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the stone, you can speak its command word, expending 1 charge and causing a frothy, turquoise bolt of force to strike a target within {@b 120 feet} of you. The target takes {@damage 2d6} cold damage and {@damage 2d6} force damage. A creature under the influence of the {@spell shield} spell or similar magic takes no damage from this effect.

item.name: Runestone of Ocean Currents (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Saltbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the stone, you can speak its command word, expending 1 charge and causing a frothy, turquoise bolt of force to strike a target within {@b 120 feet} of you. The target takes {@damage 3d6} cold damage and {@damage 3d6} force damage. A creature under the influence of the {@spell shield} spell or similar magic takes no damage from this effect.

item.name: Runestone of Ocean Currents (Legendary)
entries: This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Saltbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the stone, you can speak its command word, expending 1 charge and causing a frothy, turquoise bolt of force to strike a target within {@b 120 feet} of you. The target takes {@damage 4d6} cold damage and {@damage 4d6} force damage. A creature under the influence of the {@spell shield} spell or similar magic takes no damage from this effect.

item.name: Sceptre of the Sunken King (Very Rare)
entries: You can use the sceptre as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Power of the Depths
entries: While holding this rod, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have resistance to cold damage.
You have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

While at least waist-deep in water, you have {@b advantage} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, and can cast any of the following spells at any level between its normal level and 4th-level (save {@dc 16}): {@spell control water}, {@spell create or destroy water}, {@spell riptide|LALoTT}, {@spell water breathing}. After you do so, roll a {@dice d6}. If the result is equal to or less than the level the spell is cast at, the sceptre's eye goes dim and you can't use the rod to cast spells in this way until the next dawn.

item.name: Sceptre of the Sunken King (Rare)
entries: You can use the sceptre as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Power of the Depths
entries: While holding this rod, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have resistance to cold damage.
You have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
While at least waist-deep in water, you have {@b advantage} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.

entries.name: Blood Trail
entries: As an action, you can add one drop of a creature's blood to the candle, causing it to imprint on that creature. Once a bloodwax candle has imprinted on a creature, it can't imprint on another. You can then light the candle as an action, taking {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage as your blood is drawn from your body to form a ghastly red trail in the air, leading directly to the creature the candle has imprinted on. For every 10 minutes that the candle remains lit, it deals another {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage to the creature who lit it. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.
Once the candle has burnt for 1 hour in total, its wax is depleted and can't be used again. Each time the candle is lit and extinguished, a minimum of 10 minutes' worth of wax is consumed.

The trail is undisturbed by wind of any strength and can't pass through solid objects, but will navigate through holes as small as 1 inch in diameter. If all paths to the creature are blocked by solid objects, the trail ends as close to the creature as possible. The candle can't distinguish between identical twins or clones, and will instead create a trail to each. If the creature is on another plane, the pall of red smoke listlessly hangs in the air.

entries.name: Blood Trail
entries: As an action, you can add one drop of a creature's blood to the candle, causing it to imprint on that creature. Once a bloodwax candle has imprinted on a creature, it can't imprint on another. You can then light the candle as an action, taking {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage as your blood is drawn from your body to form a ghastly red trail in the air, leading directly to the creature the candle has imprinted on. For every 10 minutes that the candle remains lit, it deals another {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage to the creature who lit it. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.
Once the candle has burnt for 1 hour in total, its wax is depleted and can't be used again. Each time the candle is lit and extinguished, a minimum of 10 minutes' worth of wax is consumed.

The trail is undisturbed by wind of any strength and can't pass through solid objects, but will navigate through holes as small as 1 inch in diameter. If all paths to the creature are blocked by solid objects, the trail ends as close to the creature as possible. The candle can't distinguish between identical twins or clones, and will instead create a trail to each. If the creature is on another plane, the pall of red smoke listlessly hangs in the air.
While the candle is burning, you also gain the following information about the creature it's imprinted on:

items: Its current emotional state, in the form of a word or short sentence.
Whether it has fewer than all, three-quarters, one-half, or one-quarter of its hit points.
How many spell slots it has left, if any.
Whether it's under the effect of a spell, and which one.

entries.name: Beastblood Serum
entries: The dial around the plunger can be rotated to select one of the following symbols, changing the hue of the blood within: bear (iridescent green); bull (sanguine red); cat (silky purple); eagle (piercing yellow); fox (opulent orange); and owl (midnight blue). As an action, you can select a symbol and inject the blood in the syringe into a willing creature within your reach. The creature gains the benefits of the {@spell enhance ability} spell that correspond to the chosen animal for 10 minutes. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Beastblood Serum
entries: The dial around the plunger can be rotated to select one of the following symbols, changing the hue of the blood within: bear (iridescent green); bull (sanguine red); cat (silky purple); eagle (piercing yellow); fox (opulent orange); and owl (midnight blue).

As an action, you can rotate the vial to select two beast symbols simultaneously, causing the blood in the syringe to acquire both colours, which swirl around one another without blending together. When a willing creature within your reach is injected with the solution, it gains the effects of both of the selected options of the {@spell enhance ability} spell for 1 hour, but takes {@damage 4d6} necrotic damage from the unnatural mix. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dark Empowerment
entries: While wearing this mask, you have one additional 1st-level spell slot, which you regain daily at dawn. In addition, you can cast the {@spell augury} spell from the mask. Once the mask has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This mask is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. When you first don this item after attuning to it, the silver tentacles reshape and tighten around your neck. While cursed, you can't remove the mask, and each time you cast a spell, you take {@damage 1d4} psychic damage.

entries.name: Dark Empowerment
entries: While wearing this mask, you have two additional 1st-level spell slots, which you regain daily at dawn. In addition, you can cast the {@spell augury} and {@spell commune} spells from the mask. Once the mask has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn. When you cast {@spell commune} using the mask, your questions are answered by the deity or power associated with this item: an entity of death and decay.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This mask is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. When you first don this item after attuning to it, the silver tentacles reshape and tighten around your neck. While cursed, you can't remove the mask, and each time you cast a spell, you take {@damage 1d4} psychic damage.

entries.name: Dark Empowerment
entries: While wearing this mask, you have one additional 1st-level spell slot and one additional 2nd-level spell slot, which you regain daily at dawn. In addition, you can cast the {@spell true seeing} and {@spell commune} spells from the mask. Once the mask has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn. When you cast {@spell commune} using the mask, your questions are answered by the deity or power associated with this item: an entity of death and decay.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This mask is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. When you first don this item after attuning to it, the silver tentacles reshape and tighten around your neck. While cursed, you can't remove the mask, and each time you cast a spell, you take {@damage 1d4} psychic damage.

entries.name: Ethereal Haunt
entries: As an action, choose a creature within {@b 60 feet} of you that can hear you. It must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw as occult whispers wrack its psyche. The DC for this saving throw increases by 1 for each creature friendly to you also wearing cultist robes within 15 feet of you (maximum 5). On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 3d6} psychic damage, becomes {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute, and must immediately use its reaction to move a distance equal to its speed away from you, avoiding obviously dangerous ground. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage, isn't {@condition frightened}, and doesn't move away.

A creature {@condition frightened} of you in this way can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dark Devotion
entries: If you spend 1 hour in prayer to the being in whose name these robes were made (which can be done as part of a short or long rest), you gain {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Ethereal Haunt
entries: As an action, choose a creature within {@b 60 feet} of you that can hear you. It must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw as occult whispers wrack its psyche. The DC for this saving throw increases by 1 for each creature friendly to you also wearing cultist robes within 15 feet of you (maximum 5). On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 4d8} psychic damage, becomes {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute, and must immediately use its reaction to move a distance equal to its speed away from you, avoiding obviously dangerous ground. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage, isn't {@condition frightened}, and doesn't move away.

A creature {@condition frightened} of you in this way can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dark Devotion
entries: If you spend 1 hour in prayer to the being in whose name these robes were made (which can be done as part of a short or long rest), you gain {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} until you finish a short or long rest. While under the effect of Dark Devotion, you can cast {@spell command} (save {@dc 15}). Once the robes have been used to cast a spell, they can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Ethereal Haunt
entries: As an action, choose a creature within {@b 60 feet} of you that can hear you. It must make a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw as occult whispers wrack its psyche. The DC for this saving throw increases by 1 for each creature friendly to you also wearing cultist robes within 15 feet of you (maximum 5). On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 5d10} psychic damage, becomes {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute, and must immediately use its reaction to move a distance equal to its speed away from you, avoiding obviously dangerous ground. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage, isn't {@condition frightened}, and doesn't move away.

A creature {@condition frightened} of you in this way can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dark Devotion
entries: If you spend 1 hour in prayer to the being in whose name these robes were made (which can be done as part of a short or long rest), you gain {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} until you finish a short or long rest. While under the effect of Dark Devotion, you can cast the following spells (save {@dc 16}): {@spell command} and {@spell dominate person}. Once the robes have been used to cast a spell, they can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Eldritch Light
entries: When found, this candle has enough wax to burn for 1 hour. As an action, you can light this candle, causing it to emit dim light in a 15-foot radius. Whenever a creature other than you moves within 15 feet of the candle for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, the creature must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw as shadowy tentacles lash out at it. The creature takes {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more has its speed reduced to 0 feet by the tentacles until the start of its next turn.

No amount of wind can extinguish the candle. The candle can be extinguished by you as an action, by a creature within 5 feet of you that uses its action to make a successful {@dc 15} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, or by the use of a {@spell dispel magic} spell or similar magic. Each time the candle is lit and extinguished, a minimum of 10 minutes' worth of wax is consumed.

entries.name: Eldritch Light
entries: When found, this candle has enough wax to burn for 1 hour. As an action, you can light this candle, causing it to emit dim light in a 15-foot radius. Whenever a creature other than you moves within 15 feet of the candle for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, the creature must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw as shadowy tentacles lash out at it. The creature takes {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more has its speed reduced to 0 feet by the tentacles and is {@condition incapacitated} until the start of its next turn.

No amount of wind can extinguish the candle. The candle can be extinguished by you as an action, by a creature within 5 feet of you that uses its action to make a successful {@dc 20} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, or by the use of a {@spell dispel magic} spell or similar magic. Each time the candle is lit and extinguished, a minimum of 10 minutes' worth of wax is consumed.

entries.name: Lifelink
entries: When a creature willingly drinks from the chalice, its soul becomes linked to the item, causing one of the eyes to burst aflame. Once a second creature willingly drinks from the chalice, both eyes light up and the tentacles writhe and stretch. The two creatures' souls are linked together for the next 7 days.

During this time, if one of the creatures dies, the other immediately knows that this happened and how, in the form of a word or short sentence (GM's discretion). The chalice's eyes then return to a dark, waiting state.
While two souls are linked, drinking from the chalice has no effect. Unlinking the souls early can't be achieved by any means short of a {@spell wish} spell.

Once a creature drinks from the chalice, there is no limit to the interval before another creature must drink from it. Indeed, finding a dead man's chalice with one eye aflame is an intriguingly mysterious dilemma.

entries.name: Lifelink
entries: When a creature willingly drinks from the chalice, its soul becomes linked to the item, causing one of the eyes to burst aflame. Once a second creature willingly drinks from the chalice, both eyes light up and the tentacles writhe and stretch. The two creatures' souls are linked together for the next 7 days. During this time, if either creature dies, the other one does too, and both eyes return to a dark, waiting state.
While two souls are linked, drinking from the chalice has no effect. Unlinking the souls early can't be achieved by any means short of a {@spell wish} spell.

Once a creature drinks from the chalice, there is no limit to the interval before another creature must drink from it. Indeed, finding a dead man's chalice with one eye aflame is an intriguingly mysterious dilemma.

entries.name: Ritual Branding
entries: As an action, you can place a brand on yourself, choosing from the list below and taking {@damage 1d8} fire damage. The brand lasts until the next dawn, or until removed by a {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic. Once you brand yourself, you can't do so again until the next dawn.

items.name: Brand of the Devout
entries: As a bonus action while you hold this weapon, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus (minimum 2). You regain one expended spell slot of a level up to half the number of Hit Dice expended.

items.name: Brand of the Flagellated
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack with this weapon, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus, dealing an additional {@damage 2d6} damage of the attack's type per Hit Die expended.

items.name: Brand of the Zealot
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus. You have {@b advantage} on the next number of attack rolls equal to the number of Hit Dice expended that you make before the start of your next turn.

item.name: Demented Cultist's Scimitar (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls you make with this weapon.

entries.name: Ritual Branding
entries: As an action, you can place a brand on yourself, choosing from the list below and taking {@damage 1d8} fire damage. The brand lasts until the next dawn, or until removed by a {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic. Once you brand yourself, you can't do so again until the next dawn.

items.name: Brand of the Devout
entries: As a bonus action while you hold this weapon, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus (minimum 2). You regain one expended spell slot of a level up to half the number of Hit Dice expended.

items.name: Brand of the Flagellated
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack with this weapon, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus, dealing an additional {@damage 2d6} damage of the attack's type per Hit Die expended.

items.name: Brand of the Zealot
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend a number of Hit Dice up to your proficiency bonus. You have {@b advantage} on the next number of attack rolls equal to the number of Hit Dice expended that you make before the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Forbidden Flower
entries: The skull contains 7 petals. While holding it, you can use an action to pluck a petal to cast the {@spell guiding bolt} spell ({@hit 5} to hit), dealing necrotic damage instead of radiant damage. The skull regrows {@dice 1d3} plucked petals daily at dawn. If you pluck the last petal, the skull crumbles to dust and is destroyed.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This skull is cursed, and plucking three or more petals in a day extends this curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you are unwilling to part with the skull, keeping it within reach at all times, and whenever you finish a long rest, a black petal grows on your head. These black petals can't be removed physically, and any attempt to do so causes you to take {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. If you travel to a different plane while cursed, you grow an additional {@dice 1d4} black petals. When the seventh petal has grown on your head, you die and your body turns to dust, except for your skull, which becomes a new infested cultist's skull. If the curse is removed, the petals on your head and the infested cultist's skull crumble to dust and are destroyed.

entries.name: Forbidden Flower
entries: The skull contains 7 petals. While holding it, you can use an action to pluck a petal to cast the {@spell blight} spell (save {@dc 16}). The skull regrows {@dice 1d3} plucked petals daily at dawn. If you pluck the last petal, the skull crumbles to dust and is destroyed.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This skull is cursed, and plucking three or more petals in a day extends this curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you are unwilling to part with the skull, keeping it within reach at all times, and whenever you finish a long rest, a black petal grows on your head. These black petals can't be removed physically, and any attempt to do so causes you to take {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. If you travel to a different plane while cursed, you grow an additional {@dice 1d4} black petals. When the seventh petal has grown on your head, you die and your body turns to dust, except for your skull, which becomes a new infested cultist's skull. If the curse is removed, the petals on your head and the infested cultist's skull crumble to dust and are destroyed.

item.name: Juvenile Spawn Elixir (Rare)
