entries: When you drink this potion, you become the host of a parasitic scleraworm. The parasite burrows through your flesh, dealing {@damage 3d8} necrotic damage to you. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way. Upon reaching your brain, it erodes its way through your skull, appearing in the centre of your forehead.

entries.name: Mutually Beneficial?
entries: For as long as you host the parasite, you have resistance to psychic damage and can use a bonus action to speak telepathically to creatures you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. You can cast the {@spell detect thoughts} spell at will (save {@dc 15}), taking {@damage 4d8} psychic damage when you do so. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

entries.name: Proliferation
entries: Whenever you finish a long rest, you must succeed on a {@dc 10} Intelligence saving throw or gain an additional eye on your head. You automatically fail if you roll a 1 on the d20. Once you have ten additional eyes, you become completely overwhelmed by the parasite and are no longer in control of your actions.

entries.name: Removal
entries: Over the course of an hour, a creature can perform an operation to remove the parasite from a willing creature. The operating creature must make two {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine} or surgeon's tools) checks. If the creature succeeds on both checks, the scleraworm is removed and can be bottled. If only one is successful, there is no change. If the creature fails both checks, both patient and the scleraworm are killed.

item.name: Juvenile Spawn Elixir (Very Rare)
entries: When you drink this potion, you become the host of a parasitic scleraworm. The parasite burrows through your flesh, dealing {@damage 3d8} necrotic damage to you. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way. Upon reaching your brain, it erodes its way through your skull, appearing in the centre of your forehead.

entries.name: Mutually Beneficial?
entries: For as long as you host the parasite, you have resistance to psychic damage and can use a bonus action to speak telepathically to creatures you can see within {@b 60 feet} of you. You can cast the {@spell detect thoughts} spell at will (save {@dc 16}), taking {@damage 2d8} psychic damage when you do so. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

entries.name: Proliferation
entries: Whenever you finish a long rest, you must succeed on a {@dc 10} Intelligence saving throw or gain an additional eye on your head. You automatically fail if you roll a 1 on the d20. Once you have ten additional eyes, you become completely overwhelmed by the parasite and are no longer in control of your actions.

entries.name: Removal
entries: Over the course of an hour, a creature can perform an operation to remove the parasite from a willing creature. The operating creature must make two {@dc 16} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine} or surgeon's tools) checks. If the creature succeeds on both checks, the scleraworm is removed and can be bottled. If only one is successful, there is no change. If the creature fails both checks, both patient and the scleraworm are killed.

item.name: Runewarding Ring (Uncommon)
entries: This item can hold a maximum of 3 charges and loses all charges daily at dawn. As an action while holding it, you can slice open your hand, taking {@damage 1d4} slashing damage and expending a Hit Die, and smear the fresh blood across the symbol. If you do, the glowing veins flare a bright, molten red, and the ring gains {@dice 1d3} charges. This slashing damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

entries.name: Blood Ward
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and press the ring against a nonmagical surface or object, marking it with a glowing red symbol visible only to you. The next creature other than you that comes within 5 feet of the symbol must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 4d8} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A mark fades when it deals damage, after 8 hours, or if you use the ring to create a second mark.

item.name: Runewarding Ring (Rare)
entries: This item can hold a maximum of 3 charges and loses all charges daily at dawn. As an action while holding it, you can slice open your hand, taking {@damage 1d4} slashing damage and expending a Hit Die, and smear the fresh blood across the symbol. If you do, the glowing veins flare a bright, molten red, and the ring gains {@dice 1d3} charges. This slashing damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

entries.name: Blood Ward
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and press the ring against a nonmagical surface or object, marking it with a glowing red symbol visible only to you. The next creature other than you that comes within 5 feet of the symbol must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 5d10} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A mark fades when it deals damage, after 8 hours, or if you use the ring to create a second mark.

item.name: Runewarding Ring (Very Rare)
entries: This item can hold a maximum of 3 charges and loses all charges daily at dawn. As an action while holding it, you can slice open your hand, taking {@damage 1d4} slashing damage and expending a Hit Die, and smear the fresh blood across the symbol. If you do, the glowing veins flare a bright, molten red, and the ring gains {@dice 1d3} charges. This slashing damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

entries.name: Blood Ward
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and press the ring against a nonmagical surface or object, marking it with a glowing red symbol visible only to you. The next creature other than you that comes within 5 feet of the symbol must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 6d12} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A mark fades when it deals damage, after 8 hours, or if you use the ring to create a second mark.

item.name: Thurible of the Elder Deacon (Uncommon)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The flail has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Gaseous Spells
entries: While holding it, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it (save {@dc 13}): {@spell fog cloud} (1 charge), {@spell misty step} (2 charges), and {@spell stinking cloud} (3 charges). You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend.

item.name: Thurible of the Elder Deacon (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The flail has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Gaseous Spells
entries: While holding it, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it (save {@dc 15}): {@spell fog cloud} (1 charge), {@spell misty step} (2 charges), {@spell stinking cloud} (3 charges), and {@spell cloudkill} (5 charges). You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend.

item.name: Twinspawn Maulhammer (Uncommon)
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can separate this maul into two warhammers, or convert them back to a maul. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with either form of this magic weapon.

entries.name: Duplicate
entries: When you separate the warhammers, or as a bonus action when they're already separated, you can cause one of the warhammers to float in the grasp of an illusory double of yourself for 1 minute. When you create the illusion, and as bonus action on each of your turns, you can move the duplicate and hammer up to 20 feet to a point you can see and make a melee weapon attack against a target within 5 feet of the hammer. Use your Charisma modifier for attack and damage rolls made in this way. The duplicate can't be harmed, though it can be dispelled by a {@spell dispel magic} spell or similar magic. A creature that succeeds on a {@dc 13} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check or that physically interacts with the duplicate perceives that it is illusory and that the weapon is floating.
Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Twinspawn Maulhammer (Rare)
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can separate this maul into two warhammers, or convert them back to a maul. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with either form of this magic weapon.

entries.name: Duplicate
entries: When you separate the warhammers, or as a bonus action when they're already separated, you can cause one of the warhammers to float in the grasp of an illusory double of yourself for 1 minute. When you create the illusion, and as bonus action on each of your turns, you can move the duplicate and hammer up to 20 feet to a point you can see and make a melee weapon attack against a target within 5 feet of the hammer. Use your Charisma modifier for attack and damage rolls made in this way. The duplicate can't be harmed, though it can be dispelled by a {@spell dispel magic} spell or similar magic. A creature that succeeds on a {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check or that physically interacts with the duplicate perceives that it is illusory and that the weapon is floating.
Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Andvari's Gift
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the ring's command word—"plunder"—to form a trail of softly glowing golden dust that leads to the nearest source of gold worth at least 5 gp that isn't currently carried or worn by a creature. The trail extends out 100 feet from you, growing as you follow it. The trail can't pass through solid objects but will navigate through holes as small as 1 inch in diameter. If all paths to the gold source are blocked by solid objects, the trail ends as close to the gold as possible. The trail disappears immediately if the gold source is destroyed, transported to another plane, or picked up by a creature.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This ring is cursed with Andvari's avarice. When you use the Andvari's Gift property, if it's the third time it's been used in the last 24 hours, you become attuned to the ring, extending the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. If you are already attuned to the maximum number of magic items, you must choose one uncursed item to end your attunement to. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the ring, keeping it within reach at all times. In addition, you suffer from an affliction known as dragopathy, which causes you to become uncompromisingly greedy, using all your cunning to keep as much treasure for yourself as possible, and grants you disadvantage on Wisdom checks and Wisdom saving throws.

entries.name: Andvari's Gift
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the ring's command word—"plunder"—to form a trail of softly glowing golden dust that leads to the nearest source of gold worth at least 5 gp that isn't currently carried or worn by a creature, provided the source of gold is within 100 feet of you. The trail can't pass through solid objects but will navigate through holes as small as 1 inch in diameter. If all paths to the gold source are blocked by solid objects, the trail ends as close to the gold as possible. The trail disappears immediately if the gold source is destroyed, transported to another plane, or picked up by a creature.
Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Allfather's Eye (Uncommon)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to your passive Perception score while you wear this magic ring.

entries.name: Wise Beyond Years
entries: This ring has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. Immediately after you make a Wisdom check while wearing this ring, you can expend 1 charge to roll an additional d20, using the higher of the two results.

entries.name: Selective Attunement
entries: If this item was crafted using your own eye, it doesn't require attunement. However, if you ever regain your eye (such as by the {@spell Regenerate} spell), the allfather's eye requires attunement.

item.name: Allfather's Eye (Rare)
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to your passive Perception score while you wear this magic ring.

entries.name: Wise Beyond Years
entries: This ring has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. Immediately after you make a Wisdom check or a Wisdom saving throw while wearing this ring, you can expend 1 charge to roll an additional d20, using the higher of the two results.

entries.name: Selective Attunement
entries: If this item was crafted using your own eye, it doesn't require attunement. However, if you ever regain your eye (such as by the {@spell Regenerate} spell), the allfather's eye requires attunement.

item.name: Allfather's Eye (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +4 bonus to your passive Perception score while you wear this magic ring.
You gain proficiency in the Insight skill. If you are already proficient, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Insight check you make.

entries.name: Wise Beyond Years
entries: This ring has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. Immediately after you make a Wisdom check or a Wisdom saving throw while wearing this ring, you can expend 1 charge to roll an additional d20, using the higher of the two results.

entries.name: Selective Attunement
entries: If this item was crafted using your own eye, it doesn't require attunement. However, if you ever regain your eye (such as by the {@spell Regenerate} spell), the allfather's eye requires attunement.

item.name: Allfather's Own Eye (Legendary)
entries: You gain a +5 bonus to your passive Perception score while you wear this magic ring.

You can't be surprised. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws, and in the Insight and Perception skills. If you are already proficient, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Insight or Perception check you make.

entries.name: Wise Beyond Years
entries: This ring has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. Immediately after you make a Wisdom check or a Wisdom saving throw while wearing this ring, you can expend 1 charge to roll an additional d20, using the higher of the two results.

entries.name: Selective Attunement
entries: If this item was crafted using your own eye, it doesn't require attunement. However, if you ever regain your eye (such as by the {@spell Regenerate} spell), the allfather's eye requires attunement.

item.name: Branch of the Worldtree (Rare)
entries: This wand can be used as a spellcasting focus for your druid or ranger spells and has 7 charges, represented by its seven green-gold leaves. When you expend a charge, a leaf withers and drifts to the ground. Each day at dawn, new leaf buds unfurl and the wand regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the wand withers and petrifies, losing all magical properties.

entries.name: Regrowth
entries: The wand can enhance your healing spells. When you cast a spell that restores hit points and deals no damage while holding the wand, you can expend a number of charges equal to the spell's level to increase that level by 1. You can use this property only once per casting.

entries.name: King of Plants
entries: While holding the wand, you can cast the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip at will. In addition, you can expend charges to cast the {@spell plant growth} (2 charges) and {@spell tree stride} (3 charges) spells.

item.name: Branch of the Worldtree (Very Rare)
entries: This wand can be used as a spellcasting focus for your druid or ranger spells and has 9 charges, represented by its nine green-gold leaves. When you expend a charge, a leaf withers and drifts to the ground. Each day at dawn, new leaf buds unfurl and the wand regains {@dice 1d6 + 3} expended charges. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the wand withers and petrifies, losing all magical properties.

entries.name: Regrowth
entries: The wand can enhance your healing spells. When you cast a spell that restores hit points and deals no damage while holding the wand, you can expend a number of charges equal to the spell's level to increase that level by 1. You can use this property only once per casting.

entries.name: King of Plants
entries: While holding the wand, you can cast the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip at will. In addition, you can expend charges to cast the {@spell plant growth} (2 charges), {@spell tree stride} (3 charges), and {@spell transport via plants} (4 charges) spells.

item.name: Drakenhorn (Uncommon)
entries: This horn has 5 charges, symbolised by the waxed cloth wrapped five times around the horn. For each charge expended, one loop of the cloth magically burns away. Over the course of 10 minutes, you can wrap a new cloth around the horn while singing a ballad of a legendary dragon. As long as no properties of the horn are used, the horn regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges 8 hours later. Whenever a property calls for a cone, the size of the cone equals 10 times the number of charges expended in feet.

entries.name: Embolden
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges as you blow into the horn, creating a loud noise audible from as far away as 300 feet. All creatures in a cone originating from you become immune to the {@condition frightened} condition for the next minute.

entries.name: Charm Drake
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges and blow into the horn, creating a sound that is beguiling to dragons and audible to all creatures within 300 feet. Each dragon in a cone originating from you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you for 1 hour. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw is immune to the horn's Charm Drake property for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Roar
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges as you blow into the horn, creating a loud noise audible from as far away as 300 feet. Each creature in a cone originating from you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effects on itself on a success. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw is immune to the horn's Roar property for the next 24 hours.

item.name: Drakenhorn (Very Rare)
entries: This horn has 5 charges, symbolised by the waxed cloth wrapped five times around the horn. For each charge expended, one loop of the cloth magically burns away. Over the course of 10 minutes, you can wrap a new cloth around the horn while singing a ballad of a legendary dragon. As long as no properties of the horn are used, the horn regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges 8 hours later. Whenever a property calls for a cone, the size of the cone equals 10 times the number of charges expended in feet.

entries.name: Embolden
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges as you blow into the horn, creating a loud noise audible from as far away as 300 feet. All creatures in a cone originating from you become immune to the {@condition frightened} condition and are under the effects of the {@spell bless} spell for the next minute.

entries.name: Charm Drake
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges and blow into the horn, creating a sound that is beguiling to dragons and audible to all creatures within 300 feet. Each dragon in a cone originating from you must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you for 1 hour. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw is immune to the horn's Charm Drake property for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Roar
entries: As an action, you can expend any number of charges as you blow into the horn, creating a loud noise audible from as far away as 300 feet. Each creature in a cone originating from you must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of you and be affected by the {@spell bane} spell for 1 minute. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effects on itself on a success. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw is immune to the horn's Roar property for the next 24 hours.

item.name: Gullinbursti Tusks (Uncommon)
entries: You can attune to two of these daggers and they count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. While wielding two of these daggers, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with them, and you can use the Boar's Charge and Spirit of the Golden Boar properties.

entries.name: Boar's Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet straight towards a creature and then immediately hit it with an attack using this dagger, the target takes an additional {@damage 2d6} piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Spirit of the Golden Boar
entries: You call forth Gullinbursti's residual power. As an action, you can speak the command word, aim your daggers, and a golden boar charges in a 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line from you. Each creature in the line must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Gullinbursti Tusks (Rare)
entries: You can attune to two of these daggers and they count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. While wielding two of these daggers, you gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with them, and you can use the Boar's Charge and Spirit of the Golden Boar properties.

entries.name: Boar's Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet straight towards a creature and then immediately hit it with an attack using this dagger, the target takes an additional {@damage 2d8} piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Spirit of the Golden Boar
entries: You call forth Gullinbursti's residual power. As an action, you can speak the command word, aim your daggers, and a golden boar charges in a 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line from you. Each creature in the line must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Gullinbursti Tusks (Very Rare)
entries: You can attune to two of these daggers and they count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. While wielding two of these daggers, you gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with them, and you can use the Boar's Charge and Spirit of the Golden Boar properties.

entries.name: Boar's Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet straight towards a creature and then immediately hit it with an attack using this dagger, the target takes an additional {@damage 2d10} piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Spirit of the Golden Boar
entries: You call forth Gullinbursti's residual power. As an action, you can speak the command word, aim your daggers, and a golden boar charges in a 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line from you. Each creature in the line must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d6} radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Isfjell
entries: As a reaction to taking damage, or as a bonus action, you can spin the ring's jewel, causing it to expand into a hollow block of clear ice that completely surrounds you. This breaks any grapples in which you are involved and pushes any other creatures in your space out of it. The iceberg occupies your space and creatures can't pass through it, regardless of size. There is enough space within the iceberg to turn around, but you can't move more than 6 inches in any direction. Spells and other magical effects can't extend through the iceberg or be cast through it. While encased in ice, you can't take any reactions, and the only action you can take is a bonus action to cease the jewel's spinning, causing the effect to end.

The iceberg has 100 hit points, which takes as much of any triggering damage as possible. The iceberg can't regain hit points, has immunity to cold, poison, and psychic damage, and has vulnerability to fire damage. Creatures that hit the iceberg with a melee attack take {@damage 2d8} cold damage. The iceberg lasts up to 1 minute, until it is reduced to 0 hit points, or until you use a bonus action to cease the jewel's spinning. When it ends, you can choose to have it explode, launching sharp shards of ice at all creatures within 15 feet of you. Each creature must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d8} cold damage and {@damage 2d8} piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the ring spends 8 continuous hours in snow, ice, or cold water.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to the ring, you gain resistance to cold damage while you wear it.

item.name: Isfjell, Ring of Icebergs (Legendary)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this ring, as a protective layer of rime forms around you.

entries.name: Isfjell
entries: As a reaction to taking damage, or as a bonus action, you can spin the ring's jewel, causing it to expand into a hollow block of clear ice that completely surrounds you. This breaks any grapples in which you are involved and pushes any other creatures in your space out of it. The iceberg occupies your space and creatures can't pass through it, regardless of size. There is enough space within the iceberg to turn around, but you can't move more than 6 inches in any direction. Spells and other magical effects can't extend through the iceberg or be cast through it. While encased in ice, you can't take any reactions, and the only action you can take is a bonus action to cease the jewel's spinning, causing the effect to end.

The iceberg has 150 hit points, which takes as much of any triggering damage as possible. The iceberg can't regain hit points, has immunity to cold, poison, and psychic damage, and has vulnerability to fire damage. Creatures that hit the iceberg with a melee attack take {@damage 2d8} cold damage. The iceberg lasts up to 1 minute, until it is reduced to 0 hit points, or until you use a bonus action to cease the jewel's spinning. When it ends, you can choose to have it explode, launching sharp shards of ice at all creatures within 15 feet of you. Each creature must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d8} cold damage and {@damage 3d8} piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the ring spends 8 continuous hours in snow, ice, or cold water.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to the ring, you gain resistance to cold damage while you wear it.

item.name: Skinwalker Staff (Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a bard, druid, or ranger

entries.name: Spells
entries: This staff can be used as a spellcasting focus for your bard, druid, or ranger spells, has 8 charges, and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. While wielding the staff, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells, using your spell save DC: {@spell animal friendship} (1 charge), {@spell animal messenger} (2 charges), {@spell conjure animals} (3 charges), {@spell polymorph} (self only, 4 charges), or {@spell speak with animals} (1 charge).

If you expend the last charge, the skull on the staff withers and cracks, losing its magic. As part of an 8-hour ritual, the skull can be replaced by that of a beast slain within the last 24 hours. At the next dawn, the staff regains charges as normal.

item.name: Skinwalker Staff (Very Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a bard, druid, or ranger

entries.name: Spells
entries: This staff can be used as a spellcasting focus for your bard, druid, or ranger spells, has 12 charges, and regains {@dice 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. While wielding the staff, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells, using your spell save DC: {@spell animal friendship} (1 charge), {@spell animal messenger} (2 charges), {@spell commune with nature} (5 charges), {@spell conjure animals} (3 charges), {@spell dominate beast} (4 charges), {@spell polymorph} (self only, 4 charges), or {@spell speak with animals} (1 charge).

If you expend the last charge, the skull on the staff withers and cracks, losing its magic. As part of an 8-hour ritual, the skull can be replaced by that of a beast slain within the last 24 hours. At the next dawn, the staff regains charges as normal.

entries.name: Wolf Form
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell polymorph} spell on yourself. When cast in this way, the spell doesn't require concentration, and you can only transform into a dire wolf, ignoring the spell's normal restrictions on CR and creature type, with the following changes:

items: The attack modifier for your attacks is +5, and the DC is 13 for your Bite and Cold Breath actions.

entries: You can end this spell early at any time (no action required). Once this property of the cowl has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Wolf Form
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell polymorph} spell on yourself. When cast in this way, the spell doesn't require concentration, and you can only transform into a winter wolf, ignoring the spell's normal restrictions on CR and creature type, with the following changes:

items: You can speak Common, Giant, and Winter Wolf.
The attack modifier for your attacks is +7, and the DC is 15 for your Bite and Cold Breath actions.

entries: You can end this spell early at any time (no action required). Once this property of the cowl has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Snow Wolf Cowl (Very Rare)
entries: You have resistance to cold damage while you wear the cowl.

entries.name: Wolf Form
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell polymorph} spell on yourself. When cast in this way, the spell doesn't require concentration, and you can only transform into a winter wolf, ignoring the spell's normal restrictions on CR and creature type, with the following changes:

items: You can speak Common, Giant, and Winter Wolf.
Your hit points in wolf form are 100.
The attack modifier for your attacks is +9, and the DC is 16 for your Bite and Cold Breath actions.

entries: You can end this spell early at any time (no action required). Once this property of the cowl has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Spear of the Stormsinger (Uncommon)
entries: When inactive, this weapon appears and behaves as an elegantly adorned metal quarterstaff.

entries.name: Crackling Tip
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak this magic weapon's command word, causing crackling lightning to appear at its tip. The weapon becomes a spear, sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet, and deals lightning damage instead of piercing damage. While activated, whenever you make a ranged attack with this spear, a small lightning bolt shoots forth; you need not throw the weapon itself. You can use a bonus action to cause the lightning to disappear and the weapon once again becomes a quarterstaff.

entries.name: Allfather's Wrath
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell guiding bolt} spell ({@hit 5} to hit), dealing your choice of either lightning or radiant damage. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you spend 1 hour outside singing a storm song in praise of the Allfather (which can be done as part of a short or long rest). Upon completion, a lightning bolt strikes the weapon and Allfather's Wrath can be used again.

item.name: Spear of the Stormsinger (Rare)
entries: When inactive, this weapon appears and behaves as an elegantly adorned metal quarterstaff.

entries.name: Crackling Tip
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak this magic weapon's command word, causing crackling lightning to appear at its tip. The weapon becomes a spear, sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet, and deals lightning damage instead of piercing damage. While activated, whenever you make a ranged attack with this spear, a small lightning bolt shoots forth; you need not throw the weapon itself. You can use a bonus action to cause the lightning to disappear and the weapon once again becomes a quarterstaff.

entries.name: Allfather's Wrath
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell guiding bolt} spell at 5th level ({@hit 7} to hit) or {@spell lightning bolt} (save {@dc 15}). These spells deal your choice of either lightning or radiant damage. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you spend 1 hour outside singing a storm song in praise of the Allfather (which can be done as part of a short or long rest). Upon completion, a lightning bolt strikes the weapon and Allfather's Wrath can be used again.

item.name: Spear of the Stormsinger (Very Rare)
entries: When inactive, this weapon appears and behaves as an elegantly adorned metal quarterstaff.

entries.name: Crackling Tip
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak this magic weapon's command word, causing crackling lightning to appear at its tip. The weapon becomes a spear, sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet, and deals lightning damage instead of piercing damage. While activated, whenever you make a ranged attack with this spear, a small lightning bolt shoots forth; you need not throw the weapon itself. You can use a bonus action to cause the lightning to disappear and the weapon once again becomes a quarterstaff.

entries.name: Allfather's Wrath
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell guiding bolt} spell at 8th level ({@hit 8} to hit) or {@spell lightning bolt} at 5th level (save {@dc 16}). These spells deal your choice of either lightning or radiant damage. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you spend 1 hour outside singing a storm song in praise of the Allfather (which can be done as part of a short or long rest). Upon completion, a lightning bolt strikes the weapon and Allfather's Wrath can be used again.

item.name: Worldshaper (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While this weapon is on your person, you can speak, read, and write Dwarvish. In addition, you have {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet. As an action, you can feel the ground, gaining tremorsense out to a range of 15 feet until the end of your next turn. If you are underground, this distance is doubled to 30 feet.

entries.name: Stone Shaper
entries: While holding the axe, you can cast the following spells (save {@dc 16}): {@spell stone shape}, {@spell stoneskin}, and {@spell wall of stone}. After you cast one of these spells in this way, roll a {@dice d8}. If the result is less than or equal to the spell's level, this property of the axe can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This weapon is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the weapon, keeping it within reach at all times. While cursed, each time you finish a long rest, you must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. A dwarf has disadvantage on this saving throw. On a failure, you slowly begin to petrify and your speed is reduced by 5 feet. When your speed becomes 0 feet due to this curse, you become fully petrified, even if your attunement to this weapon ends. Each time {@spell lesser restoration} is cast on you, your speed increases by 5 feet, up to your normal maximum.

item.name: Worldshaper (Legendary)
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While this weapon is on your person, you can speak, read, and write Dwarvish. In addition, you have {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet. As an action, you can feel the ground, gaining tremorsense out to a range of 15 feet until the end of your next turn. If you are underground, this distance is doubled to 30 feet.

entries.name: Tunneler
entries: While wielding this axe, you gain a burrowing speed of 30 feet. You leave a tunnel big enough for a creature of equal size to walk through without squeezing.

entries.name: Stone Shaper
entries: While holding the axe, you can cast the following spells (save {@dc 17}): {@spell flesh to stone}, {@spell move earth}, {@spell stone shape}, {@spell stoneskin}, and {@spell wall of stone}. After you cast one of these spells in this way, roll a {@dice d8}. If the result is less than or equal to the spell's level, this property of the axe can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This weapon is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the weapon, keeping it within reach at all times. While cursed, each time you finish a long rest, you must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. A dwarf has disadvantage on this saving throw. On a failure, you slowly begin to petrify and your speed is reduced by 5 feet. When your speed becomes 0 feet due to this curse, you become fully petrified, even if your attunement to this weapon ends. Each time {@spell lesser restoration} is cast on you, your speed increases by 5 feet, up to your normal maximum.

item.name: Blast & Slash (Uncommon)
entries: A rudimentary firearm, this item has been modified and mounted with an axe head fashioned from scrap metal. Remnants of oak wood on the handle and the firing mechanism are all that is left from the original firearm. The result is a magical axe-blunderbuss hybrid that can only be used at point blank range due to its poor precision and limited blast power.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Point Blank Blast
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can activate its firing mechanism (no action required). When you do so, the creature takes an extra {@damage 1d6} piercing damage from the attack and must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or suffer severe burns for 1 minute. A burned creature must repeat the saving throw at the start of each of its turns, taking {@damage 1d6} fire damage on a failed save, or ending the effect on a successful one. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you spend 1 hour rearranging the mechanisms, which can be done as part of a short or long rest.

item.name: Blast & Slash (Very Rare)
entries: A rudimentary firearm, this item has been modified and mounted with an axe head fashioned from scrap metal. Remnants of oak wood on the handle and the firing mechanism are all that is left from the original firearm. The result is a magical axe-blunderbuss hybrid that can only be used at point blank range due to its poor precision and limited blast power.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Point Blank Blast
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can activate its firing mechanism (no action required). When you do so, the creature takes an extra {@damage 5d6} piercing damage from the attack and must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or suffer severe burns for 1 minute. A burned creature must repeat the saving throw at the start of each of its turns, taking {@damage 2d6} fire damage on a failed save, or ending the effect on a successful one. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you spend 1 hour rearranging the mechanisms, which can be done as part of a short or long rest.

item.name: Clockwork Wyvern
entries: This limited edition clockwork wyvern comes with a fully operational, miniature coal engine. Thanks to an intricate mix of steam-powered gears and pistons, the construct has lifelike movements and mannerisms. Unlike its non-magical cousins, this particular specimen gains a degree of self-awareness when a creature attunes to it.

entries.name: Combat
entries: In combat, the {@creature clockwork wyvern|LALoTT} shares your initiative count, but takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the Dodge action, unless you take a bonus action on your turn to command it to take an action in its stat block or the Dash, Disengage, Help, Hide, or Search action. The wyvern has 6 charges; see the wyvern's stat block for details on how it can expend and recharge this resource.

entries.name: Death and Damage
entries: If the clockwork wyvern is reduced to 0 hit points, it loses all charges and becomes inert. A wyvern that has 0 hit points regains 1 hit point when it takes lightning damage from a spell of 1st level or higher, or if 2 gp of charcoal is placed in its chest-furnace. The clockwork wyvern regains {@damage 2d6} hit points each time the {@spell mending} cantrip is cast on it. Except for these two means or its Reconstitute action, a wyvern can't regain hit points.

item.name: Heavy Mobile Drill (Uncommon)
entries: A wonder of mining technology, this drill can turn even the most meagre gnome into a walking excavator.
While wearing this item, you can't jump. However, you can use the drill arm to raise you up to 10 feet off of the ground (no action required). You can't use the drill's properties while raised.

entries.name: Weapon
entries: While wearing this item, you can use it as a simple melee weapon that doesn't require the use of your hands. You are proficient with this weapon, which deals {@damage 1d10} piercing damage on a hit. You have {@b advantage} on melee attack rolls made with the drill against creatures made of inorganic material such as stone, crystal, or metal, and it deals double damage to structures.

entries.name: Excavate
entries: As an action, you can choose a portion of nonmagical soil, sand, or rock within 5 feet of you that fits within a 10-foot cube and excavate it, depositing it up to 10 feet away.

item.name: Heavy Mobile Drill (Rare)
entries: A wonder of mining technology, this drill can turn even the most meagre gnome into a walking excavator.
While wearing this item, you can't jump. However, you can use the drill arm to raise you up to 10 feet off of the ground (no action required). You can't use the drill's properties while raised.

entries.name: Weapon
entries: While wearing this item, you can use it as a simple melee weapon that doesn't require the use of your hands. You are proficient with this weapon, which deals {@damage 1d10} piercing damage on a hit. You have {@b advantage} on melee attack rolls made with the drill against creatures made of inorganic material such as stone, crystal, or metal, and it deals double damage to structures.

entries.name: Excavate
entries: As an action, you can choose a portion of nonmagical soil, sand, or rock within 5 feet of you that fits within a 10-foot cube and excavate it, depositing it up to 10 feet away.

entries.name: Drill!
entries: As an action, you can make a melee attack with the drill against a creature within 10 feet of you. On a hit, you deal piercing damage equal to 3d10 + your Strength modifier. If the creature is wearing armour made of an inorganic substance such as stone, crystal, or metal, its armour takes a permanent and cumulative -1 penalty to its AC. The armour is destroyed if the penalty reduces its AC to 10.

entries.name: Power
entries: The crystals that power this device are temperamental, often discharging when too much power is drawn. Whenever you use the Drill! property, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, the crystals go dark and the drill becomes a dead weight; while wearing it, you can't use it as a weapon, use any of its properties, and your walking speed is halved. The crystals must take 10 lightning or radiant damage in order to recharge and resume normal function.

entries.name: Owlsight
entries: While wearing these goggles, you have {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet and attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged weapon attack rolls. In addition, you have {@b advantage} on Perception checks made to spot Small or smaller creatures.

entries.name: Owlsight
entries: While wearing these goggles, you have {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet (if you already have {@sense darkvision}, wearing the goggles increases its range by 60 feet) and attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged weapon attack rolls. In addition, you have {@b advantage} on Perception checks made to spot Small or smaller creatures.

entries.name: Thermal Lock
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose a creature within 30 feet of you and activate the goggles' thermal vision. For 1 hour, you can see that creature based on the amount of heat it emits, with low amounts of heat represented in blue and purple, and high amounts represented in yellow, orange, and red. If the creature emits heat (GM's discretion), you have {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight made to detect it, and you can always see it regardless of light conditions, obscurement, invisibility, or cover, but this vision is blocked by 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. In addition, you can see objects and surfaces that it touched within the last 10 minutes, giving you {@b advantage} on checks made to track its movements.
Most constructs, elementals, plants, and undead don't emit heat. Once this property of the goggles has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Privateer Coat (Uncommon)
entries: Worn to shield airship crewmates against cold and violent winds, this jacket is equipped with pockets and pouches fit for repair tools and weapons. Some coats are adorned with decorative shoulder pads and pins to distinguish rank and aerial prowess.

entries.name: Built for the Skies
entries: While wearing this coat, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to overcome the effects of cold weather or violent winds. You also have {@b advantage} on saving throws against effects that deal cold damage. Finally, you have {@b advantage} on Charisma checks against any creature that perceives itself to be below the rank indicated by the coat. When you find this coat, roll a {@dice d4} to determine its rank:

item.name: Privateer Coat (Rare)
entries: Worn to shield airship crewmates against cold and violent winds, this jacket is equipped with pockets and pouches fit for repair tools and weapons. Some coats are adorned with decorative shoulder pads and pins to distinguish rank and aerial prowess.

entries.name: Built for the Skies
entries: While wearing this coat, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to overcome the effects of cold weather or violent winds. You also have {@b advantage} on saving throws against effects that deal cold damage. Finally, you have {@b advantage} on Charisma checks against any creature that perceives itself to be below the rank indicated by the coat. When you find this coat, roll a {@dice d4} to determine its rank:

entries.name: Repair Adept
entries: While wearing this coat, you can cast the {@spell mending} cantrip at will.

entries.name: Aerial Messaging
entries: While wearing this coat, you can write a message of up to 25 words in the sky, creating one letter out of clouds every 30 seconds. The letters last for 1 hour on a calm day, 10 minutes in a moderate wind (at least 10 miles per hour), or 1 minute in a strong wind (at least 20 miles per hour). Once this property of the coat has been used, you can't use it again until the next dawn, unless you are operating aircraft.

item.name: Scorching Fists (Uncommon)
entries: While wearing these gloves, you can handle white-hot metal without fear of burning yourself. This item has 10 charges. Over the course of 1 minute, you can fill the gloves' fuel canisters with 10 gp of high quality oil, causing it to regain {@dice 1d8 + 2} expended charges. Once this item regains charges, it can't regain charges again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Scorching Fist
entries: While wearing this item, you can expend 1 charge (no action required) to deal an additional {@damage 1d4} fire damage whenever you hit with an unarmed strike with your fist on your turn.

entries.name: Overheat
entries: As an action, you can expend 3 charges to remove the gloves' limiter, causing a flaming blade to blossom from each one. The gloves shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. These blades last for 1 minute or until you extinguish them (no action required). They are simple melee weapons with the Finesse and Light properties, with which you are proficient. Each deals {@damage 3d6} fire damage on a hit.

item.name: Scorching Fists (Rare)
entries: While wearing these gloves, you can handle white-hot metal without fear of burning yourself. This item has 10 charges. Over the course of 1 minute, you can fill the gloves' fuel canisters with 10 gp of high quality oil, causing it to regain {@dice 1d8 + 2} expended charges. Once this item regains charges, it can't regain charges again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Scorching Fist
entries: While wearing this item, you can expend 1 charge (no action required) to deal an additional {@damage 2d4} fire damage whenever you hit with an unarmed strike with your fist on your turn.

entries.name: Overheat
entries: As an action, you can expend 3 charges to remove the gloves' limiter, causing a flaming blade to blossom from each one. The gloves shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. These blades last for 1 minute or until you extinguish them (no action required). They are simple melee weapons with the Finesse and Light properties, with which you are proficient. Each deals {@damage 3d8} fire damage on a hit.

item.name: Scorching Fists (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing these gloves, you can handle white-hot metal without fear of burning yourself. This item has 10 charges. Over the course of 1 minute, you can fill the gloves' fuel canisters with 10 gp of high quality oil, causing it to regain {@dice 1d8 + 2} expended charges. Once this item regains charges, it can't regain charges again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Scorching Fist

The gloves shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. These blades last for 1 minute or until you extinguish them (no action required). They are simple melee weapons with the Finesse and Light properties, with which you are proficient. Each deals {@damage 3d10} fire damage on a hit.

item.name: Sir Wrenchinald the Third (Rare)
entries: Sir Wrenchinald the Third is an eccentric wrench, given sentience after years of being used to create items imbued with magic.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The wrench is contained in a scabbard; unsheathing it reveals a blade and allows it to be used as a dagger instead of a mace. If you aren't attuned to the wrench when you unsheathe the dagger, you take {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage as the wrench closes around your hand.

entries.name: Foul Mouth
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to cast {@spell vicious mockery} (save {@dc 15}). When the spell is cast in this way, Sir Wrenchinald hurls insults at the target.

entries.name: Pun Master
entries: As an action, you can hold out Sir Wrenchinald toward a creature, causing it to spurt out a series of puns as you cast {@spell Tasha's Hideous Laughter|hideous laughter} (save {@dc 15}) on the creature. This property can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: The wrench is a sentient lawful neutral mace with an Intelligence of 18, a Wisdom of 10, and a Charisma of 14. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 120 feet. The wrench communicates by speaking out loud and can speak, read, and understand Binary (a language spoken in the Clockwork Arcadia), Common, and Gnomish.

entries.name: Personality
entries: While Sir Wrenchinald is being wielded, it emits a constant stream of peculiar observations and insults, directed at anyone who would listen. Sir Wrenchinald values intelligence and mechanical skill above all, and may occasionally challenge its wielder with contests of wit in order to test them. Conflict arises if it feels that its wielder doesn't match its intelligence or makes decisions it views as foolish. Although Sir Wrenchinald acts as if all challenges are beneath it, it is easily intrigued by a puzzle or strange problem.

item.name: Sir Wrenchinald the Third (Very Rare)
entries: Sir Wrenchinald the Third is an eccentric wrench, given sentience after years of being used to create items imbued with magic.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While holding this weapon, you have {@b advantage} on Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws. The wrench is contained in a scabbard; unsheathing it reveals a blade and allows it to be used as a dagger instead of a mace. If you aren't attuned to the wrench when you unsheathe the dagger, you take {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage as the wrench closes around your hand.

Conflict arises if it feels that its wielder doesn't match its intelligence or makes decisions it views as foolish. Although Sir Wrenchinald acts as if all challenges are beneath it, it is easily intrigued by a puzzle or strange problem.

item.name: Technomancer Staff (Rare)
entries: A focus for pliers of electrical currents and lightning-based magic, this staff represents the evolution of wizardcraft and artifice. While old-fashioned mages potter about with candles and arcane runes, the new blood embraces technology. This staff contains the latest in magic energy catalysts, allowing it to recycle and redirect residual magical energy.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Whenever you cast a spell that deals lightning damage while holding this quarterstaff, roll a {@dice d6} and add the result to one lightning damage roll of that spell against one of its targets.

entries.name: Lightning Rod
entries: When you or a creature within 20 feet of you takes lightning damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by 1d10 + your proficiency bonus as the staff absorbs the energy.

entries.name: Residual Charge
entries: After you cast a spell that deals lightning damage or use the Lightning Rod property, purple crackling energy gathers at the tip of this quarterstaff. The next time you hit with a melee attack using this weapon within the next minute, you deal an extra {@damage 1d6} lightning damage to the target.

item.name: Technomancer Staff (Very Rare)
entries: A focus for pliers of electrical currents and lightning-based magic, this staff represents the evolution of wizardcraft and artifice. While old-fashioned mages potter about with candles and arcane runes, the new blood embraces technology. This staff contains the latest in magic energy catalysts, allowing it to recycle and redirect residual magical energy.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Whenever you cast a spell that deals lightning damage while holding this quarterstaff, roll a {@dice d8} and add the result to one lightning damage roll of that spell against one of its targets.

entries.name: Lightning Rod
entries: When you or a creature within 20 feet of you takes lightning damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by 2d10 + your proficiency bonus as the staff absorbs the energy.

entries.name: Residual Charge
entries: After you cast a spell that deals lightning damage or use the Lightning Rod property, purple crackling energy gathers at the tip of this quarterstaff. The next time you hit with a melee attack using this weapon within the next minute, you deal an extra {@damage 1d8} lightning damage to the target.

item.name: Technomancer Staff (Legendary)
entries: A focus for pliers of electrical currents and lightning-based magic, this staff represents the evolution of wizardcraft and artifice. While old-fashioned mages potter about with candles and arcane runes, the new blood embraces technology. This staff contains the latest in magic energy catalysts, allowing it to recycle and redirect residual magical energy.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Whenever you cast a spell that deals lightning damage while holding this quarterstaff, roll a {@dice d10} and add the result to one lightning damage roll of that spell against one of its targets.
