entries.name: Residual Charge
entries: After you cast a spell that deals lightning damage or use the Lightning Rod property, purple crackling energy gathers at the tip of this quarterstaff. The next time you hit with a melee attack using this weapon within the next minute, you deal an extra {@damage 1d10} lightning damage to the target.

item.name: Bracers of the Jaguar Warrior (Uncommon)

entries.name: Jaguar Spirit Claw
entries: While wearing these bracers, you can use a bonus action to speak their first command word, causing three radiant claws to appear from the gems. For the next minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your unarmed strikes deal radiant damage instead of bludgeoning damage.
You can add your Wisdom modifier to the damage rolls of your unarmed strikes.

entries.name: Jaguardian
entries: While you wear these bracers, you can speak their second command word to cast the {@spell spirit guardians} spell (save {@dc 13}), calling forth jaguar spirits to protect you.
Once the Jaguar Spirit Claw or Jaguardian property has been used, that property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Bracers of the Jaguar Warrior (Rare)
entries: While wearing these bracers, you have a +1 bonus to your AC.

entries.name: Jaguar Spirit Claw
entries: While wearing these bracers, you can use a bonus action to speak their first command word, causing three radiant claws to appear from the gems. For the next minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your unarmed strikes deal radiant damage instead of bludgeoning damage.
You can add your Wisdom modifier to the damage rolls of your unarmed strikes.

entries.name: Jaguardian
entries: While you wear these bracers, you can speak their second command word to cast the {@spell spirit guardians} spell (save {@dc 15}), calling forth jaguar spirits to protect you.
Once the Jaguar Spirit Claw or Jaguardian property has been used, that property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Bracers of the Jaguar Warrior (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing these bracers, you have a +1 bonus to your AC.

entries.name: Jaguar Spirit Claw
entries: While wearing these bracers, you can use a bonus action to speak their first command word, causing three radiant claws to appear from the gems. For the next minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your unarmed strikes deal radiant damage instead of bludgeoning damage.
You can add your Wisdom modifier to the damage rolls of your unarmed strikes.

entries: The Jaguar Spirit Claw property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Jaguardian
entries: While you wear these bracers, you can speak their second command word to cast the {@spell spirit guardians} spell (save {@dc 16}), calling forth jaguar spirits to protect you.
Once the Jaguardian property has been used, that property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Helmet of the Dino Slayer (Rare)
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Intimidation} checks.

entries.name: Saurian Resilience
entries: When a creature whose size is larger than yours reduces you to 0 hit points with a melee attack while you're wearing this helm, make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 10 or half the damage taken (whichever is higher). On a successful save, you instead drop to 1 hit point. Success or failure, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: One with Nature
entries: You have mastered survival in hostile jungles. While wearing this helm, you can cast the {@spell animal friendship}, {@spell speak with animals}, and {@spell locate animals or plants} spells from it (save {@dc 15}). Once the helmet has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

item.name: Helmet of the Dino Slayer (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Intimidation} checks.

entries.name: Saurian Resilience
entries: When a creature whose size is larger than yours reduces you to 0 hit points with a melee attack while you're wearing this helm, make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 10 or half the damage taken (whichever is higher). On a successful save, you instead drop to 1 hit point. Success or failure, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: One with Nature
entries: You have mastered survival in hostile jungles. While wearing this helm, you can cast the {@spell animal friendship}, {@spell speak with animals}, and {@spell locate animals or plants} spells from it (save {@dc 16}). Once the helmet has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Big Game Hunter
entries: While wearing this helmet, your weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d10} damage to creatures whose size is larger than yours.

item.name: Jade Skull of the Sun King
entries: This item has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Vestigial Radiance
entries: While holding the skull, you can use an action to cast the {@spell sacred flame} and {@spell dancing lights} cantrips, using your spell save DC.

entries.name: Blazing Eyes
entries: As an action, you can brandish the skull in front of you, speak its command word, and expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell scorching ray} spell (3rd-level), which uses your spell attack modifier and deals radiant damage instead of fire damage.

entries.name: Curse
entries: Removing the crown from the jade skull curses the last creature that touched the crown until it's once again placed upon the jade skull. While cursed, the skull's magical properties are suppressed, and the crown appears on the head of the cursed creature and transforms it into an {@creature Accursed Feathered Ape|LALoTT}, multicoloured like a couatl, using the ape's game statistics instead of its own. The ape is unwilling to part with the crown and is hostile towards any creature that it can see within 60 feet. When the curse ends, the creature reverts back to its original form and gains 2 levels of {@condition exhaustion}. This curse can be removed from this item by the {@spell wish} spell or a deal with a couatl.

entries.name: Jungle Dweller
entries: You ignore difficult terrain caused by thick vegetation.

entries.name: Landscaper
entries: You have perfected the art of navigating through dense foliage and vines, and know exactly where to strike with your machete to make quick work of any plant. You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with this weapon against plants and on Tracking checks made to navigate in jungle terrain.

item.name: Jawbone Machete (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Jungle Dweller
entries: You ignore difficult terrain caused by thick vegetation.

entries.name: Landscaper
entries: You have perfected the art of navigating through dense foliage and vines, and know exactly where to strike with your machete to make quick work of any plant. You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with this weapon against plants and on Tracking checks made to navigate in jungle terrain.

item.name: Jawbone Machete (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The scimitar always deals maximum damage when you hit a plant with it.

entries.name: Jungle Dweller
entries: You ignore difficult terrain caused by thick vegetation.

entries.name: Landscaper
entries: You have perfected the art of navigating through dense foliage and vines, and know exactly where to strike with your machete to make quick work of any plant. You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with this weapon against plants and on Tracking checks made to navigate in jungle terrain.

entries.name: Leech
entries: As a bonus action, you can attach the slug to a piece of ammunition or load it as ammunition in a blowgun or sling. The next time you hit with a weapon using the ammunition, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or become numbed as the slug sinks into the target's skin. For each slug attached to a numbed creature, its speed is reduced by 5 feet and it takes {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage at the end of each of its turns. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 13} Strength check, dislodging all leeches currently attached to it or a creature within its reach on a success. The slugs have no effect on incorporeal creatures, constructs, elementals, oozes, or undead. The slugs die after being dislodged from a creature and can't be reused.

entries.name: Leech
entries: As a bonus action, you can attach the slug to a piece of ammunition or load it as ammunition in a blowgun or sling. The next time you hit with a weapon using the ammunition, the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or become numbed as the slug sinks into the target's skin. For each slug attached to a numbed creature, its speed is reduced by 15 feet and it takes {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage at the end of each of its turns. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 16} Strength check, dislodging all leeches currently attached to it or a creature within its reach on a success. The slugs have no effect on incorporeal creatures, constructs, elementals, oozes, or undead. The slugs die after being dislodged from a creature and can't be reused.

item.name: Mace of the Torchbearer (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and as an action while holding it, you can cast the {@spell sacred flame} cantrip (save {@dc 15}).

entries.name: Spread the Light
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the weapon's command word, igniting a magical flame surrounded by small floating debris. It sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. While ablaze, the mace deals an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage to any target it hits. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you are no longer holding the mace.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you. While cursed, you are unwilling to part with the mace, keeping it within reach at all times, and you can hear the Sun God speak to you through the flames. When you move within 30 feet of a source of fire at least 1 foot in diameter for the first time on your turn or start your turn there, you must make a {@dc 10} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you must spend the rest of the turn moving towards the fire by the shortest safe route. You touch the fire when it's within reach, taking {@damage 1d8} fire damage. On a successful saving throw, or after touching the fire, you come to your senses and are immune to the call of this particular fire for the next 24 hours.

item.name: Mace of the Torchbearer (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and as an action while holding it, you can cast the {@spell sacred flame} cantrip (save {@dc 16}).

entries.name: Spread the Light
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the weapon's command word, igniting a magical flame surrounded by small floating debris. It sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. While ablaze, the mace deals an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage to any target it hits. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you are no longer holding the mace.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you. While cursed, you are unwilling to part with the mace, keeping it within reach at all times, and you can hear the Sun God speak to you through the flames. When you move within 30 feet of a source of fire at least 1 foot in diameter for the first time on your turn or start your turn there, you must make a {@dc 10} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you must spend the rest of the turn moving towards the fire by the shortest safe route. You touch the fire when it's within reach, taking {@damage 1d8} fire damage. On a successful saving throw, or after touching the fire, you come to your senses and are immune to the call of this particular fire for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Bark Armour
entries: As an action, you can place the shield on your torso and speak its command word, causing the wood and vines to wrap around your figure. For 1 hour, or until you use an action to end this effect, you are unable to hold a shield, and you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain the benefits of the {@spell barkskin} spell (no concentration required).
You have {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}.

entries: When this effect ends, the overgrown barkshield falls from its place on your torso, fading to a lifeless shade of grey. This property can't be used again until the next dawn, when the shield blooms with life once more.

entries.name: Vines and Thorns
entries: While touching this shield, you can use an action to animate one of the shield's thorny vines and make a melee weapon attack ({@hit 5} to hit) against one target within 30 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} piercing damage, and, if the target is a Large or smaller creature or an object that weighs less than 500 pounds, you can pull it up to 10 feet closer to you.

entries.name: Bark Armour
entries: As an action, you can place the shield on your torso and speak its command word, causing the wood and vines to wrap around your figure. For 1 hour, or until you use an action to end this effect, you are unable to hold a shield, and you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain the benefits of the {@spell barkskin} spell (no concentration required).
You have {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}.

entries: When this effect ends, the overgrown barkshield falls from its place on your torso, fading to a lifeless shade of grey. This property can't be used again until the next dawn, when the shield blooms with life once more.

entries.name: Vines and Thorns
entries: While touching this shield, you can use an action to animate one of the shield's thorny vines and make a melee weapon attack ({@hit 8} to hit) against one target within 30 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 3d8} piercing damage, and, if the target is a Large or smaller creature or an object that weighs less than 500 pounds, you can pull it up to 10 feet closer to you.

entries.name: Bark Armour
entries: As an action, you can place the shield on your torso and speak its command word, causing the wood and vines to wrap around your figure. For 1 hour, or until you use an action to end this effect, you are unable to hold a shield, and you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain the benefits of the {@spell barkskin} spell (no concentration required).
You have {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}.

entries: When this effect ends, the overgrown barkshield falls from its place on your torso, fading to a lifeless shade of grey. This property can't be used again until the next dawn, when the shield blooms with life once more.

entries.name: Vines and Thorns
entries: While touching this shield, you can use an action to animate one of the shield's thorny vines and make a melee weapon attack ({@hit 10} to hit) against one target within 30 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d8} piercing damage, and, if the target is a Large or smaller creature or an object that weighs less than 500 pounds, you can pull it up to 10 feet closer to you.

item.name: Scorching Wrath (Uncommon)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This dagger has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Eruption
entries: As an action, you can thrust the dagger into the ground and expend 1 charge. A 5-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder of lava erupts, centred on a point you can see on the ground within 30 feet of you. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Scorching Wrath (Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This dagger has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Eruption
entries: As an action, you can thrust the dagger into the ground and expend 1 charge. A 5-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder of lava erupts, centred on a point you can see on the ground within 30 feet of you. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Scorching Wrath (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This dagger has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Eruption
entries: As an action, you can thrust the dagger into the ground and expend 1 charge. A 5-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder of lava erupts, centred on a point you can see on the ground within 30 feet of you. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d10} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Lunch Break
entries: As an action, you can put the cloak on a flat, nonmagical surface up to 5 feet wide and 10 feet long. It adapts to the surface and matches its appearance. If a creature uses its action to examine the area the cloak is in, the creature can spot the hidden carnivorous item with a successful {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check. If a creature enters the space occupied by the cloak, it must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d10} piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Afterwards, or if a creature that has spotted the item uses its action to make a {@dc 15} Dexterity check, the cloak shifts and detaches from the surface. You automatically succeed on this check.

entries.name: Take-Out
entries: When a creature enters a space within 10 feet of you, you can use your reaction to have the cloak pounce and wrap around the creature if the cloak is within 10 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check or become {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) by the cloak. A Gargantuan creature automatically succeeds on this check. While grappled in this way, the creature is {@condition restrained} and takes {@damage 2d10} acid damage at the end of each of its turns. When a creature frees itself, the cloak falls to the ground and moves 10 feet closer to you at the start of each of your turns, until it reaches you. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: The cloak must feast on the corpse of at least one Small or larger creature every 24 hours. Failing to do so causes it to slowly digest you during a long rest. When you finish a long rest, you recover one less Hit Die and take {@damage 2d6} acid damage upon waking up. Removing the cloak fails to end the curse and it always seems to find its way back to you by the end of a long rest.

entries.name: Lunch Break
entries: As an action, you can put the cloak on a flat, nonmagical surface up to 5 feet wide and 10 feet long. It adapts to the surface and matches its appearance. If a creature uses its action to examine the area the cloak is in, the creature can spot the hidden carnivorous item with a successful {@dc 16} Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check. If a creature enters the space occupied by the cloak, it must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d10} piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Afterwards, or if a creature that has spotted the item uses its action to make a {@dc 16} Dexterity check, the cloak shifts and detaches from the surface. You automatically succeed on this check.

entries.name: Take-Out
entries: When a creature enters a space within 10 feet of you, you can use your reaction to have the cloak pounce and wrap around the creature if the cloak is within 10 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check or become {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 16}) by the cloak. Medium or smaller creatures have {@b disadvantage} on ability checks to avoid or escape the cloak's grapple. A Gargantuan creature automatically succeeds on this check. While grappled in this way, the creature is {@condition restrained} and takes {@damage 3d10} acid damage at the end of each of its turns. When a creature frees itself, the cloak falls to the ground and moves 10 feet closer to you at the start of each of your turns, until it reaches you. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: The cloak must feast on the corpse of at least one Small or larger creature every 24 hours. Failing to do so causes it to slowly digest you during a long rest. When you finish a long rest, you recover one less Hit Die and take {@damage 2d6} acid damage upon waking up. Removing the cloak fails to end the curse and it always seems to find its way back to you by the end of a long rest.

item.name: Arm'onica (Uncommon)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing an arm
entries: You must have a missing arm to attune to the arm'onica. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing arm and can't be removed against your will. In addition, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells, and if you are a bard, you gain a +1 bonus to your Bardic Inspiration rolls.

entries.name: Dissonant Harmonies
entries: As an action, you can blow into the arm'onica, choosing one of the effects below (save {@dc 13}):

items.name: Harmony'ca
entries: You cast the {@spell calm emotions} spell, which affects creatures in a 30-foot cone originating from you instead of a sphere originating from a point within range.

items.name: Harm'onica
entries: You cast the {@spell thunderwave} spell at 2nd level.

Once this item has been used to cast a spell, roll a {@dice d4}. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, the arm'onica becomes clogged and this property can't be used again until the next dawn, when it magically cleans itself.

item.name: Arm'onica (Very Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing an arm
entries: You must have a missing arm to attune to the arm'onica. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing arm and can't be removed against your will. In addition, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells, and if you are a bard, you gain a +2 bonus to your Bardic Inspiration rolls.

entries.name: Dissonant Harmonies
entries: As an action, you can blow into the arm'onica, choosing one of the effects below (save {@dc 16}):

items.name: Harmony'ca
entries: You cast the {@spell dominate person} spell.

items.name: Harm'onica
entries: You cast the {@spell thunderwave} spell at 5th level.

Once this item has been used to cast a spell, roll a {@dice d10}. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, the arm'onica becomes clogged and this property can't be used again until the next dawn, when it magically cleans itself.

item.name: Eye of the Planesrunner (Uncommon)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing an eye
entries: You must have a missing eye to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing eye, you can see through it as if it were a normal eye, and it can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Planar Magic
entries: As an action, you can channel the eye's planar energy to cast the {@spell blink} spell (save {@dc 16}).

Once this item has been used to cast a spell, roll a {@dice d6}. If the result is equal to or lower than the level of the spell, the eye's energy floods into your brain; you take psychic damage equal to twice the number rolled and this property of the eye can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Eye of the Planesrunner (Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing an eye
entries: You must have a missing eye to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing eye, you can see through it as if it were a normal eye, and it can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Planar Magic
entries: As an action, you can channel the eye's planar energy to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 15}): {@spell banishment}, {@spell blink}, or {@spell contact GV|DMGer plane}.

Once this item has been used to cast a spell, roll a {@dice d8}. If the result is equal to or lower than the level of the spell, the eye's energy floods into your brain; you take psychic damage equal to twice the number rolled and this property of the eye can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Eye of the Planesrunner (Very Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing an eye
entries: You must have a missing eye to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing eye, you can see through it as if it were a normal eye, and it can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Planar Magic
entries: As an action, you can channel the eye's planar energy to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 16}): {@spell banishment}, {@spell blink}, {@spell contact GV|DMGer plane}, {@spell planar ally}, or {@spell plane shift}.

Once this item has been used to cast a spell, roll a {@dice d12}. If the result is equal to or lower than the level of the spell, the eye's energy floods into your brain; you take psychic damage equal to twice the number rolled and this property of the eye can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Flesh Symbiont, Hand (Uncommon)
item.reqAttune: by a spellcaster missing a hand
entries: You must have a missing hand to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing hand and functions as a normal hand. The results aren't always perfect; roll {@dice 1d6 + 1} to determine the number of digits your new hand has. The first digit is always a thumb, and a mouth forms in the hand's palm.

entries.name: Cost
entries: The symbiont feeds off of you, syphoning your magical energy. Each time you finish a long rest, you must make a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you lose one spell slot of the lowest level you can cast.

entries.name: Mockery
entries: You can use the hand to cast the {@spell vicious mockery} and {@spell words of discouragement|LALoTT} spells at will, using your spell save DC. If a creature succeeds on its saving throw against a {@spell words of discouragement|LALoTT} spell cast in this way, the mouth blurts out insults and mockeries at you, causing you to become timorous for 1 minute, as per the spell. As a bonus action while timorous, you can punish your insolent hand, taking {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage, restoring your sense of self-worth, and ending this effect.

entries.name: Symbiotic Nature
entries: The symbiont can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the symbiont ends and it falls off, reverting to a dormant lump of flesh.

item.name: Flesh Symbiont, Hand (Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a spellcaster missing a hand
entries: You must have a missing hand to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing hand and functions as a normal hand. The results aren't always perfect; roll {@dice 1d6 + 1} to determine the number of digits your new hand has. The first digit is always a thumb, and a mouth forms in the hand's palm.

entries.name: Cost
entries: The symbiont feeds off of you, syphoning your magical energy. Each time you finish a long rest, you must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you lose one spell slot of the lowest level you can cast.

entries.name: Mockery
entries: You can use the hand to cast the {@spell vicious mockery} and {@spell words of discouragement|LALoTT} spells at will, using your spell save DC. If a creature succeeds on its saving throw against a {@spell words of discouragement|LALoTT} spell cast in this way, the mouth blurts out insults and mockeries at you, causing you to become timorous for 1 minute, as per the spell. As a bonus action while timorous, you can punish your insolent hand, taking {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage, restoring your sense of self-worth, and ending this effect.

entries.name: Symbiotic Nature
entries: The symbiont can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the symbiont ends and it falls off, reverting to a dormant lump of flesh.

entries.name: Silver Tongue
entries: The tongue in the mouth on your hand becomes silvered. You can use the hand to cast the {@spell suggestion} spell, using your spell save DC. Once this property of the hand has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1-2, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Revive
entries: As an action, you can press this heart to the chest of a humanoid corpse within reach. The heart burrows into the corpse's chest, consuming any existing heart. Over the course of 1 hour, it grows to resemble the original heart, casting the {@spell raise dead} spell on the creature.

entries.name: Curse
entries: Pressing the heart into a creature forcibly attunes the creature to the heart and prevents it from being removed by any means. If the creature is already attuned to the maximum number of items, it loses its attunement to one at random. While attuned to the heart, the creature is cursed and has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks when it, the target of its attack, or whatever it's trying to perceive is in direct sunlight. In addition, it gains resistance to necrotic damage and can use the Symbiotic Vigor property. If a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic is cast on the heart, or a creature ends its attunement to it, it rots and is destroyed, killing its host.

entries.name: Symbiotic Vigor (Requires Attunement)
entries: The heart has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} charges daily at dawn. These charges manifest as lidless eyes appearing on random locations across your body. While the heart is inside you, you can use your reaction when you take damage to expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell false life} spell at 2nd level, gaining the temporary hit points immediately before you take the triggering damage. You can't use this property of the heart while in direct sunlight. Whenever you expend a charge, one of the eyes closes.

entries.name: Revive
entries: As an action, you can press this heart to the chest of a humanoid corpse within reach. The heart burrows into the corpse's chest, consuming any existing heart. Over the course of 1 hour, it grows to resemble the original heart, casting the {@spell resurrection} spell on the creature, but does not restore the missing heart that it replaces.

entries.name: Curse
entries: Pressing the heart into a creature forcibly attunes the creature to the heart and prevents it from being removed by any means. If the creature is already attuned to the maximum number of items, it loses its attunement to one at random. While attuned to the heart, the creature is cursed and has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks when it, the target of its attack, or whatever it's trying to perceive is in direct sunlight. In addition, it gains resistance to necrotic damage and can use the Symbiotic Vigor property. If a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic is cast on the heart, or a creature ends its attunement to it, it rots and is destroyed, killing its host.

entries.name: Symbiotic Vigor (Requires Attunement)
entries: The heart has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} charges daily at dawn. These charges manifest as lidless eyes appearing on random locations across your body. While the heart is inside you, you can use your reaction when you take damage to expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell false life} spell at 3rd level, gaining the temporary hit points immediately before you take the triggering damage. You can't use this property of the heart while in direct sunlight. Whenever you expend a charge, one of the eyes closes.

item.name: Mechanical Tail (Uncommon)
entries: When you attune to the mechanical tail, it attaches to your lower back and can't be removed against your will. You can use the tail to make grapples and have {@b advantage} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to grapple creatures of your size or smaller with it.

entries.name: Steam-Powered Strike
entries: As an action, you can sweep your tail with wicked, steam-powered ferocity. Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d8} slashing damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful one, it takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the tail has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, the tail runs out of steam and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Mechanical Tail (Rare)
entries: When you attune to the mechanical tail, it attaches to your lower back and can't be removed against your will. You can use the tail to make grapples and have {@b advantage} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to grapple creatures of your size or smaller with it.

entries.name: Steam-Powered Strike
entries: As an action, you can sweep your tail with wicked, steam-powered ferocity. Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 3d8} slashing damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful one, it takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the tail has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, the tail runs out of steam and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Mechanical Tail (Very Rare)
entries: When you attune to the mechanical tail, it attaches to your lower back and can't be removed against your will. You can use the tail to make grapples and have {@b advantage} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to grapple creatures using the tail.

entries.name: Steam-Powered Strike
entries: As an action, you can sweep your tail with wicked, steam-powered ferocity. Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d8} slashing damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful one, it takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the tail has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, the tail runs out of steam and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Mechanical Tail (Legendary)
entries: When you attune to the mechanical tail, it attaches to your lower back and can't be removed against your will. You can use the tail to grapple creatures two sizes larger than you or smaller and have {@b advantage} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to do so.

entries.name: Steam-Powered Strike
entries: As an action, you can sweep your tail with wicked, steam-powered ferocity. Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d10} slashing damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful one, it takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. Once this property of the tail has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, the tail runs out of steam and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Ornate Ceramic Prosthesis (Uncommon)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing a limb
entries: You must have a missing limb to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it reshapes itself to take the place of your missing limb, functions as a normal limb, and can't be removed against your will.

When you make an unarmed strike using this prosthesis, its plating morphs into a sharp, ceramic blade. Unarmed strikes made with the prosthesis count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage and deal slashing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Full Plating
entries: As an action, you can speak the prosthesis' command word to activate the ceramic plating. It grows to cover your entire body, giving you the appearance of an articulated porcelain doll for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain a +2 bonus to AC. Additionally, you have vulnerability to bludgeoning damage and resistance to slashing damage. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Ornate Ceramic Prosthesis (Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing a limb
entries: You must have a missing limb to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it reshapes itself to take the place of your missing limb, functions as a normal limb, and can't be removed against your will.

When you make an unarmed strike using this prosthesis, its plating morphs into a sharp, ceramic blade. Unarmed strikes made with the prosthesis count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage and deal slashing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Full Plating
entries: As an action, you can speak the prosthesis' command word to activate the ceramic plating. It grows to cover your entire body, giving you the appearance of an articulated porcelain doll for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain a +2 bonus to AC. Additionally, you have vulnerability to bludgeoning damage and resistance to fire and slashing damage. This property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Phantom Limb (Uncommon)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing a limb
entries: You must have a missing limb to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing limb, functions as a normal limb, and can't be removed against your will. As a bonus action, you can cause the phantom limb to disappear, or manifest again. If you have multiple phantom limbs, they count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items to which you can attune.

entries.name: Mage Limb
entries: As an action, you can cause the limb to detach from your body and manipulate it like the hand produced by the {@spell mage hand} spell. The limb vanishes when you dismiss it (no action required) and reappears attached to you once more.

entries.name: Limber
entries: The reach of unarmed strikes made with the phantom limb and the reach of attacks made with weapons that have the {@itemProperty L|Light property} held by the phantom limb increases by 5 feet.

item.name: Phantom Limb (Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing a limb
entries: You must have a missing limb to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it takes the place of your missing limb, functions as a normal limb, and can't be removed against your will. As a bonus action, you can cause the phantom limb to disappear, or manifest again. If you have multiple phantom limbs, they count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items to which you can attune.

entries.name: Mage Limb
entries: As an action, you can cause the limb to detach from your body and manipulate it like the hand produced by the {@spell mage hand} spell. The limb vanishes when you dismiss it (no action required) and reappears attached to you once more.

entries.name: Limber
entries: The reach of unarmed strikes made with the phantom limb and the reach of attacks made with weapons that have the {@itemProperty L|Light property} property held by the phantom limb increases by 5 feet.
Unarmed strikes made with the phantom limb and attacks made with weapons held by the phantom limb deal an extra {@damage 1d6} psychic damage to the first target they hit each turn.

item.name: Spiritual Armblade (Uncommon)
item.reqAttune: by a cleric, druid, monk, paladin, ranger, or warlock missing a hand
entries: You must have a missing hand to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it wraps itself around the site of your lost hand and can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Summon Armblade
entries: As a bonus action, you can summon a simple melee weapon made of spiritual energy from the prosthesis, or cause it to disappear. You are proficient with this weapon and use your spellcasting ability for the attack rolls made with this weapon (or your Wisdom modifier if you are a monk). The weapon deals {@damage 2d8} damage on a hit. Its appearance and damage type are linked to your alignment, as detailed below:

items: {@b Good Alignment:} A golden blade that deals radiant damage.
{@b Neutral Alignment:} A translucent-white weapon that deals force damage.
{@b Evil Alignment:} A shaft of bruised mauve that deals necrotic damage.

entries.name: Shield
entries: When a creature you can see attacks you while the weapon is summoned, you can use your reaction to shift it into a shield, gaining a +1 bonus to AC against the triggering attack. The shield reverts to a weapon immediately after.

item.name: Spiritual Armblade (Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a cleric, druid, monk, paladin, ranger, or warlock missing a hand
entries: You must have a missing hand to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it wraps itself around the site of your lost hand and can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Summon Armblade
entries: As a bonus action, you can summon a simple melee weapon made of spiritual energy from the prosthesis, or cause it to disappear. You are proficient with this weapon and use your spellcasting ability for the attack rolls made with this weapon (or your Wisdom modifier if you are a monk). The weapon deals {@damage 2d8} damage on a hit. Its appearance and damage type are linked to your alignment, as detailed below:

items: {@b Good Alignment:} A golden blade that deals radiant damage.
{@b Neutral Alignment:} A translucent-white weapon that deals force damage.
{@b Evil Alignment:} A shaft of bruised mauve that deals necrotic damage.

entries.name: Shield
entries: When a creature you can see attacks you while the weapon is summoned, you can use your reaction to shift it into a shield, gaining a +2 bonus to AC against the triggering attack. The shield reverts to a weapon immediately after.

item.name: Spiritual Armblade (Very Rare)
item.reqAttune: by a cleric, druid, monk, paladin, ranger, or warlock missing a hand
entries: You must have a missing hand to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it wraps itself around the site of your lost hand and can't be removed against your will.

You are proficient with this weapon and use your spellcasting ability for the attack rolls made with this weapon (or your Wisdom modifier if you are a monk). The weapon deals {@damage 2d10} damage on a hit. Its appearance and damage type are linked to your alignment, as detailed below:

items: {@b Good Alignment:} A golden blade that deals radiant damage.
{@b Neutral Alignment:} A translucent-white weapon that deals force damage.
{@b Evil Alignment:} A shaft of bruised mauve that deals necrotic damage.

entries.name: Shield
entries: When a creature you can see attacks you while the weapon is summoned, you can use your reaction to shift it into a shield, gaining a +2 bonus to AC against the triggering attack. The shield reverts to a weapon immediately after.

item.name: Spiritual Armblade (Legendary)
item.reqAttune: by a cleric, druid, monk, paladin, ranger, or warlock missing a hand
entries: You must have a missing hand to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, it wraps itself around the site of your lost hand and can't be removed against your will.

entries.name: Summon Armblade
entries: As a bonus action, you can summon a simple melee weapon made of spiritual energy from the prosthesis, or cause it to disappear. You are proficient with this weapon and use your spellcasting ability for the attack rolls made with this weapon (or your Wisdom modifier if you are a monk). The weapon deals {@damage 2d10} damage on a hit. You can add your spellcasting ability modifier (or your Wisdom modifier if you are a monk) to the damage rolls of attacks made with the weapon. Its appearance and damage type are linked to your alignment, as detailed below:

items: {@b Good Alignment:} A golden blade that deals radiant damage.
{@b Neutral Alignment:} A translucent-white weapon that deals force damage.
{@b Evil Alignment:} A shaft of bruised mauve that deals necrotic damage.

entries.name: Shield
entries: When a creature you can see attacks you while the weapon is summoned, you can use your reaction to shift it into a shield, gaining a +2 bonus to AC against the triggering attack. The shield reverts to a weapon immediately after.

item.name: Tail of the Dolphin
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing its legs
entries: You must be missing your legs to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, the tail grafts into place, the scales slowly petering out into your normal skin, and can't be removed against your will. You gain the shapechanger type and can breathe bGV|DMG air and water.

entries.name: Morph
entries: As an action, you can morph your tail into legs or vice-versa, gaining the benefits indicated below.

items.name: Legs
entries: You have a walking speed of 20 feet. You can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.

items.name: Tail
entries: You have a swimming speed of 60 feet and {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid being grappled or restrained while underwater. On land, you are always prone, can't stand up, and have a walking speed of 5 feet.

entries.name: Dolphin: Heads or Tails
entries: As a bonus action, you can target one creature you can see within 60 feet of you and unleash the power of the chimera. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be partially-polymorphed for 1 minute. Roll a {@dice d6} to determine the effect. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: On an even result, the creature's torso and head become fishified: it loses the ability to speak and can breathe only water. Furthermore, its Intelligence score is reduced to 1, and it can't cast spells or activate magic items, though it can still recognise its friends, follow them, and even protect them. The creature gains 10 temporary hit points. When these hit points are lost, or if the creature falls unconscious, the effect ends and it returns to normal.
On an odd result, the creature's legs become fishified, as described in the Tail option of the Morph property.

item.name: Tail of the Clownfish
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing its legs
entries: You must be missing your legs to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, the tail grafts into place, the scales slowly petering out into your normal skin, and can't be removed against your will. You gain the shapechanger type and can breathe bGV|DMG air and water.

entries.name: Morph
entries: As an action, you can morph your tail into legs or vice-versa, gaining the benefits indicated below.

items.name: Legs
entries: You have a walking speed of 20 feet. You can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.

items.name: Tail
entries: You have a swimming speed of 60 feet and {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid being grappled or restrained while underwater. On land, you are always prone, can't stand up, and have a walking speed of 5 feet.

entries.name: Clownfish: Clowning Around
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell Tasha's Hideous Laughter|hideous laughter} spell (save {@dc 15}). This property can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Tail of the Shark
item.reqAttune: by a creature missing its legs
entries: You must be missing your legs to attune to this item. Once attuned to it, the tail grafts into place, the scales slowly petering out into your normal skin, and can't be removed against your will. You gain the shapechanger type and can breathe bGV|DMG air and water.

entries.name: Morph
entries: As an action, you can morph your tail into legs or vice-versa, gaining the benefits indicated below.

items.name: Legs
entries: You have a walking speed of 20 feet. You can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.

items.name: Tail
entries: You have a swimming speed of 60 feet and {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid being grappled or restrained while underwater. On land, you are always prone, can't stand up, and have a walking speed of 5 feet.

entries.name: Shark: Blood Fury
entries: As a bonus action, you can activate your blood fury. For 1 minute, when you make a weapon attack against a creature, you have {@b advantage} on your attack roll if the creature has fewer than half of its maximum hit points. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until the next dawn.

item.name: Wings of Prey (Uncommon)
entries: When you attune to the wings, they attach to your back and can't be removed against your will. As a bonus action, you can speak the wings' command word to cause them to disappear or reappear. Additionally, the wings have 3 charges and regain {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. While the wings are present, you have a flying speed equal to 5 times the number of remaining charges in feet.

entries.name: Razor Sharp
entries: As an action while the wings are present, you can expend 2 charges to hurl a flurry of razor-sharp feathers in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Wings of Prey (Rare)
entries: When you attune to the wings, they attach to your back and can't be removed against your will. As a bonus action, you can speak the wings' command word to cause them to disappear or reappear. Additionally, the wings have 6 charges and regain {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. While the wings are present, you have a flying speed equal to 5 times the number of remaining charges in feet.

entries.name: Razor Sharp
entries: As an action while the wings are present, you can expend 2 charges to hurl a flurry of razor-sharp feathers in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d8} slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Wings of Prey (Very Rare)
entries: When you attune to the wings, they attach to your back and can't be removed against your will. As a bonus action, you can speak the wings' command word to cause them to disappear or reappear. Additionally, the wings have 6 charges and regain {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. While the wings are present, you have a flying speed equal to 5 times the number of remaining charges in feet.

entries.name: Razor Sharp
entries: As an action while the wings are present, you can expend 1 charge to hurl a flurry of razor-sharp feathers in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d8} slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Assemble the Mob!
entries: When you open this satchel as an action, you can designate up to four willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of you to be 'multiplied' for 1 hour. Each affected creature sprouts nine illusory duplicates that all have minor alterations: different clothes, hair, and heights. The duplicates surround and follow the original and seem completely real, including sounds, smells, and temperature appropriate to the creature depicted. So long as you and any multiplied creatures remain within 100 feet of the satchel, these duplicates muddle about, shouting vaguely angry phrases such as "Get them!", "Begone!", and "Why is existence vacuous and happiness illusory!?" If a duplicated creature is ever more than 100 feet away from a satchel, its duplicates vanish.

A creature that uses its action to examine the mob can determine that it is an illusion with a successful {@dc 13} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check, ascertaining which members of the mob are real creatures.

If a creature targets a member of the mob with an attack, roll a {@dice d10}. On a 10, it hits a real creature within the mob (GM's choice). On a 1-9, it hits one of the duplicates, who doesn't react, causing the creature to gain {@b advantage} on all Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks to see through the illusion, and it can immediately make one as part of its attack. In addition, when one of the illusory duplicates is hit by an attack, all illusory duplicates disappear. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Assemble the Mob!
entries: When you open this satchel as an action, you can designate up to four willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of you to be 'multiplied' for 1 hour. Each affected creature sprouts nine illusory duplicates that all have minor alterations: different clothes, hair, and heights. The duplicates surround and follow the original and seem completely real, including sounds, smells, and temperature appropriate to the creature depicted. So long as you and any multiplied creatures remain within 100 feet of the satchel, these duplicates muddle about, shouting vaguely angry phrases such as "Get them!", "Begone!", and "Why is existence vacuous and happiness illusory!?" If a duplicated creature is ever more than 100 feet away from a satchel, its duplicates vanish.

A creature that uses its action to examine the mob can determine that it is an illusion with a successful {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check, ascertaining which members of the mob are real creatures.

If a creature targets a member of the mob with an attack, roll a {@dice d10}. On a 10, it hits a real creature within the mob (GM's choice). On a 1-9, it hits one of the duplicates, who doesn't react, causing the creature to gain {@b advantage} on all Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks to see through the illusion, and it can immediately make one as part of its attack. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Inner Monster
entries: Tied up in a protective claw is a philter of bubbling red liquid. You can use an action to drink this potion, filling you with a sense of rage and selfishness as your muscles inflate and your veins bulge. You turn into a brute, the physical and mental manifestation of your most evil and selfish thoughts, for 10 minutes, and undergo the following changes:

items: Your size increases by one category and your weight is multiplied by eight.
You have {@b advantage} on Strength-based attack rolls, Strength checks, and Strength saving throws.
Your alignment changes to chaotic evil; you think of your own survival first and any trace of compassion or mercy is shredded.

entries: When the effect ends, you return to normal and make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of {@condition exhaustion}.

entries.name: Inner Monster
entries: Tied up in a protective claw is a philter of bubbling red liquid. You can use an action to drink this potion, filling you with a sense of rage and selfishness as your muscles inflate and your veins bulge. You turn into a brute, the physical and mental manifestation of your most evil and selfish thoughts, for 10 minutes, and undergo the following changes:

items: Your size increases by one category and your weight is multiplied by eight.
You have {@b advantage} on Strength-based attack rolls, Strength checks, and Strength saving throws.
Your alignment changes to chaotic evil; you think of your own survival first and any trace of compassion or mercy is shredded.
You gain {@dice 2d6} temporary hit points at the beginning of each of your turns.
Once for the duration of the serum, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you are instead reduced to 1 hit point.

entries: When the effect ends, you return to normal and make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of {@condition exhaustion}.

entries.name: Animate Bulb
entries: As a bonus action while holding this item, you can speak its command word. In response, this inanimate bulb of garlic becomes a creature that uses the {@creature garlycanthrope|LALoTT} stat block, sprouting little legs, eyes, and a panting muzzle. It is extremely friendly to you and your allies, but is skittish if any of them are undead or shapechangers. In combat, the garlycanthrope shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the Dodge action, unless you take a bonus action on your turn to command it to take one of the actions in its stat block or the Dash, Disengage, Help, Hide, or Search action.

After 1 hour, or if the garlycanthrope is reduced to 0 hit points, it becomes an inanimate bulb of garlic once more. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn. Be careful not to eat it in the meantime!

item.name: Gentleman's Umbershield (Uncommon)
entries: This wand has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Reflect
entries: When you are hit by an attack that you can see while holding the wand, you can use your reaction to open the umbrella and roll a {@dice d6}. You expend charges equal to the result of the roll, or however many remain in the wand (whichever is lower), and gain a bonus to AC equal to the number of charges expended until the start of your next turn. If this results in the triggering attack missing you, the attack bounces back at the creature that made it using the original attack roll, potentially causing the attacker to hit itself.

item.name: Gentleman's Umbershield (Rare)
entries: This wand has 12 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 6} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you have {@b advantage} on initiative rolls.

entries.name: Reflect
entries: When you are hit by an attack that you can see while holding the wand, you can use your reaction to open the umbrella and roll a {@dice d8}. You expend charges equal to the result of the roll, or however many remain in the wand (whichever is lower), and gain a bonus to AC equal to the number of charges expended until the start of your next turn. If this results in the triggering attack missing you, the attack bounces back at the creature that made it using the original attack roll, potentially causing the attacker to hit itself.

item.name: Gentleman's Umbershield (Very Rare)
entries: This wand has 20 charges and regains {@dice 1d10 + 10} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you have {@b advantage} on initiative rolls.

entries.name: Reflect
entries: When you are hit by an attack that you can see while holding the wand, you can use your reaction to open the umbrella and roll a {@dice d10}. You expend charges equal to the result of the roll, or however many remain in the wand (whichever is lower), and gain a bonus to AC equal to the number of charges expended until the start of your next turn. If this results in the triggering attack missing you, the attack bounces back at the creature that made it using the original attack roll, potentially causing the attacker to hit itself.

item.name: Graveshredder (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which can also act as a lute. If you are proficient with a stringed instrument, you are proficient with this battleaxe, can use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells, and can use the Shredder property. The battleaxe has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shredder
entries: While holding this axe, you can expend charges in the following ways:

items: As an action, you can sweep-pick a sick lick, expending 2 charges to cast the {@spell speak with dead} spell. Whenever you ask questions, the 'question and answer' takes the form of a 'call and response' duet between you the guitarist, and the corpse as a singer.

Over the course of 1 minute, you can rip a face shredding solo, expending 2 charges to cast the {@spell animate dead} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, you command creatures created by it by playing a killer riff.
As an action, you can fingerpick a mournful instrumental ballad, expending 4 charges to cast the {@spell revivify} spell. Casting the spell in this way requires its material components.

entries: When you expend 1 or more charges, you must make a Dexterity or Charisma (stringed instrument) check (your choice). The DC for this check equals 10 + 1 for each charge expended. On a success, the spell is cast as normal. On a failure, the spellcasting action, any material components, and the charges are wasted.

item.name: Graveshredder (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which can also act as a lute. If you are proficient with a stringed instrument, you are proficient with this battleaxe, can use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells, and can use the Shredder property. The battleaxe has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shredder
entries: While holding this axe, you can expend charges in the following ways:

items: As an action, you can sweep-pick a sick lick, expending 2 charges to cast the {@spell speak with dead} spell. Whenever you ask questions, the 'question and answer' takes the form of a 'call and response' duet between you the guitarist, and the corpse as a singer.

Over the course of 1 minute, you can rip a face shredding solo, expending 2 charges to cast the {@spell animate dead} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, you command creatures created by it by playing a killer riff.
As an action, you can fingerpick a mournful instrumental ballad, expending 4 charges to cast the {@spell revivify} spell. Casting the spell in this way requires its material components.

entries: When you expend 1 or more charges, you must make a Dexterity or Charisma (stringed instrument) check (your choice). The DC for this check equals 10 + 1 for each charge expended. On a success, the spell is cast as normal. On a failure, the spellcasting action, any material components, and the charges are wasted.

entries.name: Grave's Embrace
entries: Trailing from your back, ragged scraps of cloth and bandage float in an ethereal wind, even on the calmest of days. As an action, you can speak the cloak's first command word, causing shreds of it to pull away and begin entangling a Large or smaller creature you can see within 10 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained} for 10 minutes. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way takes {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns as it desiccates. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 13} Strength check, breaking the restraints and freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success.

As a bonus action, you can speak a second command word, releasing the target and ending the effects as the scraps of cloth fall lifelessly to the ground. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn, at which point the cloak regrows.

entries.name: Grave's Embrace
entries: Trailing from your back, ragged scraps of cloth and bandage float in an ethereal wind, even on the calmest of days. As an action, you can speak the cloak's first command word, causing shreds of it to pull away and begin entangling a Large or smaller creature you can see within 10 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained} for 10 minutes. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way takes {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns as it desiccates and has vulnerability to fire damage. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check, breaking the restraints and freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success.

As a bonus action, you can speak a second command word, releasing the target and ending the effects as the scraps of cloth fall lifelessly to the ground. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn, at which point the cloak regrows.

A creature {@condition restrained} in this way takes {@damage 3d6} necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns as it desiccates and has vulnerability to fire damage. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 16} Strength check, breaking the restraints and freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success.

As a bonus action, you can speak a second command word, releasing the target and ending the effects as the scraps of cloth fall lifelessly to the ground. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn, at which point the cloak regrows.

The target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained} for 10 minutes. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way takes {@damage 4d6} necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns as it desiccates and has vulnerability to fire damage. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 17} Strength check, breaking the restraints and freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success.

As a bonus action, you can speak a second command word, releasing the target and ending the effects as the scraps of cloth fall lifelessly to the ground. The Grave's Embrace property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn, at which point the cloak regrows.

item.name: Arcanomagnetic Rings (Rare)
entries: This pair of rings forms an arcanomagnet. One, a brilliant gold that reflects light in wondrous prismatic hues, is intensely magical, while the other, a dull grey that grows darker as the surrounding light brightens, is its antimagical opposite. By creating pockets of magical and antimagical energy, you can bend the Weave, causing magic to veer off its intended trajectory.
These rings can only be attuned to as a pair and count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to.

entries.name: Spellbend
entries: Whenever a creature or point you can see within 30 feet of you is targeted by a ranged spell attack, an attack using a magic ranged weapon or magic ammunition, or a spell with an area of effect centred on a point, you can use your reaction to redirect it.

You cause a ranged attack to target a different target of your choice within 10 feet of the original (after the attack roll is made but before the outcome is determined). Alternatively, you move the area of effect's point of origin up to 10 feet. After you use this reaction, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, the rings become demagnetized and can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Arcanomagnetic Rings (Very Rare)
entries: This pair of rings forms an arcanomagnet. One, a brilliant gold that reflects light in wondrous prismatic hues, is intensely magical, while the other, a dull grey that grows darker as the surrounding light brightens, is its antimagical opposite. By creating pockets of magical and antimagical energy, you can bend the Weave, causing magic to veer off its intended trajectory.
These rings can only be attuned to as a pair and count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to.

entries.name: Spellbend
entries: Whenever a creature or point you can see within 60 feet of you is targeted by a ranged spell attack, an attack using a magic ranged weapon or magic ammunition, or a spell with an area of effect centred on a point, you can use your reaction to redirect it.

You cause a ranged attack to target a different target of your choice within 20 feet of the original (after the attack roll is made but before the outcome is determined). Alternatively, you move the area of effect's point of origin up to 20 feet. After you use this reaction, roll a {@dice d10}. On a 1-2, the rings become demagnetized and can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Leaden Manacles (Uncommon)
entries: The stink of burnt flesh and vapours of boiling sweat waft from the wrists of the shackled spellcaster. As you watch, the creature mutters an incantation and the lead begins to glow once more. The creature lets out a pained wail, its concentration broken, its wrists seared.

entries.name: Arcane Forbiddance
entries: You can use an action to place these manacles on an {@condition incapacitated} creature. The shackles adjust to fit a creature of Small to Large size. Leaden manacles behave as normal manacles with the following addition: A creature that attempts to cast a spell while wearing the manacles must make a {@dc 13} ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell's magic is cancelled; the action to cast the spell is wasted but the creature doesn't expend a spell slot or a use of the spell. The creature also takes {@damage 1d4} fire damage plus an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage per level of the spell on a failed check, or half as much damage on a success.

item.name: Leaden Manacles (Very Rare)
entries: The stink of burnt flesh and vapours of boiling sweat waft from the wrists of the shackled spellcaster. As you watch, the creature mutters an incantation and the lead begins to glow once more. The creature lets out a pained wail, its concentration broken, its wrists seared.

entries.name: Arcane Forbiddance
entries: You can use an action to place these manacles on an {@condition incapacitated} creature. The shackles adjust to fit a creature of Small to Large size. Leaden manacles behave as normal manacles with the following addition: A creature that attempts to cast a spell while wearing the manacles must make a {@dc 16} ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell's magic is cancelled; the action to cast the spell is wasted but the creature doesn't expend a spell slot or a use of the spell. The creature also takes {@damage 1d4} fire damage plus an extra {@damage 2d4} fire damage per level of the spell on a failed check, or half as much damage on a success. If the creature fails the ability check, it's also {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn and expends the spell slot or a use of the spell.

entries.name: Suppression Serum
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can inject some of the dagger's fluid into the creature. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be unable to concentrate on spells until the end of its next turn. In addition, if it tries to cast a spell before the end of its next turn, it must make a {@dc 13} ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, it wastes its action but doesn't expend a spell slot or use of the spell. This property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Lockshiv (Rare)
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d8} poison damage to creatures capable of casting spells.

entries.name: Suppression Serum
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can inject some of the dagger's fluid into the creature. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be unable to concentrate on spells until the end of its next turn. In addition, if it tries to cast a spell before the end of its next turn, it must make a {@dc 15} ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, it wastes its action but doesn't expend a spell slot or use of the spell. This property can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Deflect Spell
entries: When you are hit by a spell attack or subjected to a spell or magical effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to reduce or avoid the damage taken while holding this shield, you can use your reaction to deflect some of the spell's energy. When you do so, the damage you take from the attack or effect is reduced by {@dice 2d10}.

If you reduce the damage to 0, the spell has no effect on you, and you can attempt to reflect the spell's raw energy at its origin. Make a ranged attack roll ({@hit 5} to hit) against the target, dealing force damage equal to the result of your {@dice 2d10} roll on a hit. Once this property of the shield has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Magehunter Bulwark (Rare)
entries: While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

entries.name: Deflect Spell
entries: When you are hit by a spell attack or subjected to a spell or magical effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to reduce or avoid the damage taken while holding this shield, you can use your reaction to deflect some of the spell's energy. When you do so, the damage you take from the attack or effect is reduced by {@dice 2d10}.

If you reduce the damage to 0, the spell has no effect on you, and you can attempt to reflect the spell's raw energy at its origin. Make a ranged attack roll ({@hit 7} to hit) against the target, dealing force damage equal to the result of your {@dice 2d10} roll on a hit. Once this property of the shield has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Magehunter Bulwark (Very Rare)
entries: While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

If you reduce the damage to 0, the spell has no effect on you, and you can attempt to reflect the spell's raw energy at its origin. Make a ranged attack roll ({@hit 8} to hit) against the target, dealing force damage equal to the result of your {@dice 4d10} roll on a hit. Once this property of the shield has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Magehunter Bulwark (Legendary)
entries: While holding this shield, you have a +2 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.
