If you reduce the damage to 0, the spell has no effect on you, and you can attempt to reflect the spell's raw energy at its origin. Make a ranged attack roll ({@hit 10} to hit) against the target, dealing force damage equal to the result of your {@dice 4d10} roll on a hit. Once this property of the shield has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Applying the Gag
entries: As an action, you can attempt to gag a creature you are grappling. Make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check contested by the target's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. On a success, you get the gag in place and the leather straps magically fasten.

entries.name: The Gagged
entries: The gagged creature can't verbalise in any way, which includes speaking, casting spells that require a verbal component, or even simply humming and grunting.

entries.name: Removing the Gag
entries: You and any creature you designate when you use the gag can use an action to remove it. The gagged creature or a creature within reach of it can use its action to make a {@dc 13} Strength or {@dc 10} Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check, removing the gag on a success. The {@spell knock} spell can also unfasten the leather straps, causing the gag to fall off.

entries.name: Applying the Gag
entries: As an action, you can attempt to gag a creature you are grappling. Make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check contested by the target's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. On a success, you get the gag in place and the leather straps magically fasten.

entries.name: The Gagged
entries: The gagged creature can't verbalise in any way, which includes speaking, casting spells that require a verbal component, or even simply humming and grunting. In addition, after the creature casts a spell, the gag heats up and the creature takes {@damage 1d4} fire damage plus an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage per level of the spell.

entries.name: Removing the Gag
entries: You and any creature you designate when you use the gag can use an action to remove it. The gagged creature or a creature within reach of it can use its action to make a {@dc 20} Strength or {@dc 15} Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check, removing the gag on a success. The {@spell knock} spell can also unfasten the leather straps, causing the gag to fall off.

entries.name: Pandora's Box
entries: As an action while holding this box, you can place it down and unlock it, causing it to slowly and eerily creep open. At the end of your turn, black mist spews forth in a 20-foot-radius sphere centred on the box, soon followed by writhing, ethereal purple tentacles that flail and thrash wildly.

Each creature that starts its turn in the area or moves into it for the first time on its turn must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw. If the creature is under the effect of a spell or concentrating on one, it has {@b disadvantage} on the saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 3d6} psychic damage, it loses concentration if it is concentrating on a spell, and any magical effect or spell of 3rd level or lower that is affecting it is dispelled. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and no additional effects occur.

As an action, a creature within reach of the box can attempt a {@dc 13} Strength check, closing it on a success. Otherwise, after 1 minute, these abyssal creatures satiate themselves, retreat back into the box, the effect ends, and the box can be closed normally. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Pandora's Box
entries: As an action while holding this box, you can place it down and unlock it, causing it to slowly and eerily creep open. At the end of your turn, black mist spews forth in a 20-foot-radius sphere centred on the box, soon followed by writhing, ethereal purple tentacles that flail and thrash wildly.

Each creature that starts its turn in the area or moves into it for the first time on its turn must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. If the creature is under the effect of a spell or concentrating on one, it has {@b disadvantage} on the saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 4d8} psychic damage, it loses concentration if it is concentrating on a spell, and any magical effect or spell of 5th level or lower that is affecting it is dispelled. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and no additional effects occur.

As an action, a creature within reach of the box can attempt a {@dc 15} Strength check, closing it on a success. Otherwise, after 1 minute, these abyssal creatures satiate themselves, retreat back into the box, the effect ends, and the box can be closed normally. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Coralborn Ring (Uncommon)
entries: This coralborn-crafted trinket is often gifted to visiting dignitaries to protect them during journeys through the abrasive subterranean coral tunnels.

entries.name: Coralskin
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to speak its command word, casting the {@spell coralskin|LALoTT} spell on yourself (+3 spellcasting ability modifier). Once this property of the ring has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Coralborn Ring (Rare)
entries: This coralborn-crafted trinket is often gifted to visiting dignitaries to protect them during journeys through the abrasive subterranean coral tunnels.

entries.name: Coralskin
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to speak its command word, casting the {@spell coralskin|LALoTT} spell on yourself (+5 spellcasting ability modifier). This property can be used three times and regains all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Coral Wand (Uncommon)
entries: Such wands often take months to fashion, requiring the formation of an entire coral ecosystem. From molluscs to cnidarians and seaweeds, the inhabitants of such an item share one common theme—they all carry a deadly toxicity.
While holding this wand, you can cast the {@spell poison spray} cantrip (save {@dc 13}).

entries.name: Toxic Discharge
entries: When you cast {@spell poison spray} using the wand, you can incite one of the organisms inside it to give the spell an extra boost. Roll a {@dice d4} and consult the table below to determine what additional effect the target suffers on a failed save:

rows: {@b Lingering Toxicity.} The creature is {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.
{@b Ocular Irritant.} The creature is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.
{@b Total Discharge.} The cantrip deals an extra {@damage 2d12} poison damage. The wand wilts and the Toxic Discharge property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Coral Wand (Rare)
entries: Such wands often take months to fashion, requiring the formation of an entire coral ecosystem. From molluscs to cnidarians and seaweeds, the inhabitants of such an item share one common theme—they all carry a deadly toxicity.
While holding this wand, you can cast the {@spell poison spray} cantrip (save {@dc 15}). The cantrip deals {@damage 2d12} poison damage on a failed save.

entries.name: Toxic Discharge
entries: When you cast {@spell poison spray} using the wand, you can incite one of the organisms inside it to give the spell an extra boost. Roll a {@dice d6} and consult the table below to determine what additional effect the target suffers on a failed save:

rows: {@b Lingering Toxicity.} The creature is {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.
{@b Ocular Irritant.} The creature is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.
{@b Neurotoxin.} The creature is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn.
{@b Total Discharge.} The cantrip deals an extra {@damage 3d12} poison damage. The wand wilts and the Toxic Discharge property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hag's Magic
entries: While wearing this hat, you can use it to cast the following spells (save {@dc 13}): {@spell disguise self} and {@spell suggestion}. When you use the hat to cast a spell, roll a {@dice d3}. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, the hat can't be used to cast any spells until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hermit
entries: As a bonus action while wearing the hat, you can hide inside of it, causing it to expand in order to accommodate you. Until you emerge, you gain three-quarters cover from attacks and effects originating from outside the hat, your speed can't exceed 5 feet, you are {@condition blinded}, you have {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks, and you automatically fail Dexterity saving throws. You can use your action to emerge from the hat. If you are knocked {@condition prone} while within the hat, your underbelly is exposed, and you no longer have three-quarters cover.

entries.name: Hag's Magic
entries: While wearing this hat, you can use it to cast the following spells (save {@dc 15}): {@spell bends|LALoTT}, {@spell disguise self}, and {@spell suggestion}. When you use the hat to cast a spell, roll a {@dice d6}. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, the hat can't be used to cast any spells until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hermit
entries: As a bonus action while wearing the hat, you can hide inside of it, causing it to expand in order to accommodate you. Until you emerge, you gain three-quarters cover from attacks and effects originating from outside the hat, your speed can't exceed 5 feet, you are {@condition blinded}, you have {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks, and you automatically fail Dexterity saving throws. You can use your action to emerge from the hat. If you are knocked {@condition prone} while within the hat, your underbelly is exposed, and you no longer have three-quarters cover.

When you use the hat to cast a spell, roll a {@dice d8}. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, the hat can't be used to cast any spells until the next dawn.

item.name: Ghost Cutlass (Uncommon)
entries: This item appears to be a round, rusted hilt. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause an ethereal blade to spring into existence, or make the blade disappear. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals psychic damage instead of slashing damage.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

entries.name: Find Matey
entries: As an action, you can uncork and upend the bottle, casting the {@spell find familiar} spell. When you cast the spell in this way, the familiar takes the statistics of the {@creature ship mimic offspring|LALoTT} and can attack. If the mimic is killed, a new mimic appears 8 hours after you fill the bottle with water and salt.

item.name: Shrimp Pistol (Uncommon)
entries: This pistol fires its ammunition at such tremendous velocities that cavitation bubbles form, dealing an extra {@damage 1d6} thunder damage to the first target it hits on each of your turns.

entries.name: Cavitation Blast
entries: As an action while you are underwater and holding this weapon, you can release a series of pressure waves from the pistol, causing pockets of cavitation. Each creature in a 15-foot cone must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 4d8} thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property of the pistol has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Misfire
entries: If you roll a 1 on your attack roll with this weapon, the ammunition itself disintegrates, clogging the barrel. The attack automatically misses and you must spend an action to unclog this weapon before it can be used again.

item.name: Sunken Anchor (Uncommon)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Anchor
entries: As an action, you can point this weapon at a point on a surface within 60 feet of you, causing an ethereal chain to tether you to that point for the next 10 minutes, or until you dismiss it as a bonus action. For the duration, you can't be willingly moved away from that point, except by a weight of 8,000 pounds or more, or if a creature trying to move you succeeds on a {@dc 25} Strength check, which causes the chain to snap and the effect to end. The effect also ends early if you ever willingly move more than 60 feet from the point or if you fall unconscious.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

Additionally, while holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell riptide|LALoTT} spell (save {@dc 16}) from it. Once the weapon has been used to cast this spell, it can't be used to cast it again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dinglehopper
entries: As an action, you can command this weapon to magically shrink down to the size of a common oyster fork, known as a dinglehopper, or to return to its normal size.

entries.name: Discharge
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack using this weapon, you can speak its command word as a bonus action, causing it to emit a burst of lightning. On a hit, the target and each creature within 5 feet of it (or within 10 feet of it while underwater), including you, must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d6} lightning damage.

entries.name: Lightning
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell lightning bolt} spell (save {@dc 13}). Once you do so, the Discharge and Lightning properties can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell lightning bolt} spell (save {@dc 15}) at 5th level. Once you do so, the Discharge and Lightning properties can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Lightning
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell chain lightning} spell (save {@dc 16}). Once you do so, the Discharge and Lightning properties can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Urchin Nature
entries: This magic weapon deals piercing damage instead of bludgeoning damage. You can use it to make a ranged weapon attack (range 20/60) called a spikeshot, which uses your Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls and deals {@damage 1d6} piercing damage.

entries.name: Spikey Death Balls
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can fire all the flail's spikes at a point you can see within 60 feet of you, where they blossom into a field of squirming, darting, baby urchins. On land, you cover a square up to 15 feet on a side with these spike balls. Underwater, you fill a cube up to 15 feet on a side. When a creature enters this area for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, it must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. A creature moving through the area at half speed makes this saving throw with {@b advantage}. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d4} piercing damage and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until it takes an action to remove the urchins. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced. Once this property of the weapon has been used, the weapon loses its Urchin Nature and Spikey Death Balls properties until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

item.name: Urchin Flail (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Urchin Nature
entries: This magic weapon deals piercing damage instead of bludgeoning damage. You can use it to make a ranged weapon attack (range 20/60) called a spikeshot, which uses your Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls and deals {@damage 1d6} piercing damage.

entries.name: Spikey Death Balls
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can fire all the flail's spikes at a point you can see within 60 feet of you, where they blossom into a field of squirming, darting, baby urchins. On land, you cover a square up to 20 feet on a side with these spike balls. Underwater, you fill a cube up to 20 feet on a side. When a creature enters this area for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, it must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. A creature moving through the area at half speed makes this saving throw with {@b advantage}. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} piercing damage and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until it takes an action to remove the urchins. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced. Once this property of the weapon has been used, the weapon loses its Urchin Nature and Spikey Death Balls properties until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

item.name: Urchin Flail (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Urchin Nature
entries: This magic weapon deals piercing damage instead of bludgeoning damage. You can use it to make a ranged weapon attack (range 20/60) called a spikeshot, which uses your Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls and deals {@damage 1d6} piercing damage.

entries.name: Spikey Death Balls
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can fire all the flail's spikes at a point you can see within 60 feet of you, where they blossom into a field of squirming, darting, baby urchins. On land, you cover a square up to 25 feet on a side with these spike balls. Underwater, you fill a cube up to 25 feet on a side. When a creature enters this area for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, it must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw. A creature moving through the area at half speed makes this saving throw with {@b advantage}. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d8} piercing damage and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until it takes an action to remove the urchins. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced. Once this property of the weapon has been used, the weapon loses its Urchin Nature and Spikey Death Balls properties until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

item.name: Allure (Uncommon)
entries: While wearing this circlet, you can cast the {@spell dancing lights} cantrip.

entries.name: Lure
entries: While wearing this circlet, you can use your action to briefly create an alluring pattern of glowing lights around you. Each creature within 10 feet of you who sees the pattern must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes {@condition charmed} until the start of your next turn. While {@condition charmed} in this way, the creature is {@condition incapacitated} and must use all its movement to approach you. If a creature ends its movement within 5 feet of you, it reaches towards your circlet. As a reaction, you can cause the circlet to bite down on the creature, dealing {@damage 1d6} piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} poison damage to it. Once this property of the circlet has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Allure (Rare)
entries: While wearing this circlet, you can cast the {@spell dancing lights} cantrip. You can choose any number of the lights to shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

entries.name: Lure
entries: While wearing this circlet, you can use your action to briefly create an alluring pattern of glowing lights around you. Each creature within 15 feet of you who sees the pattern must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes {@condition charmed} until the start of your next turn. While {@condition charmed} in this way, the creature is {@condition incapacitated} and must use all its movement to approach you. If a creature ends its movement within 5 feet of you, it reaches towards your circlet. As a reaction, you can cause the circlet to bite down on the creature, dealing {@damage 1d6} piercing damage and {@damage 2d6} poison damage to it. The Lure property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Allure (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing this circlet, you can cast the {@spell dancing lights} cantrip. When you cast {@spell dancing lights}, the spell doesn't require concentration but ends if you cast it again, and you can choose any number of the lights to shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

entries.name: Lure
entries: While wearing this circlet, you can use your action to briefly create an alluring pattern of glowing lights around you. Each creature within 20 feet of you who sees the pattern must make a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes {@condition charmed} until the start of your next turn. While {@condition charmed} in this way, the creature is {@condition incapacitated} and must use all its movement to approach you. If a creature ends its movement within 5 feet of you, it reaches towards your circlet. As a reaction, you can cause the circlet to bite down on the creature, dealing {@damage 1d6} piercing damage and {@damage 4d6} poison damage to it. The Lure property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Diurnal Dream
entries: As an action during the day, you can play one of the following tunes, targeting one creature that can hear you within 30 feet of you, which must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or suffer the tune's effects as described below. The effect ends at the start of your next turn, unless you immediately use your action that turn to continue playing the tune, which extends the duration until the end of your next turn. You can play a tune uninterrupted for a maximum of 1 minute. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Charming Melody
entries: The creature is {@condition charmed}. The effect ends early if you or any of your allies harm the creature. When the effect ends, the creature knows it was {@condition charmed} by you.

items.name: Gentle Lullaby
entries: The creature falls asleep and becomes {@condition unconscious}. The effect ends early if the creature takes damage or if someone uses an action to shake the creature awake.

items.name: Mesmerising Canon
entries: The creature is {@condition incapacitated}, using all its movement to follow you. If it is unable to follow you, or if doing so would harm it, it remains motionless until the effect ends. The effect ends early if the creature takes damage or is ever more than 30 feet from you.

entries.name: Nocturnal Nightmare
entries: As an action during the night, you strike a dissonant chord that scrapes at the hearing organs and cochlear nerves of all creatures in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} psychic damage and is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition frightened}. After this property has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1-2, all the small feeding medusozoa retreat back into the coral and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Diurnal Dream
entries: As an action during the day, you can play one of the following tunes, targeting one creature that can hear you within 30 feet of you, which must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or suffer the tune's effects as described below. The effect ends at the start of your next turn, unless you immediately use your action that turn to continue playing the tune, which extends the duration until the end of your next turn. You can play a tune uninterrupted for a maximum of 1 minute. The Diurnal Dream property can be used more than once a day, but once it's been used to play a tune, it can't be used to play that tune again until the next dawn.

items.name: Charming Melody
entries: The creature is {@condition charmed}. The effect ends early if you or any of your allies harm the creature. When the effect ends, the creature knows it was {@condition charmed} by you.

items.name: Gentle Lullaby
entries: The creature falls asleep and becomes {@condition unconscious}. The effect ends early if the creature takes damage or if someone uses an action to shake the creature awake.

items.name: Mesmerising Canon
entries: The creature is {@condition incapacitated}, using all its movement to follow you. If it is unable to follow you, or if doing so would harm it, it remains motionless until the effect ends. The effect ends early if the creature takes damage or is ever more than 30 feet from you.

entries.name: Nocturnal Nightmare
entries: As an action during the night, you strike a dissonant chord that scrapes at the hearing organs and cochlear nerves of all creatures in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} psychic damage and is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition frightened}. After this property has been used, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 1-2, all the small feeding medusozoa retreat back into the coral and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Prismatic Shimmer
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause it to shimmer in a pleasing and distracting array of colours for 10 minutes. For the duration, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Deception}, {@skill Performance}, and {@skill Persuasion} checks. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Nematocyst Fibres
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause its fibres to become aggressive for 1 minute. The cloak shimmers in shades of red and mauve, giving you {@b advantage} on {@skill Intimidation} checks against creatures that can see you. As part of the action to activate the fibres, and as an action on subsequent turns, you can cause the cloak's filaments to brush against a creature within 10 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the start of your next turn. Until this poison ends, the target is {@condition incapacitated}. After a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to the effects of the cloak for the next 24 hours. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of the Cnidaran (Rare)
entries: While wearing the cloak, you have a swimming speed of 30 feet.

entries.name: Prismatic Shimmer
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause it to shimmer in a pleasing and distracting array of colours for 10 minutes. For the duration, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Deception}, {@skill Performance}, and {@skill Persuasion} checks. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Nematocyst Fibres
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause its fibres to become aggressive for 1 minute. The cloak shimmers in shades of red and mauve, giving you {@b advantage} on {@skill Intimidation} checks against creatures that can see you. As part of the action to activate the fibres, and as an action on subsequent turns, you can cause the cloak's filaments to brush against a creature within 10 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the start of your next turn. Until this poison ends, the poisoned creature is {@condition stunned} instead of {@condition incapacitated}. After a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to the effects of the cloak for the next 24 hours. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of the Cnidaran (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing the cloak, you have a swimming speed of 60 feet and can breathe underwater.

entries.name: Prismatic Shimmer
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause it to shimmer in a pleasing and distracting array of colours for 10 minutes. For the duration, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Deception}, {@skill Performance}, and {@skill Persuasion} checks. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Nematocyst Fibres
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause its fibres to become aggressive for 1 minute. The cloak shimmers in shades of red and mauve, giving you {@b advantage} on {@skill Intimidation} checks against creatures that can see you. As part of the action to activate the fibres, and as an action on subsequent turns, you can cause the cloak's filaments to brush against a creature within 10 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the start of your next turn. Until this poison ends, the poisoned creature is {@condition paralysed} instead of {@condition incapacitated}. After a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to the effects of the cloak for the next 24 hours. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Shark Senses
entries: While wearing this helmet, your peripheral vision extends behind you and your sense of smell is enhanced, granting you {@b advantage} on initiative rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that involve smell. In addition, you can inhale deeply as an action, gaining blindsight to a range of 30 feet until the start of your next turn. This blindsight can only detect a creature that has blood and is below its hit point maximum.

entries.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: While wearing this helmet, you can cast the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell on yourself. Once the Blood Frenzy property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This helmet is cursed with the essence of its former occupant, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to remove the helmet. Over the course of a week, you begin to develop shark-like features.

First, your skin develops small patches of tough, iridescent scales. Next, your fingers and toes become webbed, and small fins appear on your elbow and neck. Lastly are the teeth. They become a jagged conveyor belt of razors, a natural weapon which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with it, you deal piercing damage equal to {@damage 1d6} + your Strength modifier. Once you grow these shark teeth, the curse is complete and you are constantly under the effects of the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell.

A {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic ends the curse and the effects of the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell, but doesn't undo any changes to your appearance that have already occurred, including the natural weapon.

entries.name: Hammerhead Knight's Legacy
entries: While attuned to the helmet of the hammerhead knight and {@item sharktooth|LALoTT}, you gain {@b advantage} on Dexterity saving throws against effects you can see or smell, and you can't be surprised. In addition, you can use your action to grow gills and morph your legs into a tail, or to reverse this transformation. While morphed, you gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, your walking speed can't exceed 5 feet, and you can breathe both air and water.

entries.name: Shark Senses
entries: While wearing this helmet, your peripheral vision extends behind you and your sense of smell is enhanced, granting you {@b advantage} on initiative rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that involve smell. In addition, you can inhale deeply as an action, gaining blindsight to a range of 30 feet until the start of your next turn. This blindsight can only detect a creature that has blood and is below its hit point maximum.

entries.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: While wearing this helmet, you can cast the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell on yourself. When you do, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 3 or lower, this property of the helm can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This helmet is cursed with the essence of its former occupant, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to remove the helmet. Over the course of a week, you begin to develop shark-like features.

First, your skin develops small patches of tough, iridescent scales. Next, your fingers and toes become webbed, and small fins appear on your elbow and neck. Lastly are the teeth. They become a jagged conveyor belt of razors, a natural weapon which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with it, you deal piercing damage equal to {@damage 1d6} + your Strength modifier. Once you grow these shark teeth, the curse is complete and you are constantly under the effects of the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell.

A {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic ends the curse and the effects of the {@spell blood frenzy|LALoTT} spell, but doesn't undo any changes to your appearance that have already occurred, including the natural weapon.

entries.name: Hammerhead Knight's Legacy
entries: While attuned to the helmet of the hammerhead knight and {@item sharktooth|LALoTT}, you gain {@b advantage} on Dexterity saving throws against effects you can see or smell, and you can't be surprised. In addition, you can use your action to grow gills and morph your legs into a tail, or to reverse this transformation. While morphed, you gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, your walking speed can't exceed 5 feet, and you can breathe both air and water.

item.name: Flumfpot (Uncommon)
entries: A creature can use its action to pour a potion into an empty flumfpot within its reach.

entries.name: Mastery and Movement
entries: After a creature touches the flumfpot with its lips, it becomes the flumfpot's master. The flumfpot follows its master around, hovering just over their shoulder. It has a flying speed of 30 feet and a swimming speed of 60 feet. If it is within 5 feet of its master when its master teleports, it teleports with them. Otherwise, it remains in place until touched by another creature's lips. A flumfpot that is prevented from moving will fly towards its master via the most direct route when released.

entries.name: Send Aid
entries: As a bonus action, you can command the flumfpot to move up to its speed towards a willing or {@condition unconscious} creature you can see and feed its contents to it.

item.name: Flumfpot (Rare)
entries: A creature can use its action to pour a potion into an empty flumfpot within its reach.

entries.name: Mastery and Movement
entries: After a creature touches the flumfpot with its lips, it becomes the flumfpot's master. The flumfpot follows its master around, hovering just over their shoulder. It has a flying speed of 30 feet and a swimming speed of 60 feet. If it is within 5 feet of its master when its master teleports, it teleports with them. Otherwise, it remains in place until touched by another creature's lips. A flumfpot that is prevented from moving will fly towards its master via the most direct route when released.

entries.name: Send Aid
entries: As a bonus action, you can command the flumfpot to move up to its speed towards a willing or {@condition unconscious} creature you can see and feed its contents to it.

entries.name: Filling Flumfpot
entries: An empty flumfpot magically refills itself with a random potion each day at dawn. Roll a {@dice d20} and consult the following table to determine the potion:

item.name: Tidecutter (Uncommon)
entries: This item appears to be a sword hilt stylized with tentacles. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of rushing water to spring forth, or make it disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the Finesse property. If you are proficient with shortswords or longswords, you are proficient with the tidecutter.

entries.name: Water Lash
entries: As an action, you can cause the sword's blade to elongate into a lash and make a melee weapon attack using it against a target within 20 feet of you. On a hit, if the target is a Medium or smaller creature or an object that weighs 500 pounds or less, you can pull it up to 15 feet towards you. If the target is a fixed object or a Huge or larger creature, you can pull yourself up to 15 feet towards the target.

entries.name: Waterborne
entries: When submerged in water, this longsword gains the Reach property, and the range of the Water Lash property increases to 30 feet.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

item.name: Tidecutter (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

This item appears to be a sword hilt stylized with tentacles. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of rushing water to spring forth, or make it disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the Finesse property. If you are proficient with shortswords or longswords, you are proficient with the tidecutter.

entries.name: Water Lash
entries: As an action, you can cause the sword's blade to elongate into a lash and make a melee weapon attack using it against a target within 20 feet of you. On a hit, if the target is a Large or smaller creature or an object that weighs 1,000 pounds or less, you can pull it up to 15 feet towards you. If the target is a fixed object or a Huge or larger creature, you can pull yourself up to 15 feet towards the target.

entries.name: Waterborne
entries: When submerged in water, this longsword gains the Reach property, and the range of the Water Lash property increases to 30 feet.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

item.name: Tidecutter (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

This item appears to be a sword hilt stylized with tentacles. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of rushing water to spring forth, or make it disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the Finesse property. If you are proficient with shortswords or longswords, you are proficient with the tidecutter.

entries.name: Water Lash
entries: As an action, you can cause the sword's blade to elongate into a lash and make a melee weapon attack using it against a target within 30 feet of you (40 feet underwater). On a hit, if the target is a Huge or smaller creature or an object that weighs 2,000 pounds or less, you can pull it up to 25 feet towards you. If the target is a fixed object or a Gargantuan creature, you can pull yourself up to 25 feet towards the target.

entries.name: Waterborne
entries: When submerged in water, this longsword gains the Reach property.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

item.name: Tidecutter (Legendary)
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

This item appears to be a sword hilt stylized with tentacles. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of rushing water to spring forth, or make it disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the Finesse property. If you are proficient with shortswords or longswords, you are proficient with the tidecutter.

entries.name: Water Lash
entries: As an action, you can cause the sword's blade to elongate into a lash and make a melee weapon attack using it against a target within 30 feet of you (40 feet underwater). On a hit, if the target is a Gargantuan or smaller creature or an object that weighs 10,000 pounds or less, you can pull it up to 25 feet towards you. If the target is a fixed object or a Huge or larger creature, you can pull yourself up to 25 feet towards the target. Gargantuan creatures are only pulled if they fail on a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw.

entries.name: Waterborne
entries: When submerged in water, this longsword gains the Reach property.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

item.name: Flutterby Rod (Uncommon)
entries: This rod has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the rod, you can cast the {@spell light} cantrip, targeting only the rod.

entries.name: Flutterbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the rod, you can expend 1 charge to shoot a shimmering green, butterfly-shaped flutterbolt at a target within 60 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack ({@hit 5} to hit). On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} force damage and is outlined in sparkling green butterflies. The next attack roll made against this target before the end of your next turn has {@b advantage}, and the target can't benefit from being {@condition invisible} until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Flutterby Rod (Rare)
entries: This rod has 9 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the rod, you can cast the {@spell light} cantrip, targeting only the rod.

entries.name: Flutterbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the rod, you can expend up to 2 charges to shoot shimmering green, butterfly-shaped flutterbolts at targets within 60 feet of you, creating a separate flutterbolt for each charge expended. You can direct the flutterbolts at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate ranged spell attack ({@hit 7} to hit) for each flutterbolt. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} force damage and is outlined in sparkling green butterflies. The next attack roll made against this target before the end of your next turn has {@b advantage}, and the target can't benefit from being {@condition invisible} until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Flutterby Rod (Very Rare)
entries: This rod has 12 charges and regains {@dice 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the rod, you can cast the {@spell light} cantrip, targeting only the rod.

entries.name: Flutterbolt
entries: As a bonus action while holding the rod, you can expend up to 3 charges to shoot shimmering green, butterfly-shaped flutterbolts at targets within 60 feet of you, creating a separate flutterbolt for each charge expended. You can direct the flutterbolts at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate ranged spell attack ({@hit 8} to hit) for each flutterbolt. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d10} force damage and is outlined in sparkling green butterflies. The next attack roll made against this target before the end of your next turn has {@b advantage}, and the target can't benefit from being {@condition invisible} until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Lilypad Sunhat (Common)
entries: The water in the top of this hat never sloshes and can't be poured out. It can, however, be scooped out in a drinking vessel, and contains up to four pints. Once water is removed, the hat fills back up over the course of 1 minute.

While you wear the hat outdoors during the day, it can feed you through photosynthesis. The time you take to gain a day's worth of nourishment depends on the brightness: 1 hour in bright, direct sunlight or 8 hours in overcast conditions.

item.name: Lilypad Sunhat (Uncommon)
entries: The water in the top of this hat never sloshes and can't be poured out. It can, however, be scooped out in a drinking vessel, and contains up to four pints. Once water is removed, the hat fills back up over the course of 1 minute.

While you wear the hat outdoors during the day, it can feed you through photosynthesis. The time you take to gain a day's worth of nourishment depends on the brightness: 1 hour in bright, direct sunlight or 8 hours in overcast conditions.

entries.name: Thrive in the Sun
entries: If you wear this hat in sunlight during a short rest in which you spend at least one Hit Die to regain hit points, you regain an additional {@dice 3d6} hit points at the end of the rest. Alternatively, you can choose to end the {@condition poisoned} condition on yourself or cure a disease that's currently affecting you.

item.name: Ring of Birdseye Maple (Uncommon)
entries: This ring has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Watchful Woods
entries: While wearing the ring, whenever you touch a plant or wooden surface, you can expend 1 charge to cause an eye to grow anywhere on that plant or surface within 60 feet of you. The eye lasts for 1 hour, until you cease touching the plant or surface, or until you create a new eye. You can see out of the eye, which has {@sense darkvision} out to 60 feet, for as long as you remain touching the plant or surface. While you look out of the eye, you are {@condition blinded} with regards to your own senses.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, the eye doesn't disappear if you cease touching the plant or surface, and you can use the Eyesoar property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Eyesoar
entries: So long as the eye is within 300 feet of you, you can use an action to look through the eye. While looking through the eye, you can use your action to move the eye up to 30 feet along the plant or surface.

item.name: Ring of Birdseye Maple (Very Rare)
entries: This ring has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Watchful Woods
entries: While wearing the ring, whenever you touch a plant or wooden surface, you can expend 1 charge to cause an eye to grow anywhere on that plant or surface within 60 feet of you. The eye lasts for 1 hour, until you cease touching the plant or surface, or until you create a new eye. You can see out of the eye, which has {@sense darkvision} out to 60 feet, for as long as you remain touching the plant or surface. The eye can see {@condition invisible} creatures and objects as if they were visible and can see into the Ethereal Plane. In addition, you have {@b advantage} on Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks made to peer through illusions while looking through the eye. While you look out of the eye, you are {@condition blinded} with regards to your own senses.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, the eye doesn't disappear if you cease touching the plant or surface, and you can use the Eyesoar property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Eyesoar
entries: So long as the eye is within 300 feet of you, you can use an action to look through the eye. While looking through the eye, you can use your action to move the eye up to 30 feet along the plant or surface.

entries.name: Fungal Conjuration
entries: If you can read Druidic, Elvish, Sylvan, or Undercommon, you can use an action while holding this scroll to speak the script aloud and summon one of the following mushrooms of your choice, which appears in your hand. As part of this action, you can use the mushroom as described below. The summoned mushroom withers and dies after 1 hour. Once this property of the scroll has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Boomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can throw this volatile mushroom at a point it can see within 60 feet of it, where it explodes in a 10-foot-radius sphere of flames. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

items.name: Bloomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can eat this mushroom, regaining {@dice 2d4 + 2} hit points and gaining {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws for 1 minute.

items.name: Gloomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can eat this mushroom. For 1 hour, it gains {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet. If it already has {@sense darkvision}, the range of this vision is increased by 30 feet.

entries.name: Fungal Conjuration
entries: If you can read Druidic, Elvish, Sylvan, or Undercommon, you can use an action while holding this scroll to speak the script aloud and summon one of the following mushrooms of your choice, which appears in your hand. As part of this action, you can use the mushroom as described below. The summoned mushroom withers and dies after 1 hour. Once this property of the scroll has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Boomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can throw this volatile mushroom at a point it can see within 60 feet of it, where it explodes in a 10-foot-radius sphere of flames. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 8d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

items.name: Bloomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can eat this mushroom, regaining {@dice 4d4 + 4} hit points and gaining {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws for 1 minute.

items.name: Gloomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can eat this mushroom. For 1 hour, it gains {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 90 feet. If it already has {@sense darkvision}, the range of this vision is increased by 45 feet.

entries.name: Fungal Conjuration
entries: If you can read Druidic, Elvish, Sylvan, or Undercommon, you can use an action while holding this scroll to speak the script aloud and summon one of the following mushrooms of your choice, which appears in your hand. As part of this action, you can use the mushroom as described below. The summoned mushroom withers and dies after 1 hour. Once this property of the scroll has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Boomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can throw this volatile mushroom at a point it can see within 60 feet of it, where it explodes in a 10-foot-radius sphere of flames. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 12d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

items.name: Bloomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can eat this mushroom, regaining {@dice 8d4 + 8} hit points and gaining {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws for 1 minute.

items.name: Gloomshroom
entries: As an action, a creature can eat this mushroom. For 1 hour, it gains {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 120 feet. If it already has {@sense darkvision}, the range of this vision is increased by 60 feet. A creature that eats the Gloomshroom can see {@condition invisible} creatures and objects in the range of its {@sense darkvision} as if they were visible.

item.name: Waspberry Jam (Common)
entries: This terracotta jar, 4 inches in diameter, contains {@dice 1d4 + 1} doses of a sweet, sticky, pink jam. The jar and its contents weigh ½ pound.

entries.name: Insect Trap
entries: As an action, you can spread a single dose of jam to cover a 5-foot-square surface. Tiny, venomous waspberries immediately teleport from the Plane of Fey, forming a swarm of insects that stays in the same spot as the jam and takes its turn on initiative count 0 of each round. The swarm attacks a creature it shares a space with, and otherwise takes the Dodge action. A creature that starts its turn in the swarm's area or enters the area for the first time on its turn must make a {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 1d4} piercing damage and {@damage 1d4} poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If the swarm has half its hit points or fewer, creatures take no damage in this way.

It takes the swarm 1 hour to completely consume the jam, after which it returns to the Plane of Fey. The swarm doesn't like smoke; if the area where the jam is applied is ever heavily obscured by smoke, the swarm teleports back to the Plane of Fey. If the smoke disappears, the swarm immediately returns to continue eating the jam.

item.name: Waspberry Jam (Uncommon)
entries: This terracotta jar, 4 inches in diameter, contains {@dice 1d4 + 1} doses of a sweet, sticky, pink jam. The jar and its contents weigh ½ pound.

entries.name: Insect Trap
entries: As an action, you can spread a single dose of jam to cover a 5-foot-square surface. Tiny, venomous waspberries immediately teleport from the Plane of Fey, forming a swarm of insects that stays in the same spot as the jam and takes its turn on initiative count 0 of each round. The swarm attacks a creature it shares a space with, and otherwise takes the Dodge action. A creature that starts its turn in the swarm's area or enters the area for the first time on its turn must make a {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d4} piercing damage and {@damage 2d4} poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If the swarm has half its hit points or fewer, creatures take {@damage 1d4} piercing damage and {@damage 1d4} poison damage instead. Additionally, the swarm is more active in protecting the jam; on each of its turns, it can move up to 15 feet away from the jam's space, and attacks the creature closest to it.

It takes the swarm 1 hour to completely consume the jam, after which it returns to the Plane of Fey. The swarm doesn't like smoke; if the area where the jam is applied is ever heavily obscured by smoke, the swarm teleports back to the Plane of Fey. If the smoke disappears, the swarm immediately returns to continue eating the jam.

item.name: Splinterstrike Arrow
entries: On a hit, this magic arrow deals the weapon's normal damage and then erupts in a storm of splinters and thorns. The target and each creature within 5 feet of it must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 1d10} piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. After this effect triggers, the arrow is destroyed.

item.name: Bramblestrike Arrow
entries: On a hit, this magic arrow deals the weapon's normal damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained} by magical vines until the end of its next turn. A Large or larger creature has {@b advantage} on this saving throw. While {@condition restrained} by this arrow, the target takes {@damage 1d6} piercing damage at the start of its turn. The {@condition restrained} creature, or a creature within 5 feet of it, can use its action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check, freeing it on a success. After this effect triggers, the arrow is destroyed.

item.name: Tanglestrike Arrow
entries: If the arrow hits an object or solid surface, grasping weeds and thorny vines sprout on the surface in a 5-foot-radius sphere centred on the arrow. The area becomes difficult terrain for 1 minute, and each creature within the area must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained} by the entangling plants for 1 minute. The {@condition restrained} creature, or a creature within 5 feet of it, can use its action to make a {@dc 16} Strength check, freeing it on a success. After this effect triggers, the arrow is destroyed.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a minor task, such as copying a friend's dress sense or mimicking someone's accent for a day.

entries.name: OA or EA: Envy
entries: While you hold the item, when a creature within {@b 30 feet} of you that you can see makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can use your reaction to capture the creature's reflection in the item's surface and store a copy of the result of that creature's roll before any modifiers are applied to it. You can decide to use this reaction after the die is rolled, but before the GM determines if the result succeeds or fails. Once within the next {@b minute}, after a creature you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can choose to replace its roll of the {@b d20} with this stored die roll (no action required). You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn. After you replace a die in this way, until the end of your next turn, if the creature you mirrored takes damage, you take half as much damage as psychic damage. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a medium task, such as stealing credit for someone else's achievement or publicly disparaging a friend so you look better in comparison.

entries.name: OA or EA: Envy
entries: While you hold the item, when a creature within {@b 60 feet} of you that you can see makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can use your reaction to capture the creature's reflection in the item's surface and store a copy of the result of that creature's roll before any modifiers are applied to it. You can decide to use this reaction after the die is rolled, but before the GM determines if the result succeeds or fails. Once within the next {@b minute}, after a creature you can see within {@b 60 feet} of you makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can choose to replace its roll of the {@b d20} with this stored die roll (no action required). You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn. After you replace a die in this way, until the end of your next turn, if the creature you mirrored takes damage, you take half as much damage as psychic damage. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a major task, such as murdering someone you consider more beautiful than yourself or sabotaging a happy relationship.

entries.name: OA or EA: Envy
entries: While you hold the item, when a creature within {@b 120 feet} of you that you can see makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can use your reaction to capture the creature's reflection in the item's surface and store a copy of the result of that creature's roll before any modifiers are applied to it. You can decide to use this reaction after the die is rolled, but before the GM determines if the result succeeds or fails. Once within the next {@b minute}, after a creature you can see within {@b 120 feet} of you makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, you can choose to replace its roll of the {@b d20} with this stored die roll (no action required). You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn. After you replace a die in this way, until the end of your next turn, if the creature you mirrored takes damage, you take half as much damage as psychic damage. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a minor task, such as eating a friend's food before they're finished, not sharing your birthday cake, or taking candy from a baby.

entries.name: OA or EA: Gluttony
entries: While the item is on your person, whenever you roll Hit Dice to recover hit points at the end of a short rest, you regain {@b 1} additional hit points for each die rolled. You can regain up to {@b 5} additional hit points from this property per day; the additional hit points you can regain resets each day at dawn. You require twice as much food to sustain yourself each day.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a medium task, such as stealing a party member's rations or demanding that a restaurant increase its portion size.

entries.name: OA or EA: Gluttony
entries: While the item is on your person, whenever you roll Hit Dice to recover hit points at the end of a short rest, you regain {@b 2} additional hit points for each die rolled. You can regain up to {@b 10} additional hit points from this property per day; the additional hit points you can regain resets each day at dawn. You require twice as much food to sustain yourself each day.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a major task, such as cannibalising a humanoid or stealing food from the hungry.

entries.name: OA or EA: Gluttony
entries: While the item is on your person, whenever you roll Hit Dice to recover hit points at the end of a short rest, you regain {@b 3} additional hit points for each die rolled. You can regain up to {@b 15} additional hit points from this property per day; the additional hit points you can regain resets each day at dawn. You require twice as much food to sustain yourself each day.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a minor task, such as stealing a small item, lying to your party about how much treasure you found, or acquiring a certain lost (and potentially cursed) treasure.

entries.name: OA or EA: Greed
entries: This item has {@b 3 charges} and regains {@b all} expended charges daily at dawn. While it is on your person, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cast the {@spell locate object} spell. When you cast the spell using this property, you can target only the nearest precious metals or gems not currently worn or carried by a creature. If there are no such treasures within range of the spell, you become demotivated, gaining {@b disadvantage} on all Charisma and Wisdom checks for the next hour, and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend asks for a medium task, such as purloining a family's sacred heirloom or tricking an honest salesman into buying a cursed item.

entries.name: OA or EA: Greed
entries: While it is on your person, you can cast the {@spell locate object} spell at will. When you cast the spell using this property, you can target only the nearest precious metals or gems not currently worn or carried by a creature. If there are no such treasures within range of the spell, you become demotivated, gaining {@b disadvantage} on all Charisma and Wisdom checks for the next hour, and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend asks for a major task, such as stealing a piece of royal jewellery or committing arson to benefit from insurance fraud.

entries.name: OA or EA: Greed
entries: While it is on your person, you can cast the {@spell locate object} spell at will, and it's no longer limited in what it can target, but you can still become demotivated if you name a target not within range of the spell. If there are no such treasures within range of the spell, you become demotivated, gaining {@b disadvantage} on all Charisma and Wisdom checks for the next hour, and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a minor task, such as delivering a sultry message, flirting with a nobleperson, or gifting someone a bottle of cursed perfume.

entries.name: OA or EA: Lust
entries: While the item is on your person, you can cast the {@spell charm person} spell at will (save {@dc 13}). If your target succeeds on the saving throw, you feel the sting of rejection; you are afflicted by the {@spell bane} spell for the next {@b hour} and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend asks for a medium task, such as ensuring someone drinks a love potion or courting a nobleperson to gain their favour.

entries.name: OA or EA: Lust
entries: While the item is on your person, you can cast the {@spell charm person} spell at will (save {@dc 15}). If your target succeeds on the saving throw, you feel the sting of rejection; you are afflicted by the {@spell bane} spell for the next hour and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend asks for a major task, such as seducing someone or causing someone to break a vow.

entries.name: OA or EA: Lust
entries: While the item is on your person, you can cast the {@spell charm person} spell at will (save {@dc 16}). If your target succeeds on the saving throw, you feel the sting of rejection; you are afflicted by the {@spell bane} spell for the next hour and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

While the item is on your person, you are proficient in the {@skill Persuasion} skill. If you already have this proficiency, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses {@skill Persuasion}.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend asks for a minor task, such as refusing to ask for help or healing for a day, or exaggerating about your past exploits and skills to the party's detriment.

entries.name: OA or EA: Pride
entries: While the item is on your person, you can use a bonus action to cast the {@spell bless} spell on yourself (no concentration required). When you cast the {@spell bless} spell on yourself using this item, you gain a +1 bonus to your rolls, instead of the spell's usual bonus of 1d4. While you are blessed in this way, you become hard-headed; you can't benefit from the Help action and you are always considered an unwilling target for other creatures' spells. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a medium task, such as refusing to accept help or healing for 3 days, refusing the next gift or donation offered to you, or insisting on fighting a creature of CR 4 or higher alone.

entries.name: OA or EA: Pride
entries: While the item is on your person, you can use a bonus action to cast the {@spell bless} spell on yourself (no concentration required). While you are blessed in this way, you become hard-headed; you can't benefit from the Help action and you are always considered an unwilling target for other creatures' spells. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend asks for a major task, such as refusing to ask for help or healing for 10 days or insisting on fighting a creature of CR 9 or higher alone.

entries.name: OA or EA: Pride
entries: While the item is on your person, you can use a bonus action to cast the {@spell bless} spell on yourself (no concentration required). When you cast the {@spell bless} spell on yourself using this item, you roll the d4 twice and use the higher result. While you are blessed in this way, you become hard-headed; you can't benefit from the Help action and you are always considered an unwilling target for other creatures' spells. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a minor task, such as refusing to take a watch when it's your turn, leaving dirty dishes in the sink, or not bathing for 3 weeks before an important meeting.

entries.name: OA or EA: Sloth
entries: While the item is on your person, you can cast the {@spell slow} spell (save {@dc 13}). For the spell's duration, your speed is halved and you can't take reactions. After this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend asks for a medium task, such as deliberately not taking an action in combat once a day for a week, or refusing to take the Help action for a week.

entries.name: OA or EA: Sloth
entries: While the item is on your person, you can cast the {@spell slow} spell (save {@dc 15}). For the spell's duration, your speed is halved and you can't take reactions. The Sloth property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend asks for a major task, such as purposefully falling asleep while on watch or refusing to take the Help action for a month.

entries.name: OA or EA: Sloth
entries: While the item is on your person, you can cast the {@spell slow} spell (save {@dc 16}). For the spell's duration, your speed is halved and you can't take reactions. The Sloth property can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The fiend requests a minor task, such as delivering an aggressive message, intimidating someone, or starting a bar fight.

entries.name: OA or EA: Wrath
entries: While the item is on your person, you can use a bonus action to enter a fiendish rage for {@b 1 minute}. When you activate this property and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for the duration, you gain additional movement equal to half your speed until the start of your next turn. For the duration, if you move at least {@b 15 feet} straight towards a creature and then immediately hit it with a melee attack, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. While this property is active, you lose all sense of self-preservation and are filled with bloodlust; any movement you make must be towards a hostile creature, and the only actions you can take on your turn are the Attack and Dash actions, or an action to break free of restraints or a grapple. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The devil asks for a medium task, such as assaulting someone or destroying their property.

entries.name: OA or EA: Wrath
entries: While the item is on your person, you can use a bonus action to enter a fiendish rage for {@b 1 minute}. When you activate this property and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for the duration, you gain additional movement equal to half your speed until the start of your next turn. For the duration, if you move at least {@b 15 feet} straight towards a creature and then immediately hit it with a melee attack, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. While this property is active, you lose all sense of self-preservation and are filled with bloodlust; any movement you make must be towards a hostile creature, and the only actions you can take on your turn are the Attack and Dash actions, or an action to break free of restraints or a grapple. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: The Task
entries: The devil asks for a major task, such as kidnap or murder.

entries.name: OA or EA: Wrath
entries: While the item is on your person, you can use a bonus action to enter a fiendish rage for {@b 1 minute}. When you activate this property and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for the duration, you gain additional movement equal to half your speed until the start of your next turn. When you activate the Wrath property or use the bonus action granted by it to gain movement, you can also make one melee attack with this weapon as part of the same bonus action. For the duration, if you move at least {@b 15 feet} straight towards a creature and then immediately hit it with a melee attack, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. While this property is active, you lose all sense of self-preservation and are filled with bloodlust; any movement you make must be towards a hostile creature, and the only actions you can take on your turn are the Attack and Dash actions, or an action to break free of restraints or a grapple. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Blood Bond
entries: When you damage a creature that has blood with this magic weapon, or it touches blood spilled from a creature less than 24 hours ago, the light pink crystals that make up its blade turn a deep, sanguine red as the weapon becomes bonded to the creature, which becomes its quarry. For the next 24 hours you can, as an action, toss the dagger in the air and speak its command word. For 6 seconds after, it hovers in the air, pointing in the direction of its quarry. If its quarry is on a different plane or is warded against divination magic, the dagger spins wistfully in the air. Once the dagger absorbs blood, it can't absorb another creature's blood until 24 hours have passed and it returns to its normal pink colour.

item.name: Bloodlust (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Blood Bond
entries: When you damage a creature that has blood with this magic weapon, or it touches blood spilled from a creature less than 24 hours ago, the light pink crystals that make up its blade turn a deep, sanguine red as the weapon becomes bonded to the creature, which becomes its quarry. For the next 24 hours you can, as an action, toss the dagger in the air and speak its command word. For 6 seconds after, it hovers in the air, pointing in the direction of its quarry. If its quarry is on a different plane or is warded against divination magic, the dagger spins wistfully in the air. Once the dagger absorbs blood, it can't absorb another creature's blood until 24 hours have passed and it returns to its normal pink colour.

item.name: Bloodlust (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Blood Bond
entries: When you damage a creature that has blood with this magic weapon, or it touches blood spilled from a creature less than 24 hours ago, the light pink crystals that make up its blade turn a deep, sanguine red as the weapon becomes bonded to the creature, which becomes its quarry. For the next 24 hours you can, as an action, toss the dagger in the air and speak its command word. For 6 seconds after, it hovers in the air, pointing in the direction of its quarry. If its quarry is on a different plane or is warded against divination magic, the dagger spins wistfully in the air. Once the dagger absorbs blood, it can't absorb another creature's blood until 24 hours have passed and it returns to its normal pink colour.

entries.name: Charges & Charging
entries: An emberink stationery can hold a maximum of 2 charges, contains {@dice 1d4 - 2} charges when found (minimum of 0), and regains charges when the ink pot is used to capture a fiend's blood. As fiends' bodies disappear from the Material Plane on death, fiendish blood must be extracted while the fiend is still alive. You can use an action to extract blood from a living fiend within your reach. If the fiend is unwilling, the fiend must be {@condition restrained} or {@condition incapacitated} and be below half of its maximum hit points. When blood is extracted in this way, the stationery regains a number of charges equal to a roll of one of the fiend's Hit Dice, and once it regains charges, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Contractual Obligation
entries: As an action, you can use the stationery to write on the skin of a willing humanoid. The ink burns into the flesh of your target, raking a cauterised scar, while an identical burn appears in a similar place on your own body. Both you and the creature take 1 fire damage. Choose one of the following glyphs and expend the appropriate number of charges; for 1 hour, you are both subjected to the associated magical effects.

items.name: Ward (1 charge)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, you both have a +1 bonus to AC. While you are more than 30 feet apart, you both suffer a -1 penalty to AC.

items.name: Warp (2 charges)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, each affected creature can choose whether it is enlarged or reduced, as per the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell. An affected creature can use its action to return to normal size, or to enlarge or reduce itself if it's already normal size. While you are more than 30 feet apart, both creatures return to their normal size.

items.name: Wrath (1 charge)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, you both gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. While you are more than 30 feet apart, you both suffer a -1 penalty to attack and damage rolls.
Once the stationery has been used to sign a glyph, the ink turns dark and it can't be used again until the current contract is complete (i.e., the hour has elapsed).

entries.name: Charges & Charging
entries: An emberink stationery can hold a maximum of 4 charges, contains {@dice 1d4} charges when found, and regains charges when the ink pot is used to capture a fiend's blood. As fiends' bodies disappear from the Material Plane on death, fiendish blood must be extracted while the fiend is still alive. You can use an action to extract blood from a living fiend within your reach. If the fiend is unwilling, the fiend must be {@condition restrained} or {@condition incapacitated} and be below half of its maximum hit points. When blood is extracted in this way, the stationery regains a number of charges equal to a roll of one of the fiend's Hit Dice, and once it regains charges, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Contractual Obligation
entries: As an action, you can use the stationery to write on the skin of a willing humanoid. The ink burns into the flesh of your target, raking a cauterised scar, while an identical burn appears in a similar place on your own body. Both you and the creature take 1 fire damage. Choose one of the following glyphs and expend the appropriate number of charges; for 1 hour, you are both subjected to the associated magical effects.

items.name: Ward (1 charge)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, you both have a +1 bonus to AC. While you are more than 30 feet apart, you both suffer a -1 penalty to AC.

items.name: Warp (2 charges)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, each affected creature can choose whether it is enlarged or reduced, as per the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell. An affected creature can use its action to return to normal size, or to enlarge or reduce itself if it's already normal size. While you are more than 30 feet apart, both creatures return to their normal size.

items.name: Wrath (1 charge)
entries: While you and the creature are within 30 feet of one another, you both gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. While you are more than 30 feet apart, you both suffer a -1 penalty to attack and damage rolls.
Once the stationery has been used to sign a glyph, the ink turns dark and it can't be used again until the current contract is complete (i.e., the hour has elapsed).

item.name: Heart of Stone (Uncommon)
entries: The heart can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Soultrap
