entries: When a fiend of CR 6 or lower dies within 10 feet of the heart and the heart doesn't already have a soul trapped within it, the fiend's soul becomes trapped within the heart. The heart gains a number of charges equal to the maximum number of Hit Dice the fiend possessed. If all these charges are expended, the soul is shredded and destroyed.
While you hold the stone, you can communicate telepathically with the imprisoned soul, if it is willing. You can release an imprisoned soul back to the Lower Planes as an action.

entries.name: Soulshred
entries: While you hold the heart, you can expend charges from it to cast the following spells (save {@dc 13}): {@spell bane} (1 charge), {@spell calm emotions} (2 charges), {@spell enrage|LALoTT} (2 charges).

After you cast a spell in this way, you must make a Charisma check contested by the imprisoned fiend's. On a failure, the fiend's soul escapes and is released back to the Lower Planes, and the heart loses all its remaining charges. If a soul escapes or is released while you're on one of the Lower Planes, the fiend can immediately materialise with all of its hit points in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of the heart.

item.name: Heart of Stone (Rare)
entries: The heart can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Soultrap
entries: When a fiend of CR 12 or lower dies within 10 feet of the heart and the heart doesn't already have a soul trapped within it, the fiend's soul becomes trapped within the heart. The heart gains a number of charges equal to the maximum number of Hit Dice the fiend possessed. If all these charges are expended, the soul is shredded and destroyed.
While you hold the stone, you can communicate telepathically with the imprisoned soul, if it is willing. You can release an imprisoned soul back to the Lower Planes as an action.

entries.name: Soulshred
entries: While you hold the heart, you can expend charges from it to cast the following spells (save {@dc 15}): {@spell bane} (1 charge), {@spell bestow curse} (3 charges), {@spell calm emotions} (2 charges), {@spell enrage|LALoTT} (2 charges), {@spell remove curse} (3 charges).

After you cast a spell in this way, you must make a Charisma check contested by the imprisoned fiend's. On a failure, the fiend's soul escapes and is released back to the Lower Planes, and the heart loses all its remaining charges. If a soul escapes or is released while you're on one of the Lower Planes, the fiend can immediately materialise with all of its hit points in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of the heart.

item.name: Heart of Stone (Very Rare)
entries: The heart can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Soultrap
entries: When a fiend of CR 18 or lower dies within 10 feet of the heart and the heart doesn't already have a soul trapped within it, the fiend's soul becomes trapped within the heart. The heart gains a number of charges equal to the maximum number of Hit Dice the fiend possessed. If all these charges are expended, the soul is shredded and destroyed.
While you hold the stone, you can communicate telepathically with the imprisoned soul, if it is willing. You can release an imprisoned soul back to the Lower Planes as an action.

entries.name: Soulshred
entries: While you hold the heart, you can expend charges from it to cast the following spells (save {@dc 16}): {@spell bane} (1 charge), {@spell bestow curse} (3 charges), {@spell calm emotions} (2 charges), {@spell enrage|LALoTT} (2 charges), {@spell remove curse} (3 charges), {@spell stoneskin} (4 charges). When {@spell stoneskin} is cast using this item, the target's skin resembles the obsidian of the heart.

After you cast a spell in this way, you must make a Charisma check contested by the imprisoned fiend's. On a failure, the fiend's soul escapes and is released back to the Lower Planes, and the heart loses all its remaining charges. If a soul escapes or is released while you're on one of the Lower Planes, the fiend can immediately materialise with all of its hit points in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of the heart.

entries.name: Real Reflections
entries: As an action while holding the mirror, you can speak its command word to cause its reflective surface to disappear for 1 minute. During that time, a creature that looks through the mirror while holding it has truesight out to 30 feet. Once this property of the mirror has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Real Reflections
entries: As an action while holding the mirror, you can speak its command word to cause its reflective surface to disappear for 1 minute. During that time, a creature that looks through the mirror while holding it has truesight out to 60 feet. The mirror can be used three times and regains all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Real Reflections
entries: As an action while holding the mirror, you can speak its command word to cause its reflective surface to disappear for 1 minute. During that time, a creature that looks through the mirror while holding it has truesight out to 120 feet. The mirror can be used five times and regains all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hit
entries: When you hit with this arrow, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or have shattered bone fragments lodged in obtrusive places within its body. While a creature has these fragments in it, it takes 1 piercing damage when it moves for the first time on a turn and whenever it takes the Attack action. As an action, an affected creature can make a {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Miss
entries: When you miss with this arrow, the arrow changes direction to hit the nearest surface to the target, where it explodes into sharp fragments. Each creature within 5 feet of the arrow must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d4} piercing damage.

entries.name: Hit
entries: When you hit with this arrow, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have shattered bone fragments lodged in obtrusive places within its body. While a creature has these fragments in it, it takes 2 piercing damage when it moves for the first time on a turn and whenever it takes the Attack action. As an action, an affected creature can make a {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Miss
entries: When you miss with this arrow, the arrow changes direction to hit the nearest surface to the target, where it explodes into sharp fragments. Each creature within 5 feet of the arrow must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d8} piercing damage.

entries.name: Hit
entries: When you hit with this arrow, the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or have shattered bone fragments lodged in obtrusive places within its body. While a creature has these fragments in it, it takes 4 piercing damage when it moves for the first time on a turn and whenever it takes the Attack action. As an action, an affected creature can make a {@dc 16} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Miss
entries: When you miss with this arrow, the arrow changes direction to hit the nearest surface to the target, where it explodes into sharp fragments. Each creature within 5 feet of the arrow must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d12} piercing damage.

item.name: Pluckpatch (Uncommon)
entries: This eyepatch has 2 charges, represented by two floating eyeballs, and regains {@dice 1d2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Peekaboo
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to telepathically link yourself to one eyeball. While linked, you see through the eyeball, which has {@sense darkvision} out to 120 feet, and you are blind with regards to your own vision. As a bonus action while linked to the eyeball, you can move the eyeball up to 30 feet in any direction. There is no limit to how far away from you the eye can move, but it can't enter another plane of existence. The eyeball has 2 hit points, an AC of 15, and a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) modifier of +5. After 10 minutes, if the eyeball is reduced to 0 hit points, or if you sever the connection (no action required), the eyeball disappears, temporarily returning to its fiendish donor.

item.name: Pluckpatch (Rare)
entries: This eyepatch has 4 charges, represented by four floating eyeballs, and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Peekaboo
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to telepathically link yourself to one eyeball. While linked, you see through the eyeball, which has {@sense darkvision} out to 120 feet, and you are blind with regards to your own vision. As a bonus action while linked to the eyeball, you can move the eyeball up to 30 feet in any direction. There is no limit to how far away from you the eye can move, but it can't enter another plane of existence. The eyeball has 2 hit points, an AC of 15, and a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) modifier of +7. After 10 minutes, if the eyeball is reduced to 0 hit points, or if you sever the connection (no action required), the eyeball disappears, temporarily returning to its fiendish donor.

item.name: Pluckpatch (Very Rare)
entries: This eyepatch has 7 charges, represented by seven floating eyeballs, and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Peekaboo
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to telepathically link yourself to one eyeball. While linked, you see through the eyeball, which has {@sense darkvision} out to 120 feet, and you are blind with regards to your own vision. As a bonus action while linked to the eyeball, you can move the eyeball up to 30 feet in any direction. There is no limit to how far away from you the eye can move, but it can't enter another plane of existence. The eyeball has 2 hit points, an AC of 15, and a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) modifier of +10. After 10 minutes, if the eyeball is reduced to 0 hit points, or if you sever the connection (no action required), the eyeball disappears, temporarily returning to its fiendish donor.

entries.name: Searing Vengeance
entries: As a reaction when a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, you can pull a trigger on the shield's handle, causing it to breathe a plume of flame at the creature. The creature must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} fire damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. After you use this property, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, the shield runs out of fuel and this property can't be used again until you feed the shield 1 gp of coal or other fuel and spend 1 hour tending to the flame (which can be done during a short or long rest), as the shield burns and chews its way through your offering.

entries.name: Searing Vengeance
entries: As a reaction when a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, you can pull a trigger on the shield's handle, causing it to breathe a plume of flame at the creature. The creature must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d8} fire damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. After you use this property, roll a {@dice d8}. On a 1-2, the shield runs out of fuel and this property can't be used again until you feed the shield 1 gp of coal or other fuel and spend 1 hour tending to the flame (which can be done during a short or long rest), as the shield burns and chews its way through your offering.

entries.name: Searing Vengeance
entries: As a reaction when a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, you can pull a trigger on the shield's handle, causing it to breathe a plume of flame at the creature. The creature must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d10} fire damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. After you use this property, roll a {@dice d10}. On a 1-2, the shield runs out of fuel and this property can't be used again until you feed the shield 1 gp of coal or other fuel and spend 1 hour tending to the flame (which can be done during a short or long rest), as the shield burns and chews its way through your offering.

item.name: Soulkeeper Pendant (Rare)
entries: When a humanoid dies within 5 feet of the pendant and the pendant doesn't already have a soul trapped in it, its soul becomes trapped in a flame-filled, hellish demiplane, causing the pendant's eyes to glow fiery red. Casting the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic on the pendant releases a soul from its captivity. A soul can be stored indefinitely, and time spent in captivity within the pendant doesn't count against the time limit of resurrection spells such as {@spell raise dead}. A creature who is resurrected after its soul has been stored within the pendant must make a Wisdom saving throw or develop one long-term madness effect. The DC for the saving throw equals 10 + the number of days the soul was trapped in the pendant.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Sellsoul
entries: Upon attuning to this pendant, a devil, Eebezelbub, appears to you with an offer. She will trick souls you capture into following her to the Hells in exchange for granting you minor favours. If the pendant has a soul trapped in it, you can use an action to call Eebezelbub's name and permit her entry to the pendant. Roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1, the soul refuses Eeb's offer and drifts away, causing the miniature animal's eyes to slowly dim. On a 2-6, the soul accepts her offer and the skull flashes brightly before petering out. Eebezelbub reappears {@dice 1d20} minutes later, offering to perform one of the following tasks of your choice:

items: She will deliver and return one message, as per the {@spell sending} spell, and can send the message to other planes of existence without the possibility of failure, but she can't enter good-aligned planes to deliver the message.
She will answer three questions, as per the {@spell commune} spell.
She will divulge information on a topic, as per the {@spell legend lore} spell.

entries: Eebezelbub needs time to process the souls that accept her offers, and after getting two souls from the pendant, she won't take any more until the next dawn.

item.name: Soulkeeper Pendant (Very Rare)
entries: When a humanoid dies within 5 feet of the pendant and the pendant doesn't already have a soul trapped in it, its soul becomes trapped in a flame-filled, hellish demiplane, causing the pendant's eyes to glow fiery red. Casting the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic on the pendant releases a soul from its captivity. A soul can be stored indefinitely, and time spent in captivity within the pendant doesn't count against the time limit of resurrection spells such as {@spell raise dead}. A creature who is resurrected after its soul has been stored within the pendant must make a Wisdom saving throw or develop one long-term madness effect. The DC for the saving throw equals 10 + the number of days the soul was trapped in the pendant.

Eebezelbub reappears {@dice 1d20} minutes later, offering to perform one of the following tasks of your choice:

items: She will deliver and return one message, as per the {@spell sending} spell, and can send the message to other planes of existence without the possibility of failure, but she can't enter good-aligned planes to deliver the message.
She will answer three questions, as per the {@spell commune} spell.
She will divulge information on a topic, as per the {@spell legend lore} spell.

entries: Eebezelbub needs time to process the souls that accept her offers, and after getting three souls from the pendant, she won't take any more until the next dawn.

item.name: Wings of the Hellspawn (Uncommon)
entries: Until your attunement to the wings ends, you can't be parted from them, willingly or otherwise.

entries.name: Fiendish Flight
entries: When you fall and aren't {@condition incapacitated}, you can subtract up to 100 feet from the fall when calculating falling damage, and you can move up to 2 feet horizontally for every 1 foot you descend.

entries.name: Devil's Cocoon
entries: As an action, you can cause these wings to wrap around you, forming a fleshy carapace. You gain {@dice 1d4 + 4} temporary hit points that last for 1 hour. If you use the Fiendish Flight property, you lose any temporary hit points you have from this property. Once this property of the wings has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Wings of the Hellspawn (Rare)
entries: Until your attunement to the wings ends, you can't be parted from them, willingly or otherwise.

entries.name: Fiendish Flight
entries: When you fall and aren't {@condition incapacitated}, you can subtract up to 100 feet from the fall when calculating falling damage, and you can move up to 2 feet horizontally for every 1 foot you descend.
The Fiendish Flight property has the following additional benefits:

items: When you jump, you can choose to propel yourself with your wings (no action required); you no longer require a running start to jump up to your maximum distance.

entries.name: Devil's Cocoon
entries: As an action, you can cause these wings to wrap around you, forming a fleshy carapace. You gain {@dice 3d4 + 4} temporary hit points that last for 1 hour. If you use the Fiendish Flight property, you lose any temporary hit points you have from this property. Once this property of the wings has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Wings of the Hellspawn (Very Rare)
entries: Until your attunement to the wings ends, you can't be parted from them, willingly or otherwise.

entries.name: Fiendish Flight
entries: When you fall and aren't {@condition incapacitated}, you can subtract up to 100 feet from the fall when calculating falling damage, and you can move up to 2 feet horizontally for every 1 foot you descend. The Fiendish Flight property grants you a flying speed equal to your walking speed, and has the following additional benefits:

items: When you jump, you can choose to propel yourself with your wings (no action required); you don't require a running start to jump up to your maximum distance.

entries.name: Devil's Cocoon
entries: As an action, you can cause these wings to wrap around you, forming a fleshy carapace. You gain {@dice 5d4 + 4} temporary hit points that last for 1 hour. If you use the Fiendish Flight property, you lose any temporary hit points you have from this property. Once this property of the wings has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Astrochronometer (Uncommon)
entries: While wearing this watch, you don't need to sleep (although you can only engage in light activity in order to benefit from a long rest), and you have advantage on checks and saving throws made to avoid {@condition exhaustion}. It can also be used to tell the current time in whatever plane you're in.

item.name: Astrochronometer (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing this watch, you age 10 times slower, you don't need to sleep (although you can only engage in light activity in order to benefit from a long rest), and you have advantage on checks and saving throws made to avoid {@condition exhaustion}. It can also be used to tell the current time in whatever plane you're in.

entries.name: Temporal Bubble
entries: As an action, you can attempt to cast the {@spell time stop} spell. When you do so, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1–3, the bubble collapses, the spell isn't cast, and this property can't be used again until the next dawn. Further, on a 1, you are also affected by the {@spell slow} spell for 1 minute (save {@dc 16} for subsequent saving throws), and you age by a number of years equal to one tenth of your race's normal lifespan. On a 4–6, the spell is cast successfully, and this property can't be used again for a number of days equal to the number of turns for which time was stopped.

entries.name: Float
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use a bonus action to cause the tentacles to lift you a foot off of the ground, or to cause you to land again. While floating in this way, you can traverse low-lying obstacles (such as caltrops) and difficult terrain without impediment (at the GM's discretion). In addition, while floating, your walking speed is reduced by 10 feet and you are always under the effects of the {@spell feather fall} spell. You can't float while the Distort property is active.

entries.name: Distort
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use an action to cause the tentacles to twist and shimmer, distorting your outline as you cast the {@spell blur} spell. If you are floating as per the Float property when you activate this property, you land back on the ground. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Float
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use a bonus action to cause the tentacles to lift you a foot off of the ground, or to cause you to land again. While floating in this way, you can traverse low-lying obstacles (such as caltrops) and difficult terrain without impediment (at the GM's discretion). In addition, while floating, your walking speed is reduced by 5 feet and you are always under the effects of the {@spell feather fall} spell. You can't float while the Distort property is active.

entries.name: Distort
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use an action to cause the tentacles to twist and shimmer, distorting your outline as you cast the {@spell blur} spell. If you are floating as per the Float property when you activate this property, you land back on the ground. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Astral Step
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use an action to cause the cloak to appear to implode as you cast the {@spell blink} spell. Instead of visiting the Border Ethereal, you enter a demiplane between the Astral and Material planes. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Float
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use a bonus action to cause the tentacles to lift you a foot off of the ground, or to cause you to land again. While floating in this way, you can traverse low-lying obstacles (such as caltrops) and difficult terrain without impediment (at the GM's discretion). In addition, while floating, your walking speed isn't reduced and you are always under the effects of the {@spell feather fall} spell. You can't float while the Distort property is active.

entries.name: Distort
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use an action to cause the tentacles to twist and shimmer, distorting your outline as you cast the {@spell blur} spell. If you are floating as per the Float property when you activate this property, you land back on the ground. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use an action to cause the cloak to appear to implode as you cast the {@spell blink} spell. Instead of visiting the Border Ethereal, you enter a demiplane between the Astral and Material planes. Once this property of the cloak has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Evening Moon (Uncommon)
entries: This weapon returns to your hand immediately after it is used to make a ranged attack. When you hit a creature with the Shapechanger trait with this weapon, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or instantly revert to its original form and be unable to assume a different form for 1 minute.

entries.name: Dusk
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use an action to create a 10-foot-radius sphere centred on you which is filled with dim light. This sphere lasts for 10 minutes or until you dismiss it as a bonus action, and it follows you when you move. While in this area, you can try to hide when lightly obscured, and you can take the Hide action as a bonus action. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

entries.name: Eclipse, the Moon and Stars
entries: When the magics of {@item morning star|LALoTT} and {@item evening moon|LALoTT} become intertwined through attunement to a single wielder, their innate dunamantic magics coalesce to create a miniature solar system. While you are attuned to both morning star and evening moon, you gain the following benefits:

items: The attack and damage bonus of each weapon increases by 1, to a maximum of +3.
You can use the Orbit and Eclipse properties, described below.

entries.name: Orbit
entries: While holding both evening moon and morning star, you can use a bonus action to cause evening moon to begin orbiting you. As part of this bonus action, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for as long as it remains in orbit, you can make one attack with evening moon against any creature within 15 feet of you. Evening moon orbits you at a distance of two feet, and while in orbit, you can't use it to attack except with this property. You can catch evening moon (no action required) at any point. A creature within 5 feet of you can use its action to make a {@dc 21} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, catching evening moon on a success or taking {@damage 1d6} slashing damage on a failure.

entries.name: Eclipse
entries: As a bonus action while evening moon is orbiting you, you can cause it to hover in front of you instead, casting you in a dark obscuring light and granting you the benefits of the {@spell blur} spell for 1 minute, or until you catch it or make an attack with it. Once this property of the set has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Evening Moon (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which returns to your hand immediately after it is used to make a ranged attack. When you hit a creature with the Shapechanger trait with this weapon, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or instantly revert to its original form and be unable to assume a different form for 1 minute.

entries.name: Dusk
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use an action to create a 10-foot-radius sphere centred on you which is filled with dim light. This sphere lasts for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a bonus action, and it follows you when you move. While in this area, you can try to hide when lightly obscured, and you can take the Hide action as a bonus action. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

entries: After you use the Dusk property, but before the next dusk, you can use an action to cast the {@spell invisibility} spell on yourself. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

You can use the Orbit and Eclipse properties, described below.

entries.name: Orbit
entries: While holding both evening moon and morning star, you can use a bonus action to cause evening moon to begin orbiting you. As part of this bonus action, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for as long as it remains in orbit, you can make one attack with evening moon against any creature within 15 feet of you. Evening moon orbits you at a distance of two feet, and while in orbit, you can't use it to attack except with this property. You can catch evening moon (no action required) at any point. A creature within 5 feet of you can use its action to make a {@dc 21} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, catching evening moon on a success or taking {@damage 1d6} slashing damage on a failure.

entries.name: Eclipse
entries: As a bonus action while evening moon is orbiting you, you can cause it to hover in front of you instead, casting you in a dark obscuring light and granting you the benefits of the {@spell blur} spell for 1 minute, or until you catch it or make an attack with it. Once this property of the set has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dawn
entries: As an action, you can cause morning star to shine a gloriously bright light at all creatures within a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Ultraviolet
entries: After you use the Dawn property, but before it can be used again at the next dawn, you can use an action to cause morning star to shine a focussed beam of ultraviolet light in a 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Eclipse, the Moon and Stars
entries: When the magics of {@item morning star|LALoTT} and {@item evening moon|LALoTT} become intertwined through attunement to a single wielder, their innate dunamantic magics coalesce to create a miniature solar system. While you are attuned to both morning star and evening moon, you gain the following benefits:

items: The attack and damage bonus of each weapon increases by 1, to a maximum of +3.

item.name: Gravity Cube (Rare)
entries: This cube has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can program and throw this cube at a surface you can see within 50 feet of you, where it sticks to the surface, expending charges to warp a 30-foot-radius sphere centred on that point in one of the ways described below. If an effect requires you to expend charges each turn, you expend them at the start of your turn. If you don't, the effect ends.

entries.name: Nullify (1 Charge/Turn)
entries: Gravity in the area is nullified, causing all objects and creatures to drift freely. When the cube activates, each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. A creature that enters the area for the first time on its turn while the effect lasts must also make this saving throw. On a failure, a creature rises vertically, up to 5 feet, and remains suspended in mid-air for the duration of the effect. On a success, a creature clings to the nearest surface. Suspended creatures can only move within the area by pushing off surfaces or other creatures.

entries.name: Intensify (1 Charge/Turn)
entries: Gravity in the area is intensified. For the duration, a creature that moves in the area must spend 3 feet of movement for each foot that it moves, and each creature's jump distance is divided by 3. A flying creature that starts its turn in the area, enters the area for the first time on its turn, or tries to begin flying in the area must make a {@dc 15} Strength check. On a failure, it is safely pulled to the ground and has a flying speed of 0 feet until the start of its next turn.

entries.name: Attract (2 Charges)
entries: Each creature and unsecured object within the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pulled 20 feet towards the cube. Creatures and objects that collide as a result of this each take {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Repel (2 Charges)
entries: Each creature and unsecured object within the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pushed 20 feet directly away from the cube. Creatures and objects that collide as a result of this each take {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage.

item.name: Gravity Cube (Very Rare)
entries: This cube has 9 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can program and throw this cube at a surface you can see within 50 feet of you, where it sticks to the surface, expending charges to warp a 30-foot-radius sphere centred on that point in one of the ways described below. If an effect requires you to expend charges each turn, you expend them at the start of your turn. If you don't, the effect ends.

entries.name: Repel (2 Charges)
entries: Each creature and unsecured object within the area must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be pushed 20 feet directly away from the cube. Creatures and objects that collide as a result of this each take {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Planar Infusion
entries: When you imbibe this potion as an action, your mind, body, and soul become bound with that of an entity from another plane. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table to determine the entity. You gain the statistics of that creature, as per the {@spell polymorph} spell. These effects last until 10 minutes elapse, you fail a saving throw against the {@spell banishment} spell or a similar effect (which banishes the planar entity, not you), or your assumed form is reduced to 0 hit points. {@spell Dispel magic} does not end the potion's effects.

entries.name: Consequences
entries: After the duration elapses, your mind is strained and can even break. Make a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, you take {@damage 4d6} psychic damage and are insane until you finish a long rest. While insane, you can't take actions, can't understand what other creatures say, can't read, and speak only in gibberish. A {@spell greater restoration} spell cast on you ends this effect.

entries.name: Planar Infusion
entries: When you imbibe this potion as an action, your mind, body, and soul become bound with that of an entity from another plane. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table to determine the entity. You gain the statistics of that creature, as per the {@spell polymorph} spell. These effects last until 1 hour elapses, you fail a saving throw against the {@spell banishment} spell or a similar effect (which banishes the planar entity, not you), or your assumed form is reduced to 0 hit points. {@spell Dispel magic} does not end the potion's effects.

entries.name: Consequences
entries: After the duration elapses, your mind is strained and can even break. Make a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, you take {@damage 4d6} psychic damage and are insane until you finish a long rest. While insane, you can't take actions, can't understand what other creatures say, can't read, and speak only in gibberish. A {@spell greater restoration} spell cast on you ends this effect.

entries.name: Hot Ear
entries: Whenever someone talks about you while you wear this earring—regardless of the plane on which you both currently reside—your ear becomes uncomfortably warm. As a bonus action, you can twist the earring, activating it in an attempt to listen in to the conversation. The creature that activated your hot ear must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, an invisible sensor is created within 10 feet of the target that lasts for 10 minutes. For the duration, you can hear through the sensor as if you were there. The sensor moves with the creature that triggered your hot ear, remaining within 10 feet of it for the duration. This property of the earring can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Quantum Earring of the Paranoid (Very Rare)

entries.name: Hot Ear
entries: Whenever someone talks about you while you wear this earring—regardless of the plane on which you both currently reside—your ear becomes uncomfortably warm. As a bonus action, you can twist the earring, activating it in an attempt to listen in to the conversation. The creature that activated your hot ear must succeed on a {@dc 16} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, an invisible sensor is created within 10 feet of the target that lasts for 10 minutes. For the duration, you can hear through the sensor as if you were there. The sensor moves with the creature that triggered your hot ear, remaining within 10 feet of it for the duration. This property of the earring can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Gravity Shard
entries: While holding the weapon, you can use a bonus action to produce a spearhead of force energy, which sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. While this spearhead lasts, the weapon can be used as a quarterstaff or a spear, and on a hit, it deals force damage instead of the weapons' usual bludgeoning or piercing damage. It deals an extra {@damage 1d4} force damage to the first target it hits on each of your turns. In addition, you can use either your Intelligence or Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls you make with this weapon. The energy lasts until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or sheathe the weapon.

entries.name: Gravity Shunt
entries: As an action, you can use the weapon to exert fine control on objects with your telekinetic grip, such as manipulating a simple tool, opening a door or a container, stowing or retrieving an item from an open container, or pouring the contents from a vial, as per the {@spell mage hand} cantrip.

entries.name: Gravity Shard
entries: While holding the weapon, you can use a bonus action to produce a spearhead of force energy, which sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. While this spearhead lasts, the weapon can be used as a quarterstaff or a spear, and on a hit, it deals force damage instead of the weapons' usual bludgeoning or piercing damage, plus an extra {@damage 1d4} force damage. In addition, you can use either your Intelligence or Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls you make with this weapon. The energy lasts until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or sheathe the weapon.

entries.name: Gravity Shunt
entries: As an action, you can use the weapon to telekinetically move creatures or objects within 30 feet of you.

items: {@b Creature.} You can try to move a Medium or smaller creature. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be moved up to 10 feet in any direction, including upward but not beyond the range of this property, where it falls to the ground. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

{@b Object.} You can try to move an object that weighs up to 100 pounds. If the object isn't being worn or carried, you automatically move it up to 10 feet in any direction, but not beyond the range of this property. If the object is worn or carried by a creature, the creature must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw. On a failure, you pull the object away from that creature and can move it up to 10 feet in any direction, where it falls to the ground.

entries: In addition, you can use an action to exert fine control on objects with your telekinetic grip, such as manipulating a simple tool, opening a door or a container, stowing or retrieving an item from an open container, or pouring the contents from a vial, as per the {@spell mage hand} cantrip.

entries.name: Gravity Shard
entries: While holding the weapon, you can use a bonus action to produce a spearhead of force energy, which sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. While this spearhead lasts, the weapon can be used as a quarterstaff or a spear, and on a hit, it deals force damage instead of the weapons' usual bludgeoning or piercing damage, plus an extra {@damage 1d6} force damage. In addition, you can use either your Intelligence or Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls you make with this weapon. The energy lasts until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or sheathe the weapon.

entries.name: Gravity Shunt
entries: As an action, you can use the weapon to telekinetically move creatures or objects within 60 feet of you.

items: {@b Creature.} You can try to move a Large or smaller creature. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be moved up to 20 feet in any direction, including upward but not beyond the range of this property, where it falls to the ground. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

{@b Object.} You can try to move an object that weighs up to 400 pounds. If the object isn't being worn or carried, you automatically move it up to 20 feet in any direction, but not beyond the range of this property. If the object is worn or carried by a creature, the creature must make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw. On a failure, you pull the object away from that creature and can move it up to 20 feet in any direction, where it falls to the ground.

entries: In addition, you can use an action to exert fine control on objects with your telekinetic grip, such as manipulating a simple tool, opening a door or a container, stowing or retrieving an item from an open container, or pouring the contents from a vial, as per the {@spell mage hand} cantrip.

item.name: Starfallen Force Shield (Rare)
entries: This shield has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Repulsion
entries: When a creature misses you with a melee attack while you hold this shield, you can use your reaction to pull a trigger, expending 1 charge and sending a pulse of energy through the attacking creature. The creature must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature is knocked {@condition prone}, and is disarmed of the weapon with which it made the attack, or of its spellcasting focus or component if it was a melee spell attack. The item flies 15 feet away in a random direction (roll a {@dice d8} to determine the direction where a 1 = North, 2 = Northeast, etc.)

entries.name: Starfield Bulwark
entries: As an action, you can twist the handle of the shield, expending 3 charges and causing it to deploy in a 5-foot-high barrier of force energy up to 25 feet long. Nothing can physically pass through the wall; a creature that attempts to run through or climb over the barrier takes {@damage 2d6} force damage and must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. Creatures directly behind the barrier have three-quarters cover against attacks that originate from the other side of the barrier. Any attack that misses a creature directly behind the barrier automatically hits the barrier, which has 40 hit points. The barrier remains in place until 1 minute elapses, a creature uses its bonus action to pick up the barrier on the side from which it was deployed, it is hit by the {@spell disintegrate} spell, or it loses all its hit points.

item.name: Starfallen Force Shield (Very Rare)
entries: This shield has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Repulsion
entries: When a creature misses you with a melee attack while you hold this shield, you can use your reaction to pull a trigger, expending 1 charge and sending a pulse of energy through the attacking creature. The creature must make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature is knocked {@condition prone}, and is disarmed of the weapon with which it made the attack, or of its spellcasting focus or component if it was a melee spell attack. The item flies 15 feet away in a random direction (roll a {@dice d8} to determine the direction where a 1 = North, 2 = Northeast, etc.). If the target fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it is also {@condition stunned} until the start of its next turn.

entries.name: Psionic Deflection
entries: While spinning the twinblade as part of its {@b Special: Whirl} property, whenever a creature misses you with a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to divert the projectile back to its origin. Make an attack roll against the creature using your Intelligence modifier. On a hit, the creature takes the damage from its own attack.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Psionic Shot
entries: If you attune to this item, when you make an attack with this weapon as part of the Attack action, you can mime pulling back on it as if it were a bow, causing an arrow of shimmering psionic energy to materialise, and make a ranged weapon attack, using the twinblade as a shortbow with which you are proficient. You can use either your Dexterity or Intelligence modifier for attack and damage rolls made with the twinblade in this way, and it deals psychic damage instead of piercing damage.

item.name: Starrunner's Twinblade (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Psionic Deflection
entries: While spinning the twinblade as part of its {@b Special: Whirl} property, whenever a creature misses you with a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to divert the projectile back to its origin. Make an attack roll against the creature using your Intelligence modifier. On a hit, the creature takes the damage from its own attack.

You can use either your Dexterity or Intelligence modifier for attack and damage rolls made with the twinblade in this way, and it deals psychic damage instead of piercing damage.
