entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Psionic Deflection
entries: While spinning the twinblade as part of its {@b Special: Whirl} property, whenever a creature misses you with a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to divert the projectile back to its origin. Make an attack roll against the creature using your Intelligence modifier. On a hit, the creature takes the damage from its own attack.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Psionic Shot
entries: If you attune to this item, when you make an attack with this weapon as part of the Attack action, you can mime pulling back on it as if it were a bow, causing an arrow of shimmering psionic energy to materialise, and make a ranged weapon attack, using the twinblade as a shortbow with which you are proficient. You can use either your Dexterity or Intelligence modifier for attack and damage rolls made with the twinblade in this way, and it deals psychic damage instead of piercing damage.

item.name: Translux Candle (Rare)
entries: When found, a translux candle has {@dice 1d4} charges.

entries.name: Homebound
entries: As an action, you can light the candle, expending 1 charge and causing it to cast a dim, purple light in a 10-foot-radius sphere. This light slowly brightens over the course of a minute. At the end of the minute, an eruption of glowing purple sigils forms a teleportation circle in the lit area. The circle appears on initiative 20 of the 10th round after the candle was lit and lasts until initiative 20 of the following round. Each creature in the area when the circle forms, or that moves into the area while the circle is active, is teleported to the destination teleportation circle where the candle was crafted. When the teleportation circle ends, the candle also teleports to the destination circle.

A creature can use its action to snuff out a candle within its reach that isn't being held by another creature. If the candle is held by another creature, the creature trying to snuff out the candle must succeed on a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check contested by the holding creature's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}). If the candle is snuffed out before the minute elapses, the teleportation circle never forms, and any expended charges are wasted.

item.name: Translux Candle (Very Rare)
entries: When found, a translux candle has {@dice 1d4 + 2} charges.

entries.name: Homebound
entries: As an action, you can light the candle, expending 1 charge and causing it to cast a dim, purple light in a 10-foot-radius sphere. This light slowly brightens over the course of a minute. At the end of the minute, an eruption of glowing purple sigils forms a teleportation circle in the lit area. The circle appears on initiative 20 of the 10th round after the candle was lit and lasts until initiative 20 of the following round. Each creature in the area when the circle forms, or that moves into the area while the circle is active, is teleported to the destination teleportation circle where the candle was crafted. When the teleportation circle ends, the candle also teleports to the destination circle.

A creature can use its action to snuff out a candle within its reach that isn't being held by another creature. If the candle is held by another creature, the creature trying to snuff out the candle must succeed on a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check contested by the holding creature's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}). If the candle is snuffed out before the minute elapses, the teleportation circle never forms, and any expended charges are wasted.

item.name: Translux Candle (Legendary)
entries: When found, a translux candle has {@dice 2d4 + 1} charges.

entries.name: Homebound
entries: As an action, you can light the candle, expending 1 charge and causing it to cast a dim, purple light in a 10-foot-radius sphere. This light slowly brightens over the course of a minute. At the end of the minute, an eruption of glowing purple sigils forms a teleportation circle in the lit area. The circle appears on initiative 20 of the 10th round after the candle was lit and lasts until initiative 20 of the following round. Each creature in the area when the circle forms, or that moves into the area while the circle is active, is teleported to the destination teleportation circle where the candle was crafted. When you first light the candle, you can designate any number of creatures which can use the teleportation circle. Any creatures not designated can't be teleported by it. When the teleportation circle ends, the candle also teleports to the destination circle.

A creature can use its action to snuff out a candle within its reach that isn't being held by another creature. If the candle is held by another creature, the creature trying to snuff out the candle must succeed on a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check contested by the holding creature's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}). If the candle is snuffed out before the minute elapses, the teleportation circle never forms, and any expended charges are wasted.

item.name: Arcanowax (Rare)
entries: When found, an arcanowax candle has {@dice 2d4 + 1} charges.

entries.name: Arcanowax Circle
entries: As an action, you can light the candle, expending 1 charge and causing it to shed bright light in a 10-foot-radius, 20-foot-tall cylinder for 1 minute. The area of the light replicates the effects of the {@spell magic circle} spell, except it can't affect elementals. If the candle is moved in such a way that would result in one of those creatures entering the area of the light, the candle is snuffed out.

A creature can use its action to snuff out a candle within its reach that isn't being held by another creature. If the candle is held by another creature, the creature trying to snuff out the candle must succeed on its choice of a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check contested by the holding creature's choice of Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}).
The candle's effects end immediately when the candle is snuffed out.

item.name: Arcanowax (Uncommon)
entries: The candle is made of a cheap, fast-burning wax and the fat of only one creature type. When found, an arcanowax candle has {@dice 1d4 + 1} charges. When lit, the candle affects only the creature type whose fat was used to craft it. If the candle is found, the GM chooses a single creature type it works against or rolls a {@dice d4} and consults the table below to randomly determine it.

entries.name: Arcanowax Circle
entries: As an action, you can light the candle, expending 1 charge and causing it to shed bright light in a 10-foot-radius, 20-foot-tall cylinder for 1 minute. The area of the light replicates the effects of the {@spell magic circle} spell against the chosen creature type. If the candle is moved in such a way that would result in one of those creatures entering the area of the light, the candle is snuffed out.

A creature can use its action to snuff out a candle within its reach that isn't being held by another creature. If the candle is held by another creature, the creature trying to snuff out the candle must succeed on its choice of a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check contested by the holding creature's choice of Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}).
The candle's effects end immediately when the candle is snuffed out.

entries.name: False Authority
entries: This badge is enchanted to appear as a document that gives its holder the necessary authority they require to outrank the observer. You can use a bonus action to show this badge to a creature to gain {@b advantage} on {@skill Deception} checks made to convince it that you're important. A creature that spends at least 1 minute taking a close look at the badge can see through the illusion with a successful {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check. While flashing the badge might convince indifferent creatures, creatures that are suspicious of you might demand more time to closely review your documentation for legitimacy.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to this item, you gain a +1 bonus to AC and can use the Take Cover property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Take Cover
entries: As a bonus action, you can tap the badge and shout the command words "take cover", causing the badge to become a floating shield that follows you for 1 minute, or until you use a bonus action to return it to its normal form. If you aren't wearing a shield, you gain a +5 bonus to AC for the duration. This effect ends early if you make an attack or cast a spell that affects an enemy creature. After you use this property, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: False Authority
entries: This badge is enchanted to appear as a document that gives its holder the necessary authority they require to outrank the observer. You can use a bonus action to show this badge to a creature to gain {@b advantage} on {@skill Deception} checks made to convince it that you're important. A creature that spends at least 1 minute taking a close look at the badge can see through the illusion with a successful {@dc 13} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check. While flashing the badge might convince indifferent creatures, creatures that are suspicious of you might demand more time to closely review your documentation for legitimacy.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to this item, you can use the Take Cover property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Take Cover
entries: As a bonus action, you can tap the badge and shout the command words "take cover", causing the badge to become a floating shield that follows you for 1 minute, or until you use a bonus action to return it to its normal form. If you aren't wearing a shield, you gain a +5 bonus to AC for the duration. This effect ends early if you make an attack or cast a spell that affects an enemy creature. After you use this property, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: False Authority
entries: This badge is enchanted to appear as a document that gives its holder the necessary authority they require to outrank the observer. You can use a bonus action to show this badge to a creature to gain {@b advantage} on {@skill Deception} checks made to convince it that you're important. A creature that spends at least 1 minute taking a close look at the badge can see through the illusion with a successful {@dc 16} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check. While flashing the badge might convince indifferent creatures, creatures that are suspicious of you might demand more time to closely review your documentation for legitimacy.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to this item, you gain a +1 bonus to AC and can use the Take Cover and Voice of Authority properties while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Take Cover
entries: As a bonus action, you can tap the badge and shout the command words "take cover", causing the badge to become a floating shield that follows you for 1 minute, or until you use a bonus action to return it to its normal form. If you aren't wearing a shield, you gain a +5 bonus to AC for the duration. This effect ends early if you make an attack or cast a spell that affects an enemy creature. After you use this property, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: OA: Voice of Authority
entries: While holding the badge, you can cast the {@spell command} spell (save {@dc 16}). If a creature believes you to be important due to the effects of the False Authority property, it has {@b disadvantage} on the saving throw; after the spell ends, it sees through the ruse. After you use this property, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 3 or less, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Ka-Ma-Ra (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 10 charges. As an action, you can supply the imp within it with up to 10 gp worth of paint (in a variety of colours), brimstone, and sulphur, causing the item to regain 1 expended charge for each 1 gp worth of materials provided.

entries.name: Shoot
entries: As an action, you can point the Ka-Ma-Ra in a direction of your choice and expend 1 charge to pull the trigger, flashing bright light in a 20-foot cone. At the start of your next turn, the imp hands you a small, 3" by 2" picture of whatever the Ka-Ma-Ra was pointing at. If you move or use a reaction between taking the action and the start of your next turn, the picture is blurry; you must remain perfectly still while the imp is at work. If the Ka-Ma-Ra is left unused for too long, the imp within may expend charges of its own accord to produce risqué images of other imps in an attempt to ward off boredom.

item.name: Ka-Ma-Ra (Common)
entries: The imp within the item is excessively lazy and refuses to paint more than one picture per day.

entries.name: Shoot
entries: As an action, you can point the Ka-Ma-Ra in a direction of your choice and pull the trigger, flashing bright light in a 20-foot cone. At the start of your next turn, the imp hands you a small, 3" by 2" picture of whatever the Ka-Ma-Ra was pointing at. If you move or use a reaction between taking the action and the start of your next turn, the picture is blurry; you must remain perfectly still while the imp is at work. Once the Shoot property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Investigation
entries: While looking through this magnifying glass, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Investigation} checks made to find clues and hidden messages within writing.

entries.name: Tracking
entries: While looking through this magnifying glass, you can see the tracks of all creatures that travelled through the area in the last 24 hours as glowing imprints, unless such a creature's passage was disguised by magic, and have {@b advantage} on ability checks made to identify the creatures that left those tracks.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: Lenz is a sentient chaotic good magic item with an Intelligence of 10, Wisdom of 13, and Charisma of 17. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to 60 feet, and it can speak and understand Common.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Lenz is charismatic and slightly distracting, taking great joy in pointing out entirely obvious and irrelevant facts. While holding Lenz, you can make a {@dc 13} Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check to ask it for extra help. On a success, its tips are helpful and grant you {@b advantage} on ability checks made to gather information on creatures, objects, and locations through observation for 10 minutes. On a failure, it gets distracted and starts telling a tale, such as the time it mistook a duck's footprint for that of a venomous dire platypus, giving you {@b disadvantage} on the same ability checks for 10 minutes. Once Lenz has been successfully persuaded to help, any attempts to ask for help from it again before the next dawn automatically fail.

item.name: Lenz the Inspector (Very Rare)
entries: While holding the magnifying glass, you can cast the {@spell scrying} spell (save {@dc 16}). Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Investigation
entries: While looking through this magnifying glass, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Investigation} checks made to find clues and hidden messages within writing.

entries.name: Tracking
entries: While looking through this magnifying glass, you can see the tracks of all creatures that travelled through the area in the last 24 hours as glowing imprints, unless such a creature's passage was disguised by magic, and have {@b advantage} on ability checks made to identify the creatures that left those tracks.

entries.name: Hindsight
entries: As an action, you can spin a small dial on the handle of the magnifying glass to activate it for 10 minutes, choosing a point in time up to 72 hours in the past. For the duration, whenever you look through the magnifying glass, you can see the space where you are as if you were at the same space at the chosen point in time. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: Lenz is a sentient chaotic good magic item with an Intelligence of 10, Wisdom of 13, and Charisma of 17. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to 60 feet, and it can speak and understand Common.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Lenz is charismatic and slightly distracting, taking great joy in pointing out entirely obvious and irrelevant facts. While holding Lenz, you can make a {@dc 16} Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check to ask it for extra help. On a success, its tips are helpful and grant you {@b advantage} on ability checks made to gather information on creatures, objects, and locations through observation for 10 minutes. On a failure, it gets distracted and starts telling a tale, such as the time it mistook a duck's footprint for that of a venomous dire platypus, giving you {@b disadvantage} on the same ability checks for 10 minutes. Once Lenz has been successfully persuaded to help, any attempts to ask for help from it again before the next dawn automatically fail.

item.name: Origami Beetle
entries: Over the course of a minute, you can fold this enchanted paper into the shape of a 1-inch beetle, activating its magical potential.

entries.name: Dropping Eaves
entries: With extremely acute hearing and camouflage capabilities, the origami beetle is an effective tool in covert eavesdropping. When you craft this origami animal, you can give it a short set of instructions including a destination and a wait time. For example, you could tell the beetle to "land underneath that bench, wait five minutes, then return here." The beetle has a flying speed of 15 feet, AC 10, and 1 hit point. It records all sound within 20 feet of it for up to 10 minutes. While waiting, it blends into its surroundings, and can only be noticed with a successful {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check. Upon returning to you, the beetle repeats all the sounds it recorded, shifted up two octaves.
After 1 hour elapses, the origami beetle becomes a mundane, folded piece of paper.

item.name: Origami Butterfly
entries: Over the course of a minute, you can fold this enchanted paper into the shape of a 6-inch-wingspan butterfly, activating its magical potential.

entries.name: Fluttering Diversion
entries: Slow and colourful, this butterfly gently wafts its way in a direction you point it, attempting to stay just out of arm's reach. When you craft this origami animal, you can give it a short set of instructions such as "travel along the corridor and take the first right, stopping just above the guard." The butterfly has a flying speed of 15 feet, AC 10, and 1 hit point. A creature that sees the butterfly while the butterfly is within 5 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or become distracted and try to catch the butterfly, until the butterfly is more than 5 feet away from it. Each turn that a creature is distracted by the butterfly, it must spend its movement following the butterfly and use its action to make a {@dc 13} Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check trying to catch it. A creature that is distracted in this way has {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks.

If a creature that is distracted in this way detects the presence of another creature it deems hostile, it stops being distracted. If the butterfly is caught, or after 1 hour elapses, its magical effects cease and it becomes a mundane, folded piece of paper.

item.name: Origami Gold-Leaf Dragon
entries: Over the course of a minute, you can fold this enchanted, golden paper into a dragon, activating its magical potential in one of the following ways:

entries.name: Draconic Familiar
entries: Proud and brilliant, the 6-inch-long origami dragon is usually the Bureau Director's personal assistant. When you craft this origami animal, it becomes your familiar, as per the {@spell find familiar} spell, with the following changes:

items: It uses the statistics of a {@creature gold dragon wyrmling|MM}, but is a Tiny construct with 10 hit points, a flying speed of 80 feet, a +8 Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) modifier, and a +14 Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) modifier.
There is no limit to the distance at which you can communicate with the dragon telepathically or share its senses.
As an action, the dragon can magically turn {@condition invisible} until it attacks or its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell).

The dragon doesn't have a breath weapon and can make no attacks other than the following Weaken action: {@b Weaken}. {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}1 slashing damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw or have {@b disadvantage} on Strength-based attack rolls, Strength checks, and Strength saving throws until the end of its next turn.

entries: After 1 hour, the dragon reverts to a sheet of paper and can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Get-Away Dragon
entries: When you fold this paper, you can speak its command word, causing the origami shape to manifest into a Large dragon made of paper. This creature has the statistics of a {@creature young gold dragon|MM}. The dragon is friendly to you and obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you), and rolls its own initiative in combat. If you don't issue any commands to the dragon, it defends itself from hostile creatures but otherwise takes no actions. After 1 hour, or if the dragon drops to 0 hit points, the paper loses its magic and shrinks back down into a mundane, folded piece of paper.

item.name: Origami Swallow
entries: Over the course of a minute, you can fold this enchanted paper into the shape of a 6-inch-wingspan swallow, activating its magical potential.

entries.name: Improved Familiar
entries: Swift and observant, the origami swallow is an espionage tool available only to the upper echelons of the Bureau's secret agents. When you craft this origami animal, it becomes your familiar, as per the {@spell find familiar} spell, with the following changes:

items: It uses the statistics of a {@creature hawk|MM}, but is a construct with a flying speed of 120 feet and a +6 Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) modifier.
There is no limit to the distance at which you can communicate with the swallow telepathically.

It can use its action to make a Talons attack. If it hits a creature with eyes with this attack, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.

entries: The origami swallow loses its magic 1 hour after being activated, becoming a mundane, folded piece of paper.

item.name: Origami Unicorn
entries: Over the course of a minute, you can fold this enchanted paper into a unicorn, activating its magical potential in one of the following ways:

entries.name: Ethereal Messenger
entries: Able to traverse through the Border Ethereal, this 6-inch-long device is used by agents of the Bureau to send missives back home while remaining undercover. While this origami animal is within 5 feet of you, you can speak a message no longer than twenty five words, which is magically transcribed onto the unicorn's paper, as well as the name of a creature with whom you are familiar. Once completed, the unicorn enters the Border Ethereal, galloping along its shores until it enters the target creature's plane of existence, at a point near to the creature. The recipient can unfold the unicorn to read the message and then re-fold it, giving a response no more than twenty-five words long, which the unicorn delivers to its origin.

You can send the message across any distance and even to other planes of existence that border the Ethereal Plane, but if the target is on a different plane than you, there is a 5 percent chance that the message doesn't arrive and the unicorn is lost. Each message takes 10 minutes to travel to the destination, allowing a total of three messages to be sent back and forth per day. After 1 hour, the unicorn reverts to a sheet of paper and can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Celestial Steed
entries: When you fold this paper, you can speak its command word, causing the origami shape to manifest into a Large unicorn made of paper. This creature has the statistics of a {@creature unicorn|MM} with a flying speed equal to its walking speed and doesn't have legendary actions. The unicorn is friendly to you, obeys your telepathic commands (no action required by you), and it rolls its own initiative in combat. If you don't issue any commands to the unicorn, it defends itself from hostile creatures but otherwise takes no actions. After 1 hour, or if the unicorn drops to 0 hit points, the paper loses its magic and shrinks back down into a mundane, folded piece of paper.

item.name: Sleuth's Shadowcoat (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 9 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shadow Stealth
entries: While wearing this coat in dim light or darkness, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge and turn {@condition invisible} until the start of your next turn. This invisibility ends if you enter an area of bright light, or if you attack or cast a spell. If you are still {@condition invisible} at the start of your next turn, you can choose to immediately expend 1 charge (no action required) to prolong the effect for another turn.

entries.name: Hide in Plain Sight
entries: While wearing the coat, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell chameleon skin|LALoTT} spell on yourself.

entries.name: Disappearing Act
entries: While wearing the coat, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell fog cloud} spell centred on yourself. When you cast the spell in this way, it only lasts up to 1 minute, and you can immediately move up to half your speed.

item.name: Sleuth's Shadowcoat (Rare)
entries: This item has 12 charges and regains {@dice 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shadow Stealth
entries: While wearing this coat in dim light, darkness, or bright light that isn't sunlight, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge and turn {@condition invisible} until the start of your next turn. This invisibility ends if you attack or cast a spell. If you are still {@condition invisible} at the start of your next turn, you can choose to immediately expend 1 charge (no action required) to prolong the effect for another turn.

entries.name: Hide in Plain Sight
entries: While wearing the coat, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell chameleon skin|LALoTT} spell on yourself.

entries.name: Disappearing Act
entries: While wearing the coat, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell fog cloud} spell centred on yourself. When you cast the spell in this way, it only lasts up to 1 minute, and you can immediately move up to half your speed.

item.name: Sleuth's Shadowcoat (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 14 charges and regains {@dice 1d10 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shadow Stealth
entries: While wearing this coat in dim light, darkness, or bright light that isn't sunlight, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge and turn {@condition invisible} until the start of your next turn. If you are still {@condition invisible} at the start of your next turn, you can choose to immediately expend 1 charge (no action required) to prolong the effect for another turn.

entries.name: Hide in Plain Sight
entries: While wearing the coat, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell chameleon skin|LALoTT} spell on yourself.

entries.name: Disappearing Act
entries: While wearing the coat, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell fog cloud} spell centred on yourself. When you cast the spell in this way, it only lasts up to 1 minute, doesn't require concentration, and you can immediately move up to half your speed. You can expend an additional 3 charges to cast the {@spell gaseous form} spell on yourself immediately before you move (no additional action required).

entries.name: Stun Baton
entries: You can use this wand as a simple melee weapon with the {@itemProperty F|Finesse property}, dealing 1 piercing damage on a hit. Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack using the wand, you can cause it to discharge in one of the following ways:

items: {@b Sap.} The creature must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its speed is reduced to 0 feet and it can't take bonus actions or reactions until the end of its next turn. A creature immune to being {@condition stunned} automatically succeeds on this saving throw.
{@b Zap.} The creature takes an extra {@damage 2d8} lightning damage and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn unless it is immune to being {@condition stunned}.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Stunspell
entries: If you are a spellcaster, you can attune to this item. You know the {@spell hold person} and {@spell lightning wave|LALoTT} spells, and you always have them prepared. While holding the wand, you can cast one of those spells without expending a spell slot, using your spell save DC. Once this property of the wand has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Stun Baton
entries: You can use this wand as a simple melee weapon with the {@itemProperty F|Finesse property}, dealing 1 piercing damage on a hit. Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack using the wand, you can cause it to discharge in one of the following ways:

items: {@b Sap.} The creature must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its speed is reduced to 0 feet and it can't take bonus actions or reactions until the end of its next turn. A creature immune to being {@condition stunned} automatically succeeds on this saving throw.
{@b Zap.} The creature takes an extra {@damage 3d8} lightning damage and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn unless it is immune to being {@condition stunned}.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Stunspell
entries: If you are a spellcaster, you can attune to this item. You know the {@spell hold person}, {@spell lightning wave|LALoTT}, and {@spell stunbolt|LALoTT} spells, and you always have them prepared. While holding the wand, you can cast one of those spells without expending a spell slot, using your spell save DC. Once you use the Stunspell property, roll a {@dice d6}. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Stun Baton
entries: You can use this wand as a simple melee weapon with the {@itemProperty F|Finesse property}, dealing 1 piercing damage on a hit. Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack using the wand, you can cause it to discharge in one of the following ways:

items: {@b Sap.} The creature must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its speed is reduced to 0 feet and it can't take bonus actions or reactions until the end of its next turn. A creature immune to being {@condition stunned} automatically succeeds on this saving throw.
{@b Zap.} The creature takes an extra {@damage 4d8} lightning damage and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn unless it is immune to being {@condition stunned}.

entries.name: Optional Attunement: Stunspell
entries: If you are a spellcaster, you can attune to this item. You know the {@spell hold person}, {@spell lightning wave|LALoTT}, {@spell stunbolt|LALoTT}, and {@spell hold monster} spells, and you always have them prepared. While holding the wand, you can cast one of those spells without expending a spell slot, using your spell save DC. Once you use the Stunspell property, roll a {@dice d8}. If the result is equal to or lower than the spell's level, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Banner of Bloody Transfusion (Rare)
entries: Crafted from a tough, stain-proof, white fabric, this velvet-trimmed banner measures 5 feet high and 3 feet wide. An embroidered depiction of a blood sac becomes a three-dimensional sac containing a blood-based potion while the banner is unfurled. It can be attached to a 10-foot pole to serve as a standard. Furling or unfurling the banner requires an action. While unfurled, the banner has the following properties:

entries.name: Mark of Vitality
entries: You can touch the banner as a bonus action, causing it to emanate vitality. For 10 minutes or until the banner is furled, you and your allies have {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws while within 20 feet of the banner. Once this property of the banner has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Stabilising Standard
entries: While within 20 feet of the banner, you can use a bonus action to stabilise a creature you can see that has 0 hit points and is within 20 feet of the banner.

entries.name: Transfusion
entries: You can touch the banner and mentally command its transfusion hose to connect with one willing or {@condition unconscious} creature within 20 feet of the banner, choosing one of the following effects:

items.name: Heal
entries: As a bonus action, you cause the creature to regain {@dice 4d6} hit points.

items.name: Restore
entries: As an action, you cast the {@spell lesser restoration} spell on the creature.
After this property of the banner has been activated, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 3 or lower, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Banner of Bloody Transfusion (Legendary)
entries: Crafted from a tough, stain-proof, white fabric, this velvet-trimmed banner measures 5 feet high and 3 feet wide. An embroidered depiction of a blood sac becomes a three-dimensional sac containing a blood-based potion while the banner is unfurled. It can be attached to a 10-foot pole to serve as a standard. Furling or unfurling the banner requires an action. While unfurled, the banner has the following properties:

entries.name: Mark of Vitality
entries: You can touch the banner as a bonus action, causing it to emanate vitality. For 1 hour or until the banner is furled, you and your allies have {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws while within 30 feet of the banner. Once this property of the banner has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Stabilising Standard
entries: While within 30 feet of the banner, you can use a bonus action to stabilise a creature you can see that has 0 hit points and is within 30 feet of the banner.

entries.name: Transfusion
entries: You can touch the banner and mentally command its transfusion hose to connect with one willing or {@condition unconscious} creature within 30 feet of the banner, choosing one of the following effects:

items.name: Heal
entries: As a bonus action, you cause the creature to regain {@dice 8d6} hit points.

items.name: Restore
entries: As an action, you cast the {@spell greater restoration} spell on the creature.
After this property of the banner has been activated, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 3 or lower, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Bloodletter Opener (Uncommon)
entries: This weapon has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Draw Blood
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can use it to draw blood from a willing creature within your reach. Alternatively, when you hit a creature that has blood with a weapon attack using the bloodletter opener, you can draw some of its blood into the weapon's reservoir. While the weapon contains blood, you can use a bonus action to flick a switch on its handle and expend 1 charge to either augment or corrupt the blood. Unused blood in the dagger vanishes at dawn.

items.name: Augment
entries: The blood becomes enhanced with healing properties. As an action, you can inject the blood into the creature from whom it was extracted. The creature regains {@dice 2d4} hit points and its speed increases by 15 feet for 1 minute.

items.name: Corrupt
entries: The blood becomes corrupted into a poison bespoke to the creature from whom it was extracted. When you hit that creature with an attack using the weapon, you can inject the bloodletter opener's contents into the creature. The creature must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@damage 2d8} poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}.

item.name: Bloodletter Opener (Rare)
entries: This weapon has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Draw Blood
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can use it to draw blood from a willing creature within your reach. Alternatively, when you hit a creature that has blood with a weapon attack using the bloodletter opener, you can draw some of its blood into the weapon's reservoir. While the weapon contains blood, you can use a bonus action to flick a switch on its handle and expend 1 charge to either augment or corrupt the blood. Unused blood in the dagger vanishes at dawn.

items.name: Augment
entries: The blood becomes enhanced with healing properties. As an action, you can inject the blood into the creature from whom it was extracted. The creature regains {@dice 4d4} hit points and its speed increases by 15 feet for 1 minute.

items.name: Corrupt
entries: The blood becomes corrupted into a poison bespoke to the creature from whom it was extracted. When you hit that creature with an attack using the weapon, you can inject the bloodletter opener's contents into the creature. The creature must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@damage 4d8} poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}.

item.name: Bloodletter Opener (Very Rare)
entries: This weapon has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Draw Blood
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can use it to draw blood from a willing creature within your reach. Alternatively, when you hit a creature that has blood with a weapon attack using the bloodletter opener, you can draw some of its blood into the weapon's reservoir. While the weapon contains blood, you can use a bonus action to flick a switch on its handle and expend 1 charge to either augment or corrupt the blood. Unused blood in the dagger vanishes at dawn.

items.name: Augment
entries: The blood becomes enhanced with healing properties. As an action, you can inject the blood into the creature from whom it was extracted. The creature regains {@dice 4d4} hit points and gains the benefits of the {@spell haste} spell until the end of its next turn. The creature doesn't suffer the wave of lethargy once the effect ends.

items.name: Corrupt
entries: The blood becomes corrupted into a poison bespoke to the creature from whom it was extracted. When you hit that creature with an attack using the weapon, you can inject the bloodletter opener's contents into the creature. The creature must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@damage 6d8} poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. It can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}.

entries.name: Illumination
entries: As a bonus action while you wear the cowl, you can cause it to shed bright light in a radius of up to 20 feet and dim light for an additional number of feet equal to the chosen radius, or extinguish the light.

entries.name: Psionics
entries: While you wear this cowl, you can cast the following spells (save {@dc 13}): {@spell command} and {@spell comprehend languages}. After you cast a spell in this way, roll a {@dice d4}. If you roll equal to or lower than the spell's level, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Illumination
entries: As a bonus action while you wear the cowl, you can cause it to shed bright light in a radius of up to 20 feet and dim light for an additional number of feet equal to the chosen radius, or extinguish the light.

entries.name: Psionics
entries: While you wear this cowl, you can cast the following spells (save {@dc 15}): {@spell command}, {@spell comprehend languages}, and {@spell detect thoughts}. After you cast a spell in this way, roll a {@dice d8}. If you roll equal to or lower than the spell's level, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Illumination
entries: As a bonus action while you wear the cowl, you can cause it to shed bright light in a radius of up to 20 feet and dim light for an additional number of feet equal to the chosen radius, or extinguish the light.

entries.name: Psionics
entries: While you wear this cowl, you can cast the following spells (save {@dc 16}): {@spell command}, {@spell comprehend languages}, {@spell detect thoughts}, {@spell confusion}, and {@spell dominate person}. After you cast a spell in this way, roll a {@dice d8}. If you roll equal to or lower than the spell's level, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Telepathic Shroud
entries: While wearing the cowl, you are immune to any effect that would sense your emotions, read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, or know your alignment.

item.name: Cranium Rat Cowl (Legendary)
entries: While wearing the cowl, you have {@b advantage} on saving throws against divination spells.

entries.name: Illumination
entries: As a bonus action while you wear the cowl, you can cause it to shed bright light in a radius of up to 20 feet and dim light for an additional number of feet equal to the chosen radius, or extinguish the light.

entries.name: Psionics
entries: While you wear this cowl, you can cast the following spells (save {@dc 17}): {@spell command}, {@spell comprehend languages}, {@spell detect thoughts}, {@spell confusion}, {@spell dominate person}, and {@spell dominate monster}. After you cast a spell in this way, roll a {@dice d10}. If you roll equal to or lower than the spell's level, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Telepathic Shroud
entries: While wearing the cowl, you are immune to any effect that would sense your emotions, read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, or know your alignment.

item.name: Deathfibrillator (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it in your hand, unarmed strikes you make that hit with that hand deal piercing damage instead of bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Clear!
entries: While holding a deathfibrillator, you can expend 1 or more charges in the following ways:

items.name: Zap (1 charge)
entries: You squeeze this item's handle when you hit a target with an unarmed strike using it, dealing an extra {@damage 1d4} lightning damage.

items.name: First Aid (3 charges)
entries: As an action, you touch a {@condition stunned} or {@condition paralysed} creature within your reach, sending a wave of electrostatic energy through it. The creature takes {@damage 2d8} lightning damage and is no longer {@condition stunned} or {@condition paralysed}.

item.name: Deathfibrillator (Rare)
entries: This item has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it in your hand, unarmed strikes you make that hit with that hand deal piercing damage instead of bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Clear!
entries: While holding a deathfibrillator, you can expend 1 or more charges in the following ways:

items.name: Zap (1 charge)
entries: You squeeze this item's handle when you hit a target with an unarmed strike using it, dealing an extra {@damage 3d4} lightning damage.

items.name: First Aid (3 charges)
entries: As an action, you touch a {@condition stunned} or {@condition paralysed} creature within your reach, sending a wave of electrostatic energy through it. The creature takes {@damage 2d8} lightning damage and is no longer {@condition stunned} or {@condition paralysed}.

items.name: Electrocute (6 charges)
entries: As a bonus action, you can hold the deathfibrillator's trigger, causing charge to build up. The next creature you hit with an unarmed strike using this item before the end of your turn must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes {@damage 2d8} lightning damage and is {@condition paralysed} until the end of its next turn. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition paralysed}.

entries.name: Fiendrenaline
entries: As a bonus action, you can jab this apparatus into a willing creature within your reach, dealing {@damage 1d4} piercing damage to it. For the next 1 minute, the creature's speed is doubled, it gains a +2 bonus to its Strength and Dexterity scores (up to a maximum of 22), and it ignores the effects of the {@condition exhaustion} condition. When these effects end, the creature gains 1 level of {@condition exhaustion} and must succeed on a {@dc 8} Charisma saving throw or be possessed by a fiendish spirit for 1 hour. During that time, the creature is {@condition incapacitated}, and the fiend controls its body but doesn't deprive it of awareness. The fiend can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, and it retains its alignment. It otherwise uses the creature's statistics, but doesn't gain access to its knowledge, class features, or proficiencies. After the possession ends, the fiend returns to its plane of origin. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn, when the device refills itself with refined fiend blood.

entries.name: Fiendrenaline
entries: As a bonus action, you can jab this apparatus into a willing creature within your reach, dealing {@damage 1d4} piercing damage to it. For the next 10 minutes, the creature's speed is doubled, it gains a +4 bonus to its Strength and Dexterity scores (up to a maximum of 24), and it ignores the effects of the {@condition exhaustion} condition. When these effects end, the creature gains 1 level of {@condition exhaustion} and must succeed on a {@dc 10} Charisma saving throw or be possessed by a fiendish spirit for 1 hour. During that time, the creature is {@condition incapacitated}, and the fiend controls its body but doesn't deprive it of awareness. The fiend can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, and it retains its alignment. It otherwise uses the creature's statistics, but doesn't gain access to its knowledge, class features, or proficiencies. After the possession ends, the fiend returns to its plane of origin. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn, when the device refills itself with refined fiend blood.

entries.name: Healping Hand
entries: As an action while holding the healper, you can speak the command phrase "Healp! Healp!" followed by one of the commands below. The bundle of alchemical paraphernalia springs into life for 1 minute. It is a Tiny construct with AC 13, 20 hit points, a walking speed of 30 feet, a Strength score of 3, immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

The healper takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to activate it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can give it one of the following instructions to use as its action on its next turn, which expends the listed number of charges:

items.name: Stabilise (0 charges)
entries: The healper casts the {@spell spare the dying} cantrip.

items.name: Heal (1 charge)
entries: The healper pours a healing draught into the mouth of a willing creature within its reach, restoring {@dice 2d4 + 2} hit points to the creature.

items.name: Restore (2 charges)
entries: The healper performs a minor surgery on a willing creature within its reach, ending one of the following conditions on that creature: {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralysed}, or {@condition poisoned}.

entries.name: Healping Hand
entries: As an action while holding the healper, you can speak the command phrase "Healp! Healp!" followed by one of the commands below. The bundle of alchemical paraphernalia springs into life for 1 minute. It is a Tiny construct with AC 16, 40 hit points, a walking speed of 45 feet, a Strength score of 3, immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

The healper takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to activate it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can give it one of the following instructions to use as its action on its next turn, which expends the listed number of charges:

items.name: Stabilise (0 charges)
entries: The healper casts the {@spell spare the dying} cantrip.

items.name: Heal (1 charge)
entries: The healper pours a healing draught into the mouth of a willing creature within its reach, restoring {@dice 4d4 + 4} hit points to the creature.

items.name: Restore (2 charges)
entries: The healper performs a minor surgery on a willing creature within its reach, ending one of the following conditions on that creature: {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralysed}, or {@condition poisoned}. It can alternatively end the {@condition charmed} condition, remove one effect reducing the target's hit point maximum, or reduce the target's {@condition exhaustion} level by 1.

entries.name: Healping Hand
entries: As an action while holding the healper, you can speak the command phrase "Healp! Healp!" followed by one of the commands below. The bundle of alchemical paraphernalia springs into life for 1 minute. It is a Tiny construct with AC 19, 60 hit points, a walking speed of 60 feet, a Strength score of 3, immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

The healper takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to activate it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can give it one of the following instructions to use as its action on its next turn, which expends the listed number of charges:

items.name: Stabilise (0 charges)
entries: The healper casts the {@spell spare the dying} cantrip.

items.name: Heal (1 charge)
entries: The healper pours a healing draught into the mouth of a willing creature within its reach, restoring {@dice 8d4 + 8} hit points to the creature.

items.name: Restore (2 charges)
entries: The healper performs a minor surgery on a willing creature within its reach, ending one of the following conditions on that creature: {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralysed}, or {@condition poisoned}. It can alternatively end the {@condition charmed} or {@condition petrified} condition, remove one curse effect, undo any reduction to one of the target's ability scores, remove one effect reducing the target's hit point maximum, or reduce the target's {@condition exhaustion} level by 1.

item.name: Heart Rob (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to piercing damage.

entries.name: Heartbreaking
entries: When you attack a creature that has at least one heart with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you stab the creature directly in the heart. The creature immediately gains 1 level of {@condition exhaustion} and begins suffocating. If its heart is healed with the {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic, it stops suffocating. A creature is immune to this effect if it is immune to piercing damage, doesn't have or need a heart, has legendary actions, or the GM decides that the creature is too big for its heart to be punctured with this weapon. Such a creature instead takes an extra 14 piercing damage from the hit.

item.name: Heart Rob (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to piercing damage.

entries.name: Heartbreaking
entries: When you attack a creature that has at least one heart with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you stab the creature directly in the heart. The creature immediately gains 1 level of {@condition exhaustion} and begins suffocating. If its heart is healed with the {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic, it stops suffocating. A creature is immune to this effect if it is immune to piercing damage, doesn't have or need a heart, has legendary actions, or the GM decides that the creature is too big for its heart to be punctured with this weapon. Such a creature instead takes an extra 21 piercing damage from the hit.

item.name: Heart Rob (Legendary)
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to piercing damage.

entries.name: Heartbreaking
entries: When you attack a creature that has at least one heart with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you stab the creature directly in the heart. The creature immediately gains 2 levels of {@condition exhaustion} and begins suffocating. If its heart is healed with the {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic, it stops suffocating. A creature is immune to this effect if it is immune to piercing damage, doesn't have or need a heart, has legendary actions, or the GM decides that the creature is too big for its heart to be punctured with this weapon. Such a creature instead takes an extra 28 piercing damage from the hit.

entries.name: Pathogen Proof
entries: While wearing this garb, you are immune to disease. In addition, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to resist curses. Each day at dawn, this garb is magically cleaned and pressed, ready for action on the wards.

entries.name: Useful Things
entries: The garb has pockets and pouches containing useful things. It has one antitoxin, one dose of essence of ether poison, one healer's kit (with 5 uses), and one {@item potion of healing}. Each day at dawn, the garb replenishes all removed items, and items that were removed from the garb but not used spoil and become useless.

item.name: Hospitaller's Garb (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing this garb, you have {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws.

entries.name: Pathogen Proof
entries: While wearing this garb, you are immune to disease. In addition, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to resist curses. Each day at dawn, this garb is magically cleaned and pressed, ready for action on the wards.

entries.name: Useful Things
entries: The garb has pockets and pouches containing useful things. It has one antitoxin, one dose of essence of ether poison, one healer's kit (with 5 uses), and one {@item potion of healing}. Each day at dawn, the garb replenishes all removed items, and items that were removed from the garb but not used spoil and become useless.

item.name: Hospitaller's Garb (Legendary)
entries: While wearing this garb, you have {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws and resistance to poison damage.

entries.name: Pathogen Proof
entries: While wearing this garb, you are immune to disease. In addition, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to resist curses. Each day at dawn, this garb is magically cleaned and pressed, ready for action on the wards.

Each day at dawn, the garb replenishes all removed items, and items that were removed from the garb but not used spoil and become useless.

entries.name: Triage
entries: While wearing this mask, you can cast the {@spell detect poison and disease} spell at will. The spell ends early if you remove the mask.

item.name: Plague Docteur Masque (Rare)
entries: While wearing this mask, you have {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense darkvision}, wearing the mask increases its range by 60 feet.

entries.name: Triage
entries: While wearing this mask, you can cast the {@spell detect poison and disease} spell at will. The spell ends early if you remove the mask.

item.name: Plague Docteur Masque (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing this mask, you have {@b advantage} on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned} and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense darkvision}, wearing the mask increases its range by 60 feet.

entries.name: Triage
entries: While wearing this mask, you can cast the {@spell detect poison and disease} spell at will. The spell ends early if you remove the mask.

entries.name: Knightly Medicine
entries: As an action while holding Ser Boncou Rage, you can speak the command phrase "Bon courage!" followed by one of the commands below. The armed and armoured glass bottle bounds into life for 1 minute. It is a Tiny construct with AC 15, 15 hit points, a walking speed of 30 feet, a Strength score of 5, immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

Ser Boncou Rage takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to activate it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can give it one of the following instructions to use as its action on its next turn, which expends the listed number of charges:

items.name: Snap Out of It! (1 charge)
entries: Ser Boncou Rage yells at a creature within 30 feet of it. If that creature can hear the yell and is {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, or {@condition stunned} by an effect that lets it repeat the saving throw to end the condition at the end of each of its turns, the creature can immediately repeat one such saving throw of its choice.

items.name: Once More into the Breach! (2 charges)
entries: Ser Boncou Rage lets out a gallant yell. Up to four creatures of its choice within 30 feet of it, other than itself, gain a {@dice d4} if they can hear the yell. Once within the next minute, a creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the {@dice d20} before deciding to use the die, but must decide before the GM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can't use this die in conjunction with a Bardic Inspiration die.

items.name: Freedom! (3 charges)
entries: Ser Boncou Rage pours its contents into the mouth of a willing creature whose space it shares. For 1 minute, the creature is immune to the {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened} conditions.

entries.name: Knightly Medicine
entries: As an action while holding Ser Boncou Rage, you can speak the command phrase "Bon courage!" followed by one of the commands below. The armed and armoured glass bottle bounds into life for 1 minute. It is a Tiny construct with AC 15, 30 hit points, a walking speed of 30 feet, a Strength score of 5, immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

A creature can't use this die in conjunction with a Bardic Inspiration die.

entries: Ser Boncou Rage lets out a gallant yell. Up to six creatures of its choice within 30 feet of it, other than itself, gain a {@dice d8} if they can hear the yell. Once within the next minute, a creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the {@dice d20} before deciding to use the die, but must decide before the GM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can't use this die in conjunction with a Bardic Inspiration die.

entries.name: Venomous Intentions
entries: As an action while holding the torture tincture, you can speak the command phrase "Poison please". This minuscule glass bottle creeps into life for the next minute. It is a Tiny construct with AC 13, 10 hit points, walking and climbing speeds of 30 feet, a Strength score of 1, a Dexterity score of 20, proficiency in the Stealth skill (+7 bonus), immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It contains one dose of assassin's blood poison and regains an expended dose daily at dawn. The DC for the poison is 13 when administered by the torture tincture.

The torture tincture takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to summon it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can command it to deposit a poison in a vessel, food, or drink as its action on its next turn.

entries.name: Venomous Intentions
entries: As an action while holding the torture tincture, you can speak the command phrase "Poison please". This minuscule glass bottle creeps into life for the next minute. It is a Tiny construct with AC 13, 20 hit points, walking and climbing speeds of 30 feet, a Strength score of 1, a Dexterity score of 20, proficiency in the Stealth skill (+7 bonus), immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It contains one dose of malice poison and regains an expended dose daily at dawn. The DC for the poison is 15 when administered by the torture tincture.

The torture tincture takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to summon it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can command it to deposit a poison in a vessel, food, or drink as its action on its next turn.

entries.name: Venomous Intentions
entries: As an action while holding the torture tincture, you can speak the command phrase "Poison please". This minuscule glass bottle creeps into life for the next minute. It is a Tiny construct with AC 13, 40 hit points, walking and climbing speeds of 30 feet, a Strength score of 1, a Dexterity score of 20, proficiency in the Stealth skill (+7 bonus), immunity to poison and psychic damage, immunity to all conditions except {@condition grappled} and {@condition restrained}, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, it loses its magic and is destroyed. It contains one dose of torpor poison and regains an expended dose daily at dawn. The DC for the poison is 16 when administered by the torture tincture.

The torture tincture takes its turn immediately after yours. As part of the action to summon it, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can command it to deposit a poison in a vessel, food, or drink as its action on its next turn.

entries.name: Ice Glide
entries: When you step onto slippery terrain, such as ice or that created by the {@spell grease} spell, while wearing these boots, magical blades of frost instantly form under your feet, allowing you to glide smoothly and easily on the hazardous ground. While these blades are out, you ignore difficult terrain caused by slippery terrain. In addition, while on that type of terrain, you have {@b advantage} on Dexterity saving throws, you automatically succeed on saving throws made to avoid falling {@condition prone}, and your walking speed is increased by 10 feet.

entries.name: Slip and Slide
entries: As an action while wearing these boots, you can stomp your feet on the ground and release a burst of chilling magic that freezes the ground within 15 feet of you, covering it in a thick layer of slippery ice and turning it into difficult terrain.

When the ice appears, each creature standing in its area must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or fall {@condition prone}. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there must also make the saving throw. This effect lasts for 1 minute, after which the ice melts away. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Argent Gliders (Rare)
entries: These boots have 2 charges and regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

When the ice appears, each creature standing in its area must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or fall {@condition prone} and take {@damage 4d6} cold damage on a failed save, or take half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there must also make the saving throw but doesn't take this damage again. This effect lasts for 1 minute, after which the ice melts away.

item.name: Azulash (Uncommon)
entries: While grasping the handle of this weapon, you can use a bonus action to cause a lightning whip to form from its tip, or make it disappear. This magic whip adds 10 feet to your reach when you attack with it on your turn (instead of 5 feet), deals lightning damage instead of slashing damage, and deals an extra {@damage 1d4} lightning damage to the first target it hits on each of your turns.

entries.name: Static Field
entries: While holding the whip, a 20-foot-radius sphere of static electricity centred on you causes creatures' hair to stand on end. Whenever a creature you can see moves into or within this sphere, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge, forcing the creature to make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw as the static discharges towards it. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d4} lightning damage and its speed is reduced by 15 feet until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced.

entries.name: Patient Observer
entries: While holding this weapon, you gain the patient observational habits of a hunting blue dragon. As a bonus action, you can attempt to discern weaknesses and openings in a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. Make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check contested by that creature's Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check. If you succeed, you gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make against that creature before the end of your current turn.

item.name: Azulash (Rare)

entries.name: Protective Lining
entries: The magic of the cloak shields its wearer against extreme cold and heat. While wearing it, you and everything you wear and carry are unharmed by temperatures as low as -50 degrees Celsius and as high as 70 degrees Celsius.

entries.name: Sound Sleep
entries: This cloak can be rolled around you and used as a cosy sleeping bag. When you spend a short rest using the cloak as a sleeping bag, if you roll any Hit Dice to regain hit points, you can reroll any 1s or 2s. You must use the new rolls. When you spend a long rest using the cloak as a sleeping bag, you recover 1 additional spent Hit Die at the end of the rest.

entries.name: Adaptable Conversationalist
entries: While wearing the cloak, you gain a brass dragon's appetite for conversation. You can cast the {@spell comprehend languages} spell from the cloak. While under the effect of the spell, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks made to convey information through gestures or mimicry. After you use this property to cast the {@spell comprehend languages} spell, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-3, you can't use this property again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Protective Lining
entries: The magic of the cloak shields its wearer against extreme cold and heat. While wearing it, you and everything you wear and carry are unharmed by temperatures as low as -50 degrees Celsius and as high as 70 degrees Celsius. In addition, the Protective Lining property reduces the cold and fire damage you take by 3.

entries: While wearing the cloak, you gain a brass dragon's appetite for conversation. You always have {@b advantage} on ability checks made to convey information through gestures or mimicry. You can also cast {@spell comprehend languages} or {@spell tongues} from the cloak. After you use this property to cast the {@spell tongues} spell, you can't use this property to cast the {@spell tongues} spell again until the next dawn. After you use this property to cast the {@spell comprehend languages} spell, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-3, you can't use this property to cast {@spell comprehend languages} again until the next dawn.

item.name: Bronze Bulwark (Uncommon)
entries: This shield has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Repelling Blast
entries: When you are hit by a melee attack while holding this shield, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and release a blast of repulsive energy, forcing the attacker to make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw if it is within 10 feet of you. On a failed save, the attacker is pushed up to 30 feet directly away from you and knocked {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Deflection
entries: You can also use this shield's repelling property to attempt to deflect projectiles while holding it. When you're hit by a ranged weapon attack roll, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge to force the attacker to reroll the attack roll and use the new roll's result.

entries.name: Surf's Up!
entries: The shield is also capable of producing a gentle repelling field when placed on a liquid, allowing it to hover a few inches above the surface. While hovering in this way, you can stand on the shield and speak its command word to make it glide across the liquid. You control the movement and trajectory of the shield, which has a speed of 60 feet and can carry up to 400 pounds. If you take damage while standing on the shield in this way, you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or fall {@condition prone} off of the shield.

item.name: Bronze Bulwark (Rare)
entries: This shield has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries: When you are hit by a melee attack while holding this shield, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and release a blast of repulsive energy, forcing the attacker to make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw if it is within 10 feet of you. item.name: Crystal Seer (Uncommon)
entries: The spyglass has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Channel the Sun
entries: While holding this spyglass during the daytime, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to channel the cleansing power of sunlight. A beam of positive energy erupts from the tip of the spyglass towards a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. If that creature is under an effect that allows it to make a saving throw at the start or end of its turn to end it, it can immediately make an additional saving throw, with {@b advantage}, and without suffering from the effects of a failed saving throw.

entries.name: Study the Stars
entries: This property can only be used at night. You can spend 10 minutes examining the constellations through the spyglass, searching for signs or omens to interpret. At the end of that time, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell augury} spell. Looking through the spyglass to use this property always reveals the clear sky outside, regardless of where you are or the current weather.

item.name: Crystal Seer (Rare)
entries: The spyglass has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Channel the Sun
entries: While holding this spyglass during the daytime, you can use a bonus action or an action to expend 1 charge to channel the cleansing power of sunlight. A beam of positive energy erupts from the tip of the spyglass towards a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. If that creature is under an effect that allows it to make a saving throw at the start or end of its turn to end it, it can immediately make an additional saving throw, with {@b advantage}, and without suffering from the effects of a failed saving throw.

entries.name: Study the Stars
entries: This property can only be used at night. You can spend 10 minutes examining the constellations through the spyglass, searching for signs or omens to interpret. At the end of that time, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell augury} spell. Alternatively, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell divination} spell, which has a casting time of 1 hour, during which time you must use the spyglass to study the stars. Looking through the spyglass to use either property always reveals the clear sky outside, regardless of where you are or the current weather.

entries.name: Tales of the Past
entries: The book holds information on one unique area. Whenever you make an Intelligence check to recall or learn information about that recorded area, you gain {@b advantage} on that check if you take 10 minutes to consult the book.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: While you are attuned to this book, you can use the Write & Record and Glimpse of the Past properties.

entries.name: OA: Write & Record
entries: While holding the book, you can take 1 hour to attune your senses to the land within 1 mile of you, concentrating on this process as if you were concentrating on a spell. At the end of the duration, a portion of the book's pages are filled with fragments of information regarding the area's flora, fauna, geography, history, customs, and folklore. This area becomes the area described in the Tales of the Past property.

entries.name: OA: Glimpse of the Past
entries: While holding the book, you can cast the {@spell hallucinatory terrain} spell from it, but only to create an illusion of a portion of the area recorded in the book's Tales of the Past property. The spell lasts 1 hour and requires your concentration when cast in this way. Choose a timeframe within the last 24 hours and make an Intelligence check. The resulting illusion replicates the state of the area at the time of your choice, even showing creatures and events that took place in it, with a level of detail dependent on the result of your check, as indicated by the table below. Once this property of the book has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

rows: Creatures appear as hazy blobs. Movement is staggered and erratic. All sound is white noise.

Creatures are defined enough to make out details such as race, build, age, and clothing, but faces are still blurry. Movement is smooth, but imprecise. It's possible to make a distinction between types of sounds (speaking, music, ambient noise, etc.), but words are indiscernible.

Faces are visible, and details such as eye and hair colour, and the rough shape of facial features, are discernible. Movement is precise, but smaller details such as facial expressions or subtle gestures are not captured. Words are understandable, but voices are difficult to identify.
Creatures are perfectly represented, down to the smallest facial detail. Subtle expressions and fine gestures are meticulously displayed. All sound is crisp and clear.

entries.name: Tales of the Past
entries: The book holds information on up to three unique areas. Whenever you make an Intelligence check to recall or learn information about one of the recorded areas, you gain {@b advantage} on that check if you take 10 minutes to consult the book.

This area becomes one of the areas described in the Tales of the Past property; if recording a new area would put the book over its limit, you choose an older location to be erased from the book.

item.name: Golden Glory (Rare)
entries: The helmet has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Draconic Roar
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this helmet, you can expend 1 charge to let out a fierce, earth-shaking roar that weakens your foes' bodies and resolve while emboldening your allies. Each hostile creature within 15 feet of you that can hear you must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, whenever the creature makes an attack roll before the end of its next turn, it must roll a {@dice d4} and subtract the number rolled from the result. In addition, when you roar, up to three willing creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you that can hear you are inspired and strengthened. Whenever one of those creatures makes an attack roll before the end of its next turn, it can roll a {@dice d4} and add the number rolled to the result.

entries.name: Wealthy Diet
entries: While wearing this helmet, you know the location of precious gemstones worth at least 10 gp within 30 feet of you (including through total cover). In addition, you're able to consume these gems to bolster your vitality. As an action, you can eat a gem you're holding to regain hit points equal to one tenth of its value in gp. Once you've recovered 50 hit points using this property of the helmet, you can't regain more hit points in this way until the next dawn. If eating a gem would cause the number of hit points gained to go over this limit, you only gain hit points up to the limit.
