entries.name: Planar Portals
entries: As an action while wearing this ring, you can swing your hand down in a clawing motion to open a portal to another plane of existence in an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of you. The portal is a 5-foot diameter opening, through which you can see your destination. Its entrance, which can only be interacted with on the side from which it was created, remains open for 1 minute. For the duration, any creature that can fit through the opening can cross the portal, appearing in an unoccupied space closest to the portal on the other side. This portal leads to a random location on your choice of the Dreamscape, the Ethereal Plane, the Plane of Fey, or the Material Plane. Once this property has been used to open a portal, it can't be used to open a portal to any plane until the next dawn.

entries.name: Sweet Dreams
entries: As an action, you can use the ring to cast the {@spell dream} spell (save {@dc 15}). Once this property of the ring has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Planar Portals
entries: As an action while wearing this ring, you can swing your hand down in a clawing motion to open a portal to another plane of existence in an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of you. The portal is a 5-foot diameter opening, through which you can see your destination. Its entrance, which can only be interacted with on the side from which it was created, remains open for 1 minute. For the duration, any creature that can fit through the opening can cross the portal, appearing in an unoccupied space closest to the portal on the other side. This portal leads to a random location on your choice of the Dreamscape, the Ethereal Plane, the Plane of Fey, or the Material Plane. Once this property has been used to open a portal, it can't be used to open a portal to the same plane until the next dawn.

entries.name: Sweet Dreams
entries: As an action, you can use the ring to cast the {@spell dream} spell (save {@dc 16}), with the following changes. Once this property of the ring has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: Instead of entering or inducing a trance state, you create a portal that looks and functions similarly to the ones described by the Planar Portals property, leading to the dreamscape of the creature targeted by the spell. The portal remains open for the duration of the spell, and any creature can cross it, but only up to 8 creatures can occupy the same dream at once. All creatures in the dream can act as messengers, but a dreaming creature can only be affected by one nightmare. Creatures can leave the dream through the portal at any point. If a creature is still in the dream when the spell ends, it is forcefully ejected, appearing in an unoccupied space next to where the portal stood.

Instead of spawning nightmares, a messenger can make itself look familiar and comforting to the target. If it does, the target's dreams become soothing and more restorative. When the target wakes up, it gains 10 temporary hit points.

item.name: Pale Rage (Uncommon)
entries: Once on each of your turns, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} cold damage to a target you hit with this magic weapon.
The morningstar has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Unrelenting Fury
entries: Like a white dragon, you are able to channel a singular focus on slaughtering an enemy. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make with this weapon against the same creature before the end of the current turn. If you hit with that attack, you can reroll any 1s or 2s that you roll on the weapon's damage die or the extra cold damage it deals. You must use the new rolls.

item.name: Pale Rage (Rare)
entries: You can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} cold damage to any target you hit with this magic weapon.
The morningstar has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Unrelenting Fury
entries: Like a white dragon, you are able to channel a singular focus on slaughtering an enemy. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make with this weapon against the same creature before the end of the current turn. If you hit with that attack, you can reroll any 1s or 2s that you roll on the weapon's damage die or the extra cold damage it deals. You must use the new rolls.

item.name: Pale Rage (Very Rare)
entries: You can deal an extra {@damage 1d8} cold damage to any target you hit with this magic weapon.
The morningstar has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Unrelenting Fury
entries: Like a white dragon, you are able to channel a singular focus on slaughtering an enemy. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make with this weapon against the same creature before the end of the current turn. If you hit with that attack, you can reroll any 1s or 2s that you roll on the weapon's damage die or the extra cold damage it deals. You must use the new rolls.

item.name: Sapphire Seeker (Uncommon)
entries: The compass has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Strategic Pinpoint
entries: The compass's divination magic helps you detect objects. While holding it, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell locate object} spell from the compass.

entries.name: Tinnitus
entries: This compass mimics a sapphire dragon's ability to manipulate sound. Whenever a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you makes a Dexterity, Wisdom, or Intelligence check or saving throw, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and emit a subtle pulse from the compass. If the creature can hear, the pulse creates a disorienting ringing in that creature's ears, giving it {@b disadvantage} on the check or saving throw.

item.name: Sapphire Seeker (Rare)
entries: The compass has 8 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Strategic Pinpoint
entries: The compass's divination magic helps you detect objects and creatures of your choice. While holding it, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell locate object} spell, or 3 charges to cast the {@spell locate creature} spell from the compass.

entries.name: Tinnitus
entries: This compass mimics a sapphire dragon's ability to manipulate sound. Whenever a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you makes a Dexterity, Wisdom, or Intelligence check or saving throw, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and emit a subtle pulse from the compass. If the creature can hear, the pulse creates a disorienting ringing in that creature's ears, giving it {@b disadvantage} on the check or saving throw.

item.name: Sapphire Seeker (Very Rare)
entries: The compass has 10 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Strategic Pinpoint
entries: The compass's divination magic helps you detect objects and creatures of your choice. While holding it, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell locate object} spell, or 3 charges to cast the {@spell locate creature} spell from the compass.

entries.name: Tinnitus
entries: This compass mimics a sapphire dragon's ability to manipulate sound. Whenever a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you makes a Dexterity, Wisdom, or Intelligence check or saving throw, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and emit a subtle pulse from the compass. If the creature can hear, the pulse creates a disorienting ringing in that creature's ears, giving it {@b disadvantage} on the check or saving throw.

entries.name: Way Out
entries: This compass can provide guidance when you are lost. If you aren't in a location that is touched by starlight, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and have the compass guide you to the nearest such location (GM's discretion). Alternatively, if you are on a plane of existence other than the Material Plane, you can use an action to expend 2 charges and have the compass point to the nearest portal to the Material Plane.

item.name: Tortuga Cannon (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hydro Cannon
entries: As an action while holding this device, you can expend 1 charge to shoot a jet of highly-pressurised water at a creature you can see within 10 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or take {@damage 4d6} bludgeoning damage, be pushed up to 20 feet directly away from you, and fall {@condition prone}. When you use this property, you can press a button hidden on the inside of the sleeve and expend additional charges to modify the water jet in one of the ways below:

items.name: Steam Blast (1 Charge)
entries: The water erupting from the cannon is boiling hot. The target must also make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Being underwater doesn't grant resistance against this damage.

items.name: Entangling Kelp (2 Charges)
entries: The water erupting from the cannon contains magically-reinforced strands of kelp, which wrap themselves around the target's body. If the target fails the saving throw against the cannon, it is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. The target or another creature within reach of it can use its action to make a {@dc 13} Strength check, freeing the target on a success.

item.name: Tortuga Cannon (Rare)
entries: This item has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hydro Cannon
entries: As an action while holding this device, you can expend 1 charge to shoot a jet of highly-pressurised water at a creature you can see within 10 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or take {@damage 6d6} bludgeoning damage, be pushed up to 20 feet directly away from you, and fall {@condition prone}. When you use this property, you can press a button hidden on the inside of the sleeve and expend 1 additional charge to modify the water jet in one of the ways below:

items.name: Steam Blast
entries: The water erupting from the cannon is boiling hot. The target must also make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Being underwater doesn't grant resistance against this damage.

items.name: Entangling Kelp
entries: The water erupting from the cannon contains magically-reinforced strands of kelp, which wrap themselves around the target's body. If the target fails the saving throw against the cannon, it is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. The target or another creature within reach of it can use its action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check, freeing the target on a success.

item.name: Tortuga Cannon (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 12 charges and regains {@dice 1d10 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hydro Cannon
entries: As an action while holding this device, you can expend 1 charge to shoot a jet of highly-pressurised water at a creature you can see within 10 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or take {@damage 6d6} bludgeoning damage, be pushed up to 20 feet directly away from you, and fall {@condition prone}. When you use this property, you can press a button hidden on the inside of the sleeve and expend 1 additional charge to modify the water jet in one of the ways below:

items.name: Steam Blast
entries: The water erupting from the cannon is boiling hot. The target must also make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Being underwater doesn't grant resistance against this damage.

items.name: Entangling Kelp
entries: The water erupting from the cannon contains magically-reinforced strands of kelp, which wrap themselves around the target's body. If the target fails the saving throw against the cannon, it is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. The target or another creature within reach of it can use its action to make a {@dc 16} Strength check, freeing the target on a success.

entries.name: Fickle Faces
entries: While wearing this mask, you have advantage on ability checks using a certain skill, depending on which face is at the front. When you first attune to the mask, and each day at dawn while you remain attuned to it, you can select the mask's expression. You can use a bonus action to shift a different expression to the front, but spiked chains drag across your skin when you do so, and you take {@damage 1d6} slashing damage.
Once the Fickle Faces property has been used to gain advantage on an ability check, you can't use it to gain advantage on that kind of check again until you shift the expression.

entries.name: Fickle Faces
entries: While wearing this mask, you have advantage on ability checks using a certain skill, depending on which face is at the front. When you first attune to the mask, and each day at dawn while you remain attuned to it, you can select the mask's expression. You can use a bonus action to shift a different expression to the front, but spiked chains drag across your skin when you do so, and you take {@damage 2d6} slashing damage.

entries.name: Fickle Faces
entries: While wearing this mask, you have advantage on ability checks using a certain skill, depending on which face is at the front. When you first attune to the mask, and each day at dawn while you remain attuned to it, you can select the mask's expression. You can use a bonus action to shift a different expression to the front, but spiked chains drag across your skin when you do so, and you take {@damage 2d6} slashing damage.

entries.name: Blood of the Guilty
entries: As an action while wearing this mask, you can cause blood to pour from the eyes, noses, and mouths of each face. Each creature of your choice within 15 feet of you that can see you must make a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d8} psychic damage and begins to bleed from its eyes as well, becoming {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition blinded}. A creature without blood automatically succeeds on this saving throw. A {@condition blinded} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property of the mask has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Bloodhound's Caress (Uncommon)
entries: This claw has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Arterial Slash
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to attempt to slice a crucial blood vessel, forcing the creature to make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature begins to bleed profusely for 1 minute. While bleeding, the creature takes {@damage 1d6} slashing damage at the end of each of its turns, and any time it regains any hit points, it regains only half as many. A creature without blood automatically succeeds on this saving throw. A creature can end this effect on itself or a willing creature within 5 feet of it by using its action to make a successful {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check.

item.name: Bloodhound's Caress (Rare)
entries: This claw has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Arterial Slash
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to attempt to slice a crucial blood vessel, forcing the creature to make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature begins to bleed profusely for 1 minute. While bleeding, the creature takes {@damage 1d10} slashing damage at the end of each of its turns, and any time it regains any hit points, it regains only half as many. A creature without blood automatically succeeds on this saving throw. A creature can end this effect on itself or a willing creature within 5 feet of it by using its action to make a successful {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check.

item.name: Bloodhound's Caress (Very Rare)
entries: This claw has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Arterial Slash
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to attempt to slice a crucial blood vessel, forcing the creature to make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature begins to bleed profusely for 1 minute. While bleeding, the creature takes {@damage 2d6} slashing damage at the end of each of its turns, and any time it regains any hit points, it regains only half as many. A creature without blood automatically succeeds on this saving throw. A creature can end this effect on itself or a willing creature within 5 feet of it by using its action to make a successful {@dc 16} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check.

item.name: Bloodhound's Widebrim (Uncommon)
entries: This hat has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hunter's Spirit
entries: As an action while wearing this hat, you can expend 1 charge to summon a spectral red hunter to your side for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a bonus action. This hunter takes the form of either a bloodhound or a falcon (your choice), doesn't occupy its space, can't interact physically with creatures or objects, and never leaves your side. As a bonus action, you can make the hunter invisible to all creatures but you, or visible again. Depending on the chosen form, the spectral hunter grants you different benefits:

items.name: Bloodhound
entries: When you make a {@skill Perception} check that relies on smell, you can do so through your spectral companion, gaining {@b advantage} on the check. In addition, you can track a scent whose origin is up to 1 mile away.

items.name: Falcon
entries: When you make a {@skill Perception} check that relies on sight, you can do so through your spectral companion, gaining {@b advantage} on the check. In addition, you can see clearly up to 3 miles away, as long as no obstructions are present, and make out small details of objects or creatures at that distance.

item.name: Bloodhound's Widebrim (Rare)
entries: This hat has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hunter's Spirit
entries: As an action while wearing this hat, you can expend 1 charge to summon a spectral red hunter to your side for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a bonus action. This hunter takes the form of either a bloodhound or a falcon (your choice), doesn't occupy its space, can't interact physically with creatures or objects, and never leaves your side. As a bonus action, you can make the hunter invisible to all creatures but you, or visible again. Depending on the chosen form, the spectral hunter grants you different benefits:

items.name: Bloodhound
entries: When you make a {@skill Perception} check that relies on smell, you can do so through your spectral companion, gaining {@b advantage} on the check. In addition, you can track a scent whose origin is up to 1 mile away.

items.name: Falcon
entries: When you make a {@skill Perception} check that relies on sight, you can do so through your spectral companion, gaining {@b advantage} on the check. In addition, you can see clearly up to 3 miles away, as long as no obstructions are present, and make out small details of objects or creatures at that distance.

entries.name: Sic 'Em
entries: As a bonus action while the spectral hunter is summoned, you can order it to attack a creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack against the target ({@hit 7} to hit). The effects of the attack depend on the form of the spectral hunter, which disappears after the effect ends:

items.name: Bloodhound
entries: On a hit, the attack deals {@damage 5d4} force damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone} and have a speed of 0 feet until the end of its next turn, as the bloodhound holds it in place.

items.name: Falcon
entries: On a hit, the attack deals {@damage 2d4} force damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn, as the falcon claws at its eyes.

item.name: Bloodhound's Widebrim (Very Rare)
entries: This hat has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hunter's Spirit
entries: As an action while wearing this hat, you can expend 1 charge to summon a spectral red hunter to your side for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a bonus action. This hunter takes the form of either a bloodhound or a falcon (your choice), doesn't occupy its space, can't interact physically with creatures or objects, and never leaves your side. As a bonus action, you can make the hunter invisible to all creatures but you, or visible again. Depending on the chosen form, the spectral hunter grants you different benefits:

items.name: Bloodhound
entries: When you make a {@skill Perception} check that relies on smell, you can do so through your spectral companion, gaining {@b advantage} on the check. In addition, you can track a scent whose origin is up to 10 miles away.

items.name: Falcon
entries: When you make a {@skill Perception} check that relies on sight, you can do so through your spectral companion, gaining {@b advantage} on the check. In addition, you can see clearly up to 30 miles away, as long as no obstructions are present, and make out small details of objects or creatures at that distance.

entries.name: Sic 'Em
entries: As a bonus action while the spectral hunter is summoned, you can order it to attack a creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack against the target ({@hit 8} to hit). The effects of the attack depend on the form of the spectral hunter, which disappears after the effect ends:

items.name: Bloodhound
entries: On a hit, the attack deals {@damage 10d6} force damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone} and have a speed of 0 feet until the end of its next turn, as the bloodhound holds it in place.

items.name: Falcon
entries: On a hit, the attack deals {@damage 6d6} force damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn, as the falcon claws at its eyes.

item.name: Candlebrand Ring (Uncommon)
entries: This ring has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. As a bonus action while wearing the ring, you can speak the ring's command word, causing the waxen god's emblem to become searing hot until you remove it, or until you use a bonus action to repeat its command word and deactivate it. You must concentrate on the effects of the brands, as if on a spell.

entries.name: Brand of Protection
entries: While the ring is heated, you can use a bonus action to touch it to a willing creature within your reach and expend 1 charge. The target takes {@damage 1d4} fire damage and you cast {@spell shield of faith} on the creature (requiring your concentration) for 10 minutes.

entries.name: Brand of Castigation
entries: While the ring is heated, you can use an action to make a melee spell attack ({@hit 5} to hit) against a creature within 5 feet of you and expend 1 charge. On a hit, the creature takes {@damage 2d4} fire damage and is cursed (requiring your concentration) for 1 minute. While cursed in this way, its AC is reduced by 2. The creature can make a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the curse on a success.

item.name: Candlebrand Ring (Rare)
entries: This ring has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. As a bonus action while wearing the ring, you can speak the ring's command word, causing the waxen god's emblem to become searing hot until you remove it, or until you use a bonus action to repeat its command word and deactivate it.

entries.name: Brand of Protection
entries: While the ring is heated, you can use a bonus action to touch it to a willing creature within your reach and expend 1 charge. The target takes {@damage 1d4} fire damage and gains the benefits of the {@spell shield of faith} spell for 10 minutes (no concentration required).

entries.name: Brand of Castigation
entries: While the ring is heated, you can use an action to make a melee spell attack ({@hit 7} to hit) against a creature within 5 feet of you and expend 1 charge. On a hit, the creature takes {@damage 2d4} fire damage and is cursed for 1 minute. While cursed in this way, its AC is reduced by 2. The creature can make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the curse on a success.

item.name: Candlebrand Ring (Very Rare)
entries: This ring has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. As a bonus action while wearing the ring, you can speak the ring's command word, causing the waxen god's emblem to become searing hot until you remove it, or until you use a bonus action to repeat its command word and deactivate it.

entries: While the ring is heated, you can use a bonus action to touch it to a willing creature within your reach and expend 1 charge. The target takes {@damage 1d4} fire damage and gains the benefits of the {@spell shield of faith} spell for 10 minutes (no concentration required).

entries: While the ring is heated, you can use an action to make a melee spell attack ({@hit 8} to hit) against a creature within 5 feet of you and expend 1 charge. On a hit, the creature takes {@damage 2d4} fire damage and is cursed for 1 minute. While cursed in this way, its AC is reduced by 2. The creature can make a {@dc 16} Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the curse on a success.

item.name: Candlelight Mitre (Uncommon)
entries: This hat has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Cleanse the Sins
entries: While wearing the hat, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge and light a searing flame at the top of it. For 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to put it out, this flame casts bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. At the end of each of your turns while this effect is active, each creature in the flame's bright light (including you) must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} fire damage.

entries.name: Burn the Lies
entries: While wearing the hat, you can use an action to expend 2 charges and cause two orbs of flame to form in the mitre's eyes, taking {@damage 2d6} fire damage in the process. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to end it. While the mask's eyes are ablaze, you have {@b advantage} on Intimidation and Insight checks, and you can see {@condition invisible} creatures and objects and automatically pierce through visual illusions.

item.name: Candlelight Mitre (Rare)
entries: This hat has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Cleanse the Sins
entries: While wearing the hat, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge and light a searing flame at the top of it. For 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to put it out, this flame casts bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. At the end of each of your turns while this effect is active, each creature in the flame's bright light (including you) must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 3d6} fire damage.

entries.name: Burn the Lies
entries: While wearing the hat, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and cause two orbs of flame to form in the mitre's eyes, taking {@damage 2d6} fire damage in the process. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to end it. While the mask's eyes are ablaze, you have {@b advantage} on Intimidation and Insight checks, and you can see {@condition invisible} creatures and objects and automatically pierce through visual illusions.

item.name: Candlelight Mitre (Very Rare)
entries: This hat has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Cleanse the Sins
entries: While wearing the hat, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge and light a searing flame at the top of it. For 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to put it out, this flame casts bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. At the end of each of your turns while this effect is active, each creature in the flame's bright light (including you) must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 3d6} fire damage.

entries: While wearing the hat, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and cause two orbs of flame to form in the mitre's eyes, taking {@damage 2d6} fire damage in the process. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to end it. While the mask's eyes are ablaze, you have {@b advantage} on Intimidation and Insight checks, and you can see {@condition invisible} creatures and objects and automatically pierce through visual illusions.

item.name: Clutching Crown (Rare)
entries: This item has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. The veil on the crown gives other creatures {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made against you, without hindering your vision.

entries.name: Touch of the Grave
entries: While wearing this crown, you can cast the {@spell chill touch} cantrip (2d8 damage, {@hit 5} to hit) at will. When you cast this cantrip, you can expend up to 2 charges to empower it in one of the following ways:

items.name: Many Hands
entries: For each charge expended, you can target one additional creature with the cantrip.

items.name: Grasping Hands
entries: For each charge expended, a target that fails its saving throw takes an extra {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage and, if you expended at least 2 charges, the target is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Clutching Crown (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. The veil on the crown gives other creatures {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made against you, without hindering your vision.

entries.name: Touch of the Grave
entries: While wearing this crown, you can cast the {@spell chill touch} cantrip (2d8 damage, {@hit 6} to hit) at will. When you cast this cantrip, you can expend up to 4 charges to empower it in one of the following ways:

items.name: Many Hands
entries: For each charge expended, you can target one additional creature with the cantrip.

items.name: Grasping Hands
entries: For each charge expended, a target that fails its saving throw takes an extra {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage and, if you expended at least 2 charges, the target is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn; if you expended at least 4 charges, the target is also {@condition paralysed} until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Clutching Crown (Legendary)
entries: This item has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. The veil on the crown gives other creatures {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made against you, without hindering your vision.

entries.name: Touch of the Grave
entries: While wearing this crown, you can cast the {@spell chill touch} cantrip (2d8 damage, {@hit 7} to hit) at will. When you cast this cantrip, you can expend up to 4 charges to empower it in one of the following ways:

items.name: Many Hands
entries: For each charge expended, you can target one additional creature with the cantrip.

items.name: Grasping Hands
entries: For each charge expended, a target that fails its saving throw takes an extra {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage and, if you expended at least 2 charges, the target is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn; if you expended at least 4 charges, the target is also {@condition paralysed} until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Grasping Mantle (Rare)
entries: This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Helping Hands
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can cast the {@spell mage hand} cantrip at will, or expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell bigby's hand||arcane hand} spell (save {@dc 15}). When either spell is cast in this way, the hand it produces is always somehow wrong: it might lack fingernails, have extra fingers, or have its thumb on the opposite side. In addition, when you cast the {@spell bigby's hand||arcane hand} spell using the cloak, it deals necrotic damage instead of force damage, and you take {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage immediately before you begin concentrating on the spell.

item.name: Grasping Mantle (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Helping Hands

entries.name: Sapping Finger
entries: The hand touches a creature within 5 feet of it with one finger, attempting to drain it of energy. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or fall {@condition prone} and have its speed reduced to 0 feet until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Grasping Mantle (Legendary)

entries.name: Rip Cage
entries: While holding the flail, you can use an action to magically rip your own heart from your chest and transfer it to the cage in the weapon without killing yourself, taking {@damage 2d4} necrotic damage in the process. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way. While your heart is in the cage, your creature type is undead, and the flail deals an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage on a hit. Your heart remains in the cage until you use a bonus action to take it back into yourself, or until you finish a short or long rest.

entries: While your heart is in the cage, you can cast the {@spell chill touch} cantrip at will ({@hit 7} to hit). When cast in this way, the spell creates a ghostly heart with writhing blood vessels instead of a skeletal hand. In addition, when you hit a creature with an attack using the flail, you can expend 1 charge to immediately cast the {@spell chill touch} cantrip targeting that creature ({@hit 7} to hit, no action required).

entries.name: Share the Pain
entries: When a creature hits you with a melee attack while you're holding the shield, its spikes extend to pierce that creature in return, dealing {@damage 1d8} piercing damage to it. Once this happens, it can't happen again until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Share the Pain
entries: When a creature hits you with a melee attack while you're holding the shield, its spikes extend to pierce that creature in return, dealing {@damage 1d8} piercing damage to it. Once this happens, it can't happen again until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Locked In
entries: While holding the shield, you can use an action to force a creature within 5 feet of you to make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the shield opens like an iron maiden and swallows the target, which is transported to a torturous demiplane, as per the {@spell banishment} spell, for 1 minute or until your concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Once this property of the shield is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Share the Pain
entries: When a creature hits you with a melee attack while you're holding the shield, its spikes extend to pierce that creature in return, dealing {@damage 1d8} piercing damage to it. Once this happens, it can't happen again until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Locked In
entries: While holding the shield, you can use an action to force a creature within 5 feet of you to make a {@dc 16} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the shield opens like an iron maiden and swallows the target, which is transported to a torturous demiplane, as per the {@spell banishment} spell, for 1 minute or until your concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). The Locked In property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Beetle Buckler (Uncommon)
entries: This shield has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Under Wing
entries: While wielding this shield, when a creature you can see hits a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can use your reaction and expend 1 charge to have the shield open its wings in front of the target, giving it a +2 bonus to AC against that attack and potentially causing it to miss.

item.name: Beetle Buckler (Rare)
entries: This shield has 4 charges and regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Under Wing
entries: While wielding this shield, when a creature you can see hits a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can use your reaction and expend 1 charge to have the shield open its wings in front of the target, giving it a +2 bonus to AC against that attack and potentially causing it to miss.

entries.name: Soaring Scarab
entries: You can speak this shield's command word to don it or doff it as a bonus action. When you doff the shield in this manner, you can cause it to attach to your back and spread its wings, granting you a flying speed of 15 feet on your turn only, as long as you and anything you're wearing or carrying don't weigh over 400 pounds.

item.name: Beetle Buckler (Very Rare)
entries: This shield has 6 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Under Wing
entries: While wielding this shield, when a creature you can see hits a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can use your reaction and expend 1 charge to have the shield open its wings in front of the target, giving it a +2 bonus to AC against that attack and potentially causing it to miss.

entries.name: Soaring Scarab
entries: You can speak this shield's command word to don it or doff it as a bonus action. When you doff the shield in this manner, you can cause it to attach to your back and spread its wings, granting you a flying speed of 30 feet on your turn only, as long as you and anything you're wearing or carrying don't weigh over 800 pounds.

entries.name: Overgrowth
entries: As an action while wearing these gloves, you can touch a creature, forcing it to make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition restrained} for 1 minute as brambles from one of the gloves spread to it, enveloping it in a mass of vines and thorns. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way can use an action to make a {@dc 13} Strength check, ending the effect on a success. While the creature is {@condition restrained}, you can use a bonus action to control the writhing brambles in one of the manners described below. Once this property of the gloves has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Constrict
entries: The thorny brambles tighten around the {@condition restrained} creature, dealing {@damage 2d6} piercing damage to it.

items.name: Lash
entries: A bramble whip lashes out against a different creature that you can see within 20 feet of the {@condition restrained} one. Make a weapon attack roll against it ({@hit 5} to hit), dealing {@damage 4d6} slashing damage on a hit.

entries.name: Overgrowth
entries: As an action while wearing these gloves, you can touch a creature, forcing it to make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition restrained} for 1 minute as brambles from one of the gloves spread to it, enveloping it in a mass of vines and thorns. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way can use an action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check, ending the effect on a success. While the creature is {@condition restrained}, you can use a bonus action to control the writhing brambles in one of the manners described below. The Overgrowth property can be used twice—once for each glove—before the next dawn.

While the creature is {@condition restrained}, you can use a bonus action to control the writhing brambles in one of the manners described below. entries.name: Flight of the Giant Moth
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause it to transform into a {@creature giant moth|LALoTT}, which appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of you. The moth is friendly to you and your companions, it obeys your verbal commands, and it disappears after 1 hour, if you dismiss it as an action, or when it drops to 0 hit points, at which point it transforms back into the cloak. Once this property of the cloak is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of the Moth Rider (Rare)
entries: While wearing this cloak, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Intimidation} checks.

entries.name: Flight of the Giant Moth
entries: As an action while wearing this cloak, you can cause it to transform into a {@creature giant moth|LALoTT}, which appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of you. The moth is friendly to you and your companions, it obeys your verbal commands, and it disappears after 1 hour, if you dismiss it as an action, or when it drops to 0 hit points, at which point it transforms back into the cloak. Once this property of the cloak is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Fearsome Visage
entries: You can use an action to cause the pattern on the cloak to shift into the shape of a terrifying face and animate it as you desire, such as by replicating your facial expressions or lip movements. When you do, you can cast the {@spell fear} spell (save {@dc 15}), targeting each creature of your choice that can see you within 15 feet of you, instead of those in a 30-foot cone. Once this spell is cast in this way, it can't be cast again until the next dawn.

entries: While wearing this cloak, you have {@b advantage} on {@skill Intimidation} checks.

The moth is friendly to you and your companions, it obeys your verbal commands, and it disappears after 1 hour, if you dismiss it as an action, or when it drops to 0 hit points, at which point it transforms back into the cloak. Once this property of the cloak is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

When you do, you can cast the {@spell fear} spell (save {@dc 16}) without requiring concentration, targeting each creature of your choice that can see you within 15 feet of you, instead of those in a 30-foot cone. Once this spell is cast in this way, it can't be cast again until the next dawn.

item.name: Herculean Horn (Uncommon)
entries: This lance has 2 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Slinging Scarab
entries: When you hit a Medium or smaller creature with an attack using this lance, you can expend 1 charge to try to throw it behind you, forcing the creature to make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is tossed onto an unoccupied space within 15 feet of you and lands {@condition prone}.

item.name: Herculean Horn (Rare)
entries: This lance has 2 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aerial Agility
entries: You only need one hand to wield this lance if you're flying. Additionally, if you fly at least 15 feet straight toward a target and immediately attack it using this lance, you gain {@b advantage} on the attack roll.

entries.name: Slinging Scarab
entries: When you hit a Medium or smaller creature with an attack using this lance, you can expend 1 charge to try to throw it behind you, forcing the creature to make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is tossed onto an unoccupied space within 15 feet of you and lands {@condition prone}.

item.name: Herculean Horn (Very Rare)
entries: This lance has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aerial Agility
entries: You only need one hand to wield this lance if you're flying. Additionally, if you fly at least 15 feet straight toward a target and immediately attack it using this lance, you gain {@b advantage} on the attack roll.

entries.name: Slinging Scarab
entries: When you hit a Large or smaller creature with an attack using this lance, you can expend 1 charge to try to throw it behind you, forcing the creature to make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is tossed onto an unoccupied space within 15 feet of you and lands {@condition prone}.

item.name: Kabuto (Uncommon)
entries: This helmet has 1 charge and regains it daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lock Horns
entries: Whenever a creature hits you with an attack using a melee weapon while you're wearing this helmet, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and gain a +2 bonus to AC against that attack as you attempt to catch the weapon with the horn, potentially causing the attack to miss. If the attack misses, the creature must make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it is disarmed of the weapon, which is thrown to an unoccupied space of your choice within 20 feet of you.

entries.name: Stalwart Scarab
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition grappled} condition, being knocked {@condition prone}, or being moved against your will.

item.name: Kabuto (Rare)
entries: This helmet has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

If the attack misses, the creature must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it is disarmed of the weapon, which is thrown to an unoccupied space of your choice within 20 feet of you.

entries.name: Stalwart Scarab
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition grappled} condition, being knocked {@condition prone}, or being moved against your will.

item.name: Kabuto (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This helmet has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lock Horns

If the attack misses, the creature must make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it is disarmed of the weapon, which is thrown to an unoccupied space of your choice within 20 feet of you.

entries.name: Stalwart Scarab
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition grappled} condition, being knocked {@condition prone}, or being moved against your will.

item.name: Raven's Gown (Uncommon)
entries: This dress has 2 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Unseelie Gift
entries: As an action while wearing this dress, you can expend 1 charge to pluck one of its feathers and give it to a willing creature, whereupon a bond is created between the feather and its recipient. A feather always finds its way back to its recipient if discarded; the bond can only be broken if the recipient gives the feather to a different, willing creature, whereupon the new creature becomes the bonded recipient. Plucked feathers regrow each day at dawn. A feather vanishes, breaking the bond, after its power is used in one of the following ways:

items.name: Follow
entries: You target a feather within 60 feet of you and teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of its carrier.

items.name: Fray
entries: You target a feather within 60 feet of you and its carrier takes {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. You gain temporary hit points equal to three times the amount, which last for 1 hour.

item.name: Raven's Gown (Very Rare)
entries: This dress has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Unseelie Gift
entries: As an action while wearing this dress, you can expend 1 charge to pluck one of its feathers and give it to a willing creature, whereupon a bond is created between the feather and its recipient. A feather always finds its way back to its recipient if discarded; the bond can only be broken if the recipient gives the feather to a different, willing creature, whereupon the new creature becomes the bonded recipient. Plucked feathers regrow each day at dawn. A feather vanishes, breaking the bond, after its power is used in one of the following ways:

items.name: Follow
entries: You target a feather within 60 feet of you and teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of its carrier.

items.name: Fray
entries: You target a feather within 60 feet of you and its carrier takes {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. You gain temporary hit points equal to three times the amount, which last for 1 hour.

items.name: Read
entries: You target a feather within 1 mile of you and become able to read its carrier's thoughts, as per the {@spell detect thoughts} spell (save {@dc 16}), for 10 minutes. The carrier has {@b disadvantage} on saving throws against this effect.

items.name: Watch
entries: You target a feather within 1 mile of you and cast the {@spell scrying} spell (save {@dc 16}) on its carrier, which has {@b disadvantage} on the saving throw. When cast in this way, the spell replaces your senses with the target's, instead of showing images on a surface.

entries.name: Night Light
entries: The lantern exists in one of three states: unravelled, shaded, or cocooned. As a bonus action while holding this lantern, you can cause it to change states.
When unravelled, this lantern sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet.
When shaded, the lantern sheds dim light in a 30-foot radius, which only the creature carrying it can see.
When cocooned, the lantern sheds no light.

entries.name: Night Light
entries: The lantern exists in one of three states: unravelled, shaded, or cocooned. As a bonus action while holding this lantern, you can cause it to change states.
When unravelled, this lantern sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet.
When shaded, the lantern sheds dim light in a 30-foot radius, which only the creature carrying it can see.
When cocooned, the lantern sheds no light.

entries.name: Eclipse Swarm
entries: While holding this lantern, you can cast the {@spell insect plague} spell (save {@dc 15}). When cast in this way, the spell creates moths instead of locusts. Once this property of the lantern has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Night Light
entries: The lantern exists in one of three states: unravelled, shaded, or cocooned. As a bonus action while holding this lantern, you can cause it to change states.
When unravelled, this lantern sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet.
When shaded, the lantern sheds dim light in a 30-foot radius, which only the creature carrying it can see.
When cocooned, the lantern sheds no light.

entries.name: Eclipse Swarm
entries: While holding this lantern, you can cast the {@spell insect plague} spell (save {@dc 16}). When cast in this way, the spell creates moths instead of locusts. Once this property of the lantern has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Moth to a Flame
entries: As an action while holding the unravelled lantern, you can target a creature within 30 feet of you that can see the lantern, forcing it to succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you for 10 minutes. While {@condition charmed} in this way, a creature must use all of its movement and action to approach the lantern on each of its turns, and can't willingly move away from it. In addition, the creature is also {@condition blinded} while within 5 feet of the lantern, unable to focus on anything but its radiance. The creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns if it took damage since its last turn, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property of the lantern has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Arcane Treaders (Uncommon)
entries: While wearing these boots, you ignore difficult terrain caused by spells or other magical effects. These boots have 2 charges and regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Retrace Steps
entries: At the start of each of your turns, the boots produce an ethereal afterimage of themselves, which stays in the same spot, mimicking the motions of your feet as you move. You can expend 1 charge to teleport to the space of the afterimage, as long as it's unoccupied; you can do so as a bonus action, or as a reaction when you trigger a trap or an opportunity attack as part of your movement (potentially avoiding the trap or attack). The afterimage fades after you teleport to it or at the end of your turn.

item.name: Arcane Treaders (Rare)
entries: While wearing these boots, you ignore difficult terrain caused by spells or other magical effects. These boots have 3 charges and regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Retrace Steps
entries: At the start of each of your turns, the boots produce an ethereal afterimage of themselves, which stays in the same spot, mimicking the motions of your feet as you move. You can expend 1 charge to teleport to the space of the afterimage, as long as it's unoccupied; you can do so as a bonus action, or as a reaction when you trigger a trap or an opportunity attack as part of your movement (potentially avoiding the trap or attack). The afterimage fades after you teleport to it or at the end of your turn.

entries.name: Transitory State
entries: After you use the boots to teleport, you momentarily acquire the ethereal appearance of the afterimage, existing in a state of transition between the two locations. The next attack roll made against you before the start of your next turn is made with {@b disadvantage}.

item.name: Arcane Treaders (Very Rare)
entries: While wearing these boots, you ignore difficult terrain caused by spells or other magical effects. These boots have 5 charges and regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Retrace Steps
entries: At the start of each of your turns, the boots produce an ethereal afterimage of themselves, which stays in the same spot, mimicking the motions of your feet as you move. You can expend 1 charge to teleport to the space of the afterimage, as long as it's unoccupied; you can do so as a bonus action, or as a reaction when you trigger a trap or an opportunity attack as part of your movement (potentially avoiding the trap or attack). The afterimage fades after you teleport to it or at the end of your turn.

entries.name: Transitory State
entries: After you use the boots to teleport, you momentarily acquire the ethereal appearance of the afterimage, existing in a state of transition between the two locations. The next attack roll made against you before the start of your next turn is made with {@b disadvantage}.

entries.name: Cantrip Codex
entries: As a bonus action while within 30 feet of it, you can verbally command the book to spread its pages and follow you, or have it become inanimate. While following you, the book has a flying speed of 30 feet and attempts to stay within 30 feet of you at all times. You can give the book a spell scroll containing a cantrip that has a casting time of one action or bonus action and doesn't deal damage, and order it to incorporate it into its pages, destroying the scroll in the process. Once it's done so, you can use an action to command the book to cast that cantrip if it's following you, or cast it yourself while holding the inanimate book. The book can only have one cantrip within it at a time. Giving it a new one erases the previous one from its pages.

entries.name: Pass Notes
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell sending} spell while holding the book. When you cast the spell in this way, one of the book's pages tears off and folds into a paper aeroplane, which then disappears and reappears next to its intended target. The target of the spell has 1 minute to reply by writing a message on the back of the page. After this limit elapses, the aeroplane disappears. The word limit for either message equals five times the result of the writer's Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check, instead of twenty-five. Once this property has been used to cast this spell, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Cantrip Codex
entries: As a bonus action while within 30 feet of it, you can verbally command the book to spread its pages and follow you, or have it become inanimate. While following you, the book has a flying speed of 30 feet and attempts to stay within 30 feet of you at all times. You can give the book a spell scroll containing a cantrip that has a casting time of one action or bonus action and doesn't deal damage, and order it to incorporate it into its pages, destroying the scroll in the process. Once it's done so, you can use an action to command the book to cast that cantrip if it's following you, or cast it yourself while holding the inanimate book. The book can hold two cantrips within it instead of one. If you teach it a third one, you choose which of the two it already holds is erased.

entries.name: Pass Notes
entries: As an action, you can cast the {@spell sending} spell while holding the book. When you cast the spell in this way, one of the book's pages tears off and folds into a paper aeroplane, which then disappears and reappears next to its intended target. The target of the spell has 1 minute to reply by writing a message on the back of the page. After this limit elapses, the aeroplane disappears. The word limit for either message equals five times the result of the writer's Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check, instead of twenty-five. The Pass Notes property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Five-Shooter
entries: You can use these gloves as a spellcasting focus for your spells. When you do so, the spells are shot from the tips of your outstretched fingers.

entries.name: Quickdraw
entries: As a reaction when you roll for initiative while wearing these gloves, you can cast a cantrip that you know with a casting time of one action or bonus action.

entries.name: Five-Shooter
entries: You can use these gloves as a spellcasting focus for your spells. When you do so, the spells are shot from the tips of your outstretched fingers.

entries.name: Quickdraw
entries: As a reaction when you roll for initiative while wearing these gloves, you can cast a cantrip that you know with a casting time of one action or bonus action.

entries.name: Steady Aim
entries: As a bonus action while wearing these gloves, you can stretch one of your arms fully, pointing your finger at a creature and holding your elbow with your other hand to aim carefully. You gain {@b advantage} on the next ranged spell attack roll you make before the end of your next turn. After you activate this property, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1, you can't use this property again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Transmogrification
entries: When you drink this potion, a random part of your body acquires the characteristics of an animal for 24 hours. Roll a {@dice d6} to determine which part of you transforms and what special abilities you gain as a result.

rows: Your ears transform into elephant ears, which you can flap to take flight. You gain a flying speed of 10 feet.

You grow a marsupial pouch on your stomach, which functions as a {@item bag of holding}. When the potion's effect ends, the contents of the pouch are randomly spilled into unoccupied spaces within 5 feet of you.
Your skin becomes scaly and can change hues like a chameleon's. You gain {@b advantage} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide.

You grow a thick, muscular tail like that of a dinosaur's. You can use a bonus action to make an unarmed strike with the tail, which deals bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 1d4} + your Strength modifier on a hit.
You grow porcupine quills all over your body. A creature within 5 feet of you that hits you with a melee attack takes 1 piercing damage.

The nails on both your feet and hands elongate to resemble a sloth's claws. You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and have {@b advantage} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to climb.

entries.name: Transmogrification
entries: When you drink this potion, two random parts of your body acquire the characteristics of animals for 24 hours. Roll on the table twice, rerolling any duplicates, to determine which parts of you transform and what special abilities you gain as a result.

rows: Your ears transform into elephant ears, which you can flap to take flight. You gain a flying speed of 10 feet.

You grow a marsupial pouch on your stomach, which functions as a {@item bag of holding}. When the potion's effect ends, the contents of the pouch are randomly spilled into unoccupied spaces within 5 feet of you.
Your skin becomes scaly and can change hues like a chameleon's. You gain {@b advantage} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide.

You grow a thick, muscular tail like that of a dinosaur's. You can use a bonus action to make an unarmed strike with the tail, which deals bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 1d4} + your Strength modifier on a hit.
You grow porcupine quills all over your body. A creature within 5 feet of you that hits you with a melee attack takes 1 piercing damage.

The nails on both your feet and hands elongate to resemble a sloth's claws. You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and have {@b advantage} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to climb.

item.name: Kill Quill (Uncommon)
entries: The sword has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Retractable Tip
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can have it change into a mundane looking quill, or back into its original shape. While in the shape of a quill, it can't be used to make weapon attacks.

entries.name: Illustrated Bestiary
entries: As an action while holding this sword in either form, you can expend 1 charge to eject ink from its tip into an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. The ink then rapidly grows and coalesces into the shape of a beast, as long as there is room to accommodate it. The creature can be a {@creature hunter shark|MM} or {@creature rhinoceros|MM} (player's choice when summoned). Willing creatures can choose to be pushed to unoccupied spaces within 5 feet of the creature to allow it to grow. A creature summoned by this property is a construct instead of a beast, is friendly to you and your companions, and takes its turn immediately after yours. You control how the creature moves and behaves during its turn (no action required by you). The ink creature dissolves at the end of your next turn.

Willing creatures can choose to be pushed to unoccupied spaces within 5 feet of the creature to allow it to grow. A creature summoned by this property is a construct instead of a beast, is friendly to you and your companions, and takes its turn immediately after yours. You control how the creature moves and behaves during its turn (no action required by you). The ink creature dissolves at the end of your next turn.

A creature summoned by this property is a construct instead of a beast, is friendly to you and your companions, and takes its turn immediately after yours. You control how the creature moves and behaves during its turn (no action required by you). The ink creature dissolves at the end of your next turn.

item.name: Orb of the Prescient Protector (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. You can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Future Shield
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to choose a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you and look into its future, predicting an attack and placing a magical ward over it for the next minute. When that creature is hit by an attack during this time, it gains a +5 bonus to its AC until the end of the turn, potentially causing the triggering attack to miss. This effect ends early after it causes an attack to miss or if you use this property again.

item.name: Orb of the Prescient Protector (Rare)
entries: This item has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. You can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Future Shield
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to choose a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you and look into its future, predicting an attack and placing a magical ward over it for the next minute. When that creature is hit by an attack during this time, it gains a +5 bonus to its AC until the end of the turn, potentially causing the triggering attack to miss. This effect ends early after it causes an attack to miss or if you use this property again.

item.name: Orb of the Prescient Protector (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. You can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

entries.name: Future Shield
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to choose a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you and look into its future, predicting an attack and placing a magical ward over it for the next minute. When that creature is hit by an attack during this time, it gains a +5 bonus to its AC until the end of the turn, potentially causing the triggering attack to miss. This effect ends early after it causes an attack to miss or if you use this property again.

entries.name: Calculated Deflection
entries: When you successfully prevent an attack from hitting with the item's Future Shield property, you can use your reaction to see into the future once again and deflect it in a favourable way. Choose a creature within range of the attack, other than the attacker or the original target. That creature becomes the new target of the attack, using the attacker's original attack roll.

item.name: Summoner's Sickle (Uncommon)
entries: This weapon has 1 charge and regains it daily at dawn.

entries.name: Magical Armament
entries: This weapon has the {@itemProperty F||Finesse property} and can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells. If you know or have prepared a spell from the school of necromancy or conjuration, you are considered proficient with this sickle.

entries.name: Raise Hell
entries: As an action while holding this sickle, you can expend 1 charge to summon a {@creature swarm of skeletal imps|LALoTT} that claw their way out of the sickle's blade and congregate in a space within 5 feet of you. The swarm disappears after 1 hour, when you lose concentration on this effect (as if concentrating on a spell), or when it drops to 0 hit points. The swarm is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and avoids danger.

item.name: Summoner's Sickle (Rare)
entries: This weapon has 2 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Magical Armament
entries: This weapon has the {@itemProperty F||Finesse property} and can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells. If you know or have prepared a spell from the school of necromancy or conjuration, you are considered proficient with this sickle.

entries.name: Raise Hell
entries: As an action while holding this sickle, you can expend 1 charge to summon a {@creature swarm of skeletal imps|LALoTT} that claw their way out of the sickle's blade and congregate in a space within 5 feet of you. The swarm disappears after 1 hour, when you lose concentration on this effect (as if concentrating on a spell), or when it drops to 0 hit points. The swarm is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and avoids danger.

item.name: Summoner's Sickle (Very Rare)
entries: This weapon has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Magical Armament
entries: This weapon has the {@itemProperty F||Finesse property} and can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells. If you know or have prepared a spell from the school of necromancy or conjuration, you are considered proficient with this sickle.
