entries: As an action while holding this sickle, you can expend 1 charge to summon a {@creature swarm of skeletal imps|LALoTT} that claw their way out of the sickle's blade and congregate in a space within 5 feet of you. The swarm disappears after 1 hour, when you lose concentration on this effect (as if concentrating on a spell), or when it drops to 0 hit points. The swarm is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and avoids danger.

entries.name: Liftoff!
entries: While holding this firework, you can use an action to point it at a creature within 30 feet of you and speak its command word, causing a firework to shoot at the target. Make a ranged spell attack against the target ({@hit 5} to hit). On a hit, the target is lifted 30 feet up in a straight line by the firework. The firework then explodes in a shower of colourful sparks and is destroyed. Each creature within 15 feet of the target must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} fire damage and {@damage 3d6} thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The creature lifted up by the firework automatically fails on this saving throw, then falls to the ground unless it can hold itself aloft. The firework explodes prematurely if the target hits another creature or an obstacle during its ascent.

entries.name: Liftoff!
entries: While holding this firework, you can use an action to point it at a creature within 30 feet of you and speak its command word, causing a firework to shoot at the target. Make a ranged spell attack against the target ({@hit 7} to hit). On a hit, the target is lifted 30 feet up in a straight line by the firework. The firework then explodes in a shower of colourful sparks and is destroyed. Each creature within 15 feet of the target must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} fire damage and {@damage 4d6} thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The creature lifted up by the firework automatically fails on this saving throw, then falls to the ground unless it can hold itself aloft. The firework explodes prematurely if the target hits another creature or an obstacle during its ascent.

entries.name: Liftoff!
entries: While holding this firework, you can use an action to point it at a creature within 30 feet of you and speak its command word, causing a firework to shoot at the target. Make a ranged spell attack against the target ({@hit 8} to hit). On a hit, the target is dragged up to 30 feet in a path of your choice (vertically, horizontally, or diagonally) by the firework. The firework then explodes in a shower of colourful sparks and is destroyed. Each creature within 15 feet of the target must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} fire damage and {@damage 4d6} thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The creature dragged by the firework automatically fails on this saving throw, then falls to the ground unless it can hold itself aloft. The firework explodes prematurely if the target hits another creature or an obstacle during its ascent.

item.name: Barrier Bracer (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hexpansive Defence
entries: While wearing this bracer, when you or a creature you can see within 5 feet of you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to expend up to 5 charges, causing its shield to grow progressively larger with each one. The target of the attack gains a +1 bonus to AC against the triggering attack for each charge expended.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Barrier Bracer (Rare)
entries: This item has 12 charges and regains {@dice 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Hexpansive Defence
entries: While wearing this bracer, when you or a creature you can see within 5 feet of you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to expend up to 5 charges, causing its shield to grow progressively larger with each one. The target of the attack gains a +1 bonus to AC against the triggering attack for each charge expended.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Repulsive Field
entries: When the bonus to AC from the Hexpansive Defence property causes an attack to miss, if the attacker is within 5 feet of its target, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pushed back 10 feet and knocked {@condition prone}.

item.name: Flashfreezer (Uncommon)
entries: The staff has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Nitrogen Spray
entries: As a bonus action while holding this staff, you can expend 1 charge to spray a cloud of freezing mist from its tip in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or its speed is reduced by {@dice 1d6} x 5 feet until the end of its next turn, as small patches of ice form along its body. A creature whose speed is reduced to 0 feet by this effect is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the staff, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the staff has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Flashfreezer (Rare)
entries: The staff has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Nitrogen Spray
entries: As a bonus action while holding this staff, you can expend 1 charge to spray a cloud of freezing mist from its tip in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 1d6} cold damage and its speed is reduced by {@dice 1d6} x 5 feet until the end of its next turn, as small patches of ice form along its body. A creature whose speed is reduced to 0 feet by this effect is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the staff, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the staff has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Flashfreezer (Very Rare)
entries: The staff has 9 charges and regains {@dice 1d8 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Nitrogen Spray
entries: As a bonus action while holding this staff, you can expend 1 charge to spray a cloud of freezing mist from its tip in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d6} cold damage and its speed is reduced by {@dice 1d6} x 5 feet until the end of its next turn, as small patches of ice form along its body. A creature whose speed is reduced to 0 feet by this effect is also {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the staff, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the staff has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Frost & Flame (Rare)
entries: These two daggers count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. When you're holding both of these daggers, each one deals {@damage 1d4} extra damage to the first target it hits each turn. The extra damage of frost is cold damage, and the extra damage of flame is fire damage. The daggers have 3 charges and regain {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thermic Shock
entries: As an action while holding both of these daggers, you can expend 1 charge to supercharge them and lash out with both of them at once. Make a melee attack with the daggers against a target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d4} fire damage and {@damage 4d4} cold damage and, if it's a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls until the start of your next turn as it reels from the abrupt difference in temperatures.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the daggers, which immediately destroy the gemstone and regain 1 charge. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Frost & Flame (Very Rare)
entries: These two daggers count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. When you're holding both of these daggers, each one deals {@damage 1d4} extra damage to every target it hits. The extra damage of frost is cold damage, and the extra damage of flame is fire damage. The daggers have 4 charges and regain {@dice 1d3 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thermic Shock
entries: As an action while holding both of these daggers, you can expend 1 charge to supercharge them and lash out with both of them at once. Make a melee attack with the daggers against a target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d4} fire damage and {@damage 4d4} cold damage and, if it's a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or have {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls until the start of your next turn as it reels from the abrupt difference in temperatures.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the daggers, which immediately destroy the gemstone and regain 1 charge. Once this property of the daggers has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hexaminator (Uncommon)
entries: This mask has 2 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Detect Everything
entries: While wearing this mask, you can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells: {@spell detect evil and good}, {@spell detect magic}, {@spell detect poison and disease}, or {@spell find traps}. When you cast the {@spell find traps} spell in this way, you can see the location of traps within range, which appear surrounded by a red glow. Spells cast in this way don't require concentration and end early if you remove the mask.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 100 gp or more into the mask, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the mask has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hexaminator (Rare)
entries: This mask has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Detect Everything
entries: While wearing this mask, you can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells: {@spell detect evil and good}, {@spell detect magic}, {@spell detect poison and disease}, or {@spell find traps}. When you cast the {@spell find traps} spell in this way, you can see the location of traps within range, which appear surrounded by a red glow. Spells cast in this way don't require concentration and end early if you remove the mask.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 100 gp or more into the mask, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the mask has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hexaminator (Very Rare)
entries: This mask has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Detect Everything
entries: While wearing this mask, you can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells: {@spell detect evil and good}, {@spell detect magic}, {@spell detect poison and disease}, or {@spell find traps}. When you cast the {@spell find traps} spell in this way, you can see the location of traps within range, which appear surrounded by a red glow. Spells cast in this way don't require concentration and end early if you remove the mask.

entries.name: Hex Marks the Spot
entries: While wearing this mask, you can cast {@spell find the path}. When you cast the spell in this way, you see a faint trail of glowing haze leading you to the destination. The spell doesn't require concentration, and ends early if you remove the mask. Once this property of the mask has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 100 gp or more into the mask, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the mask has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hexecutioner Blade (Uncommon)
entries: This sword has 12 charges and regains {@dice 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Growing Pains
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to cause an oversized energy blade to form around the sword until the end of your turn. While this blade exists, the sword becomes a greatsword without the {@itemProperty 2H||Two-handed} and {@itemProperty H||Heavy} properties, and with the {@itemProperty L||Light} and {@itemProperty F||Finesse} properties. You are considered proficient with this greatsword.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the sword, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 2 charges. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hexecutioner Blade (Rare)
entries: This sword has 12 charges and regains {@dice 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Growing Pains
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to cause an oversized energy blade to form around the sword until the end of your turn. While this blade exists, the sword becomes a greatsword without the {@itemProperty 2H||Two-handed} and {@itemProperty H||Heavy} properties, and with the {@itemProperty L||Light} and {@itemProperty F||Finesse} properties. It deals an extra {@damage 1d6} force damage to the first target it hits each turn. You are considered proficient with this greatsword.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the sword, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 2 charges. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hex Runners (Uncommon)
entries: This item can function as a pair of prosthetic legs or can encase already existing legs. Either way, it can't be removed against your will while you remain attuned to it. While wearing the legs, your walking speed is increased by 5 feet.

entries.name: Kick into Overdrive
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this item, you can force the arcane crystals to pump even more energy into them. You take {@damage 1d4} fire damage as they overheat, and your walking speed increases by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Hex Runners (Rare)
entries: This item can function as a pair of prosthetic legs or can encase already existing legs. Either way, it can't be removed against your will while you remain attuned to it. While wearing the legs, your walking speed is increased by 5 feet.
This item has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Pulse Kick
entries: When you make an unarmed strike using your legs, you can expend 1 charge (no action required) to eject a pulse of energy from the crystals and propel your leg, adding more speed and power to your attack. On a hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d8} bludgeoning damage and, if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Kick into Overdrive
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this item, you can force the arcane crystals to pump even more energy into them. You take {@damage 1d4} fire damage as they overheat, and your walking speed increases by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries: This item can function as a pair of prosthetic legs or can encase already existing legs. Either way, it can't be removed against your will while you remain attuned to it. While wearing the legs, your walking speed is increased by 5 feet.
This item has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries: When you make an unarmed strike using your legs, you can expend 1 charge (no action required) to eject a pulse of energy from the crystals and propel your leg, adding more speed and power to your attack. On a hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d8} bludgeoning damage and, if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Kick into Overdrive
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this item, you can force the arcane crystals to pump even more energy into them. You take {@damage 1d4} fire damage as they overheat, and your walking speed increases by 20 feet until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hovercutter (Uncommon)
entries: The dagger has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Flick of the Wrist
entries: This dagger is composed of a gauntlet and a blade, has no handle, and can't be wielded by holding it. Instead, the blade hovers a few inches above the gauntlet at all times, can't be removed against your will, and can't be used to make ranged attacks except with the Daggerflight property. While the blade is hovering over the gauntlet and that hand is free, you can use it to attack normally.

entries.name: Daggerflight
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to make a ranged attack with the dagger. Hit or miss, you can then expend 1 additional charge to make another ranged attack with the dagger (no action required) against a different target within 20 feet of the first one. You can make a total of up to three attacks in this way, after which the dagger returns to hover over the gauntlet. The dagger manoeuvres around obstacles mid-flight, and ranged attacks made with it ignore half and three-quarters cover.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the dagger, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the dagger has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hovercutter (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The dagger has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Flick of the Wrist
entries: This dagger is composed of a gauntlet and a blade, has no handle, and can't be wielded by holding it. Instead, the blade hovers a few inches above the gauntlet at all times, can't be removed against your will, and can't be used to make ranged attacks except with the Daggerflight property. While the blade is hovering over the gauntlet and that hand is free, you can use it to attack normally.

entries.name: Daggerflight
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to make a ranged attack with the dagger. Hit or miss, you can then expend 1 additional charge to make another ranged attack with the dagger (no action required) against a different target within 20 feet of the first one. You can make a total of up to three attacks in this way, after which the dagger returns to hover over the gauntlet. The dagger manoeuvres around obstacles mid-flight, and ranged attacks made with it ignore half and three-quarters cover.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the dagger, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the dagger has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hovercutter (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The dagger has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Flick of the Wrist
entries: This dagger is composed of a gauntlet and a blade, has no handle, and can't be wielded by holding it. Instead, the blade hovers a few inches above the gauntlet at all times, can't be removed against your will, and can't be used to make ranged attacks except with the Daggerflight property. While the blade is hovering over the gauntlet and that hand is free, you can use it to attack normally.

entries.name: Daggerflight
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to make a ranged attack with the dagger. Hit or miss, you can then expend 1 additional charge to make another ranged attack with the dagger (no action required) against a different target within 20 feet of the first one. You can make a total of up to three attacks in this way, after which the dagger returns to hover over the gauntlet. The dagger manoeuvres around obstacles mid-flight, and ranged attacks made with it ignore half and three-quarters cover.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the dagger, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the dagger has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Preying Mantis (Rare)
entries: This item can function as a prosthetic forearm or can be inserted into your arm as part of the attuning process. Either way, it can't be removed against your will while you remain attuned to it. The item has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Mantis Jab
entries: While wearing the prosthesis, you can use a bonus action to extend or retract the blade within it, which functions as a shortsword while it's extended. While the blade is extended, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and make a special melee attack with it. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 1d8} poison damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. A spellcaster that fails this save takes {@damage 2d8} poison damage instead and can't cast spells of 1st level or higher until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 250 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Preying Mantis (Very Rare)
entries: This item can function as a prosthetic forearm or can be inserted into your arm as part of the attuning process. Either way, it can't be removed against your will while you remain attuned to it. The item has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Mantis Jab
entries: While wearing the prosthesis, you can use a bonus action to extend or retract the blade within it, which functions as a shortsword while it's extended. While the blade is extended, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and make a special melee attack with it. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 1d8} poison damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. A spellcaster that fails this save takes {@damage 2d8} poison damage instead and can't cast spells of 1st level or higher until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 250 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Bard's Best Friend
entries: This item is a musical instrument that you can play by tapping and rubbing its crystals. It can mimic the sounds of three different instruments, randomly determined upon the item's creation, or produce its own limpid, melodious tone. As a bonus action, you can command the instrument to follow you or go inert. While following you, it stays within your reach at all times, hovering a few feet above the ground. You are considered proficient with this instrument if you are a bard.

item.name: Tableturner (Rare)
entries: This item has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bard's Best Friend
entries: This item is a musical instrument that you can play by tapping and rubbing its crystals. It can mimic the sounds of three different instruments, randomly determined upon the item's creation, or produce its own limpid, melodious tone. As a bonus action, you can command the instrument to follow you or go inert. While following you, it stays within your reach at all times, hovering a few feet above the ground. You are considered proficient with this instrument while attuned to it.

entries.name: Remix
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you casts a spell or makes a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to subtly influence it with your music, expending 1 charge for each level of the spell being cast (minimum of 1), or 1 charge for a weapon attack. If the creature can hear you, it must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you pick new targets or a new point of origin for the attack or spell.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 250 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Tableturner (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bard's Best Friend
entries: This item is a musical instrument that you can play by tapping and rubbing its crystals. It can mimic the sounds of three different instruments, randomly determined upon the item's creation, or produce its own limpid, melodious tone. As a bonus action, you can command the instrument to follow you or go inert. While following you, it stays within your reach at all times, hovering a few feet above the ground. You are considered proficient with this instrument while attuned to it.

entries.name: Remix
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you casts a spell or makes a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to subtly influence it with your music, expending 1 charge for each level of the spell being cast (minimum of 1), or 1 charge for a weapon attack. If the creature can hear you, it must make a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you pick new targets or a new point of origin for the attack or spell.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 250 gp or more into the item, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 1 charge. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Aurumplate Robes (Rare)
entries: These robes can hold a maximum of 5 charges and lose all charges daily at dawn. When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher while wearing the robes, they gain a number of charges equal to the level of the spell.

entries.name: Discharge
entries: As a reaction when a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can expend any number of charges. The creature must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 1d10} force damage for every charge expended on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Recharge
entries: As a reaction when you fail a Constitution saving throw made to maintain concentration on a spell, you can expend a number of charges equal to the level of the spell (minimum of 1) to reroll the saving throw.

item.name: Aurumplate Robes (Very Rare)
entries: You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing the robes.

These robes can hold a maximum of 5 charges and lose all charges daily at dawn. When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher while wearing the robes, they gain a number of charges equal to the level of the spell.

entries.name: Discharge
entries: As a reaction when a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can expend any number of charges. The creature must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 1d10} force damage for every charge expended on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Recharge
entries: As a reaction when you fail a Constitution saving throw made to maintain concentration on a spell, you can expend a number of charges equal to the level of the spell (minimum of 1) to reroll the saving throw.

item.name: Bismuth Crown (Uncommon)
entries: The crown has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Mesmerise
entries: While wearing this crown in bright or dim light, you can use an action and expend 3 charges to cause the crystals to shift and shimmer hypnotically, casting {@spell hypnotic pattern} (save {@dc 13}) with the following changes:

items: The spell affects each creature within 10 feet of you that can see you, instead of a 30-foot cube. If you move more than 10 feet away from an affected creature, the effect ends for it.

The crown continues to shift and shimmer for the duration of the spell. Whenever a creature starts its turn within 10 feet of you while this is happening, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and force it to make a saving throw against the spell.

item.name: Bismuth Crown (Rare)
entries: The crown has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Mesmerise
entries: While wearing this crown in bright or dim light, you can use an action and expend 3 charges to cause the crystals to shift and shimmer hypnotically, casting {@spell hypnotic pattern} (save {@dc 15}) with the following changes:

items: The spell affects each creature within 10 feet of you that can see you, instead of a 30-foot cube. If you move more than 10 feet away from an affected creature, the effect ends for it.

The crown continues to shift and shimmer for the duration of the spell. Whenever a creature starts its turn within 10 feet of you while this is happening, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and force it to make a saving throw against the spell.

item.name: Bismuth Crown (Very Rare)
entries: The crown has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Mesmerise
entries: While wearing this crown in bright or dim light, you can use an action and expend 3 charges to cause the crystals to shift and shimmer hypnotically, casting {@spell hypnotic pattern} (save {@dc 16}) with the following changes:

items: The spell affects each creature within 10 feet of you that can see you, instead of a 30-foot cube. If you move more than 10 feet away from an affected creature, the effect ends for it.

The crown continues to shift and shimmer for the duration of the spell. Whenever a creature starts its turn within 10 feet of you while this is happening, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and force it to make a saving throw against the spell.

entries.name: Insulated Coating
entries: As an action while wearing this crown, you can cause the bismuth crystals to extend all over your body, forming a protective coating for 10 minutes. During this time, you have resistance to cold and fire damage, and if you use the Mesmerise property, all of the crystals covering you shift and shimmer. Once this property of the crown has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Blessed Bastion (Rare)
entries: You can use this shield as a spellcasting focus for your cleric and paladin spells.

entries.name: Symbol of Good
entries: The mere sight of this shield gives evil pause. While you're wielding the shield, hostile fiends and undead that can see it have {@b disadvantage} on initiative rolls. When you roll initiative, you gain {@dice 1d8} temporary hit points for each creature affected by this property, up to a maximum of {@dice 5d8}. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: If you are a cleric, you can enhance your attunement to this shield by proving that evil flees before your holy righteousness. In order to do so, you must use your Channel Divinity: Turn Undead feature to turn a total of 20 hostile undead while attuned to the shield. While enhanced, this shield gains the Vade Retro property:

entries.name: EA: Vade Retro
entries: As an action, you can speak the shield's command word to emit a pulse of divine magic from it, which affects all fiends and undead within 15 feet of you in one of the ways described below. Once this property of the shield has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: If a creature is undead, the ground surges upward to wrap around it, swallowing it into the grave it never should have left. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be {@condition restrained} for 1 minute. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way can use its action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check, freeing itself on a success. Undead more than 10 feet from the ground are unaffected by this property.

If a creature is a fiend, it is partially banished back to its plane of origin. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or become translucent and wispy for 1 minute. While in this state, it can move and interact with the world as normal, but its attacks, spells, and abilities deal half damage. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

item.name: Blessed Bastion (Very Rare)
entries: While wielding this shield, you have resistance to necrotic damage. You can use this shield as a spellcasting focus for your cleric and paladin spells.

entries.name: Symbol of Good
entries: The mere sight of this shield gives evil pause. While you're wielding the shield, hostile fiends and undead that can see it have {@b disadvantage} on initiative rolls. When you roll initiative, you gain {@dice 1d8} temporary hit points for each creature affected by this property, up to a maximum of {@dice 5d8}. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: If you are a cleric, you can enhance your attunement to this shield by proving that evil flees before your holy righteousness. In order to do so, you must use your Channel Divinity: Turn Undead feature to turn a total of 20 hostile undead while attuned to the shield. While enhanced, this shield gains the Vade Retro property:

entries.name: EA: Vade Retro
entries: As an action, you can speak the shield's command word to emit a pulse of divine magic from it, which affects all fiends and undead within 15 feet of you in one of the ways described below. Once this property of the shield has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items: If a creature is undead, the ground surges upward to wrap around it, swallowing it into the grave it never should have left. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be {@condition restrained} for 1 minute. A creature {@condition restrained} in this way can use its action to make a {@dc 16} Strength check, freeing itself on a success. Undead more than 10 feet from the ground are unaffected by this property.

If a creature is a fiend, it is partially banished back to its plane of origin. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Charisma saving throw or become translucent and wispy for 1 minute. While in this state, it can move and interact with the world as normal, but its attacks, spells, and abilities deal half damage. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

item.name: Cosmic Starknife (Uncommon)
entries: The starknife has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Galactic Disc Throw
entries: As an action while holding the starknife, you can expend 1 charge to channel the cosmic energy in the weapon and throw it in a 15-foot-long line. When you do, the starknife spins rapidly and takes the shape of a disc galaxy as it travels. Make a ranged weapon attack with the starknife against each creature in the line. After it reaches the end of the line, the starknife stops and spins in place until the end of the turn, at which point it returns to you in a straight line. When it does, make another ranged weapon attack with the starknife against each creature in that line. Attacks made with this property deal slashing damage instead of piercing damage. If the spinning starknife hits a Medium or larger object at any point, it stops spinning and flies back to your hand.

item.name: Cosmic Starknife (Rare)
entries: The starknife has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. Immediately after you make a ranged weapon attack with the starknife, you can have it fly back to your hand.

entries.name: Galactic Disc Throw
entries: As an action while holding the starknife, you can expend 1 charge to channel the cosmic energy in the weapon and throw it in a 20-foot-long line. When you do, the starknife spins rapidly and takes the shape of a disc galaxy as it travels. Make a ranged weapon attack with the starknife against each creature in the line. After it reaches the end of the line, the starknife stops and spins in place until the end of the turn, at which point it returns to you in a straight line. When it does, make another ranged weapon attack with the starknife against each creature in that line. Attacks made with this property deal slashing damage instead of piercing damage, and deal an extra {@damage 1d4} force damage on a hit. If the spinning starknife hits a Medium or larger object at any point, it stops spinning and flies back to your hand.

item.name: Cosmic Starknife (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The starknife has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. Immediately after you make a ranged weapon attack with the starknife, you can have it fly back to your hand.

entries.name: Galactic Disc Throw
entries: As an action while holding the starknife, you can expend 1 charge to channel the cosmic energy in the weapon and throw it in a 25-foot-long line. When you do, the starknife spins rapidly and takes the shape of a disc galaxy as it travels. Make a ranged weapon attack with the starknife against each creature in the line. After it reaches the end of the line, the starknife stops and spins in place until the end of the turn, at which point it returns to you in a straight line. When it does, make another ranged weapon attack with the starknife against each creature in that line. Attacks made with this property deal slashing damage instead of piercing damage, and deal an extra {@damage 1d4} force damage on a hit. If the spinning starknife hits a Medium or larger object at any point, it stops spinning and flies back to your hand.

item.name: Magebind Shackles (Rare)
entries: These shackles function similarly to a mundane pair of manacles, but have 50 hit points instead of 15 and move with the {@condition restrained} creature if it teleports.

entries.name: Cold Clasp
entries: As an action while holding these shackles, you can speak their command word to attempt to teleport them directly onto a fey or spellcaster you can see within 10 feet of you. The target creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or be suddenly {@condition restrained} by the shackles. The shackles typically fasten themselves around a creature's wrists and neck, but can change their form to accommodate different sizes and anatomies.

The {@condition restrained} creature can attempt a {@dc 15} Charisma check to escape them, teleporting them back to the original wielder on a success. Breaking out of the shackles with a successful Strength check only forces them open and doesn't destroy them. In addition, every time the {@condition restrained} creature takes an action, bonus action, or reaction, the shackles deal {@damage 1d4} force damage to it. If any of those are taken to cast a spell, the shackles deal {@damage 2d4} damage instead.
This property of the shackles can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Magebind Shackles (Very Rare)
entries: These shackles function similarly to a mundane pair of manacles, but have 50 hit points instead of 15 and move with the {@condition restrained} creature if it teleports.

entries.name: Cold Clasp
entries: As an action while holding these shackles, you can speak their command word to attempt to teleport them directly onto a fey or spellcaster you can see within 10 feet of you. The target creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Charisma saving throw or be suddenly {@condition restrained} by the shackles. The shackles typically fasten themselves around a creature's wrists and neck, but can change their form to accommodate different sizes and anatomies.

The {@condition restrained} creature can attempt a {@dc 16} Charisma check to escape them, teleporting them back to the original wielder on a success. Breaking out of the shackles with a successful Strength check only forces them open and doesn't destroy them. In addition, every time the {@condition restrained} creature takes an action, bonus action, or reaction, the shackles deal {@damage 1d10} force damage to it. If any of those are taken to cast a spell, the shackles deal {@damage 2d10} damage instead.
This property of the shackles can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Mete-ore Bracers (Uncommon)
entries: These bracers have 2 charges and regain {@dice 1d2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Gravity Manipulation
entries: As an action while wearing the bracers, you can expend 1 charge and touch a creature to produce one of the following effects:

items.name: Zero Gravity
entries: You cast the {@spell levitate} spell (save {@dc 13}) on that creature.

items.name: Augmented Gravity
entries: The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be overwhelmed by immense gravitational pressure, becoming {@condition restrained} for 1 minute or until you lose concentration on this effect, as if concentrating on a spell. A creature {@condition restrained} by this effect takes {@damage 2d6} force damage from the pressure at the start of each of its turns.

item.name: Mete-ore Bracers (Rare)
entries: These bracers have 4 charges and regain {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Gravity Manipulation
entries: As an action while wearing the bracers, you can expend 1 charge and touch a creature to produce one of the following effects:

items.name: Zero Gravity
entries: You cast the {@spell levitate} spell (save {@dc 15}) on that creature.

items.name: Augmented Gravity
entries: The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be overwhelmed by immense gravitational pressure, becoming {@condition restrained} for 1 minute or until you lose concentration on this effect, as if concentrating on a spell. A creature {@condition restrained} by this effect takes {@damage 2d6} force damage from the pressure at the start of each of its turns.

item.name: Mete-ore Bracers (Very Rare)
entries: These bracers have 4 charges and regain {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Gravity Manipulation
entries: As an action while wearing the bracers, you can expend 1 charge and touch a creature to produce one of the following effects:

items.name: Zero Gravity
entries: You cast the {@spell levitate} spell (save {@dc 16}) on that creature.

items.name: Augmented Gravity
entries: The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be overwhelmed by immense gravitational pressure, becoming {@condition restrained} for 1 minute or until you lose concentration on this effect, as if concentrating on a spell. A creature {@condition restrained} by this effect takes {@damage 2d6} force damage from the pressure at the start of each of its turns.

entries.name: Sudden Release
entries: When the effect of the Gravitation Manipulation property ends on a creature, you can choose to end it with a burst of gravitational force in the opposite direction. If the creature was affected by Zero Gravity, it's slammed to the ground instead of floating down gently, and takes falling damage as normal. If the creature was affected by Augmented Gravity, it's thrown 20 feet into the air, after which it falls unless it has a way of holding itself aloft.

item.name: Mithral Exsanguinator (Uncommon)
entries: While you are below half your hit point maximum, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Power from Pain
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can sacrifice your own vitality to empower it. You take {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage, and the next time you hit with an attack using this weapon before the end of your turn, it deals an extra {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. Damage you take from this property ignores temporary hit points and can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you are below half of your hit point maximum when you use this property, the extra damage increases to {@damage 3d6}.

item.name: Mithral Exsanguinator (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While you are below half of your hit point maximum, this bonus increases to +2.

entries.name: Power from Pain
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can sacrifice your own vitality to empower it. You take {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage, and the next time you hit with an attack using this weapon before the end of your turn, it deals an extra {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. Damage you take from this property ignores temporary hit points and can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you are below half of your hit point maximum when you use this property, the extra damage increases to {@damage 3d6}.

item.name: Mithral Exsanguinator (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While you are below half of your hit point maximum, this bonus increases to +3.

entries.name: Power from Pain
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can sacrifice your own vitality to empower it. You take {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage, and the next time you hit with an attack using this weapon before the end of your turn, it deals an extra {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. Damage you take from this property ignores temporary hit points and can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you are below half of your hit point maximum when you use this property, the extra damage increases to {@damage 3d6}.

item.name: Spellbreaker (Uncommon)
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} damage to fey and spellcasters. It has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Antimagic Strike
entries: When you hit a creature concentrating on a spell with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to give it {@b disadvantage} on its Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration.

item.name: Spellbreaker (Rare)
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage to fey and spellcasters. It has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Antimagic Strike
entries: When you hit a creature concentrating on a spell with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to give it {@b disadvantage} on its Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration. If the creature's concentration is broken as a result of this, choose one of the following options:

items.name: Absorb
entries: The war pick absorbs some of the spell's magic. Your next attack that hits with it before the end of your next turn deals an extra {@damage 3d6} force damage.

items.name: Disrupt
entries: The war pick disrupts the flow of magic within the target's body. The creature you hit can't cast spells that require concentration again until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Spellbreaker (Very Rare)
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage to fey and spellcasters. It has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Antimagic Strike
entries: When you hit a creature concentrating on a spell with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to give it {@b disadvantage} on its Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration. If the creature's concentration is broken as a result of this, choose one of the following options:

items.name: Absorb
entries: The war pick absorbs some of the spell's magic. Your next attack that hits with it before the end of your next turn deals an extra {@damage 3d6} force damage.

items.name: Disrupt
entries: The war pick disrupts the flow of magic within the target's body. The creature you hit can't cast spells that require concentration again until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Catclaw & Sharkfin (Rare)
entries: These two claws count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. The claws can change shape so that either one can be worn on either hand.

entries.name: Set Up
entries: Attacks made with catclaw deal force or slashing damage (your choice). When you hit a creature with this weapon, it becomes marked until the end of the current turn. A marked creature can't take reactions.

entries.name: Finishing Touch
entries: Attacks made with sharkfin deal piercing or slashing damage (your choice). If a creature is marked by catclaw, you deal an extra {@damage 2d6} damage on a hit.

item.name: Catclaw & Sharkfin (Uncommon)
entries: These two claws count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. The claws can change shape so that either one can be worn on either hand.

entries.name: Set Up
entries: Attacks made with catclaw deal force or slashing damage (your choice). When you hit a creature with this weapon, it becomes marked until the end of the current turn.

entries.name: Finishing Touch
entries: Attacks made with sharkfin deal piercing or slashing damage (your choice). If a creature is marked by catclaw, you deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage on a hit.

item.name: Catclaw & Sharkfin (Very Rare)
entries: These two claws count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. The claws can change shape so that either one can be worn on either hand.

entries.name: Set Up
entries: Attacks made with catclaw deal force or slashing damage (your choice). When you hit a creature with this weapon, it becomes marked until the end of the current turn. A marked creature can't take reactions.

entries.name: Finishing Touch
entries: Attacks made with sharkfin deal piercing or slashing damage (your choice). If a creature is marked by catclaw, you have {@b advantage} on attack rolls against it with sharkfin, and deal an extra {@damage 2d6} damage on a hit.

item.name: Dingotamatone (Rare)
entries: This curious item can be used as a musical instrument by squeezing its head to produce sound and placing your fingers on the stem to control the pitch. If you are proficient with a musical instrument, you are proficient with the dingotamatone, which can be used as a spellcasting focus for bard spells. This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Dance-Off
entries: As an action, you can choose a creature within 30 feet that can hear you and that you can see, expending 1 charge to challenge it to a dance competition. The dingotamatone begins to play a song in a random key, and you and the selected creature must make contested Charisma ({@skill Performance}) checks. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table below to determine the key of the song and the outcomes of the contested check. If the effect requires a saving throw, the DC is 15.

rows: The victor casts {@spell acid rain|LALoTT}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell bestow curse} on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell confusion}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell depth charge|LALoTT}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell enrage|LALoTT}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell fireball}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell gravity smash|LALoTT}, centred on the loser.
Select any key of your choice, other than this one.

item.name: Dingotamatone (Uncommon)
entries: This curious item can be used as a musical instrument by squeezing its head to produce sound and placing your fingers on the stem to control the pitch. If you are proficient with a musical instrument, you are proficient with the dingotamatone, which can be used as a spellcasting focus for bard spells. This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries: As an action, you can choose a creature within 30 feet that can hear you and that you can see, expending 1 charge to challenge it to a dance competition. The dingotamatone begins to play a song in a random key, and you and the selected creature must make contested Charisma ({@skill Performance}) checks. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table below to determine the key of the song and the outcomes of the contested check. If the effect requires a saving throw, the DC is 13.

rows: The victor casts {@spell acid arrow} ({@hit 5} to hit) on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell blindness/deafness} on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell calm emotions}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell darkness}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell entangle}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell faerie fire}, centred on the loser.
The victor casts {@spell grease}, centred on the loser.
Select any key of your choice, other than this one.
