entries.name: Grand Finale
entries: Whenever a creature casts a spell with the Dance-Off property, roll a {@dice d20}. If the result is equal to or lower than the level of the spell, that creature triggers a wild magic surge.

item.name: Dungeoneer's Shield (Rare)
entries: This shield has 6 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sharp Surprise
entries: As a bonus action while wielding this shield, you can extend or retract the blade within it. While the blade is extended, it can be used as a magical shortsword. If you are proficient with shortswords or shields, you are considered proficient with this shield and shortsword.

entries.name: Tier List
entries: As a bonus action while wielding the shield, you can expend 1 charge to ask a question about a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. The question must be about one of the attributes listed in the table below, and the answer you receive is always in the form of a letter indicating what interval the attribute is in.

item.name: Dungeoneer's Shield (Uncommon)
entries: This shield has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sharp Surprise
entries: As a bonus action while wielding this shield, you can extend or retract the blade within it. While the blade is extended, it can be used as a magical shortsword, but the shield increases your AC by 1 instead of 2. If you are proficient with shortswords or shields, you are considered proficient with this shield and shortsword.

entries.name: Tier List
entries: As a bonus action while wielding the shield, you can expend 1 charge to ask a question about a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. The question must be about one of the attributes listed in the table below, and the answer you receive is always in the form of a letter indicating what interval the attribute is in.

item.name: Dungeoneer's Shield (Very Rare)
entries: While wielding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This shield has 6 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sharp Surprise
entries: As a bonus action while wielding this shield, you can extend or retract the blade within it. While the blade is extended, it can be used as a magical shortsword. If you are proficient with shortswords or shields, you are considered proficient with this shield and shortsword.

entries.name: Tier List
entries: As a bonus action while wielding the shield, you can expend 1 charge to ask a question about a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. The question must be about one of the attributes listed in the table below, and the answer you receive is always in the form of a letter indicating what interval the attribute is in.

entries.name: Heroic Recovery
entries: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll made with the shield's shortsword, you can narrate an incredible and unlikely recovery from your fumble in a grandiose and flowery manner. If you do, you can reroll the attack roll with {@b advantage}. Once this property of the shield has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Griffon's Cloak (Rare)
entries: This cloak has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Planar Passport
entries: When this cloak visits a plane of existence, it becomes capable of acting as the material component for the {@spell plane shift} spell, attuned to that particular plane. If found, the cloak is already attuned to {@dice 1d4} different planes of existence.

entries.name: Controlled Blink
entries: While wearing the cloak, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell blink} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, you roll a second {@dice d20} at the end of each of your turns and choose which result to take.

entries.name: Planar Passport
entries: When this cloak visits a plane of existence, it becomes capable of acting as the material component for the {@spell plane shift} spell, attuned to that particular plane. If found, the cloak is already attuned to {@dice 1d4} different planes of existence.

entries.name: Controlled Blink
entries: While wearing the cloak, you can cast the {@spell blink} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, you roll a second {@dice d20} at the end of each of your turns and choose which result to take. Once it's been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Griffon's Cloak (Very Rare)
entries: This cloak has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Planar Passport
entries: When this cloak visits a plane of existence, it becomes capable of acting as the material component for the {@spell plane shift} spell, attuned to that particular plane. If found, the cloak is already attuned to {@dice 1d4} different planes of existence.

entries.name: Controlled Blink
entries: While wearing the cloak, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell blink} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, you roll a second {@dice d20} at the end of each of your turns and choose which result to take.

entries.name: One Way Ticket
entries: While wearing the cloak, you can cast the {@spell plane shift} spell (save {@dc 17}). After you do so, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the cloak loses this property. On any other result, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dungeoneering 101
entries: When you first attune to this helm, choose one of the following skills: {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Perception}, {@skill Stealth}, or {@skill Survival}. While wearing the helm, you are proficient in the chosen skill and have {@b advantage} on ability checks that use it.

entries.name: Helpful Nudge
entries: While wearing the helm, when a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your reaction to turn the d20 to one of the faces adjacent to the original result, taking the number on it as the result of the roll. For example, if a creature rolls a 14, you can turn it into a 4, 6, or 20. Once this property of the helm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Helpful Nudge
entries: While wearing the helm, when a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your reaction to turn the d20 to one of the faces adjacent to the original result, taking the number on it as the result of the roll. For example, if a creature rolls a 14, you can turn it into a 4, 6, or 20. Once this property of the helm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Helm of Iron Will (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to AC while wearing the helm.

entries.name: Dungeoneering 101
entries: When you first attune to this helm, choose one of the following skills: {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Perception}, {@skill Stealth}, or {@skill Survival}. While wearing the helm, you are proficient in the chosen skill and have {@b advantage} on ability checks that use it.

entries.name: Helpful Nudge
entries: While wearing the helm, when a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your reaction to turn the d20 to one of the faces adjacent to the original result, taking the number on it as the result of the roll. For example, if a creature rolls a 14, you can turn it into a 4, 6, or 20. Once this property of the helm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Archmage's Hand
entries: While wearing these gloves, you can cast the {@spell mage hand} cantrip, with the following changes:

items: You can create two spectral hands instead of one, and you can control any number of them as a bonus action.

The spell lasts until you are {@condition unconscious} or choose to end it (no action required). If a hand is ever more than 30 feet away from you, it immediately disappears and reappears in your space.
The hands are stronger than normal, and each is capable of lifting a weight in pounds equal to 10 x your proficiency bonus.
The hands are more dexterous than normal, and you can use them to perform ability checks that you would be able to perform using your hands.
When you control the hands, you can use each one to make a melee spell attack ({@hit 7} to hit) against a target within 5 feet of it, dealing {@damage 1d6} force damage on a hit.

entries.name: Archmage's Hand
entries: While wearing these gloves, you can cast the {@spell mage hand} cantrip, with the following changes:

items: You can create one spectral hand, and you can control it as a bonus action.

The spell lasts until you are {@condition unconscious} or choose to end it (no action required). If a hand is ever more than 30 feet away from you, it immediately disappears and reappears in your space.
The hands are stronger than normal, and each is capable of lifting a weight in pounds equal to 10 x your proficiency bonus.
The hands are more dexterous than normal, and you can use them to perform ability checks that you would be able to perform using your hands.
When you control the hand, you can use it to make a melee spell attack ({@hit 5} to hit) against a target within 5 feet of it, dealing {@damage 1d6} force damage on a hit.

entries.name: Archmage's Hand
entries: While wearing these gloves, you can cast the {@spell mage hand} cantrip, with the following changes:

items: You can create three spectral hands instead of one, and you can control any number of them as a bonus action.

The spell lasts until you are {@condition unconscious} or choose to end it (no action required). If a hand is ever more than 30 feet away from you, it immediately disappears and reappears in your space.
The hands are stronger than normal, and each is capable of lifting a weight in pounds equal to 10 x your proficiency bonus.
The hands are more dexterous than normal, and you can use them to perform ability checks that you would be able to perform using your hands.
When you control the hands, you can use each one to make a melee spell attack ({@hit 8} to hit) against a target within 5 feet of it, dealing {@damage 1d6} force damage on a hit.

entries.name: Moonlight Map
entries: As an action while holding this item in dim or bright light, you can point it at a flat surface within 10 feet of you, causing light to shine through the magnifying glass and form the image of a map on that surface for 10 minutes or until you speak a command word to make it disappear. This map represents the area in a 100-foot square centred on you, at your elevation. If a creature, object, or area is warded against divination magic, it doesn't show on the map. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Moonlight Map
entries: As an action while holding this item in dim or bright light, you can point it at a flat surface within 10 feet of you, causing light to shine through the magnifying glass and form the image of a map on that surface for 10 minutes or until you speak a command word to make it disappear. This map represents the area in a 200-foot square centred on you, at your elevation. When it forms, choose one of the additional effects below.

items: The size of the map is increased to a 300-foot square.
The map shows traps and other hazards in the area marked in red.
The map shows secret doors and passages in the area marked in yellow.
The map shows creatures in the area at the time it formed as small white dots.

entries: If a creature, object, or area is warded against divination magic, it doesn't show on the map. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Mapifying Glass (Rare)
entries: This item has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Moonlight Map
entries: As an action while holding this item in dim or bright light, you can expend 1 charge and point it at a flat surface within 10 feet of you, causing light to shine through the magnifying glass and form the image of a map on that surface for 10 minutes or until you speak a command word to make it disappear. This map represents the area in a 200-foot square centred on you, at your elevation. When it forms, choose one or more of the additional effects below. For each effect chosen beyond the first, you must expend 1 additional charge.

items: The size of the map is increased to a 300-foot square.
The map shows traps and other hazards in the area marked in red.
The map shows secret doors and passages in the area marked in yellow.
The map shows creatures in the area at the time it formed as small white dots.

entries: If a creature, object, or area is warded against divination magic, it doesn't show on the map.

item.name: Monkey's Paw (Rare)
entries: The arm has 2 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Costly Attunement
entries: When you attune to this item, its corrupted, abyssal flesh merges with your own, replacing a missing arm or consuming an existing one (your choice). An arm consumed in this way doesn't reappear when your attunement to the item ends.

entries.name: Trick Weapon
entries: As a bonus action, you can cause the arm to enlarge to absurd proportions or revert to its normal size. The arm has different properties depending on its size, as described below:

items.name: Normal
entries: You can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells from the arm (save {@dc 15}): {@spell counterspell}, {@spell fear}, or {@spell hypnotic pattern}.

items.name: Enlarged
entries: Your arm becomes a maul with which you are proficient, and can't be used to wield other weapons or shields, or otherwise handle items or perform the somatic component of spells. This maul has the {@itemProperty R||Reach property} and doesn't have the {@itemProperty 2H||Two-handed property}.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This arm is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed in this way, you become tremendously unlucky. The GM decides how this lack of luck manifests itself. In addition, if you cast the {@spell wish} spell to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell, it comes into effect in a way that brings you as much misfortune as possible while still fitting your literal wording.

item.name: Monkey's Paw (Very Rare)
entries: The arm has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Costly Attunement
entries: When you attune to this item, its corrupted, abyssal flesh merges with your own, replacing a missing arm or consuming an existing one (your choice). An arm consumed in this way doesn't reappear when your attunement to the item ends.

entries.name: Trick Weapon
entries: As a bonus action, you can cause the arm to enlarge to absurd proportions or revert to its normal size. The arm has different properties depending on its size, as described below:

items.name: Normal
entries: You can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells from the arm (save {@dc 16}): {@spell counterspell}, {@spell fear}, or {@spell hypnotic pattern}.

items.name: Enlarged
entries: Your arm becomes a +1 maul with which you are proficient, and can't be used to wield other weapons or shields, or otherwise handle items or perform the somatic component of spells. This maul has the {@itemProperty R||Reach property} and doesn't have the {@itemProperty 2H||Two-handed property}.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This arm is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed in this way, you become tremendously unlucky. The GM decides how this lack of luck manifests itself. In addition, if you cast the {@spell wish} spell to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell, it comes into effect in a way that brings you as much misfortune as possible while still fitting your literal wording.

item.name: Monkey's Paw (Legendary)
entries: The arm has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Costly Attunement
entries: When you attune to this item, its corrupted, abyssal flesh merges with your own, replacing a missing arm or consuming an existing one (your choice). An arm consumed in this way doesn't reappear when your attunement to the item ends.

entries.name: Trick Weapon
entries: As a bonus action, you can cause the arm to enlarge to absurd proportions or revert to its normal size. The arm has different properties depending on its size, as described below:

items.name: Normal
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to the attack and damage rolls of any weapon with the Finesse property that you wield with it (to a maximum of +3), and you have {@b advantage} on Sleight of Hand checks. You can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells from the arm (save {@dc 17}): {@spell counterspell}, {@spell fear}, or {@spell hypnotic pattern}.

items.name: Enlarged
entries: Your arm becomes a +2 maul with which you are proficient, and can't be used to wield other weapons or shields, or otherwise handle items or perform the somatic component of spells. This maul has the {@itemProperty R||Reach property} and doesn't have the {@itemProperty 2H||Two-handed property}.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This arm is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed in this way, you become tremendously unlucky. The GM decides how this lack of luck manifests itself. In addition, if you cast the {@spell wish} spell to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell, it comes into effect in a way that brings you as much misfortune as possible while still fitting your literal wording.

entries.name: Artistic Licence
entries: As an action while holding the paintbrush, you can paint one monstrous feature of your choice from the list below on a willing creature within 5 feet of you, granting it a special benefit. The feature and its benefits fade after 10 minutes, or when a new feature is painted on the creature. Once you've used this property to paint a feature, the paintbrush can't produce that feature again until the next dawn.

items.name: Claws, Horns, or Tail
entries: The creature gains a natural weapon that deals bludgeoning (tail), piercing (horns), or slashing (claws) damage equal to {@damage 1d6} + its Strength modifier. It can use a bonus action to make an attack with this natural weapon.

items.name: Extra Eyes
entries: The creature gains darkvision out to 30 feet and {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight.

items.name: Fins and Gills
entries: The creature gains a swimming speed of 30 feet and can breathe underwater.

items.name: Wings
entries: The creature gains a flying speed of 15 feet.

entries.name: Artistic Licence
entries: As an action while holding the paintbrush, you can paint one monstrous feature of your choice from the list below on a willing creature within 5 feet of you, granting it a special benefit. The feature and its benefits fade after 1 hour, or when a new feature is painted on the creature. Once you've used this property to paint a feature, the paintbrush can't produce that feature again until the next dawn.

items.name: Claws, Horns, or Tail
entries: The creature gains a natural weapon that deals bludgeoning (tail), piercing (horns), or slashing (claws) damage equal to {@damage 1d8} + its Strength modifier. It can use a bonus action to make an attack with this natural weapon.

items.name: Extra Eyes
entries: The creature gains darkvision out to 60 feet and {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight.

items.name: Fins and Gills
entries: The creature gains a swimming speed of 60 feet and can breathe underwater.

items.name: Wings
entries: The creature gains a flying speed of 30 feet.

entries.name: Artistic Licence
entries: As an action while holding the paintbrush, you can paint one monstrous feature of your choice from the list below on a willing creature within 5 feet of you, granting it a special benefit. The feature and its benefits fade after 1 hour, or when a new feature is painted on the creature. When you paint a monstrous feature, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 5 or 6, you can paint that feature again. On a 1 to 4, the paintbrush can't produce that feature again until the next dawn.

items.name: Claws, Horns, or Tail
entries: The creature gains a natural weapon that deals bludgeoning (tail), piercing (horns), or slashing (claws) damage equal to {@damage 1d8} + its Strength modifier. It can use a bonus action to make an attack with this natural weapon.

items.name: Extra Eyes
entries: The creature gains darkvision out to 60 feet and {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight.

items.name: Fins and Gills
entries: The creature gains a swimming speed of 60 feet and can breathe underwater.

items.name: Wings
entries: The creature gains a flying speed of 30 feet.

item.name: Portable Potion Press (Uncommon)
entries: You can use this item as a set of {@item alchemist's supplies|PHB} and as a {@item poisoner's kit|PHB}.

entries.name: Waste Not
entries: As an action, you can insert a body part from a creature of CR 1 or higher that has been dead for no more than 1 hour into the alchemical receptacle on this backpack. Over the course of 1 hour, the backpack presses and distils this body part into restorative fluid. Once this process is complete, roll one of the Hit Dice of the creature the body part belonged to and add the result to the backpack's pool of points, which is stored in a small, detachable bottle. This pool can hold up to 30 points. As an action, a creature can remove this bottle and drink its contents, gaining temporary hit points equal to the number of points in the pool, which last for 1 hour. If you are wearing the backpack, you can do this as a bonus action through the use of a drinking tube.

item.name: Portable Potion Press (Rare)
entries: You can use this item as a set of {@item alchemist's supplies|PHB} and as a {@item poisoner's kit|PHB}.

entries.name: Waste Not
entries: As an action, you can insert a body part from a creature of CR 1 or higher that has been dead for no more than 1 hour into the alchemical receptacle on this backpack. Over the course of 1 hour, the backpack presses and distils this body part into restorative fluid. Once this process is complete, roll two of the Hit Dice of the creature the body part belonged to and add the result to the backpack's pool of points, which is stored in a small, detachable bottle. This pool can hold up to 40 points. As an action, a creature can remove this bottle and drink its contents, gaining temporary hit points equal to the number of points in the pool, which last for 1 hour. If you are wearing the backpack, you can do this as a bonus action through the use of a drinking tube.

item.name: Portable Potion Press (Very Rare)
entries: You can use this item as a set of {@item alchemist's supplies|PHB} and as a {@item poisoner's kit|PHB}.

entries.name: Waste Not
entries: As an action, you can insert a body part from a creature of CR 1 or higher that has been dead for no more than 1 hour into the alchemical receptacle on this backpack. Over the course of 1 hour, the backpack presses and distils this body part into restorative fluid. Once this process is complete, roll four of the Hit Dice of the creature the body part belonged to and add the result to the backpack's pool of points, which is stored in a small, detachable bottle. This pool can hold up to 50 points. As an action, a creature can remove this bottle and drink its contents, gaining temporary hit points equal to the number of points in the pool, which last for 1 hour. If you are wearing the backpack, you can do this as a bonus action through the use of a drinking tube.

item.name: Bloom Boots (Uncommon)
entries: These boots have 4 charges and regain {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Induce Growth
entries: While wearing these boots and standing on the ground, you can expend 1 or more charges to stomp one of your feet and produce one of the following effects:

items: You cast the {@spell plant growth} spell, centred on yourself and as an action only (3 charges).
As a bonus action, you cause a large vine to grow beneath your feet, lifting you up to 30 feet straight up. This vine remains for 1 minute, after which it withers and disappears (1 charge).

As a reaction when you're subjected to an effect that would move you against your will, you cause small vines to wrap around your feet, preventing you from being moved until the start of your next turn (2 charges).

item.name: Bloom Boots (Rare)
entries: These boots have 6 charges and regain {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Plantstep
entries: While wearing these boots, grass grows and flowers bloom from the ground where you step, remaining for a few seconds before disappearing. You can move through plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard. In addition, you have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed when climbing trees and other plants, as small branches and leaves quickly grow to support you, then wither.

entries.name: Induce Growth
entries: While wearing these boots and standing on the ground, you can expend 1 or more charges to stomp one of your feet and produce one of the following effects:

items: You cast the {@spell plant growth} spell, centred on yourself and as an action only (3 charges).
As a bonus action, you cause a large vine to grow beneath your feet, lifting you up to 30 feet straight up. This vine remains for 1 minute, after which it withers and disappears (1 charge).

As a reaction when you're subjected to an effect that would move you against your will, you cause small vines to wrap around your feet, preventing you from being moved until the start of your next turn (2 charges).

item.name: Bloom Boots (Very Rare)
entries: These boots have 8 charges and regain {@dice 1d4 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Plantstep
entries: While wearing these boots, grass grows and flowers bloom from the ground where you step, remaining for a few seconds before disappearing. You can move through plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard. In addition, you have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed when climbing trees and other plants, as small branches and leaves quickly grow to support you, then wither.

entries.name: Induce Growth
entries: While wearing these boots and standing on the ground, you can expend 1 or more charges to stomp one of your feet and produce one of the following effects:

items: You cast the {@spell plant growth} spell, centred on yourself and as an action only (3 charges).
You cast the {@spell spike growth} spell, centred on yourself (save {@dc 16}, 3 charges).
As a bonus action, you cause a large vine to grow beneath your feet, lifting you up to 30 feet straight up. This vine remains for 1 minute, after which it withers and disappears (1 charge).

As a reaction when you're subjected to an effect that would move you against your will, you cause small vines to wrap around your feet, preventing you from being moved until the start of your next turn (2 charges).

item.name: Huntsman's Goggles (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thermal Vision
entries: As a bonus action while wearing these goggles, you can expend 1 charge to activate them. For 1 minute, you can see creatures and objects within 60 feet of you coloured in a spectrum from red for warm creatures to blue for cold ones. This grants you {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight made to detect creatures that emit heat, and you can always see those creatures regardless of light conditions, obscurement, or invisibility, but not through total cover. Some constructs, elementals, and most undead exist at ambient temperature and can't be seen with this property (GM's discretion).

entries.name: Target Acquired
entries: Alternatively, while wearing these goggles, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell hunter's mark} spell. When you cast the spell in this way, the extra damage dealt by it is cold damage, and you can see the target with the goggles' Thermal Vision property even if you normally couldn't.

item.name: Huntsman's Goggles (Rare)
entries: This item has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thermal Vision
entries: As a bonus action while wearing these goggles, you can expend 1 charge to activate them. For 1 minute, you can see creatures and objects within 60 feet of you coloured in a spectrum from red for warm creatures to blue for cold ones. This grants you {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight made to detect creatures that emit heat, and you can always see those creatures regardless of light conditions, obscurement, or invisibility, but not through total cover. Some constructs, elementals, and most undead exist at ambient temperature and can't be seen with this property (GM's discretion).

When you cast the spell in this way, the extra damage dealt by it is cold damage, and you can see the target with the goggles' Thermal Vision property even if you normally couldn't.

item.name: Huntsman's Goggles (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thermal Vision
entries: As a bonus action while wearing these goggles, you can expend 1 charge to activate them. For 1 minute, you can see creatures and objects within 60 feet of you coloured in a spectrum from red for warm creatures to blue for cold ones. This grants you {@b advantage} on Perception checks that rely on sight made to detect creatures that emit heat, and you can always see those creatures regardless of light conditions, obscurement, or invisibility, but not through total cover. Some constructs, elementals, and most undead exist at ambient temperature and can't be seen with this property (GM's discretion).

entries.name: Target Acquired
entries: Alternatively, while wearing these goggles, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell hunter's mark} spell. When you cast the spell in this way, the extra damage dealt by it is cold damage, and you can see the target with the goggles' Thermal Vision property even if you normally couldn't.

entries.name: Drain Heat
entries: When a creature under the effects of the {@spell hunter's mark} spell cast with the goggles is reduced to 0 hit points, you can choose to end the spell and drain some of its body heat to revitalise yourself, regaining {@dice 2d4} hit points.

item.name: Living Flute (Uncommon)
entries: This item has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Song of Spring
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to play a beautiful tune on the flute, which causes its flowers to bloom and emit a sweet, soothing scent. Each other creature within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by every creature it can see for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature or any of its companions are harmed or targeted by a spell or ability by one of the creatures it's {@condition charmed} by, it's no longer {@condition charmed} by that creature. Once this property of the flute has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Petal Storm
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to play a frantic tune on the flute, which causes its flowers to shed their petals. These petals fly in a flurry towards a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, forcing it to make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw (the target can choose to fail this save). On a failed save, the creature is lifted up by the petals and carried 30 feet in any direction of your choice.

item.name: Living Flute (Rare)
entries: This item has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Song of Spring
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to play a beautiful tune on the flute, which causes its flowers to bloom and emit a sweet, soothing scent. Each other creature within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by every creature it can see for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature or any of its companions are harmed or targeted by a spell or ability by one of the creatures it's {@condition charmed} by, it's no longer {@condition charmed} by that creature. Once this property of the flute has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Petal Storm
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to play a frantic tune on the flute, which causes its flowers to shed their petals. These petals fly in a flurry towards a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, forcing it to make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw (the target can choose to fail this save). On a failed save, the creature is lifted up by the petals and carried 30 feet in any direction of your choice.

item.name: Living Flute (Very Rare)
entries: This item has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Song of Spring
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to play a beautiful tune on the flute, which causes its flowers to bloom and emit a sweet, soothing scent. Each other creature within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by every creature it can see for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature or any of its companions are harmed or targeted by a spell or ability by one of the creatures it's {@condition charmed} by, it's no longer {@condition charmed} by that creature. Once this property of the flute has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Petal Storm
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to play a frantic tune on the flute, which causes its flowers to shed their petals. These petals fly in a flurry towards a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, forcing it to make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw (the target can choose to fail this save). On a failed save, the creature is lifted up by the petals and carried 30 feet in any direction of your choice.

item.name: Quick-Grow Acorns (Uncommon)
entries: These acorns are usually found in bundles of {@dice 1d4 + 1}.

entries.name: Timber!
entries: As an action, you can plant one of these acorns in an unoccupied space on the ground within your reach, causing it to rapidly grow into an {@creature awakened tree} bearing gorgeous autumn foliage. The tree is an ally to you and your companions, and it shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). At the end of its turn, it rapidly decays and falls in a 15-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line in a direction of your choice. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Quick-Grow Acorns (Very Rare)
entries: These acorns are usually found in bundles of {@dice 1d4 + 1}.

entries.name: Timber!
entries: As an action, you can plant one of these acorns in an unoccupied space on the ground within your reach, causing it to rapidly grow into a {@creature treant} without the Animate Trees action, bearing gorgeous autumn foliage. The tree is an ally to you and your companions, and it shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). At the end of its turn, it rapidly decays and falls in a 15-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line in a direction of your choice. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d6} bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Scarf of Winter
entries: You can socket this scarf onto the chestpiece of a suit of armour. While wearing that armour, you ignore the effects of extreme cold.

entries.name: Frost Overload
entries: When you take cold damage, you can use your reaction to reduce it by {@damage 2d6}, as the small ice crystals serving as the scarf's tassels absorb some of the energy. The crystals are then overloaded and shatter, and this property of the scarf can't be used again.

item.name: Scorch Helm (Uncommon)
entries: This helmet has 2 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Warm Summer Knight
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to spread the flames on the helmet over your entire body, coating you in golden fire for 1 minute. During this time, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage.
When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, it takes {@damage 1d6} fire damage.
When you successfully grapple a creature, it takes {@damage 1d6} fire damage. A creature grappled by you or grappling you takes {@damage 1d6} fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

item.name: Scorch Helm (Rare)
entries: This helmet has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Warm Summer Knight
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to spread the flames on the helmet over your entire body, coating you in golden fire for 1 minute. During this time, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage.
When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, it takes {@damage 1d8} fire damage.
When you successfully grapple a creature, it takes {@damage 1d8} fire damage. A creature grappled by you or grappling you takes {@damage 1d8} fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

item.name: Scorch Helm (Very Rare)
entries: This helmet has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Warm Summer Knight
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to spread the flames on the helmet over your entire body, coating you in golden fire for 1 minute. During this time, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage.
When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, it takes {@damage 1d8} fire damage.
When you successfully grapple a creature, it takes {@damage 1d8} fire damage. A creature grappled by you or grappling you takes {@damage 1d8} fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

entries.name: Swallow's Flight
entries: This weapon has the Thrown (range 20/60) property. When thrown, it functions similarly to a boomerang, spinning as it flies through the air and returning to your hand after the attack hits or misses.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Flock On property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Flock On
entries: As an action while holding the sword, you can speak its command word, causing the flocks of metallic swallows to fly towards a creature of your choice within 30 feet of you, circling and harassing it for 1 minute, or until the creature is reduced to 0 hit points, you are {@condition unconscious}, or you speak the command word again (no action required). At the start of each of its turns, the creature must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 2d6} slashing damage and has {@b disadvantage} on ability checks and attack rolls until the start of its next turn. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Springwing (Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Swallow's Flight
entries: This weapon has the Thrown (range 20/60) property. When thrown, it functions similarly to a boomerang, spinning as it flies through the air and returning to your hand after the attack hits or misses.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Flock On property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Flock On
entries: As an action while holding the sword, you can speak its command word, causing the flocks of metallic swallows to fly towards a creature of your choice within 30 feet of you, circling and harassing it for 1 minute, or until the creature is reduced to 0 hit points, you are {@condition unconscious}, or you speak the command word again (no action required). At the start of each of its turns, the creature must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d6} slashing damage and has {@b disadvantage} on ability checks and attack rolls until the start of its next turn. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Springwing (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Swallow's Flight
entries: This weapon has the Thrown (range 20/60) property. When thrown, it functions similarly to a boomerang, spinning as it flies through the air and returning to your hand after the attack hits or misses.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Flock On property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Flock On
entries: As an action while holding the sword, you can speak its command word, causing the flocks of metallic swallows to fly towards a creature of your choice within 30 feet of you, circling and harassing it for 1 minute, or until the creature is reduced to 0 hit points, you are {@condition unconscious}, or you speak the command word again (no action required). At the start of each of its turns, the creature must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d6} slashing damage and has {@b disadvantage} on ability checks and attack rolls until the start of its next turn. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Staff of Decay (Rare)
entries: This staff has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the staff withers away and is destroyed.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While holding this staff, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it (save {@dc 15}): {@spell aura of impurity|LALoTT} (4 charges), {@spell inflict wounds} (1 charge), or {@spell ray of enfeeblement} (2 charges).

entries.name: Corpse Expert
entries: While holding this staff, you can use an action to touch a corpse and immediately know how long ago it died, in hours if it's been dead for less than two days, and in days otherwise.

item.name: Staff of Decay (Very Rare)
entries: This staff has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the staff withers away and is destroyed. You have resistance to necrotic damage while holding this staff.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While holding this staff, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it (save {@dc 16}): {@spell aura of impurity|LALoTT} (4 charges), {@spell blight} (4 charges), {@spell inflict wounds} (1 charge), or {@spell ray of enfeeblement} (2 charges).

entries.name: Corpse Expert
entries: While holding this staff, you can use an action to touch a corpse and immediately know how long ago it died, in hours if it's been dead for less than two days, and in days otherwise.

item.name: Sundial Ring (Rare)
entries: This ring functions as a sundial even when not exposed to the sun, allowing you to tell the time in any location, regardless of light conditions, as long as you can see the ring.

entries.name: Circadian Clock
entries: While wearing this ring, you gain different benefits depending on the time of day, as described below. This property only works on planes of existence that have a day-night cycle.

items.name: Dawn
entries: As the day begins, you are inspired to make the most of it. You gain inspiration and {@dice 2d10} temporary hit points, both of which last until daytime.

items.name: Daytime
entries: As the sun shines brightly in the sky, you are at your strongest. You gain a +1 bonus to AC, and when you deal damage with a weapon attack, you can reroll the weapon's damage dice, taking the second result.

items.name: Dusk
entries: As the daylight dwindles, you become more alert and wary of your surroundings. You have {@b advantage} on initiative rolls and Perception checks.

items.name: Night
entries: As darkness covers the land, you cling to any vestiges of light and warmth that you can find. You gain darkvision out to 30 feet. If you already have darkvision from another source, its range is increased by 30 feet.

item.name: Sundial Ring (Very Rare)
entries: This ring functions as a sundial even when not exposed to the sun, allowing you to tell the time in any location, regardless of light conditions, as long as you can see the ring.

entries.name: Circadian Clock
entries: While wearing this ring, you gain different benefits depending on the time of day, as described below. This property only works on planes of existence that have a day-night cycle.

items.name: Dawn
entries: As the day begins, you are inspired to make the most of it. You gain inspiration and {@dice 2d10} temporary hit points, both of which last until daytime.

items.name: Daytime
entries: As the sun shines brightly in the sky, you are at your strongest. You gain a +1 bonus to AC, and when you deal damage with a weapon attack, you can reroll the weapon's damage dice, taking the second result.

items.name: Dusk
entries: As the daylight dwindles, you become more alert and wary of your surroundings. You have {@b advantage} on initiative rolls and Perception checks.

items.name: Night
entries: As darkness covers the land, you cling to any vestiges of light and warmth that you can find. You gain darkvision out to 30 feet. If you already have darkvision from another source, its range is increased by 30 feet.

entries.name: Daylight Savings
entries: The ring absorbs sunlight to later release. While wearing the ring, you can cast {@spell daylight}. When you cast the spell in this way, its light is sunlight. While you are within the area of the spell's bright light, it counts as daytime for the purposes of the ring's Circadian Clock property. Once this property of the ring has been used, it can't be used again until the ring has spent 8 hours in sunlight.

item.name: Sunsplitter (Uncommon)
entries: This spear has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Luminous Refraction
entries: As an action while outside during the day, you can lift this spear towards the sky and expend 1 charge, causing a beam of pure radiance to descend from the sun into the spear's crystal. The crystal splits the beam into your choice of 2 to 6 smaller beams, each of which targets a different creature you can see within 30 feet of you. Each creature must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking an amount of radiant damage dependent on how many beams you produce on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success. If the larger beam is split into two, each smaller beam deals {@damage 6d4} radiant damage. For each additional beam produced beyond the second, roll one fewer d4 for each beam's damage.

item.name: Sunsplitter (Rare)
entries: This spear has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Luminous Refraction
entries: As an action while outside during the day, you can lift this spear towards the sky and expend 1 charge, causing a beam of pure radiance to descend from the sun into the spear's crystal. The crystal splits the beam into your choice of 2 to 6 smaller beams, each of which targets a different creature you can see within 30 feet of you. Each creature must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking an amount of radiant damage dependent on how many beams you produce on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success. If the larger beam is split in two, each smaller beam deals {@damage 6d6} radiant damage. For each additional beam produced beyond the second, roll one fewer d6 for each beam's damage.

item.name: Sunsplitter (Very Rare)
entries: This spear has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Luminous Refraction
entries: As an action while outside during the day, you can lift this spear towards the sky and expend 1 charge, causing a beam of pure radiance to descend from the sun into the spear's crystal. The crystal splits the beam into your choice of 2 to 6 smaller beams, each of which targets a different creature you can see within 60 feet of you. Each creature must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking an amount of radiant damage dependent on how many beams you produce on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success. If the larger beam is split in two, each smaller beam deals {@damage 6d8} radiant damage. For each additional beam produced beyond the second, roll one fewer d8 for each beam's damage.

entries.name: Aura of Decay
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this hat, you can cause it to emit an aura of decay, or deactivate the aura. While the aura is active, whenever a creature within 10 feet of you takes damage, it takes an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage, and whenever a creature within 10 feet of you regains hit points, it regains {@dice 1d4} fewer hit points. In addition, you can use an action to cause any number of corpses within the aura to disintegrate, preventing them from being resurrected or raised as undead.

entries.name: Aura of Decay
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this hat, you can cause it to emit an aura of decay, or deactivate the aura. While the aura is active, whenever a creature within 10 feet of you takes damage, it takes an extra {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage, and whenever a creature within 10 feet of you regains hit points, it regains {@dice 1d6} fewer hit points. In addition, you can use an action to cause any number of corpses within the aura to disintegrate, preventing them from being resurrected or raised as undead.

entries.name: Necrotic Burst
entries: When you take damage that reduces you to 0 hit points while wearing this hat, it lets out a wave of retaliatory necrotic energy. Each hostile creature within 10 feet of you must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking necrotic damage equal to the damage that reduced you to 0 hit points (maximum of 15 damage) on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property of the hat has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Aura of Decay
entries: As a bonus action while wearing this hat, you can cause it to emit an aura of decay, or deactivate the aura. While the aura is active, whenever a creature within 20 feet of you takes damage, it takes an extra {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage, and whenever a creature within 20 feet of you regains hit points, it regains {@dice 1d8} fewer hit points. In addition, you can use an action to cause any number of corpses within the aura to disintegrate, preventing them from being resurrected or raised as undead.

entries.name: Necrotic Burst
entries: When you take damage that reduces you to 0 hit points while wearing this hat, it lets out a wave of retaliatory necrotic energy. Each hostile creature within 10 feet of you must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking necrotic damage equal to the damage that reduced you to 0 hit points (maximum of 30 damage) on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property of the hat has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Brawler's Wraps
entries: While you wear these wraps, whenever you deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage with an unarmed strike, improvised weapon, or a technique that uses Brawler dice, that damage is magical.

item.name: Arm of the Grey Knight (uncommon)
entries: {@i Component: undead arm of the grey knight}

A shining beacon of tenacity and unwavering resolve, this amputated arm fights on with the strength of many men no matter how many times it's cut down or crushed into a pulp. Stubborn to a fault, it refuses to listen to anyone but the creature attuned to it, and occasionally makes rude gestures at passersby behind its owner's back, attempting to goad them into a fight.

entries.name: Helping Hand
entries: The arm is an object with AC 14 and 20 hit points. If it's ever more than 30 feet away from you, it moves 20 feet towards you at the start of each of your turns by crawling along the ground. As a bonus action, you can command it to move up to 20 feet and perform one of the following tasks:

items: The arm manipulates an object in the same way as the {@spell mage hand} cantrip.
The arm punches a creature within 5 feet of it, making an attack roll against it ({@hit +5} to hit) and dealing {@damage 1d6 + 3} bludgeoning damage on a hit.

The arm attempts to grab a creature within 5 feet of it, which must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the arm latches on to it, reducing its speed by 10 feet. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 13} Strength check, pulling the arm off of it or a creature within its reach on a success. The arm can't perform other tasks while holding onto a creature.

entries.name: Just a Flesh Wound
entries: If the arm is reduced to 0 hit points, it can't move or be commanded, but its remarkable resilience and regenerative abilities ensure that this doesn't last for long. The arm recovers all its hit points after {@dice 1d4} hours. A spellcaster can also use an action to touch the arm and spend a spell slot of any level to cause the arm to recover all of its hit points.

item.name: Arm of the Grey Knight (rare)
entries: {@i Component: undead arm of the grey knight}

A shining beacon of tenacity and unwavering resolve, this amputated arm fights on with the strength of many men no matter how many times it's cut down or crushed into a pulp. Stubborn to a fault, it refuses to listen to anyone but the creature attuned to it, and occasionally makes rude gestures at passersby behind its owner's back, attempting to goad them into a fight.

entries.name: Helping Hand
entries: The arm is an object with AC 14 and 20 hit points. If it's ever more than 30 feet away from you, it moves 20 feet towards you at the start of each of your turns by crawling along the ground, walls, and ceilings. As a bonus action, you can command it to move up to 20 feet and perform one of the following tasks:

items: The arm manipulates an object in the same way as the {@spell mage hand} cantrip.

The arm punches a creature within 5 feet of it, making an attack roll against it ({@hit +7} to hit) and dealing {@damage 1d6 + 5} (or {@damage 1d8 + 5} if it's wielding a longsword) bludgeoning damage on a hit.

The arm attempts to grab a creature within 5 feet of it, which must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the arm latches on to it, reducing its speed by 20 feet. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check, pulling the arm off of it or a creature within its reach on a success. The arm can't perform other tasks while holding onto a creature.

entries.name: Just a Flesh Wound
entries: If the arm is reduced to 0 hit points, it can't move or be commanded, but its remarkable resilience and regenerative abilities ensure that this doesn't last for long. The arm recovers all its hit points after {@dice 1d4} hours. A spellcaster can also use an action to touch the arm and spend a spell slot of any level to cause the arm to recover all of its hit points.

item.name: Coconut of Alacrity
entries: {@i Component: beast (horse) bone}

The original concept for this bizarre item is said to come from a faraway land where horses are so rare that buying one is more expensive than producing low-end magical paraphernalia. When both halves of this coconut are clapped together, they produce the sound of a trotting mount and increase one's velocity to match that of an unladen swallow.

entries.name: Mighty Gallop
entries: This item can be socketed on mounts. When hung from a Small or larger creature that is serving as a mount, that creature can take the Dash action as a bonus action on its turn. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Swallow's Velocity
entries: Using two hands, you can hold each coconut half and clap them together repeatedly to increase your travel pace over long distances. For as long as you continue to do this, you and up to 6 friendly creatures of your choice that can hear the clapping can travel 3 miles per hour at a slow pace (27 miles per day), 4 miles per hour at a normal pace (32 miles per day), or 6 miles per hour at a fast pace (45 miles per day). The sound of the clip-clopping coconuts can be heard up to 100 feet away; other creatures within this area that can hear are alerted to the sound and can't be surprised by creatures benefitting from this magic item while it is in use.

item.name: Osseous Spike (rare)
entries: {@i Component: astral bone (unique - bonemonger)}

The essence of the malicious bonemonger always hungers for more victims, and this craving persists even after death. Those who hold this jagged lance can feel its insatiable desire to be bathed in blood, and see shadowy claws crawl across its surface when it has gone too long without...
This lance has {@b 3 charges} and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Syphoning Splinters
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this lance, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause the tip of the lance to explode into sharp splinters; the creature must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d4} piercing damage and some of the splinters are embedded inside of it for {@b 1 minute}. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects. A creature with splinters inside of it takes {@damage 2d4} necrotic damage at the end of each of its turns, and you regain hit points equal to half the necrotic damage dealt this way as the splinters syphon the victim's vitality into you. A creature with splinters inside it or another creature within 5 feet of it can use an action to make a {@dc 15} Wisdom (Medicine) check, removing the splinters on a success.

item.name: Osseous Spike (very rare)
entries: {@i Component: astral bone (unique - bonemonger)}

The essence of the malicious bonemonger always hungers for more victims, and this craving persists even after death. Those who hold this jagged lance can feel its insatiable desire to be bathed in blood, and see shadowy claws crawl across its surface when it has gone too long without...
This lance has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Syphoning Splinters
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this lance, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause the tip of the lance to explode into sharp splinters; the creature must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d4} piercing damage and some of the splinters are embedded inside of it for {@b 1 minute}. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects. A creature with splinters inside of it takes {@damage 2d4} necrotic damage at the end of each of its turns, and you regain hit points equal to half the necrotic damage dealt this way as the splinters syphon the victim's vitality into you. A creature with splinters inside it or another creature within 5 feet of it can use an action to make a {@dc 16} Wisdom (Medicine) check, removing the splinters on a success.

entries.name: Crippling Impalement
entries: As an action while holding this lance, you can cause its shadowy hands to extend and try to grab a creature that you can see within {@b 20 feet} of you, forcing it to make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is seized by the hands, pulled to within 5 feet of you, and has an arm or leg (your choice) skewered by the lance, taking {@damage 6d4} piercing damage. The shadowy hands then release the creature. Until the end of its next turn, the creature has disadvantage on attack rolls made with the use of its arms if you skewered its arm, or has its speed reduced to 0 feet if you skewered its leg. Once this property of the lance has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Robes of the Enchanter (uncommon)
entries: {@i Component: astral heart (unique - bonemonger)}

Many scholars believe the name of this extravagant garment to be a grave misnomer, as it has no apparent relation whatsoever to enchantment magic. In truth, the robes are named after the man who devised and created the first set, a legendary sorcerer of tremendous power and curious explosive proclivities... as well as astonishing ignorance regarding the schools of magic.

entries.name: Spontaneous Detonation
entries: As an action while wearing these robes, you can wave your arms dramatically to create a small explosion at a point you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. Each creature within 5 feet of the point must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d8} fire damage, get pushed back 10 feet, and be knocked {@condition prone}.

item.name: Robes of the Enchanter (rare)
entries: {@i Component: astral heart (unique - bonemonger)}

Many scholars believe the name of this extravagant garment to be a grave misnomer, as it has no apparent relation whatsoever to enchantment magic. In truth, the robes are named after the man who devised and created the first set, a legendary sorcerer of tremendous power and curious explosive proclivities... as well as astonishing ignorance regarding the schools of magic.

entries.name: Spontaneous Detonation
entries: As an action while wearing these robes, you can wave your arms dramatically to create a small explosion at a point you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. Each creature within 5 feet of the point must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d8} fire damage, get pushed back 10 feet, and be knocked {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Explosive Step
entries: While wearing these robes, you can cast the {@spell misty step} spell. When you do, you can create an explosion as per the Spontaneous Detonation property, either in the space you leave or the space you arrive at. You automatically succeed on the saving throw against it. Once this property of the robes has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Somewhat Holy Hand Grenade
entries: {@i Component: lucky astral paw (unique - bonemonger)}

The Book of Armaments, an ancient holy text, speaks of a blessed weapon capable of wreaking destruction upon the evils of the world, an object so powerful that all enemies of the heavens will be blown to tiny bits, in the gods' mercy. This attempt at recreating it has been corrupted by the malicious energy of the bonemonger... but perhaps that isn't a bad thing.

entries.name: Duality of Man
entries: As an action, you can take out the holy pin, count to three, no more, no less, and lob the grenade at a point you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. When you do, choose one of the two options below:

items.name: Radiant Rebuke
entries: The grenade explodes in a burst of searing light and each creature within {@b 20 feet} of it must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 6d8} radiant damage. If it has an evil alignment, it takes {@damage 8d8} radiant damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn instead. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition blinded}.

items.name: Shadowy Embrace
entries: The grenade explodes in a mess of shadowy claws and each creature within {@b 20 feet} of it must make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 6d8} necrotic damage. If it has a non-evil alignment, it takes {@damage 8d8} necrotic damage and is {@condition restrained} by the claws until the end of its next turn instead. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition restrained}.
The grenade is then destroyed.

entries.name: False Glamour
entries: When you first don this dress after attuning to it, and each day at dawn as long as you're wearing it, it casts the {@spell sanctuary} spell (save {@dc 13}) on you, with the following changes:

items: It lasts for 8 hours instead of 1 minute.
You're endowed with an aura of magnetic charisma while the spell lasts, granting you {@b advantage} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks.
It doesn't end early if you make an attack or cast a spell that affects an enemy creature, but it ends if a creature hits you with an attack.

When the spell ends, your face is briefly replaced with a horrifying ophidian visage. Each creature of your choice within 15 feet of you that can see you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This dress is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you until you're targeted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you can't remove the dress, and if you look at your own reflection while not under the effect of the dress' {@spell sanctuary} spell, you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition restrained} as your body starts to turn to stone. You must repeat the saving throw at the end of your next turn, becoming {@condition petrified} on a failure or ending the effect on a success. The petrification lasts until the next dawn, when the dress casts {@spell sanctuary} on you again. Once you succeed on a saving throw against the curse, you can't be affected by it again until the next dawn.

entries.name: False Glamour
entries: When you first don this dress after attuning to it, and each day at dawn as long as you're wearing it, it casts the {@spell sanctuary} spell (save {@dc 15}) on you, with the following changes:

items: It lasts for 8 hours instead of 1 minute.
You're endowed with an aura of magnetic charisma while the spell lasts, granting you {@b advantage} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks.
It doesn't end early if you make an attack or cast a spell that affects an enemy creature, but it ends if a creature hits you with an attack.

When the spell ends, your face is briefly replaced with a horrifying ophidian visage. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you that can see you must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. A creature that fails its saving throw takes {@damage 4d6} psychic damage and is {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A {@condition frightened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This dress is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you until you're targeted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you can't remove the dress, and if you look at your own reflection while not under the effect of the dress' {@spell sanctuary} spell, you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition restrained} as your body starts to turn to stone. You must repeat the saving throw at the end of your next turn, becoming {@condition petrified} on a failure or ending the effect on a success. The petrification lasts until the next dawn, when the dress casts {@spell sanctuary} on you again. Once you succeed on a saving throw against the curse, you can't be affected by it again until the next dawn.

entries: You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing the dress and no armour.

entries.name: Inkstaintaneous Blast
entries: As an action, you can light the fuse on this bomb and toss it up to 30 feet away, causing it to detonate in a 15-foot-radius explosion. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or be covered in ink and {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. A creature covered in ink can't benefit from being {@condition invisible}. A creature {@condition blinded} by this effect can make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@condition blinded} condition on itself on a success.

entries.name: Delayed Inksplosion
entries: Alternatively, you can use an action to set this bomb down and specify a special trigger for its detonation. This can be a visual trigger that occurs within 15 feet of the bomb, such as a visible creature moving into that range, or a special command word spoken by a creature as a bonus action while within 30 feet of the bomb. When the conditions for the trigger are met, the bomb explodes as per the Inkstaintaneous Blast property.

entries.name: Inkstaintaneous Blast
entries: As an action, you can light the fuse on this bomb and toss it up to 30 feet away, causing it to detonate in a 20-foot-radius explosion. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or be covered in ink and {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. A creature covered in ink can't benefit from being {@condition invisible}. A creature {@condition blinded} by this effect takes {@damage 1d8} poison damage at the start of each of its turns, and can make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@condition blinded} condition on itself on a success.

item.name: Lernaean Pendant (Uncommon)
entries: This pendant has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Better Than One
entries: While you are wearing this pendant and make a saving throw against an effect that would cause you to become {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, or {@condition frightened}, you can expend 1 charge as a reaction to grow a second head. Each of your heads makes a separate saving throw against the condition, after which you choose one of them to shrivel and fall off, taking the result of the saving throw for the head that remains.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, when you take fire damage, you can't regain hit points or use the Better Than One property until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Lernaean Pendant (Rare)
entries: This pendant has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Better Than One
entries: While you are wearing this pendant and make a saving throw against an effect that would cause you to become {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, or {@condition frightened}, you can expend 1 charge as a reaction to grow a second head. Each of your heads makes a separate saving throw against the condition, after which you choose one of them to shrivel and fall off, taking the result of the saving throw for the head that remains. When you use this property, you also regain {@dice 1d6} hit points.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, when you take fire damage, you can't regain hit points or use the Better Than One property until the end of your next turn.

Each of your heads makes a separate saving throw against the condition, after which you choose one of them to shrivel and fall off, taking the result of the saving throw for the head that remains. When you use this property, you also regain {@dice 3d6} hit points.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, when you take fire damage, you can't regain hit points or use the Better Than One property until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Reactive Strikes
entries: While wielding this weapon, you gain a special reaction that you can take once on each round. This special reaction can only be used to make an opportunity attack with the flail, and you can't use it on the same turn that you take your normal reaction.

entries.name: Reactive Strikes
entries: While wielding this weapon, you gain a special reaction that you can take once on each round. This special reaction can only be used to make an opportunity attack with the flail, and you can't use it on the same turn that you take your normal reaction.

entries.name: Clamp Down
entries: When you hit a creature with an opportunity attack using this flail, you deal an extra {@damage 1d4} piercing damage to it and can choose to immediately grapple the target (escape {@dc 15}), as the hydra heads bite down on it. The grapple ends if you let go of the flail. While a creature is grappled in this manner, you can't use the flail to attack a different target.

item.name: Many-Headed Flail (Very Rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Reactive Strikes
entries: While wielding this weapon, you gain a special reaction that you can take once on each round. This special reaction can only be used to make an opportunity attack with the flail, and you can't use it on the same turn that you take your normal reaction.

entries.name: Clamp Down
entries: When you hit a creature with an opportunity attack using this flail, you deal an extra {@damage 1d10} piercing damage to it and can choose to immediately grapple the target (escape {@dc 16}), as the hydra heads bite down on it. The grapple ends if you let go of the flail. While a creature is grappled in this manner, you can't use the flail to attack a different target.

entries.name: Labyrinth Dweller
entries: While wearing this helm, you can perfectly recall any path you've travelled and can't get lost by nonmagical means. In addition, you automatically succeed on Intelligence checks made to escape the {@spell maze} spell, and have {@b advantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) checks made to spot and open secret doors and passages.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Maze Architect property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Maze Architect
entries: While wearing this helm, you can cast the {@spell passwall} spell from it. When the spell is cast in this way, you can end it early as a bonus action, and when a creature first enters the passage created by the spell, you can deal {@damage 1d6} psychic damage to it. Once this property of the helm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This helm is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, whenever you start your turn and can see a creature primarily coloured or dressed in red, you must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you must spend your turn moving towards that creature and, if possible, making melee attacks against it. Once you succeed on this saving throw, you can't be affected by the curse again for 24 hours.

entries: You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this helm.

entries.name: Offensive Defence
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} damage to creatures it hits that have a lower AC than yours.

entries.name: Triumphant Roar
entries: When you roll a 20 on an attack roll made with this weapon, the lion's visage lets out a ferocious roar. Each creature other than you that can hear it within 20 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.

entries: When you roll a 20 on an attack roll made with this weapon, the lion's visage lets out a ferocious roar. Each creature other than you that can hear it within 20 feet of you takes {@damage 1d6} thunder damage and must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.
This armour has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lurker in the Deep
entries: While wearing this armour, you have the following benefits:

items: You can't float in water and instead sink at a rate of {@b 60 feet} per round. Walking on the bed of a water body doesn't cost you extra movement.

entries.name: Sunken King's Favour
entries: As an action while wearing the armour, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell bigby's hand||arcane hand} spell ({@hit 7} to hit). When cast in this way, the spell is modified as follows:

items: You create two hands instead of one. They take the form of Medium spectral tentacles that sprout from the shoulders of the Breastplate. They occupy your space, move with you, and can't be moved away from you. You can only command one of the tentacles with your bonus action each turn.
The reach of 'Clenched Fist', 'Forceful Hand', and 'Grasping Hand' is increased to {@b 15 feet}.

entries: This weapon has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Blessing of the Deep
entries: As a bonus action, you can coat this blade with water from a water source within your reach and make a quick prayer to the Deep One, expending 1 charge and turning the coating to flawless ice for {@b 1 minute}. While coated in ice, the blade deals an extra {@damage 1d6} cold damage to any target it hits.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
