entries.name: Turtle's Steam
entries: As an action, you can slash at the air or water in front of you with the claw, creating a {@b 20-foot cone} of hot steam. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 3d10} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Being fully immersed in water doesn't grant resistance against this fire damage. This property of the club can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Sea Serpent's Might
entries: As an action, you can slam the tooth into the ground or thrust it into a body of water, creating a shockwave in a {@b 30-foot cone}. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 5d10} thunder damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't knocked prone. Once this property of the club has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Kraken's Storm
entries: As an action, you can swing the beak forwards, creating a crackling lightning storm in a {@b 40-foot cone}. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 6d10} lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property of the club has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

This magic weapon has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges after it spends 8 continuous hours in fiery embers. As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to ignite the blade for {@b 10 minutes}, or until you sheathe it. Flames erupt along the sword's length; it sheds bright light in a {@b 40-foot radius} and dim light for an additional 40 feet, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage on a hit.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: If you undergo a branding ritual to become bonded to the blade, it gains the Brand property. In addition, when the weapon regains charges, it regains all expended charges. Once you have undergone this branding ritual, you can't willingly end your attunement to the blade until another creature willingly undertakes the branding ritual, at which point it becomes attuned to the sword instead.

entries.name: EA: Brand
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell heat metal} spell (save {@dc 13}).

As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to ignite the blade for {@b 10 minutes}, or until you sheathe it. Flames erupt along the sword's length; it sheds bright light in a {@b 40-foot radius} and dim light for an additional 40 feet, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d10} fire damage on a hit.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: If you undergo a branding ritual to become bonded to the blade, it gains the Brand property. In addition, when the weapon regains charges, it regains all expended charges. Once you have undergone this branding ritual, you can't willingly end your attunement to the blade until another creature willingly undertakes the branding ritual, at which point it becomes attuned to the sword instead.

entries.name: EA: Brand
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell heat metal} spell (save {@dc 17}) at 5th level.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Hypothermia
entries: When you hit with an attack using this weapon, you can attempt to absorb the target's heat. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 1d8} cold damage, have its speed halved, and be unable to take reactions until the end of your next turn. After you use this property, roll a d6. On a 1-2, you can't use this property again until the next dawn. This property has no effect on creatures with immunity to cold damage.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: When you slay a giant while wielding this blade, you can choose to accept the Allfather's blessing, enhancing your attunement to it. If you accept the blessing, you gain access to the Fallen Brothers property. Once you accept the blessing, you can't willingly end your attunement to the blade until another creature kills a giant while wielding the weapon and chooses to accept the Allfather's blessing, at which point it becomes attuned to the sword instead.

entries.name: EA: Fallen Brothers
entries: As an action, you can kneel and utter a prayer. In response, the gates of Valhalla briefly open and a wave of spectral warriors attack all creatures in a {@b 30-foot cone} originating from you. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d8} cold damage and {@damage 2d8} radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until you spend 1 hour recounting the tales of past heroes, an act of prayer to the Allfather (which can be done as part of a short or long rest).
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: EA: Hero's Armour
entries: When an attack would reduce you to 0 hit points, fate and frostblessed intervene, covering you in a carapace of ice the moment before the attack hits. You take no damage from the attack and your AC increases by 10 until the end of your next turn. Once this property has triggered, it can't trigger again until the next dawn.

This axe has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges after it spends 8 continuous hours in snow, ice, or cold water. As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to cause cold mist to emanate from the axe for {@b 10 minutes}. It glows a soft blue, shedding bright light in a {@b 10-foot radius} and dim light for an additional 10 feet, and deals an extra {@damage 1d4} cold damage on a hit.

entries.name: Suneater
entries: As an action, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell darkness} spell. When you cast the spell in this way, you can choose to expend 1 additional charge. If you do, you can see through the magical darkness of the spell while holding the suneater axe.

entries.name: Gleipnir
entries: When you make an attack with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to launch the axe's blade from the wolf's mouth, attached to the weapon by a long thread. Instead of a melee weapon attack, make a ranged weapon attack against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you, using your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or become {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) as the thread wraps itself around it. A creature that fails this saving throw by 5 or more is {@condition restrained} while it is grappled in this way. Huge or larger creatures have disadvantage on the saving throw. The grappled creature or a creature within reach of it can attempt a {@dc 15} Strength check, ending the grapple on a success. The grapple also ends if you pull the axe's blade back towards the weapon (no action required) or if you let go of the axe.

While you have a creature grappled in this way, you can't attack with the suneater axe. As a bonus action, you can try to reel the creature in, making a Strength (Athletics) check, contested by one from the grappled creature. On a success, you pull the target up to 15 feet towards you.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The axe has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.
While holding this weapon, you have resistance to radiant damage.

While holding this bow, you can cast the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip. In addition, when an arrow is nocked on this bow, the arrow immediately lights up, emanating blue mist and shedding bright light in a {@b 15-foot radius} and dim light for an additional 15 feet. This light lasts for {@b 1 minute} after the arrow is shot from the bow.

entries.name: Winter Hunt
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} cold damage to each creature it hits that is in freezing temperatures or is standing on snow or ice.

entries.name: Ice Shower
entries: As an action, you can choose a point you can see within the normal range of this weapon and fire a volley of up to 6 arrows. These arrows levitate in the air when they enter a {@b 10-foot radius} centred on that point. For {@b 1 minute}, or until all the arrows are used, whenever a creature other than you enters the area for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, one arrow shoots at it. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d4} piercing damage and {@damage 1d4} cold damage. Once this property of the bow has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

When you hit a Small or larger creature with an attack using this weapon, it gains {@b 1 charge} as the pressure within builds, up to a maximum of {@b 3 charges}. If no charges are expended for {@b 1 minute}, the weapon loses all charges as it depressurises.

entries.name: B.O.N.K
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to activate its built in engine and boost the next attack you make with it before the start of your next turn, which deals an extra {@damage 1d8} bludgeoning damage on a hit.

entries.name: B.O.O.O.N.K
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can expend 3 charges to fire a bolt of lightning in a {@b 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line}. Each creature in the line must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d8} lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

When you hit a Small or larger creature with an attack using this weapon, it gains {@b 1 charge} as the pressure within builds, up to a maximum of {@b 4 charges}. If no charges are expended for {@b 1 minute}, the weapon loses all charges as it depressurises.

entries.name: Shadowglide
entries: Whenever you fall from a height of 10 feet or more while wearing this armour, you can use a reaction to glide. While gliding, you move 1 foot horizontally for each 1 foot you fall, take no falling damage when you land, and have {@b advantage} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks to move silently. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Take-off
entries: As a bonus action, you can activate your wings and gain a flying speed of {@b 30 feet} for {@b 10 minutes}. Once this property of the armour has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Death From Above
entries: While wearing this armour, you can use a bonus action while in the air to fold yourself into a diving position and move straight down up to {@b 30 feet}. If you make a melee weapon attack against a creature immediately after moving in this way and hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage for every 10 feet you moved, and you can immediately move another 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If the attack misses, you crash, taking {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet you moved and landing {@condition prone}.

entries: While holding this weapon, you can cause it to regain expended charges by swinging it vigorously for {@b 10 minutes} without interruption. At the end of this time, make a {@dc 10} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check. The item regains {@b 1d4} expended charges on a successful check, or half as many charges on a failed one. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries: You expend 1 charge. For the duration, each time you hit with an attack using this weapon, you deal an extra {@damage 1d4} lightning damage.

items.name: 2 Clicks

entries.name: Glimmerbolt
entries: When you take the Attack action on your turn while holding this crossbow, you can speak its first command word, expending 1 charge and causing a piece of radiant ammunition to load into the crossbow. The next attack you make with the crossbow before the end of your turn deals radiant damage instead of piercing damage, and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} radiant damage on a hit.

entries.name: Lumoflare
entries: As an action while holding this crossbow, you can speak its second command word and expend 3 charges to fire an especially bright bolt at a point within {@b 60 feet} of you, where it detonates. Each creature in a {@b 20-foot radius} must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn. After this flash, the light, now dimmer, hangs in the air for {@b 1 minute}, shedding bright light for 20 feet, and dim light for an additional 20 feet.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Fencing
entries: As an action, you can stab palisade into the ground, causing a 1-inch-thick wrought iron fence to burst from the earth at a point you can see within {@b 60 feet} of you, twisting into a latticework of thorny vines. It can be either 30 feet long and 10 feet high or form a ringed wall up to 20 feet in diameter and 20 feet high. Each 5-foot-square section has an AC of 10, 15 hit points, and immunity to psychic and poison damage. The wall doesn't block line of sight, but Small or larger creatures can't move through it.

When the fence appears, each creature within its area is pushed to one side of the fence (your choice) and must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} piercing damage. A creature attempting to climb the fence must make a {@dc 13} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, with {@b advantage} if it has a climbing speed. On a failure, the creature slips, taking {@damage 2d8} piercing damage and falling {@condition prone} at the base of the fence.

Ranged attack rolls that pass through the fence are made with {@b disadvantage}. The fence persists for {@b 10 minutes}, until you lose concentration (as if concentrating on a spell), or until it is destroyed, whereupon it crumbles into dust. Once you have used this property, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical weapon.

Once you have used this property, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Freeflow
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to cause its form to shift to that of any melee weapon with which you are proficient that lacks the {@itemProperty 2H|Two-handed} and {@itemProperty H|Heavy} properties.

entries.name: Throw
entries: When you make an attack with this weapon, you can instead flick a shard of the quicksilver blade towards a target, making a ranged weapon attack (range 30/90) that uses your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. On a hit, it deals the weapon's normal damage plus an extra {@damage 2d6} poison damage, or {@damage 4d6} if the target is a shapechanger. Once this property is used once, the quicksilver blade becomes a +1 dagger with no other properties until the next dawn, when it regrows to its original form and regains all expended uses of this property.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Freeflow
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to cause its form to shift to that of any melee weapon with which you are proficient that lacks the {@itemProperty 2H|Two-handed} and {@itemProperty H|Heavy} properties.

entries.name: Throw
entries: When you make an attack with this weapon, you can instead flick a shard of the quicksilver blade towards a target, making a ranged weapon attack (range 30/90) that uses your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. On a hit, it deals the weapon's normal damage plus an extra {@damage 2d6} poison damage, or {@damage 4d6} if the target is a shapechanger. Once this property is used three times, the quicksilver blade becomes a +2 dagger with no other properties until the next dawn, when it regrows to its original form and regains all expended uses of this property.
You have {@b advantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) checks made to perform surgical procedures using this sword.

entries.name: Amputation
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can speak its command word, causing it to momentarily become incredibly sharp and able to cut through limbs with even a glancing blow. The target must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} slashing damage and loses a randomly determined limb (use the table below). On a success, it takes half as much damage and doesn't lose a limb. Creatures with legendary resistances automatically succeed on this saving throw. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

How the creature is affected depends on the creatures' anatomy and is up to the GM's discretion. If the GM decides the creature can't lose a limb, this property deals an extra {@damage 6d6} slashing damage instead of {@damage 2d6}. Below are examples for a humanoid.

rows: The target's walking speed is halved and it has {@b disadvantage} on Dexterity saving throws.

The target drops whatever it is holding in this hand (if any) and has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls using its arms. If the target has a feature or trait that allows it to make multiple attacks using its arms, it makes one fewer.
You cut off one of the creature's heads. The creature dies if it can't survive without the lost head.

entries.name: Avoiding Amputation
entries: If you don't want to include amputation in your game, replace the slashing damage with psychic damage and have the Amputation property instead temporarily disable the target's limb until it finishes a short or long rest.
You have {@b advantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) checks made to perform surgical procedures using this sword.

entries.name: Amputation
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can speak its command word, causing it to momentarily become incredibly sharp and able to cut through limbs with even a glancing blow. The target must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d12} slashing damage and loses a randomly determined limb (use the table below). On a success, it takes half as much damage and doesn't lose a limb. Creatures with legendary resistances automatically succeed on this saving throw. This property can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

How the creature is affected depends on the creatures' anatomy and is up to the GM's discretion. If the GM decides the creature can't lose a limb, this property deals an extra {@damage 6d12} slashing damage instead of {@damage 2d12}. Below are examples for a humanoid.

rows: The target's walking speed is halved and it has {@b disadvantage} on Dexterity saving throws.

entries.name: Avoiding Amputation

entries.name: Soultrap
entries: This weapon can store a maximum of {@b 10 charges}. When you use this weapon to kill a creature capable of casting spells, the creature's soul is trapped in the weapon, which gains a number of charges equal to half the creature's total number of Hit Dice. As these charges are expended, the creature's soul is shredded. A creature whose soul is trapped or shredded can't come back to life by any means short of a {@spell true resurrection} spell or similar magic.

entries.name: Empower
entries: When you hit a target with an attack using this weapon, you can expend up to 3 charges, dealing an extra {@damage 1d6} force damage for each charge expended.
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Soultrap
entries: This weapon can store a maximum of {@b 20 charges}. When you use this weapon to kill a creature capable of casting spells, the creature's soul is trapped in the weapon, which gains a number of charges equal to half the creature's total number of Hit Dice. As these charges are expended, the creature's soul is shredded. A creature whose soul is trapped or shredded can't come back to life by any means short of a {@spell true resurrection} spell or similar magic.

entries.name: Empower
entries: When you hit a target with an attack using this weapon, you can expend up to 5 charges, dealing an extra {@damage 1d8} force damage for each charge expended.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Dispel Magic
entries: When you hit a target that is under the effect of one or more spells with an attack using this weapon, you can choose to dispel one of the spells affecting it if the spell is of 3rd level or lower. If you do, the target takes {@damage 1d6} force damage for each level of the spell (dispelled cantrips deal no extra damage). Once a spell is dispelled, roll a {@dice d4}. If the number is equal to or lower than the level of the dispelled spell, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Dispel Magic
entries: When you hit a target that is under the effect of one or more spells with an attack using this weapon, you can choose to dispel one of the spells affecting it if the spell is of 5th level or lower. If you do, the target takes {@damage 1d6} force damage for each level of the spell (dispelled cantrips deal no extra damage). Once a spell is dispelled, roll a {@dice d6}. If the number is equal to or lower than the level of the dispelled spell, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Dispel Magic
entries: When you hit a target that is under the effect of one or more spells with an attack using this weapon, you can choose to dispel one of the spells affecting it if the spell is of 7th level or lower. If you do, the target takes {@damage 1d6} force damage for each level of the spell (dispelled cantrips deal no extra damage). Once a spell is dispelled, roll a {@dice d8}. If the number is equal to or lower than the level of the dispelled spell, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Absorption
entries: While wearing this armour, when you are subjected to a magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success and only half as much damage on a failure. Once this property has triggered, it can't trigger again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Spellbreak
entries: While wearing this armour, acid, cold, fire, lightning, and thunder damage you take from magical sources, as well as magical healing you receive, is reduced by 2.

entries.name: Absorption
entries: While wearing this armour, when you are subjected to a magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success and only half as much damage on a failure. Once this property has triggered three times, it can't trigger again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Spellbreak
entries: While wearing this armour, acid, cold, fire, lightning, and thunder damage you take from magical sources, as well as magical healing you receive, is reduced by 3.

entries.name: Absorption
entries: While wearing this armour, when you are subjected to a magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success and only half as much damage on a failure. Once this property has triggered three times, it can't trigger again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Spellbreak
entries: While wearing this armour, you have resistance to force damage. Acid, cold, fire, lightning, and thunder damage you take from magical sources, as well as magical healing you receive, is reduced by 3. In addition, whenever you are hit by a magical melee weapon, the attacker must roll a {@dice d20}. On a 10 or lower, the weapon's magic is suppressed for {@b 1 hour}, causing it to temporarily lose its magical effects immediately after the attack.

entries.name: Spelldrain
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this ammunition, it projects a {@b 10-foot-radius sphere} of magic suppression, centred on the creature, for {@b 1 minute}. When a creature in the sphere tries to cast a spell, it must make a {@dc 15} ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell fails. On a success, it casts the spell as normal. After this property of the ammunition is used, the ammunition is destroyed.

entries.name: Spelldrain
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this ammunition, it projects a {@b 20-foot-radius sphere} of magic suppression, centred on the creature, for {@b 1 minute}. When a creature in the sphere tries to cast a spell, it must make a {@dc 16} ability check using its spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell fails, and the spell slot is absorbed by the black hole. On a success, it casts the spell as normal. After this property of the ammunition is used, the ammunition is destroyed.

entries.name: Fearflame
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the weapon's first command word, causing an eerie green flame to shimmer along the blade's length for {@b 1 minute}. For the duration, whenever you hit with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} psychic damage and must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of you until the start of your next turn. This property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Find Steed
entries: As an action, you can speak the scythe's second command word and cast the {@spell find steed} spell. When the spell is cast in this way, it always summons an amphibian seamare, which has the statistics of a {@creature giant sea horse|MM} with the following changes:

items: It has a walking and swimming speed of 60 feet.
It can breathe both air and water.

It can use an action to roar, forcing each creature of its choice within 60 feet of it that can hear it to succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of the seamare for {@b 1 minute}. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to this action for 24 hours.

entries: Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

entries.name: The Seize
entries: While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to enhance one piece of loaded ammunition with debilitating, arthritic energy that causes the target's joints to seize solid. If the next attack you make with the weapon hits, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition restrained} until the end of your next turn. If the target fails by 5 or more, it is {@condition paralyzed} instead of restrained. After this property has been used, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 3 or lower, the lobster's colour fades to grey, and this property can't be used again until the next dawn.
This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage to any target it hits.

entries.name: Maim
entries: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using this weapon, you can speak its command word, causing its teeth-like protrusions to rip through the target. Roll a {@dice d4} and consult the following table to determine the effect:

rows.name: Hamstring
entries: The creature's speed is reduced to 0 feet until the end of its next turn.

rows.name: Rend
entries: The creature has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

rows.name: Sever
entries: If the creature has blood, it begins bleeding for {@b 1 minute}. Each time it takes bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage while bleeding, it takes an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage. The target or a creature within reach can use an action to make a {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check, stopping the bleeding on a success. The Maim property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hydrodynamic
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon.

entries.name: Hammerhead Knight's Legacy
entries: While attuned to the {@item helmet of the hammerhead knight|LALoTT} and sharktooth, you gain {@b advantage} on Dexterity saving throws against effects you can see or smell, and you can't be surprised. In addition, you can use your action to grow gills and morph your legs into a tail, or to reverse this transformation. While morphed, you gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, your walking speed can't exceed 5 feet, and you can breathe both air and water.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Sykl Arrow
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge to cause a thorny arrow to grow from the bow, choosing whether it is a Bramble or Splinter arrow. The next attack you make with the bow has additional effects on a hit, as described below:

items.name: Bramble
entries: The arrow transforms into grasping vines. The target of the attack must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained} by the vines until the end of its next turn. A Large or larger creature has {@b advantage} on this saving throw.

items.name: Splinter
entries: The arrow erupts in a storm of splinters and thorns. The target of the attack and each creature within 5 feet of it must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 1d10} piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
This weapon has {@b 7 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Overgrown
entries: As an action, you can expend 3 charges to cause three thick-limbed, thorny arrows to grow from the bow and fire them at a point within {@b 120 feet} of you. Where the arrows land, a mass of syklthorn brambles grow, creating an effect identical to the {@spell wall of thorns} spell (save {@dc 15}), except it deals {@damage 4d8} damage instead of the spell's normal damage.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to xyxlbark armour, it nourishes you, causing you to gain {@b 3 temporary hit points} at the start of each of your turns while wearing it.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to xyxlbark armour, it nourishes you, causing you to gain {@b 4 temporary hit points} at the start of each of your turns while wearing it.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to xyxlbark armour, it nourishes you, causing you to gain {@b 5 temporary hit points} at the start of each of your turns while wearing it. In addition, you can speak and understand Sylvan and can cast the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip while attuned to the item and wearing it.
This weapon has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges after it spends 8 continuous hours buried in soil.

entries.name: Witherblight Spore
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to make a special attack with this weapon. On a hit, the target takes the attack's normal damage and is injected with a rapidly growing witherblight spore. As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can will the spore to grow, consuming the target from the inside out; the host takes {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage and deals only half damage with weapon attacks that use Strength until the end of its next turn.

A creature that hosts a spore can make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ridding itself of the spore on a success. Creatures that don't have flesh or that are immune to necrotic damage or disease are immune to this effect. If you inject a spore into a second creature, the first spore dies and the target is no longer affected.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges after it spends 8 continuous hours buried in soil.

The power of such a weapon is linked to the number of eyes used to craft it, with each open eye representing one unexpended charge. This weapon has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bonerip
entries: As a bonus action when you hit a target with this weapon, you can call the name of the demon to whom the eyes belonged and expend 1 charge. The weapon mobilises, boney teeth and necrotic energy ripping through the target's defences. The target gains a permanent and cumulative -1 penalty to its AC. Its AC can't be reduced to less than 10 + its Dexterity modifier. The target can repair its armour with enough rest (for natural armour) or at an appropriate armoursmith's (for worn armour) at the GM's discretion.

entries.name: Syphon
entries: If you hit a creature that has blood with an attack using this weapon on your turn and that attack leaves the creature below half of its maximum hit points, you can use a bonus action to cause the weapon to latch onto the creature and begin syphoning its blood. The creature is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). While grappled, the creature takes {@damage 3d6} necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns and you regain hit points equal to half the necrotic damage dealt. You can't use the blood drinker to attack another target, and you must hold on to the blood drinker with at least one hand to maintain the grapple; if you let go of the blood drinker with both hands, the grapple ends. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Syphon
entries: If you hit a creature that has blood with an attack using this weapon on your turn and that attack leaves the creature below half of its maximum hit points, you can use a bonus action to cause the weapon to latch onto the creature and begin syphoning its blood. The creature is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). While grappled, the creature takes {@damage 4d6} necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns and you regain hit points equal to half the necrotic damage dealt. You can't use the blood drinker to attack another target, and you must hold on to the blood drinker with at least one hand to maintain the grapple; if you let go of the blood drinker with both hands, the grapple ends. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: SoarBones
entries: You can use this weapon to cast the {@spell phantom steed} spell. The weapon grows skeletal wings and feet, turning into a vicious looking crow named SoarBones. In addition to the steed's normal statistics, it has a flying speed equal to its walking speed. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

While grappled, the creature takes {@damage 4d6} necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns and you regain an equal number of hit points. You can't use the blood drinker to attack another target, and you must hold on to the blood drinker with at least one hand to maintain the grapple; if you let go of the blood drinker with both hands, the grapple ends. entries.name: Feed the Beast
entries: This weapon has {@b 10 charges}. Over the course of a short or long rest, any creature can cause bloodshot to regain expended charges by dripping their own blood down its barrel and spending Hit Dice. Roll each Hit Die spent in this way, and the weapon regains a number of charges equal to the total. After absorbing 3 Hit Dice worth of blood, the weapon can't absorb more blood in this way until the next dawn.

Alternatively, over the course of a minute, you can extract blood from the corpse of a creature that has been dead for no longer than {@b 10 minutes}. When you do so, the weapon gains a number of charges equal to the roll of one of the dead creature's Hit Dice. After absorbing the blood from a corpse in this way, the weapon can't do so again until {@b 1 hour} has passed.

entries.name: Shot Selection
entries: This weapon doesn't have the Reload or Ammunition properties. Instead, in order to make a ranged weapon attack with it, you must expend charges in one of the following ways:

items.name: Bloodshard (1 charge)
entries: On a hit, the weapon deals its normal damage.

items.name: Septic Shock (3 charges)
entries: On a hit, the weapon deals its normal damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn as its blood becomes corrupted. A creature immune to disease automatically succeeds on this saving throw.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

This armour has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Consume Energy
entries: When you take cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage, you can use your reaction to speak the armour's first command word and expend 1 charge. The mouth opens, consuming some of the incoming energy. You gain resistance to the triggering damage and one of the armour's eyes lights up with the stored energy. The armour has one eye, and an eye can't store more energy if it already is storing some.

entries.name: Unleash Energy
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can utter the armour's second command word, unleashing the energy stored in all of the armour's eyes. For each eye that was storing energy, the creature takes {@damage 1d6} damage of the stored type, after which the stored energy disappears.
This armour has {@b 4 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries: The damage die of this weapon is increased by one size (e.g., a longbow deals {@damage 1d10} damage, a shortbow deals {@damage 1d8}). The bow has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Siege Brace
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 charge and speak the bow's command word, causing a skeletal, demonic hand to burst from the ground or another surface and hold the bow steady until you use another bonus action to dismiss it. The bow can't be moved while grasped by the skeletal hand. While in this 'siege mode', each time you make a ranged weapon attack using this bow, you can make one additional attack against the same target (no action required). When you do so, both attacks are made with {@b disadvantage}.

inherits.reqAttune: by a creature with a Strength score of 13 or higher
entries: The damage die of this bow is increased by two sizes instead of one, (e.g., a longbow deals {@damage 1d12} damage and a shortbow deals {@damage 1d10}). The bow has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Siege Brace

While in this 'siege mode', each time you make a ranged weapon attack using this bow, you can make one additional attack against the same target (no action required). When you do so, both attacks are made with {@b disadvantage}.
While holding this magic weapon, you can use an action to cast the {@spell produce flame} cantrip ({@hit 5} to hit).

entries.name: Posthumous Mirth
entries: As an action, you can shake the weapon to cast the {@spell Tasha's hideous laughter|hideous laughter} spell (save {@dc 13}), causing the skull's jaw to rattle up and down, and a malevolent cackling sound to fill the air. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
While holding this magic weapon, you can use an action to cast the {@spell produce flame} cantrip as a 5th-level caster ({@hit 7} to hit).

entries.name: Posthumous Mirth

This property can be used four times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.
While holding this magic weapon, you can use an action to cast the {@spell produce flame} cantrip as an 11th-level caster ({@hit 8} to hit).

This property can be used seven times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Stardust Ammo
entries: Instead of normal ammunition, flarefuses consume stardust to fire. A sufficient amount of stardust for a single shot is generally worth 1 gp. You load stardust into the firearm as you would normal ammunition.

entries.name: Firing Modes
entries: Refractive prisms allow a flarefuse to fire in one of two modes; focused or flared. As a bonus action, you can switch between these firing modes. Regardless of the firing mode, each shot uses up the same amount of stardust.

items.name: Focused
entries: The weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} radiant damage on a hit.

items.name: Flared
entries: Instead of making an attack roll, you shoot a wave of radiant shrapnel in a {@b 15-foot cone}. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} radiant damage.

entries: While holding the weapon, you can use a bonus action to cause it to switch from an ethereal blade of nebulous energy that deals psychic damage on a hit to a hardened, crystalline structure that deals the sword's normal damage type on a hit, or vice-versa.

entries.name: Dopplerstrike
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you make an attack with a starforged weapon, you can choose to simultaneously use your movement to move 5 feet directly towards or away from your target as part of the attack, generating a blue-shift strike or red-shift strike, respectively.

items.name: Blue Shift
entries: To perform a blue-shift strike, your target must be within your weapon's reach at the end of this 5-foot movement but does not need to be within your weapon's reach when you start this movement. You gain a +1 bonus to your attack roll, which deals an extra {@damage 1d6} radiant damage on a hit.

items.name: Red Shift
entries: To perform a red-shift strike, your target must be within your weapon's reach at the start of this 5-foot movement but does not need to be within your weapon's reach at the movement's conclusion. On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} cold damage and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn. A creature that loses its reaction in this way does so before your movement triggers an opportunity attack.

items.name: Blue Shift
entries: To perform a blue-shift strike, your target must be within your weapon's reach at the end of this 5-foot movement but does not need to be within your weapon's reach when you start this movement. You gain a +3 bonus to your attack roll, which deals an extra {@damage 3d6} radiant damage on a hit.

items.name: Red Shift
entries: To perform a red-shift strike, your target must be within your weapon's reach at the start of this 5-foot movement but does not need to be within your weapon's reach at the movement's conclusion. On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 3d6} cold damage and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn. A creature that loses its reaction in this way does so before your movement triggers an opportunity attack.
This magic weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} psychic damage to any target it hits. This damage is doubled against undead with the Incorporeal Movement trait.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: A phantom blade is a sentient, lawful neutral item with an Intelligence of 14, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 12. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of {@b 120 feet}. The weapon can speak, read, and understand Common.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Phantom blades see themselves as righteous defenders of inanimate objects and take umbrage at being used to break down doors or crack open chests. In addition, their own experience with death makes them particularly sensitive to inflicting it upon others. Whenever you use the phantom blade to kill a creature that isn't undead, the sword attempts to haunt you. You must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or develop a short-term madness effect. Once the sword has attempted to haunt a creature, it can't do so again until the next dawn.
This magic weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} psychic damage to any target it hits. This damage is tripled against incorporeal undead instead of doubled.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: A phantom blade is a sentient, lawful neutral item with an Intelligence of 14, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 12. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of {@b 120 feet}. The weapon can speak, read, and understand Common.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Phantom blades see themselves as righteous defenders of inanimate objects and take umbrage at being used to break down doors or crack open chests. In addition, their own experience with death makes them particularly sensitive to inflicting it upon others. Whenever you use the phantom blade to kill a creature that isn't undead, the sword attempts to haunt you. You must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or develop a short-term madness effect. Once the sword has attempted to haunt a creature, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Elemental Ammunition
entries: Instead of normal ammunition, this weapon uses special capsules of elemental energy. The weapon can hold one capsule at a time, which can be loaded into it as an action and, while using these capsules, you ignore the Loading or Reload properties of the weapon if it has them. Each capsule contains 6 charges, and new capsules can be requisitioned from the quartermaster or purchased on the grey market for 5 gp each. Capsules come in five flavours: acid, cold, fire, lightning, and thunder. Each attack with the piecemaker expends 1 charge from the loaded capsule, and deals damage of the capsule's type instead of its normal damage type.

entries.name: Conflict Avoidance
entries: If the target of your attack is an unarmed creature, the weapon doesn't fire and the attack is wasted. The piecemaker is sophisticated enough to detect whether a creature has natural weapons, such as a bear's claws and bite. If a creature's natural weapons are restricted—such as handcuffed claws, or a muzzled maw—the piecemaker deems the target to be unarmed. Unarmed strikes notably aren't classified as weapons. For this reason, the Shadow Enclave's agents often manage to escape confrontations with the FBI unscathed.

entries.name: Elemental Ammunition
entries: Instead of normal ammunition, this weapon uses special capsules of elemental energy. The weapon can hold one capsule at a time, which can be loaded into it as an action and, while using these capsules, you ignore the Loading or Reload properties of the weapon if it has them. entries.name: Enhance Ammo
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can enhance the capsule currently loaded into it. Each of the attacks you make that expend charges from that capsule has an additional effect depending on its flavour. Removing an enhanced capsule from the weapon removes the enhancement as well. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Acid Dissolution
entries: When you hit a creature wearing nonmagical metal armour with an acid charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On an 11 or higher, its armour suffers a cumulative -1 penalty to the AC it offers. The armour is destroyed if the penalty reduces its AC to 10.

items.name: Cold Deceleration
entries: A creature hit by a cold charge must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have its speed reduced to 0 feet until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Fire Explosion
entries: The target hit by a fire charge and each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d6} fire damage.

items.name: Lightning Inhibition
entries: A creature hit by a lightning charge must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be unable to take reactions until the start of its next turn. A creature that fails this saving throw by 10 or more is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn unless it is immune to being {@condition stunned}.

items.name: Thunder Disorientation
entries: A creature hit by a thunder charge must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition deafened} until the end of its next turn and have {@b disadvantage} on the next attack roll or ability check it makes before then.
The piecemaker mk.3 has enhanced targeting optics, granting you a +3 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using this ammunition, the elixir within is injected into the creature, which suffers its effects. A creature can choose to make itself an easy target against this ammunition, giving you {@b advantage} on the attack roll. If the attack misses, the elixir is ejected and wasted. Either way, the ammunition behaves like ordinary magical ammunition until the next dawn, when it is magically refilled.

entries.name: Red Restoration
entries: On a hit, the attack doesn't deal any damage. Instead, the target is cured of one disease or condition (target's choice). The condition can be {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralyzed}, or {@condition poisoned}.

When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using this ammunition, the elixir within is injected into the creature, which suffers its effects. A creature can choose to make itself an easy target against this ammunition, giving you {@b advantage} on the attack roll. If the attack misses, the elixir is ejected and wasted. Either way, the ammunition behaves like ordinary magical ammunition until the next dawn, when it is magically refilled.

entries.name: Teal Torpor
entries: On a hit, this ammunition deals the attack's normal damage. In addition, the creature must succeed a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or suffer the effects of the {@spell hold person} spell until the end of its next turn.

When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using this ammunition, the elixir within is injected into the creature, which suffers its effects. A creature can choose to make itself an easy target against this ammunition, giving you {@b advantage} on the attack roll. If the attack misses, the elixir is ejected and wasted. Either way, the ammunition behaves like ordinary magical ammunition until the next dawn, when it is magically refilled.

entries.name: Blue Benevolence
entries: On a hit, the attack doesn't deal any damage. Instead, the target regains {@dice 4d4 + 4} hit points and gains the benefits of the {@spell bless} spell for {@b 1 minute}.

When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using this ammunition, the elixir within is injected into the creature, which suffers its effects. A creature can choose to make itself an easy target against this ammunition, giving you {@b advantage} on the attack roll. If the attack misses, the elixir is ejected and wasted. Either way, the ammunition behaves like ordinary magical ammunition until the next dawn, when it is magically refilled.

entries.name: Indigo Insanity
entries: On a hit, this ammunition deals the attack's normal damage. In addition, the creature must succeed a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or suffer the effects of the {@spell confusion} spell for {@b 1 minute}. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using this ammunition, the elixir within is injected into the creature, which suffers its effects. A creature can choose to make itself an easy target against this ammunition, giving you {@b advantage} on the attack roll. If the attack misses, the elixir is ejected and wasted. Either way, the ammunition behaves like ordinary magical ammunition until the next dawn, when it is magically refilled.

entries.name: Purple Petrification
entries: On a hit, this ammunition deals the attack's normal damage. In addition, the creature must succeed a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or suffer the effects of the {@spell flesh to stone} spell. You must concentrate on this effect as you would on a spell.

Suspended in an invisible gravimantic field, the shards of gemstone that float around this item refract a kaleidoscopic display of light in shades of mauve, indigo, and lilac. The concentric disks mimic a solar system, a homage to the amethyst dragons and their adoration of celestial bodies.
This item has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Irresistible Force
entries: As a bonus action while holding it, you can expend 1 charge to target a creature you can see within 60 feet of you, forcing it to make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is pulled or pushed up to 20 feet directly toward or away from you (your choice). A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

entries.name: Planetary Prison
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can cause the stones hovering around its tip to detach from their metal disks and increase in size as they fly towards a Medium or smaller creature you can see within 30 feet of you, attempting to coalesce around it in a rocky prison. The creature must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, with {@b disadvantage} if it's an aberration. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 6d6} bludgeoning damage and is {@condition restrained} inside a 5-foot diameter sphere of rock, which hovers a few feet above the ground. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't trapped.

A creature trapped in this way is restrained and can't breathe. Attacks, spells, and other effects can't extend through the sphere or be cast through it. As a bonus action while a creature is trapped, you can cause the sphere to contract and crush the creature within, dealing {@damage 2d6} bludgeoning damage to it. The sphere lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it (no action required), causing the stones to shrink and teleport back to this item. A trapped creature can use an action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check, freeing itself on a success. Once this property of the item has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

To complement this robust but aerodynamic suit of armour, the ingenious smith Brimbor devised a pair of intricate mechanical wings, powerful enough to raise their wearer aloft and imbue them with dizzying speed and agility. They produce a soft, soothing hum when gliding through the air.
The armour has {@b 10 charges} and regains {@b 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aerial Dash
entries: As a bonus action while wearing it, you can expend 1 charge to gain a flying speed of 30 feet until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Trick of the Flight
entries: When you make a Dexterity saving throw while wearing this armour, you can use your reaction to expend 2 charges in order to fly up to 20 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If this movement takes you out of the area of the effect that triggered the saving throw, you gain {@b advantage} on the saving throw and, if you would take half damage on a successful save, you instead take no damage on a success.

If this movement takes you out of the area of the effect that triggered the saving throw, you gain {@b advantage} on the saving throw and, if you would take half damage on a successful save, you instead take no damage on a success.

entries.name: Safe Landing
entries: When you would take damage from falling, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge to try and use the wings to avoid the impact at the last second. Make a {@dc 13} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. On a success, you take no damage from the fall.

This magic axe deals an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage to any target it hits. While holding the axe, you have {@b advantage} on Intimidation checks against humanoids that perceive themselves as having a lower social standing than you (GM's discretion).
The axe has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Kneel Before Me
entries: As a bonus action while holding this axe, you can target a creature you can see within 30 feet of you and expend 1 charge to channel the tyrannical might of a red dragon. When you do, your axe flares with intense flames and an illusory burning crown momentarily manifests over your head. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or fall {@condition prone}, prostrating itself before you.

entries.name: Punish the Defiant
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you succeeds on a saving throw against one of your spells or effects and you are holding this axe, you can use your reaction to expend 2 charges to force it to make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw as hot air surrounds the target. The creature takes {@damage 3d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Toxic Shot
entries: As an action while holding this firearm, you can release a burst of toxic spores in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d6} poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't poisoned. A poisoned creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property of the firearm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Toxic Shot
entries: As an action while holding this firearm, you can release a burst of toxic spores in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 7d6} poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't poisoned. A poisoned creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property of the firearm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Sludge Shot
entries: As an action while holding this firearm, you can release thick, sticky sludge in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be {@condition restrained} by the sludge. A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within 5 feet of it on a success. Once this property of the firearm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Torpor Shot
entries: As an action while holding this firearm, you can release a cloud of addling spores in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or come under the effect of the {@spell slow} spell for 1 minute (no concentration required). An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property of the firearm has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
This magic sword deals an extra {@damage 1d6} acid damage to the first target it hits on each of your turns.
The sword has {@b 4 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Killsense
entries: While holding this sword, you always know which hostile creature within 30 feet of you that isn't behind total cover currently has the fewest hit points.

entries.name: Exploit Weakness
entries: As a bonus action while you are within 30 feet of the creature that this sword indicates has the fewest hit points, you can expend 1 charge to gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make against that creature before the end of your current turn.

entries.name: Sweet Savagery
entries: Whenever you score a critical hit against a creature with this weapon, your soul sings with the sweet savagery of battle and you gain {@damage 1d6} temporary hit points, which last for 1 minute.

The articulated mechanical hand at the tip of this item sporadically animates with bursts of negative energy. The fingers fondly caress the flawless topaz orb fixed to the palm, which pulses hypnotically with a gentle, steady glow.

entries.name: Entrap Essence
entries: While holding this item, you can absorb the last breath of a dying creature. When you reduce a Small or larger creature to 0 hit points, you gain {@b 5 temporary hit points}.

entries.name: Induce Decay
entries: You can use this item to feed off of the imitation of life you shield yourself with in order to harm and destroy. When you hit a creature with an attack while you are holding this item, you can expend up to 3 temporary hit points to deal extra necrotic damage to the creature equal to the number of temporary hit points expended.

The articulated mechanical hand at the tip of this item sporadically animates with bursts of negative energy. The fingers fondly caress the flawless topaz orb fixed to the palm, which pulses hypnotically with a gentle, steady glow.

entries.name: Entrap Essence
entries: While holding this item, you can absorb the last breath of a dying creature. When you reduce a Small or larger creature to 0 hit points, you gain {@b 5 temporary hit points}.

entries.name: Induce Decay
entries: You can use this item to feed off of the imitation of life you shield yourself with in order to harm and destroy. When you hit a creature with an attack while you are holding this item, you can expend up to 3 temporary hit points to deal extra necrotic damage to the creature equal to the number of temporary hit points expended.

entries.name: Entropic Shield
entries: The negative energy in the topaz orb shapes itself into a magical barrier around you. While you have temporary hit points from this item, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

The articulated mechanical hand at the tip of this item sporadically animates with bursts of negative energy. The fingers fondly caress the flawless topaz orb fixed to the palm, which pulses hypnotically with a gentle, steady glow.

This magic weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} poison damage to the first target it hits on each of your turns.

entries.name: Breath of Betrayal
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can exhale a subtle, poisonous mist that permeates the area around you. A single creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn. A creature that starts its turn while poisoned in this way must immediately use its reaction to make a weapon attack against another creature of your choice within range (or a random one if you don't choose a creature or are incapacitated). Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Cunning Feint
entries: While holding this weapon, you gain the sly intelligence of a green dragon. As a bonus action, you can attempt to deceive a creature within 60 feet of you that can see you, tricking it into defending early against your attacks. Make a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check, contested by that creature's Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check. If you succeed, you gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make against that creature before the end of your current turn.
This magic weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} poison damage to any target it hits.

entries.name: Breath of Betrayal

Each creature within 20 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn. entries.name: Breath of Betrayal

A creature that starts its turn while poisoned in this way must immediately use its reaction to make a weapon attack against another creature of your choice within range (or a random one if you don't choose a creature or are incapacitated). This firearm has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Dead Stop
entries: A special chamber within the firearm is loaded with sharp iron nails rather than normal ammunition. Once on each of your turns when you make an attack against a creature with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to fire a nail instead, which magically increases in size to become a 9-inch-long metal spike, dealing an extra {@damage 1d6} piercing damage on a hit.

In addition, if the target is a Large or smaller creature that isn't immune to the grappled or restrained conditions, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pinned against an adjacent surface (GM's discretion). If there's no surface to pin the creature to, it automatically succeeds on this saving throw. While pinned in this way, the creature's speed is 0 feet. A creature within 5 feet of the pinned creature (including itself) can use an action to make a {@dc 13} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check, removing the spike and freeing the creature on a success.
This firearm has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Dead Stop
entries: A special chamber within the firearm is loaded with sharp iron nails rather than normal ammunition. Once on each of your turns when you make an attack against a creature with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to fire a nail instead, which magically increases in size to become a 9-inch-long metal spike, dealing an extra {@damage 2d6} piercing damage on a hit.

In addition, if the target is a Large or smaller creature that isn't immune to the grappled or restrained conditions, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pinned against an adjacent surface (GM's discretion). If there's no surface to pin the creature to, it automatically succeeds on this saving throw. While pinned in this way, the creature's speed is 0 feet. A creature within 5 feet of the pinned creature (including itself) can use an action to make a {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check, removing the spike and freeing the creature on a success.
This firearm has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@b 1d8} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Dead Stop

In addition, if the target is a Large or smaller creature that isn't immune to the grappled or restrained conditions, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be pinned against an adjacent surface (GM's discretion). If there's no surface to pin the creature to, it automatically succeeds on this saving throw. While pinned in this way, the creature's speed is 0 feet. A creature within 5 feet of the pinned creature (including itself) can use an action to make a {@dc 16} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check, removing the spike and freeing the creature on a success.
When fresh blood is spilled on the pristine blade of this sword, its true, corroded appearance is unveiled, a reminder that a glamorous appearance doesn't guarantee noble intentions.
This item has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Decaying Smite
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this sword, you can expend 1 charge and one spell slot to deal necrotic damage to the target, in addition to the attack's damage. The extra damage is {@damage 3d6} for a 1st-level spell slot, plus {@damage 1d6} for each spell level higher than 1st, to a maximum of {@damage 7d6}. The damage increases by {@damage 1d6} if the target is a celestial, to a maximum of {@damage 8d6}.

entries.name: Eldritch Empowerment
entries: You can hold this sword by the blade to use it as a spellcasting focus, taking {@damage 1d8} slashing damage when you do so, casting the spell out of the pointing finger on the hilt. When you do so, you can expend 1 charge to cause the spell to be cast at one level higher (up to a maximum of 4th level).
When fresh blood is spilled on the pristine blade of this sword, its true, corroded appearance is unveiled, a reminder that a glamorous appearance doesn't guarantee noble intentions.

Sinister yet holy, the waxen god blesses those who endure their molten ministrations, restoring their fortitude, protecting their skin, and purifying their words. When lit, the candlesmoke of this sceptre carries the subtle, sweet scent of charred flesh while its tendrils form the screaming visages of those it has blessed.

Instead of charges, after the wax from a candle has been used, that candle can't be used again until the next dawn. Dropping wax on a creature deals {@damage 1d10} fire damage to it in addition to its other effects.

entries.name: Wax On
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can drop some wax from one of its candles on a willing creature within 5 feet of you. The effects of this wax depend on the colour of the candle you choose:

items.name: White
entries: The wax of this cleansing candle seeps into the skin to purify the body. The creature gains the benefits of the {@spell lesser restoration} spell.

items.name: Orange
entries: The wax of this warding candle expands to form a protective layer over the creature. The creature gains the benefits of the {@spell barkskin} spell for 10 minutes (no concentration required).

items.name: Red
entries: The wax of this overbearing candle forms a barrier over the creature's mouth, preventing it from telling any lies but letting the truth pass. The creature is under the effects of the {@spell zone of truth} spell for 10 minutes.

entries.name: Living Candle
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell living candle|LALoTT} spell (save {@dc 16}).
This item has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Puppet of the Rose
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can expend up to 2 charges. For each charge, one thin, thorny vine shoots towards a different creature you can see within 20 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack against that creature ({@hit 5} to hit), which takes {@damage 2d6} piercing damage and must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw on a hit. On a failed save, a black rose vine grows where the creature was hit and remains for 1 minute, wrapping around the creature's limbs.

While a creature is under this effect, you can manipulate those vines to exert limited control over it. Whenever it would be able to use its reaction, you can use your own reaction to force it to do so, treating all creatures as hostile for the purposes of opportunity attacks. A creature under this effect can't take reactions other than the ones you command it to, and it repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
This item has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@b 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Puppet of the Rose
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can expend up to 2 charges. For each charge, one thin, thorny vine shoots towards a different creature you can see within 20 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack against that creature ({@hit 7} to hit), which takes {@damage 3d6} piercing damage and must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw on a hit. On a failed save, a black rose vine grows where the creature was hit and remains for 1 minute, wrapping around the creature's limbs.

While a creature is under this effect, you can manipulate those vines to exert limited control over it. Whenever it would be able to use its reaction, you can use your own reaction to force it to do so, treating all creatures as hostile for the purposes of opportunity attacks. A creature under this effect can't take reactions other than the ones you command it to, and it repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
This item has {@b 10 charges} and regains {@b 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

Make a ranged spell attack against that creature ({@hit 8} to hit), which takes {@damage 4d6} piercing damage and must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw on a hit. On a failed save, a black rose vine grows where the creature was hit and remains for 1 minute, wrapping around the creature's limbs.

While a creature is under this effect, you can manipulate those vines to exert limited control over it. Whenever it would be able to use its reaction, you can use your own reaction to force it to do so, treating all creatures as hostile for the purposes of opportunity attacks. This weapon has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Wax and Wane
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can cause the orb at its centre to shift, representing one phase of the moon. The weapon has different abilities depending on which phase is currently represented. You can activate the properties that require a bonus action as part of the bonus action taken to change the phase of the moon.

items.name: Full Moon
entries: While in this phase, the entire orb is lit up. The weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} radiant damage to each target it hits. As a bonus action while holding the weapon, you can expend 1 charge to imbue the blade with moonlight; the next creature you hit with an attack using this weapon before the start of your next turn must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Crescent Moon
entries: While in this phase, a crescent shape is lit up. The weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} slashing damage to each target it hits. As a bonus action while holding the weapon, you can expend 1 charge to shoot a blade of sharp light from its tip at a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack ({@hit 8} to hit) against the target, dealing {@damage 4d6} slashing damage on a hit.

items.name: New Moon
entries: While in this phase, the orb is unlit. The weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage to each target it hits. As a bonus action while holding the weapon, you can expend 1 charge to become {@condition invisible} until the start of your next turn. You stop being invisible if you attack or cast a spell.
This weapon has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

A creature that tries to wield the sword without being attuned to it takes {@damage 1d6} piercing damage at the end of each of its turns as thorny vines around the hilt dig into its skin.

entries.name: Flutterstrike
entries: This sword has the Thrown property (range 20/60 feet), and it flies back to your hand immediately after you use it to make a ranged attack.

entries.name: Radiant Lure
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can cause its hilt to cast dim light in a 10-foot radius, or to cease casting light. While the sword is casting light, you can use an action to summon a moth (use the {@creature spider|MM} stat block, without the Bite action option or the Web Sense and Web Walker traits, and with a flying speed of 30 feet). As part of this action, you can cast the {@spell animal messenger} spell on it. Once this spell is cast in this manner, it can't be cast again until the next dawn.

This axe has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Rising Leaves
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can expend 1 charge and swing it in the air to conjure a whirlwind of leaves in the space of a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, forcing it to make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is lifted 30 feet straight up into the air, after which it falls normally. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

entries.name: Falling Leaves
entries: While holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell feather fall} spell at will, targeting only yourself. The spell ends early if you let go of the axe.
This axe has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@b 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Rising Leaves

On a failed save, the creature is lifted 30 feet straight up into the air, after which it falls normally. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

entries.name: Falling Leaves
entries: While holding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell feather fall} spell at will. You can target up to 5 creatures, as normal, when you use the axe to cast the spell.
This bow has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.
The first attack you make with this bow on each of your turns wreathes the arrow in blue flame and deals an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage on a hit.

entries.name: Unseelie Lights
entries: As a bonus action while carrying this bow, you can expend 1 charge to imbue your next attack with it with the ghostly light from the bow's bulbs. If you hit a creature with it, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or come under the effects of the {@spell faerie fire} spell for 1 minute. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
This bow has {@b 4 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.
All attacks you make with this bow deal an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage on a hit.

entries.name: Unseelie Lights
entries: As a bonus action while carrying this bow, you can expend 1 charge to imbue your next attack with it with the ghostly light from the bow's bulbs. If you hit a creature with it, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or come under the effects of the {@spell faerie fire} spell for 1 minute. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
This bow has {@b 4 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.
All attacks you make with this bow deal an extra {@damage 1d4} fire damage on a hit.
Attacks made with this bow automatically hit creatures under the effect of the Unseelie Lights property.

Illusion magic fools the senses, enchantments trick the mind, and this device uses both to warp the reality of its weak-willed targets. No two people can agree on what this item looks like: it tailors its illusions to appear in a form that each observer finds most mesmerising.
This item has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Forget
entries: While holding this item, you can manipulate a creature's memory, erasing realisations and reasserting illusions. When a creature within 60 feet of you that you can see succeeds on a saving throw against an illusion spell or an ability check to disbelieve an illusion, you can use your reaction and expend 1 charge to force that creature to reroll the {@dice d20} with {@b disadvantage}. On a failure, the creature doesn't know it initially succeeded.

entries.name: Remember
entries: As an action when you speak a lie while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge to implant a false memory in the mind of a creature within 30 feet of you that can hear you. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or believe the lie you tell. A creature you are fighting has {@b advantage} on this saving throw. The lie must be worded in such a manner as to sound reasonable. A creature reacts to the lie and acts upon its new beliefs in a way that follows its natural inclinations, alignment, or beliefs. The false memory disappears after 1 hour; during this time, the creature experiences illusions that reinforce the lie.
