The lie must be worded in such a manner as to sound reasonable. A creature reacts to the lie and acts upon its new beliefs in a way that follows its natural inclinations, alignment, or beliefs. The false memory disappears after 1 hour; during this time, the creature experiences illusions that reinforce the lie.

Illusion magic fools the senses, enchantments trick the mind, and this device uses both to warp the reality of its weak-willed targets. No two people can agree on what this item looks like: it tailors its illusions to appear in a form that each observer finds most mesmerising.
This item has {@b 4 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Forget
entries: While holding this item, you can manipulate a creature's memory, erasing realisations and reasserting illusions. When a creature within 60 feet of you that you can see succeeds on a saving throw against an illusion spell or an ability check to disbelieve an illusion, you can use your reaction and expend 1 charge to force that creature to reroll the {@dice d20} with {@b disadvantage}. On a failure, the creature doesn't know it initially succeeded.

entries.name: Remember
entries: As an action when you speak a lie while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge to implant a false memory in the mind of a creature within 30 feet of you that can hear you. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or believe the lie you tell. A creature you are fighting has {@b advantage} on this saving throw. The lie must be worded in such a manner as to sound reasonable. A creature reacts to the lie and acts upon its new beliefs in a way that follows its natural inclinations, alignment, or beliefs. The false memory disappears after 1 hour; during this time, the creature experiences illusions that reinforce the lie.

This gold and onyx length of wood is ornately carved with the likeness of black dragon, and houses four large gemstones: a ruby, emerald, sapphire, and diamond. Each gem flickers with an inner light, a core of elemental magic. Blending evocation and transmutation magic, this device allows the destructive forces of the elements to be shaped to the whim of its wielder.
This item has {@b 3 charges} and regains {@b 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spellcrafter
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge and activate one of the gems to create an elemental blast, choosing either a damage type or the type of area the effect occupies from the table below. Roll a {@dice d4} to randomly determine the parameter you didn't choose; for example, if you choose a 40-foot cone, randomly determine the damage type. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d6} damage of the respective type on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

rows: 10-foot-radius, 60-foot-high cylinder centred on a point of your choice within 120 feet of you
20-foot-radius sphere centred on a point of your choice within 60 feet of you

entries: This sling has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Omnicast
entries: You can use this weapon as a spellcasting focus for your spells. When you do so, the spell takes the appearance of a little sphere of swirling magic, and is shot from the weapon. In addition, you can use an action to insert a spell scroll into the compartment in the weapon's handle. While the weapon contains a spell scroll, you can cast the spell in it regardless of whether or not it's on your class's spell list. If you are normally unable to cast spells, you must make the ability check to determine whether you cast the spell from the scroll successfully for any level of spell, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability. The scroll is destroyed as normal once the spell is cast.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Scholastic Madness property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: Scholastic Madness
entries: Once per turn, when you make an attack with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to imbue the ammunition with unpredictable power from one of the eight schools of magic. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table below to determine the outcome.

rows: Hit or miss, the projectile returns to orbit you, granting you a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

On a hit, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or be teleported to an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within 20 feet of it. The chosen space must be on a surface or in a liquid that can support the target without the target having to squeeze.
You gain {@b advantage} on all attacks made with this weapon against the target, including this one, until the end of your next turn.
On a hit, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you until the end of its next turn.
On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d4} acid, {@damage 1d4} cold, {@damage 1d4} fire, and {@damage 1d4} lightning damage.
On a hit, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.
On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage, and you regain an equal number of hit points.
On a hit, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be transformed into a rat, as per the {@spell polymorph} spell (no concentration required), until the end of its next turn.

entries: This weapon has {@b 12 charges} and regains {@b 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Thinking with Portals
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to create a small blue portal in an unoccupied space you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you, which lasts until the start of your next turn or until you create another blue portal. When you make an attack roll with the sword, you can have it create a temporary orange portal and attack through it, allowing you to target creatures within your reach of the blue portal as if you were in its space. Additionally, creatures provoke opportunity attacks from you when moving away from the blue portal.
This weapon has {@b 12 charges} and regains {@b 1d8 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. Attacks made through the portals deal an extra {@damage 1d4} force damage on a hit.

entries.name: Thinking with Portals

entries.name: Emergency Exit
entries: While holding this sword, you can cast the {@spell dimension door} spell. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
This sword has {@b 3 charges} and regains {@b 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lockjaw
entries: As an action while holding the sword, you can expend 1 charge to perform a special melee weapon attack with the sword, which splits down the middle and tries to close itself around the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 6d6} piercing damage and is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). While a creature is grappled by the sword, you can't use the sword to attack another creature. You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with the sword against a creature grappled by it, and those attacks deal piercing damage instead of their normal damage type, as the sword's teeth chew through the target.
This sword has {@b 3 charges} and regains {@b 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lockjaw
entries: Using the Lockjaw property doesn't take an action; instead, it replaces one of the attacks you make when you take the Attack action on your turn. You can expend 1 charge to perform a special melee weapon attack with the sword, which splits down the middle and tries to close itself around the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 6d6} piercing damage and is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). While a creature is grappled by the sword, you can't use the sword to attack another creature. You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with the sword against a creature grappled by it, and those attacks deal piercing damage instead of their normal damage type, as the sword's teeth chew through the target.
This sword has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Haemoblade
entries: When you reduce a Small or larger creature that has blood to 0 hit points with an attack using this sword, some of the target's blood temporarily coalesces around the blade, sharpening it. You gain {@b advantage} on the next attack roll you make with this sword before the end of your next turn, and it deals an extra {@damage 2d6} damage if that attack hits.

entries: When you reduce a Small or larger creature that has blood to 0 hit points with an attack using this sword, some of the target's blood temporarily coalesces around the blade, sharpening it. entries.name: Eradicate Bloodline
entries: As an action, you can sink this sword into the corpse of a humanoid that has blood within your reach. Each creature of the same race (GM's discretion) within 20 feet of the corpse must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 8d8} necrotic damage as its blood withers in its veins. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn. Creatures that are a "half" race (e.g. half-orc or half-elf) count as both parent races, but take half as much damage on a failed save.
This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Maker's Wrath
entries: This weapon deals double damage to objects, structures, and constructs.

entries.name: Blade Storm
entries: Once on each of your turns when you take the Attack action, you can expend 1 charge to replace one of your attacks with a special strike, shooting the broken fragments of the sword in a 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line originating from you. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success.
This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success.

Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success.

This sword has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. Whenever you make an attack roll with this weapon without being attuned to it, you take {@damage 1d4} piercing damage as bees sting you.

entries.name: Honey Coating
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to launch a spray of sticky honey in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or be covered in honey until the end of its next turn, when the honey dissolves. A creature covered in honey has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws, and its speed is halved.

entries.name: Sugar Rush
entries: While holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell sugar rush|LALoTT} spell. When you cast the spell in this way, it has a range of touch, as you feed some of the sword's honey to the target.

entries: Once on each of your turns when you take the Attack action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to replace one of your attacks with a ray of randomly coloured light that you shoot from the sword at a target you can see within 60 feet of you. The target must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table below to determine which ray is shot at the target.

rows: The target takes {@damage 6d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries: This weapon has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Brutal Wounds
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to inflict a grievous wound upon it. Whenever the wounded creature takes an action, bonus action, or reaction that doesn't deal damage, or the first time on its turn that it uses any movement, it takes {@damage 2d4} necrotic damage. The wounded creature or another creature within reach of it can take an action to make a {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check, ending the effect on a success. If the wounded creature regains 5 or more hit points from a single source, the wound is also healed. A creature can suffer from only one such wound at a time.

entries.name: Scent of Pain
entries: While holding this weapon, you have an uncanny ability to detect creatures by their suffering. You have {@b advantage} on all ability checks made to track a creature that has been wounded by the Brutal Wounds property of this weapon in the last hour, even if the wound has been healed.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Three Strikes
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this sword, you can expend 1 charge to attempt to curse the target with misfortune. It must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or have {@b disadvantage} on the next three attack rolls it makes within 1 minute, after which the curse ends.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges}, each represented by a red and black spectral skull that floats around the blade, and regains {@b 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Deadly Sins
entries: As a bonus action when you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to afflict the creature with a sinful desire. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table below. Each sin's saving throw has a DC of 13. Each of the effects lasts until the end of your next turn.

rows: Envy. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be afflicted by the {@spell bane} spell.
Gluttony. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become ravenously hungry and spend its action on its next turn looking for food and stuffing its face.
Greed. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or spend its movement and action on its next turn trying to grab and abscond with a valuable item it can see.
Lust. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you.
Sloth. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or its speed is halved, it can't take reactions, and it can take only a bonus action or action on its turn, not both.

Pride. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become hard headed, during which time it is unable to benefit from the Help action, withdraw from combat, or be the willing target of any spell or effect from another creature.
Wrath. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or use its reaction at the start of each of its turns to make one attack against a random creature within its reach/range.
Roll twice on this table, rerolling any 8s or duplicates.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, it gains the Sssugessstion property.

entries.name: Sssugessstion
entries: As an action, you can cause the blade's snake to turn invisible and slither to a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, where the snake whispers to it, casting the {@spell suggestion} spell (save {@dc 13}). If the suggestion isn't a sinful one, the creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw. You can use this property once, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.
This weapon has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Deadly Sins
entries: As a bonus action when you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to afflict the creature with a sinful desire. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table below. Each sin's saving throw has a DC of 15. Each of the effects lasts until the end of your next turn.

rows: Envy. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be afflicted by the {@spell bane} spell.
Gluttony. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become ravenously hungry and spend its action on its next turn looking for food and stuffing its face.
Greed. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or spend its movement and action on its next turn trying to grab and abscond with a valuable item it can see.
Lust. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you.
Sloth. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or its speed is halved, it can't take reactions, and it can take only a bonus action or action on its turn, not both.

Pride. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become hard headed, during which time it is unable to benefit from the Help action, withdraw from combat, or be the willing target of any spell or effect from another creature.
Wrath. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or use its reaction at the start of each of its turns to make one attack against a random creature within its reach/range.
Roll twice on this table, rerolling any 8s or duplicates.

entries.name: Deadly Sins
entries: As a bonus action when you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to afflict the creature with a sinful desire. Roll a {@dice d8} and consult the table below. Each sin's saving throw has a DC of 16. A creature afflicted by a sin that lasts 1 minute can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the sinful effect on a success.

rows: Envy. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be afflicted by the {@spell bane} spell for 1 minute.
Gluttony. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become ravenously hungry and spend its action on its next turn looking for food and stuffing its face with whatever it can find.
Greed. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or spend its movement and action on its next turn trying to grab and abscond with a valuable item it can see.
Lust. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you for 1 minute.
Sloth. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or, for 1 minute, its speed is halved, it can't take reactions, and it can take only a bonus action or action on its turn, not both.

Pride. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become hard headed for 1 minute, during which time it is unable to benefit from the Help action, withdraw from combat, or be the willing target of any spell or effect from another creature.
Wrath. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or, for 1 minute, use its reaction at the start of each of its turns to make one attack against a random creature within its reach/range.
Roll twice on this table, rerolling any 8s or duplicates.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, it gains the Sssugessstion property.

entries.name: Sssugessstion
entries: As an action, you can cause the blade's snake to turn invisible and slither to a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, where the snake whispers to it, casting the {@spell suggestion} spell (save {@dc 16}). If the suggestion isn't a sinful one, the creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw. You can use this property twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.
This weapon has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Safety Violation
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to cause the machinery mounted along the blade to activate. When you do, choose lightning or fire. Until the start of your next turn, attacks made with this sword deal an extra {@damage 1d8} damage of the chosen type. In addition, whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon while it's activated, the target must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, if you chose lightning, the target is {@condition stunned} until the start of your next turn. If you chose fire, the creature is set aflame and takes {@damage 1d8} fire damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute. It or a creature within reach of it can use its action to pat out the flames.
This weapon has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Safety Violation
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to cause the machinery mounted along the blade to activate. When you do, choose lightning or fire. Until the start of your next turn, attacks made with this sword deal an extra {@damage 2d8} damage of the chosen type. In addition, whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon while it's activated, the target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, if you chose lightning, the target is {@condition stunned} until the start of your next turn. If you chose fire, the creature is set aflame and takes {@damage 2d8} fire damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute. It or a creature within reach of it can use its action to pat out the flames.
This weapon has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@b 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Safety Violation
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to cause the machinery mounted along the blade to activate. When you do, choose lightning or fire. Until the start of your next turn, attacks made with this sword deal an extra {@damage 2d8} damage of the chosen type. In addition, whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon while it's activated, the target must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, if you chose lightning, the target is {@condition stunned} until the start of your next turn. If you chose fire, the creature is set aflame and takes {@damage 3d8} fire damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute. It or a creature within reach of it can use its action to pat out the flames.
This weapon has {@b 7 charges}, represented by the colourful gems along the blade that become dull when expended, and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Open Chakra
entries: While holding this sword, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and unlock a portion of your inner potential by casting the {@spell guidance|} cantrip, targeting yourself.

entries.name: Enlightened State
entries: When you expend the last of the weapon's charges, you gain {@b inspiration}. In addition, if the weapon has no charges, your feet hover 1 foot above the ground while you hold it and aren't {@condition prone}. You can move by walking on air or simply gliding forward, and ignore the effects of any hazards on the ground, such as difficult terrain or the effects of the {@spell spike growth} spell.
This weapon has {@b 7 charges}, represented by the colourful gems along the blade that become dull when expended, and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Open Chakra
entries: While holding this sword, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and unlock a portion of your inner potential by casting the {@spell guidance|} cantrip, targeting yourself. When you cast the guidance cantrip using the sword, it requires no concentration and you regain {@damage 1d4} hit points.

entries.name: Enlightened State
entries: When you expend the last of the weapon's charges, you gain {@b inspiration}. In addition, if the weapon has no charges, your feet hover 1 foot above the ground while you hold it and aren't {@condition prone}. This weapon has {@b 7 charges}, represented by the colourful gems along the blade that become dull when expended, and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Enlightened State
entries: When you expend the last of the weapon's charges, you gain {@b inspiration}. In addition, if the weapon has no charges, your feet hover 1 foot above the ground while you hold it and aren't {@condition prone}. You can move by walking on air or simply gliding forward, and ignore the effects of any hazards on the ground, such as difficult terrain or the effects of the {@spell spike growth} spell. While hovering with the Enlightened State property, you can't be knocked {@condition prone} against your will.

entries.name: Surrender Self
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can speak its command word to encase yourself in gold until the start of your next turn as the sword liquefies and flows over your body. While you are encased in this way, you are {@condition petrified}, you are immune to all damage except force damage, and you remain aware of your surroundings. In addition, when a hostile creature starts its turn within 15 feet of you, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be compelled to spend its turn moving toward you and attacking you. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Surrender Self
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can speak its command word to encase yourself in gold until the start of your next turn as the sword liquefies and flows over your body. While you are encased in this way, you are {@condition petrified}, you are immune to all damage except force damage, and you remain aware of your surroundings. In addition, when a hostile creature starts its turn within 15 feet of you, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be compelled to spend its turn moving toward you and attacking you. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Forfeit Fortune
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can melt 25 gold pieces (or gold equivalent) and fire a stream of liquid gold at a target within 30 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack ({@hit 7} to hit). On a hit, the target takes {@damage 6d6} fire damage and is covered in molten gold for 1 minute or until it or another creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to clean off the gold. A target covered in gold takes {@damage 3d6} fire damage at the start of each of its turns. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Surrender Self

In addition, when a hostile creature starts its turn within 15 feet of you, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or be compelled to spend its turn moving toward you and attacking you. At the start of your next turn after using this property, you can choose to remain encased in gold until the start of your next turn. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Chainbreaker Strike
entries: You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with this sword against creatures {@condition grappling} an unwilling target. The first time each turn you hit such a creature, you deal an extra {@damage 2d6} radiant damage to it, and the grappled creature can use its reaction to automatically escape from the grapple.

entries.name: Chainbreaker Strike
entries: You have {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with this sword against creatures {@condition grappling} an unwilling target. The first time each turn you hit such a creature, you deal an extra {@damage 4d6} radiant damage to it, and the grappled creature can use its reaction to automatically escape from the grapple.

entries.name: To the Rescue!
entries: Once on each of your turns, you can move up to half your speed toward a hostile creature {@condition grappling} an unwilling target, without expending any movement.

entries.name: Limitless Shapes
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can cause the paint that makes up its blade to shift, changing its size and shape to transform the weapon into any type of sword.

entries.name: Boundless Imagination
entries: While holding this sword, you can cast the {@spell minor illusion} cantrip at will. In addition, you can use the sword to cast the {@spell disguise self} and {@spell silent image} spells (save {@dc 13}). Once the sword has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Captivating Imagery
entries: When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher using the Boundless Imagination property, you can tailor the spell's illusion to a specific creature. Choose a creature that can see the illusion within 30 feet of you. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Illusory Appearance
entries: As an action, you can disguise this weapon to look like a mundane walking stick with a magical illusion. The illusion lasts until you end it as a bonus action or you fall unconscious. The changes wrought by this effect fail to hold up to physical inspection. To discern that the weapon is disguised, a creature can use its action to inspect it and must succeed on a {@dc 20} Intelligence (Investigation) check.

entries.name: Steal Life
entries: When you hit a non-construct creature with an attack roll using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to syphon the target's life force. The target must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. You regain hit points equal to half the necrotic damage taken.
This weapon has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@b 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

An old wives' tale, the Needlemonger is said to catch wandering children and sew them into their own shadows so that they can become playthings of the unseelie court. Despite an innocuous appearance, this long, narrow blade specialises in immobilising targets and disabling limbs.
This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Darts and Crafts
entries: While holding this sword, you can pull pins out of the cushion at its hilt and use them as magical darts. You are considered proficient with these darts. When you hit a creature with one of these darts, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell pins & needles|LALoTT} cantrip on it (save {@dc 13}; no action required). A dart that's been removed from the cushion disappears at the end of your turn, and a new one appears in the cushion to replace it.

entries.name: Shadow Stitcher
entries: When you hit a creature in dim or bright light with this sword, you can expend 1 charge to manifest ethereal threads that attempt to stitch the creature to its own shadow. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or have its speed reduced to 0 feet until the end of its next turn.

As a bonus action, you can cause the twinblade to separate at the hilt, becoming two separate shortswords, or to rejoin into a single twinblade.

entries.name: Twinblade: Soul Slicer
entries: When you take the Whirl special bonus action with this twinblade and succeed on the Dexterity check, you can also use an action to begin spinning it with such incredible speed that they can cut right through to a creature's essence. Until the start of your next turn, your speed is halved and whenever you and a creature move within 5 feet of each other, you can force it to make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} slashing damage and {@damage 4d6} psychic damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. A creature can only be damaged by this property once per turn. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Shortswords: Shear Nerve
entries: While holding these blades in their shortsword form, you can use an action to perform a special cut against a creature within 5 feet of you. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or perceive its hands as having been cut off until the end of your next turn. While this effect lasts, the creature can't perform any tasks that require the use of its hands (GM's discretion), such as manipulating weapons or material components, performing the somatic components of spells, or attacking with claws or fists. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries: While holding this sword, you can cast the following spells (save {@dc 13}, {@hit 5} to hit with spell attacks): {@spell bane} and {@spell bless}; {@spell cure wounds} and {@spell inflict wounds}; {@spell darkness} and {@spell daylight}. These spells are grouped in pairs, and once you've cast a spell, you can't cast either spell in that pair again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Dramatic Score
entries: When you roll a 1 or a 20 on an attack roll made with this magic weapon against a creature of CR 1 or above, it produces a powerful song appropriate for the moment, depending on what you rolled:

items.name: Natural 1
entries: The tense, suspenseful song makes you brace against the strength of your enemies. You and another creature of your choice that can hear the song within 30 feet of you gain {@damage 2d4} temporary hit points that last for 1 minute.

items.name: Natural 20
entries: The epic, victorious song replenishes your energy and helps you press your advantage. You and another creature of your choice that can hear the song within 30 feet of you regain {@damage 1d4} hit points, and the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d4} damage.

entries: When you roll a 1 or a 20 on an attack roll made with this magic weapon against a creature of CR 1 or above, it produces a powerful song appropriate for the moment, depending on what you rolled:

items.name: Natural 1
entries: The tense, suspenseful song makes you brace against the strength of your enemies. You and another creature of your choice that can hear the song within 30 feet of you gain {@damage 4d4} temporary hit points that last for 1 minute.

items.name: Natural 20
entries: The epic, victorious song replenishes your energy and helps you press your advantage. You and another creature of your choice that can hear the song within 30 feet of you regain {@damage 2d4} hit points, and the attack deals an extra {@damage 2d4} damage.

entries.name: Dramatic Score
entries: When you roll a 1 or a 20 on an attack roll made with this magic weapon against a creature of CR 1 or above, it produces a powerful song appropriate for the moment, depending on what you rolled:

items.name: Natural 1
entries: The tense, suspenseful song makes you brace against the strength of your enemies. You and another creature of your choice that can hear the song within 30 feet of you gain {@damage 4d4} temporary hit points that last for 1 minute. When you roll a 1, you and the other creature you choose also gain {@b advantage} on the next saving throw you make within the next minute.

items.name: Natural 20
entries: The epic, victorious song replenishes your energy and helps you press your advantage. You and another creature of your choice that can hear the song within 30 feet of you regain {@damage 3d4} hit points, and the attack deals an extra {@damage 3d4} damage.

A creature holding this blade has {@b advantage} on Carving checks on corpses that have scales. When you make weapon attacks with this blade against creatures with scales, the critical hit range is one greater than normal (for example, from a 20 on a d20 to a 19 or 20).

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, it gains the Autotomic Tail and Scaleskin properties. In addition, when you finish a long rest, you find that you have grown a tail and that your skin is covered in tiny, hard scales. These structures disappear when you finish a long rest while not attuned to the sword.

entries.name: OA: Autotomic Tail
entries: As a bonus action while you are {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained} by a creature, you can choose to take {@damage 1d10} necrotic damage and end the condition, leaving the creature holding your self-amputated tail. Once you use this property, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, when your tail grows back.

entries.name: OA: Scaleskin
entries: While you're not wearing heavy armour, any slashing or piercing damage you take is reduced by 1.

entries.name: Reap What You Sow
entries: When you hit a creature of CR 1 or higher with an attack using this sword, it gains 1 charge as it drains some of the target's vitality. If you rolled a 20 on the attack roll, it gains 2 charges instead. The sword can hold a maximum of 7 charges, each one represented by a glowing gem set into the blade. As a bonus action while holding the sword, you can expend 7 charges to regain {@damage 3d6} hit points.

entries.name: Reap What You Sow
entries: When you hit a creature of CR 1 or higher with an attack using this sword, it gains 1 charge as it drains some of the target's vitality. If you rolled a 20 on the attack roll, it gains 2 charges instead. The sword can hold a maximum of 7 charges, each one represented by a glowing gem set into the blade. As a bonus action while holding the sword, you can expend 7 charges to regain {@damage 4d6} hit points.

entries.name: Reap What You Sow
entries: When you hit a creature of CR 1 or higher with an attack using this sword, it gains 1 charge as it drains some of the target's vitality. If you rolled a 20 on the attack roll, it gains 2 charges instead. The sword can hold a maximum of 7 charges, each one represented by a glowing gem set into the blade. As a bonus action while holding the sword, you can expend 7 charges to regain {@damage 6d6} hit points.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Event Horizon
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge and speak its command word to exert the gravity of a black hole on your foes. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pulled in a straight line to a space within 5 feet of you. If a creature being pulled towards you hits an obstacle or another creature, it stops moving.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Event Horizon
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge and speak its command word to exert the gravity of a black hole on your foes. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pulled in a straight line to a space within 5 feet of you and have its speed reduced to 0 feet until the start of your next turn. If a creature being pulled towards you hits an obstacle or another creature, it stops moving.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Event Horizon
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge and speak its command word to exert the gravity of a black hole on your foes. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be pulled in a straight line to a space within 5 feet of you and have its speed reduced to 0 feet until the start of your next turn, taking {@damage 3d6} force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If a creature being pulled towards you hits an obstacle or another creature, it stops moving.

While grasping the hilt of this sword, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of pure lightning to form, or make the blade disappear. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals lightning damage instead of its normal damage type.
The sword has {@b 3 charges} and regains {@b 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Blade of Lightning
entries: As a bonus action while holding this sword, you can expend up to 2 charges to channel more energy into the blade. Until the end of the turn, the reach of attacks made with this sword increases by 5 feet for each charge expended, and the first time you hit a target with an attack roll using this sword, it deals an extra {@damage 2d8} lightning damage for each charge expended.

entries.name: Lightning Rod
entries: As a reaction when you see another creature within 20 feet of you take lightning damage, you can divert the flow of energy towards yourself. You gain resistance to lightning damage until the end of your next turn, take the lightning damage instead of the creature, and the sword regains 1 expended charge per 10 damage taken this way. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Drop the Anchor
entries: As a reaction when you would be moved against your will, you can slam this sword into an adjacent surface. If you do, you can't be moved away from that surface against your will until the start of your next turn or you let go of the sword.

entries.name: Bubble Trouble
entries: While holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Otiluke's resilient sphere||resilient sphere} spell (save {@dc 13}). When you do, a bubble forms from the tip of the sword, growing into the sphere of force that encases the target. The sphere can only last until the end of your next turn.
This weapon has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. This sword deals slashing damage instead of its normal damage type.

entries.name: Drop the Anchor

entries.name: Aberrant Infection
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this sword, you can expend 1 charge to force the creature to make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target is infected with a portion of the entity in the blade for 1 minute. As a bonus action, you can deal {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage to an infected creature within 60 feet of you, and you regain hit points equal to half the damage taken. An infected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: That-which-hungers is a sentient chaotic neutral weapon with an Intelligence of 16, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 18. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of {@b 120 feet}. This weapon communicates telepathically with its wielder and can read and understand Common and Deep Speech.

entries.name: Personality
entries: That-which-hungers came to the Material Plane to feast on the souls of mortals, but found their corruption unpalatable, and soon discovered a superior alternative: sugar. It desires to taste as many different sweet treats as possible, and will put its power in the service of any wielder who enables its ravenous hunger.
This sword has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Shadow of the Colossus
entries: While holding this sword, you have {@b advantage} on ability checks made to climb onto and remain atop creatures larger than you, as well as ability checks made to avoid or escape being {@condition grappled} by them.

entries.name: The Bigger They Are...
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 charge to make a special weapon attack with this sword against a creature larger than you. On a hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 3d6} damage and the creature falls {@condition prone}. In addition, each hostile creature that is larger than you, can see you, and is within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the start of your next turn. The target of the attack has {@b disadvantage} on this saving throw.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

This sword deals an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage to the first target it hits on each turn, as ghostly hands angrily claw at anything within reach.

entries.name: Familiar Apparition
entries: As an action, you can target a humanoid you can see within 60 feet of you, causing a ghostly apparition to emerge from the rift in the blade into a space within 5 feet of you. The apparition resembles the target, but bears wounds and disfigurements suggesting how it might die. It doesn't occupy its space, and other creatures can move through it normally.

When the apparition first emerges, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of it for 1 minute. A creature frightened in this way takes {@damage 2d6} psychic damage at the start of each of its turns if the apparition is within 20 feet of it and it can see the apparition. You can use a bonus action on each of your turns to move the apparition up to 20 feet to a space you can see. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The apparition disappears when the target is no longer frightened of it, or if it's ever more than 60 feet away from you. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
This sword deals an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage to each target it hits, as ghostly hands angrily claw at anything within reach.

entries.name: Lysogenic Alchemy
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to inject the creature with the biological material contained within. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be infected with a disease for 1 minute, during which you can't infect another creature. As an action, you can sink the sword into the corpse of a creature that died while infected and extract a restorative liquid into the detachable pommel or another appropriate container, producing a {@item potion of healing}. Potions produced by the sword lose their potency at the next dawn.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Lysogenic Alchemy

Potions produced by the sword lose their potency at the next dawn.
This weapon has {@b 5 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

While holding this book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells and, if you are a wizard, you can also use this sword as a spellbook.

entries.name: Spellblade
entries: As a bonus action while holding the book, you can form a blade of magical force from its pages, or make the blade disappear. You can use your spellcasting ability in place of your Strength or Dexterity when making attack rolls with it. On a hit, the sword deals force damage instead of its normal damage type.

entries.name: Malleable Magic
entries: When you deal damage to a creature using a spell, you can change the sword's damage type to that of the spell until you cause the blade to disappear or you use a bonus action to change its damage type back to force.

entries.name: Mana Drain
entries: When you hit a spellcasting creature with this sword, you can choose to drain some of its magical power. If the creature has spell slots, it loses its highest unexpended spell slot of 2nd level or lower, and you regain one expended slot of the same level or lower. If it doesn't have spell slots, it loses one use of a spell of your choice of 2nd level or lower, and you regain one spell slot of the same level as that spell or lower. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
While holding this book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells and, if you are a wizard, you can also use this sword as a spellbook.

entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to cause the butterfly wings on the sword to flap. rows: The target and another random hostile creature within 30 feet of you must each succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or fall {@condition prone}.

The target takes {@damage 4d6} bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (GM's discretion) from a falling object, accidental attack, or similar mishap.
You have {@b advantage} on your next attack roll, and the target has {@b disadvantage} on its next attack roll, before the end of your next turn.

entries: This weapon has {@b 30 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. The firearm doesn't have the Ammunition or Reload properties. Instead, it shoots rays of magical energy, dealing force damage instead of its normal damage type, and you must expend 1 charge each time you make an attack with it.

entries.name: Hex-Rays
entries: A few of the crystals in this weapon have been infused with elemental magic. When you make an attack with this weapon, you can choose to empower it with one of the following options. Once an option has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Cold
entries: The attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} cold damage and the target's speed is halved until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Fire
entries: The attack deals an extra {@damage 3d6} fire damage.

items.name: Lightning
entries: The attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} lightning damage and it's made with {@b advantage} if the target is wearing armour made of metal.

items.name: Poison
entries: The attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} poison damage and the target is {@condition poisoned} until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge
entries: As an action, you can slot a gemstone worth 50 gp or more into the firearm, which immediately destroys the gemstone and regains 10 charges. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries: Attacks made with this bow deal an extra {@damage 1d6} lightning damage if the target is more than 30 feet away from you. In addition, attacking at long range doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on your attack rolls made with this bow.

entries.name: Cover Blower
entries: As a bonus action while holding the bow, you can expend 1 charge to supercharge it, in preparation for a devastating shot. The next attack roll you make with the bow before the end of your turn can target a creature through total cover and ignores the effects of the {@spell shield} spell and nonmagical cover thinner than 1 foot, piercing through it to hit the target on the other side. In addition, if the target is more than 30 feet away from you, the attack deals an extra {@damage 4d6} lightning damage instead of {@damage 1d6}.

entries.name: Concussive Blast
entries: As an action while holding this sword, you can expend 1 charge to fire a blast of concussive energy at a point you can see within 30 feet of you. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere centred on that point must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the point and knocked {@condition prone}. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more is also {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Hextech Recharge

entries.name: Point Blank Blast
entries: As a reaction when a creature moves within 5 feet of you, you can expend 1 charge to fire a concentrated blast from this weapon at it. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 30 feet directly away from you and knocked {@condition prone}. If the creature fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it's also {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Maze of the Mind
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using the axe, you can force the target to make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is disoriented for 1 minute and immediately moves up to its speed directly away from you, stopping early if it encounters an obstacle. The first time the disoriented creature tries to move on its turn, it must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature must use its movement to move in a random direction, stopping early if it encounters an obstacle. On a successful save, the creature is no longer disoriented. Once this property of the axe has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Maze of the Mind
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using the axe, you can force the target to make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is disoriented for 1 minute and immediately moves up to its speed directly away from you, stopping early if it encounters an obstacle. The first time the disoriented creature tries to move on its turn, it must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature must use its movement to move in a random direction, stopping early if it encounters an obstacle. On a successful save, the creature is no longer disoriented. Once this property of the axe has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

The Maze of the Mind property can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Searing Winglash
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak the armour's command word, causing the decorative wings on its back to be enveloped in undulating flames for 1 minute. During this time, whenever a hostile creature moves within 10 feet of you for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there, the burning wings lash out at it. The creature must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 1d8} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property of the armour has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Rise from the Ashes
entries: When you drop to 0 hit points, you can choose to have yourself and everything you're wearing and carrying go up in flames and turn into a pile of ash. In this state, you are an object instead of a creature, can't take damage, and don't make death saving throws. If the ashes are scattered, they magically reform in the same space. At the start of your next turn, you regain 15 hit points and emerge from the ashes fully-formed, retaining all of your equipment and item attunements. When this happens, the Searing Winglash property also activates. This happens even if you've already used it that day, and doesn't count against the number of times it can be used, but lasts only until the start of your next turn. Once this property of the armour has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This armour is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you're targeted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you have vulnerability to cold damage. In addition, whenever you take cold damage, you can't use either of the armour's properties until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Ashes to Ashes
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage to undead creatures. In addition, damage from this weapon bypasses the Undead Fortitude trait as if it were radiant damage, and undead creatures reduced to 0 hit points by it are instantly killed.

entries.name: Ashes to Ashes
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage to undead creatures. In addition, damage from this weapon bypasses the Undead Fortitude trait as if it were radiant damage, and undead creatures reduced to 0 hit points by it are instantly killed.

entries.name: Blaze of Glory
entries: As an action, you can cause this weapon to transform into a shaft of pure fire and throw it at a creature within 120 feet of you. Make a ranged weapon attack against that creature. On a hit, the weapon deals its normal damage plus {@damage 3d8} fire damage, and a corona of flames erupts around the target. Each other creature within 15 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d8} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The weapon then turns to ashes, and reforms 1d4 hours later next to you. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d8} fire damage to undead creatures. In addition, damage from this weapon bypasses the Undead Fortitude trait as if it were radiant damage, and undead creatures reduced to 0 hit points by it are instantly killed.

Each other creature within 15 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d8} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The weapon then turns to ashes, and reforms 1d4 hours later next to you. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
This item has {@b 2 charges} and regains {@b 1d2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Kraken's Haze
entries: As an action while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge to produce a cloud of black ink in a 10-foot-radius sphere centred on a point within 30 feet of you. The area of the cloud is heavily obscured, and any creature other than you that enters it for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn. The cloud lasts until the end of your next turn, after which it vanishes, leaving a coating of black ink on any solid surface in its space that lasts for 1 minute.

entries.name: Ink Step
entries: As a bonus action while holding this item, you can expend 1 charge to produce a small puddle of ink at your feet and sink into it, reemerging at an unoccupied space of your choice within 30 feet of you that is within the ink cloud produced by the Kraken's Haze property or on an ink-coated surface it leaves behind after vanishing.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by the {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you are unwilling to part with the item, keeping it within reach at all times. Whenever you become {@condition frightened} or surprised, the item expels a small cloud of ink on your eyes, and you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn.
This item has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@b 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Golden Lining
entries: Due to the comfortable fur that lines the inside of this armour, wearing it during a long rest has no detrimental effects. In addition, when you take nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage while wearing the armour, that damage is reduced by {@damage 1d4}. Once the armour reduces damage in this way, it can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This armour is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you until you're targeted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, you can't remove the armour and have {@b disadvantage} on ability checks made to avoid or escape being {@condition grappled}. In addition, your Constitution modifier is considered to be 0 for the purposes of determining how long you can hold your breath or survive while choking.
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armour.

This bow has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@b 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Serpentine Shot
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you make an attack with the bow, you can expend 1 charge to imbue the arrow with the bow's magic. After the attack hits or misses, the fired arrow transforms into a {@creature poisonous snake}, which appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of the target. The snake shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It's hostile only to the target of the initial attack and doesn't obey commands, always spending each of its turns moving towards and attacking its target. The snake disappears if it drops to 0 hit points, or after 1 minute.

entries.name: Volley of Snakes
entries: As an action while holding this bow, you can shoot an arrow and cause it to split into multiple copies in a 60-foot cone. The arrows then transform into snakes and assemble into a {@creature swarm of poisonous snakes}, which appears in a space of your choice within the cone. The swarm is friendly to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys verbal commands that you issue to it (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action. The swarm disappears if it drops to 0 hit points, or after 1 minute. Once this property of the bow has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
This bow has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@b 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aegis of Agony
entries: As an action while wearing this armour, you can take {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage and gain twice as many temporary hit points, which last for 1 hour. Damage you take from this property ignores temporary hit points and can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you are below half of your hit point maximum when you use this property, you can do so as a bonus action instead of an action, and you gain an extra {@damage 1d6} temporary hit points.

You gain a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armour. While you are below half of your hit point maximum, this bonus increases to a +2. If the armour normally imposes {@b disadvantage} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks or has a Strength requirement, the blood mithral version doesn't.

entries.name: Aegis of Agony
entries: As an action while wearing this armour, you can take {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage and gain twice as many temporary hit points, which last for 1 hour. Damage you take from this property ignores temporary hit points and can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you are below half of your hit point maximum when you use this property, you can do so as a bonus action instead of an action, and you gain an extra {@damage 1d6} temporary hit points.

You gain a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armour. While you are below half of your hit point maximum, this bonus increases to a +3. If the armour normally imposes {@b disadvantage} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks or has a Strength requirement, the blood mithral version doesn't.

entries.name: Aegis of Agony
entries: As an action while wearing this armour, you can take {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage and gain twice as many temporary hit points, which last for 1 hour. Damage you take from this property ignores temporary hit points and can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you are below half of your hit point maximum when you use this property, you can do so as a bonus action instead of an action, and you gain an extra {@damage 1d6} temporary hit points.
You can use this weapon as a spellcasting focus for your cleric and paladin spells.

entries.name: Intimidating Radiance
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} radiant damage to fiends and undead. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon and roll a 6 on the extra damage die, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement: Paladin
entries: If you are a paladin, you can enhance your attunement to this weapon by using it to make an attack that deals 40 or more damage to a hostile fiend or undead in a single hit, proving that you can vanquish great evil. While enhanced, this weapon gains the Ultimate Smite property.

entries.name: EA: Ultimate Smite
entries: Anytime you could use your Divine Smite class feature against a fiend or undead, you can choose to use this property instead. Your attack deals an extra {@damage 3d8} radiant damage to the target, and it must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, holy energy continues to reverberate within the target, and it takes {@damage 1d8} radiant damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
You can use this weapon as a spellcasting focus for your cleric and paladin spells.

entries.name: Intimidating Radiance
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} radiant damage to fiends and undead. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon and roll a 6 on the extra damage die, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement: Paladin
entries: If you are a paladin, you can enhance your attunement to this weapon by using it to make an attack that deals 50 or more damage to a hostile fiend or undead in a single hit, proving that you can vanquish great evil. While enhanced, this weapon gains the Ultimate Smite property.

entries.name: EA: Ultimate Smite
entries: Anytime you could use your Divine Smite class feature against a fiend or undead, you can choose to use this property instead. Your attack deals an extra {@damage 6d8} radiant damage to the target, and it must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, holy energy continues to reverberate within the target, and it takes {@damage 1d8} radiant damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You can use this weapon as a spellcasting focus for your cleric and paladin spells.

entries.name: Intimidating Radiance
entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d6} radiant damage to fiends and undead. When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon and roll a 6 on either of the dice, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn. The creature automatically fails this saving throw if you roll a 6 on both dice.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement: Paladin
entries: If you are a paladin, you can enhance your attunement to this weapon by using it to make an attack that deals 50 or more damage to a hostile fiend or undead in a single hit, proving that you can vanquish great evil. While enhanced, this weapon gains the Ultimate Smite property.

entries.name: EA: Ultimate Smite
entries: Anytime you could use your Divine Smite class feature against a fiend or undead, you can choose to use this property instead. Your attack deals an extra {@damage 6d8} radiant damage to the target, and it must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, holy energy continues to reverberate within the target, and it takes {@damage 1d8} radiant damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
This blade has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spellblade
entries: When you cast a cantrip that requires you to make a single spell attack while holding this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to replace that spell attack with a melee weapon attack using this sword. On a hit, the target takes the weapon's damage and suffers the effects of the cantrip as normal.
This blade has {@b 10 charges} and regains {@b 1d8 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spellblade
entries: When you cast a cantrip that requires you to make a single spell attack while holding this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to replace that spell attack with a melee weapon attack using this sword. On a hit, the target takes the weapon's damage and suffers the effects of the cantrip as normal.

entries.name: Spellblade
entries: When you cast a cantrip that requires you to make a single spell attack while holding this weapon, you can replace that spell attack with a melee weapon attack using this sword. On a hit, the target takes the weapon's damage and suffers the effects of the cantrip as normal.

entries.name: Crystal Seed
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this hammer, you can implant a small piece of crystal within the target, which slowly grows to envelop it. For 1 minute, the target must make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw at the start of each of its turns. On a failed save, the creature's speed is reduced to 0 feet as crystals start to grow around its limbs. If the creature's speed is already 0 feet from this effect, it becomes {@condition restrained} instead, as the crystals begin to envelop the rest of its body. If the creature is already {@condition restrained} by this effect, it becomes {@condition incapacitated} and {@condition blinded} as well, as the crystals form a full casing around it, and the creature has total cover against attacks and other effects originating from outside its crystal prison. After 1 minute, or if the creature succeeds on this saving throw a total of three times, the effect ends and the crystals shatter. Once this property of the hammer has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Crystal Seed
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this hammer, you can implant a small piece of crystal within the target, which slowly grows to envelop it. For 1 minute, the target must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw at the start of each of its turns. On a failed save, the creature's speed is reduced to 0 feet as crystals start to grow around its limbs. If the creature's speed is already 0 feet from this effect, it becomes {@condition restrained} instead, as the crystals begin to envelop the rest of its body. If the creature is already {@condition restrained} by this effect, it becomes {@condition incapacitated} and {@condition blinded} as well, as the crystals form a full casing around it, and the creature has total cover against attacks and other effects originating from outside its crystal prison. After 1 minute, or if the creature succeeds on this saving throw a total of three times, the effect ends and the crystals shatter. Once this property of the hammer has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Crystal Seed
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using this hammer, you can implant a small piece of crystal within the target, which slowly grows to envelop it. For 1 minute, the target must make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw at the start of each of its turns. On a failed save, the creature's speed is reduced to 0 feet as crystals start to grow around its limbs. If the creature's speed is already 0 feet from this effect, it becomes {@condition restrained} instead, as the crystals begin to envelop the rest of its body. If the creature is already {@condition restrained} by this effect, it becomes {@condition incapacitated} and {@condition blinded} as well, as the crystals form a full casing around it, and the creature has total cover against attacks and other effects originating from outside its crystal prison. After 1 minute, or if the creature succeeds on this saving throw a total of three times, the effect ends and the crystals shatter. Once this property of the hammer has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Reconfigure
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can speak its command word to cause its crystalline structure to change, transforming it into one of the following hammer weapons of your choice:

items: A {@item light hammer} with the {@itemProperty F||Finesse} property.
A {@item warhammer} with the {@itemProperty L||Light} property.
A {@item maul} without the {@itemProperty H||Heavy} property.

entries: This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage to the first target it hits on each of your turns. It has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Suffer the Consequences
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose a creature within 30 feet of you that can hear you and expend 1 charge to name an action that it's forbidden from taking. This can be as vague as the Attack or Cast a Spell actions, or a more specific ability such as a breath weapon. If the creature takes the forbidden action before the start of your next turn, you can immediately use your reaction to make an attack against it using the hammer. If the creature is outside of the reach of the hammer but within 30 feet of you, a spectral gavel appears in the air next to it, allowing you to make this attack at a distance before disappearing. The attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} psychic damage on a hit.
This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage to any target it hits. It has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Suffer the Consequences
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose a creature within 60 feet of you that can hear you and expend 1 charge to name an action that it's forbidden from taking. This can be as vague as the Attack or Cast a Spell actions, or a more specific ability such as a breath weapon. If the creature takes the forbidden action before the start of your next turn, you can immediately use your reaction to make an attack against it using the hammer. If the creature is outside of the reach of the hammer but within 60 feet of you, a spectral gavel appears in the air next to it, allowing you to make this attack at a distance before disappearing. The attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} psychic damage on a hit.
This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage to any target it hits. It has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@b 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Suffer the Consequences
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose a creature within 60 feet of you that can hear you and expend 1 charge to name an action that it's forbidden from taking. This can be as vague as the Attack or Cast a Spell actions, or a more specific ability such as a breath weapon. If the creature takes the forbidden action before the start of your next turn, you can immediately use your reaction to make an attack against it using the hammer. If the creature is outside of the reach of the hammer but within 60 feet of you, a spectral gavel appears in the air next to it, allowing you to make this attack at a distance before disappearing. The attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} psychic damage on a hit.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aquatic Predator
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon. In addition, while underwater, the weapon has the Thrown (range 20/60) property, and returns to your hand immediately after you make a ranged attack with it.

entries.name: Hunter's Aura
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to tap into the primal hunger of the shark, becoming more adept at sensing blood and weakness until the start of your next turn. Until then, you have {@b advantage} on Perception checks made to detect creatures within 60 feet of you that don't have all of their hit points, as well as {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with the hammer against them. While you are underwater, the range of this effect extends to 120 feet.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aquatic Predator
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon. In addition, while underwater, the weapon has the Thrown (range 20/60) property, and returns to your hand immediately after you make a ranged attack with it.

entries.name: Hunter's Aura
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to tap into the primal hunger of the shark, becoming more adept at sensing blood and weakness until the start of your next turn. Until then, you have {@b advantage} on Perception checks made to detect creatures within 60 feet of you that don't have all of their hit points, as well as {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with the hammer against them. While you are underwater, the range of this effect extends to 120 feet.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Aquatic Predator
entries: Attacking while underwater doesn't impose {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls you make with this weapon. In addition, while underwater, the weapon has the Thrown (range 20/60) property, and returns to your hand immediately after you make a ranged attack with it.

entries.name: Hunter's Aura
entries: As a bonus action while holding this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to tap into the primal hunger of the shark, becoming more adept at sensing blood and weakness until the start of your next turn. As part of the bonus action taken to activate this property, you can move up to your speed towards a creature within the range of the aura that doesn't have all of its hit points. Until then, you have {@b advantage} on Perception checks made to detect creatures within 60 feet of you that don't have all of their hit points, as well as {@b advantage} on attack rolls made with the hammer against them. While you are underwater, the range of this effect extends to 120 feet.

entries.name: Ice Shot
entries: This weapon doesn't use normal ammunition. Instead of firing bolts, when you make an attack with it, an icicle forms already loaded into the crossbow and ready to fire. At the start of your turn, you can choose to ignore the Loading property of the crossbow until your next turn, taking {@damage 1d6} fire damage as the weapon vents heat into you to form more icicles.

entries.name: Ice Shot
entries: This weapon doesn't use normal ammunition. Instead of firing bolts, when you make an attack with it, an icicle forms already loaded into the crossbow and ready to fire. At the start of your turn, you can choose to ignore the Loading property of the crossbow until your next turn, taking {@damage 1d6} fire damage as the weapon vents heat into you to form more icicles.

entries.name: Deer Head Lights
entries: As an action while holding the crossbow, you can cause the deer's eyes to emit bright light in a 30-foot cone, or turn the light off. When you activate the light, you can choose to have it flair with momentary intensity, forcing each creature in the cone to make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn, and its speed is reduced to 0 feet while blinded in this way. Once the crossbow has been used to blind creatures, it can't be used to do so again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Ice Shot
entries: This weapon doesn't use normal ammunition. Instead of firing bolts, when you make an attack with it, an icicle forms already loaded into the crossbow and ready to fire. At the start of your turn, you can choose to ignore the Loading property of the crossbow until your next turn, taking {@damage 1d6} fire damage as the weapon vents heat into you to form more icicles.

entries.name: Deer Head Lights
entries: As an action while holding the crossbow, you can cause the deer's eyes to emit bright light in a 30-foot cone, or turn the light off. When you activate the light, you can choose to have it flair with momentary intensity, forcing each creature in the cone to make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw.
