On a failed save, a creature is {@condition blinded} for 1 minute, and its speed is reduced to 0 feet while blinded in this way. A creature blinded in this way can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once the crossbow has been used to blind creatures, it can't be used to do so again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Icicle Barrage
entries: At the start of your turn while holding this crossbow, you can choose to take {@damage 2d6} fire damage as the weapon vents even more heat into you. If you do, you can make two extra attacks with the crossbow when you take the Attack action before the end of your next turn. Once this property of the crossbow has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

inherits.reqAttune: by a creature with a Strength score of 16 or higher
entries: {@i Component: astral brain (unique - bonemonger)}

The Lady of the Lake, her arm clad in the purest shimmering samite, once bestowed a blade just like this one unto the rightful ruler of the land, that he might wield it in defence of the downtrodden and oppressed. This replica is just as good as the real thing... but its craftsman seems to have forgotten a tiny little detail.
This sword, which deals bludgeoning damage instead of piercing or slashing damage, deals an extra {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage to the first target it hits on each of your turns.

entries.name: Moderate Executive Power
entries: As the owner of this mighty sword, you command respect and obedience... but the comically large boulder does detract from it a little. While carrying the sword, you have advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made to convince others to perform tasks or favours for you.

magicvariant.name: The Sword out of the Stone

inherits.reqAttune: by a creature with a Strength score of 16 or higher
entries: {@i Component: astral brain (unique - bonemonger)}

The Lady of the Lake, her arm clad in the purest shimmering samite, once bestowed a blade just like this one unto the rightful ruler of the land, that he might wield it in defence of the downtrodden and oppressed. This replica is just as good as the real thing...
This sword deals an extra {@damage 1d6} radiant damage to each target it hits.

entries.name: Supreme Executive Power
entries: As the owner of the mighty sword, you command respect and obedience beyond compare, and no authority is higher than yours. While carrying the sword, you have advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks, Charisma saving throws, and saving throws made against spells of the Enchantment school of magic.

entries.name: Conjure Watery Tart
entries: While holding this sword, you can cast the {@spell conjure elemental} spell (water elemental only). The elemental takes the shape of an elegant woman, and if your concentration on the spell is broken, it doesn't turn hostile toward you and your companions, but instead spends the remainder of the spell's duration following and berating you for perceived misuses of power. Once this property of the sword has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemFluff.name: Dagger of the Deep
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity bone}
A green jewel sits like an eye in the handle of this dagger, giving life to the carved bone cuttlefish that forms its grip.

itemFluff.name: Eyes of the Forgotten
entries: {@i Component: aberration eye}

The barnacle-like yellow eyes that adorn this ring flick to and fro with reptilian suddenness. When you make eye contact with one of them, brief feelings of drowsiness, terror, and nausea wash over you before the eye darts away once more.

itemFluff.name: Lance of the Crab Knight
entries: {@i Component: beast (giant crab) pincer}
The chitin of the giant centenary crab is some of the hardest found in the ocean, formed under the extreme pressure experienced thousands of feet below sea level.

itemFluff.name: Lance of the Crab Knight (Very Rare)
Rapier of the Yellow King
entries: {@i Component: aberration main eye}
The guard of this slender, sickly, yellow blade houses a snake's eye that seems to follow its quarry around the room.

itemFluff.name: Rapier of the Yellow King (Very Rare)
Ring of the Sunken King
entries: {@i Component: aberration tentacle}

This barnacle-encrusted band of rusted metal is always cold and wet to the touch. The way the metal twists into itself has a certain 'tentacle' quality that is only enhanced by the way it adheres to your skin when you don the ring.

itemFluff.name: Runestone of Ocean Currents
entries: {@i Component: elemental volatile mote of water}
An ancient, malachite runestone from the depths of the sunken city, this stone thrums with energy and leaves whichever pocket or pouch it is stored in wet with frothy saltwater.

itemFluff.name: Sceptre of the Sunken King
entries: {@i Component: aberration tentacle}

An ancient nautilus, a scion of the Deep One, wraps the driftwood shaft of this sceptre in its wet, rubbery tentacles. Though this nautilus appears dead and unmoving when dry, its tentacles slowly writhe while submerged in water.

itemFluff.name: Sceptre of the Sunken King (Very Rare)
Breastplate of the Sunken King
entries: {@i Component: aberration chitin}
When donning this armour, you hear the faint rumble of distant thundering waves as it passes over your head.

itemFluff.name: Breastplate of the Sunken King (Very Rare)
Fishfolk Sword
entries: {@i Component: elemental volatile mote of water}

Coastal fishfolk typically repurpose scavenged steel from less fortunate travellers into wicked blades, adorning the hilts with coral and seashells. It is common practice to bless such a blade with water before battle; sometimes this can even attract the Deep One's blessing.

itemFluff.name: Sea Serpent's Tooth
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon teeth}
Carved from the tooth of a giant sea snake, this huge club sports a thriving ecosystem of halophilic moss, barnacles, and seaweed.

itemFluff.name: Dragon Turtle's Claw (Rare)
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (dragon turtle) claws}
Carved from the claw of a giant dragon turtle, this huge club sports a thriving ecosystem of halophilic moss, barnacles, and seaweed.

itemFluff.name: Kraken's Beak (Legendary)
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity (kraken) beak}
Carved from the beak of a giant kraken, this huge club sports a thriving ecosystem of halophilic moss, barnacles, and seaweed.

itemFluff.name: Bloodwax
entries: {@i Component: fiend fat}

Even unlit, this candle emits an overpowering scent of sulphur and rust, which only grows more intense as the crimson wax melts under a steady flame. Its magical properties far outweigh its foul odour, however, and even the steep price one must pay in blood to use it.

itemFluff.name: Blood Minister Syringe
entries: {@i Component: phial of beast blood}

Made of silver and glass, this peculiar apparatus magically refills with blood. A small dial around the plunger depicts carved images of beasts. Legend says blood from the sacred animal spirits of each of these species was extracted in the crafting of this macabre, performance-enhancing syringe.

itemFluff.name: Ceremonial Mask
entries: {@i Component: undead undying flesh}
This smiling silver mask is lined on its back side with stained, purple velvet. It is typically donned by a cult's leader, allowing them the ability to communicate with the dark powers they worship.

itemFluff.name: Cultist Robes
entries: {@i Component: aberration hide}

Thick, purple cloth; occult, gold embroidery; and deep pockets for hiding sacrificial daggers; the cultist's robes are everything a disenfranchised, young acolyte could hope for. As one rises through the cult's ranks, one's embroidery gets more intricate and the enchantments more powerful.

itemFluff.name: Dark Candle
entries: {@i Component: undead rancid fat}

In shadowy meeting rooms beneath respectable establishments, in crypts and catacombs around the tombs of the dead, and lining hallways filled with the bodies of adventurers, these tentacle-like purple-black candles burn with a dim malice.

itemFluff.name: Dead Man's Chalice
entries: {@i Component: undead ethereal ichor}

The face of the Deep One adorns this mottled bronze chalice, which is filled with a purple liquid that can't be spilled. Its eyes come ablaze as one drinks from its briny depths, matching the bright amber jewels that adorn its stem. Used by pirate captains during parleys, the dead man's chalice is a guarantee against foul play.

itemFluff.name: Demented Cultist's Scimitar
entries: {@i Component: pouch of aberration teeth}
This obsidian blade reflects light with an opalescent sheen. The hilt is made of dead driftwood bound with ragged fabric.

itemFluff.name: Infested Cultist's Skull
entries: {@i Component: humanoid (cultist) bone}
This humanoid skull is infested by a parasitic purple plant from an unknown plane. The longer you stare, the more you could swear that the petals sway in response to an unseen wind.

itemFluff.name: Juvenile Spawn Elixir
entries: {@i Component: aberration egg}
The pale, fleshy worm in this bottle moves sinuously through its sickly-green contents, turning to look at the nearest living creature with its unnerving, alien eyes.

itemFluff.name: Runewarding Ring
entries: {@i Component: phial of fiend blood}
This stone ring is marked with the brand of a mysterious cult dedicated to the worship of infection and decay. Fashioned of cracked, grey stone, glowing red veins furrow its craggy surface.

itemFluff.name: Thurible of the Elder Deacon
entries: {@i Component: aberration fat}
This solid steel incense dispenser dangles at the end of a black metal chain. Heady perfume follows in the wake of the bearer of this weighty weapon.

itemFluff.name: Twinspawn Maulhammer
entries: {@i Component: aberration bone}
Made of the fossilised remains of a Deep One's spawn, the holes within this double-headed maul sparkle with illusory motes of light.

itemFluff.name: Andvaranaut
entries: {@i Component: celestial bone}

Two legends follow this ring. The first tells of humble folk using the ring to lead modest lives free from discomfort and hardship. The second tells of mighty leaders brought low by their own greed. Which is true—if either—is anyone's guess.

itemFluff.name: Allfather's Eye
entries: {@i Component: humanoid (stormsinger) eye}

When a stormsinger desires greater wisdom, they sacrifice their own eye and seek the Allfather's blessing. If their song is answered, the eye doesn't rot, and can be crafted into a ring or other adornment known as an allfather's eye.

itemFluff.name: Branch of the Worldtree
entries: {@i Component: plant bark}

Whether or not this branch truly came from the mythical Worldtree is unknown. However, it exudes potent, magical energy capable of manipulating plants and of great feats of healing. As it is used to channel a spell, the tips of its branches glow green-gold and the air is perfumed with the scent of mountain pines.

itemFluff.name: Drakenhorn
entries: {@i Component: dragon horn}
Sculpted in the shape of a dragon's head, this elaborate brass-and-gold horn sparks small flames whenever it is blown.

itemFluff.name: Gullinbursti Tusks
entries: {@i Component: beast (giant boar) tusk}

Commonly found as pairs, these rough, gold-and-ivory daggers are said to have been forged using leftover materials from the forging of Gullinbursti, the great Golden Boar. A restless aura suffuses these blades, from its golden-haired hilt to its ivory points.

itemFluff.name: Isfjell, Ring of Icebergs
entries: {@i Component: elemental (ice mephit) core of water}

Crafted in the Frozen Forge, this ring is capable of entombing its wearer in a protective iceberg, or isfjell as it is known in the Giant tongue. Such rings are found in all sizes; a ring crafted for a giant might be worn as a torc by Loic Giantslayer, or as a belt by Mitji the Tiny. This malleability arises thanks to the wisps of water vapour that run between the ring's jagged ends and its geodesic jewel.

itemFluff.name: Skinwalker Staff
entries: {@i Component: beast pelt}
Used in shamanistic rituals to commune with wildlife, this staff is adorned with animal skulls and bones.

itemFluff.name: Snow Wolf Cowl
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity (winter wolf) pelt}

Few warriors can claim to have single-handedly bested a full-grown snow wolf. Those that do can have the head of such a monster enchanted by a clan shaman, granting them the power to transform into the beast. Such cowls are prized trophies amongst the frozen Norse clans, symbols of might and status.

itemFluff.name: Spear of the Stormsinger
entries: {@i Component: dragon (blue or bronze) breath sac}

Stormsingers—disciples of the Allfather—are said to be able to channel the wrath of storms into holy weapons. If one of their sacred sites is threatened, the air is filled with a berserker's chant as they throw themselves into combat with a zealousness inspired by the prospect of entry into the Allfather's halls.

itemFluff.name: Worldshaper
entries: {@i Component: humanoid (dwarf) heart}

When gods still walked the world, dwarven smiths forged an axe to rival the powers of deities. With it, they carved a new world of rock; canyons sheared under its mighty blows, mountains were beheaded, and great stone caverns were founded where only impenetrable rock had stood before. Incensed by the dwarves' hubris, the gods cursed the axe and extracted promises from all dwarvenkind that they would never again reach for the power of the gods.

itemFluff.name: Aldrnari Blade
entries: {@i Component: dragon (red, gold, or brass) breath sac}

This exceptionally well-crafted steel blade bears four runes at the end of its length. These runes represent the wielder's ideals and can be found burned into the skin of its true owner. In northern clans, when such a weapon is passed from parent to child, it is accompanied by a branding ritual, bonding the blade to its new owner. Entire lineages can be identified by the brands that mark their skin.

itemFluff.name: Frostblessed
entries: {@i Component: dragon (white or silver) breath sac}

The stories say that the first and true frostblessed sword—for many enchanters have tried to recreate its legendary effects—was a blessing from the Allfather himself. When the blade of a great warrior shattered as he fought a stampeding horde of frost giants, the Allfather looked fondly upon the hero's plight. The Allfather encased the blade in a razor-sharp shard of unmelting ice capable of chilling its foes and summoning fallen warriors from the Allfather's halls.

itemFluff.name: Frostrune Axe
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (white or silver) scales}

The Frozen Forge is the site of frost giants' cold forging secrets, a manufactory in the heart of a frigid volcano. As they hammer these ingots, they sing the Song of Steel, an ancient melody that prevents the cold, brittle metal from shearing beneath their mighty blows. By pressing the rune-engraved metal into one's skin, one creates a nigh-unbreakable bond with the Frozen Forge.

itemFluff.name: Suneater Axe
entries: {@i Component: pouch of celestial teeth}

When the mighty wolf Fenrir threatened the world, the dwarves fashioned an impossibly strong thread with which to keep him bound. This thread, known as Gleipnir, was crafted from the sound of a cat's footfall, the roots of a mountain, and the breath of a fish, amongst other things. With the leftover material, the Weapons of Binding were made. The suneater axe is one such weapon, a depiction of the wolf Sköll, who the dwarves believe will one day devour the sun.

itemFluff.name: Winter's End
entries: {@i Component: bundle of plant roots}
Woven from the first branches to bloom at the end of winter, this living bow is strung with a length of snow-spider's silk.

itemFluff.name: Blast & Slash
entries: {@i Component: construct gears}

itemFluff.name: Clockwork Wyvern
entries: {@i Component: construct lifespark}

itemFluff.name: Heavy Mobile Drill
entries: {@i Component: elemental core of earth}

itemFluff.name: Owlsight Goggles
entries: {@i Component: beast (giant owl) eye}

"Don't go out at night or the giant owls will get ya," has been said by more than one exhausted rakin parent in a mostly-unsuccessful attempt to curb their offspring's exploratory and mischievous tendencies. Unfortunately for rakin, this isn't an exaggeration: giant owls are the number one predators of forest-dwelling tanukin. The naming of this acuity-enhancing eyewear is undoubtedly a nod at this cruel twist of nature by the infamous and ungracious artificer, L'Arsène Upin.

itemFluff.name: Privateer Coat
entries: {@i Component: beast (lion) hair}

itemFluff.name: Scorching Fists
entries: {@i Component: phial of construct oil}

Made of ceramic and metal plating, these gloves allow their wearer to smelt, weld, and otherwise manipulate metal without fear of burning oneself. In underground battle pits, these gloves see more unconventional uses: with the limiters removed, the glove's knuckle-exhausts can form blades of devastating flame.

itemFluff.name: Sir Wrenchinald the Third
entries: {@i Component: fey psyche}

itemFluff.name: Technomancer Staff
entries: {@i Component: elemental core of air}

itemFluff.name: B.O.N.K.
entries: {@i Component: construct gears}

After the failed launch of the B.O.N.K. Mk. 1, which had a history of blowing the user's hands off or melting their face, genius artificer Anton Spark unveiled its successor. The Mk. 2 is bigger, faster, and, allegedly, "safer".

itemFluff.name: Shadowstalker Garb
entries: {@i Component: construct instructions}

This mechanical garb was tailor-made to allow assassins to glide over their target without being heard, before eliminating them with a swift, swooping attack. Anton Spark, the famous tinkerer, modelled this design after a snowy owl silently snatched a doughnut from his hand.

itemFluff.name: Sparkblade
entries: {@i Component: dragon (blue or bronze) breath sac}
As arcanodynamos have become more common, there has been an explosion in devices making use of this magic-generating technology. From lanterns to rollerskates, and now: sparkblades.

itemFluff.name: Bracers of the Jaguar Warrior
entries: {@i Component: beast (panther) pelt}

These golden bracers, adorned with layers of spotted fur and three glowing green gems, are worn by the fearsome Jaguar Warriors. Tasked with defending the Sun God's temple against intruders and wild animals, these bracers imbue these warriors with the ability to summon jaguar spirits from the fey realms.

itemFluff.name: Bracers of the Jaguar Warrior (Very Rare)
Helmet of the Dino Slayer
entries: {@i Component: pouch of beast (dinosaur) scales}

This helmet is made from various dinosaur parts by an expert craftsperson. It offers protection against GV|DMGerwise deadly blows from creatures larger than you. Wearing such a helmet is proof of superior hunting and survival skills in the deadly jungle.

itemFluff.name: Helmet of the Dino Slayer (Very Rare)
Jade Skull of the Sun King
entries: {@i Component: pouch of celestial (couatl) feathers}

This jade skull wears the crown of a fallen Sun King. Legend says that each king had to prove themselves worthy of the blessing of a couatl so that they may craft their own crown from its gifted feathers. The stories also tell of the power stored in these precious artefacts, and of the hubris of thieves. Those for whom the crowns were not intended are subject to bestial curses should they try to don these royal raiments.

itemFluff.name: Jawbone Machete
entries: {@i Component: pouch of beast (dinosaur) teeth}

A tool for travel and warfare, this machete possesses a homemade quality, reminiscent of the deepest parts of the jungle. Rough-cut bone, teeth, and feathers are bonded with tree resin and magic into a versatile instrument, rarely far from the side of its creator.

itemFluff.name: Leeching Slug
entries: {@i Component: phial of undead congealed blood}

This slug is a popular weapon amongst jungle tribes. Easy to grow, and with very poor mobility, they don't pose a great threat unless you fall head-first into an infested puddle. They can easily be used as projectiles for blowguns or slings and, with some tinkering, can be affixed to arrows and bolts. Despite the low damage the leeches inflict, their numbing saliva is very potent, slowing any creature to which it attaches.

itemFluff.name: Mace of the Torchbearer
entries: {@i Component: elemental volatile mote of fire}

To gain their full potency, these gold-plated granite weapons must be blessed by a Sun Priest for three nights and three days over the summer solstice. The Torchbearers, holy warriors of the Sun God, develop a zealous compulsion to spread their diety's light the longer they wield such a weapon.

itemFluff.name: Overgrown Barkshield
entries: {@i Component: plant bark}
This crude shield made of bark and wood teems with life as if it were still part of a tree. The smell of sap and various fragrant flowers perfumes the air around it.

itemFluff.name: Scorching Wrath
entries: {@i Component: pouch of monstrosity (remorhaz) teeth}
Within this sharp-edged bone dagger lies an inert lump of indigo obsidian. In the heat of battle, the obsidian erupts into a continual flow of molten stone to which you are impervious.

itemFluff.name: Venus Fool's Trap
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant leaves}

Brutal and unnatural magic can be used to transform a living plant creature into a garment. Made from a shapeshifting species of carnivorous plants, this red and green cloak pulses, moving around your body of its own accord. Yet the metamorphosis is far from perfect; this cloak constantly hungers for flesh.

itemFluff.name: Ceremonial Macuahuitl
entries: {@i Component: phial of humanoid blood}

Unlike the traditional, functional macuahuitls, this exceptionally heavy specimen replaces wood and bone with gold and jade. Used for ritual sacrifice, this weapon retains the magic bestowed by years of appeasing the Sun God.

itemFluff.name: Arm'onica
entries: {@i Component: phial of humanoid blood}

Pipes 'Fourarms' McGruff: infamous bardtender, caring cat-lover, and middling musician. Renowned for serving customers, petting his cat, and playing the harmonica simultaneously, his world was turned upside down in an unfortunate mimic-related piano tuning accident. Fortunately, a young Anton Spark was quick to Pipes' rescue, developing a tool capable of calming rowdy clients and ousting tricksy mimics. Inspired by local folk tales of a ridiculous, six-armed, top-hat-wearing creature, this prosthesis is known as the "Arm'onica".

itemFluff.name: Eye of the Planesrunner
entries: {@i Component: humanoid (spellcaster) eye}
This crystal sphere of shifting scenes depicts the landscape of the plane it last visited. An iridescent lattice borders the eye, developing in complexity the more it sees of the planes of existence.

itemFluff.name: Flesh Symbiont, Hand
entries: {@i Component: pouch of humanoid teeth}

Crafted by Thumblemore the wizard, this cursed lump of dormant flesh needs a host to emerge from its slumber. It slowly attaches itself to the stump of a creature's lost hand, shifting hue and morphing its shape to represent the lost limb.

itemFluff.name: Flesh Symbiont, Heart
entries: {@i Component: humanoid heart}
This symbiont is the abominable side product of a crazed necromancer's arcane experimentation. Brimming with eyes and teeth, this beating lump of flesh vaguely resembles a humanoid heart.

itemFluff.name: Mechanical Tail
entries: {@i Component: phial of construct blood}

This item is another creation of the eccentric Anton Spark, tinkerer extraordinaire. This mechanical tail is powered by a magic-infused steam core. Any creature can wear it on its back and, once attuned, use it as if it was born with a tail.

itemFluff.name: Ornate Ceramic Prosthesis
entries: {@i Component: construct (clay golem) flesh}

Developed by a mysterious puppeteer, the painted patterns that decorate the ceramic surface of this appendage seem to shift from day to day, and vary in texture and hue from one individual to another.

itemFluff.name: Phantom Limb
entries: {@i Component: undead (ghost) ethereal ichor}

This nebulous, floating cluster of green-blue light can take the form of a hand, arm, foot, leg, or similar absent appendage. Despite its spectral appearance, the phantom limb is quite solid—a fact that becomes painfully evident to unfortunate creatures on the receiving end of its strikes.

itemFluff.name: Spiritual Armblade
entries: {@i Component: celestial hair}
These magical prostheses were gifted to the warrior-servants of the divine in recompense for limbs lost in battle.

itemFluff.name: Tail of the Mermalia
entries: {@i Component: beast fin}

When the rakin ousted Dr. Stein from his tower, he had only one means of escape: swim. His last act in the battle was to sever his own legs, attune to the tail, flop into a well, and slink out into the sea, tail between his... well. He didn't have legs anymore.

itemFluff.name: Wings of Prey
entries: {@i Component: pouch of beast (giant eagle) feathers}

Bestowed upon defenders of city walls and mountain rangers alike, these wings provide great mobility, as well as packing a secret weapon. The appearance of the wings varies from one individual to another, shifting in colour to mirror their soul.

itemFluff.name: Angry Mob Starter Pack
entries: {@i Component: fey egg}
With safety pitchforks and protest signs strapped to the back, this plain leather satchel's unassuming exterior hides a secret within: it houses an illusory mob.

itemFluff.name: Beast Serum
entries: {@i Component: phial of humanoid (shapechanger) blood}

Doctor Jake Kill advertises his proprietary blend of bull's pituitary and caffeine as a "sure fire way to lose friends and alienate people, especially if those people are a swarm of marauding goblins." Oddly enough, he seems more than a little keen to get rid of this particular concoction...

itemFluff.name: Garlycanthrope
entries: {@i Component: plant poison gland}

Originally commissioned by an infamous vampire huntress, Dr. Francis N. Stein's garlycanthropes have proved themselves the 'must-have commodity of the season' for monster hunters everywhere. Their ability to detect undead and shapechangers has saved many potential victims from the jaws of a mimic, or from walking into a vampire's lair.

itemFluff.name: Gentleman's Umbershield
entries: {@i Component: aberration hide}
Rain, arrows, or maces, this wand can deflect them all. Disguised as an oiled blue silk umbrella, as any gentleman might carry, this magical implement gives you a heightened sense of danger.

itemFluff.name: Graveshredder
entries: {@i Component: pouch of undead teeth}

Morticai Ignaceous Fiéd dreamed of superstardom. Unfortunately, Mort I. Fiéd's father had different plans. Namely: digging graves, filling the graves, and watching the graves to make sure they didn't undig themselves. And so, Mort cobbled together a lute from old grave-digging shovels, channelled his teen spirit into angst-filled melodies, and surprised himself by animating the whole graveyard. Thus was formed the travelling six-piece musical act: "Avenged Sixfold".

itemFluff.name: Undertaker's Cloak
entries: {@i Component: undead (mummy) rancid fat}

Mort I. Fiéd had learned one thing whilst under his father's tutelage; in this world, corpses don't always stay corpses. When the dead start rising, you want someone to have your back. Someone... or something.

itemFluff.name: Glimmerbolt
entries: {@i Component: celestial hair}

Another weapon from Sister Victoria's arsenal, this simple crossbow has been blessed by the gods. At your command, the slack string of the crossbow glows bright gold and snaps into its catch; a glimmering quarrel of brilliant, radiant energy coalesces in place.

itemFluff.name: Palisade
entries: {@i Component: construct plating}

This polearm looks to have been shaped from the wrought iron of a graveyard's fence. Legend says the fence was magically enchanted by a wandering cleric, Sister Victoria, so that it would keep incarcerated the walking dead.

itemFluff.name: Quicksilver Blade
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (silver) scales}
Made of true quicksilver—an alloy of mithril, mercury, and silver—this weapon's blade is in constant, fluid motion.

itemFluff.name: Sawbones' Bonesaw
entries: {@i Component: giant tooth}

Speckled with rust and blood, this magical saw has a wickedly keen, serrated edge. It is said to be the instrument of choice of back alley doctors and black market suppliers. Amputation operations can be conducted almost instantaneously; plenty of time before the town guard started sticking their nose in.

itemFluff.name: Arcanomagnetic Rings
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (magnetite) scales}

itemFluff.name: Leaden Manacles
entries: {@i Component: giant nail}

itemFluff.name: Lockshiv
entries: {@i Component: ooze vesicle}

This bladed syringe holds a lead-heavy concoction that inhibits a creature's ability to interact with the Weave. With intricate mechanics, the injection mechanism can be pre-loaded, allowing the weapon to release its magic-inhibiting effects when thrown.

itemFluff.name: Magehunter Bulwark
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity (sphinx) bone}

Like a leaden wall, Spellbreakers would encircle evil magic users, sending spells ricocheting up into the night sky. Eventually, when the enemy's magic was spent, these red and black shields would thrust forward, hammering their foe to the ground where they could be shackled. Though few are now found, these magehunter bulwarks can be a thorn in the side of even the mightiest lich.

itemFluff.name: Speak No Evil
entries: {@i Component: giant tooth}
This headgear - a gag consisting of two leaden 'hands' connected by thick leather straps - is designed to make speech impossible.

itemFluff.name: Weave Stripper
entries: {@i Component: aberration tentacle}

Part trap, part torture device, part magic removal mechanism, the weave stripper is a gold-banded, lead-lined box that houses a portal to a remote, antimagical corner of the Abyss. The denizens of this void feed on the Weave, forcibly removing it from the creatures around which it is woven.

itemFluff.name: Lichslayer
entries: {@i Component: undead (lich) undying heart}

The faces of trapped souls can be seen floating beneath the polished surface of this deep, inky glass weapon. The secrets of fashioning soulveil, the faceted material from which these weapons are made, predate even the Spellbreakers. Before wielding such a weapon against a magic user, they must have first been tried by a Spellbreaker Council and found guilty of the most dire crimes. Death by this weapon is absolute, a final curtain.

itemFluff.name: Magehunter Blade
entries: {@i Component: pouch of monstrosity (sphinx) claws}

Made of a Weave-intersecting alloy, these armaments were the go-to weapon for the mage-hunting Order of the Spellbreakers. Fanatical elements within the order have recently taken power, bringing with them an abhorrence for all things arcane. Having largely destroyed their magical arsenal, such weapons have become quite rare and are only wielded by the few who cling to the old ways.

itemFluff.name: Plate of the Magehunter
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity (sphinx) pelt}
Made of a dark grey, lead-rich metal, this armour seems especially dull around magical sources of light.

itemFluff.name: Voidshot
entries: {@i Component: aberration (beholder) main eye}
This jagged black, glass arrowhead drinks in light and seems lit as if with an unholy nimbus.

itemFluff.name: Coralborn Ring
entries: {@i Component: beast chitin}
This coralborn-crafted trinket is often gifted to visiting dignitaries to protect them during journeys through the abrasive subterranean coral tunnels.

itemFluff.name: Coral Wand
entries: {@i Component: beast poison gland}

Such wands often take months to fashion, requiring the formation of an entire coral ecosystem. From molluscs to cnidarians and seaweeds, the inhabitants of such an item share one common theme—they all carry a deadly toxicity.

itemFluff.name: Hat of the Hermit Hag
entries: {@i Component: fey (hag) brain}

Though this headpiece may easily be mistaken for an average conch, its external dimensions bely its roomy interior; it is much bigger on the inside. Hermit hags love to decorate their hats with trophies and trinkets traded, tricked, or otherwise taken from unfortunate adventurers.

itemFluff.name: Hat of the Hermit Hag (Very Rare)
Ghost Cutlass
entries: {@i Component: undead (ghost) ethereal ichor}

A curved blade of ethereal green smoke rises from its barnacle-encrusted hilt, swimming with ghastly, ghostly visages. Legend says these faces belong to those who fell while wielding the blade, a part of their soul trapped forever in its decrepit metal.

itemFluff.name: Mimic Ship-in-a-Bottle
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity (mimic) egg}

Each time this ship-in-a-bottle is observed, something is slightly different. One time, it might have three masts. Another time, the figurehead might have changed from dragon to narwhal, or the rigging has become a light shade of mauve. The only constants are the cannons, its malevolent 'grin', and the tentacles. This ship is, in fact, a baby mimic that hasn't quite mastered the art of 'mimicking'.

itemFluff.name: Shrimp Pistol
entries: {@i Component: pouch of aberration claws}
A biomantic fusion of crustacean and firearm, this magic weapon thrums with stored energy, ready to release a devastating strike like its pistol shrimp namesake.

itemFluff.name: Sunken Anchor
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity antler}
Covered in flakes of blue-green bronze and barnacles, this sturdy implement looks like it's seen better days.

itemFluff.name: Trident of the Electric Eel
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (blue) teeth}
Imbued with the natural lightning-based magic of the eelfolk, this brass-headed, magical trident is enchanted to never succumb to the corrosive nature of the briny sea.

itemFluff.name: Trident of the Electric Eel (Very Rare)
Urchin Flail
entries: {@i Component: beast stinger}
A sphere of dark purple bristling with needle-sharp spines is connected to a driftwood shaft by a rusted chain.

itemFluff.name: Scythe of the Headless Seahorseman
entries: {@i Component: beast (giant sea horse) fin}
This rusted scythe, with its driftwood handle that supports a plethora of sea life, is surprisingly light and hydrodynamic.

itemFluff.name: Scythe of the Headless Seahorseman (Very Rare)
Allure
entries: {@i Component: fey eye}
As the saying goes: "Lights on the surface serve a purpose. Lights in the deep lure then reap." This magical headpiece, while not the most attractive, has a certain allure.

itemFluff.name: Coralborn Harp
entries: {@i Component: fey hair}

Coralborn have two genres of music: diurnal and nocturnal. Diurnal celebrates the reef's colour, community, and biodiversity; it is often uplifting and joyful. Nocturnal illustrates the hunt, when sharks prowl, and the millions of tiny medusozoa that make up the coral feed. Though this harp can play any type of music, the time of day can accentuate its magical capabilities.

itemFluff.name: Cloak of the Cnidaran
entries: {@i Component: fey (sea hag) skin}

Made of magical cnidaran shimmerweave, this cloak shifts its hue with the emotions of its attuned wearer. The Deep King's court jester used to wear one such raiment, making his acrobatic and charismatic displays all the more entertaining.

itemFluff.name: Hammerhead Knight's Legacy
entries: While attuned to the {@item Helmet of the Hammerhead Knight (Rare)|LALoTT|helmet of the hammerhead knight} and {@item Sharktooth|LALoTT|sharktooth}, you gain advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects you can see or smell, and you can't be surprised. In addition, you can use your action to grow gills and morph your legs into a tail, or to reverse this transformation. While morphed, you gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, your walking speed can't exceed 5 feet, and you can breathe both air and water.

itemFluff.name: Helmet of the Hammerhead Knight
entries: {@i Component: pouch of beast (giant shark) scales}

A legend of the seven seas spoken of only in hushed tones, the Hammerhead Knight is said to be a strange hybrid of man and shark. Lingering mists of blood in the water are often interpreted as signs of his passage, and though tales invariably describe him vanquishing pirates and scoundrels, most prefer to avoid him... just in case.

itemFluff.name: Flumfpot
entries: {@i Component: aberration tentacle}

Flumfpots are usually found hovering around a corpse. If you can't see the corpse, then it's almost definitely because it's been so long the corpse has disintegrated into dust. This is because a flumfpot is magically enchanted to always follow the last creature whose lips it touched.

itemFluff.name: Tidecutter
entries: {@i Component: elemental core of water}

With the sound of waves crashing violently against a rocky cliff, the sword's blade rushes forth like the tide, inevitable and unstoppable. Not even tempered steel can resist its edge, which slices through obstacles like a hot knife through melted butter.

itemFluff.name: Coral Crusher
entries: {@i Component: beast chitin}

Through biomantic infusions, the coral that comprises this weapon can grow rapidly, quickly replenishing itself under the right conditions. Coralborn have mixed feelings towards such magic weapons. Some believe that it is sacrilege to damage a reef, while others argue that the foes these weapons shred are prevented from doing any harm to anything, including reefs. The jury is still out.
These heavy weapons, with their fragile, spiky exteriors, send splinters of coral flying, hurting all creatures within close proximity. Safety glasses are recommended.

itemFluff.name: Crosstacean
entries: {@i Component: beast (giant crab) pincer}
The longevity of lobsters is well known. Using biomancy, this phenomenon can be reversed, resulting in a weapon that can magically age and necrose a target.

itemFluff.name: Sharktooth
entries: {@i Component: pouch of beast (giant shark) teeth}
Fashioned especially for the Hammerhead Knight, this blade caused its victims to bleed, allowing the knight, with its superior sense of smell, to track a would-be assassin back to its lair.

itemFluff.name: Seashell Weapon
entries: {@i Component: beast fin}
Fashioned from the pearlescent bloodshells of the Scarlet Reef, this weapon of the coralborn is enchanted to be especially hydrodynamic underwater.

itemFluff.name: Flutterby Rod
entries: {@i Component: pouch of fey (pixie) dust}

Within the sparkling crystal ball atop this gnarled thornwood rod is a living diorama of fey life. Fairies flit from toadstool to petal, tentacled chipmunks pluck nuts from the ends of spindly branches, and shimmering butterflies sup at dew drops sheltered under multihued leaves. With a flourish, a wielder can send such a butterfly streaking towards a target.

itemFluff.name: Lilypad Sunhat
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant leaves}

itemFluff.name: Ring of Birdseye Maple
entries: {@i Component: bundle of plant (awakened tree) roots}
Pale green, eye-like tourmalines have been encapsulated in maple roots sung by seelie fey into pleasing knots and braids.

itemFluff.name: Scroll of Fungi
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant hyphae}

From morel to flycap, pixie's parasol to displacer's digestif, this magical vellum parchment sprouts an assortment of new mushrooms with each new dawn. Its true power, however, is in summoning magic mushrooms. To those without knowledge of the required languages, the letters on the scroll shift and move, seemingly without meaning.

itemFluff.name: Waspberry Jam
entries: {@i Component: phial of plant sap}

itemFluff.name: Xyxlwood Arrow
entries: {@i Component: bundle of plant roots}

itemFluff.name: Rootbound Multiweapon
entries: {@i Component: bundle of plant roots}

The Plane of Fey is an innately magical domain teeming with flora that would enthuse even the most elitist horticulturist. In the aftermath of a battle, once all the useful knick knacks have been salvaged, the plants move in to feast on the rich, blood-soaked earth, sometimes waiting only a matter of days. Seeking roots can bind up broken weapons into clumps that, when properly harvested, can shift into any configuration.

itemFluff.name: Thornbow
entries: {@i Component: plant bark}

Unseelie fey—those with a love for the cold and ugly facets of the world—cultivate the malevolent syklthorn, a green-brown creeper with bright red thorns. Syklthorn is a carnivorous plant that snatches creatures who venture too close before eviscerating them with its bladelike thorns. Weapons made from this plant are just as bloodthirsty.

itemFluff.name: Xyxlbark Armour
entries: {@i Component: plant bark}

Armour made from the cured bark of the xyxl tree is stronger and lighter than steel and possesses the ability to nourish its wearer. The source of the xyxl tree's immense durability is the mithral particles it incorporates into a rigid lattice throughout its wood. The art of curing the slow-growing xyxl bark is known to but a few denizens of the Plane of Fey to which it is native.

itemFluff.name: Xyxlwood Sporeshiv
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant spores}

In the glimmering Plane of Fey, xyxl trees have one nemesis: the witherblight fungus. The hyphae creep through the trees' vascular systems, before blooming in one fungal explosion that turns the entire plant into a glowing, orange fruiting body. Clever sylvan craftspeople have developed a means of enchanting xyxlwood weapons to host this fungus and inject the necrotic spores under a pierced foe's flesh.

itemFluff.name: Sins
entries: Sins are a form of "item modification", a modular way of adding to the properties an item possesses. In return for completing a sinful task appointed by a fiend, a creature can attune to—or enhance their attunement to—a magic item, which gains a powerful new property.

entries.name: The Task
entries: A fiend's ultimate goal with a task is to form a contract with a player character; they give the character powers and request that a specific task be performed within a certain time limit (GM's choice). If the task is completed, the item retains the property for the duration of the character's attunement to it. If the task isn't completed within the time limit (including being failed outright), the character's soul is forfeit, and the GM may choose what infernal outcome awaits the character. The tasks are always secret; if the recipient of the task willingly or unwillingly divulges information about its task to any other creature, the creature automatically fails the task. The tasks accompanying each sin are just suggestions—feel free to come up with your own tasks.

entries.name: Rarity & Power
entries: The sins scale using rarity, with more powerful boons being granted at higher rarities. The greater the item's rarity (and therefore its power), the more extreme and unpleasant the task should be; the fiend wants the character to fail! In general, it is advisable to match the sin rarity to the item rarity.

entries.name: Optional or Enhanced Attunement
entries: If the item wouldn't usually require attunement, then the addition of this item modification gives the item the "optional attunement" property; a character who completes the assigned task immediately attunes to the item, which gains a new property (usually named after the sin in question). If the item already requires attunement, then a creature that accepts the devil's task can't willingly unattuned from the item for the duration of the task. When the task is completed, the item becomes enhanced with the new property.

Either way, the item's new property persists for as long as the character remains attuned to the item; if the character loses attunement to the item for any reason, it must accept and complete a new task to regain the property.

itemFluff.name: Abyssal Bane
entries: {@i Component: fiend (demon) bone}

Fashioned of bone, stone, and demon eyes, abyssal weapons are gestated by unholy bonesmiths within the infernal depths of the Hells. Fortified by magic to prevent the brittle materials from shattering, such weapons are carved with the true name of the demon whose eyes were 'donated' to its conception.

itemFluff.name: Blood Drinker
entries: {@i Component: fiend (pitcrow{@sup *}) beak}

The blade of this magic weapon is fashioned from the beak of a pitcrow, a nomad of the Lower Planes that feasts on the flesh and blood of fallen and wounded fiends. This polearm is able to pierce the flesh of wounded foes and absorb their blood, transferring the life force to its wielder.
{@note {@sup *}See Throne of the Devourer in Motes of the Divine.}

itemFluff.name: Bloodshot
entries: {@i Component: phial of celestial blood}

Ever parched, this sinister firearm greedily drinks any lifeblood it's offered, and an otherworldly mechanism inside it converts it into deadly shards, which it fires instead of bullets. The slightest trickle of crimson is enough for the pupils on this hellish weapon to dilate and for its eyes to appear wild and bloodshot.

itemFluff.name: Demonbane Armour
entries: {@i Component: fiend (demon) bone}

Hewn from a deformed demon's skull, this armour allows its bearer to rival the resistances of fiends. Energy is drawn in through its mouth and channelled to the eyes, where it is stored until unleashed in an elemental rebuke.

itemFluff.name: Demonic Siege Bow
entries: {@i Component: fiend (demon) horn}

What this weapon lacks in manoeuvrability, it makes up for in power. Wrought from the horns, bones, and sinew of demons, this weapon holds malice in every fibre. With its very own bracing mechanism, a user can free up a hand to fire using both strings at once.

itemFluff.name: Hellscape Chatterbane
entries: {@i Component: pouch of fiend teeth}

When metal is in short supply, devils and demons alike turn to more plentiful resources of the Lower Planes: teeth, bones, and molten rock. The skull of a hellscape chatterbane is often the focus of the magical enchantments weaved into it, producing flame from its eye sockets and laughter from its mouth.

itemFluff.name: Bloodlust
entries: {@i Component: phial of monstrosity (minotaur) blood}

When mined, blood crystal appears as a pale, almost colourless pink. However, after touching blood, its colour temporarily shifts to a red deeper than most rubies. For this reason, it's favoured among vampires as a reminder of when to feed.

itemFluff.name: Emberink Stationery
entries: {@i Component: phial of fiend blood}

Filtered from fiendish blood, this pot of fiery orange ink melts most mundane quills that dare to dip into its contents. Only its compatriot, the enchanted pitbat quill which forms part of the stationery, can contain the lava-like liquid. An emberink stationery is often used as a training tool for new devils, promoting teamwork and the virtues of adhering to a contract.

itemFluff.name: Heart of Stone
entries: {@i Component: fiend soul}

When a fiend dies near this fist-sized lump of obsidian, its soul is incarcerated within its inky confines. A creature that attunes to this petrified devil's heart learns the name of any soul imprisoned within it and can drain the soul's lifeforce to create powerful magic. However, with each interaction, there is always a chance that the fiend can break free of this stony jail.

itemFluff.name: Mirror of Truesight
entries: {@i Component: fiend eye}

Nothing so delicate as glass or so pure as silver survives long on the Lower Planes. That in itself makes this magic item a rarity. Held by a handle of claw-like fingers, this magic mirror grants its user the ability to see who is truly the fairest of them all.

itemFluff.name: Pitbone Arrow
entries: {@i Component: pouch of fiend (pitcrow) feathers}

These crude arrows thrum with energy, ready to shatter upon impact. The flights—pitcrow feathers—are enchanted to flare when a target is missed, turning the arrow to the ground where it erupts in a cloud of bone fragments. Hit or miss, these single-use arrows are a nuisance on the battlefield.

itemFluff.name: Pluckpatch
entries: {@i Component: fiend skin}

This plain, worn, leather eyepatch is an unassuming addition to any treasure hoard. However, each day at dawn, eyeballs are plucked from 'donor' fiends in the Hells in order to slowly orbit around your head. Whether fiends are willing donors or victims of a crafty wizard's devilish machinations is an unanswered moral dilemma.

itemFluff.name: Shield of Retaliation
entries: {@i Component: fiend heart}

Legend says that when the war twixt fiends and celestials ravaged the Material Plane, a great infernal general had these shields forged for his captains. Through them he observed the battlefield, and with them he smote the angels that dared challenge his armies. Whether or not the fiendish general still watches through these shields, or even still lives, is a question yet to be answered.

itemFluff.name: Soulkeeper Pendant
entries: {@i Component: phial of celestial blood}
Tools of devilish origin, these mundane-looking miniature animal skulls are designed to increase the throughput of souls to the Hells.

itemFluff.name: Wings of the Hellspawn
entries: {@i Component: fiend skin}

The membranes of these wings are almost transparent in bright daylight, the bones appearing like fleshless claws. Come night or thick shadow, the membranes coalesce into blood-red curtains of opaque, ragged flesh. As you attune to this item, bands of sinew wrap around your form, binding you to these grotesque bone-and-membrane wings.

itemFluff.name: Astrochronometer
entries: {@i Component: construct gears}

Time within the Astral Plane is often described as 'wishy-washy, timey-wimey' by astral kobolds, or as 'temporally impeded' by the more technically minded star gnomes. What they mean is that it's really, really slow. By creating a pocket of the astral plane around one's person, one can effectively suspend the effects of time on one's body.

itemFluff.name: Cloak of the Dunamancer
entries: {@i Component: aberration hide}

Wrought from starlight and comet dust, this almost weightless cloak ignores the wind, rippling in response to unseen solar flares. In response to its wearer's thoughts, the cloak's dunamantic magic can levitate its wearer and distort how light interacts with the fabric.

itemFluff.name: Evening Moon
entries: {@i Component: pouch of celestial dust}

This three-quarter chakram can sustain itself in flight, returning to its wielder after lacerating its target. Equally lethal in both melee and ranged combat, this weapon is favoured by assassins of the astral sea for its ability to create a moving sphere of shadow, aiding its wielder to slip through enemy lines unseen, even in broad daylight.

itemFluff.name: Morning Star
entries: {@i Component: elemental core of fire}

Within these four, free-floating, ever-moving, gyroscopic rings is contained the source of morning star's power: a ball of fusion; a miniaturised star. A creature attuned to the weapon can induce tiny solar flares that can blind or burn those in its path.

itemFluff.name: Gravity Cube
entries: {@i Component: construct plating}
Using the same dunamantic technology as powers the vessels of the astral plane, this cube is capable of manipulating gravity within a confined area.

itemFluff.name: Potion of Planar Infusion
entries: {@i Component: phial of aberration mucus}
Meticulously extracted from the touch of starlight, the sound of a vacuum, and the light from a black hole, this silver liquid forms a swirling, spiral galaxy when left to settle.

itemFluff.name: Quantum Earring of the Paranoid
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity antenna}
Crafted for Lord Innes Ecure, this earring regularly scans the intention-spectrum for references to the creature whose ear or hearing orifice it pierces.

itemFluff.name: Quantum Earring of the Paranoid (Very Rare)
Singularity
entries: {@i Component: aberration brain}
With a thought, this staff opens up a tiny portal to the event horizon of a black hole, moulding the warped space-time into a spearhead of flickering force energy.

itemFluff.name: Starfallen Force Shield
entries: {@i Component: elemental primordial dust}

The Starfallen are a sect of astronautical zealots sworn to prevent the incursion of aberrations from the far reaches of the universe. Though they are unknown to most folk on the Material Plane, it is their vigilance which ensures the relative normality the plane's denizens can enjoy. The force shield is one of their astral armaments, designed to disarm and incapacitate foes.

itemFluff.name: Starrunner's Twinblade
entries: {@i Component: aberration brain}

An astral raider deprived of their dragon needs to travel light. The starrunner's twinblade is the ideal, multifunctional tool; a devastating blade, bow, and shield rolled into one. With such a diverse weapon, one can hunt food, clear a watering hole, and defend oneself, all while retaining mobility.

itemFluff.name: Translux Candle
entries: {@i Component: aberration fat}

Translux candlewax is refined from the rendered fat of planes-travelling beings. This wax is poured in the centre of a permanent teleportation circle under the light of the stars, causing the resulting candle to become locked to that teleportation circle.

itemFluff.name: Flarefuse
entries: {@i Component: aberration heart}
Flarefuses are firearms powered by tiny stars. These balls of fusion consume stardust, the remains of exploded stars, that can be conveniently harvested from drifting sunfrond plants.

itemFluff.name: Psionblade
entries: {@i Component: pouch of monstrosity (phase spider) teeth}

As locomotion in the astral plane is powered by thought, wayfarers of this vast, waterless sea tend to be of greater than normal intelligence. Hence it follows that their armaments should be manipulated by their greatest asset—thought itself—in place of the strength of their arm.

itemFluff.name: Starforged Weapon
entries: {@i Component: pouch of fiend dust}

With an enormous array of mirrors (and a touch of magic), starlight can be focussed to such an intensity that the iron within a trapped comet can be gradually wrought into any shape. Such metal is known as starforged iron and is imbued with a unique property that activates as a creature moves towards or away from its target.

itemFluff.name: Arcanowax
entries: {@i Component: celestial, fey, fiend, and undead fat}

It's always reassuring to enter the territory of extraplanar entities with a lit arcanowax candle. However, due to the cripplingly expensive reagents required to make such a candle—namely oils from celestials, fey, fiends, and undead—the Bureau keeps these under tight wraps, leading to a thriving trade in arcanowax knockoffs on the grey market.

itemFluff.name: Badge of Authority
entries: {@i Component: fey brain}

This brass badge is infused with a lesser known branch of magic spanning the schools of illusion and enchantment: the branch of salesmanship. Just as a salesman might beguile you by spinning fantastical tales of the benefits of snake oil or distract you with beautiful images of long-maned old men, so too does this badge mislead those who look at it. Commonly used by agents of the Fantastical Bureau of Investigation, it also has defensive properties for when such investigations take a turn.

itemFluff.name: Ka-Ma-Ra
entries: {@i Component: fiend eye}
"Please... paint the picture!" Sergeant Flotsam pleaded.
"Can't," replied the red-scale imp. "On strike."

"Listen, I point you at the dead body, you pick up your paintbrush, and you paint. I can see you've got a full stock of paint in there," said Flotsam, peering in through a small window in the Ka-Ma-Ra's side.
"What's that in the corn- Is that what I think it is?!"

"S'private," said the imp. "Listen. Union rates are three ounces of brimstone and a half ounce of sulphur every ten pictures. It's not my fault you kept pressing the trigger while I was in your pack. I'm almost out of black paint now!"

All modern technology is accompanied by growing pains, and the Ka-Ma-Ra is no different. Summoned from the hells and bound into a half-foot-cubed wooden box, the imps that power these pictograph-producing devices tend not to have 'helpful' on their list of traits. By pulling a trigger, a small hammer within the box kicks the imp out of bed, whereupon it paints a picture of whatever the Ka-Ma-Ra is pointing at. Make sure to turn the flash on or end up with a picture in greyscale!

itemFluff.name: Lenz the Inspector
entries: {@i Component: aberration eye}

Though the Bureau has many inspectors—magical magnifying glasses that help spot clues—Lenz is a unique creation. Usually the last inspector to be taken from the quartermaster's stock, Lenz enjoys talking to new people. They rarely enjoy talking to it...

itemFluff.name: Origami Beetle
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant leaves}

itemFluff.name: Origami Butterfly
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant leaves}

itemFluff.name: Origami Gold-Leaf Dragon
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant leaves}

itemFluff.name: Origami Swallow
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant leaves}

itemFluff.name: Origami Unicorn
entries: {@i Component: pouch of plant leaves}

itemFluff.name: Sleuth's Shadowcoat
entries: {@i Component: undead (shadow) ethereal ichor}

Sometimes, a detective's best tool is remaining undetected. The sleuth's shadowcoat allows its wearer to take full advantage of shadows, to hide in plain sight, and to disappear in a puff of smoke (well... fog) when things go awry. Also, it has plenty of secret and obvious pockets in which to store things. Very useful.

itemFluff.name: Taz's Zealous Retribution
entries: {@i Component: construct lifespark}

A common field tool, the TZR, as it is referred to by FBI agents, is the creation of the Bureau's very own inventor: Tazon Regalton. Designed primarily for incapacitating fleeing evil-doers, the TZR also packs a punch when necessary.

itemFluff.name: Phantom Blade
entries: {@i Component: undead (ghost) ethereal ichor}

Branton bodged this project up so badly he actually managed to kill the sword. "Swords aren't even alive," I hear you say. You're right—they're not. Branton Quark's control of magic is so porous that he not only managed to grant an inanimate object life but, while it was revelling in its new-found consciousness, kill it almost immediately. The sword is now livid and haunts any who take the life of a sentient being. This blade is an almost permanent fixture in the FBI evidence lockers; no one is quite sure if sword murder is actually an offence.

itemFluff.name: Piecemaker
entries: {@i Component: elemental core of fire}

Originally named the 'Peacemaker', this field combat device has built in protections to prevent its agents from exceeding their mandate. If the magic within the firearm detects that a target is unarmed, the agent is unable to discharge the weapon.

itemFluff.name: Banner of Bloody Transfusion
entries: {@i Component: phial of humanoid blood}

For the courageous combat nurses of the Night Hospitaller, ward work is but a pastime between battles. Armed with a pure white banner emblazoned with a blood-red sigil, a Hospitaller war chaplain is a rallying point on the battlefield: their magic helps keep fear of death a distant nightmare.

itemFluff.name: Bloodletter Opener
entries: {@i Component: phial of humanoid blood}

Dripping with sanguine liquid, bloodletters were used to help flush out debilitating veinworms, a plague throughout the Remembered Realms, in a practice called bloodflushing. This operation could be extremely traumatic. Thus magical bloodletters—ones that retained a part of the host's blood in a reservoir to be fortified and augmented—were developed to help restore life should the operation be unsuccessful.

itemFluff.name: Cranium Rat Cowl
entries: {@i Component: aberration (rat) brain}

From the pelt and brain of a cranium rat is crafted this morbidly cute headgear. During 'The Occupation', such cowls were worn as a means of avoiding detection by the hive-minded, telepathic bjorg, a race bent on subjugating all free thought. The Night Hospitaller would ferry escapees across bjorg lines before delivering them to an underground steam-powered locomotive.

itemFluff.name: Deathfibrillator
entries: {@i Component: construct lifespark}

A versatile tool, the deathfibrillator is as capable of reinvigorating a dazed creature's motor skills as it is at sending them into a paralytic stupor. Designed by none other than Anton Spark himself, this medical apparatus sees as much use in brawling pits as it does sedating overstimulated patients on the wards of the Night Hospitaller.

itemFluff.name: Epipentagram
entries: {@i Component: fiend bone}

Fiends' blood has some surprising uses. When properly decocted, it can cause hyper-stimulation of the nervous and muscular systems, temporarily relieving the effects of exhaustion, only to have them come back with renewed gusto later on.

itemFluff.name: Happy Healper
entries: {@i Component: celestial liver}

When the wards are packed, healpers are deployed. Each of these temporarily-sentient constructs act as an extra pair of hands for an overwhelmed sister of the Night Hospitaller, administering aid and stabilising those on the brink of death.

itemFluff.name: Heart Rob
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity (shapechanger) heart}

When this blade comes into contact with cardiac tissue, it immediately sends it into spasms, causing a heart attack and preventing pulmonary circulation. Such weapons are especially useful for preventing the spread of lycanthropy and vampirism by inhibiting the cursed toxins from spreading throughout a potential host's body.

itemFluff.name: Hospitaller's Garb
entries: {@i Component: fiend hair}

The Night Hospitaller are a busy lot, but that's no excuse for slovenly dress or being unprepared. The sisterhood commissioned this self-cleaning, self-replenishing raiment to provide the sisters with protection against the diseases they fought and, more importantly, to avoid the chores of washing and pressing their uniforms.

itemFluff.name: Plague Docteur Masque
entries: {@i Component: beast (giant rat) eye}

As any disease control specialist can attest, identifying a disease vector is half the battle in fighting it. During the veinworm plague, the symbol of the crow became synonymous with the Night Hospitaller—a representation of their hook-nosed masks and a symbol of both fear and hope. These masks give their wearers the ability to detect disease, even in the incubation stage, allowing plague-carriers to be identified and quarantined before they become infectious. In many places, these masks are still regarded as a symbol of authoritarianism and terror.

itemFluff.name: Ser Boncou Rage
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity (shapechanger) liver}

Dealing with lycanthropy, curses from the Far Realms, or mutations caused by the sentient, self replicating Mut-X mutagen can jade even the most resilient nurse of the Night Hospitaller. These potions of bottled courage, all of which go by the name Ser Boncou Rage, can imbue a terrified sister with the immense determination required to cure lycanthropy on a full moon or protect them from the wiles of an especially charming vampire.

itemFluff.name: Torture Tincture
entries: {@i Component: monstrosity poison gland}

This two-inch-tall phial of oily liquid has a decidedly untrustworthy air to it. Using its bandage scarf, it can climb up any surface, before depositing a dose of poison in whatever vessel, meal, or concoction its wielder chooses. Stories say such a magical contraption was used to poison a king who'd locked himself in a high tower, and that this was the origin of the veinworm plague.

itemFluff.name: Longshot
entries: {@i Component: phial of aberration mucus}

During the veinworm plague, a cult of trypanophobic disease-worshippers refused to get vaccinated, becoming reservoirs in which the pathogen could reproduce and adapt. The Night Hospitaller, a sect of nocturnal combat nurses, developed a syringe-like ammunition as a means of delivering vaccinations from afar. Since then, this clinical tool has taken many guises, including the magically self-filling longshot.

itemFluff.name: Amethyst Cosmos
entries: {@i Component: dragon (amethyst) heart}

Suspended in an invisible gravimantic field, the shards of gemstone that float around this item refract a kaleidoscopic display of light in shades of mauve, indigo, and lilac. The concentric disks mimic a solar system, a homage to the amethyst dragons and their adoration of celestial bodies.

itemFluff.name: Copper Cuirass
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (copper) scales}

To complement this robust but aerodynamic suit of armour, the ingenious smith Brimbor devised a pair of intricate mechanical wings, powerful enough to raise their wearer aloft and imbue them with dizzying speed and agility. They produce a soft, soothing hum when gliding through the air.

itemFluff.name: Crimson Tyrant
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (red) claws}

This fiery axe is the masterpiece of the renowned elven smith, Rhuinon, and their final entry into the ongoing contest to curry the favour of the mysterious Forgemaster. Meticulously crafted to evoke the arrogant ferocity of the red dragon, Rhuinon is sure this formidable weapon will grant them a resounding victory over their competitors.

itemFluff.name: Deepshot
entries: {@i Component: phial of dragon blood}

Accompanied by a musty aroma, this magical, dark iron firearm produces a multitude of dusky mushrooms each day. Releasing a toxic torrent from each crop of toadstools, the spore-filled salvo produces effects reminiscent of the debilitating deep dragons of The Low.

itemFluff.name: Midnight Corrosion
entries: {@i Component: dragon (black) breath sac}

Rhuinon's second submission to the Forgemaster was a cruel and vicious sword inspired by the malevolent black dragon. Every crushing swing made with this sword fills its wielder with an exhilarating glee and a burgeoning desire to cause more pain. Tempered in a magical acidic mixture, the sword's dark blade constantly exudes mild caustic fumes.

itemFluff.name: Topaz Despoiler
entries: {@i Component: dragon (topaz) heart}

The articulated mechanical hand at the tip of this item sporadically animates with bursts of negative energy. The fingers fondly caress the flawless topaz orb fixed to the palm, which pulses hypnotically with a gentle, steady glow.

itemFluff.name: Viridian Deceiver
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (green) scales}

This polearm embodies the treacherous, deceitful essence of the green dragon. Foul poison drips from its curved, golden blade, warning that beneath its beautiful surface, this weapon is just as dangerous and deadly as it is elegant.

itemFluff.name: Argent Gliders
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (silver) scales}

These meticulously crafted boots imbue their wearer with the nimble grace of the majestic silver dragon. Made by the elven smith Brimbor to honour the Forgemaster, they are always comfortably cool and a snug fit, enchanted to perfectly adapt to any type of terrain or activity.

itemFluff.name: Azulash
entries: {@i Component: dragon (blue) breath sac}

At first glance, this vitriolic whip appears to be an ornate handle or baton, constructed out of polished, glossy blue scales of lapis lazuli. With lightning speed, a lash of crackling electricity can spark into life, channeling the fiercely territorial nature of the blue dragon.

itemFluff.name: Brazen Shelter
entries: {@i Component: dragon (brass) fat}

This long, flowing cloak was crafted in the image of the gregarious brass dragon. Peering closely at the fabric reveals an intricate pattern of thousands of tiny scales that gleam brightly in the light. Though thick and ornate, the cloak is remarkably lightweight and doesn't impede its wearer's movements.

itemFluff.name: Bronze Bulwark
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (bronze) scales}

This mighty shield was forged in the image of the stalwart bronze dragon; an impenetrable defence against evil and tyranny. A wielder can harness its repulsive powers to deflect missiles, repel enemies, or hover above liquids.

itemFluff.name: Crystal Seer
entries: {@i Component: dragon (crystal) eye}

Contemplative and curious, this striking spyglass sparkles with dazzling, prismatic beauty. In the lofty high elf halls of Kri'astula, the renowned jewelsmith, Nerys, harnessed the light of the sun, stars, and crystal dragons to craft this far-seeing device. Perceiving objects distant in both time and space, a crystal seer is coveted by knowledge-seekers the world over.

itemFluff.name: Emerald Archive
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (emerald) scales}

This heavy tome is expertly bound in fine leather, crafted using viridescent enamel to resemble the scales of the emerald dragon. Its pages of thick vellum wait to be filled with precious knowledge. The gem at the centre of the cover can project beautiful illusionary illustrations of the book's contents, enhancing the reading experience, and causing Humperdink to label it a 'fancy pop-up book'.

itemFluff.name: Golden Glory
entries: {@i Component: pouch of dragon (gold) scales}

This resplendent helmet radiates the regal glory of the gold dragon. A paradigm of the art of smithing, Brimbor themself can attest to having created no finer work, nor better chance of winning the Forgemaster's contest. This helm grants its wearer the ability to inspire others and also imparts a curious appetite for gemstones.

itemFluff.name: Moonstone Dreamer
entries: {@i Component: dragon eye}

Three azure tears in the fabric of space hover tremulously over the flawless moonstone set into this ring. A mesmerising spiral pattern swirls within the gem, occasionally coalescing into indistinct scenes of exotic landscapes, or perhaps offering a glimpse into the dreams of its wearer...

itemFluff.name: Pale Rage
entries: {@i Component: dragon (white) bone}

First of Rhuinon's draconic armaments was this icy morningstar, perpetually patinated with a crisp crust of hoarfrost. Suffused with the animalistic rage of the white dragon, this weapon imbues its wielder with a single-minded, myopic brutality.

itemFluff.name: Sapphire Seeker
entries: {@i Component: dragon (sapphire) heart}

Freely floating inside a 1-inch sphere of crystal is an intricately faceted sapphire; a spinning top imbued with swathes of divination magic. When this compass is activated, the top whirls at breakneck speed in a dazzling azure cyclone, before coming to a stop with unerring precision.

itemFluff.name: Tortuga Cannon
entries: {@i Component: dragon (dragon turtle) heart}

When the fire of a drake-kin meets the briny depths of the ocean, seas broil and kelp writhes with draconic ferocity. Fashioned from the scales of a dragon turtle, this sleeve-like device can produce jets of water with the force of a cannon, scalding and entangling its target.

itemFluff.name: Amalgamask
entries: {@i Component: ooze (amalgamooze) vesicle}

In order to remain as impartial bastions of righteousness, the Knights Penitent are forbidden from emotional outbursts or impassioned self-expression. Those most committed to this form of self-sacrifice wear this many-faced mask, which they shift to convey the appropriate emotion and prevent their true thoughts from ever being known.

itemFluff.name: Bloodhound's Caress
entries: {@i Component: construct (bloodhound) claw}

The Bloodhounds, an enigmatic order of monster hunters, track their quarry by the scent of their spilled blood. The bloodhound's caress is designed to draw blood from its victims, ensuring a trail of blood can be followed back to the victim's lair, where any young can be dispatched before they grow to be menaces.

itemFluff.name: Bloodhound's Widebrim
entries: {@i Component: construct (bloodhound) pelt}

Adorned with the teeth of a bloodhound and the feathers of a falcon, this ragged hat honours some of the most tenacious trackers in the animal kingdom and calls upon their gifts in the service of monster hunters far and wide. Wisps of crimson occasionally form in the eyes of its wearer, only to fade a moment later.

itemFluff.name: Candlebrand Ring
entries: {@i Component: elemental (pontiff) molten eye}

With a word, this two-finger signet ring becomes incandescent with the heat of the waxen god's ire. Its brand can bless a creature or, if the brand is twisted and distorted in the process of being applied, curse the creature.

itemFluff.name: Candlelight Mitre
entries: {@i Component: elemental (pontiff) candleplume}

Fanatical to the extreme, the ruthless Priests of Candlelight display the uncaring visage of their waxen god on this intricate ceremonial hat. Their Creed Sanitari dictates that such acolytes must sear their flesh daily to remain pure and worthy of their deity's attention.

itemFluff.name: Clutching Crown
entries: {@i Component: celestial (devourer) bone}

Deeply uncomfortable by design, this crown torments its wearer with the constant drumming of skeletal fingers on metal, which echoes in the ears in an unnatural fashion. Occasionally, the sharp thumbs of its osseous claws cut the wearer's skin in a sadistic caress that absorbs the droplets of blood they draw.

itemFluff.name: Grasping Mantle
entries: {@i Component: celestial (devourer) skin}

The soft rustling of fingers on fur is an ever-present distraction for the wearer of this cloak, as the many hands on the mantle move with a will of their own, grasping at its red velvet and caressing all they can reach in disturbing and uncomfortable ways.

itemFluff.name: Heartbeater
entries: {@i Component: ooze (amalgamooze) heart}

Carrying a heartbeater is an honour bestowed upon the most devoted of the Knights Penitent. It symbolises their eternal punishment, which is both an agonising cage and a source of great power. Only those willing to suffer for their convictions and sacrifice their own humanity can unlock the true power of this macabre weapon.

itemFluff.name: Maiden's Tower
entries: {@i Component: ooze (amalgamooze) bone}

The Order of the Knights Penitent believes that only constant punishment can keep sinful impulses at bay and lead to a life of righteousness. Perched atop this shield's spiked exterior is a serene, silver face. When the shield encases a creature, bloody tears pour from its empty eyes, and its cold metallic lips occasionally twitch into a smile when it causes a victim especially great agony.

itemFluff.name: Bloodhound's Immobiliser
entries: {@i Component: construct (bloodhound) bone}

The Bloodhounds are not just hack-and-slash monster hunters; they're experts on monster habits, anatomy, and harvesting. This nail gun grants these hunters the ability to immobilise their prey, allowing the horrors to be studied in greater detail. The more experienced the bloodhound, the more decrepit their firearm; whenever a foe of especially great might is felled by the gun, a notch spontaneously forms on its dark metal.

itemFluff.name: Hand of the King
entries: {@i Component: celestial (devourer) brain}
When fresh blood is spilled on the pristine blade of this sword, its true, corroded appearance is unveiled, a reminder that a glamorous appearance doesn't guarantee noble intentions.

itemFluff.name: Waxen Sceptre
entries: {@i Component: elemental (pontiff) core of wax}

Sinister yet holy, the waxen god blesses those who endure their molten ministrations, restoring their fortitude, protecting their skin, and purifying their words. When lit, the candlesmoke of this sceptre carries the subtle, sweet scent of charred flesh while its tendrils form the screaming visages of those it has blessed.

itemFluff.name: Beetle Buckler
entries: {@i Component: fey (Scarab) carapace}

Carved from hardened chitin, this robust shield is easily mistaken for a large beetle; the sublime fey craftsmanship is augmented by enchantments that cause the wings to buzz and flutter. These animate wings provide both enhanced defensive capabilities and a means of flitting across thorny battlefields.

itemFluff.name: Bramble Lace Gloves
entries: {@i Component: pouch of fey (Bramble Queen) thorns}

Despite the lack of soil or water, the dark roses on these luxurious gloves are alive, feeding gently off of the energy of their wearer. This symbiotic relationship has no adverse effects on the wearer aside from some aggravated tiredness at the end of the day—a small price to pay for the powerful fey magic held within.

itemFluff.name: Cloak of the Moth Rider
entries: {@i Component: fey (Adelifae) wings}

The reclusive giant moths of the Plane of Fey are a perfect representation of the mysterious, gloomy whimsy of their home plane and this cloak, made from their fur, is a rare treasure. To slay one of these noble beasts is a nigh unforgivable offence to the unseelie fey. However, for a giant moth to willingly bestow its fur to a creature is a tremendous honour, and a sign of the friendship of the fey.

itemFluff.name: Cloak of the Moth Rider (Very Rare)
Herculean Horn
entries: {@i Component: pouch of fey (Scarab) claws}

Hardened by alchemical processes and complex enchantments alike, the chitinous tip of this lance, fashioned in the likeness of the hercules beetle, has been known to perforate inch-thick plates of pure adamantine.

itemFluff.name: Kabuto
entries: {@i Component: fey (Scarab) horn}

The sight of two fighting beetles is an impressive and surprisingly humbling one, and many unseelie fey can't help but marvel at the magnificent displays of strength put on by such tiny creatures. Inspired by these intense combats, this helm is enchanted with the fierce fighting spirit of these noble and tenacious insects.

itemFluff.name: Raven's Gown
entries: {@i Component: pouch of fey feathers}

Crafting this elegant if sinister dress is painstakingly complex: each individual feather must be collected from a different, willing raven that has been suffused with the magic of the Plane of Fey. These feathers can be given as gifts, which, like so many gifts from the unseelie, benefits the giver instead of the receiver.

itemFluff.name: Silkspun Lantern
entries: {@i Component: fey (moth) egg}

This enchanting lantern stirs a deep fascination in the hearts of those who behold it, and can be used to lure unsuspecting humanoids to the Plane of Fey. Weak-willed creatures who so much as glance at its mesmerising lilac hues find themselves haunted by wistful memories of its light and a desire to be near it.

itemFluff.name: Black Bloom
entries: {@i Component: fey (Bramble Queen) psyche}

The black roses of the Plane of Fey thrive in the bodies of creatures; their roots pierce flesh and their vines strangle limbs in a dominating display. As much as unseelie fey enjoy tricks and subtle manipulation, a more forceful approach is sometimes required.

itemFluff.name: Faerie Moon
entries: {@i Component: fey eye}

Whether seen as a sacred symbol, a source of power, or simply an ideal of beauty and mystique to strive for, the moon nevertheless holds a special place in the hearts of many fey. Those most devoted to it wield weapons that harness this radiant, lunar magic using a delicate crystal orb.

itemFluff.name: Flutterblade
entries: {@i Component: fey (Adelifae) proboscis}

The delicate insect wings on the hilt of this elegant weapon flutter with scintillating shimmers. Wielded by retainers of the Unseelie Court, these swords can shoot through the air under their own propulsion, returning to their wielder after making a ranged foray.
