subclassFeature.name: Path of the Brewmaster
entries: The right drink is welcome on any occasion. Mayhaps you want to invigorate your body ahead of a bloody brawl, ruminate over a moral quandary, celebrate a sensational dragon slaying, or fill your veins with liquid bread after a hard day's slog through the elements. Knowing which drink to suggest for which occasion—and having that drink on hand—is what separates a lay brewer from a brewmaster.

For a brewmaster, the act of brewing—a process which can take months—is a practice in mindfulness. Carrying their barrels and demijohns from place to place, you would be mistaken to construe these individuals as peaceful. This apparent tranquillity is but the calm before the storm, for when a brewmaster enters a rage, they do so fueled by the fermented products of their labours.

subclassFeature.name: Brewer
entries: {@i 3rd-level Path of the Brewmaster feature.}

Years spent experimenting in breweries and kombucha clubs have given you a taste for fermentation and experience in bar room brawls. You gain proficiency with {@item brewer's supplies|PHB} and improvised weapons, and you have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls you make with improvised weapons. When you reach 6th level in this class, attacks made with improvised weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming {@b resistance} and {@b immunity} to nonmagical attacks and damage. When you reach 10th level in this class, the attack and damage bonus increases to +2.

subclassFeature.name: Ancestral Spirits
entries: {@i 3rd-level Path of the Brewmaster feature.}

The act of refining ingredients, maintaining special yeast cultures, and carefully monitoring a brew's progress is a ritualistic practice through which you find connection with your forebears and the natural world around you. The beverages you brew are fortified with the spirits of your ancestors, imparting enhanced benefits to you.

When you finish a long rest, you can use {@item brewer's supplies|PHB} to prepare a number of beverages equal to the number shown for your barbarian level in the Rages column of the Barbarian table (maximum 6), choosing from the list below (you can choose the same option more than once). When you enter a rage, you can imbibe one of these beverages (choosing from those that you prepared) as part of the bonus action you use to enter the rage, gaining its benefits for the duration of the rage. Undrunk beverages lose their potency when you finish a long rest.

items.name: Kefir
entries: A nutritious, creamy beverage, kefir fortifies your body. You have {@b advantage} on Constitution saving throws.

items.name: Kombucha
entries: A stimulating blend of fermented teas, this bubbly drink hones your reactions. You gain an additional reaction each round that can be used only to make opportunity attacks. You can take only one reaction per turn.

items.name: Mead
entries: Sweet, honey mead fuels your body. Your walking speed increases by 10 feet and opportunity attacks made against you have {@b disadvantage}.

items.name: Sake
entries: Strongly alcoholic, this clear liquid lends you a bloody, single-mindedness. You have {@b advantage} on Wisdom saving throws.

items.name: Stout
entries: Dark, liquid bread is a bona fide meal replacement. You gain {@dice 1d4} temporary hit points at the start of each of your turns. If you have fewer than half your maximum hit points, you instead regain {@dice 1d4} hit points. These dice both increase to {@dice 1d8} when you reach 10th level in this class.

subclassFeature.name: Brewer's Gut
entries: {@i 6th-level Path of the Brewmaster feature.}

Over time, you've imbibed an amalgam of experimental yeasts that have fortified your gut. When you finish a short or long rest, you gain one of the following benefits of your choice, which lasts until you finish a short or long rest:

entries.name: Beer Jacket
entries: You have {@b resistance} to cold damage.

entries.name: Guts of Steel
entries: You have {@b resistance} to poison damage.

entries.name: Liquid Courage
entries: You are {@b immune} to the {@condition frightened} condition. As a bonus action, you can give a frightened creature that is friendly to you and within your reach a good-natured slap, allowing it to repeat the saving throw against the condition with {@b advantage}.

subclassFeature.name: Drinking Buddies
entries: {@i 10th-level Path of the Brewmaster feature.}

Sharing is caring. During a short or long rest, you can spend 30 minutes crafting a pitcher of beverages for up to 10 creatures. At the end of the rest, all creatures that drink your beverage gain your choice of one of the following benefits until it next finishes a short or long rest. Undrunk beverages lose their potency when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Ginger Ale
entries: Fiery and stimulating, one can react quickly. The creature has {@b advantage} on initiative rolls.

entries.name: Pulque
entries: Good accompaniment for a drinking song. The creature gains a bonus to Charisma checks equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum bonus of +1).

entries.name: Warm Cider
entries: A wholesome beverage. The creature gains 3 temporary hit points at the start of each of its turns.

subclassFeature.name: Fermented Binge
entries: {@i 14th-level Path of the Brewmaster feature.}

You have grown a strain of super yeast within yourself that you can activate when you enter a rage. When you drink one of the beverages from your Ancestral Spirits feature, you can enhance the drink to gain an additional benefit based on its type.

entries.name: Kefir
entries: You are {@b immune} to poison damage and the {@condition poisoned} condition, and you have {@b advantage} on saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition exhaustion} condition on yourself.

entries.name: Kombucha
entries: Creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they move within your reach.

entries.name: Mead
entries: Your walking speed increases by an additional 10 feet (20 feet total increase), and you can take the {@action Disengage} action as a bonus action on each of your turns.

entries.name: Sake
entries: You have {@b resistance} to psychic damage and are {@b immune} to the {@condition charmed} condition.

entries.name: Stout
entries: The number of hit points regained or temporary hit points gained from this drink is doubled.
After you have used this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: College of Mixology
entries: A bard of the College of Mixology considers themselves every bit an artist as any painter or musician. After all, does the playful glint of a glass bottle pirouetting through the air in a perfect arc not compare to a graceful arabesque? Does the exquisite blend of carefully curated flavours not bring to mind the mixing of colours on a canvas? And does the feeling of a gentle buzz not relax the soul like the delicately plucked strings of a harp?

Adventurous experimenters and eccentric visionaries at heart, mixologists often clash in matters of palate and presentation with bards of the College of Cuisine. These sister colleges maintain a heated rivalry, periodically holding grand competitions that result in meals so sublime, they have been known to make seasoned connoisseurs cry at first taste.

subclassFeature.name: Flair Bartending
entries: {@i 3rd-level College of Mixology feature.}

With impeccable showmanship and nary a spilled drop, you've learned to dazzle audiences by dexterously manipulating your mixology tools. You gain proficiency with {@item brewer's supplies|PHB} and can use them as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.

As an action, you can begin a mixological show which lasts up to 1 minute. You must use your action at the start of each of your turns to continue this performance; if you don't, it ends immediately. When the performance starts, make a Dexterity ({@item brewer's supplies|PHB}) check. Creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you that can see you whose passive {@skill Insight} score is lower than the result of your check are distracted by your performance, gaining {@b disadvantage} on {@skill Perception} checks for the duration of the show.

If you roll a 1 on the d20, you drop some of your tools and the effect fails to occur. The threshold to drop your tools subsequently increases by 1 each time you make a Dexterity ({@item brewer's supplies|PHB}) check using this feature, resetting to 1 when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Pick Your Poison
entries: {@i 3rd-level College of Mixology feature.}

Expertly combining only the finest ingredients, you've learned to make delicious beverages that delight even the dourest dwarf. You can use a set of {@item brewer's supplies|PHB} to prepare a special cocktail over the course of 1 minute. When you do, select up to two different ingredients from the table below, which you magically conjure to mix the cocktail. A creature can drink a cocktail as an action, gaining the effects of the chosen ingredients, which last for 10 minutes or until the creature drinks another cocktail.
A cocktail that isn't consumed loses its potency when you finish a long rest.

rows: The creature regains {@dice 1d8} hit points, which aren't lost after the effect ends.
The creature gains {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 30 feet. If it already has darkvision, its range is increased by 30 feet.
The creature gains {@dice 2d6} temporary hit points.
The creature gains {@b advantage} on initiative rolls.
The creature gains a swimming speed equal to its walking speed.
The creature gains a +1 bonus to the damage rolls of its weapon attacks.
The creature's walking speed is increased by 5 feet.
The creature's jump distance is doubled.

entries: You can use this feature to create a cocktail a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Barside Therapy
entries: {@i 6th-level College of Mixology feature.}

You are a seasoned expert at dealing with the inebriated and have a few tricks up your sleeve to make the sober become intoxicated. You have {@b advantage} on ability checks made to interact socially with {@condition poisoned} creatures and, when you make a cocktail with your Pick Your Poison feature, you can overload it with magically potent alcohol instead of choosing ingredients. A creature that drinks this cocktail must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your bard spell save DC or be {@condition poisoned} for 10 minutes.

subclassFeature.name: Quick Fix
entries: {@i 6th-level College of Mixology feature.}

Pain you as it might, you can rush your creative process to prepare one of your cocktails on the spot. As a bonus action, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to prepare a cocktail with your Pick Your Poison feature, and either drink it or toss it to a willing creature you can see within 30 feet of you, who can immediately use its reaction to drink it.

subclassFeature.name: Garnish
entries: {@i 14th-level College of Mixology feature.}

Presentation matters, and you've learned to take your drinks to the next level with some tasteful embellishments, magical replicas of creature parts that you conjure out of thin air. Whenever you make a cocktail with your Pick Your Poison feature, you can select one of the ingredients from the table below as garnish, which provides an additional effect for the cocktail's duration.

rows: The creature gains {@sense blindsight} to a range of 30 feet.
The creature gains a flying speed equal to its walking speed.
The effects of the cocktail last for {@dice 1d4} hours instead of 10 minutes.
Choose acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison when you make the cocktail. The creature gains {@b resistance} to that damage type.
The creature gains {@b advantage} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

entries.name: Optional Rule: Cocktail Potions
entries: Whenever you make a cocktail with a garnish using your Pick Your Poison feature, you can choose to substitute the garnish for an equivalent creature part harvested using the Harvesting rules in {@i Heliana's Guide to Monster Hunting}, and expend a spell slot of 3rd-level or higher in the process. This expends the creature part, but the resulting cocktail doesn't lose its potency when you finish a long rest.
You can have a number of cocktails prepared in this way equal to your Charisma modifier. If making a new one would put you over this limit, the oldest cocktail loses its potency.

subclassFeature.name: Festivity Domain
entries: Worshipping deities like Dionysus, Bacchus, Sucellus, and Inari, clerics of the Festivity domain spread joy and frivolity wherever they roam. If not kept in check, the most insignificant gathering can spin out of control to become a roiling crowd of jubilation as the cleric miraculously finds food and drink with which to incite a celebration.

Indeed, tales abound of clerics of Festivity infiltrating fortified citadels under siege, instigating exuberant carousing, and leaving their fellow merrymakers collapsed and exhausted after expending every last iota of energy. It is then a simple task for a waiting army to enter the city and lock their fatigued foes in chains. As the ancient proverb goes, "there is no celebration comparable to a cleric's celebration, for a cleric's celebration does not cease".

subclassFeature.name: Domain Spells
entries: {@i 1st-level Festivity Domain feature.}
You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Festivity Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.

entries.caption: Festivity Domain Spells
rows: {@spell create food and water}, {@spell hypnotic pattern}
{@spell freedom of movement}, {@spell Humperdink's irresistible revelry|LALoTT}

subclassFeature.name: Reveler's Resilience
entries: {@i 1st-level Festivity Domain feature.}

You refuse to let your carousing be interrupted by something as trivial as a little pain. When you fail a saving throw to maintain concentration on a spell, you can use your reaction to reroll that saving throw. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Life of the Party
entries: {@i 1st-level Festivity Domain feature.}

You're an expert celebrant, armed with a plethora of tricks to liven up any party and a silver tongue to charm other guests. You learn the {@spell flare|LALoTT} cantrip (see Appendix A). This doesn't count against the number of cantrips you know.
In addition, you can use your Wisdom modifier in place of your Charisma modifier when making Charisma checks and Charisma saving throws.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Invoke Festivities
entries: {@i 2nd-level Festivity Domain feature.}

You can use your Channel Divinity to conjure an atmosphere of celebration and merrymaking, lifting the spirits of your allies and imbuing them with the power of revelry. As an action, you present your holy symbol and choose up to three friendly creatures you can see within 30 feet of you and one of the festivals below. The chosen creatures gain the benefits of the chosen festival until the end of your next turn.

items.name: Blossom Festival
items.entry: You celebrate the growth of new life after the harshness of winter, and the beauty and vigour of youth. A creature regains {@dice 1d8} hit points at the start of its turn and deals an extra {@damage 1d8} radiant damage the first time it hits with an attack on its turn.

items.name: Midsummer Festival
items.entry: You celebrate long days and hot weather, as well as the sense of freedom they provide. A creature's speed increases by 15 feet, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage the first time it hits with an attack on its turn.

items.name: Harvest Festival
items.entry: You celebrate nature's bounty and reap the rewards of your hard work. A creature deals an extra {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage the first time it hits with an attack on its turn, and it gains temporary hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt, which last for 1 minute.

items.name: Midwinter Festival
items.entry: You celebrate your community, banding together to endure the coming cold. A creature gains a +1 bonus to its AC and deals an extra {@damage 1d8} cold damage the first time it hits with an attack on its turn.

subclassFeature.name: Afterparty
entries: {@i 6th-level Festivity Domain feature.}

Your celebrations have become so momentous that their effects linger even after they come to an end, leaving your allies invigorated and your foes exhausted. When a creature stops being under the effect of your Channel Divinity or a spell of 1st level or higher that you cast, one of the following effects takes place:

items: If the creature is friendly to you, it gains {@dice 2d6} temporary hit points that last for 10 minutes.
If the creature is hostile to you, it gains {@b disadvantage} on the next attack roll it makes within 10 minutes.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: {@i 8th-level Festivity Domain feature.}
Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: The Grand Revelry
entries: {@i 17th-level Festivity Domain feature.}

When you use your Invoke Festivities, you can choose to augment it, transforming it into the festival of the year. If you do, the effects last for 1 minute instead of until the end of your next turn, and, in addition to the friendly creatures, you can also choose up to three hostile creatures you can see within 30 feet of you, inviting them to take part in your celebrations. Each hostile creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or suffer the following effects for the duration, depending on the chosen festival:

items.name: Blossom Festival
items.entry: The creature is {@condition charmed} by you, and whenever it takes radiant damage, it takes an extra {@damage 1d8} radiant damage.

items.name: Midsummer Festival
items.entry: The first attack roll made against the creature each turn is made with {@b advantage}. Whenever the creature takes fire damage, it takes an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage.

items.name: Harvest Festival
items.entry: The creature has {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls, and whenever it takes necrotic damage, it takes an extra {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage.

items.name: Midwinter Festival
items.entry: The creature's speed is reduced to 0 feet, and whenever it takes cold damage, it takes an extra {@damage 1d8} cold damage.

entries: A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Tavern Brawler
entries: The taste of ale-soaked floorboards is a familiar flavour to Tavern Brawlers. Whether they've been thrown to the ground in a glorious takedown, blocked an improvised weapon with their teeth, or tackled a foe into a pile of flailing limbs, Tavern Brawlers cherish the memory of every scuffle that hasn't been knocked out of their brains.

Though most inns and pubs decry barroom brawls as unnecessarily damaging and expensive affairs, celebrated carpenter and philosopher Lottaw Ood proposed that without the constant kerfuffles, woodworkers and the entire lumber industry might collapse. In fact, more shrewd carpentry guilds have opened "Brawl-Inns"; taverns with dedicated brawling areas and a microtransaction economy that encourages fighters to purchase chairs with which to clout their fellow patrons.

subclassFeature.name: Barroom Wrestler
entries: {@i 3rd-level Tavern Brawler feature.}

entries.name: Brawler Dice
entries: You've learned techniques that are fueled by special dice called Brawler dice, which are {@dice d4}s. You have a number of these dice equal to twice your proficiency bonus. You regain all your expended Brawler dice when you finish a long rest.
When you reach certain levels in this class, the size of your Brawler dice increases: at 5th level ({@dice d6}), 11th level ({@dice d8}), and 17th level ({@dice d10}).

entries.name: Saving Throws
entries: Some of your techniques require your target to make a saving throw to resist the technique's effects. The saving throw DC is calculated as follows:

entries.name: Brawler Techniques
entries: The techniques below use your Brawler dice. Creatures more than one size larger than you automatically succeed on any saving throws or contested checks against these techniques' effects.

items.name: Bind
entries: {@i Prerequisite: target {@condition grappled}.} When you make an unarmed strike with a free hand against a creature you are grappling, you can expend one Brawler die to force an additional contested grappling check instead of an attack roll. On a success, you deal the strike's normal damage plus extra damage equal to one roll of your Brawler die, and the creature is {@condition restrained} until the grapple ends or you use one of the hands you have grappling the target for something else.

items.name: Chokehold
entries: {@i Prerequisite: target {@condition restrained}.} While you have a humanoid {@condition restrained} (for example, by your Bind technique), you can use an action to expend one Brawler die and attempt to cut off the creature's blood supply. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier plus two rolls of your Brawler die and immediately begins to be strangled until it is no longer {@condition restrained} by you. On a success, it takes half as much damage and doesn't begin to be strangled.

A creature that is being strangled remains conscious for a number of rounds equal to its Constitution modifier (minimum 1), and falls {@condition unconscious} at the start of its next turn. A creature that is made {@condition unconscious} in this manner regains consciousness if it takes any damage. If you continue to choke a creature beyond this point, it begins suffocating. If you release the creature, it remains {@condition unconscious} for {@dice 1d20 + 10} minutes.

items.name: Smash
entries: {@i Prerequisite: hit with improvised weapon melee weapon attack.} When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack using an improvised weapon, you can expend one Brawler die to deal extra bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (GM's decision) equal to one roll of your Brawler die. The object takes an equal amount of damage, possibly destroying it.

items.name: Takedown
entries: {@i Prerequisite: target {@condition grappled}.} When you make an unarmed strike against a creature you are grappling, you can expend one Brawler die to force an additional contested grappling check instead of an attack roll. On a success, you deal the strike's normal damage plus extra damage equal to one roll of your Brawler die as you throw the target to the ground where it lands {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Strangulation & Suffocation
entries: Strangulation is a new concept introduced for this subclass and acts like a less extreme version of suffocating. In both cases, when a creature begins to be strangled or begins suffocating, it can act as normal for a number of rounds equal to its Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 round). At the start of the creature's turn after this period has elapsed, the creature falls {@condition unconscious}.

The difference between being strangled and suffocating is that when a suffocating creature falls {@condition unconscious} it also drops to 0 hit points, is dying, and can't regain hit points or be stabilized until it can breathe again, whereas a strangled creature remains {@condition unconscious} for {@dice 1d20 + 10} minutes, and wakes if it takes any damage.

{@b Chokehold Example.} On round 1, the Tavern Brawler, who has a thug {@condition restrained}, uses the chokehold technique on the thug. The thug fails its Constitution saving throw and begins to be strangled. Because the thug has a +2 Constitution modifier, it has 2 rounds to act before it falls {@condition unconscious}. The thug takes its turn after the fighter, so on its turn of rounds 1 and 2, the thug tries and fails to break the Tavern Brawler's grapple, meaning it stays {@condition restrained} and continues to be strangled. At the start of the thug's turn on round 3, the thug falls {@condition unconscious}. If the Tavern Brawler continues to maintain the grapple and thus keep the thug {@condition restrained}, the thug begins suffocating. At the start of the thug's turn on round 5, the thug would succumb to suffocation, drop to 0 hit points, and begin dying.

subclassFeature.name: Inn-habitor
entries: {@i 3rd-level Tavern Brawler feature.}

Your familiarity with patrons of bars and inns has left you adept at sensing their motives. You have {@b advantage} on {@skill Insight}, {@skill Intimidation}, and {@skill Persuasion} checks against creatures that have been drinking.

subclassFeature.name: Tavern Tussler
entries: {@i 3rd-level Tavern Brawler feature.}

You've learned to use whatever weapons might be close at hand, often surprising foes with your ingenuity. You are proficient with improvised weapons and your unarmed strikes and improvised weapon attacks can deal damage equal to {@dice 1d4} + your Strength modifier on a hit. This damage die increases when you reach 5th level ({@dice 1d6}), 11th level ({@dice 1d8}), and 17th level ({@dice 1d10}) in this class.

entries.name: Improviser
entries: You can pick up an object within your reach as part of the attack you make with that object. In addition, the first time you make an attack with an improvised weapon you picked up on that same turn, that attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

subclassFeature.name: Twisting Grapples
entries: {@i 7th-level Tavern Brawler feature.}

You can attempt to grapple a creature as a bonus action on your turn, and you have {@b advantage} on attack rolls you make against creatures you are grappling. In addition, when you successfully apply the {@condition grappled} condition to a creature, you can choose to painfully twist, dealing bludgeoning damage to the creature equal to a roll of your unarmed strike die plus your Strength modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Uncanny Technique
entries: {@i 10th-level Tavern Brawler feature.}

You can add double your proficiency bonus to contested Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks you make. When you make an ability check to grapple a creature or to escape a grapple, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

subclassFeature.name: Tavern Regular
entries: {@i 15th-level Tavern Brawler feature.}

Years spent anticipating conflict in inns and taverns has given you a preternatural danger sense. You have {@b advantage} on initiative rolls, and you gain proficiency in the {@skill Insight} skill. If you have this proficiency from another source, you can add twice your proficiency bonus to checks you make using that skill.

As a bonus action on your turn, you can make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check against the passive {@skill Deception} of a creature you can see. On a success, you learn the creature's disposition towards you, such as whether it means you harm, views you as a friend, or wants something from you.

subclassFeature.name: Grandstand Performer
entries: {@i 18th-level Tavern Brawler feature.}
When you roll initiative and have no Brawler dice remaining, you regain two Brawler dice.
You learn new techniques that use your Brawler dice.

entries.name: Piledriver
entries: {@i Prerequisite: target {@condition restrained}.} When you have a creature {@condition restrained} (for example, by your Bind technique) and take the {@action Attack} action, you can forgo two of your attacks to expend one Brawler die in a complex crushing manoeuvre. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier plus two rolls of your Brawler die and is {@condition stunned} until the end of your next turn. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition stunned}.

entries.name: Throw
entries: {@i Prerequisite: target {@condition grappled}.} When you have a creature {@condition grappled} and take the {@action Attack} action, you can forgo one of your attacks to expend one Brawler die and force the creature to make a Strength saving throw as you attempt to launch it. On a failure, you throw the creature horizontally away from you in a straight line up to 30 feet if it is a smaller size category than you, 20 feet if it is the same size as you, or 10 feet if it is one size category larger than you. The creature collides with the first solid object or creature in its path, causing both targets to take bludgeoning damage equal to one roll of your Brawler die plus your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Wrecking Ball
entries: When you move at least 10 feet directly towards a creature immediately before making an attack as part of the {@action Attack} action, you can forgo the attack to expend one Brawler die as you throw your entire body at your target. Make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check contested by the target's Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) or Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check (its choice). If you succeed, the target takes bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier plus two rolls of your Brawler die, and you both land {@condition prone}.

subclassFeature.name: Way of the Artisan
entries: To a monk who follows the Way of the Artisan, taking raw materials and giving them shape and purpose is considered the noblest of pursuits. The act of creation is the purest form of meditation, an exercise in creativity and discipline that hones both mind and spirit, leading the craftsman on a never ending journey of self-discovery.

An adherent of this philosophy seeks true perfection in their craft while accepting that this is a fundamentally unachievable goal. What matters is that they grow and improve themselves along the way, creating art and tools that better the world in the process. In this manner, artisan monks are much like the objects they craft, for they too start off full of raw potential that is ultimately given shape and purpose.

entries.name: Optional Rule: Master Craftsman
entries: Whenever you start crafting a magic item using the rules in {@i Heliana's Guide to Monster Hunting} or in Chapter 3 of this book, you can expend 1 or more ki points, devoting some of your spiritual energy to the item's creation. If you do, you gain a bonus to the enchanting or manufacturing check you make at the end of the process to create the item equal to the number of ki points invested; your maximum number of ki points is reduced by the same amount until you make that check.
The maximum number of ki points you can invest at once is based on your monk level, as detailed in the table below:

subclassFeature.name: Tools of the Trade
entries: {@i 3rd-level Way of the Artisan feature.}

A worker is only as good as their tools, and yours are an extension of your own body, such is the graceful ease with which you manipulate them. You gain proficiency with two types of artisan's tools of your choice.

In addition, you can use sets of artisan's tools you're proficient with as weapons, using everyday objects in unconventional ways to catch your foes off guard. They count as monk weapons for you, but their damage die is one size larger than your Martial Arts die. (For example, if your Martial Arts die is a d6, the damage die of your artisan's tools is a d8.) They deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (your choice each time you hit with them), and when you gain the Ki-Empowered Strikes feature at 6th level, it also applies to attacks made with your artisan's tools.

subclassFeature.name: Soulful Craft
entries: {@i 3rd-level Way of the Artisan feature.}

When you say that you put a small piece of your soul into each of your creations, you mean that very literally. When you finish a long rest, you can use your artisan's tools to craft a number of Tiny objects up to your Wisdom modifier (minimum one). These objects take the shape of things you would be able to create with your tools—for example, you can use a set of woodcarver's tools to create a wooden figurine, or a set of cobbler's tools to create a shoe—and each one gains 1 ki point, which is stored within it. When you spend a ki point as part of your Deflect Missiles, Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, or Step of the Wind features, you can choose to expend that ki from one of your objects, as long as you have that object on your person. An object with no ki points in it is destroyed.
You can have a maximum number of objects created in this way equal to your Wisdom modifier. If creating a new object would put you over this limit, you must choose another object to be destroyed.
When you reach 11th level in this class, each object you create using this feature starts with 2 ki points instead of 1.

subclassFeature.name: Strike of Inspiration
entries: {@i 6th-level Way of the Artisan feature.}

You draw both power and inspiration from the pieces you create, allowing you to make your martial arts more intricate and formidable. When you spend a ki point from one of your Soulful Craft objects, you gain additional benefits depending on what you use it for:

items.name: Deflect Missiles
items.entry: You have {@b advantage} on the attack roll, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d10} damage.

items.name: Flurry of Blows
items.entry: You can replace one or both of the unarmed strikes with attacks made with your artisan's tools.

items.name: Patient Defense
items.entry: While you have the benefit of the {@action Dodge} action, if a creature misses you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to make an attack against it using your artisan's tools.

items.name: Step of the Wind
items.entry: You take both the {@action Dash} and {@action Disengage} actions.

subclassFeature.name: Art History
entries: {@i 11th-level Way of the Artisan feature.}

Every scratch and bump in a work of art tells a story, and you have become an expert at deciphering and interpreting these untold tales. You can spend 10 minutes touching a Medium or smaller creature-made object and meditating to try and discern its history. At the end of that time, make a DC 15 Wisdom check. You have {@b advantage} on the check if you are proficient with a set of artisan's tools that could be used to craft that object. If the object was created with your Soulful Craft feature, you automatically succeed on the check, but the object is destroyed in the process. If you succeed, you gain the following benefits:

items: You learn the name, creature type, and appearance of each creature that touched the object within the last 24 hours.
You learn every location the object has been in for the last 24 hours, and its trajectory, if it was moved.

You can choose a point in time within the last 24 hours, and receive a vision of events unfolding within 30 feet of the object during that time, as if you were standing in the object's space. This vision includes sight, sound, and smell, and lasts for 10 minutes or until you decide to end it.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest, and you can't use it on the same object again.

subclassFeature.name: Dying Art
entries: {@i 17th-level Way of the Artisan feature.}

Nothing lasts forever. A true artist must learn to detach themselves from what they have created and look ahead to what they have yet to bring to life. When you are reduced to 0 hit points as a result of taking damage, you can choose to draw vigor from one of your creations to fight on. If you have one of your Soulful Craft objects on your person, you can destroy it, preventing yourself from falling {@condition unconscious} and regaining {@dice 2d8} hit points for each ki point in the object. You can then immediately move up to half your speed without triggering opportunity attacks.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Charlatan
entries: Fraudster, cheat, scammer, shyster... to be a confidence trickster is to alienate yourself from society one gullible mark at a time. However, as the saying goes, "you can't cheat an honest man", so they probably deserved it, right? Plus, pulling off a perfectly planned scam can be so fun.

Charlatans are masters of planned deceit and misdirection. A Charlatan might prepare several disguises and waltz into a jewelry store, wait for a perfectly timed explosion to distract the proprietor, then replace a priceless tiara with a poorly-forged replica. Hot-footing it out the door, the Charlatan might switch outfits quicker than the proprietor can say "Oh my word, where's that priceless tiara I tricked a down-on-her luck noble into selling for a pittance?". The Charlatan might then direct the pursuing, nonplussed guards into a side alley, before fencing the heirloom and heading to a bar to celebrate a job well done.
In combat, Charlatans appear as harmless bystanders before using concealed weapons to surprise foes, striking with devastating consequences.

subclassFeature.name: Premeditation
entries: {@i 3rd-level Charlatan feature.}

Conning is like cooking, and a trickster's tools are the ingredients with which they cook up a plot, creating delectable morsels that the gullible are all too keen to consume. You gain proficiency with the {@item disguise kit|PHB} and your choice of the {@item forgery kit|PHB} or any artisan's tools. When you reach 9th, 13th, and 17th level in this class, choose another tool from this list with which to gain proficiency.
In addition, you have {@b advantage} on any ability checks made to find a willing buyer for your purloined or otherwise misappropriated goods.

subclassFeature.name: Hidden Weapons
entries: {@i 3rd-level Charlatan feature.}

Long sleeves and concealed pockets turn a Charlatan into a walking inventory of hidden paraphernalia. When you have a free hand and take the {@action Attack} action, you can turn one attack against a creature into a special hidden weapon attack using a weapon concealed upon your person.

To do so, the weapon must have the thrown and finesse properties. When you do so, make a Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check against your target's passive {@skill Perception} score. On a success, you make the attack roll with {@b advantage}.

entries.name: Teamwork Makes the Scheme Work
entries: A Charlatan can achieve much more as part of a team. Starting at 9th level, if you succeed on this Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check by 10 or more, the target becomes confused and the next attack roll against it by a creature other than you before the start of your next turn has {@b advantage}.

At 17th level, any number of attacks you make can be turned into a hidden weapon attack, instead of only one when you take the {@action Attack} action, provided the weapon has the thrown and finesse properties.

subclassFeature.name: Costume Change
entries: {@i 9th-level Charlatan feature.}

The ability to play multiple roles, and switch between them on the fly, expands the repertoire of scams a Charlatan can undertake. When you finish a long rest and you have a {@item disguise kit|PHB}, you can prepare a number of personas equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). These personas are replete with mannerisms, accents, quick-change costumes, and even different smells. They can have different clothing and other belongings, they can seem 1 foot shorter or taller than you, and they can appear thin, fat, or in between.

entries.name: Quick Change
entries: As an action, you can change into one of your personas. So long as a creature can't see you when you take this action (for example, if you are hidden or behind opaque total cover), the creature doesn't recognise you as the same person when it sees you in your new persona. If a hostile creature can't fathom how you might have escaped, it is likely to be suspicious of you, even in your new persona. A creature can use its action to inspect your appearance, discerning your disguise if it succeeds on an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier). A very suspicious creature may detain you anyway (GM's discretion), and a creature inclined to violence (such as a hunting owlbear) will likely continue to attack despite any apparent difference in appearance.

subclassFeature.name: Surprise!
entries: {@i 13th-level Charlatan feature.}

You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Charisma modifier. In addition, when you succeed on your Hidden Weapons ability check, you score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.

subclassFeature.name: Harmless
entries: {@i 17th-level Charlatan feature.}

You are a master of appearing harmless. When you roll initiative, until an enemy observes you making an attack, dealing damage to another creature, or casting a spell that affects a creature other than you, it considers you harmless. While it views you this way and it can see at least one other creature that it is hostile towards, it must first succeed on a Wisdom saving throw before it can target you with an attack or other effect (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier).

subclassFeature.name: Swarmslinger
entries: It takes a malleable and open mind to bond with a swarm of creatures; one needs to tune out the hubbub of bite-sized information and learn to listen to the group as a whole. How did you discover you could control masses of creatures? Perhaps you were a perfumier whose arrangement of scents guided the actions of bees. Maybe you were training as a bard when you found that those who liked your music most were a literal pack of rats. Or maybe you made your clothing a home for a family of creatures who now look to you for leadership. Whatever it is, your command of hordes of creatures gives you the ability to overrun any foe.

subclassFeature.name: Swarmcatcher
entries: {@i 3rd-level Swarmslinger feature.}

You understand the hivemind behaviour of a swarm and can tame swarms of creatures as if they were a single creature using your Tame Creature feature. A creature doesn't automatically succeed on its Charisma saving throw to avoid becoming your companion as a result of having the swarm type, but all other restrictions still apply. In addition, you can attempt to tame Medium swarms starting at 3rd level.

Unlike normal swarms, swarms that are your companions can regain hit points and gain temporary hit points. Swarms typically can't wield weapons, and providing armour for each individual member of a swarm would likely be prohibitively expensive; your GM has the final decision on these matters.

subclassFeature.name: Overwhelm
entries: {@i 3rd-level Swarmslinger feature.}

When it comes to swarms, quantity is better than quality. When your swarm companion makes an attack roll against a creature whose space it is sharing, hit or miss, the attack deals a minimum amount of damage equal to the ability modifier your swarm companion used to make the attack roll plus your proficiency bonus. If the swarm has half its hit points or fewer, this damage is halved. The minimum damage can't be increased in any way, other than by increasing the ability modifier or your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Swarmslinger Spells
entries: {@i 3rd-level Swarmslinger feature.}

You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Swarmslinger Spells table. Each spell counts as a tamer spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of tamer spells you know.

entries.caption: Swarmslinger Spells
footnotes: {@i {@sup **}These spells are themed around swarms; {@spell shield} might summon a swarm of bees to deflect the attack, while {@spell spirit guardians} could manifest as a swarm of flying piranhas.}
{@i {@sup ***}This must be a swarm and isn't limited to beasts.}

subclassFeature.name: Plague of Pests
entries: {@i 7th-level Swarmslinger feature.}

As an action, you magically conjure duplicates of your swarm companion's individual members. Until the start of your next turn, its size increases by two categories (up to Gargantuan, ignoring the maximum companion size restriction), and it gains temporary hit points equal to its hit point maximum. As an action on its turn, your swarm companion can make three melee weapon attacks divided as it wishes among targets in its space.
Once you use this action, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. Starting at 18th level, you can use this action twice before a rest.

subclassFeature.name: Distracting Onslaught
entries: {@i 10th-level Swarmslinger feature.}

Your swarms can interfere with those whose spaces they share. When a creature whose space your swarm companion shares makes an attack roll, your companion can use its reaction to impose {@b disadvantage} on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Swarmform
entries: {@i 14th-level Swarmslinger feature.}

As an action, you conjure a swarm of Tiny creatures that flit about you in an opaque, protective cloud. For the next minute, you have a flying speed of 30 feet; you have {@b resistance} to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage; you have {@b advantage} on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws; and you are heavily obscured. The swarm is translucent to you, allowing you to see through it.

As a bonus action on each of your turns for the feature's duration, you can launch a portion of the swarm with lethal intent, forcing a creature you can see within 120 feet of you to make a Dexterity saving throw against your tamer spell save DC. The creature takes 13 ({@damage 3d8}) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (your choice when you take the action) on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Once you use this action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

monster.name: Clockwork Wyvern
languages: understands the languages of the creature that is attuned to it but can't speak

trait.name: Afterburners
entries: The wyvern can use a bonus action to expend {@b 1 charge} and double its flying speed until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Fuel
entries: Over the course of {@b 8 hours} (which can be done as part of a long rest), the wyvern can consume high-quality charcoal worth at least 1 gp to regain {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges. If the wyvern drops to 0 charges, it falls prone and unconscious until it is recharged in this manner.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The wyvern is {@b immune} to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The wyvern has {@b advantage} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Mechanical Claw
entries: {@atk ms} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) slashing damage.

action.name: Dragonling Breath
entries: The wyvern expends {@b 3 charges} and exhales fire in a {@b 15-foot cone}. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Reconstitute
entries: The wyvern expends {@b 1 charge}, regaining {@dice 2d6 + 3} hit points.

trait.name: Photosynthesise (3/Day)
entries: As a bonus action when in direct sunlight, the ape can absorb the sun's energy, regaining 10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ape makes two Grab attacks.

action.name: Grab
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d6 + 6}) bludgeoning damage and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 17}).

action.name: Bash
entries: The ape takes two creatures grappled by it and savagely smashes them against one anGV|DMGer, forcing each one to make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw. A creature takes 25 ({@damage 3d12 + 6}) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Solar Beam {@recharge 5}
entries: With a roar, a beam of brilliant light blasts from the ape's mouth in a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long line. Each creature in the line must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 13 ({@damage 3d8}) fire damage and 13 ({@damage 3d8}) radiant damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. If the ape starts its turn in direct sunlight, this action automatically recharges.

trait.name: Eyes of the Grave
entries: The garlycanthrope always knows the location of any undead within 60 feet of it that isn't behind total cover and that isn't protected from divination magic. It growls gently in the direction to warn its owner.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While motionless, the garlycanthrope is indistinguishable from a normal head of garlic.

trait.name: Garlicol
entries: Due to the garlycanthrope's pungent aroma, vampires and their spawn have {@b disadvantage} on attack rolls while within 15 feet of it.

trait.name: Nose of the Changed
entries: The garlycanthrope can smell the presence of any shapechangers within 120 feet of it. It gives a soft bark in the direction from which their scent comes.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 0} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1 piercing damage.

action.name: Pong (1/Day)
entries: The garlycanthrope releases a puff of strong-smelling powder in a 30-foot radius. For the next 8 hours, any non-garlycanthrope creature that passes through the area has {@b disadvantage} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks made to locate and/or track any creature using smell for 1 hour.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The mimic can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Broadside
entries: If the mimic has moved less than half its speed on its turn, it can make an attack with its cannons as a bonus action. When it does so, its speed is reduced to 0 feet for the remainder of the turn.

trait.name: Ramming Charge
entries: If the mimic moves at least 15 feet straight toward a target then immediately hits it with a Ram attack, it has {@b advantage} on the attack roll and the target takes an extra 5 ({@damage 2d4}) bludgeoning damage on a hit. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 11} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Ram
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 0} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1 bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Cannons
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 20/80 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

monster.name: Giant Moth
languages: understands Common and Sylvan but can't speak

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The moth can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage.

trait.name: Perfectionist
entries: When the anveleon rolls a 1 on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, it must succeed on a {@dc 11} Wisdom saving throw or take 2 ({@damage 1d4}) psychic damage. If this damage reduces the anveleon to 0 hit points, the anveleon is stable. Conversely, when the anveleon rolls a 20 on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, it gains 2 ({@damage 1d4}) temporary hit points.

action.name: Hammer
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Hone
entries: The anveleon quickly hones a weapon that is unattended or held by a willing creature within 5 feet of it. The next attack made with that weapon before the start of the anveleon's next turn deals an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) damage on a hit.

action.name: Invisibility (1/Day)
entries: The anveleon magically turns invisible until it attacks or until its {@status concentration} ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the anveleon wears or carries is invisible with it.

trait.name: Intoxicating Fumes
entries: When the caskadon takes damage from a critical hit, potent, intoxicating vapours are released. Each creature within 5 feet of the caskadon must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of the caskadon's next turn.

trait.name: Leak
entries: While the caskadon is below half its hit point maximum, it slowly leaks some of its contents, giving other creatures advantage on ability checks made to track it or detect signs of its passage.

action.name: Trunk
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Contact Burn
entries: While the oventurer's fire is lit, a creature that starts its turn grappling the oventurer takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Tongs
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Ignition
entries: The oventurer lights a fire within itself, or puts it out. While the fire is lit, the oventurer sheds bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. In addition, other creatures have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to see or smell it.

trait.name: Bonemonger
entries: Whenever the dire bunny first sees a bone, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or become distracted until it possesses the bone or the bone is no longer in its line of sight. While distracted, it spends its movement and actions trying to get closer to the bone and sequester it. A distracted dire bunny whose owner issues a command to it repeats the saving throw at the end of its next turn, ending the effect on a success and becoming immune to the allure of any bone for the next hour.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) piercing damage.

action.name: Detect Thoughts (1/Day)
entries: The dire bunny learns the surface thoughts of one creature it can see within 30 feet of it for 1 minute. It can attempt to probe deeper into the creature's mind, using an action to force the target to make a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw, per the {@spell detect thoughts} spell.

trait.name: Arcanomagnetic Repulsion
entries: Attacks against the swarm made with ferrous weapons or ammunition are made at disadvantage.

trait.name: Superconductor
entries: If the swarm takes 10 or more fire damage in a single instance, it falls {@condition prone} and, until the end of its next turn, it is {@condition incapacitated} and its flying speed is reduced to 0 feet.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny utensil. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Jabs
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

trait.name: Arcanomagnetic Repulsion
entries: Attacks against the weapon made with ferrous weapons or ammunition are made at disadvantage.

trait.name: False Object
entries: If the weapon is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the weapon move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to discern that the weapon isn't an ordinary weapon.

trait.name: Superconductor
entries: If the weapon takes 10 or more fire damage in a single instance, it falls {@condition prone} and, until the end of its next turn, it is {@condition incapacitated} and its flying speed is reduced to 0 feet.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The weapon makes two melee attacks. It has one of the following attack options, depending on its anatomy.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) slashing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) force damage.

action.name: Maul
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d12 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) force damage.

action.name: Pike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) force damage.

trait.name: Arcanomagnetic Repulsion
entries: Attack rolls against the swarm made with ferrous weapons or ammunition are made at disadvantage.

trait.name: False Object
entries: If the swarm is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the swarm move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to discern that the swarm isn't an ordinary pile of weapons.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Small weapon. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Superconductor
entries: If the swarm takes 10 or more fire damage in a single instance, its flying speed is reduced to 0 feet, it has disadvantage on attack rolls, and it can't use its Supercharge action. These effects last until the end of the swarm's next turn.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swarm makes two Force Strikes attacks.

action.name: Force Strikes
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) force damage, or 9 ({@damage 2d8}) force damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Supercharge {@recharge 6}
entries: The swarm whirls and gathers lightning. Each creature within 10 feet of the swarm must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Unattended ferrous objects are thrown 15 feet away from the swarm. If the object is being held or worn, the creature holding or wearing it must make a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the object is thrown 15 feet away from the swarm.

trait.name: Antimagic Susceptibility
entries: The swarm is {@condition incapacitated} while in the area of an {@spell antimagic field}. If targeted by {@spell dispel magic}, the swarm must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the caster's spell save DC or fall {@condition unconscious} for 1 minute.

trait.name: False Object
entries: If the swarm is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the swarm move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to discern that the swarm isn't an ordinary pile of weapons.

trait.name: Sharp Edges
entries: At the start of the swarm's turn, it deals 4 ({@damage 1d8}) piercing damage to each creature in its space. Additionally, when a creature moves into or within the swarm's space, it takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) slashing damage for every 5 feet it moves.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Small weapon. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swarm makes two Strikes attacks.

action.name: Strikes
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) damage, or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer. Choose one of the d6s; the number rolled on that die determines the attack's damage type: 1-2, bludgeoning; 3-4, piercing; 5-6, slashing.

trait.name: Adhesive (Object Form Only)
entries: The swarm adheres to anything that touches it. A Huge or smaller creature adhered to the swarm is also {@condition grappled} by it (escape {@dc 13}). Ability checks made to escape this grapple have disadvantage.

trait.name: False Appearance (Object Form Only)
entries: If the swarm is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the swarm move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to discern that the swarm is animate.

trait.name: Grappler
entries: The swarm has advantage on attack rolls against any creature {@condition grappled} by it.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: The swarm can use its action to {@spell polymorph} into a pile of Tiny objects or back into its true, amorphous form. Its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny mimic. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Bites
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) acid damage, or 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) acid damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

monster.name: Bonemonger Phalange

trait.name: Go for the Throat
entries: The bonemonger deals an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) damage when it hits a target with a Bite attack and has advantage on the attack roll.

trait.name: Insightful Telepathy
entries: The bonemonger is constantly under the effects of the {@spell detect thoughts} spell (save {@dc 13}). As a bonus action, it can probe deeper into the mind of any creature it can detect in its lair. If the creature fails its saving throw, the bonemonger has advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the start of the bonemonger's next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the bonemonger fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Osteomancer (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the bonemonger would be reduced to 0 hit points, its current hit point total instead resets to 88 hit points, and it regains any expended uses of Legendary Resistance. Additionally, the bonemonger can now use the option in the "Mythic Actions" section for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 1,800 XP (2,900 XP total) for defeating the bonemonger after its Osteomancer activates.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bonemonger makes one Bite attack and uses Mi.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Mi
entries: The bonemonger proclaims "Mi!" at one creature it can see that can hear it within 90 feet of it. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or take 4 ({@damage 1d8}) thunder damage and 4 ({@damage 1d8}) psychic damage, and become {@condition frightened} of the bonemonger until the end of the bonemonger's next turn.

legendaryHeader: The bonemonger can take 2 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The bonemonger regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Grasping Bones
entries: The bonemonger targets a surface within 60 feet of it, causing a skeletal limb to burst out and grasp at a creature within 5 feet of the surface. The creature must make a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) slashing damage and becoming {@condition restrained} until the end of the bonemonger's next turn on a failure, or taking half as much damage and not being {@condition restrained} on a success.

legendary.name: Springstrike
entries: The bonemonger moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks and then makes one attack with its Bite.

legendary.name: Animate Bones (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The bonemonger animates bones it can see with 90 feet of it, creating a {@creature skeleton} that follows the bonemonger's telepathic commands.

mythicHeader: If the bonemonger's Osetomancer trait has activated in the last hour, it can use the option below as a legendary action.

mythic.name: Bonetide (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: A wave of animated bones ripples out from the bonemonger in either a 60-foot cone or 20-foot-radius sphere (bonemonger's choice). Each other creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) slashing damage and being knocked {@condition prone} on a failure, or taking half as much damage and not being knocked {@condition prone} on a success.

monster.name: Bonemonger Ulna

trait.name: Go for the Throat
entries: The bonemonger deals an extra 10 ({@damage 3d6}) damage when it hits a target with a Bite attack and has advantage on the attack roll.

trait.name: Insightful Telepathy
entries: The bonemonger is constantly under the effects of the {@spell detect thoughts} spell (save {@dc 17}). As a bonus action, it can probe deeper into the mind of any creature it can detect in its lair. If the creature fails its saving throw, the bonemonger has advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the start of the bonemonger's next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the bonemonger fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Osteomancer (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the bonemonger would be reduced to 0 hit points, its current hit point total instead resets to 150 hit points, it gains 60 temporary hit points as bones coalesce around it, and it regains any expended uses of Legendary Resistance. Additionally, the bonemonger can now use the options in the "Mythic Actions" section for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 13,000 XP (20,200 XP total) for defeating the bonemonger after its Osteomancer activates.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bonemonger makes one Bite attack and uses Mi.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Mi
entries: The bonemonger proclaims "Mi!" at one creature it can see that can hear it within 90 feet of it. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or take 9 ({@damage 2d8}) thunder damage and 9 ({@damage 2d8}) psychic damage, and become {@condition frightened} of the bonemonger until the end of the bonemonger's next turn.

entries: The bonemonger moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks and then makes one attack with its Bite.

legendary.name: Animate Bones (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The bonemonger animates bones it can see with 90 feet of it, creating a {@creature minotaur skeleton} that follows the bonemonger's telepathic commands.

mythicHeader: If the bonemonger's Osetomancer trait has activated in the last hour, it can use the options below as a legendary action.

mythic.name: Bare the Throat
entries: The bonemonger uses its Grasping Bones and Springstrike legendary action options.

entries: A wave of animated bones ripples out from the bonemonger in either a 60-foot cone or 20-foot-radius sphere (bonemonger's choice). Each other creature in the area must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) slashing damage and being knocked {@condition prone} on a failure, or taking half as much damage and not being knocked {@condition prone} on a success.

monster.name: Bonemonger Femur

trait.name: Go for the Throat
entries: The bonemonger deals an extra 21 ({@damage 6d6}) damage when it hits a target with a Bite attack and has advantage on the attack roll.

Additionally, the bonemonger can now use the options in the "Mythic Actions" section for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 41,000 XP (63,000 XP total) for defeating the bonemonger after its Osteomancer activates.

entries: The bonemonger makes one Bite attack and uses Mi.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d6 + 5}) piercing damage.

The creature must succeed on a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw or take 13 ({@damage 3d8}) thunder damage and 13 ({@damage 3d8}) psychic damage, and become {@condition frightened} of the bonemonger until the end of the bonemonger's next turn.

legendaryHeader: The bonemonger can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The bonemonger regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

mythic.name: Bare the Throat
entries: The bonemonger uses its Grasping Bones and Springstrike legendary action options.

mythic.name: Bonetide (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: A wave of animated bones ripples out from the bonemonger in either a 90-foot cone or 30-foot-radius sphere (bonemonger's choice). Each other creature in the area must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw, taking 52 ({@damage 15d6}) slashing damage and being knocked {@condition prone} on a failure, or taking half as much damage and not being knocked {@condition prone} on a success.

monster.name: Brain Devourer
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Emotional Wall
entries: The brain devourer has advantage on saving throws it makes to avoid or end the {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened} conditions.

action.name: Neuropathic Touch
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature that has a brain. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) psychic damage.

action.name: Devour Emotion
entries: The brain devourer feasts on the emotions of one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 12} Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) psychic damage and the brain devourer consumes one of its emotions. The brain devourer chooses an emotion from the following list and the target suffers the associated effect. The brain devourer can't choose an emotion that a target has already lost through this action.

items.name: Compassion
entries: The target can't take the Help action or actions that would grant another creature hit points or temporary hit points.

items.name: Fear
entries: The target is immune to the {@condition frightened} condition and can't willingly move to a space beyond 30 feet of the brain devourer.

items.name: Rage
entries: The target can't take reactions and has disadvantage on its attack rolls.

items.name: Shame
entries: The target can't take the Disengage action and attack rolls made against it have advantage.

The effect lasts for 1 minute or until the brain devourer dies, after which the target regains its lost emotion. If all four of a target's emotions are consumed this way, it is {@condition incapacitated} and has a speed of 0 feet until it regains at least one emotion. A {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar effect restores an affected target's emotions.

bonus.name: Consume Brain
entries: The brain devourer consumes the brain of one humanoid corpse in its space. It learns everything the humanoid knew in life and gains 10 temporary hit points. Until the start of its next turn, the brain devourer has advantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws.

monster.name: Storm of Brain Devourers
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The storm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the storm can move through any opening large enough for a Small brain devourer. The storm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points except through its Consume Brain bonus action.

action.name: Dazing Touches
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}21 ({@damage 6d6}) psychic damage, or 10 ({@damage 3d6}) psychic damage if the storm has half of its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Mind Storm {@recharge 5}
entries: The storm unleashes a wave of psychic energy. Each creature within 15 feet of the storm must make a {@dc 13} Intelligence saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 6d8}) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more is also {@condition incapacitated} until the end of the storm's next turn.

bonus.name: Consume Brain
entries: The storm consumes the brain of one humanoid corpse in its space. It learns everything the humanoid knew in life and gains 20 temporary hit points. Until the start of its next turn, the storm has advantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws.

monster.name: Broodling
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The broodling can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Hive Mind
entries: Broodlings' minds connect to other broodlings within 5 feet of them, forming a chain of thought. The broodling gains a +3 bonus to Intelligence checks and saving throws, and to its Entrance save DC for each other broodling in such a chain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The broodling makes two attacks: one with its Bite and one with its Sting.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Sting
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}1 piercing damage and the creature must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws until the end of the broodling's next turn.

action.name: Entrance (1/Day)
entries: The broodling targets one creature it can see within 10 feet of it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 6} Wisdom saving throw or be magically charmed by the broodling until the broodling dies or until it is on a different plane of existence from the target. The charmed target is under the broodling's control and can't take reactions.

Whenever the charmed target takes damage, the target can repeat the saving throw. On a success, the effect ends. No more than once every 24 hours, the target can also repeat the saving throw when it is 30 or more feet from the broodling.

monster.name: Swarm of Broodlings
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The swarm can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny broodling. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Bites and Stings
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}21 ({@damage 6d6}) piercing damage, or 10 ({@damage 3d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws until the end of the swarm's next turn.

action.name: Mass Entrance (1/Day)
entries: The swarm targets any number of creatures it is aware of within 10 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be magically charmed by the swarm. A charmed target falls under the swarm's control and can't take reactions.

A charmed target can repeat the saving throw whenever it takes damage or whenever the swarm takes 15 or more damage in a single instance. On a success, the effect ends on it. No more than once every 24 hours, the target can also repeat the saving throw when it is 30 or more feet away from the swarm. The charm ends if the swarm dies or is on a different plane of existence from a charmed target.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The cherub doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

action.name: Shining Bolt
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) radiant damage.

action.name: Celestial Blessing (1/Day)
entries: The cherub casts the {@spell bless} spell without requiring material components and using Charisma as its spellcasting ability, targeting only one creature it touches. The spell ends early if the cherub is no longer touching the target.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The swarm doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny cherub. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Shining Bolts
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) radiant damage, or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) radiant damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Celestial Protection (1/Day)
entries: The swarm casts the {@spell protection from evil and good} spell without requiring material components and using Charisma as its spellcasting ability, targeting one creature in its space. The spell ends early if the target leaves the swarm's space.

reaction.name: Radiant Ward {@recharge 5}
entries: When an ally within 5 feet of the swarm is targeted by an attack and the swarm can see the attacker, the swarm imposes disadvantage on the attack roll. This reaction has no effect if the attacker can't be {@condition blinded}.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: The eclipse can't use its blindsight while {@condition deafened}.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: If the eclipse is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the eclipse move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to discern that the eclipse isn't an ordinary rock formation such as a cluster of stalactites or stalagmites.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The eclipse can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the eclipse can move through any opening large enough for a Small darkmantle. The eclipse can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The eclipse makes two Crush attacks. If both attacks hit the same target and that target is no more than one size larger than the eclipse, the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}) and the eclipse's speed is reduced to 0 feet. While {@condition grappled} this way, a target's head is engulfed, it is {@condition blinded}, and it is unable to breathe.

action.name: Crush
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the eclipse's space. {@h}21 ({@damage 6d6}) bludgeoning damage, or 10 ({@damage 3d6}) bludgeoning damage if the eclipse has half of its hit points or fewer.

bonus.name: Darkness Aura {@recharge 6}
entries: A 10-foot radius of magical darkness extends from the eclipse, moves with it, and spreads around corners. {@sense Darkvision} can't penetrate this darkness, and no natural light can illuminate it. If any of the darkness overlaps with an area of light created by a spell of 2nd level or lower, the spell creating the light is dispelled.

trait.name: Morbid Curiosity
entries: The death shroud is easily distracted by fresh corpses.
