Whenever it first detects a corpse that has been dead for less than 1 hour (and that it didn't witness die), it must succeed on a {@dc 11} Wisdom saving throw or get distracted. While distracted, it spends its movement and actions trying to get close to, and inspect, the corpse for a cause of death. A distracted death shroud in a corpse's space uses its action to make Intelligence ({@skill Medicine}) checks (DC decided by the GM). On a success, it stops being distracted and becomes immune to the allure of that corpse forever.

trait.name: Turn Immunity
entries: The death shroud is immune to effects that turn undead.

action.name: Slap
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Lich Slap
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 30 ft., one creature. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage and the target can't regain hit points until the start of the death shroud's next turn.

trait.name: Morbid Curiosity
entries: The swarm is easily distracted by fresh corpses. Whenever it detects a corpse that has been dead for less than 1 hour (and that it didn't witness die), it must succeed on a {@dc 11} Wisdom saving throw or become distracted. While distracted, it spends its movement trying to get close to the corpse and use its Inspect Corpse action.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny death shroud. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Turn Immunity
entries: The swarm is immune to effects that turn undead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swarm makes two Slaps attacks.

action.name: Slaps
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 0 ft, one target in the swarm's space. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) bludgeoning damage or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Lich Slap {@recharge 5}
entries: Each creature within 5 feet of the swarm must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) necrotic damage and its hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken. If a creature's hit point maximum is reduced to 0 by this effect, the creature dies. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest.

bonus.name: Inspect Corpse
entries: The swarm makes an Intelligence ({@skill Medicine}) check (DC determined by the GM) to inspect a corpse within 5 feet of it for a cause of death. On a success, the swarm is no longer distracted and becomes immune to its morbid curiosity regarding that corpse forever.

trait.name: Acidic Form
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the dropling deals 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage to any creature or nonmagical object it is attached to.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The dropling can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The dropling can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The dropling doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage, and the dropling attaches to the target. While the dropling is attached, it has half cover, it moves with the target, and it can't attack another target. A creature within 5 feet of an attached dropling can use an action to remove it by making a successful {@dc 12} Strength check. The dropling can also use 5 feet of movement to detach itself from a target.

trait.name: Acidic Form
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the swarm deals 9 ({@damage 2d8}) acid damage to each creature and nonmagical object in its space.

trait.name: Amorphous Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The swarm can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Pseudopods
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) bludgeoning damage, or 4 ({@damage 1d8}) bludgeoning damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) acid damage. The target is also {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}), and it is {@condition restrained} until this grapple ends.

bonus.name: Divide (Medium Swarm Only)
entries: If the swarm has 10 or more hit points, it splits into two Small swarms. Each new swarm retains the statistics of the original swarm, except it has hit points equal to half the original swarm's, rounded down. The new swarms can't occupy each other's space.

bonus.name: Recombine (Small Swarm Only)
entries: The swarm spends 5 feet of movement and moves into the space of another Small swarm of black droplings created by the Divide bonus action. Both swarms combine into one Medium swarm. The new swarm retains the statistics of both swarms, except it has the combined hit points of both swarms. If the original swarms were under any effects, such as those of a spell or an ability score reduction, those effects are transferred to the new swarm.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The dropling can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The dropling can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The dropling doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage.

trait.name: Amorphous Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The swarm can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Pseudopods
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) bludgeoning damage, or 4 ({@damage 1d8}) bludgeoning damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) acid damage. The target is also {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 12}), and it is {@condition restrained} until this grapple ends.

bonus.name: Divide (Medium Swarm Only)
entries: If the swarm has 10 or more hit points, it splits into two Small swarms. Each new swarm retains the statistics of the original swarm, except it has hit points equal to half the original swarm's, rounded down. The new swarms can't occupy each other's space.

bonus.name: Recombine (Small Swarm Only)
entries: The swarm spends 5 feet of movement and moves into the space of another Small swarm of ochre droplings created by the Divide bonus action. Both swarms combine into one Medium swarm. The new swarm retains the statistics of both swarms, except it has the combined hit points of both swarms. If the original swarms were under any effects, such as those of a spell or an ability score reduction, those effects are transferred to the new swarm.

trait.name: Amorphous Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Freeze
entries: If the swarm takes cold damage, it partially freezes. Until the end of its next turn, its speed is halved and its Misty Form trait doesn't function.

trait.name: Misty Form
entries: The swarm's body distorts vision. Even when the swarm is in plain sight, it takes a successful {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check to spot the swarm if it has neither moved nor attacked. Additionally, a creature in the swarm's space has disadvantage on attack rolls.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Pelt
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}10 ({@damage 4d4}) bludgeoning damage or 5 ({@damage 2d4}) bludgeoning damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

trait.name: Amorphous Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The swarm sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius.

trait.name: Kindling Form
entries: A creature that starts its turn in the swarm's space must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its turn. A creature immune to fire damage automatically succeeds on this saving throw.

trait.name: Lightweight
entries: The swarm automatically fails any saving throws it makes to resist being pushed by wind. Its Kindling Form trait doesn't function while the swarm is in an area of moderate or stronger wind (15 miles per hour or more).

trait.name: Water Susceptibility
entries: For every 5 feet the swarm moves in water, or for every gallon of water splashed on it, it takes 1 cold damage.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Singe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}21 ({@damage 6d6}) fire damage, or 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: If the swarm is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the swarm move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to discern that the swarm isn't a pile of rocks.

trait.name: Rubble Form
entries: If a creature starts its turn in the swarm's space, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny pebble. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Slams
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}10 ({@damage 4d4}) bludgeoning damage, or 5 ({@damage 2d4}) bludgeoning damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 12}).

trait.name: Amorphous Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Arcing Form
entries: A creature that starts its turn in the swarm's space must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition paralyzed} until the end of its turn or until it takes any damage. A creature immune to lightning damage automatically succeeds on this saving throw.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Shocks
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}21 ({@damage 6d6}) lightning damage, or 10 ({@damage 3d6}) lightning damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, and the target can't take reactions until the start of the swarm's next turn.

monster.name: Rumble of Fulgurites
languages: understands Auran and Terran but can't speak

trait.name: Charged Body
entries: A creature that touches the swarm or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) lightning damage.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Small fulgurite. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The swarm doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Shards
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage, or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 11 ({@damage 2d10}) lightning damage.

bonus.name: Lightning Streak {@recharge 5}
entries: The swarm teleports up to 30 feet to a space of its choice. Each creature within 10 feet of the space the swarm appears in must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) lightning damage, or 11 ({@damage 2d10}) lightning damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: If the greyshade strangler is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the greyshade strangler move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) check to discern that the greyshade strangler isn't an ordinary patch of plants.

trait.name: Heartbeat Sense
entries: The greyshade strangler knows the location of any creature with a heartbeat within 30 feet of itself.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}1 piercing damage and the greyshade strangler attaches to the target. Until removed, it moves with the target and can't attack another target. A creature within 5 feet of the greyshade strangler can remove it by making a successful {@dc 8} Strength check. The greyshade strangler can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the swarm remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal patch of thorny undergrowth. A creature with a passive Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) score of 13 or higher can identify the swarm as a swarm of greyshade stranglers when the creature comes within 60 feet of the swarm and can see it.

trait.name: Heartbeat Sense
entries: The swarm knows the location of any creature with a heartbeat within 30 feet of itself.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny greyshade strangler. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Thorns
entries: A creature that starts its turn in the swarm's space takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) slashing damage.

action.name: Bites
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft, one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer. Additionally, the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 9}).

reaction.name: Snap
entries: When a creature with a heartbeat enters the swarm's space, the swarm makes one Bites attack against it.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The housedrake has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Flametongue {@recharge 6}
entries: The housedrake fills its mouth with fire. Until the end of its turn, its Bite attack deals an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage on a hit, and the housedrake can use its action to harmlessly heat a nonmagical object it touches.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The swarm has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Small housedrake. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Bites
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target in the swarm's space. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage, or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 9 ({@damage 1d10 + 1d6}) damage. The damage type is determined by the value on the {@dice d10}: 1-2, poison; 3-4, cold; 5-6, acid; 7-8, lightning; 9-10, fire.

action.name: Elemental Wreath {@recharge 5}
entries: The swarm exhales elemental energy in a swirling maelstrom around itself. Each creature in the swarm's space must make a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw, taking 26 ({@damage 1d10 + 6d6}) damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The damage type is determined by the value on the {@dice d10}: 1-2, poison; 3-4, cold; 5-6, acid; 7-8, lightning; 9-10, fire.

trait.name: Devil's Sight
entries: Magical darkness doesn't impede the swarm's darkvision.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The swarm has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny imp. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Stings
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target in the swarm's space. {@h}10 ({@damage 4d4}) piercing damage, or 5 ({@damage 2d4}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) poison damage.

bonus.name: Mass Shapechange (1/Day)
entries: The swarm merges and casts {@spell polymorph} on itself, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability and requiring no material components, transforming into a {@creature giant rat}, {@creature giant raven}, or {@creature giant spider}. It retains its ability to speak.

monster.name: Swarm of Skeletal Imps
languages: understands Common and Infernal but can't speak

trait.name: Death Leech
entries: When a creature within 15 feet of the swarm is reduced to 0 hit points, the swarm regains 7 ({@damage 2d6}) hit points.

trait.name: Devil's Sight
entries: Magical darkness doesn't impede the swarm's darkvision.

trait.name: Liftoff
entries: The swarm can drag a creature it is grappling without having its speed halved.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The swarm has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny imp. The swarm can't gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swarm makes one attack and attempts to grapple one creature in its space.

action.name: Necrotic Stings
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage, or 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer. The swarm regains hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting (Psionics)
headerEntries: As long as it has more than half of its hit points remaining, the swarm casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 12}):

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The swarm has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny space hamster. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Bites
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 0 ft, one target in the swarm's space. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage, or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) psychic damage.

bonus.name: Telekinetic Scamper {@recharge 5}
entries: The swarm flies up to 20 feet.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The pitcrow doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The pitcrow can mimic simple sounds it has heard, such as a person whispering, a baby crying, or an animal chittering. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful {@dc 12} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The pitcrow has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the pitcrow's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Vicious Mockery
entries: The pitcrow targets one creature that can hear it within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or take 5 ({@damage 2d4}) psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The swarm doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Multitudinous Mimicry
entries: The swarm can simultaneously mimic one or more simple sounds it has heard, such as a person whispering, a crowd chattering, animals chittering, horses galloping, or an orchestra playing. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful {@dc 12} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The swarm has advantage on attack rolls against another creature if at least one of the swarm's allies is within 5 feet of the target creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny pitcrow. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swarm makes two Beaks attacks. Alternatively, it uses Vicious Mockery four times, or two times if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Beaks
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +4} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) piercing damage, or 9 ({@damage 2d8}) piercing damage if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Vicious Mockery
entries: The swarm targets one creature that can hear it within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 3d4}) psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The murder doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Multitudinous Mimicry
entries: The murder can simultaneously mimic one or more simple sounds it has heard, such as a person whispering, a crowd chattering, animals chittering, horses galloping, or an orchestra playing. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The murder has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the murder's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The murder can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny pitcrow. The murder can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The murder makes two Swarm of Beaks attacks. Alternatively, it uses Vicious Mockery six times, or three times if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Swarm of Beaks
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}27 ({@damage 6d8}) piercing damage, or 13 ({@damage 3d8}) piercing damage if the murder has half its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Vicious Mockery
entries: The murder targets one creature that can hear it within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 3d4}) psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Startleable
entries: The spiketooth has disadvantage on saving throws made to avoid the {@condition frightened} condition.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage. If the spiketooth moved at least 20 feet straight toward the target immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must then succeed on a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Startleable
entries: The bristle has disadvantage on saving throws made to avoid the {@condition frightened} condition. When {@condition frightened}, the bristle uses all its movement and its Stampede action to move in a random direction (GM's discretion).

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The bristle can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the bristle can move through any opening large enough for a Small spiketooth. The bristle can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bristle makes two Bites attacks.

action.name: Bites
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage, or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage if the bristle has half of its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) piercing damage or 9 ({@damage 2d8}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer. If the bristle moved at least 20 feet straight toward the target immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) piercing damage.

action.name: Stampede {@recharge 4}
entries: The bristle moves in a straight line up to its speed. Each creature whose space it moves through must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone} if it is Large or smaller. On a successful save, a creature takes only half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. This damage is reduced to 11 ({@damage 2d10}) if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer.

trait.name: Force Weakness
entries: When the swarm takes 6 or more force damage in a single instance, it must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw, or it falls {@condition prone} (temporarily overriding its condition immunity) and can take either an action or bonus action on its next turn, not both.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny steel dart. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Beaks
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 0 ft, one target in the swarm's space. {@h}10 ({@damage 4d4}) piercing damage, or 5 ({@damage 2d4}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) thunder damage.

reaction.name: Doom Song
entries: The swarm emits a terrifying song out to 30 feet. Creatures in the area that hear the song must succeed on a {@dc 11} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of the swarm for 1 minute. A {@condition frightened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful, the creature is immune to this swarm's Doom Song for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Good Sense
entries: The sun jelly knows the location of any good creatures within 30 feet of it.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The sun jelly sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.

action.name: Tendril
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 0} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}1 radiant damage.

trait.name: Good Sense
entries: The swarm knows the location of any good creatures within 30 feet of it.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The swarm sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny sun jelly. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Tendrils
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 0 ft, one target in the swarm's space. {@h}10 ({@damage 4d4}) radiant damage, or 5 ({@damage 2d4}) radiant damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Celestial Flare {@recharge 5}
entries: The swarm glows with brilliant light, which has no effect on celestials or good creatures. Each other creature within 5 feet of the swarm must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or take 13 ({@damage 3d8}) radiant damage. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more is also {@condition blinded} until the end of the swarm's next turn.

trait.name: Death Burst
entries: When the sunsprite dies, it explodes in a burst of light. Each creature within 5 feet of the sunsprite must make a {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: When the sunsprite is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: When the sunsprite isn't invisible, it sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1 piercing damage.

action.name: Sunbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +5} to hit, range 40/160 ft., one target. {@h}1 piercing damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) radiant damage.

action.name: Invisibility
entries: The sunsprite magically turns invisible until it attacks, or until its {@status concentration} ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the sunsprite wears or carries is invisible with it.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The swarm doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The swarm sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny sunsprite. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Volatile Bursts
entries: Whenever the swarm takes 5 or more damage in a single instance, it releases an explosive burst of light. Each creature within 5 feet of the swarm must make a {@dc 11} Dexterity saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Daggers
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 0 ft, one target in the swarm's space. {@h}10 ({@damage 4d4}) piercing damage, or 5 ({@damage 2d4}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Sunbow Volley
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +6} to hit, range 40/160 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage, or 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) radiant damage.

action.name: Venomous Sting
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +3} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}1 piercing damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 9} Constitution saving throw or take 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage. A target that fails this save by 5 or more and takes any poison damage from it also feels a fierce itch around the sting for 13 ({@damage 3d8}) hours.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny twilight wasp. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Venomous Stings
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 0 ft, one target in the swarm's space. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage, or 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer. The target must make a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A target that fails this save by 5 or more and takes this poison damage also feels a fierce itch around the sting for 13 ({@damage 3d8}) hours. While the itch persists, the target has disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand} or {@skill Stealth}) checks.

action.name: Hypnotic Dance {@recharge 5}
entries: If the swarm is in dim light, it forms a hypnotic display. Each creature within 10 feet of the swarm that can see the swarm must succeed on a {@dc 10} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} for 1 minute or until the creature takes damage. While {@condition charmed} in this way, the creature is {@condition incapacitated} and has a speed of 0 feet. Once a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to this swarm's Hypnotic Dance for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Stinger
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) piercing damage. The target must make a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The flight can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the flight can move through any opening large enough for a Small wyvnkin. The flight can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The flight makes one Bites and Claws attack, and one Stingers attack.

action.name: Bites and Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 6d6}) slashing damage, or 10 ({@damage 3d6}) slashing damage if the flight has half of its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Stingers
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage, or 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer. The target must make a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. On a failed save, a target is also {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute, and it is {@condition blinded} while {@condition poisoned} in this way. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the poison on itself on a success.

spellcasting.name: Spells
headerEntries: The apostle is an 11th-level spellcaster. It uses Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}, +7 to hit with spell attacks). The apostle has the following spells prepared:

trait.name: Life of the Party
entries: The apostle uses its Wisdom modifier instead of Charisma when making Charisma checks and saving throws (+4 to checks, +7 to saving throws, included in the stat block).

action.name: Flail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Flare
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit +7} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d8}) radiant damage. On a critical hit, the target is covered in glittering mystical light, granting advantage on attack rolls against the target until the end of the apostle's next turn.

action.name: Sacred Flame
entries: The target must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 13 ({@damage 3d8}) radiant damage. Targets gain no benefit from cover for this saving throw.

action.name: Festival of Blossoms (2/Day)
entries: The apostle chooses up to three creatures it can see within 30 feet of it. Until the end of the apostle's next turn, each creature deals 4 ({@damage 1d8}) radiant damage the first time it hits with an attack on its turn. In addition, at the start of each creature's next turn, it regains 4 ({@damage 1d8}) hit points.

reaction.name: Reveler's Resilience (4/Day)
entries: Trigger: The apostle fails a saving throw made to maintain its {@status concentration}. Response: The apostle rerolls the saving throw, using the new result.

trait.name: Ki (6/Short or Long Rest)
entries: The journeyman can use its Ki to power its Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind bonus actions, as well as its Deflect Missiles reaction.

trait.name: Unarmored Defense
entries: While it isn't wearing armour or wielding a shield, the journeyman's AC includes its Wisdom modifier.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The journeyman makes two Carpenter's Tools attacks. It can replace one attack with Flurry.

action.name: Carpenter's Tools
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) bludgeoning or piercing damage (journeyman's choice when it makes the attack).

action.name: Flurry (2/Day)
entries: The journeyman makes one Carpenter's Tools attack against up to three different creatures within its reach. It gains a +2 bonus to AC until the start of its next turn.

bonus.name: Flurry of Blows
entries: After taking the Multiattack action, the journeyman uses Ki to make two Carpenter's Tools attacks.

bonus.name: Patient Defense
entries: The journeyman uses Ki to take the Dodge action. If a creature misses the Journeyman with an attack roll before the start of its next turn, the journeyman can make one Carpenter's Tools attack against it as a reaction.

bonus.name: Step of the Wind
entries: The journeyman uses Ki to take the Dash and Disengage actions.

reaction.name: Deflect Missiles
entries: Trigger: The journeyman is hit by a ranged weapon attack. Response: It reduces the attack's damage by 14 ({@damage 1d10 + 9}). If the damage is reduced to 0, the journeyman catches the missile if it is small enough for it to hold in one hand and it has at least one hand free. If caught in this way, the journeyman can use Ki to make a ranged attack ({@hit +5} to hit, range 20/60 ft.) with the weapon or piece of ammunition, as part of the same reaction, dealing 10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) damage of the weapon or ammunition's type on a hit.

trait.name: Brewer
entries: The brewmaster's Improvised Weapon attacks have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls (included in the attacks), and count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

trait.name: Unarmored Defense
entries: While it isn't wearing armour, the brewmaster's AC includes its Constitution modifier.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The brewmaster makes two Maul attacks and one Improvised Weapon attack. It can replace one Maul attack with Shockwave.

action.name: Maul
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d12 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus an extra 2 bludgeoning damage if the brewmaster is raging.

action.name: Improvised Weapon
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) damage (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing depending on the weapon; GM's discretion) plus an extra 2 damage if the brewmaster is raging.

action.name: Shockwave (2/Day)
entries: The brewmaster slams its maul into a surface. Each creature within 10 feet of the brewmaster must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage and be pushed 10 feet directly away from the brewmaster.

bonus.name: Brewmaster's Mead Rage (4/Day)
entries: The brewmaster enters a rage. For 1 minute, the brewmaster has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage; gains {@dice 1d4} temporary hit points at the start of each of its turns; increases its walking speed by 10 feet; and deals an extra 2 damage with its Strength-based weapon attacks (included in the attacks). These effects end early if the brewmaster is knocked {@condition unconscious}, or if it ends its turn without having attacked a hostile creature or taken damage since the end of its last turn.

trait.name: Brawler Techniques (4/Day)
entries: The brawler has two special techniques that it can perform:

items: {@b Smash.} When the brawler hits a creature with its Improvised Weapon, it smashes the object used for the attack, dealing 2 ({@damage 1d4}) damage of the attack's type to the target and the object, potentially destroying the object.

{@b Takedown.} When the brawler makes a Knuckle Duster attack against a creature it is grappling, it can force the target to make a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The brawler makes two attacks with any combination of Knuckle Duster, Improvised Weapon, or Grapple. It can attempt to escape a grapple in place of any of these attacks.

action.name: Knuckle Duster
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Improvised Weapon
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit +4} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) damage (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing depending on the weapon; GM's discretion). This attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

action.name: Grapple
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}The target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 12}). If the brawler is grappling two creatures, it can't make Knuckle Duster or Improvised Weapon attacks against other creatures.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: If the swarm is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the swarm move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) check to discern that the swarm isn't an ordinary patch of thorny plants.

trait.name: Thorny
entries: At the start of the swarm's turn, it deals 2 ({@damage 1d4}) piercing damage to each creature in its space. Additionally, when a creature moves into or within the swarm's space, it takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) piercing damage for every 5 feet it moves.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny brambleweed. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Thorn Lashes
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +3} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage, or 3 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage if the swarm has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) piercing damage.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: If the thicket is motionless at the start of combat, it has advantage on its initiative roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the swarm move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) check to discern that the thicket isn't an ordinary patch of thorny plants.

trait.name: Thorny Overgrowth
entries: At the start of the thicket's turn, it deals 7 ({@damage 3d4}) piercing damage to each creature in its space. Additionally, when a creature moves into or within the thicket's space, it takes 7 ({@damage 3d4}) piercing damage for every 5 feet it moves.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The thicket can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the thicket can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny brambleweed. The thicket can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Thorn Lashes
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +6} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the thicket's space. {@h}21 ({@damage 6d6}) bludgeoning damage, or 10 ({@damage 3d6}) bludgeoning damage if the thicket has half of its hit points or fewer, plus 7 ({@damage 3d4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Wall Form (1/Day)
entries: The thicket arranges itself into an opaque wall of thorns that originates from one 5-foot space it currently occupies and extends out in a 5-foot-wide line that is up to 15 feet tall and up to 45 feet long. While in this form, a creature that attempts to move into or through the thicket's space for the first time on a turn must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength check to do so; if the creature fails the check, its movement ends in the thicket's space and it becomes {@condition grappled} by the thicket (escape {@dc 15}). The thicket reverts to its original form after 10 minutes, if it uses an action to do so, or if it dies. When the thicket reverts, it reforms in the space in the middle of the wall and ceases grappling creatures.

monsterFluff.name: Anveleon

entries: Ze anveleon? Oh ja, I love havink it around! Stubbing mein toe on ein invisible anvil is sehr fun! Und ven it keeps everyone up at night for three days straight hammerink avay to forge ein simple teaspoon? Simply wunderbar!

Clever creatures with an affinity for metalworking, anveleons can frequently be found blending into the walls of smithies and watching masters at work, hoping to pick up some new tricks. Their hardened scales and unusual shape allow them to use their own bodies in the pursuit of their craft, to which they devote themselves with unapologetic perfectionism. Indeed, an anveleon can spend days making minor tweaks and improvements to one of its creations, only to undo them all and start anew the next.

Anveleons often carry tools of their own making, which they wield using their tails against foes bearing poorer quality equipment with ill-concealed enthusiasm. In battle, however, they favour tending to their allies' weapons, ensuring that they are in perfect condition. Your blades will never lack for sharpness with an anveleon around!
If any of the anveleon's traits or actions require a saving throw, it is always against the anveleon's save DC, as calculated below.

entries.name: Save DC
entries.text: 8 + the tamer's proficiency bonus + the anveleon's Constitution modifier

entries.name: Monster Trainer
entries: In addition to the improvements you can normally give your companions, you can choose to grant the anveleon the following improvements using your Monster Trainer feature:

entries.name: Forgemaster
Prerequisite:
entries: Become a tamer's companion.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (tamer).
The anveleon can be used as a set of smith's tools, and its tamer has advantage on any ability checks using it in this way.

In addition, over the course of a short or long rest, the anveleon can magically enhance a metallic weapon or piece of ammunition. The next attack roll made with that weapon or ammunition is made with advantage. A weapon or piece of ammunition loses this benefit when the anveleon's tamer finishes a long rest.

entries.name: Growth I
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The anveleon's size increases to Small, its Hit Die size increases to a d6 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its attacks increases to a d6.

entries.name: Thunderstrike
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

As an action, the anveleon can make a Hammer attack against itself, which automatically hits, producing a thunderous ring. Each creature within 5 feet of the anveleon must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take thunder damage equal to the damage the anveleon dealt to itself. When the anveleon's tamer reaches 9th level in the tamer class, the thunder damage increases to double the damage the anveleon dealt to itself.

entries.name: Blacksmiting
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (bonus action).

As a bonus action, the anveleon can cast the {@spell branding smite} spell on a willing creature within 5 feet of it. The creature benefits from the spell as if it had been the one to cast it, but the anveleon maintains {@status concentration} on the spell for it. Once the anveleon casts this spell, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Multiattack
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
As an action, the anveleon makes two attacks.

entries.name: Whetstone I
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion), Active (bonus action).
The anveleon can use its Hone action as a bonus action. When the anveleon's tamer reaches 9th level in the tamer class, the extra damage of the Hone action increases to 3 ({@damage 1d6}).

entries.name: Autotomy
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
The anveleon can use an action to shed its tail, automatically ending the {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained} condition on itself. The tail grows back immediately after.

entries.name: Growth II
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer, Growth I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The anveleon's size increases to Medium, its Hit Die size increases to a d8 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its attacks increases to a d8.

entries.name: Well-Tempered I
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).
The anveleon gains a +2 bonus to AC while it isn't wearing armour.

entries.name: Camouflage
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
The anveleon can use its Invisibility action at will.

entries.name: Whetstone II
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer, Whetstone I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The extra damage of the anveleon's Hone action increases to 4 ({@damage 1d8}). Additionally, when the anveleon hones a weapon, the next attack made with that weapon before the start of the anveleon's next turn scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

entries.name: Well-Tempered II
Prerequisite:
entries: 17th-level tamer, Well-Tempered I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The anveleon gains a +2 bonus to AC while it isn't wearing armour. This improvement is in addition to the bonus from Well-Tempered I. Additionally, the anveleon gains resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

monsterFluff.name: Caskadon

entries: Caskadons are essentially the perfect party guests. They love to have fun, come with free booze, and if a brawl breaks out... Well, one way or another, when a caskadon joins the party, someone's getting smashed!

Good times follow caskadons wherever they go. With a penchant for harmless mischief and an infectious, jubilant spirit, caskadons are welcome in taverns and feast halls the world over. These gentle and affable creatures prefer to settle conflicts over a couple of stout drinks, but aren't above barreling into a fray and putting some rabble rousers in their place with their mighty trunks. Woe to those who incur the wrath of a caskadon!
If any of the caskadon's traits or actions require a saving throw, it is always against the caskadon's save DC, as calculated below.

entries.name: Save DC
entries.text: 8 + the tamer's proficiency bonus + the caskadon's Constitution modifier

entries.name: Monster Trainer
entries: In addition to the improvements you can normally give your companions, you can choose to grant the caskadon the following improvements using your Monster Trainer feature:

entries.name: Bottoms Up
Prerequisite:
entries: Become a tamer's companion.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (tamer).
The caskadon can be used as a set of brewer's supplies, and its tamer has advantage on any ability checks using it in this way.

In addition, over the course of a short or long rest, the caskadon can brew an invigorating beverage for a creature to enjoy. A creature that drinks this beverage immediately after the rest gains one Brew Die, which is a {@dice d4} and lasts until the creature finishes a short or long rest. When the creature makes an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, it can expend its Brew Die to roll it and add the number rolled to the result.

entries.name: Growth I
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The caskadon's size increases to Small, its Hit Die size increases to a d6 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its Trunk attack increases to a d6.

entries.name: Happy Hour
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

As an action, the caskadon can spray alcohol from its trunk in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be soaked with alcohol until the end of its next turn. The next time a soaked creature takes fire damage, it takes an extra 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage. In addition, a creature that fails its save suffers an effect depending on the type of alcohol sprayed (tamer's choice):

items.name: Sticky Beer
entries: The creature's speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Tart Wine
entries: The creature takes {@damage 2d6} acid damage.

items.name: Potent Scotch
entries: The creature is {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.
Once the caskadon uses this action, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a short or long rest.
When the caskadon's tamer reaches 5th level in the tamer class, the extra fire damage increases to 14 ({@damage 4d6}).

entries.name: Growth II
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer, Growth I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The caskadon's size increases to Medium, its Hit Die size increases to a d8 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its Trunk attack increases to a d8.

entries.name: Multiattack
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
As an action, the caskadon makes two attacks.

entries.name: Bar Brawler I
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).
The caskadon can replace an attack it makes with a grapple, and has advantage on ability checks made to grapple creatures smaller than itself.

In addition, the caskadon becomes proficient with improvised weapons. Creatures that the caskadon is grappling and that are at least two sizes smaller than it are considered improvised weapons for the caskadon. The damage die for these weapons is equal to the size of the creature's Hit Dice, and they can't be thrown. When the caskadon hits with an attack using a creature as a weapon, that creature takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Hard Liquor
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer, Happy Hour.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

When the caskadon uses its Happy Hour action, it can choose to increase the pressure of the spray, changing it from a 30-foot cone into a 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line, and from a Dexterity saving throw into a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone} in addition to the other effects. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}.
When the caskadon's tamer reaches 13th level in the tamer class, the bludgeoning damage increases to 14 ({@damage 4d6}).

entries.name: Trojan Elephant
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

As an action, the caskadon can retract its trunk and legs and close its ears, making itself appear like a barrel. While in this form, its speed is 0 feet, it can't take actions other than reverting to its normal form, and it's indistinguishable from a normal barrel while it remains motionless.

While the caskadon is in this form, a creature within 5 feet of it can use its action to enter it, gaining total cover against effects outside the caskadon. The caskadon can hold up to two creatures one size smaller than it, or up to four creatures at least two sizes smaller than it. Breathing creatures inside the caskadon can survive there for up to 1 hour, after which they begin to suffocate. Any creatures inside the caskadon when it reverts to its normal form are ejected into unoccupied spaces of their choice within 5 feet of the caskadon.

entries.name: Bar Brawler II
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer, Bar Brawler I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The caskadon can use creatures one size smaller than itself as improvised weapons. The damage that a creature takes when the caskadon hits with an attack using it as a weapon increases to 4 ({@damage 1d8}).
In addition, the caskadon can throw a creature it is using as an improvised weapon with a normal range of 10 feet and a long range of 20 feet.

entries.name: Growth III
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer, Growth II.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The caskadon's size increases to Large, its Hit Die size increases to a d10 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its Trunk attack increases to a d10.

entries.name: Growth IV
Prerequisite:
entries: 17th-level tamer, Growth III.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The caskadon's size increases to Huge, its Hit Die size increases to a d12 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its Trunk attack increases to a d12.

monsterFluff.name: Oventurer

entries: If you can look past the unsightly smoke, overpowering burnt smell, constant metallic creaking, ashes everywhere, conspicuous light, and suffocating heat, it makes for a great companion!

Devoid of taste buds and unable to eat, oventurers are peculiar constructs with a keen interest in culinary arts. They enjoy a rising popularity as hired help in taverns and restaurants, or as companions in adventuring parties tired of subsisting on the same dry old rations. Be careful when offering feedback on their meals, however—oventurers are far from defenceless, and might turn their scorching fire and combat expertise against overly harsh critics. No one likes a snob, least of all an oventurer!
If any of the oventurer's traits or actions require a saving throw, it is always against the oventurer's save DC, as calculated below.

entries.name: Save DC
entries.text: 8 + the tamer's proficiency bonus + the oventurer's Constitution modifier

entries.name: Monster Trainer
entries: In addition to the improvements you can normally give your companions, you can choose to grant the oventurer the following improvements using your Monster Trainer feature:

entries.name: Easy-Bake
Prerequisite:
entries: Become a tamer's companion.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (tamer).
The oventurer can be used as a set of cook's utensils, and its tamer has advantage on any ability checks using it in this way.

In addition, over the course of a short or long rest, the oventurer can cook a delicious snack for up to four creatures, which takes 1 minute to consume. A creature that eats a snack gains temporary hit points equal to the tamer's proficiency bonus. If the snack isn't consumed immediately after the rest, it cools down and doesn't grant any benefits.

entries.name: Smokescreen I
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
While the oventurer's fire is lit, it can cast the {@spell fog cloud} spell, centred on itself and requiring no components. When the spell is cast in this way, it produces dark smoke instead of fog.
Once the oventurer casts this spell, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a long rest.

entries.name: Superheat I
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).
While the oventurer's fire is lit, its attacks deal an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) fire damage on a hit.

entries.name: Coal Toss
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
The oventurer gains a ranged weapon attack: Coal Toss. The damage die of the Coal Toss attack increases with the die size specified in the oventurer's Growth improvement.
{@b Coal Toss.} Ranged Weapon Attack: Str modifier + PB to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 1d4 + Str modifier bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Growth
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The oventurer's size increases to Small, its Hit Die size increases to a d6 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its attacks increases to a d6.

entries.name: Multiattack
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
As an action, the oventurer makes two attacks.

entries.name: Smokescreen II
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer, Smokescreen I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

When a creature enters the area of a {@spell fog cloud} created by the oventurer for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
In addition, the oventurer regains the ability to cast the spell when its tamer finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Superheat II
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer, Superheat I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion), Active (bonus action).

The extra fire damage dealt by the oventurer's attacks while its fire is lit increases to 3 ({@damage 1d6}). In addition, while the oventurer's fire is lit, it can use a bonus action to cast the {@spell heat metal} spell, with a range of touch and requiring no components.
Once the oventurer casts this spell, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a long rest.

entries.name: Convection
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

While the oventurer's fire is lit, it can blow a gale of heated air from within itself, casting the {@spell gust of wind} spell, which requires no components. A creature that makes a saving throw against this spell also takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Once the oventurer casts this spell, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a long rest.

entries.name: Hot Plate
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The oventurer gains a +2 bonus to AC as long as it's not wearing armour. In addition, while the oventurer's fire is lit, a creature that hits it with a melee attack takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

entries.name: Easy-Banquet
Prerequisite:
entries: 17th-level tamer, Easy-Bake.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

When the oventurer cooks a snack over the course of a long rest, roll a {@dice d4}. On a 4, the oventurer instead cooks an entire meal for up to four creatures, which takes 1 hour to consume and grants the benefits of the {@spell heroes' feast} spell. A creature can choose to consume only a small portion, which nonetheless depletes its portion of the meal, takes 1 minute, and grants the benefits of a snack from the Easy-Bake improvement.

monsterFluff.name: Dire Bunny

entries: Yes, a dire bunny is a great enchanting buddy, valuable rogue-like combatant, and excellent at getting the truth out of folks. But what no-one talks about is their ability to plough a field. One word and thousands of bony limbs dig it all up. Just don't take them to a graveyard, they get... distracted.
Type: Aberration
Creature Component: Bonemonger
Bonus Tamer Improvement: Sneak Attack

Astral hermits are a strange and esoteric species. Mercantile in nature, those that visit the Material Plane choose forms that appear innocuous and unthreatening, to better sell their enchanting services. The hermit from which this specimen, dubbed the 'dire bunny', has been crafted had a peculiar fascination with bones that has been surprisingly difficult to remove.
If any of the dire bunny's traits or actions require saving throws, it is always against the dire bunny's save DC, as calculated below.

entries.name: Save DC
entries.text: 8 + the tamer's proficiency bonus + the dire bunny's Constitution modifier

entries.name: Monster Trainer
entries: In addition to the improvements you can normally give your companions, you can choose to grant the dire bunny the following improvements using your Monster Trainer feature:

entries.name: Sneak Attack
Prerequisite:
entries: become a tamer's companion.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

Once on its turn, the dire bunny can deal an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) damage to one creature it hits with a Bite attack if it has advantage on the attack roll. The dire bunny doesn't need advantage on the attack roll if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet of it, that enemy isn't incapacitated, and the dire bunny doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll. The extra damage increases by {@damage 1d6} when the dire bunny's tamer reaches 3rd level ({@damage 2d6}), 5th level ({@damage 3d6}), 9th level ({@damage 4d6}), 13th level ({@damage 5d6}), and 17th level ({@damage 6d6}) in the tamer class.

entries.name: Astral Enchanter I
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action), Passive (tamer).

While the dire bunny is summoned, its tamer has a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Enchanting|HelianasGuidetoMonsterHunting} checks. In addition, as an action, the dire bunny can cast the {@spell identify} spell, requiring no material components. After it uses this action, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a long rest.

entries.name: Mi!
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

As an action, the dire bunny proclaims "Mi!" at one creature it can see within 60 feet of it. If the target can hear the proclamation, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 4 ({@damage 1d8}) thunder damage and 4 ({@damage 1d8}) psychic damage, and become {@condition frightened} of the dire bunny until the end of the dire bunny's next turn.

entries.name: Veracious Question
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (bonus action), Passive (companion & tamer).

The dire bunny gains proficiency in the Persuasion and Intimidation skills and, while the bunny is summoned, its tamer can add half its proficiency bonus to ability checks it makes that use the Insight, Intimidation, or Persuasion skills. In addition, when the dire bunny asks a question, it can use a bonus action to imbue the question with magical truth. If the question's recipient can hear (or otherwise perceive) and understand the question, it must make a Charisma saving throw, blurting out a short, truthful answer on a failure, or answering as it pleases (if at all) on a success. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw. After the dire bunny has used this bonus action twice, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Grasping Bonetide I
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

As an action, the dire bunny summons a wave of animated bones that ripples out from it in either a 30-foot cone or 15-foot-radius circle (dire bunny's choice). Skeletal limbs burst out from surfaces in the area, slashing and grasping at creatures in contact with those surfaces. Each of those creatures must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) slashing damage and becoming {@condition restrained} until the end of the dire bunny's next turn on a failure, or taking half as much damage and not becoming {@condition restrained} on a success. After the dire bunny uses this action, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a short or long rest. When the dire bunny's tamer reaches 9th level in the tamer class, the damage of this action increases to 17 ({@damage 5d6}).

entries.name: Boney Carapace I
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).
The dire bunny gains a +2 bonus to its AC while it isn't wearing armour.

entries.name: Springstrike
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer, Sneak Attack.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action), Passive (companion).
The dire bunny's speed increases by 10 feet. As an action, the dire bunny can move up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks and make one Bite attack.

entries.name: Uncanny Dodge
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (reaction).
When an attacker that the dire bunny can see hits it with an attack, the dire bunny can use its reaction to halve the attack's damage against it.

entries.name: Zone of Retributive Truth
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer, Veracious Question.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (bonus action).

As a bonus action, the dire bunny manifests an aura that lasts for 1 minute and extends 20 feet from the dire bunny, moving with it. As part of the bonus action to create the aura and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, the dire bunny can ask one creature it can see within the aura a question. If the creature can hear (or otherwise perceive) the question and understands it, it feels compelled to answer truthfully. If it doesn't answer before the end of its next turn, or answers untruthfully, it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be thrown up to 20 feet in a direction of the dire bunny's choice. After the dire bunny has manifested this aura, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Astral Enchanter II
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer, Astral Enchanter I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action), Passive (tamer).

While the dire bunny is summoned, its tamer has a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Enchanting|HelianasGuidetoMonsterHunting} checks. This is in addition to the bonus from Astral Enchanter I. As an action, the dire bunny can cast the {@spell identify} spell at will, requiring no material components.

entries.name: Grasping Bonetide II
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer, Grasping Bonetide I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The damage of the dire bunny's Grasping Bonetide action increases to 24 ({@damage 7d6}), and the size of the area increases to a 50-foot cone or a 20-foot-radius circle. The dire bunny gains a +1 bonus to the save DC of this action. When the dire bunny's tamer reaches 17th level in the tamer class, the damage of this action increases to 35 ({@damage 10d6}).

entries.name: Boney Carapace II
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer, Boney Carapace I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).
The dire bunny gains a +2 bonus to its AC while it isn't wearing armour. This is in addition to the bonus from Boney Carapace I.

entries.name: Astral Enchanter III
Prerequisite:
entries: 17th-level tamer, Astral Enchanter II.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action), Passive (tamer).

While the dire bunny is summoned, its tamer has a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Enchanting|HelianasGuidetoMonsterHunting} checks. This is in addition to the bonuses from Astral Enchanter I & II. As an action, the dire bunny can cast the {@spell locate object} spell, requiring no material components. This spell ends early when the dire bunny is dismissed.

entries.name: Bone Cage
Prerequisite:
entries: 17th-level tamer, Boney Carapace II.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion), Active (action).

The dire bunny gains a +1 bonus to its AC while it isn't wearing armour. This is in addition to the bonuses from Boney Carapace I & II. As an action, the dire bunny creates a magical effect identical to the {@spell bone cage|HelianasGuidetoMonsterHunting} spell with a range of self (30-foot-radius sphere). After it uses this action, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a long rest.

monsterFluff.name: Swarm of Magnetite Cutlery
entries: Swarms of magnetite objects are a fighter's worst nightmare. The rare metal used in the construction of these implements gives them powerful magnetic properties that repel attacks from ferrous weapons, rendering the mightiest mauls and sharpest arrows nigh unusable. No foe is without a chink in its armour, however; intense heat can momentarily suppress the magnetic properties of the metal. One powerful blast of fire can immobilise an animated object, buying an adventurer precious seconds to strike... or run for their life.

entries.name: Magnetite Cutlery
entries: From the depths of the dining rooms flies a swarm of steel knives, forks, spoons, and the most lethal of all: sporks. Such objects can be bound by protective enchantments to guard an area, or under the direct control of a creature capable of manipulating the arcanomagnetic spectrum, such as a magnetite dragon.

items.name: Magnetite Weapons
entries: Animated weapons are a common staple in a mage's lair. In an instant, a well-stocked armoury transforms into a whirling flurry of flying swords, pikes, and hammers. The movement of the magnetic fields generates powerful bursts of lightning in the swarm's epicentre, emitting forceful pulses that launch ferrous objects at deadly speeds. When whipped into an enormous swarm, their greater number allows them to redistribute the negative effects of heat to avoid complete immobilisation.

monsterFluff.name: Arsenal of Flying Weapons
entries: An animated, levitating object is deadly on its own, but its effectiveness compounds when united by a swarming hivemind. Powerful mages can summon such whirling dervishes from the forgotten debris of battlefields.

entries.name: Flying Weapons
entries: An ancient orcish war idiom summarises the arsenal of flying weapons: "Even the toughest warrior falls to the untrained chaos of an army." With over fifty weapons in its retinue, such a swarm is an unrestrained cyclone of wood and metal, blades banging against spear points, mace heads clashing into hafts. This tornado of death is a striking menace on any battlefield, more of an obstacle than a combatant. When not commanded by its creator, the swarm searches eagerly for the next battle.

monsterFluff.name: Swarm of Baby Mimics
entries: Mimica mutafigura are patient predators that use disguise to surprise unwary creatures. A mimic will then adhere themselves to their prey, making up for their slow speed and inability to pursue a quarry. They are popular choices for warding dungeons and tombs from treasure hunters; black dragons even breed mimics to populate their lairs as false hoards, stalagtites, and dragon eggs.
Most mimics reproduce through a budding process, producing hundreds of children. Baby mimics disguise themselves as objects related to the parent's, such as coins to a chest or fruits to a basket.

monsterFluff.name: Bonemonger Phalange
entries: Little is known about astral hermits thanks to their propensity to set up shop in hard-to-reach locations. They are far-travelling aberrations, each developing extremely specific obsessions such as light, words, or bones. Astral hermits study their area of interest in meticulous detail, using it as a medium through which to learn more of the world.

entries.name: Corporeal Forms
entries: A male astral hermit is made up of two bodies: his heart and his hearth. An astral hermit's hearth is a maze-like construction at the centre of which is a portal to the Astral Plane. The astral hermit's heart is how he presents himself to the world. Usually, this is in a form that is inviting to creatures of that plane, a tactic which ensures plentiful trade.

entries.name: Reproduction
entries: Male and female astral hermits lead two very different existences. A male astral hermit's hearth is his bower, and his heart can never travel further than a few hundred feet from it. The abundance and richness of its treasures (which to the common person may appear as worthless relics) help to impress females of the species, which flit through the Astral Plane searching for portals into the hearth's of other hermits. When a union is formed, eggs are produced and jettisoned into the astral expanse.

entries.name: Crafters and Enchanters
entries: Astral hermits are innately magical beings, able to imbue items or creatures with enchantments shaped by their subject of obsession. Unable to travel far from their hearths, astral hermits create reputations of skill with enchanting and use that as the currency with which they trade for relics that further fuel their obsession.

entries.name: Bonemonger
entries: The bonemonger's obsession is bones. Fossilised, inscribed, splintered, or fresh, the bonemonger can read the history of every bone that passes him, learning much of the creatures to which they once belonged. The bonemonger has also learnt to animate these bones with his necromantic magic, allowing them to act as guards, workers, and scouts. The bonemonger chooses the form of a tiny white rabbit as his heart, an unassuming form that is nevertheless extremely deadly when threatened.

monsterFluff.name: Brain Devourer
entries: Brain devourers are among the most gruesome denizens of The Low—wrinkled, grey brains supported by legs formed from twisted nerves. Seeing one instantly strikes fear into seasoned veterans, a delicious sentiment which is promptly drained by the hungry abomination. A single brain devourer can syphon the emotions of an entire city until its inhabitants are mindless husks.

entries.name: Astral Transcendance
entries: Brain devourers were once humanoids on a quest for cognitive transcendance. Gradual biomantic alterations ultimately caused them to shed all semblance of physical form, leaving only what they consider an intermediate step in humanoid evolution. Brain devourers believe the final step requires the consumption of a massive amount of brains, after which they will finally leave their bodies behind and ascend to the Astral Sea.

entries.name: Ravenous Hordes
entries: When a city's guards prove too tough to crack, brain devourers are driven to form ferocious swarms by their voracious hunger. In a group, they trade subtlety for sheer numbers, and the resulting condensation of psychic energy manifests as a massive brain storm. To avoid this, mind flayers carefully manage brain devourer populations by feeding them periodically.

monsterFluff.name: Broodling
entries: Subservient to their aboleth broodmothers, these ray-like amphibians scour the swamps they call home in search of creatures with high biomass or interesting features.
